Excellent apartment. Excellent professional approach. Comfortable house
Very beautiful apartments

This hotel may not be a five star hotel but it has a character and touch of the owner's dedication to make the guests' stay a comfortable and pleasant one. I loved the cleanliness and the friendly attitude. Breakfast is simple, sausage and scrambled eggs with different kinds of breads, jams and jelly, fresh juices and coffee. It filled me up, so I'm not going to complain.  If I want more varieties, I can  pay more money and stay at an expensive hotel for some extra cheese for breakfast. But I chose this hotel and happy to get what they're offering. Acropolis, Plaka, Monastriki 10 minutes away and Uber cost about 6 euros. The rooms are good size and the best thing is the little private patio to chill.  There's a "safe locker" in the room. Internet speed is very good. My experience here is very satisfying and I highly recommend this hotel to the "non fussy" travellers for a comfortable, inexpensive stay in Athens.

I don't know who rated this hotel as high as they did which caused me to choose the hotel but... The air conditioner leaked on the bed and night table it is clearly an ongoing issue as you can see rotting wood down by the floor. The hotel is on a dirty side street. The older male manager or owner is not pleasant, at all! The girl who worked days was pleasant and helpful! I do not recommend Hotel Nicola.

This is possibly one of the best value for money hotels that I have stayed at on my travels -- and I've been on the road since July 2017. The place is spacious and clean. The WiFi works! The breakfast was generous, the bar prices very reasonable. Best thing about this hotel is the staff -- they are all friendly and ready to help. Also a mine of info about what to do and see in Athens. Highly recommended.

Nice and cozy hotel with balcony at very reasonable price compared to other hotels in Athens. If you are not too picky, you will be fine. Free breakfast and the area is safe and closed to bus and taxi. Service is awesome as owner and staff will go above and beyond to help with request to make guest happy. Rooms are well cleaned. My suggedtions for improvement are: better wifi which is not strong and brither lights around lobby area.

This is a small gem for the people looking for a clean and neet bed and breakfast in Athens Greece  it is a family managed hotel where all the family make you feel that you are an extended family member not a guest very courteous understanding of guests needs they do their best to accommodate you and serve you with great attitude very helpful speak English. I do recommend this place for families and business
I love this place..very nice n comfort..the best hospitality ever❤..very central u can easily rich the center n all around places..thanks..
There were bedbucks, a lot!!! I had more than 70 bites, after this I tried to talk with the owner and he didn’t answer me and don’t want to talk about it, very bad treatment!
Very simple accomodation,  but comfortable enough and inexpensive.
Nice place, but there is crime in the neighborhood, because lately there is smuggling and drug trade nearby... But there is police
Excellent apartment. Very close to the metro. Direct access.

This Hostel is nice and cheap. Most important: Beds, toilets and rooms are very clean. They pay much attention to that. Common space is basic but you can socialize if you want. Staff is helpful too. Kitchen is missing many things. As about the location I really like it. You can get cheap food and drinks around. Also the metro station is less than a minute away.
I would definitely come back for all the reasons above.

I don’t think I could have asked for a better experience especially considering the price. The staff are incredible, I felt safe, met the loveliest people and made memories I’ll never forget. The rooms are simple and clean. There’s a communal space downstairs where I worked when needed (and the wifi works perfectly). I walked to Syntagma and Plaka every day with no trouble, while staying vigilant as a woman traveling alone. This is a student area and it’s nice to see them hanging out at cafes and bars close by. Zorbas Hostel has a special place in my heart.

Been in the country for almost 2 months and stayed in 7 different hostels. I can honestly say Zorbas ranks #1 out of all of them. The A/C works wonderfully, the rooms and beds are comfortable, and there’s always a staff member awake if you need anything. They are very kind and helpful. Every other hostel I’ve stayed in has major drawbacks that keep me from feeling totally comfortable but not Zorbas.

Pro tip: spend the extra few euros and upgrade to the 4 person room. Trust me on this. My first stay was in the 6-person room and you share the upstairs bathroom with a lot of other people (there’s more downstairs though) and there’s only one charging strip. The 4 person rooms have charging outlets by the beds, they’re cozier, and have a bathroom.

I like this place. Rooms are clean, bed comfortable and sheets are fresh. People very helpful and sweet. Good price. It is quiet place and I had good sleep. The only issue was shower glass that was only on the one side of shower and it is impossible to shower without making wet everything around.

Great reception desk team, pretty clean place and close to Victoria station. Definitely recommend if you are looking for an affordable hostel in downtown Athens. Just be wary that the area is somewhat considered unsafe, so keeping an eye to your belongings while around isn't a bad idea.

There’s something special about this place. And the people who work here. It’s without a doubt the best two-star hotel I have ever stayed at.

And for women out there, some of the rooms have the best make-up table there exists in a hotel!

The only improvement I would do would be to change doors / isolate better for hallway noise. But still, at night it’s super quiet. The cleaning personal are the kindest. So are the staff in reception. I love the 70’s art deco vibe in the rooms. I would definitely stay again.

Clean, tidy hotel.
Lovely staff, very helpful and friendly.
Little bit away from main tourist area, 2 - 4 km easy walk depending where you are going.
Felt safe walking around.
Close to all public transport options.
Great food options, all nearby .
Will stay here again.
Room on smaller side, but we were only sleeping there, out most days.
The shower had great water pressure and good hot water.
Housekeeping is great.

Our stay at the Victory inn was very pleasant. Our room was very large with ample space for our luggage. The bed was comfortable but the pillows quite thin. As we only stayed for two nights we made do. The bathroom was a bit on the smaller side but fine for a couple.

Good proximity to public trans, easy places to eat and supermarket. Nice local neighborhood 2 blocks away; restaurants, bars, art etc. Place was very clean, staff extremely acommodating. AC works well, room was comfortable and quiet with all standard amenities and private balcony. Simple and fresh selection of local breakfast options served daily. Perfect for a night or 2 while traveling.
34 room boutique hotel! Smallish rooms but very clean and tidy. Not far (walking distance from most of the major Athens attractions. Good food options nearby!

I really enjoyed my stay at Petaluda, it was just right for me. Izabella and Manolis are lovely people and great hosts who gave all directions for arriving, it's around a 15 minute walk from Victoria station. For the budget price you're paying, it's perfect. The place is cosy, charming and clean with access to a shared kitchen and bathroom that were always very clean, I only ran into some guests once with a friendly hello but besides that you have your privacy. The room is totally fine and spacious, and I had my own private balcony with a view over the rooftops and streets. Bed was comfy enough for me!

There are people in other reviews complaining about the area being "dangerous" but I didn't feel this, Athens has it's gritty charm many places and I like that, the local people are fine and friendly but I guess some people are frightened by a little graffiti and diverse communities... Go pay €150 per night in Monistraki if that's a problem for you.

Manolis and Izabella also kindly let me check out an hour later and I really appreciated that, when I need to I'll stay again for sure! Thanks. See you again

This is a great hostel for travellers with small budget.  The hosts are very friendly and kind natured and willing to accommodate requests within their capacity. They don’t stay on premises, but if you call them, they will be happy to come to the place and open the front door when you first arrive to check-in.
The property itself is clean and quiet, with 2 common bathrooms and a kitchen for 5 guest rooms in total.

The street facing rooms may sometimes have traffic noise.

The owner was super lovely and made us feel very welcome. The bathrooms are shared, but there are two bathrooms for the four rooms here, so you won't have to share woth with many people. The bathrooms were also really nice and clean, and so were the rooms. Air conditioning worked well, beds were comfortable and we really enjoyed our stay.

I had such a positive experience at Iza's place. I turned up at dinner time with the wrong booking dates. Iza quickly whipped up a room ready for me last minute and managed to secure me the room for the 4 nights I originally needed, in the middle of busy summer! Talk about looking after you. She was so caring and made sure I was relaxed and comfortable. I felt like I was being welcomed by family to clean and comfortable home. I cried from the gratitude I felt!
To the other comments about the area - I am a solo female traveller and walked around late at night with no issue. There are amazing businesses right downstairs and all around owned by wonderful people. There were families playing football in the park late at night to give you a sense of the area.
Thank you so much Iza! I will be back in the future :)

Loved it! Room was spacious (had a nice, quiet balcony) and the entire place was pristine. Pretty good breakfast, there’s a shared kitchen where I could keep my food in the fridge. Great value for money!

Quiet hours disturbance all the time by deranged Erasmus students that behave without any respect.

Take your apartments away from buildings that families actually live inside and want to sleep.

I booked this house in december and I red the review only few days before going there. The house was okay there was some mold around but the flat was decent. The house was completly dark but was not the fault of the agency.
But despite the decent condition of the flat the big problem was the agency. When you come they call you to sign up some documents and they tell you that for the bed sheets and the cleaning lady you have to pay. They will remove this money from the deposit. The cleaning lady is mandatory and is a way for the agency to control the tentants and what they are doing. The bed sheets are dirty and they are almost transparent because they washed it thousands times. The agency and the cleaning lady they entered all the time without knocking or ringing the bell, with their own keys. At the end of my staying there they came (with their keys without announcing them) to see for some reparations inside the flat. They checked and they told me that they had to do some reparetion in the empty room of my flatmates. The day after I found them inside my flat and they removed the painting from the ceiling in my bathroom. I was really angry because they didn't tell me anything so I went to the office to talk with them and they told me was my fault because I didn't open the window during the shower. They also said that the cleaning lady took a picture every week and the mold situation was every week worst. They did the reparation in my last three days there and they left a disaster around the house. There was dust all around, smeel of painting and when they finished they didn't clean anything. You have to write to them for the deposit back because they didn't ask for it. So at the end I can say: run away from the agency they are the worst and with the fact that they always enter without asking you will not feel respect and in your space. They will not give you your privacy.

This agency is disgusting. I would not recommend it if you are planning to live in Athens, specially if you are Erasmus. My experience here was awful. At first they are kind because they want to STEAL your money. They obligate you by signing a contract which later they won’t fulfill. My roommates and me paid the deposit they claimed and after our stay they took 100 euros from each person without any valid and legal explanation. Apart from this, we had to ask many times to please DO NOT ENTER to our house with their own set of keys. Also, if you compare it with other agencies or particulars, it is more expensive and the quality of the flat does not accomplish the price-quality.
I am still having a horrible experience with them and I hope they lose their customers.

Be careful with them! (ERasmus Rooms Athens - picture uploaded bellow)...
Writing this as a warning to all my Erasmus colleagues.
They extra charge you for things they don't mention until the signing of the contract (like borrowing/washing towels and linen for 60 euros, cleaning, etc.). Furthermore, you're lucky if you get a fully functioning apartment. Most of them are old, in buildings where neighbours don't really appreciate students and complain/call the police and the apartments themselves are falling apart! This is the biggest issue. They have poorly maintained ours for months, trying to fix the toilets, both of the refrigerators weren't working, it took them almost a month to provide us with wifi and so on.
Don't expect them to refund you for anything, after they kick you out they wont send any word until they give you your deposit back without explanation.

Please be careful, this company is misleading. I was an Erasmus student in Athens, I lived there for 4 months, in Omonia. First I found the company via internet, everything was fine with them. I paid a deposit in the value of 1 month's rent. Upon arrival, they contacted me immediately to come sign the documents. They state uniform accommodation prices everywhere, but upon arrival they start charging meaningless fees, e.g. for bed linen, cleaning lady (she really cleaned), towels, etc. The room was relatively fine, but during my entire stay they weren’t able to fix a toilet bowl (even though I warned them several times afterwards). The bathroom wasn’t in the best condition. At the end of my stay, I was completely surprised that they didn’t send me the deposit within the agreed time. They sent it to me only after I contacted them, of course later than stated in the contract. I didn't even get half of the agreed amount back. Their justification was additional nonsensical charges for linen, towels, bank charges and they even said they had to specially clean my “dirty” room. When I contacted them again for a refund, they didn’t reply me. I definitely don’t recommend this company for accommodation in Athens, it’s not so cheap and u cannot trust hem. My Erasmus colleagues had much nicer accommodation and even much cheaper, where I spent more time. Take care.
Wonderful and stylish rooms to let with great atmosphere , mostly made by the owners. Good value for money. Highly recomended.
Location is ok, it's more like air BnB. It have kitchen and wash machine which is useful.
Its nice
Cool

Very close to the Attiki metro stop, about 10 minutes away, and 2 minutes away there is a large supermarket and many bars and bakeries, as well as a pharmacy right next to the gate. The apartment is comfortable, the building has an elevator and the rooms have air conditioning and a large space with a good built-in wardrobe and very comfortable beds. The kitchen is also very well equipped, there is even a washing machine and everything you need to hang clothes on the balcony. The only drawbacks are the mosquitoes and that the apartment does not have much power of light, you cannot use the washing machine and oven at the same time or it will break. Otherwise it is very good.

Bit out of the centre of Athens, metro is about 10 minutes away, parking is possible next door (paid, leave your keys), rooms are  mixed bunch. We stayed here twice during our trip to Greece. The first room we had was reasonably modern, but don’t expect a view. There even was a window into the interior of the hotel. The second room was older, extremely noisy, not enough outlets, smelly and small bathroom. Hope for a renovated room.

We had a good time at the hotel! I dont know about the other rooms, but our room was modern and clean and we even had a balcony. The room service was very nice and cleaned every day. The breakfast is simple but good. We saw two different men who worked at the reception and both of them were very friendly and helped us every time we had a request :)
The breakfast was probably the worst thing about this hotel. Very little variety, and I got a raw egg once, so be careful. The overall experience was okay, but nothing more.

It's a great place if you are on a budget and super close to transit.  About 15 min bus to Plaka and Monastiraki. All rooms have terraces, which is nice and they are generally clean. The bathrooms could use a spruce up and they didn't have a safe in the room for our passports. You can see the Parthenon from the roof.

Good hotel in an area well connected to the center. It is not very close to the touristic heart of Athens, but there are busses and trolleys (super close to the hotel) that take around 15 minutes to Syntagma or around it, as well as 10 minutes walk to the metro connecting the area with the city center. Excellent service! Clean and quiet - with the exception of some cars noise in the night which was OK for us. Basic decent breakfast, but there are various shops and bakeries in the area to eventually supplement that, including a nice coffee shop across the street. The rooms are quite large, several have balconies, and are cleaned every day which is refreshing. The hotel has 24h staff, which is good. Has an elevator, and a small terrace on its rooftop on the 5th floor, which offers a lovely view over Athens.
Nice and comfy place (lot of space to 10 people). Very good location. Recommend to travelling families

Deceptive company that tries to deceive all its tenants (Erasmus students) and treats them in inhumane ways. I was living in one of their apartments throughout the year and they did not fix some humidity and during the week of July that I stayed in another of their apartments they did not clean it all week and I was living among dirt and garbage. Furthermore, the treatment on their part was despicable, having received threats on the last day of my stay there.
Spacious and clean apartment in a good location and good handling

Lovely lovely!!! Employees were so kind and helpful, the hotel is just two stops away from the center so it's less pricey and quieter but not too far at all. The room itself is sweet and I had a nice balcony to sit on and get some fresh air. Very happy with my stay here! :)
The staff was really kind and helpful, the neighbourhood is silent and easily accessible by metro.

The room is very comfortable with excellent water pressure and plenty of hot water.  It would appear that they have just done a remodeling.
I had to check in late, and the desk was very professional and understanding.  He was also helpful in pointing out places of interest in Athens.  The location is on a quiet street and off the beaten path.  I stayed here 4 nights and will would return.

Very clean and nice rooms.
No elevator. Very quiet place.
All the staff of the hotel are very kind and thay give you hot and friendly feelings.
It is very near to the underground train that goes directly to the airport and the city center.

Good service,its close from train station
and all Market,im thankeful 🙏✅

Athens Hive is a fantastic hotel! The rooms are spotless, spacious, and very comfortable. The staff is incredibly friendly and helpful, always ready to assist with anything you need. The location is ideal, close to major attractions and public transportation. I will definitely choose it again for my next visit to Athens!

Building is new and clean, rooms are spacious and cosy. It takes just 8 minutes to reach the Attiki metro station that provides direct connections with the most important places such as Monastiraki Square, Syntagma Square or Pireus Port. In the room you will find well equipped kitchen (basic dishes, toster, kettle, fridge, cooker), bathroom (towels, shampoo, conditioner, shower gel, toilet paper and even hair dryer included) and bedroom with a TV, wardrobe and air conditioning (that saves your life during the summer). The whole building is surprisingly quiet. The host is really lovely, she left a welcome gift in the room (cakes, water and juice), she quickly responses for the messages and is always willing to help, don't forget to ask her about restaurant recommendations :)

everything was very good.  apartment clean and exactly like in the photos.  additionally, a welcome cookie, water, coffee, tea and sugar in the apartment were a very nice surprise.  we spent a very nice time in the apartment.  Thank you very much and we wholeheartedly recommend it.  location close to the subway, bus stops.  a supermarket next door.  great pubs in the area.  host very polite and helpfull helpful.

New apartment type hotel. Lovely staff and generally not bad for its price but the cooking facilities are quite limited (no chopping boards, knife, microwave etc) and also the whole place gets really cold (there’s an air con in the bedroom but) and shower often has no hot water.....and there’s no lift so it could difficult if you have walking issues or heavy luggage
The best place for renting

Worst airbnb I have ever stayed at, and I’ve stayed at many. The apartment was cramped and small. We stayed for five nights total and the host was unwilling to provide extra towels, so we had damp towels the whole stay. The shower leaked all over the bathroom. There was no hot water and despite following the host’s instructions perfectly, the host was uncooperative with checking on the hot water or helping us. We followed the instructions provided, and he said the hot water should work for a 15 minute period before shutting off, but the longest the hot water ever stayed on was 1-2 minutes. It was a very unpleasant stay because we could not have a hot shower after a long day. The kitchen was dirty with food that expired last year still in the fridge and an unclean sink. The bed was uncomfortable and we had to use the “couch” pillows because not enough bed pillows were provided to sleep with. The “couch” was a palate with a cushion on it and the “table” was a wood palate on the floor. Curtains were see through so the apartment stayed bright all night, even with the wooden slats. In contrast, other hostels and air bnbs we booked along our trip were the same price but a world of a difference. Host continues to try to gaslight us that there could be no fault with his airbnb, and refuses to look into the hot water or clean his kitchen. Save your money and stay elsewhere for a more comfortable stay.

This was fantastic value for the price. The man that runs the rooms is incredibly helpful and informative. He described many places to see and eat and purchase groceries. He went out of his way to try to help me find other accomodation when he was booked out and even graciously drove us to these new premises free of charge. I i have never experienced such  magnificient service before. He made us feel very welcome .

nothing wad too much trouble for him.
Absolutely stunning what one can get for such price. The host is one of a kind as well. Beyond helpful and courteous.

Very good. It's cheap, clean, and the owner, Mr. Taki, is friendly. The location is also quite good. Airport pick-up service was 20 euros. Highly recommended. You can walk to the bus terminal heading north. This would also be an advantage.
Nice area
Very clean and big apartment, very close to the underground, very friendly landlord. Quiet area, shops nearby.
Metro nearby

Great location right next to Viktoria station, with quick access to centre of town. The room was nice and big, clean and just what we needed for a few nights in Athens. Thank you to the host for being hospitable and welcoming.
Great service. Thank you for helping me with a last minute booking.

5 stars for service is definitely not enough considering how much time Mrs. Iza spent with us. A wonderful woman, knows the city and its history. both ancient and modern. I would definitely recommend it to anyone going to Athens!! :)

We were very pleased to spend two nights at Petaluda House 3. Excellent Comfort and spaciousness of the rooms, unknown to me at this price. I recommend it with a clear conscience.

Ps. Iza, if you are reading this - marry me 💙

Great location- short walk to train, tram and metro. Very clean, well kept, comfortable place and cordial staff.  Cozy coffee lounge at ground floor.. Was upgraded to bigger room, which was actually quite big and very comfortable, although gets faint traffic noise.. was upgraded on room.  Huge beg, Plush bathroom, coffee maker, fridge… very comfortable stay overall.. would definitely recommend..!

Clean, modern rooms with everyday cleaning if You wish to. Hotel reception stuff is friendly and helpful. Great contact with no problems. Location is close to center and main tourist attractions only 30min walk to Acropolis.

We thoroughly enjoyed our 7 night stay in the middle of April. The room was large, clean and comfortable. The staff were friendly and helpful, and the location was pretty much perfect. Close to the metro and within easy walking distance of the city centre, with plenty of restaurants nearby. I would definitely stay again.

The room was very clean a welcoming. So was the guy in the reseption.
The balconi is bigger than the room and is nice to sit in at evening.
Locted a few blocks from the Metro. Many bus stops near by.
Not a walking distance from the akropolis area.

Nice hotel, the lobby area and the room were very clean, the hotel is located relatively close to the center and the metro station is really close, Harris was very kind and welcoming, was available for any question and was always right to help. I will definitely come again.

If you are considering this hotel and have children with you, read this first.

We needed a hotel at a reasonable distance from the airport because we had a long journey ahead of us with our children. So we decided while driving to book a room at this hotel. We needed to sleep 6 hours before we had to leave again.

We arrived at the location late at night and there were people in the streets giving us nasty looks as we tried to find a parking spot. Once we found a place to park, we felt unsafe in the area. The first thing that happens is that a man comes up to my wife and says something in his language while licking his mouth.

When we arrived at the entrance, which is the most beautiful part of the entire hotel, we were greeted by a very pleasant receptionist.

He said that the area is not safe at night. We were given the room key and a control for the air conditioning (which didn't work at first), and were sent up into the world's smallest elevator.

The first thing that greeted us at the room door was the door lock/door handle hanging loosely from the door.

The room had decent beds, but the rest felt very indelicate. The room smelled of sewage, it was extremely worn all over and the bathroom had a cockroach jumping around in the shower.

We heard a lot of noise from other guests. it took time before we were able to sleep and we got maybe 3-4 hours of sleep. We just wanted out and away from this place.

We also parked in the middle of a market that set up on the corner, which locked us in for the morning. Fortunately, there was a nice fellow who helped us out.

There was an incredible amount of queues at 7 o'clock and it took us almost an hour and a half to get to the airport.

We had a stressful and horrible experience.

The first day I arrived, the cleaners did not leave clean towels (see pics), did not thoroughly clean the basin, there is a drain that smells like sewage and there is a chair on the balcony that shouldn't be there-especially if you are checking in with a child. The waters are turned without notice and water pressure in the shower is not always great. This was reported to Reception and the drains were slightly fixed but the smell is worse in the evening (I had to cover the drain and burn incense sticks in the bathroom and leave the balcony door open). Until the third day, the cleaners stopped cleaning my room until check-out. I decided not to report it just in case they are prejudice and contaminate my products in my room so I left it but I complained about the water pressure. Receptionist are ok. I booked this hotel so I walk to my daughter's family home and put my head down. If you want to put your head down, maybe it's OK. But if want full hospitality, this is not the place. If you want a drink, they offer a bottle of water for €1 and no extras. Please think what you want from your travels before booking here. The good thing about the hotel, you have many shops and restaurants plus access to transportation.

The hotel is quite simple and modest, it’s ok to stay for a couple of days.
The staff is very friendly and helpful,the speak English well:)
I (a young woman) was in Greece all alone and my luggage was lost, so the staff helped me to call the airport and find out all the information, they calmed me down and my bags arrived just in time directly to the hotel!
So if you are reading this, a huge THANK YOU one more time !❤️

Julia from Moscow
September 2023

This hotel is one of the bad one After taking online payments from us and going to the hotel, the money was charged again. They said that they would provide water for drinking, but when we asked for water, they told us drink water from the bathroom.

The staff were pleasant but the rooms were in a sad state. The area, once part of the great metropolis now is an area you shouldn't walk at night. Also, though I made the reservation through an app and it showed as paid, when I arrived there was no reservation and I had to pay extra
Horrible ,when I went to the camp the food wasn't good

Comfortable apartments to accommodate seven with children or a group of up to 6 people. Quiet and cozy place. 100 m to the Agios Nikolos metro station, which allows you to be in the historical center of Athens in 10-15 minutes.
My wife in shock after blocked her room and no elecrtricity in this apt.

Don’t take this appartement if you don’t want to come infront of the building and stay there waiting with no body answering during 2h !!!

The guy reply me when we arrive at the harbor, I’d told him that we’ll arrive in 30min.. we spoke about the appartement .. he even ask me the adresse of which appartement he wasn’t sur about it !

But then .. when we arrive there no body answering any more !! Waiting there at the door building tired after long trip ! We go somewhere else ..

Good if you’re travelling on a budget, but stay at your own risk. Hotel is located at a somewhat dodgy area and the room was also quite old. Not sure why but the TV turned on by itself at midnight. AC was really cold tho, I had a comfortable sleep regardless.
The AC was noisy, carpet dirty, and our shower door exploded while opening it. There was a cute white cat outside the window which made the experience a bit better.

The service and cleaning service was great! However, the room looked like it needed some maintenance. A new clean carpet is needed. Also, my plan was to come with a car, but i decided to come to Athens by train. This turned out perfect for me because the hotel advertised “having parking” but it didn’t. There was no parking on the street or near the hotel. Overall, it is close to the city…20-30 minutes walking and it is next to a police station (which i felt safe) and the hotel service completed my requests.

Worst and dirtiest room we have ever stayed in. Beware and do not book this hotel. The location is fine and central enough to walk to all the sights if you are good on foot (approx. 45 min.).
But there were blood spots on the carpet, hair in the bed and really weird substances all over the furniture and floor. Also the toilet had spots of dried bodily liquids on the seat. Also the receptionist weirdly met with some girls in their room after his shift. Yes it’s cheap but even for this low price it’s a scam. We actually tried to change rooms, but the other room was no better. In my opinion there is no way the other positive reviews can be real. Don’t be fooled!

1. Takes time for hot water to arrive on the shower
2. The blanket is way too small
3. Freezing cold in the room. There is an AC but i could not turn it to heating (i can say the staff could have helped me if i ask, just chose not to because putting many layers of clothes fix this). The first night i woke up at 5.30 because of the cold and could not sleep in that weather. The next nights i put 3 layers of clothes on me while sleeping
4. Once after the room service woman cleaned me room i smelled ciggarets smell in there. Did not let her in since then
5. One of the reception guy is nag. He imsist of giving him the key every time i leave the room to go outside, so i have to get my key at the reception every time i enter. The other receptionists dont do it, he is the only one, and he insisted even after i told him he is the only one keeping my key "for security purposes". He also have me wait to get my signature after i extend my stay, while the day before when i extend my stay no signature was taken from me from the other receptionsit. This guy ruined my experience in this hotel. All the receptionist need to have the same work procedures, and not every one do what he wish or think is right.

Only good thing about this hotel is the price, but you can find other hotels with good price that do not have so much issues.

Respose to response-
1. as far as I remember, I did not extend the reservation 3 times. This is a lie. I only extend once. During the extention I had gone through that nag receptionist behaviour, after which I didn't return to this hotel.
2. When checked in I was not informed that I need to leave the key in reception every time I leave. As I mentioned- no receptionist did that except for the nag one.
3. Taking an extra cover would not solve the blanket being too small
Brother NONO

The staff is helpful and friendly. The apartment itself is not bad, even if it lacks the right details that can make the difference. It is penalized by the condominium (which needs a good refresh), and by the area, which is noisy both from the outside and from the adjacent apartments

Very nice apartment.
Equipped and clean.
The area is a bit ugly.
Far from the center and full of hostile people.
The apartment is very sweet
Luck, luck

One of the worst hotels I have ever been. It is not clean enough (the carpets are dirty and old), there are blood stains on the walls (dead mosquitos? cockroaches?). The WC had an old plastic door that was not closing properly. The balcony door was not closing properly either. There is, of course, no elevator. The reception desk is most of the times, empty. One can just take any key and go to any room, for all I know. The price: 40 euros per night. For what?!
Good hotel and lovely staff, will use again on my next trip. The area is fantastic with some great restaurants and bars nearby.
Worst hotel ever been. Extremely Dirty and unpleasant.  GET AWAY FROM THAT HOTEL. It is NOT accepted even from refugees.
such an amazing friendly lovely place.
Beautiful, clean, and quiet, close to buses going to the center, and close to public parks and very wonderful cafes.

Very good location.
Clean and spacious room and shower.
Windows not very well isolated as the noise from the street can be heard.
All in all was good, we had a good short stay.
Good value for money.
Recommend it.
Cheers
Small apartment that offers everything for one person or a couple. Very clean and friendly owner!

Clean room, practical because many shops nearby as well as the tram 15 minutes away on foot.
Located on Grand Boulevard and despite the double glazing it is very noisy day and night

Good room for a few nights in Athens.
Price/quality ratio is good.

Located within walking distance of Victoria metro station, via this station you can quickly reach all important places.
Good price and service ratio, clean room close to the electric

We have booked 5 nights for vacation in this hotel. I have controversal feelings for this place. The room was kinda okay however the bathroom was really small and the basin was outside of the bathroom in the room. The room looked into an inner wall and other houses walls so wasnt the nicest view. It is a self-checkin hotel, there was nobody there when we arrived, we recieved all the info by email to checkin so who would need local support, this is not the best place. The elevator is supersmall, only 1 person can fit in, people with wheelchair cannot use it. The breakfast was okay. Only the manager speaks in english from the staff who was there only in the weekdays morning for some hours therefor there were communication issues. They were kind and tried to be helpful however forgot to prepare breakfast boxes for us despite we asked it more times 3-4 days in advance. The hotel located 10 mins walk from the closest metro station and 2 stations away from the center but the surroundings are not the best and nicest, not beautiful at all and the beach is 1+ hour far. In overall this hotel can fit for 1-2 nights for experienced travellers/business trips but definitely not for longer staying for families or couples.

1900 Athenian House was an absolute gem! Kristos received us with a huge smile on his face and a very welcoming aura. The rooms were all relatively close to each other as per our request, and they also allowed us to check in earlier than expected. The breakfast was SOO YUMMY! Everything very fresh from the eggs, sausages, bacon, pastries, and even yogurt- wide variety and fresh. The rooms look modern and have nice bathrooms etc. The sizes vary, and there is some graffiti in the area. The Monastraki Square is about a 10 minute car ride. And they were also very helpful with coordinating the airport shuttles.

What an absolutely misleading website and write-up. Although clean, the hotel is situated in a very dodgy area of Athens, and I was advised by the taxi driver not to go out and walk at night alone. My room stank of cigarette smoke. I was placed in a noisy room facing the road and could hear everyone talking as they walked past. The building is pretty and well maintained but is in a terrible area..I didn't even stay beyond 2 hours. Tourist attractions were far and only reachable by public transport or taxi. I also.struggled to reach the hotel prior to arrival and also afterwards following up on my feedback.

URGENT: EVERYONE DO NOT GO THERE, VERY VERY VERY RUDE CUSTOMER SERVICE & ABUSIVE MANNER. To all tourists in the area be very careful of the misleading pictures. Hope this helps anyone who plans to book here.
Last minute booking at 2am! The guy I spoke to on the phone went out of his way to help me and get me a room for the night. Great service. The room was pretty nice too.
I really like the place
That they are worse than a brothel

I had a fantastic stay at Figleaf. The staff was friendly and attentive, and my room was clean, comfortable, and well-equipped. The location was perfect, close to Athens center. Highly recommend for anyone visiting Athens.

hotel is located in downtown athens, well within walking distance from the usual points of interest any tourist should visit. prices are affordable and premises are clean and well equipped.  staff is also very polite and helpfull. all in all a nice experience
Modern and convenient; extremely helpful solution for business trips, including colleagues visiting Athens. Strongly recommended.
Wonderful place in central Athens,  I loved everything; I highly recommend Fig leaf for its clean, cozy rooms. I will come back for sure!
Excellent stay, super clean, well equipped, and friendly staff. Close to downtown attractions.

The accommodation is recommended for a city trip for people who don't necessarily want to live in the center but in a typical neighborhood of Athens. Good facilities, short distances to public transport and the location on a traffic-calmed street are a big plus. Athens is a noisy city and you can still hear it in your accommodation, but you quickly get used to it. We are always safe
felt and the communication with the host was great.

And already the second time I go to this apartment with Airbnb, my son has always booked it for me! It is a very nice, spacious and clean apartment... the first time it was impeccable in terms of cleanliness and nothing was missing such as supplies, napkins, toilet paper, hairdryer, etc., while this time we went, the cleanliness and supplies left a little 'to be desired
The apartment has everything you need to spend a few days in Athens. The location is a bit bad, quite far from the center and little feeling of security at night.
Beautiful apartment, spacious, dark and climbing stairs
Kaya S
Great rooms for affordable prices, with a lovely cafe right underneath
Examining choice. Very good service from the owner

The interior of this apartment was nice, which was the only positive. We almost did not get out of the car when we arrived at the apartment because the neighborhood was so seedy and uninviting to say the least. Barred windows and graffiti on the entirety of all buildings surrounding this place. There was a big metal door that we had extreme difficulty locking and unlocking. If we did not have two men with us, we would never have been able to get in and out. No response from the owner when we called about that.  Blue Ark offered to arrange a tour for us through Athens. A driver came to pick our group of 6 up at 8:30 AM as arranged. First  we picked up a woman introduced as our tour guide. We stopped for a few minutes for the changing of the guards at the Parliament building and then the driver took us to the foot of the Acropolis. There the tour guide said we had to go in on our own to get our tickets and then read the plaques as we walked through the Acropolis. She could not enter with us because she forgot her tour guide badge . We then proceeded to walk up the hill and stand on line in the heat for 35 minutes only to find out that entry to the Acropolis is time stamped. So instead of getting into the Acropolis at 9:30 the time that we were dropped off there, we had to call the driver back because our time of entry was not until 11:45 AM. These tickets could have been purchased online and any reputable tour guide would know that and also be aware that there are lines to get the tickets and entry times. When they returned to get us, we requested that we stop for a sandwich because now we were going to be out much longer than anticipated . This was scheduled to be a 4 hour tour and the cost for six of us was €290. By the time we sat in over an hour of traffic to get sandwiches  we had to eat them in the car so that we could get back to the acropolis for our entry at 11:45 a.m. That ended our “tour”.  The driver and tour guide refused to come back to pick us up after we went through the Acropolis on our own because the 4 hour tour time was going to expire at 12:30, before we were out of the Acropolis. We paid them €290 in cash and then had to pay €60 for two cabs back to the apartment. Both Blue Ark and the car service company Fidelis Executive Transfer adamantly refused to provide any compensation to our group for this One Way ride to the Acropolis.

From the moment we arrived, Akis made us feel at home.  The place was perfect for our party of six. Very relaxing after a day of travel and walking around Athena. It is rare to find a place that is exactly as advertised. The sauna is exquisite as are the esthetics.  It's in a good location just a few stops on the metro or bus and you are downtown near the Acropolis.
Comfortable

Excellent house! One of our best stays in Athens so far. Everything was perfect. An old mansion renovated! It is modern while maintaining the glamor and style of the mansion. Very large house, with comfortable spaces, careful decoration, many facilities and with excellent cleanliness. The sauna makes the difference!! Initially we had some hesitations about the area, but in the end it didn't create any insecurity issue for us. There is a private parking and a supermarket nearby.
The owner, particularly kind and hospitable, welcomed us in a very good mood and patiently provided us with all the information we needed.
We look forward to visiting again!

Just perfect.. The best stay ever. We will definitely prefer it on our next trip!!! The host was willing to help us with anything we needed. The house is huge and spotlessly clean.  Akis, until the next time!!!! Thanks for all..

Moody owner. We are on vacation. Let’s not add to the stress. Not one of our four drivers knew where this place was. We were looking forward to dining here. There was some loss in communication with the organizer but owner added unnecessary drama to the mishap.

Wonderful place. Even better service from the reception through the cleaning service to the very roof, where the friendly staff will welcome you in the bar at the very top of the hotel. The queues to this place should be longer than to the Acropolis. All the best to the owners and staff as well as to all future hotel guests. You won't find anything better in Athens for an overnight stay. Restaurants in the area and you can get to the main attractions by taxi for 7 €. This level of service should be commendable.

Impossible to get an answer from them on the phone or through email. I showed up to the hotel at midnight as my flight arrived in Athens an hour prior and nobody was at the reception…. I had to book a night in another hotel. I’ve sent them an email and try to call them, no response from them…I personally feel like it’s a scam…

My husband and I booked our room more than a month in advance. Due to the strikes, there were an influx of other travelers who made their booking 24 hours or less. Because we were arriving after 20:00 (reception closes then), the hotel sent us a message with our room number. They said our room key would be in the door and the front door would be open. This immediately didn't sit right with me for safety reasons. (Anyone could come in and take a room.) When we arrived, we found someone already in our room. We weren't about to kick her out and get a dirty room, but I also have no idea whether she actually reserved the room because she never showed me proof. However, taking her at her word, we found one room left with the key in it. We decided to take it. About an hour later, we received a knock on our door. A second lady showed me the message from Reception that we were, in fact, in her room. We didn't want to screw over someone else just because our plans were ruined by another traveler and lack of planning from the Reception desk. We ended up giving the lady the room and went straight to the airport without a bed to sleep in.

I could understand if this was a small village and reception closed at 20:00, but this is a capital city. Also, leaving keys and doors unlocked is very unsafe, especially because you can see in the hotel from the street. For boasting about being "tech-forward," this hotel isn't even able to solve a simple problem of checking guests in accurately. I am thoroughly disappointed that I emailed the hotel twice with no response. We received no refund and we didn't even end up staying here.

Reception closes at 8. Does it mentioned it anywhere when you book? No. Have spent last 1.5 hour trying to figure out how to get to my room. Turns out my travel agent didn't forward an email saying that the key will be at the door. Literally that. In an apartment building where anyone can get in (I did, just had to push the door) and the key was outside the door. I opened and let myself in.
Inside there are no instructions or password for wifi. Signal for phone or internet is only by de window.
The bar at he rooftop advertised to be open from 4pm to midnight is of course closed.
There is not a place where you can buy water in the building.
No one answers to any of the phones in the website or the one in the confirmation.
Furniture is nice and new.

We loved our stay at the apartment. It was clean, comfortable and fully furnished with everything needed. The location is perfect with the metro close by. the area has everything from mini markets to shopping, restaurants... A very big thank you to George and Yuliia, who always made sure we felt at ease and at home and always ready to help us.
We booked very last minute and ended up having great experience. They’re really nice and the apartment was our favorite we stayed in Athens.

The apartment was perfect, very comfortable, and equipped with everything you need. Near a metro station and several bus stops make it easy to move in Athens. Finally, Ted and George were always a message away to answer any question.

Amazing location, with Victoria station only a 5 minutes walk from the apartment.
My Mum was staying at this apartment, and I went to visit her a few times. The apartment is absolutely spotless and fresh.
Next time I'm in Athens, I'll definitely be staying here. 😊

Place was amazing, everything was clean and neat. Location is perfect, its a 5 minute walk to the metro station and about 1 hour away from the airport.

The host is really nice and ✨cozy✨ themselves, really helpful and are there for you whenever you need them!

Loved the place, 100% worth it!
great airbnb appartments offered
Love it
Rest shoe leather slippers low prices for sore feet
If you what to see how an  80's communism apartment looks like, I'll recommend this place. The clean wasn't home.

It is a very nice hotel with a suitable price. The location is among a lively area with restaurants and small shops near to the hotel. The metro station is not far. The people who are managing the place are very kind and helpful. They advised me to go to the coffee shops, suitable shops, places to visit and Information about the transportation. Because I booked the room and booked for two friends, they picked up us from the airport.
The breakfast is good with suitable price.

Truly a great spot! I was originally booked for 4 nights, stayed an extra three after. Great value, very nice rooms with aircon and great housekeeping. Neighbourhood has some nice streets not too far, they will point you to the nice spots. Many public transport options. Owners are super helpful and friendly, also great to talk to. Note, owners changed since April this year so some of the old reviews no longer valid! They are really wanting to improve the place.

The rooms are very basic but clean. Unfortunately our room smelled like smoke and you can gear the street very well from the room which is not quiet. But the service is really good, very nice breakfast. They were very friendly and accommodating. The location is very central!

Nice place, everything is clean and the people working here really nice. It is well placed to go to the Liosion Station (KTEL bus). Only problem : the noise, you can hear almost everything that happen in the corridor and the stairs.
Friendly staff, very clean, quiet and comfortable hotel. Very good location in the center of Athens and close to the bus stop, metro and supermarket. highly recommended!

Very kind and helpful hosts, it is perfect for 2 people. I would totally suggest it to anyone who want to explore Athens. It is in a small walking distance from metro station and Athens center. Can't wait to go back!
Very nice design with attention to detail. Cosy and really practical near the city center and commute. Hosts extremely helpful!
The apartment is excellent! Spotlessly clean, fully equipped and right in the center of Athens! Great people and very kind! I highly recommend it!!!
Very nice and clean apartment! In a good location! I highly recommend it!

This hotel may not be a five star hotel but it has a character and touch of the owner's dedication to make the guests' stay a comfortable and pleasant one. I loved the cleanliness and the friendly attitude. Breakfast is simple, sausage and scrambled eggs with different kinds of breads, jams and jelly, fresh juices and coffee. It filled me up, so I'm not going to complain.  If I want more varieties, I can  pay more money and stay at an expensive hotel for some extra cheese for breakfast. But I chose this hotel and happy to get what they're offering. Acropolis, Plaka, Monastriki 10 minutes away and Uber cost about 6 euros. The rooms are good size and the best thing is the little private patio to chill.  There's a "safe locker" in the room. Internet speed is very good. My experience here is very satisfying and I highly recommend this hotel to the "non fussy" travellers for a comfortable, inexpensive stay in Athens.

I don't know who rated this hotel as high as they did which caused me to choose the hotel but... The air conditioner leaked on the bed and night table it is clearly an ongoing issue as you can see rotting wood down by the floor. The hotel is on a dirty side street. The older male manager or owner is not pleasant, at all! The girl who worked days was pleasant and helpful! I do not recommend Hotel Nicola.

This is possibly one of the best value for money hotels that I have stayed at on my travels -- and I've been on the road since July 2017. The place is spacious and clean. The WiFi works! The breakfast was generous, the bar prices very reasonable. Best thing about this hotel is the staff -- they are all friendly and ready to help. Also a mine of info about what to do and see in Athens. Highly recommended.

Nice and cozy hotel with balcony at very reasonable price compared to other hotels in Athens. If you are not too picky, you will be fine. Free breakfast and the area is safe and closed to bus and taxi. Service is awesome as owner and staff will go above and beyond to help with request to make guest happy. Rooms are well cleaned. My suggedtions for improvement are: better wifi which is not strong and brither lights around lobby area.

This is a small gem for the people looking for a clean and neet bed and breakfast in Athens Greece  it is a family managed hotel where all the family make you feel that you are an extended family member not a guest very courteous understanding of guests needs they do their best to accommodate you and serve you with great attitude very helpful speak English. I do recommend this place for families and business

Location and rooms are fine, pool leaves a lot to be desired, sunbeds are falling apart and filthy and screaming kids everywhere with nobody to offer you a towel at all. The staff member at check in was rude and on the day of check out two of the staff members had a huge fight in front of the guests resulting in the receptionist in tears trying to serve people.

Quiet hours disturbance all the time by deranged Erasmus students that behave without any respect.

Take your apartments away from buildings that families actually live inside and want to sleep.

I booked this house in december and I red the review only few days before going there. The house was okay there was some mold around but the flat was decent. The house was completly dark but was not the fault of the agency.
But despite the decent condition of the flat the big problem was the agency. When you come they call you to sign up some documents and they tell you that for the bed sheets and the cleaning lady you have to pay. They will remove this money from the deposit. The cleaning lady is mandatory and is a way for the agency to control the tentants and what they are doing. The bed sheets are dirty and they are almost transparent because they washed it thousands times. The agency and the cleaning lady they entered all the time without knocking or ringing the bell, with their own keys. At the end of my staying there they came (with their keys without announcing them) to see for some reparations inside the flat. They checked and they told me that they had to do some reparetion in the empty room of my flatmates. The day after I found them inside my flat and they removed the painting from the ceiling in my bathroom. I was really angry because they didn't tell me anything so I went to the office to talk with them and they told me was my fault because I didn't open the window during the shower. They also said that the cleaning lady took a picture every week and the mold situation was every week worst. They did the reparation in my last three days there and they left a disaster around the house. There was dust all around, smeel of painting and when they finished they didn't clean anything. You have to write to them for the deposit back because they didn't ask for it. So at the end I can say: run away from the agency they are the worst and with the fact that they always enter without asking you will not feel respect and in your space. They will not give you your privacy.

This agency is disgusting. I would not recommend it if you are planning to live in Athens, specially if you are Erasmus. My experience here was awful. At first they are kind because they want to STEAL your money. They obligate you by signing a contract which later they won’t fulfill. My roommates and me paid the deposit they claimed and after our stay they took 100 euros from each person without any valid and legal explanation. Apart from this, we had to ask many times to please DO NOT ENTER to our house with their own set of keys. Also, if you compare it with other agencies or particulars, it is more expensive and the quality of the flat does not accomplish the price-quality.
I am still having a horrible experience with them and I hope they lose their customers.

Be careful with them! (ERasmus Rooms Athens - picture uploaded bellow)...
Writing this as a warning to all my Erasmus colleagues.
They extra charge you for things they don't mention until the signing of the contract (like borrowing/washing towels and linen for 60 euros, cleaning, etc.). Furthermore, you're lucky if you get a fully functioning apartment. Most of them are old, in buildings where neighbours don't really appreciate students and complain/call the police and the apartments themselves are falling apart! This is the biggest issue. They have poorly maintained ours for months, trying to fix the toilets, both of the refrigerators weren't working, it took them almost a month to provide us with wifi and so on.
Don't expect them to refund you for anything, after they kick you out they wont send any word until they give you your deposit back without explanation.

Please be careful, this company is misleading. I was an Erasmus student in Athens, I lived there for 4 months, in Omonia. First I found the company via internet, everything was fine with them. I paid a deposit in the value of 1 month's rent. Upon arrival, they contacted me immediately to come sign the documents. They state uniform accommodation prices everywhere, but upon arrival they start charging meaningless fees, e.g. for bed linen, cleaning lady (she really cleaned), towels, etc. The room was relatively fine, but during my entire stay they weren’t able to fix a toilet bowl (even though I warned them several times afterwards). The bathroom wasn’t in the best condition. At the end of my stay, I was completely surprised that they didn’t send me the deposit within the agreed time. They sent it to me only after I contacted them, of course later than stated in the contract. I didn't even get half of the agreed amount back. Their justification was additional nonsensical charges for linen, towels, bank charges and they even said they had to specially clean my “dirty” room. When I contacted them again for a refund, they didn’t reply me. I definitely don’t recommend this company for accommodation in Athens, it’s not so cheap and u cannot trust hem. My Erasmus colleagues had much nicer accommodation and even much cheaper, where I spent more time. Take care.

There’s something special about this place. And the people who work here. It’s without a doubt the best two-star hotel I have ever stayed at.

And for women out there, some of the rooms have the best make-up table there exists in a hotel!

The only improvement I would do would be to change doors / isolate better for hallway noise. But still, at night it’s super quiet. The cleaning personal are the kindest. So are the staff in reception. I love the 70’s art deco vibe in the rooms. I would definitely stay again.

Clean, tidy hotel.
Lovely staff, very helpful and friendly.
Little bit away from main tourist area, 2 - 4 km easy walk depending where you are going.
Felt safe walking around.
Close to all public transport options.
Great food options, all nearby .
Will stay here again.
Room on smaller side, but we were only sleeping there, out most days.
The shower had great water pressure and good hot water.
Housekeeping is great.

Our stay at the Victory inn was very pleasant. Our room was very large with ample space for our luggage. The bed was comfortable but the pillows quite thin. As we only stayed for two nights we made do. The bathroom was a bit on the smaller side but fine for a couple.

Good proximity to public trans, easy places to eat and supermarket. Nice local neighborhood 2 blocks away; restaurants, bars, art etc. Place was very clean, staff extremely acommodating. AC works well, room was comfortable and quiet with all standard amenities and private balcony. Simple and fresh selection of local breakfast options served daily. Perfect for a night or 2 while traveling.
34 room boutique hotel! Smallish rooms but very clean and tidy. Not far (walking distance from most of the major Athens attractions. Good food options nearby!

Worst airbnb I have ever stayed at, and I’ve stayed at many. The apartment was cramped and small. We stayed for five nights total and the host was unwilling to provide extra towels, so we had damp towels the whole stay. The shower leaked all over the bathroom. There was no hot water and despite following the host’s instructions perfectly, the host was uncooperative with checking on the hot water or helping us. We followed the instructions provided, and he said the hot water should work for a 15 minute period before shutting off, but the longest the hot water ever stayed on was 1-2 minutes. It was a very unpleasant stay because we could not have a hot shower after a long day. The kitchen was dirty with food that expired last year still in the fridge and an unclean sink. The bed was uncomfortable and we had to use the “couch” pillows because not enough bed pillows were provided to sleep with. The “couch” was a palate with a cushion on it and the “table” was a wood palate on the floor. Curtains were see through so the apartment stayed bright all night, even with the wooden slats. In contrast, other hostels and air bnbs we booked along our trip were the same price but a world of a difference. Host continues to try to gaslight us that there could be no fault with his airbnb, and refuses to look into the hot water or clean his kitchen. Save your money and stay elsewhere for a more comfortable stay.
Great family villa! Easy drive and check-in. Very friendly and helpful hosts!
Great Villa

author_name: Atical Hi-Tech Sanitary Jiujiang Jianxhi Atical
text: Best pool villa with great Athens  view
Fantastic place with pool.
Amazing view

Good if you’re travelling on a budget, but stay at your own risk. Hotel is located at a somewhat dodgy area and the room was also quite old. Not sure why but the TV turned on by itself at midnight. AC was really cold tho, I had a comfortable sleep regardless.
The AC was noisy, carpet dirty, and our shower door exploded while opening it. There was a cute white cat outside the window which made the experience a bit better.

The service and cleaning service was great! However, the room looked like it needed some maintenance. A new clean carpet is needed. Also, my plan was to come with a car, but i decided to come to Athens by train. This turned out perfect for me because the hotel advertised “having parking” but it didn’t. There was no parking on the street or near the hotel. Overall, it is close to the city…20-30 minutes walking and it is next to a police station (which i felt safe) and the hotel service completed my requests.

Worst and dirtiest room we have ever stayed in. Beware and do not book this hotel. The location is fine and central enough to walk to all the sights if you are good on foot (approx. 45 min.).
But there were blood spots on the carpet, hair in the bed and really weird substances all over the furniture and floor. Also the toilet had spots of dried bodily liquids on the seat. Also the receptionist weirdly met with some girls in their room after his shift. Yes it’s cheap but even for this low price it’s a scam. We actually tried to change rooms, but the other room was no better. In my opinion there is no way the other positive reviews can be real. Don’t be fooled!

1. Takes time for hot water to arrive on the shower
2. The blanket is way too small
3. Freezing cold in the room. There is an AC but i could not turn it to heating (i can say the staff could have helped me if i ask, just chose not to because putting many layers of clothes fix this). The first night i woke up at 5.30 because of the cold and could not sleep in that weather. The next nights i put 3 layers of clothes on me while sleeping
4. Once after the room service woman cleaned me room i smelled ciggarets smell in there. Did not let her in since then
5. One of the reception guy is nag. He imsist of giving him the key every time i leave the room to go outside, so i have to get my key at the reception every time i enter. The other receptionists dont do it, he is the only one, and he insisted even after i told him he is the only one keeping my key "for security purposes". He also have me wait to get my signature after i extend my stay, while the day before when i extend my stay no signature was taken from me from the other receptionsit. This guy ruined my experience in this hotel. All the receptionist need to have the same work procedures, and not every one do what he wish or think is right.

Only good thing about this hotel is the price, but you can find other hotels with good price that do not have so much issues.

Respose to response-
1. as far as I remember, I did not extend the reservation 3 times. This is a lie. I only extend once. During the extention I had gone through that nag receptionist behaviour, after which I didn't return to this hotel.
2. When checked in I was not informed that I need to leave the key in reception every time I leave. As I mentioned- no receptionist did that except for the nag one.
3. Taking an extra cover would not solve the blanket being too small

Lovely apartment easy access to Athens centre. Supplies put in the fridge from katarina the owner as we had a late flight in. She also booked our taxi from the airport for us and gave us loads of helpful tips to get us set for a good stay

Fabulous apartment with all amenities. Very handy for city sights. Taxi from here to central Athens inexpensive but you can also walk to the archaeological museum and get on one of the sightseeing buses or take the local bus service. Great if you want to feel local rather than be a tourist.

We stayed here with our family for 4 days. The apartment is so comfortable, clean and very nice decorated. It has all the amenities you will need and more. Located very close to the city sites. The hosts are the most amazing people, helped us with everything we needed and gave us many useful tips. We left with the best memories and surly will return soon.
Very good apartment. Plenty of space to store his suitcases. Nice staff. The inner city can be reached within minutes by taxi.
It is really really a nice place. The owner is so kind and helpful. It think is the best accomodation in Athens.

One of the worst hotels I have ever been. It is not clean enough (the carpets are dirty and old), there are blood stains on the walls (dead mosquitos? cockroaches?). The WC had an old plastic door that was not closing properly. The balcony door was not closing properly either. There is, of course, no elevator. The reception desk is most of the times, empty. One can just take any key and go to any room, for all I know. The price: 40 euros per night. For what?!
Good hotel and lovely staff, will use again on my next trip. The area is fantastic with some great restaurants and bars nearby.
Worst hotel ever been. Extremely Dirty and unpleasant.  GET AWAY FROM THAT HOTEL. It is NOT accepted even from refugees.
such an amazing friendly lovely place.
Beautiful, clean, and quiet, close to buses going to the center, and close to public parks and very wonderful cafes.

The apartment is a lovely place to stay. Nadia is a very warm and welcoming person who makes sure that everything is perfect.
The place has a beautiful decoration, and the bed is very comfortable. Also it has a lot of small details like some food in the fridge, sewing kit, ironing, etc.
The public transport is just in front of the building and you can reach the city center in a short ride.
Thank you very much Nadia! :)
We loved our stay at Nadia's. The apartment is beautiful and it's very easy to get to the center of town on transit. Nadia is a very sweet, welcoming host.

Everything was perfect !
A very bright and beautiful apartment fully equipped in the center of Athens.
Very easy public transportation and the host was very polite and helpful.

Nadia was waiting at the apartment on arrival and was there to take the keys when I left, even though it was early morning. Apartment in a quiet side street, with parking available in front of the entrance.
I will be short: all 5 star reviews are true. Great place and great landlord. Highly recommend
Really wonderful! From any point of view! A hairdryer and a shampoo stand in the bathroom would make it perfect!
Spacious house near a supermarket and 50 meters from public transport. Above all, the house is very clean and has air conditioning.

Beautiful apartment. Very clean. Has everything u need. The host is amazing. Super nice and helpful. The location is a bot far from the center but if you are looking for , a local atmosphere with great local coffee this is the spot

🇪🇷🇱🇻This apartment is very neat, clean and equipped with everything you need. The workers are obedient. In short, we are very satisfied and recommend every traveler to come here.
And let (Greece) Athens be your vacation choice.

We rented two apartments for together 8 people for our 5-day stay, which was without exaggeration exceptional. Giulia from the owner company wrote us every needed information via Whatsapp for our self check-in via a smartphone app. Moreover, she communicated with us throughout our entire stay and helped instantly with everything. Please, give her a raise. The apartments were fully renovated, spatious, with beatiful built-in furniture, a practical kitchen with induction cooktop, dishwasher and nespresso machine, also a washing machine and a big nice bathroom with a walk-in shower. The block of flats is located in a local non-touristy neighbourhood cca. 30 minutes from the main city sights by public transport (bus no. 35).
Thank you again, we would surely gladly return here in the future.
Great apartment! Good location! I would recommend this accommodation when traveling to Athens!!
Fabulous. Comfortable, clean, very well equipped. Easy to reach the city center.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at this wonderful hotel, and I must say, it was an unforgettable experience! The staff were incredibly kind and attentive, going above and beyond to ensure that every need was met. One of the highlights was the stunning rooftop pool, which offered breathtaking views of Athens—truly a perfect spot to unwind and soak in the beauty of the city. I wholeheartedly recommend this hotel to anyone seeking a delightful stay with exceptional service. It’s a gem that should not be missed!. special thanks for the courtesy of Maria and Spiro at the reception.

Apartment hotel with big rooms and nice pool in a nutshell . Supermarket at the hotel is an advantage . 15 min from the “center” I think 4.5 fit the hotel .
There are enough comments but I wanted to comment on a few of them .

Cleanings: no issue on our end but I guess it’s per person experience.

Breakfast: we didn’t book it and it cost 13 euro per person for it to deliver to your door . It’s incl the regular stuff and it sounds very expensive but there is a coffee and bakery outside the hotel .

Pool : saw some comments that it was close and without a pool bar . We were at peak season and it was open , no pool bar it’s true but again you have supermarket at the hotel .

Cockroaches : I saw one comment and not sure where it comes from . For us the hotel was super clean and high quality facilities

Mixed Experience at Panorama Project Athens

We recently stayed at Panorama Project Athens and had a mixed experience. The hotel's location is a highlight, providing authentic Athens right on your doorstep. However, there were several downsides that impacted our stay.

Upon arrival, we found the towels to be dirty, and no top sheet on the bed and a broken safe which beeped incessantly until they ripped the battery out. Our room wasn't cleaned daily we put the request light on only to come back and find it turn off and room not done but the battery back in the beeping safe! our room, which was supposed to have a view, instead overlooked other people's rooms. The shower setup was particularly frustrating, as you had to hold the shower head to take a shower—an inconvenient and awkward arrangement.

The pool area, although wonderfully cold and refreshing, had seating that needed a thorough cleaning. Breakfast was another letdown; there was no choice given, and we were never asked if we wanted coffee or orange juice. Who doesn’t want coffee in the morning?

Overall, while the location is excellent and the pool pleasant, there are several areas in need of improvement to make the stay more enjoyable.

Hotel is in a good location. Not too far of a walk from Athens centre. Mini market downstairs is a definite plus. Breakfast, although we didn't get it is €13 per person per day which sounds pretty expensive as food in Athens from the many many bakeries and cafes is very cheap.

The room was clean and tidy albeit with a few scuff marks on the walls from suitcases etc. Nice and sizeable too. Beds aren't dressed with a duvet, just a top sheet folded up on the end that you put on yourself. There was what looked to be a blanket in the cupboard however who knows how long it's been since it was washed last.

Parking charges were a surprise as €15 per day (this charge was not mentioned at all on the booking.com advert which definitely should be as this could add up a big extra cost very quickly, a bit deceiving).

Staff were friendly and helpful so can't be faulted there.

Really nice and modern hotel!! Was really nice to start the day with a swim on the rooftop and to watch the sunset from there. It is true that of you look in the distantie you have a view of the acropolis but honestly dont book this hotel if you really want a direct vieuw cause in my opionion its a little bit of a stretch.

Last minute booking for a quick business trip in Athens, didn't know what to expect from the place, but let me tell you it was fairly priced , brand new with all amenities I could use , cozy clean and downright beautiful , next time in Athens I know where Ian staying .

Unreservedly recommended for a pleasant stay in Athens, with easy access to MMM, fully renovated and equipped, and especially neat according to hygiene rules. The owners are particularly helpful, polite, they responded immediately to our every communication. I will prefer it again for my next stay in the capital.

My stay at the accommodation was very pleasant! Everything was spotlessly clean, the owners were caring and helpful. The house was completely renovated with a very nice taste. I would highly recommend it!
Tasteful and spotlessly clean apartment in a central location close to the market and with access to public transport. Pleasant surprise the first morning.

I recently had the opportunity to stay at Anna Apartments in Athens and I must admit that it was an excellent choice! The apartment is in a great location - Kipseli is a charming district, close to everything. You can quickly reach the most important attractions, such as the Acropolis and Plaka, and there are plenty of shops, cafes and restaurants in the area.

The apartment was clean, stylishly decorated and fully equipped, which made me feel at home. An ideal place for both short and long stays. I really liked how the owner, Anna, took care of all the details and was extremely helpful at all times. Thanks to her, the stay was even more pleasant.

If you are planning a visit to Athens, I highly recommend Anna Apartments - great location, comfort and excellent service! I will definitely come back on my next visit to Greece.
Beautifully appointed apartment, everything you need is in it! Exceptionally clean and situated among restaurants and cafes for your convenience!!!
Completely renovated, modern and clean, really comfortable and cozy apartment, in an excellent location, on a pedestrian street, with many restaurants and bars nearby.

Super nice, clean and well maintained with good location and facilities. You can check in at any time using the door code, which makes arrival much easier. It's a pleasant walk to the city center, it takes about 45 minutes to get to the sights. You can also take the bus, whose stop is a maximum of 2 minutes' walk from the accommodation.
Very nice apartments, look as good as in the pictures. Nice and helpful people who work here too!

My husband and I stayed at the end of January and we were completely satisfied. Brand new apartment, spotlessly clean with all amenities. Amazing location, on the beautiful Fokionos Negri with all kinds of shops at our feet. In a very close distance there is a very economical parking lot for the car, although for the center we found it very convenient to use public transport with a 3-minute walk.
Good
The cinema flat is a very nice place to stay in Athens. Not luxury,  but everything at hand and the buses around the corner.
Beautiful and clean apartment
Pleasantly. A surprisingly interesting location. There are plenty of pubs nearby. There are several buses to the center (Omonia, Syntagma), but like in Athens - they go whenever they want.

Warm and welcoming place. Clean space, nice decoration. The beds are crossed with perfectly clean sheets. The kitchen and the bathroom, although small, are fully equipped. The owners are very helpful and kind. I highly recommend it. I will definitely be there again.
It has everything for a comfortable stay. Cozy home, home style.
