Moody owner. We are on vacation. Let’s not add to the stress. Not one of our four drivers knew where this place was. We were looking forward to dining here. There was some loss in communication with the organizer but owner added unnecessary drama to the mishap.

Wonderful place. Even better service from the reception through the cleaning service to the very roof, where the friendly staff will welcome you in the bar at the very top of the hotel. The queues to this place should be longer than to the Acropolis. All the best to the owners and staff as well as to all future hotel guests. You won't find anything better in Athens for an overnight stay. Restaurants in the area and you can get to the main attractions by taxi for 7 €. This level of service should be commendable.

Impossible to get an answer from them on the phone or through email. I showed up to the hotel at midnight as my flight arrived in Athens an hour prior and nobody was at the reception…. I had to book a night in another hotel. I’ve sent them an email and try to call them, no response from them…I personally feel like it’s a scam…

My husband and I booked our room more than a month in advance. Due to the strikes, there were an influx of other travelers who made their booking 24 hours or less. Because we were arriving after 20:00 (reception closes then), the hotel sent us a message with our room number. They said our room key would be in the door and the front door would be open. This immediately didn't sit right with me for safety reasons. (Anyone could come in and take a room.) When we arrived, we found someone already in our room. We weren't about to kick her out and get a dirty room, but I also have no idea whether she actually reserved the room because she never showed me proof. However, taking her at her word, we found one room left with the key in it. We decided to take it. About an hour later, we received a knock on our door. A second lady showed me the message from Reception that we were, in fact, in her room. We didn't want to screw over someone else just because our plans were ruined by another traveler and lack of planning from the Reception desk. We ended up giving the lady the room and went straight to the airport without a bed to sleep in.

I could understand if this was a small village and reception closed at 20:00, but this is a capital city. Also, leaving keys and doors unlocked is very unsafe, especially because you can see in the hotel from the street. For boasting about being "tech-forward," this hotel isn't even able to solve a simple problem of checking guests in accurately. I am thoroughly disappointed that I emailed the hotel twice with no response. We received no refund and we didn't even end up staying here.

Reception closes at 8. Does it mentioned it anywhere when you book? No. Have spent last 1.5 hour trying to figure out how to get to my room. Turns out my travel agent didn't forward an email saying that the key will be at the door. Literally that. In an apartment building where anyone can get in (I did, just had to push the door) and the key was outside the door. I opened and let myself in.
Inside there are no instructions or password for wifi. Signal for phone or internet is only by de window.
The bar at he rooftop advertised to be open from 4pm to midnight is of course closed.
There is not a place where you can buy water in the building.
No one answers to any of the phones in the website or the one in the confirmation.
Furniture is nice and new.

This place and the service provided absolutely brilliant...This renovated mansion is tucked away in a central location in Athens where you are walking distance to all attractions and shopping...With one word : Feels like as if it is our home.

I had the change to be one of the first ever residents of this amazing mansion, in the center of Athens, in Kypseli. Evangelos, the owner was an amazing host, whereas the amenities provided by the property was top end. I highly recommend it to all Athens travellers.
Wonderful house with special architecture, very kind and helpful owner and very close to the center. Ideal for those who want to move close to the center without a car.

An ideal apartment for someone who would like to visit Athens. The best Airbnb in the city of Athens! Thank you for your hospitality!

Beautiful small, cute apartment in a quiet place, for a stay of one person as well as a couple. We didn't miss anything, and Aris and Anna were kind and helpful in everything we needed, they helped us immediately, even with information on how to move to the center of Athens. Very close from the center, even on foot it's a nice walk around 35 minutes there. Lycabettus and Areos field also very close.
Quiet neighborhood, beautiful apartment, excellent service!
Excellent condition apparent very close to the metro station,very friendly staff and quick response! Good prices also I recommend for everyone who needs something secure and comfortable to stay.
Amazing apartment, comfortably furnished and carefully decorated! The location is ideal and at an affordable price!
Excellent apartment, excellent hospitality, Mr. Konstantinos
Beautiful apartment with everything you need in a central area!

Location and rooms are fine, pool leaves a lot to be desired, sunbeds are falling apart and filthy and screaming kids everywhere with nobody to offer you a towel at all. The staff member at check in was rude and on the day of check out two of the staff members had a huge fight in front of the guests resulting in the receptionist in tears trying to serve people.
Perfect place, fantastic design! The most comfortable choice for staying in Athens.. Just 7' from metro station you will definitely love this place
Perfect apartments, have everything you need and more. Quite and cozy.

The place overall is ok, but wouldn’t want to return there.
Good(fine): a lot of space for sleeping; there is AC in every bedroom; fully equipped kitchen; good WiFi connectivity; near the metro station; you can book day in day.
Bad: absolutely terrible ventilation windows and tunnel! (you literally can hear everything from other floors), there wasn’t any litter bin in the kitchen or in any room except the bathroom; and the building and street are dirty.
In total, this place is not too bad, but maybe for it’s price it is perfect

Good size apartment, reactive host, near metro station, 3-4 metro stops from city center, near cafes, supermarkets, gyros eating places. Bathroom can be improved. Toilet doesn't flush well, shower is unmovable and water was spraying onto the shower curtain rather than onto you.
Air bnb, nice apartment.

Do not open the ventilation window in the shower!!

We loved the apartment and it's location. Makis is a wonderful, very helpful and attentive host. We had a great time in Athens and look forwarding to visiting again in the near future. Makis thank you for all your help and great advice!

The perfect place to stay! I've been visiting this place the last 4 years, at least twice per year and it is PERFECT! It is the best place to meet new people, have fun and party as well! The owners there are too friendly and awesome except one, who thinks he is a singer (but he is not). After the first hour though you will love him as well. I am so excited when I visit this place, highly recommended!

This place is amazing! Everybody is cheerful all the time and they sing for no reason! They will order food for me and get me around the town. They even get me to their office while working! Amazing, 5/5 would stay on their couch again!!! You haven't seen Athens...no scratch that..Greece!..no scratch that..LIFE! You haven't seen LIFE if you have yet to sleep on that couch!

We stayed as a group of 5 for three nights in the Pnyx apartment. The apartment was beautiful, very clean and very big. We had a small balcony, there were a variation of hair styling tools, a washing machine and a dishwasher.
The host provided some olive oil, salt and pepper, tea and instant coffee. There was also a nescafe capsule machine which we didn't use.
There is a big supermarket in walking distance, the metro line 3 is 12 min by walking.
There are many coffee places around here.

Check-in and out was with pin codes and very smoothly.
Towels very provided as well.
Thank you for this amazing stay!!

I would like to express my sincere thanks and appreciation for our wonderful stay in the wonderful apartment. The apartment was large and clean, the rooms were spacious and comfortable. We also thank the hostess for leaving useful instructions and directions that helped us a lot during our stay. We greatly appreciate your quick response and willingness to help at any time. It was a wonderful experience, and we will be happy to come back again in the future.
Great location and interior design. Can do better with the noise insulation especially towards the front road given the traffic

We stayed there for 5 nights. Perfect for families with two children. It is a bit far from historical center, near to the stadium of Panathinaikos FC, but with Metro it is only 3 stops, and bus takes about 25 min. The appartement was super clean and modern. We enjoyed it a lot. Special thanks to the very friendly and helpful host (Ofeliya), which grant us late check-out as we had a late evening flight.
Excellent accommodation in a very good location for several people in Athens
Amazing apartment, spotlessly clean and tasteful. Easy access, 2' from Panormos metro. I could not forget the kind and helpful owner. I highly recommend it.
Flawless

We booked in good faith.  However, the apartment building cellar is connected to a parking place for motorbikes, the entire complex fills up with exhaust fumes.  It leaks into the apartment, only opening the windows and doors was effective at dispelling the fumes.  It's situated just off a main thoroughfare, which is convenient but noisy - and of course, the apartment heats up.
The balcony was flooded, blocked from draining, so we couldn't sit outside.  Although the apartment was surface clean, plates had old food on them and I started noticing more small things that weren't hygienic - and then the internet crashed.  By this point we had a headache from the fumes and wanted to cancel and leave.
We called the owner, 2 men turned up that couldn't smell anything, (they must work in a sewage facility to have such lack of smell), and only offered that we could contact the cleaning company.
This apartment is unhealthy, and the owner is unhelpful.  Due to their reaction, I don't think this is the first complaint they've had, they were prepared.  Since we checked reviews before we booked, I'm posting this as a warning.  It's not so much the cleanliness, that's an easy fix, it's not being able to breathe clean air!
We're out of money and had the stress of finding new accommodation and a hotel for the night. Luckily our new apartment owner and even the hotel with the late book in, were  a lot more helpful and kind.
Very nice, comfortable and functional. We were very pleased. When an issue arose our hostess resolved it immediately. We will go again for sure.

We had a wonderful time on this beautiful island, Maraki with her wonderful smile and her service, made us feel at home, her hospitality was wonderful and whatever information we wanted about the island she was willing to inform us, we are leaving with the best memories and we can't wait to come again!!!❤️😘

Spacious & comfortable rooms, sufficiently equipped and spotlessly clean. Good location with excellent eateries around and easy access to transportation. At 10-5% cheaper this would be the Athens killer deal!

I had a great week staying there! The apartment is fully furnished and equipped with all the necessities, even hair dryer and coffee machine! I’m impressed 🩷
Too bad I didn’t take any photos but it looks just like the pictures on the internet.

The location is great too; good neighbourhood; there’s a bus stop 2 mins away by foot and a big gym located nearby just 4 mins walk. Getting to the city center like Monastiraki and Syntagma takes less than 30 mins by bus.

If you drive, you can find cool cafes and patisseries in less than 5 mins. There are good restaurants around the place and a few supermarkets in the area. I surely will stay here again on my next visit!

We stay here every year. The room we get with a kitchen and 2 rooms in one is fabulous! The balcony is huge. The kitchen is nice and the showers are neat. The AC works very well, and that is key! The service is great but there is nobody there from 9 pm to 9 am, I believe . They leave 4 extra pillows in the room, and the mattresses are the best we have slept on in all of the hotels we have stayed at! There's a full refrigerator with snacks and drinks! They are charged to the room but it's not too expensive.  There is a washing machine in the room as well not sure if it's a 2 in 1 with a drier. Its not loud at night like other parts of Athens where you can't sleep because all the noise. We slept great! Can't wait to be back next year!

Nice rooms, great service! Very friendly!  They were so helpful holding our bags since we came in on a cruise mid morning.  We dropped the bags, and they held them until almost 7pm for us! I would highly recommend!

If you drive, you can find cool cafes and patisseries in less than 5 mins. There are good restaurants around the place and a few supermarkets in the area. I surely will stay here again on my next visit!

I vacationed with my family in this "hotel", last year in August. The accommodation itself is very good. However, the reason I gave the lowest rating is that we had a very unpleasant experience with the owner of the "hotel". Namely, although I arranged everything directly with the owner, although I called two days before arrival and received an email that the room would be ready at the agreed time, when we arrived (and we were traveling with a 10-month-old baby), we received a short notice that is our room occupied until the next day and that we can spend the night in accommodation in the immediate vicinity!?! We agreed to it because with a baby in Greece in August, we didn't even want to try to find another accommodation. And maybe I wouldn't even write all this if another group of guests didn't have the same problem during our stay there. At the same time, the owner's attitude about the whole situation was that this often happens because it is the season and there is a lot of interest!?! The owner himself did not find it necessary to formally apologize, let alone anything more... During check-out, when the husband went to settle all the expenses, he had another unpleasant contact with the owner, who shouted that we had no right to be angry, that he has the right to do as he wants, that if we don't like it, we never have to come again, and he didn't even say goodbye when he left. And although I would have more to write, I think I have said enough for some future guests. We have been vacationing in Greece for years, I have always arranged accommodation directly with the owners, and this, unfortunately, was our first negative experience....

In the room was quite space, bed was comfortable and staff was nice. Now the cons. Quite a lot of mosquitos there, but service has electric device againts them, so no problem. Moreover In the room were little ants and this is quite bad, because sleeping with mind of beeing bitten by this isnt so nice. And finally the placement of the hotel is bad, outside of the hotel was quite ugly and brakfast box definitely doesnt worth the money spent.

Worst hotel experience I have had. The room had recently been smoked in and they refused to move us to another room. The front desk guy was very nice but the manager refused to speak to us. Definitely do not stay here
We booked a non smoking room and when we arrived it smelled so strongly of cigarette smoke. They did not have any other rooms available and wouldn’t refund us. Such a sad experience.

We stayed here for two nights prior to a cruise. The price was very reasonable so we weren’t expecting much. We arrived to the hotel VIA their car service which was amazing! We got to the hotel just to be met with the friendliest staff you could find. We went to our room which we absolutely loved and had everything we needed, along with a great view of the Acropolis. We also used their car service for a 5 hour tour of Athens as well as to get to the ship. It was all worth every penny. The only negative things there were was the breakfast, and possibly the surroundings. Some. May love the breakfast however the offerings just weren’t to my liking. The Surroundings initially make you think you’re in a rough area, however I don’t believe you really are. I felt safe the entire time and even walked 1.9 Kilometers to the center for food and browsing. We plan on staying here The next time we come and would recommend this to anyone. We absolutely loved this place.

Nice enough place, a bit dingey in appearance though. The staff is truly lovely and there's a coffee, wine, spirits bar in the lobby. Sadly not a place for folks with cat allergies, they have a pet cat that lounges on everything in the lobby and there's no enclosure for luggage keeping.
Really a decent place to say, great size rooms just not my ideal.

The Penthouse is excellent and has everything for a nice stay in Athens. It's well located, close to the Acropolis and near the metro and subway station. It was only missing a microwave but other than that, the place was excellent and strongly recommended for couples or groups of 4. Sotiris, the manager is a great person as well and always helpful

Good value for money

Really nice room, absolutely fitting for 2 people! Everything was clean and nice. The only small things we saw/ noticed were: the air conditioner didn’t work that well, there was a weird smell coming out of the bathroom and the ceiling was cracking (the paint).
The apartment had a really big balcony with clothing lines and given clothespins, really good because we could hang it there and it would dry in no time. The room has 2 balcony doors, which made it easier to ventilate the room.
The way to the apartment is really nice too, we could just use the elevator, which was really necessary because it was way to hot to walk the stairs every time.

It's a hotel that matches the price you pay for it. It's about a 30-minute walk to the city center. The hotel was clean, with a soft bed and towels included. The only drawback was a cigarette smell upon arrival, but it disappeared after airing out the room. The room has air conditioning, a small kitchen, a table, chairs, a TV, a wardrobe (a bit small for two people), a bed, and nightstands. The bathroom also has a washing machine. During our 10-day stay, we had an issue with the electricity once, but it was resolved the same day. Towards the end of our stay, we had a minor issue with a neighbor who played loud music at night, but after reporting it to the host, the problem was immediately resolved. I want to highlight the prompt, quick, and hassle-free communication with the owner, who was always available and responsive to our inquiries. Regarding the location, at first glance, it might not seem very friendly, but we had no safety issues. By the end of our stay, I even started to feel at home.

OVERBOOKING AND LAST MINUTE CANCELATION
Unprofessional, petty owner! I had booked the room for 3 days for a concert that i was attending 4 months in advance. When I called the morning of the reservation to ask on how to get to the room, a very rude man told me that the kichen was set on fire! and I couldn't stay there and when I ask for compensation he said he did nothing wrong and was simply giving me my money back.
However, when I called booking they informed me that the owner claimed an overbooking case and no damage was reported to the premices. Lying unreliable host.
STAY AWAY!
Nice clean apartment with basic equipments. Metro station is in about 15 minutes.

Generally an ok hotel, the bed was covered by 2 sheets that didn't completely fit and had to be resettled if you moved incorrectly. The size was very nice and the kitchen was also pretty good. The location is close to one of the metro stations, and is also a ~15-30 minute walk to whatever you want to see in the downtown area.

Totally value for money! Cleanliness,Friendly staff, located in the centre of Athens (Metaxourgio) and with private parking (covered) for the guests . Inside has all the necessary stuff such as towels, cotton robe and slippers , soap, shower gel/shampoo and cookies with two bottles of mineral water. I would definitely visit the hotel again! Absolutely recommended for short stay visitors!

Kind and discreet. That's why it is always preferred! The rooms could be more in some "concepts" . The mirrors are super but in a few rooms they could be more convenient in different places. As for the TV it should have more choices. There are 4 channels , but sometimes work 2 or 3 of them. One solution is the USB stick that client could have but in some rooms they are located exactly in front of the wall and you cannot put it.  Another solution is smart TVs could connect to smart phones. But this is only for mentioning. In general, I keep the discreet point that works super with the staff that has the hotel

There i spend the best second and Moments in my life 👌🏻 peace Greece ❤️

Absolutely Fantastic stay. Lovely apartment that was surprisingly quiet for so central a location. The owners went well above and beyond the call of hospitality,  I cant thank them enough. Well recommended!
We really enjoyed our stay at the apartment, it is very clean, nice and cozy. Totally recommend!

Where to start? It will be obvious to say that the reason why you book this apartment is clear: the hot tub with a view of the acropolis. Well, if you are also interested in these two things, think twice or thrice before booking here. Because the hydromassage doesn't work and the view is not like in the photo. As for the view, you can see for yourself from the attached photos: do you notice any differences? I do. Someone must have had fun with Photoshop.
Regarding the hot tub, we noticed that at a certain point the tub, although THE WATER LEVEL WAS NORMAL AND NOT ON THE MARGIN, the tub was LEAKING and the apartment was flooding. Furthermore, the bubble system only makes noise, but once immersed you realize that it is particularly weak and the water surface remains intact, smooth, like a lake. In short, you don't enjoy the hydromassage, but only a normal bathtub with background noise. What can I say? Once we called the owner, she didn't even try to listen to our complaints for a second, but immediately denied the evidence and treated us with arrogance and rudeness. I underline that I have never and I mean NEVER had problems with structures on booking, despite the fact that I have done dozens upon dozens. This was my worst experience. But obviously the ownership will be ready to deny this review too, I imagine. However, I remain with a clear conscience. Enjoy Athens, a beautiful city, but don't spend the night here, trust me.

We left the same just in the evening. First the photos prove how much Photoshop they used to attract people. We tried to upload them to the booking review as well but once the comment was uploaded, the photos disappeared. water drips and around it a lake was created which we collected ourselves. I must emphasize that we had filled it to the limits that had been set for us and after entering we realized that it was not as strong as a machine. Disappointed, we closed it and stayed in the water to relax. The surface of the water in no way approached the rim of the tub. At one point we heard a sound as if something was dripping and when I turned to look I saw the water below and the curtain was wet (below). The hostess didn't sit down to listen to us and immediately went into defense that we spilled it and that's why the curtain was wet because the water was leaving from above. And now I'm wondering, if the water left from above, wouldn't the curtain be wet starting from the height of the bathroom? Food for thought...But no they tried to make us liars and crazy and of course when we asked for some compensation he refused and continued to blame us. I couldn't understand what was going on and of course we packed up and left the same day.Unfortunately we should have taken the booking at that time but we were so flustered that we were unimaginable.So the owner of course caught up with us to complain against us and now our word against hers. A tip really away from such people.

Great space, tasteful, warm and friendly.
Cleanliness and freshness in all rooms!
The kitchen was more equipped than we expected, with treats, fruit and a wonderful wine! The bathroom is excellent and very functional! The bed is comfortable and suitable for rest and relaxation. The hot tub... was the icing on the cake as you could create an awesomely romantic atmosphere with all the trimmings (bubble bath candles and music).
The hostess was always friendly and willing to give directions and clarifications.
One of its biggest advantages is the underground parking, with an internal elevator to the rooms.
First time our stay in Athens was so comfortable and relaxing!
We will definitely prefer it again!
Nice staff! Room with a decent kitchen. Everything ok👍

The apartment was very close to all the “must see” places of Athens and close to transportation. The apartment was extremely clean, spacious and the beds were very comfortable. The hosts were very nice and left us some food, drinks and Greek goodies for our stay! they also provided advice on the best places and restaurants to visit. I would give more starts if I could! Would definitely recommend and would definitely return for a second stay!

My family and I really enjoyed our stay at BMV Apartments. Christos was a great host. He was very accommodating as we landed quite early and he had our rooms ready when we arrived. The complimentary snacks, fresh fruit and water was a great surprise. The location is perfect. As you are in walking distance of all of Athens attractions but also out of the busy city. We will be returning when we next say in Athens.

Chris, host was fantastic he told us how to get to all the local sites. The apartment was spotless and the bowl of fruit with the basket of tasty treats were an excellent surprise. I would recommend these apartments and am definitely going back again next time I am in Athens.

It’s been a long time since I stayed in a condo with such aura and class. Many thanks to the Hosts; both were extremely polite, friendly and helpful. There were also some treats in the apartment on the first day we arrived! I could not recommend BMV Apartments enough! Can’t wait to visit Athens again.

In a great location, in the city center! Very clean and beautiful! The hosts (Christos & Eugenia) so friendly and welcoming... Warm welcome and willing to help us with our every need!
With a big smile we renew our stay there and on our next visit to the city!
Value for money!!!!!
A tidy and spotlessly clean apartment in a very convenient location!! You can even see the Acropolis from the balcony!!
Very nice place, we had a great time here. Amalia is very helpful, and provided us with a lot of information. You can see Acropolis through the window, straight from your bed!

Very nice, clean, quiet and cozy apartment. Perfect for the couple. Everything as on picture. The owner Amaila is very nice and kind person. She’s in contact with you at all the time. Good speed Wi-Fi. Very close to the nearest super market and bakery. The neighborhood is safe. Location very close to the city center - 10 minutes walk. Beautiful and big balcony - perfect for breakfast and dinner with a bootle of wine. Second balcony is small but very beautiful view on the Acropolis. Elevator inside the building. We are highly recommended.
Good location, beautiful view from the terrace, trouble-free communication with the owner, clean place, recommendable! :)
We had a great time, thank you very much for everything!!!

Great rooms, a very good value for money. The people who run the place are generous and kind.
The area has some bars very close and a young vibe. Very recommended!

A great value for money!

Our room was spotlessly clean and offered a good night's sleep thanks to the perfect location—central yet surreptitiously quiet. Shower was great (that coconut soap was a nice bonus!) and we even had a pretty large fridge, extra toilet paper and a hairdryer, although we didn't use them. There's also a 24/7 rooftop area with Acropolis view for the guests at the 4th floor, that we look forward to using next time!

A small tip: our nightstand lamp didn't work due to a burnt out bulb. Checking for malfunctions like this can be a good addition to the maid's checklist. But that wasn't anything serious.

I would like to thank the Navitas team!

Rooms are very nice and clean. Small but very good function. The bed is very comfortable.
There is one contact via WhatsApp. You can ask all what you need and get the Codes for the doors.
The location is direct near to bars. They are loud until 3am. But it’s nice to go out. Drinks and food is near by. Also a mini market.
I would recommend this place and wanna come back, if I stay again in Athen.
Best regards
Sebastian

They were really nice to communicate but the room was really small and noisy, located far from the main tourist attractions. We haven’t got enough toilet paper or basic equipments for apartment (like kettle or cutlery) although we asked. The internet wasn’t good. This is out of season, and you can rent another hotel apartment that will be more value for money.

Hostel personnel is kind and friendly. Rooms are quite good but little tight. Only problem is outside noise from bars that cannot be dampened. You have to wait untill 2 am to be able to sleep. They are really loud outside 🙄. Again, inside, everything is fine.
The worst hotel experience I have ever had. The room wreaked of cigarette smoke and they would not let us move or refund us

Arrived late night, staff was waiting for us. The apartment was a few blocks away from the hotel reception, in fact google maps was taking us to the apartment and not to reception. Luckily the clerk caught us before we walked by. Elevator in the apartment building couldn’t handle both of us and our luggage. Luckily it was set to return to the bottom floor if it had a problem. We walked up the three flights of stairs but sent our luggage in the elevator. The room was comfortable and clean with good AC and a great shower with plenty of hot water. There was a small kitchen with cutlery and dishes. We walked to the M3 in the morning to head to the airport.

Adorable, friendly, extremely accommodating and reasonable. Maria was a sweetheart that greeted and registered us. She's an asset to McQueen and a gracious representation of Greek hospitality.
HIGHLY recommend these apartments/hotel

Not recommend. The area is full with garages. The apartment itself was OK but at the same price we could have something better in a better location. The first room we got was smelly from cigarettes and the AC was not that strong
Very kind and helpful person.

Thanks for making our stay better! Very beautiful and warm apartment and impeccable cleanliness. Very convenient location for access to the center of Athens, as it is next to the electric station. We will definitely prefer you again!
Excellent choice!!! Thank you....A house close to everything with all amenities and spotlessly clean. I don't think anyone would want anything more on their vacation.

Well done! Thank you for everything... Only good words about our stay... First of all, spotlessly clean apartment in a great location very close to everything. But above all I want to mention your flexibility in checking out without any charge, extremely rare since we travel often. Definitely our first choice when we visit Athens again👏👏👏

The air conditioning is noisy and the walls are thin. However, for this price, you get what you are willing to pay for (around $40/night if I remember it correctly). The hotel is near the M3 station, which is a direct line to the center and to the airport as well. However, the neighborhood seems to be deserted (may be due to the period though)
Great value for money. Very basic apartments but clean. Good location in Gazi.
Fair price, clean room. If you’re looking for a cheap, nice, little room it’s just perfect.
Nice rooms and very good location

I received what was in the description. The small room, approx. 20 min walk from the tourist center, but it was decent, clean, ok for the price paid. Ghetto neighborhood, but no one bothered us. Check-in and check-out is done in someone else's presence, without a key, because the entrance to the building and the room is done with a code.

It was a great, central place to stay, hence why I left a positive review on AirBnB. However, after I checked out, I received a $200 request for a damaged bathroom floor that I did not cause. Wouldn't stay with a dishonest host as a risk of the same thing happening to you.

The best spot  downtown for you. Exploring Athens never was easier due to the unique location of the property. Combines comfort with luxury and a unique private jacuzzi with an amazing viw of Acropolis!
Amazing place with an amazing host!
Modern space with an amazing view!
Nice decoration, comfortable, air conditioning and convenient kitchen, close to Larissa station.

Magnificent apartment with modern renovation! There is everything you need for life, down to the smallest detail, even a first aid kit. All household appliances are new. There is an elevator. Everything is very clean. Quiet, calm area. Very convenient location. The price is reasonable. Thanks to the owner for the nice bonuses!

Amazing Apartment. Great for 5 people (1 couple and three adults). The balcony had amazing views and the place became home for the 9 nights we booked. All beds were comfy enough and we were supplied with everything we need. Definetly reccomend 💃
Very nice cozy spot. Room was very clean, and if you get a chance to meet Hermes, consider yourself lucky, because he is the best!
Very clean and comfortable apartment with all comforts. Not the best location aesthetically but within an easy walk from the central attractions.
Very clean space with a fantastic view of the Acropolis. Bright, spacious, with parking available and quick access to central spots for nightlife in the city. Also suitable for work on the PC.

Thank you very much for hosting us in your place!

the space is exactly as shown in the photos, with an incredible view of the Acropolis from the living room and from the private rooftop (exterior space)!

Clean and comfortable apartment, very friendly hosts gave us recommendations for the area!

we highly recommend it and will definitely be back!

Staff were very nice, the rooms were well-updated, the breakfast quite complete, and the roof-top bar and welcome drink (for Accor members) quite pleasant in the evening.
The only issue is when we decided to walk back from Monastiraki at night, which took us through a couple dodgy streets, but nothing of concern.  Overall the location of the property is average.

overall, it was a mixed experience. The room was spacious, clean, and comfortable, and the service from the staff was excellent. However, the breakfast was a major letdown. The food was not tasty at all, and unfortunately, I even got sick after eating it. Honestly, I would have preferred to skip breakfast altogether. While the hotel is a bit far from the main attractions, the quality of the room and service made the stay pleasant. If you’re not too concerned about breakfast, it’s a solid option.

I had a good stay in this hotel, also because I got upgraded to a suite. This room was slightly outdated, but spacious, clean and the bed was good. The breakfast buffet was very nice with a great choice of products. The view from the rooftop pool and terrace is beautiful, with the Acropolis in the distance. The hotel staff is efficient but not overly friendly. The area around the hotel is somewhat sketchy, so we took taxis to move around (which are rather cheap).

Spent 3 days at the hotel after two weeks in Cyclades. Everything was great. The staff were friendly and knowledgeable. Due to the heatwave, we spent all our time in the hotel and enjoyed our time in the rooftop terrace and pool. A great place to prepare for the journey home.
We will definitely be back!

I had the best experience in Novotel! Everything was great! The stuff was unbelievable good in their jobs!The guys in the pool were really helpful and full of joy! They make the start of my day wonderful and really upgrade the whole experience! I will totally visit again this hotel

Great hotel, made by the staff. There was one blunt woman on reception when we arrived, but all the others were wonderful.

There was a nice chap there most of the time and he couldn’t do enough for us. The cleaners,  restaurant staff, and especially the ladies working at (a terrific) breakfast every morning made such a difference.

Can’t believe the reviews moaning constantly. We stayed several nights, so let me address a few.

The lift was fine. Wasn’t particularly slow. The pool was great and it has to close at some stage during the day to be cleaned. You could swim in it and look across to the Parthenon. Cool! The food was good, including that we purchased in the top floor restaurant. Yes, the rooms are a little dated, but they are clean and spacious and importantly the shower and bed were decent.

What more could you ask…

Finally, Athens is a working city, populated by several million people in the urban area. It will have ‘rough’ areas. But the hotel isn’t in one, as some reviews say. It’s just off a main shopping thoroughfare. We walked back to it, late, every night. There are homeless people, but they didn’t even talk to us, let alone threaten us.

Personally, I felt safer late at night than I do walking through my home town in England. Also, while we were there, we saw wonderful people helping them with food and especially water.

This part of the city has almost a grid pattern and there are plenty of alternative routes to walk to and from the hotel on. We walked past them, as I say, many times.

If you want a solid hotel, with great staff, smashing breakfast, rooftop views, in the centre of a vibrant, colourful, friendly city, then I think you could do a lot worse. We liked it very much.

Recently, I stayed in the Novus City Hotel in Athens, Greece, for 6 nights. The hotel's location is awesome, just 200 meters from the metro station. The room is clean and provides all modern facilities. The buffet breakfast contains a lot of options, and the food quality is very good. The rooftop restaurant gives a clear view of the Acropolis at night, and I recommend having your dinner once to enjoy the view with Greek food/salad. Last but not least, jelly chocolates at the reception are an excellent way to welcome the guests to the Hotel. Overall, I found the Novus City Hotel to be very good for travelers and recommended it for your stay in Athens, Greece.

Unsafe area!!! Every local we met told us this is the most unsafe area in the city. One block up there are homeless drug addicts all around. They didn’t seem aggressive but it was unsightly and sad. This hotel looks very nice on the surface but everything is cheap underneath. The woman at the front desk was very rude unfriendly and unhelpful. All of the other staff were excellent especially the front of house manager he was very nice. The upstairs restaurant pool and view were all great. the food was good and portions were huge. The furniture in the room was falling apart and there’s a window in the shower!? 🤷🏽‍♀️ our key deactivated every day and we had to go to the desk to reactivate it. The elevator is extremely slow but it runs fine and is clean.

We stayed here for two nights before starting our island hopping tour. The rooms are clean and spacious. The breakfast ample with a good variety. It is in a bit of a down trodden area but we turned right out of the hotel as well as left and wasn't threatened, just very sad to see so many lonely people

Location  is not great from the point of view of security being 200m from a drug addiction colony.

However  the stass are fantastic and gave obe approach warnings not to turn right when leaving the hotel and only turn left.

The pool has a great view of the Acropolis at night

The hotel is a nice budget place, near the centre for a reasonable price. Breakfast is ok, but the same every day, enough not to be hungry. The good thing you do not to be  on time, all food is brought even if you are after  the time. The staff is  friendly and really nice, speak a lot of languages. The neighborhood is disgusting, be aware of your suitcases, immigrants and drug addicts may steal if you do not pay attention.

The staff in the hotel is good and the morning breakfast offered is quite tasty as well. The main attraction about the hotel is it's proximity to all major historical and archeological tourist attraction in Athens and it's competitive pricing. The locality around the hotel though feels a little unsafe, especially late at night. The room we used was basic and the decor was old, but everything was fully functional. Do not expect any view kind of good view as most of the rooms face other buildings or the congested street

In the winter (I can't speak for the summer), this is an excellent budget hotel.  At an exceedingly low price, I got friendly service, an ample breakfast, a decent room, a hot shower, and a location close to downtown.  The neighborhood is not upscale, but it is quite decent--far better than some comments would have you believe.  I forgot pants and 2 shirts in the closet when I left for Crete.  When I came for a second stay 3 weeks later, the staff, who had kept them not knowing that I would return, delivered them to my new room.  In the budget category, this hotel is hard to beat.

The staff is amazing. Some of the best people we have ever met. Main problem we had was that the a.c isn't working great in some rooms. Also it's not in the greatest of neighborhoods. We enjoyed this place despite those facts.

Decent enough. Breakfast includes eggs, sausages, cheese, cereal, basic items. Bedroom was a bit spare and only had one visible outlet halfway up the wall which was annoying. The hotel is located in a rather dismal looking area, but the main square and metro are less than ten minutes away.

The hotel is a walkable distance from the metro station. There are quite a lot of mini marts also nearby.
The room was a little musty, but the service was really nice. All in all, it was of good value.

Avoid at all cost no value for money even as a two star. This is definitely the worst hotel I have ever experienced.

First of experienced one staff member to be rude in his mannerism, you would have thought he paid me to be here.

Rooms are outdated as well as dirty ants spiders probably bed bugs.

I'm not sure if you will get a good night sleep due to the random noise that occurs from the neighbours.

For what you pay the area is not in the best locations in terms of concerns of safety.

Myself is OK however a lady,  elderly couple, or a family with young children. I'm not so sure if that to be the case.

An unease amount of homeless people and alot of people in large amount of people on drugs down most streets.

Feel as though ripped of the price just not worth it because on a walk out you then come back to a none nice hotel.

Yes you do see alot of nice hotels in the same area but the experience changes if you pay that €10-15 more on a better decent hotel at least you feel comfortable when sleeping.

To clarify this was a stop over however your better of not doing that here.

Not sure what else to mention other than don't come here.

One of the best hotels of the city
I got there last minute with my father and sister and it was great.
What can I say,how cleaner it was ,how quiet it was and the staff was amazing.
The hotel was near the city center. I could walk to many places. I enjoyed my stay.
Definitely going there any time I visit Athens.

Upfront this hotel is probably circa 1970 so presents as “dated” however it was immaculately clean, the beds were very comfortable, air conditioning worked well, staff were exceptionally helpful and it has 24hr reception. We only spent 1 night here arriving at midnight and out by 10am. For its budget price it was very good value. Did find some decent places for food within a short walk.

This review is very much based on price point.

The first thing you should know, this is not a good area at all. For myself, I wouldn't care so much. However, I brought my girlfriend and very much wished I had booked into a more expensive hotel in a better area.

The area was very dirty, full of drug use, and definitely questionable for night walking.

Now on the price point of less than 50€ per night in Athens and only a 20 min walk from the Acropolis, this hotel is great!

The rooms are nice (for the price) and the service is good.

Although located in a difficult area with many drug addicted lying on the streets, this hotel is well-kept, quiet, tidy and clean. The staff is very friendly and does their best to satisfy the clients' wishes. They do not offer breakfast but the coffee shop Dodici can be accessed by means of a passageway directly from the hotel. Besides the neighbourhood is full of coffee shops and eating facilities. The hotel is very close to the Metaxourgeio metro station and the OSE railroad station. The hotel has a favourable price-performance ratio.

A staple for me in the last 4 years, whenever available.
Never had a bad experience here; top notch cleanliness and very polite staff.
The surrounding area can be dodgy, especially at night, but at the end of the day this is a central point of a touristy metropolis.
Considering the price tag, couldn't recommend it more.

For the special rate of 56 euro for my overnight stay I was very satisfied 3 important things for me number 1 rooms were immaculately clean . 2. Front of staff Valentina and the Gentlemen are always friendly and welcoming and extremely helpful I was offerded a late complementry check out also  as it's also  my second visit to the Minoa. Regarding the Location is great and a few minutes walk to the Metro to connect you to Synigma square Acroplis ect, some reviews have implicated the surronding neighborhoods it's Athens harden up . I had no proplems this time around or on my last stay.
Arrived late (1am) and needed a place to sleep for one night, great 24hr check-in, clean rooms, very friendly and helpful people. Good value at the price point
Definitely good services, very close to the metro station, good price 💯 but back streets are dangerous u need to be careful

Amazing little hotel, only a 5 min walk from Metro stations. Great buffet breakfast and the best reception staff ever!  Area has its ups  and downs but felt safe the whole time.Local restaurants are also a must.. If you love city breaks this is the hotel for you..

TERRIBLE HOTEL EXPERIENCE

Our group of 100 students had a distressing stay at this hotel.

Firstly, the hotel is located in a very unsafe area of Athens, rife with junkies. These individuals roamed the streets, frequently engaging in theft and yelling at all hours. We could even see them openly using drugs from our balcony.

Upon entering our rooms, we were met with a foul odor of mold and cigarettes. Opening the balcony door was not an option due to the pervasive smell from outside.

We were forced to order food to the hotel, as it was unsafe to walk the streets, even in the evening.

The room itself was filthy. The sheets and mattress were stained with vomit, urine, and other substances. There was hair everywhere, the mattress had cigarette burns, and there were holes in it.

Housekeeping was extremely rude and disrespectful when we requested new sheets. The receptionist was unhelpful and even yelled at us for inspecting the bed.

At least 10 other rooms reported bedbugs. Proceed with caution!

The locks one some of the rooms were not working, the doors could be opened without a keycard.

The air conditioning couldn't be controlled and ran all night, leaving me with a sore throat. Additionally, there was mold around the unit.

Breakfast was mediocre, though the food was fresh.

Overall, this hotel is a tourist trap. Avoid it at all costs!

Had a lovely stay here with the family . Staff were lovely, very pleasant and ever so helpful . Hotel room was very clean , fresh towels etc daily . Breakfast was lovely. Metro and other transport within three mins walk from the hotel itself

The hotel is located in the worst neighborhood in all of Greece. Junkies and and homeless people seen roaming arround all the time. I would feel unsafe walking around on my own. The hotel however was quite nice, with a really nice bathroom and a rather spacious room. The food selection was not the biggest but it tasted great. No issues with the staff at all.

The hotel is fine. The apartments are not. While the apartment itself is acceptable, the entrance is horrible, you must walk through a street filled with homeless individuals and filthy and unpleasant, we prepaid for 3 nights and left after the first  losing payment for the remaining nights.

It's a very unique low-cost Hotel perfect for a very cheap night in Athens Greece around 50 bucks a night very cheap and it's a good hotel for that price whatever you get that's what you pay for the rooms are decent every room comes almost every room comes with a ball can you so that is good with stunning beautiful views from the sundown and the mountains around Athens Greece decent bathroom shower TV small bar refrigerator plugs by the beds comfortable beds you know decent it's in the center it's literally 5 minutes from do you know these small streets the real vibes from Athens Greece and the nice lobby to chill out what's a good massage you're over there that was the job for you instead of a real massage
The service is great

Hotel was reasonable.
Bakery next door was to die for. Great food and sweets.
Location of hotel - was NOT safe when  walking around at night.
Average 3* hotel.  However the bathroom was filthy. Long black hair on shower walls and all over the floors.  This indicates that the room was not cleaned properly. Good service.

Good value hotel, clean room, nice bathrooms, and decent service with a rooftop bar that has a view of the acropolis  but if you are expecting anything above average than this isn’t the place for you. Just about everything is pretty average for a hotel. My biggest gripe is the WiFi in the hotel. It is atrocious, for the amount of money they make on tourist their WiFi is horrendous. I ran a speed test on my phone and didn’t get more than 5mb download. I couldn’t even load the airport webpage to check in with my flight. I couldn’t use FaceTime and webpages either didn’t load or took minutes to load. Using apps wasn’t as bad but the second you try to use something like chrome or explorer good luck. if you need WiFi for any business don’t even try their WiFi it will not cut it, you will have to use your phones hotspot and hopefully you have an international plan with unlimited data. I guess free WiFi is free WiFi.
Didn’t stay here, but went for brunch. €10 per person. It was a good continental breakfast with hot eggs and coffees.

Didn't have good experience with this hotel at all.
Carpet in the room and blankets aren't washed for years. It stinks so bad and there is no air conditioning (it got broken apparently).
Food is fine, I can't say it was bad.

I came during a peak season when all hotels were booked, so I ended up staying at the only available hotel in the area. It lacked amenities like air conditioning and only accepted cash. The night staff were unfriendly and unsmiling, making for an unpleasant experience. However, the morning staff, particularly a cheerful old lady, improved the mood. The hotel itself was outdated. I was relieved my stay was only for one night.

Expect basic accommodations if you book here.

❗️❗️❗️good to know before staying there❗️❗️❗️
- Cockroach-No airconditioning-Weird stuff -

The neighbourhood was disgusting and seemed dangerous, sadly there were a lot of homeless people. We did not feel safe there.
In the whole hotel the airconditioning was not working, although they have provided a fan, it was dirty. Also the stuff was weird they did not not speak so good english although it was an international hotel… there were cockroach on the floor (in the hall and also in the room...)
The experience there was awful, I definitely do not recommend this place to stay in Athens.

1.  Someone broke into my room in middle of night and when I woke up ran away
2 no air conditioner they lie that it's only going to be broken while your staying at hotel                      3   don't take credit card they make up story that there computer is broken and credit card machine is broken.                                                   4. Dangerous area.                              V v

Someone came into my room almost 440a.m. in the morning was alone thanks God I had my own personal lock so whoever it was wasn't able to open it more than 3 inches and I had an alarm that went off and I woke up what a disgusting creepy way to wake up didn't know what to do so I called my embassy to keep tabs on me to make sure I'm safe and barricaded my room and stayed up the whole night from then didn't know if I should call the front desk cuz thought maybe it's one of the workers of the hotel and wasn't sure if to call police or not then in the morning once I was sure the streets were busy already I left the hotel with all my belongings to go get money from the ATM machine to pay the hotel the worker stopped me why I'm leaving of my luggage and why I can't leave it with him to watch I didn't want to trust him after what happened with this intruder trying to get into my room at night so I declined but he kept on persisting first I thought he was trying to be nice but then he told me he doesn't trust me because I didn't pay it so I told him he could come with me to ATM machine prior to that I didn't want to tell where I'm going cuz I didn't feel comfortable with them knowing I'm going to the time machine if the break in had something to do with them the night before but now I realized that it was actually better because the area is it dangerous area in Athens after that I paid and left and my whole day was basically ruined from going around touring because of the break in since I only slept like two and a half hours just happy to be alive who knows what could have happened if that intruder would have got him before I woke up who knows what he was up to but it's not the only problem this hotel there is no air conditioner the place stinks it doesn't look As good as a pictures and they don't take credit card only cash even though it doesn't say when booking the also a dangerous area

I was pleasantly surprised by this hotel. We didn't spend a huge amount of time here, out exploring Athens for the best part of the 2 days. But our room was clean and functional, our bathroom was huge and also clean. The beds weren't super comfy but did the purpose. Once we got the air con working, this was a joy. The hotel is dated but has some great ceiling decorations and character to it. We asked for extra towels and were provided these. The location isn't bad, right near a metro station. It is possible to walk through to the main area of Athens from here also, although you do see some less favourable neighbourhoods. The worst part of this hotel was definitely lugging our luggage up 3 floors on that spiral staircase. It's incredible value for money considering.

You get what you pay for. Old rooms but nice and clean for the most part. Nice warm shower although no hold up top so had to be hand held the whole time, no biggie. Staff were friendly. Location isn’t amazing but only about a 5 minute walk from the metro which takes you anywhere you might wanna see.

Hotel is two-stars, so it doesn’t deserve more than that. Super noisy, both in and outside. Staff doesn’t care about this and keep talking among themselves at high tones. It’s magical but your clothes will smell cigarette smoke, I concur. Bathrooms and toilets are shared. Clean but old. They could be a very very beautiful hotel with high ceilings and antique look of the halls and stairs. But sadly the hotel is not well maintained.

BED BUGS.

My goodness, where to start with this place? First of all, it was infested with bed bugs. We were moved through three separate rooms on two different floors, and each had full-grown bed bugs in them. On top of that the management/desk manager barely spoke English and offered absolutely zero assistance with this issue. They just brushed it off and tried to get more money from us when we had to stay an extra night in order to get our clothes and luggage treated because of this problem. Again, absolutely no help at all. Terrible service in a parasite-infested hotel. Don't go here.

DO NOT GO!!!
Ugly run down filthy hotel nothing like the pictures found stains on the pillows and sheets and smells of cigarettes, showers were tiny and leaking from the faucet which kept flooding the bathroom. Drop ceiling in the room was dipped in the middle almost falling off, Only one power socket in the room and it was juri rigged to the door(not even stuck straight)

The rooms you see are the single rooms in the hotel. That part seemed nice. We rented an apartment and it was really trash. It's a complexe apart from the hotel. Shower head has nothing to hold on to. Hole in the floor where the drain is. Bathroom door couldn't shut and handle was loose. Held by a small 1 inch lock with a hole in the door. Beds were extremely stiff. Exposed wires everywhere. Outlets hanging. Main entry door to the room is secure though. That's the only positive.
€150 x night, plus €7 for the breakfast (breakfast at home is much richer than that), not even an espresso available, only from the machine. The rooms are horrible. Never again!

Great price for what you get. Ariston reception staff were amazing and kind. Friendly and professional. Thanks also for the free bottle of water!

Only constructive feedback would be to please remove the metal object in the shower of room 36 as this cut me whilst showering.

Apart from that, amazing staff and a really good price for the service. Close to the metro also.

Thanks again.

Great Location & very clean and tidy. Would have been a fantastic hotel if only our night would have been nicer. Maybe we were just unlucky with our room, but the AC only worked while we were in the room. Thus when we arrived after a day out in Athens, the room was a cozy 27 Celsius, way too hot to sleep. Cooling it down proved challenging, however, as the AC was directly above the bed and projected its chilly wind of blizzard directly onto our bodies. This plus the noise from the roof top restaurant directly above us and the very hard bed that would have made any monk jealous, made it quite hard to sleep.

Very overpriced for what it was - small basic room, noisy from other guests, basic amenities, not in a great area. Redeeming features - room was very clean, small balcony was nice, and the staff were very kind and helpful.

Hotel Katerina is outstanding. Exceeded our expectations. We've got a free upgrade to junior suite which was really great. The room was squeaky clean,  comfortable beds and freshly ironed sheets.
The breakfast was very tasty.
The safe hotel parking is extra helpful for the area. Pretty good walking distance to the centre. The staff was really polite and informative. They provided extra care and attention.  We would definitely stay here again.

Has aircon, location not that good, but Easy to get on the hop on, hop off bus route! Metro close by. Super breakfast. Extremely friendly staff. They go the extra mile. Giannis the Manager, Krista’s and Giorgos. We were able to leave our luggage in the reception area. Also has a laundromat across the road!
Robin and Dee from South Africa
its okay just the fact that homeless people are a few meters away from the hotel wich would be fine if they didnt smell

Staff all lovely and helpful with organising taxis. Breakfast bar has reasonably good variety. The rooms do seem outdated, they could probably change the curtains and paint the brown wooden furniture to a lighter colour to make it look fresh.
There is a metro nearby, but you could also walk 20-30 min to the centre. Some of the area seems dodgy, so we walked one day and took taxis another day. It costs no more than €10.
Personel at this hotel are very kind and supportive. The whole time was very clean and the breakfast is truly good.

STAY AWAY

I stayed there last night !
bed was missing a plank (as the bed was bending) that was the balcony,I didn’t bother and I did it myself.
in the evening there was a heavy engine working all night (after midnight I arrived) that wouldn’t let us sleep. I tried to call to ask what’s happening but the phone was not working as well.
The flush was  not working in the bathroom as well in result to all the  room smell after I used the bathroom without being able to flush.The cleaning lady this morning said that on the high floors the flush doesn’t work in general.
if you check my profile I am not a complainer but despite the fact that the young lady at the reception was very kind, the manager on the phone was attacking and lying that everything works properly besides the phone which he said “in busy hotels these things happen”.

very disrespectfully he said “what do you want,I’ll give you 10€ discount” .
just describing my experience

I missed my bus to Karditsa when I landed in Athens and a friendly taxi driver brought me here.   I asked to take me to a clean inexpensive spot to sleep for the night.   I was not disappointed.  The staff spoke English and understood my poor Greek.  The room was clean and the balcony was nice.   My only complaint was that it seemed a little out of date and the air condition was not working.   Thankful for the nice breeze that night

Not a good spot. We told a girl the first night our room had bed bugs and asked if we could be moved. To her credit she was very nice and said they didn’t have any available that night, but the next night we could. We then asked the next day about changing and the owner said no. He also wouldn’t offer us any sort of refund for the bed bugs, and told us if we wanted a different room the following day (we had reserved for 3 nights) it would cost us more money. He also didn’t seem even slightly surprised / caught off guard when we told him about the bed bugs. The younger girl we dealt with was great and you could tell the next day she felt really bad when the owner showed zero sympathy. Shes the only reason we gave a 2 star rather than 1 for service, but I would strongly recommend staying anywhere else.

We are very happy to were there. The Service there is very good . They helped us many many Times. The 2 Girls which worked at the Reception are very friendly . Special Thanks from us to both and also a great Thank you to the girl from the Roomservice , it was excellent . Greetings from us to all There.
Carmen & Robert Ilias

I don't know why this Hotel does not have 5 stars, because this Hotel exeeds all of my expectations.

Super air conditioner, nice, clean room with view of THE ACROPOLIS and oh man, the reception person is very friendly.

Excellent Hotel in Athens for this price!🤩

The hotel was quite comfortable to relax in with no any distraction or noise nearby and had the necessary facilities. I liked the way the hotel rooms were organized, and thanks for getting our request well in advance of arrival. Regarding the service cleaning, there should have been slightly better routines, the cleaners are hardly visible to the guests in the hotel, it is good that they are doing their job, but we have noticed that they knock and open the door at the same time without us knowing when they plan to clean our room. The other negative view is that the shower could have been prepared a little better as the water runs out to the basin and onto the floor in some cases without a glass protector or shower curtain. Otherwise, I appreciate the breakfast service at the hotel, they had a very delicious and hearty breakfast that was served every morning, we really liked this and the staff who work there are very nice and calm people.

Me and my partner recently visited this hotel for a weeks stay in Athens. The room was basic and we had booked a double room but instead we were given two single beds pushed together. The comfort of the beds was fine for us both, worth noting here that we are a young couple. The shower was powerful, hot and the hotel provided clean towels. They also provided a hairdryer but the one in our room was broke, I asked the first morning for a new one and they gave us one. There was a mini fridge in the room which was great to keep a couple of bits cool and the air conditioning worked great. The staff were friendly and helped if needed. The hotel is a 15 min walk from the centre which was ideal for us. The breakfast was nice, we are vegetarian and there was enough options for variety thought out the week.

This hotel will be ideal for anybody who wants somewhere to stay while in Athens but are not looking for any luxury.

Great value for money as it's centrally located within bus stops and metro station within few minutes of walk. Staff was generous to change my room from single beds to double bed, as my booking agent erroneously booked single beds.
Note that the lane around the hotels doesn't seem to be safe and be cautious while roaming around at night

i would really not advice going out past midnight because the area is generally known for being dangerous at night. that said, at day time you’re close to the center, the metro, and the big shops. rooms were good and breakfast was great, although the coffee was super watery. they should consider getting rid of the fat balding guy at the reception though.

Cons:
- The rooms were very very basic and the cig smoke would come right into the room.
-tv wouldn't work in any other language.
-Breakfast was not good and if you're not there the first hour, it's heavily picked over
-Bedding is very itchy and towels are hard
-Location I would give a 3 out of 5
-No microwave
-Rooms could be cleaner, but thats mostly a dust issue (note, I didn't open the windows)
-Noise carries A LOT. If there are ppl in the hall, it will feel like theyre in your room

Pros:
- Spacious rooms and BIG bathrooms
-Accept night walkins
-Market/Cafe diagonally across the street
-Has mini fridge
-Air con worked well
-A bit more on the pricier side for what you get, but still not bad
-Staff were not rude. Though we didn't interact with them much

Things to note:
- This area is a bit loud
-This is an area where there are A LOT of the unhoused community. They were not aggressive at all. Some places yell at them, but I don't think they deserved it.
-The train in this area isn't great and is a hot spot for pickpockets
-To get to the busy and social area, you will realize that youre on the outskirt of the area.

It’s easy to walk to restaurants, train station and all the attractions that bring people to Athens. The staff was super helpful and kind. The air conditioner worked really well. The WiFi went in and out. The area is a bit dodgy. I wouldn’t want to walk around there at night. You get what you pay for

Okay hotel in a neighbourhood with many sketchy people, not recommended to arrive at too late times, especially because one day after arriving around 2:00 am the hotel doors were closed and the number I had to call was always busy after even 5 tries. Breakfast is 8 euros, and you would expect more variety or some warm food like scrambled eggs for this price. Rooms on the fourth floor are nice with nice view from balcony, but the cards have to be reprogrammed every day at the reception. In the second room they put me the AC wasn't working, the TV signal was horrible. I would only recommend it to brave people who have plan B when their hotel locks them out for the night.

So the city is just sadly so dirty, tagged and filled with the homeless. It felt dirty but not dangerous. The hotel itself was nice as a previous review art was interesting but it was lying around in places. It looked like they had not gotten done putting it up. The television did not  work and no idea how to call down. The staff was lovely and kind. We did not ever partake in the breakfast but there were so many awesome bakeries/coffee shops. It was easy to get around but we will probably stay in plaka or mythraoki next visit. It just felt more vacation spot.

For me a bed has to be comfortable and the AC needs to work and it’s quiet. It meets alll those needs. Breakfast is good and the service from the owner lady is 5*
They could use minor your ups like the grout was moldy looking in the shower but that’s common.
For the price it’s a great value and I keep coming back. Not in the core center and not the nicest neighborhood but again the Hotel is clean and safe.

Good hotel but a bit pricey for what it offers (approx €100 euros a night including taxes)
You need to walk up a set of maybe 5-8 steps to get to the hotel so not really wheelchair friendly.
Receptionist and housekeeping were respectful and helpful
Room was a bit tight and toilets had no lock and the door was glass albeit frosted glass.
They don’t have money exchange service.
Lift doors have no sensors so you must be careful.
Nice quiet rooms
Has a swimming pool
Cosy reception area

The immediate surroundings of this hotel were disturbing, disconcerting, and perceived as perilous by city residents. The hotel itself was tolerable, moderately clean, very friendly staff, and has delicious breakfast choices.  No clean or pleasant views, thin walls, and a rudimentary shower with barely a shower pan underfoot. My vacation was soured during my stay there. Good value if you can brave it.

The hotel itself is basic and perfect for travelling on a budget. Very close to everything you need. The immediate area can feel a bit ‘unpolished’ but coming from a city myself I didn’t mind this, and I never had any trouble. The rooms were clean, and fresh towels were supplied each day. The breakfast was basic but everything you might need to get your day started. Staff are very friendly and accommodating. Would recommend if on a budget, but if money is not an issue, there are many nicer, more modern places to stay in Athens.

A good value, basic hotel. The location is good for all the major sights and the city centre, but the immediate area outside the hotel is very run down. Wasn’t an issue for us, but some may find it a bit unsettling.
Rooms clean and comfortable. Very quiet and the staff in the hotel were friendly. Breakfast we thought was good. For the price, it’s a good hotel.

The rooms were decent and did the trick. AC worked amazing. The main issue was the shower did not keep water in well and flooded the bathroom pretty easily, so make sure you open the drain.

The service was great and the staff was always friendly and ready to help us. The breakfast in the morning was fine, better than most US hotel breakfasts but not incredible.

The location for sightseeing was great for the price - only a 20 minute walk from the acropolis and 10 minutes from nice areas to walk around and hang out. However, the specific street the hotel is on is out of the ordinary with the amount of homeless. I walked back later in the night multiple times and saw lots of people getting a free stay outside the hotel. This, in combination with the need for a security guard in the nighttime hours and a police presence on multiple nights makes me reluctant to recommend this. I was satisfied for the price but would not recommend a group of girls stay here and walk down this street at night unless they taxi straight to the door.

I would stay again - good for a frugal traveler.

Hotel rate with breakfast included is great value for the money!
You pay a small tourist tax when you check in but it’s cheaper than Rome or most other European cities.
There is no bar restaurant in the hotel.
The room is basic. Shower is small but lots of hot water. The staff are excellent and help in anyway they can. Breakfast (included for us on Air Canada Vacations) was very good. Location is a 5 minute walk to Omonoias Square and subway and 15 minutes to Ministraki Square.
Wifi included.

The area outside the hotel is rundown and we saw a couple of needle users across the street a block up. There are open businesses on the street with people sitting outside so there are people around. We were there a week and no one ever bothered us! Turn right out of the hotel when walking and the main drag is literally a short block and a half walk. Pay attention to the uneven sidewalks. Security guard on duty at night out front of hotel.

I would have given this hotel 5 stars if it were not for the welcome we received upon our arrival.  Our overseas flight landed at 8 am and we were at this hotel by 10.  We could not get a room until 2 pm!  This was after a long day of traveling.  After speaking with other hotel guests, this seems to be a persistent problem.  Suggestions, perhaps?  Even if you could spare a glass of water and a cool place to sit would have been nice. Maybe a free breakfast or cup of coffee/tea?  I appreciate that the rooms need cleaned, but I'm sure there were some  people that left early.  An early arrival room for weary travelers would be appreciated.  Also, Theo must have been tired of dealing with weary travelers as he wasn't very pleasant.
The positives - rooms are extremely nice with a Jacuzzi tub and a shower with body sprays. Beds are super comfortable.  Rooms were clean and nicely prepared.
Rooftop pool, bar, and dining area are just beautiful to relax after a day of sightseeing.
All other staff were more than helpful and very kind.

This was our first time staying at this hotel chain and we were pleasantly surprised. The customer service was top notch, every single person was attentive and went out of their way to assist us. The rooms were very nice and the beds were very comfy. The roof that bar was really nice and had great views. I personally wouldn’t stay at this hotel again due to the location. I would stay at this chain but I’m sure there’s far greater locations. The area surrounding the hotel has lots of homeless and druggies just lingering and sleeping in the streets.

Loved this hotel. Very convenient for walks to the city centre and historical sites in Athens. The sky lounge is very popular for an afternoon swim and sun. It becomes magical at night for a romantic dinner with a view of the Acropolis.

We enjoyed our stay very much. Staff was very polite. Our room was very clean and comfortable. We liked the overhead shower but could not get our handheld to work. Housekeeping came twice a day. A nice tray of cookies and dried fruit awaited us when we arrived. We brought our own universal plug adapter but were disappointed that there were no USB ports in the room or outlet in the bathroom.
The pool was wonderful! Hot tub looked inviting but water was not warm. Food and drinks on the rooftop we're good but menu options were limited.
Breakfast buffet was Amazing!

I had a great stay in Athens—my second visit after 11 years, and the experience was just as good as I remembered.

Location: While the hotel is slightly removed from the city’s main attractions, it's only a 15-minute walk or a quick 5-minute metro ride to the lively parts of town. The Omonia metro station is conveniently close by.

Room: The room was well-furnished and spacious enough to accommodate an extra bed for our child. The bathroom was also quite large, though the hydro-massage shower felt outdated and a bit tricky to navigate as it’s quite high to climb. However, the cleanliness was excellent throughout.

Breakfast: The breakfast offered a good variety of hot and cold dishes—perfect if you want to avoid the hassle of searching for breakfast in the city early in the morning. The only downside was the coffee, which, like in other Melia hotels, wasn’t great. I’m still not sure if it’s the machine or the coffee beans they use.

Rooftop Pool: One of the highlights was the rooftop pool, a rarity in Athens. Although it’s small, it’s a refreshing escape on those hot days, with the added bonus of a bar. However, I wasn’t thrilled that non-hotel guests can access the pool for a fee, as it made the area feel a bit less secure.

Overall a pleasant experience. Fantastic rooftop bar and restaurant with great food and stunning views of the acropolis. The service staff were all very friendly and d helpful. The breakfast buffet leaves a bit of room for improvement. The rooms are clean and have a comfy bed and seem to be well soundproofed as I did not hear my neighbors which is often a nuisance in some other hotels. Would stay there again.

From reading some of these reviews a week before I arrived I thought I was going to be staying in the worst hotel on earth, the reality was very much the opposite.

The room was a really decent size for a European city hotel! The bathroom was fine, some room for improvement in that area but perfectly adequate!

The reception staff were extremely helpful and friendly, the breakfast buffet was also very good!

The rooftop is the star of the show, we ate at the restaurant twice during our week in Athens, the food there was exquisite and the view is sensational! Also the staff made us feel very welcome and special! I didn't use the pool but other members of my party did and have no complaints but it can get busy on certain days and times.

Location is perfect! The metro is right outside the hotel, which is very accessible! 4 stops to the acropolis, what more could you want?!

Also top tip, there's a cafe just on the corner of the hotel which is actually part of the Stanley, it's the perfect place to get a quick bite to eat!

The hotel is great. Best service. Rooftop facilities are the best!

The bar is too expensive. That's really a shame.
The rooms are okay (superior), bathroom way too small. Minibar with free drinks is actually just water refill daily. Expectstion on that benefit was too high.

Location is okay. Near the subway which is very nice.

The amenity space on the rooftop is the best feature of this hotel. The pool area is really large and very nice. It's very busy so it's not easy getting a spot to sit but it's worth the effort.
The free breakfast on the rooftop is also very good. Very good selection of food and the views are amazing.
The rooms themselves are dated and the bathrooms are so extremely small. One would expect a more luxury feeling for this price point in terms of being modern and larger. The front lobby feels dated as well.
Seems they have spent all their money on the rooftop.
There is a subway right out front so that's helpful but the area is a 5 out of 10 you really need to walk about 20 minutes before you get anywhere nice.

The best thing about this hotel is its rooftop pool that looks on to the acropolis. The showers were also incredible, but the beds weren't particularly comfy.

The food was amazing. We had this incredible orzo and shrimp dish for lunch. It was incredible.
