Only mins walk to the Larissa Train Station. Rooms are ok except there was persistent smell of cigarette. You can get hot water from the reception at the ground floor. And bonus: they allow you to leave your luggages for storage if you need to get away for a while.

The room was cheap. We payed €65 per night but even this felt too much for what we got. The only good thing about this place is that it’s close to the Athens Central Station.

The walls were paper thin. When I say paper I mean single ply see thru tissue paper. The mattress was the worst possible combination of a concrete slab and a cardboard box. The bathroom was useable but tiny and the provided shampoo and soap were astoundingly bad. The room, bathroom, and hotel infrastructure in general felt very dated.

The only good things I have to say is that the staff was friendly, our room was ready immediately, and the place was relatively clean.

Perhaps worst of all, was the rest of the clientele at the Hotel. We were awoken multiple times between 11:30pm and 1am by doors slamming. Not just closing forcefully, but ridiculously loud and hard slams. One after another after another. Then, at 5:45am, we were awoken by a shouting match between the two guests in the room above us. Obviously this aspect is out of the control of the hotel, but it’s something to keep in mind when considering the price of the room.

Good location, but the bathroom was beyond dirty, the air vents were closed shut with duct tape, later it turned out that there were cockroaches in the room. We asked to get changed into another, cockroach-free room, but that side not happen, we checked out after 2 nights and moved to a different hotel. There was no sorry, no assistance, no refund, nothing. We suspect the receptionists do not speak English, all our inquiries have been ignored. Avoid, if you can.

Extremely clean room. Clean bedsheets , clean towels, the balconies is a plus and bigger plus is the metro in 2 minutes and the huge supermarket opposite. The price also was very reasonable. But that's it. Don't expect something more than this

1. The rooms were very old and even had fungus in the bathrooms
2. We were charged 3 Euros per room per night as Tax (which in all other Hotels across Greece is 3 Euros)
3. There were cockroaches in the room
4. Locality was very deserted after 8PM

Overall for the prices being charged, other Hotels were far better.

Nice hotel and great for the price. Rooms are adequate, clean and with everything you need for a city break. Where this hotel sets it apart is the amazing roof top bar and pool area. Wonderful views to end a busy day of site seeing, with a nice cold glass of wine. 👌

The rooms were clean and sufficiently equipped. The location is a bit far from the acropolis but it does have a great rooftop view of it and is just four stops away on the metro. It's a little farther from the airport (about 45 minutes). The rooftop pool is also nice though small and the surrounding deck gets SUPER hot so wear your flip flops until you step in! The food servers were fantastic but the front desk courtesy was hit or miss. One of the gentlemen who worked it was super friendly and helpful and the other was very aloof.

Stayed here for 2 nights in one room as a family of 4. Honestly this hotel was nothing special - sounds harsh but in our opinion the photos are deceiving. The rooftop and breakfast are the best parts so I’ll start there, the rooftop has friendly and attentive staff when ordering food/drinks and a beautiful view of the Acropolis with ample seating and a small pool. The included breakfast is the best we have ever had at a hotel. Tons of variety with sweets and fresh fruit, breads, eggs, meat, cheese, Greek yogurt, specialty coffee etc. The breakfast is the only reason I didn’t give 2/5. The not so good part - If you’re travelling with small kids who will want to splash and play in a pool - note that this pool is small and other people in the pool will be splashed. The room was very cramped and lacked outlets (no outlet in the washroom) so many of us had to take turns charging our devices. We also had 4 single beds, which we didn’t mind, but they weren’t very comfy. Overall we agreed that this is a “bare minimum” hotel - but the breakfast and rooftop were nice, as well as the location. Very close to the subway which was a plus!!

Nice hotel highlighted by an amazing rooftop bar and pool with a city view including the acropolis - manned by helpful and friendly bar staff. The location is good enough, whilst not central it is close to the efficient metro. Breakfast wasn’t amazing however cannot think of any other negatives for the value of this hotel!

The rooms are not all of the same quality. We had two, and the last one needed some attention.
The location is not great!
Rooftop is pleasant. It has a view of the Acropolis. Unfortunately, the pool is way too small!

I suspected bc they can’t find our prepaid booking, we got a small room with an antique bathroom, just icky! Towels were stiff & old looking, didn’t use them. I used paper napkins I brought instead.

My husband was doing the booking and only looked at location, not the reviews nor pictures of the place. And because we arrived almost midnight to leave to cruise port at 9:30 am. It was doable for the night.
If you are on a budget and looking for alternatives to hostel, this could work for you. Be sure to check a day or 2 before arrival ensuring they have your reservation. Don’t want to be without a room if you arrived late like us

A perfect place to stay overnight in Athens.  The room was tired (cracked shower base and bathroom door with notches cut in it so it could be opened/closed without hitting the toilet), but it had everything I needed, including a fridge, air-conditioning and a comfy bed (the pillows were like bricks tbh!). I took my own food and drink.
The guy on reception was curt, but, thanks to Google maps, I found my way to Omonia station easily, and from there to the airport for only €9!

Good hotel, the price is very good for the service, you can say its the best hotel for that price and that area.
Highly recommended

Very basic hotel, quite clean. Old decor, shower is very small and the room did smell of smoke. The room has air conditioning and a balcony. Hotel staff were mostly friendly and helpful. The location isn't ideal especially for a solo female traveler. Closest square is Monastiraki which is a good 20-25 minutes away. Price is reasonable.

We came to Athens for a short city break, so we were looking for accommodation that was close to the centre and not too expensive. The hotel was good value for money, the room was small, but for such a short stay - when you just go home to sleep - it was fine. The problem was with the area, huge rubbish, dirt, just bad faces, scary people, druggies. It was such a bad neighbourhood that we didn't even go downstairs at night. Otherwise, I recommend it to everyone, but be prepared that you might not dare to walk down the street.

I recently stayed at Parnon Hotel and had a generally positive experience. The hotel’s location is a major plus, as it’s conveniently close to many of the main attractions, making it easy to explore the city. However, the hotel itself is a bit dated, and the internet connection was unreliable in some rooms. We also noticed that some areas around the hotel didn’t feel very safe. On the bright side, the breakfast was excellent, and the service was top-notch. Overall, it’s a good choice if you prioritize location and great service.

Had a great stay here, beds were comfy, room had everything you needed, plenty of sockets, fridge, hair dryer etc And the breakfast wasn't to bad either with some hot food included. The thing that made up for it was the staff, everyone was polite and helpful, and the reception team was only happy to help and give you their time! For a three star hotel this was a lot of bang for your buck!

Conveniently located close to the metro station.  My friend & I had a wonderful stay at this hotel.   The room was impeccably clean   the breakfast was superb plus the staff were professional and helpful.   I'll definitely stay here a second time.   Thank you!

I've been in Athens for 20 days now, I've stayed in many high-class hotels, but I've never seen such service in any hotel. Wonderful service especially at the reception, wonderful people, special thanks to Angelos and Nektarios Thank you guys. If I return to Athens one day, I will only return here. Cleanliness 10/10

Amazing small hotel,
Staff are sooo friendly nothing Is to much, they give you map and show you best ways to get to places things to do
Rooms are good size balcony views are nice.
Breakfast is refreshing and someone is  always there to help

Great hotel

We only booked a day room for our last day in Athens, and it provided everything we needed. The room was okay, had air conditioning (although it was weak, but it made the room definitely cooler and more comfortable than the outside temperature). The bathtub was leaking a little, but it wasn't a huge issue. The location of the hotel is great - close to metro station and will make it easy for you to tourist around.
As there's no 24-hour reception, you might need to wait a bit at the door when you get there. After all, you get what you pay for and this offers good value for the price.

This is the worst hotel in the world. I was supposed to stay one night but I couldn’t because this hotel is horrible. Nothing worked, air conditioning was broken, safe box didn’t work, the bathroom had a leak in the ceiling and if you go out you must leave the room key. But the worst of all was the owner or the manager, a rude man. He insulted me and almost hit me because I told him the safe box was not working so I couldn’t leave the room keys.
I should have reported him to the police but I didn’t have enough time to do it cause I had a flight next morning.

Please don’t stay in this hotel!!!! The owner/manager is very dangerous

Not everybody's experience is the same, but I had a wonderful visit here. The room was adequate, with a nice open air balcony.  The service friendly, and the location was perfect to go down and visit the shops, museums, and sites.

first off picture on the net is photo shopped does not look anything like the picture. The only thing I liked about the hotel was the location and the fact that we had a balcony.  there are alot of great restaurants in walking distance especially Alexander the GREAT Restaurant which I highly recommend. breakfast was horrible for me. I don't know what kind of milk they served but it was not moo milk🤮🤮. they advertised a restaurant but was not opened. the owners were really friendly and very helpful 🙂

We stayed in the hotel for 7 nights. The hotel has a wonderful sphere. Our room wasn't big, but cozy, with lovely interior details. There was a bathtub in the bathroom, of a very comfortable shape. A small mini-fridge, so we could keep there berries and yogurt.  A lovely balcony with 2 chairs.   Comfortable mattresses on old stile beds,  sleeping there was nice. Air-co worked properly. Wi-fi worked perfectly everywhere. The owners/ personnel are friendly and helpful. After mentioning that I'd like to have tea in the room, the lady owner suggested to lend me a kettle for the room, and she lent us also a knife. I felt there at home, and would stay there again.

We got the room after they told us the apartment we booked at the near neighbourhood is not available.
To be honest, after we passed on that neighborhood on our way to the hotel we were glad since that street seems really sketchy and quite crappy.
The room itself in the hotel is pleasant, vintage looking, clean with great wifi and a small balcony.
The stuff were friendly and welcoming, the only thing to improve is the vegan options in the breakfast buffet.

The rooms are small but really comfortable. Air conditioning and smart TV. I really liked the breakfast with plenty of options. The view from the top floor is also really nice. I recommend it to everyone. The neighbourhood is a bit ugly if you have a car you need to look for a parking lot.

Terrible Service – Beware of the Apartment Scam: This hotel also manages apartments, and when we arrived at the reception to collect the keys for our apartment, we were told that they had double-booked it, and the apartment was no longer available (despite us booking for two nights). Instead, they put us in a hotel room that was clearly meant for staff, not guests.

After reading other reviews, it seems this "missing apartment keys" scam has happened to several other people as well, so beware if you're planning to book an apartment here!

To make matters worse, the receptionist was rude and acted annoyed, as if the situation was somehow our fault instead of their mistake.

It's disgraceful. I can’t understand how this place is still in business. On top of that, it's located in a very run-down area, with junkies hanging around right outside the entrance. I won’t be returning here, ever.

The hotel is in a less than wonderful area, with people sleeping on the streets close by. It's in a handy location to metro and bus for exploring the sights.
The rooms are OK, breakfast quite good, but cold. Don't have the buffet dinner, again cold and very limited choice. Staff were pleasant and helpful.

The only good thing about the hotel is friendly staff. Maybe also the dolmadaki we ate at breakfast. The rooms were very tiny. The bathroom is not proper. While showering, the water spreads all over the bathroom floor. No slippers or flip flops. Wet floor no slippers no hygiene unfortunately. The district in which the hotel exists is full of homeless people and junkies. They gather towards evening. We were afraid while returning to hotel in the evening. Streets were not safe. No coffee table,  no table no chairs in the room or balcony. No place to eat or drink sth in the room. No table for women to put makeup stuff in the room.
All night we couldnt sleep because of narkoman people who used drags and prostitutes. They are crying loudly and fighting.  I dont know what to do impossible to sleep
All the staffs are friendly. Very affordable. The room is neat. Walking distance from main tourist spots and train station.
Terrible is the word
XXX Hotel but if the night catches you it's a good choice.
Good

I stay here every time I am in Athens. It is very affordable and about a block from the metro. It is a 10 minute walk to Psiri and 15 to Monasteraki. The people are amazing. I highly recommend it. The pics show the view from my room.

The hotel is very simple as the breakfast. But the treatment of the receptionist was extremely rude at me when I notice that calling a Uber was half of her taxi, and when other costumers heard me she started to scream in front of everyone. Not classy at all and super disrespectful. Never staying there for sure. I do NOT recommend this hotel

Here’s the translation to English:

"Very modest rooms. For showering in the bathroom, I recommend bringing your own slippers. Breakfast is mediocre... All in all, acceptable for an overnight stay, nothing more..."

The hotel is clean, friendly staff, and has a delicious fresh breakfast.  Good internet.

It is located in a transitioning neighborhood across from Wyndham.

I definitely recommend the Apollo Hotel.

Really good hotel, close too the centre of athens. Around 20 mins walk to the centre so not so bad. The hotel itself is budget but we loved it, cost room, aircon, TV, bathroom with amazing shower. I would highly recommend this hotel. Perfect base for city exploring. The staff were nice and friendly and the cleaners were nice.
Lovely  boutique  hotel. Small but very adequate  rooms, with a balcony. Much better than average breakfast included. Great location, very helpful staff. Highly recommended.

Clean room and good air conditioning. Good bathroom.
We got 2 singles, rather than a double/queen which was odd.
Nearabouts Omonia square/metro. Dodgy part of town, lots of drug use. Taxi driver told me not to walk the streets alone.
But I found it fine in the daytime, it’s actually fine. It was so central - I walked to all the sites including Acropolis etc. Perfect location that way. Just be careful about pickpockets/etc and stick to main roads, and travel in the daytime.
Breakfast was continental buffet.
And we were able to have cereal/coffee/biscuits when we needed a really early checkout.
One odd thing - encountered a hostile receptionist when returning to the hotel who demanded to recheck my room number/that I was a hotel patron… a bit of a shame as otherwise I thought people were fine.

The experience was extremely bad. Have you guys ever heard of any hotel charges extra 25 euros for bring people back. I can understand if the reception asked the second person to check in for security reasons, but what is the point for them to ask for extra charge? The receptionist said you paid for one person’s price and you didn’t pay for the second one. Are they charging not based on the number of room but on the number of person? Strange, absolutely SCAM. On top of that, the room is not clean at all. limescale in the baths and toilet, disgusting. AVOID THEM!!!

Great hotel and good sized rooms for solo travelers. The only thing I personally did not like was the view of the abandoned block of flats in the back of the hotel where my room was. But overall it was great.

They have a great breakfast variety with good coffee and juice selection. Very friendly and welcoming staff.

First of all: apart from one lady, all staff members were friendly and accommodating. The hotel is very central and there's a great breakfast buffet. There's a tiny elevator which helps you to get your luggage upstairs. The bed in my second room was very comfy (the first room looked nice but the bed was hard as a rock). Overall, good value for money. 👍

Now my issues: The first night I was in a room with a very hard bed. It was very uncomfortable and I could barely sleep. When I told the receptionist in the morning I'd like to change rooms, she first told me that all rooms had the same mattresses but eventually agreed to let me move. In the second room the bed was super comfy, so she definitely didn't say the truth about all mattresses being the same. Also, after moving (on my second night) someone opened my door around midnight even though I had locked it from the inside. It was someone who had a key card to my room, not sure whether another guest's key card accidentally worked on my door or if it was a staff member. Anyway, I screamed and the person left. Afterwards, I didn't feel safe in the hotel anymore. When I told the staff about it the next morning, she said that was not possible and didn't take my concern seriously. Instead , she implied I was making it up. Very disappointing reaction! I asked for the camera recordings of that night to prove that I was telling the truth and she told me there were no cameras upstairs. What the heck?! 🤨

Otherwise I really liked the hotel and all the other staff members were friendly, but this one lady clearly doesn't like people. 🤷
I love this place..very nice n comfort..the best hospitality ever❤..very central u can easily rich the center n all around places..thanks..
There were bedbucks, a lot!!! I had more than 70 bites, after this I tried to talk with the owner and he didn’t answer me and don’t want to talk about it, very bad treatment!
Very simple accomodation,  but comfortable enough and inexpensive.
Nice place, but there is crime in the neighborhood, because lately there is smuggling and drug trade nearby... But there is police
Excellent apartment. Very close to the metro. Direct access.
Stayed there between 9-11.09.2024 .Friendly staff welcomes you. Rooms ate clean and tidy, free wifi you can use. Close to Monastraki and metro station. Very very good breakfast.

Tiny rooms but clean and tidy.
Good breakfast and friendly staff.
Great location near omonia metro
Lots of food options and shops .

This hotel was absolutely beautiful, clean and very comfortable beds . Maid service was 100% front of desk friendly and very accommodating and on our last day allowed us to keep our luggage at hotel and return just before our flight . It can be very noisy as it is on Main Street but all of hotels are mainly on Main Street . Close to all site seeing if that’s what you are going to see . 100% would return that’s for sure 💜 comes with balcony if requested x
Stayed here for four nights during a tour and found the place to be nice.  Friendly staff and the breakfast buffet is nicely stocked.  I would stay here again most certainly.
Great hotel, nice rooms excellent service, a bonus we arrived at 9:30 am and were allowed to chechin right away. Good location close to main sites , recommended

We needed the hotel for an emergency (by the way we were of family of 5) and they told 60 euros at first, but when we said we would take it, they increased the price of up to 81.5 euros.

Not just that, but the room was horrible, not clean and smelt like this weird smell. The air-condition didn't work because of how old it was, and the bathroom had previous paper towels of people's waste in the toilet bin.

One of my worst experiences in my life I wouldn't recommend it.

Good location as its very close to the metro station.
The rooms arent very clean. The bedsheets are either stained or have holes in them.
The toilet is small and not great either. Shower is very small and the curtain is very short which leads to the whole bathroom floor getting wet.
There is a lot of noise from outside if you keep your window open. Every night of our stay the trasb would get picked up and that was very loud.
The noise also comes from appartments in the neighbouring buildings.
From our room we faced an appartment where the people made noise untill very early in the am,music or loud talking.
However for us its was okay and we managed to get a good sleep and start rather fresh every day.

The staff is quite friendly  but really can't do much to the dilapidating place. The room was very stuffy
The showers are very small and one has to use a hand held shower head. I had a problem of ants all over the room. Though it is near the train station and bus station, the neighbourhood just gives of an unsafe vibe.
The little  bar fridge was a nice touch though. Only stay if really in a tight situation.

Really unfriendly staff. I could not understand what they where saying because their English was to bad. The girl at the enterance started screaming at me. Really unfriendly welcoming. Bad dirty rooms. Insane this hotel still exists. No breakfast like stated.

During my stay I was keen to sightseeing for a 3-day trip thus, I have chosen a cheap accommodation in the outskirts of Athens.

Pro: The Hotel totally met my expectations and the reviews all around the web. The location of the hotel is nice, very close to one of the metro stations (5 mins walk only). The room was clean & tidy, while the staff was helpful and all of them spoke well English.

Cons: I chose a room with bathroom facility: the bathroom is well equipped but extremely small, and the showering place is very little... The water always went out to the floor. The breakfast is okay, but there is not a wide range of variety of foods.

Conveniently located in the middle of a city , can easily get around with buses or metro.
Walking distance to many of the famous attractions like Acropolis, museums and many others.
Plenty of good restaurants nearby. Hence , this hotel is an ideal location to stay and explore this beautiful city, Athens.

Prices : reasonable , a very good breakfast is included
Rooms : spacious, bathrooms are quite nice.
Staff is generally OK. They would help you when you need.
Special thanks should go to their wonderful housekeeping staff. They do a perfect job !!

Family friendly: they provided a good cot bed for our toddler ( need to request)
Hotel looks a bit old , has a vintage appearance. However, it’s clean and well arranged.

If we had to stay here again , we would definitely choose this hotel.

Only main problem we faced was , the surrounding can be a bit noisy at night , and wouldn’t feel safe with various kind of people roaming around the area. But police is there all the time , hence no security issues occurred during our stay .
Efharisto!!!

I did not know that all the hotels were full. Fortunately, I went to them and there was a lady at the reception and explained my situation to him. She was really helpful with me and allowed us to spend the first night in the reception hall because all the rooms were full. On the second day we got a room and everything was really clean. Everything was there. I advise everyone to go to them because the place is also close to the tourist places. 🥇😀🙏🩵

Having stayed there for around four days, I can confirm that the negative comments you're probably reading here are either coming from clueless tourists or people who have expectations way too high.

For a three star hotel, I'd say this is the definitive choice for anyone. The prices are incredibly cheap, the breakfast is just a solid ten out of ten, it's clean and comfortable, the staff are some of the most kindhearted people I've come across and they clean the rooms often.

Only minor nitpick I have is in regards to the walls of the room, which could receive a bit of a paint job.

But aside from that? I'm satisfied.
Any issues I thought I'd have didn't happen, and others that those other reviewers seem to mention are not the problems of the hotel but rather the area itself.
It's Omonoia after all, the very epitome of crime in Athens. What the heck do you expect?! Sunshine and rainbows?!

Pros:
Price - Got a room  for a very good deal for a day.
Rooms - I had booked the Double Room but was very glad when the lady at the reception upgraded our room to  superior room.
Rooms were very spacious.
Food - Breakfast was not that great but i must appreciate that the receptionist was very kind and made arrangements to pack  food for me and my wife and to deliver it to us in advance as we had to board an early morning flight
Location - Location was just a block away from the main street which i loved as it was not so noisy but yet very easily walkable to the main junction. Lots of Taxis available all the time.

Cons:
Nothing much to comment

The staff was extremely polite and friendly !!!

The maids did an excellent job upkeeping the room and overall the quality of the room!

The hallway was nice, and they were kind enough to offer us a place to temporarily store our luggage until our ship arrived !

My huge complaint came from the room it could use a bit of painting !! It looked absolutely horrible, which made me subtract one star from the overall experience however, price to quality ratio it was worth it.

We stayed for 4 nights,  breakfast and wifi included, and we left super satisfied with the overall experience. I just wish the room was in a slightly better state. If you pay close attention to the wall picture, you can see it looks horrible.  The breakfast was also AMAZING they had an amazing selection, and my belly thanked them!!!!

DO NOT STAY HERE YOU WILL REGRET IT!!!!!

We couldn’t stay here. We checked in and checked out, there was so many reasons we couldn’t put ourselves through the stay. Firstly, all of our furniture was falling apart. I got locked in the bathroom because the handle fell off the door and then the only chair provided collapsed, the sheets are ripped. The floors were dirty, it was horrible. The photos online are catfishes and the hotel is in a disgusting location.
Within an hour we were harassed by people on drugs for money, our arrival taxi even detoured to avoid the area. We found a SYRINGE outside of the hotel and felt so unsafe.

Oh and the best part: they refuse to offer any compensation claiming that the hotel is fine. Even ignoring the exterior, the only provided furniture is falling apart and is broken!

Do not stay here unless you want to risk your safety both inside and outside of the hotel. Do not bring children here. Do not book here, it was the worst hotel I’ve been to in my life and I’ve travelled the world.

The owner has rudely dismissed our request for even a partial refund, so I’d expect a rude reply on here too. Please take my advice.

The rooms are quite spartan, but it's expected given the very low price. The staff was very lovely and let us leave the bags there on our last day. The hotel is located in a bit of sketchy area of the town, unfortunately. It felt a bit unsafe when we arrived late at night.

The staff are amazing, the cleaner will eventually ignore the do not disturb sign, be careful where you step in the near vicinity of the hotel, drug users about. If you want local food for breakfast head left out of the hotel then left again, a little ways up is a great cafe.

I stayed at this hotel in Athens from 20th to 24th June for a business trip with my colleagues. If I had come for a holiday alone or with my child, I would have been terrified. This hotel is situated in Athens' drug trafficking area, making the experience absolutely dreadful.
Every evening, returning to the hotel was a horrifying ordeal. No one does anything about the addicts injecting heroin in every corner. The streets surrounding the hotel are filled with rubbish, syringes everywhere, and addicts sleeping beneath the hotel.
We informed the reception because we thought there was a dead girl in front of the hotel doors. Their response was to do nothing: no action was taken. We had to call the police ourselves. The hotel staff are friendly but completely indifferent to the dramatic and dangerous situation around the hotel.
We were not warned at all about what we would encounter. Terrible experience. Avoid this place at all costs.

Stayed there for one night only as we had to catch next flight to Fira. Didn’t expected much. Had to check in late night because of flight timings but it was smooth as they were aware about our flight timings. We didn’t booked the breakfast but decided in morning to have and it was not a problem for hotel. Overall, room space, air conditioning, accessibility and breakfast was good. Breakfast price was 6 Euros and the offering was good considering it was self service. (Boiled egg, bread cake, bread, butter, jam, cheese, coffee, tea, juice, salat (cucumber and tomatoes), greek curd, cornflakes, chocos etc.

Very nice apartment and a very friendly host. For example, the host brings you clean towels if you need some. The apartment is clean and the nearest metro station (Victora) is a 5 minute walk, so the location is also pretty decent.

Better than sleeping out on the streets. But absolutly not recommendable because of different reasons. Loud and annoying traffic all the day (no sleeping). And not the safest part of Athens especially in the evening and night.
It's the best place to stay for a few days. Near downtown. Nice apartment and big. I strongly  recommend you.
Very comfortable place with a lot of benefits like air conditioning and good help if needed, also fully equipped
The apartment isn't bad, otherwise I would never come to Athens again, we were robbed on arrival, we're not the only ones apparently.... Bad experience..
Horrible apartment...
Excellent apartment. Excellent professional approach. Comfortable house
Very beautiful apartments
Budget hotel in a good part of Athens.  I arrived really late (1am) and the staff was very helpful.  Bed and pillows were okay. Overall, the room was adequate and the cost was very reasonable.

I stayed at this hotel for six nights. For budget travellers just needing a place to sleep, this hotel is perfect. Although the bedsheets were stained and slightly torn, they were changed almost every night. The 24-hour reception is also a huge plus, especially given the less reputable location. They only let you in the building if you ring the bell and they check the security cameras constantly. Thankfully, we didn't encounter any issues during our stay. In short, if hygiene isn't your top priority and you only need a place to sleep, this hotel offers great value for your money.

DO NOT stay at this place. Hotel was in very poor condition. The bedsheets had holes in them, the toilet flush didn’t work and you’d have to raise the tank cover to flush. The main lights in the room didn’t work. The air conditioning was rusty and very old, causing a very unusual smell. There were wires coming out of the walls where the outlets were located and the towels were smelly.

The owner of the hotel was very fussy. We stayed 3 times, using the hotel as a hub to move around. Every time, he asked us to pay a €1,50 fee on top of the reservation and tried to charge us for a night we didn’t book. He woke us up at 6am due to his loud noise on the street. Any other place in Athens would be a better pick.

Absolutely God awful experience. Place reeked of cigarette smoke in the walls and mildew. It was so gross that both I and the friend that I traveled with slept on top of the comforter. We promptly checked out first thing in the morning to find somewhere new to shower and stay.
I travel alone and want a place I can sleep and shower. Not the height of luxury but clean and staff polite and helpful. Would use again.

Very bad location, it's a street of homelessness' that doing drugs all the night, they don't hurt but it's very bas situation to walk in such street to your hotel at night.

There is no service at all, because this hotel have nothing to offer for customer, the elevator is small, 80*80cm max, the rooms old, the bed is bad and not comfortable, the water leaking outside from the bathroom.

The front desk staff was kind and helpful, but it's not their fault that I gave this review to this hotel.

Incredible small shower,  i gurss 40 X 40 cm. Room is ok, central in Athens,  15-20 minutes walk to almost everything.
Don't think about asking the Hotel for transfer to/from airport.  They don't do it, even if it say so on their page. I asked via email twice,  no reply.  When we left i asked the reception but they didn't want to do it.  An Uber is 42 €, so if you are familiar with metro, you save a lot of money
Friendly staff,
Clean room
Good value for the money.

Even if we paid for it pretty cheap I think the hotel could have been in other conditions. Our main door was broken, same the toilet door, we were unable to close it to have privacy, bad smell, bedsheet not clear at all and bed was not comfortable.
About the neighborhood was a mess, full of yonkis, homeless and people naked and yelling. Not recommended even if it’s cheap.

What can I say? It's a very affordable place to stay for a 1-2 persons, who need a clean, safe, friendly place to sleep, to take a shower and keep their stuff. My room was clean, bright and cozy, had a small balcony, two single-beds, AC, TV, internet and a bathroom - honestly I didn't need anything else. Staff was very friendly and reception works 24/7. One thing that I've found missing (and I would be willing to pay extra for) was breakfast - on the first floor I even saw a small dining hall with a bar(?), so I guess it wouldn't be such a huge problem to add it to the offer :)

And regarding the neighborhood... Yes, it's extremely dirty and disgusting. Especially during the late hours there are dozens of homeless folks, drunks & junkies. I always tried to return to the hotel before dark..... But I wouldn't necessarily blame it on the hotel itself - the entire block between Omonia and Larissa Station looks like a southamerican slums... Blame city authorities for it!

The hotel was clean but no kettle, no hairdryer, no basic things to enjoy your stay .  The shower is so small that you keep banging the side panels .  If you want luxury or a drink in the morning ... Do not stay here.  As for the neighbourhood , we'll DO NOT TAKE YOUR KIDS HERE ! .  Junkies are shooting up openly in this area, and needles are left  on the pavement .  It is the  Bronx of Athens .  Avoid this area and book somewhere nicer .   On a plus side, the lady in the hotel reception is a lovely lady .

You get what you pay for. 200 eur for 6 days, you probably already know this is the cheapest option for a hotel in Athens. Location-wise it's magnificent. Next to the Omonia metro station with easy connection to Acropolis, Plaka, Monasteraki and other sites. I expected rooms to be a bit shabby, so that wasn't an issue for me. The cleaning ladies are super nice and have regularly maintained our room. Slightly hiccoughy WiFi but we have unlimited data in the EU so it wasn't an issue. Air-con worked flawlessly and it was included in the price.
Not bad, considering downtown location, you got everything, prices ok, no bed bugs, water was warm, bathroom clean, WI Fi slow though, but old school hotel is recommended...

Bed bugs. That’s the only criteria that I have in a hotel, that it not have bed bugs. I did get bit by bed bugs here and it’s very itchy & painful now, so I wish I’d stayed somewhere else.  Bed bugs aside, I encountered the following Claridge Hotel:

-there’s only sheets on the bed and an optional old blanket from WWII in the closet
-a tremendous amount of noise
-vagrants/addicts/refugees milling around the area (and pulling their pants down right out front pissing)
-a toilet that continuously ran and bathroom light bulbs that burnt out
-a paint job that hasn’t been touched up since the Berlin Wall was still up
-weak wifi that you have to compete with others to get to a website
-old key system where you leave the metal key with the front desk when you go out.

None of these things would have bothered me enough to cancel the stay except for the bed bugs. Of course I inspected the sheets when we arrived but I didn’t see any so I didn’t mind the 70 year old furniture and old peeling paint.

chances are, if you’re reading this review you already know this hotel is the cheapest option in athens that’s not a hostel, and comes with a private bathroom; you’ve seen the low star reviews & you’re trying to decide if you should chance it & stay here because maybe you think everyone else is a bunch of spoiled whiny complainers.
I’m giving them a generous 2 stars because
1. the staff is nice.  both the helpful front desk & the housekeepers that do clean
2. the convenient location near the train, restaurants, stores, museums, walking distance to Acropolis
3. the cheap price, lowest on the travel booking sites
My spouse permanently revoked my hotel booking privileges after this place.   He said “if you have respect for yourself don’t stay here”. But my kids age 16 & 26 were fine with it.

Location wise, it is very accessible, a few steps away from Omonoia Station. Not to expect inside the hotel since it’s a 2 star, good for sleeping. The Old Omonoia restaurant is just outside and the food was tasteful with a good size of serving.

The 3 receptionist throughout the day were all very kind people. The rooms were alright quality with it needing a fresh lick of paint and upgraded TVs. The air conditioning was old but I saw new boxes of air conditioning in the hallway so I think they will be upgraded. The location was also great with a lovely view of a beautiful fountain and it was right next to great restaurants.

The Penthouse is excellent and has everything for a nice stay in Athens. It's well located, close to the Acropolis and near the metro and subway station. It was only missing a microwave but other than that, the place was excellent and strongly recommended for couples or groups of 4. Sotiris, the manager is a great person as well and always helpful
Great view

Good value for money
It could be more careful and entertaining
The location is a little outside the tourist area.

great hotel with super friendly staff, all touristic sites are in walking distance. superb water pressure in the shower. breakfast buffet is large. maybe one little thing they can do is to place a ashtray outside for the smokers. great place to stay when you are in athens

The free breakfast is a joke. It’s disgusting. I will never come here again. This hotel cost too much per day. The food is horrible as far as the breakfast in the morning. too many people and it appears they have never eaten before, but it’s not Greek traditional food very a lot of bread products. The hard boiled eggs are over cooked as an example, very disappointed. I do not blame the help, They were overwhelmed and did the best they could with restocking and turning over tables. The rooftop upstairs bar great place. The views are amazing..

Great location  near to metro but average room quality for the price.
Has many rooms and kept clean.
The roof too has a distant view of the Acropolis. Many restaurants and tavernas nearby also the market is walking distance.

I really didn't like the room I was in, mostly because it didn't even remotely resemble what was advertised on the hotel's website. I was given a very poor room.
Apart from that, sheets were stained, I saw blood and who knows what else on those. The bottom sheet had little holes in it. I'd recommend changing sheets when they're no longer fit for use.
The carpet was full of hair and dirty.
The shower temperature would constantly change, which didn't add to the experience.
Pillows were uncomfortable.
Around 8.30 in the morning, the cleaning staff simply entered my room, like that.

Now, the staff is the only positive note on an otherwise very substandard stay. Staff was polite and helpful throughout the experience and I am very grateful for them.
One note: the rooftop restaurant is beautiful.

The rooms was tiny! Not what you expected regarding the pictures.
The carpet wasn't clean, there were clumps of hair in it. There was a pubic hair still on the toilet.
The bathroom was maybe 1.5m2 big. The shower was tiny tiny corner in a triangle shape to save space. So when you showered you touched that dirty shower curtain and I wished that I could take a shower all over again, but I would touch it again because it was so small (less than 0.5m2).

The breakfast was ok, which gives them 2 stars instead of 1.

Metro station is 50m from the hotel, which is very convenient. The rooms are reasonably new and spacious.
Overall a good option for travelers on a budget who are after a 3 stars comfort. I personally wouldn't consider this a 4 stars hotel.
The only disappointing bit is the buffet: the croissants are not fresh, and the coffee is really really bad. If you want to order coffee from the bar, you need to pay extra...

For the price we paid for our holiday it was worth it. The area is not the best but haven't had any trouble. The metro is 30 seconds away from the front door so that's brilliant. Room was very clean. Came with air conditioning that worked well and it was away from you so just leave it on to keep the room cool. Had its own fridge which was surprisingly spacious for a mini fridge, can get big bottle's in it. Wardrobe with a safe in it that you put your own code in and it's own bathroom with shower. Read a review that said it had low water pressure but not had any troubles. If you are on a medium budget then this is a very good choice. Tons of food places around and on 2 stops away from the party district with the 360 bar that has an amazing view of the Acropolis

Small rooms, but no hot water in the shower. Breakfast is okay, better than average. There are no fancy views from the rooms. Neighborhood may be a bit unattractive, but it is safe in a way if you take your precautions. Staff is super helpful and always kind. The hotel is really close to the metro station, and going to Akropolis would take you max 20 minutes. Good value for money.

I booked a trip to Greece in this hotel in 2020, canceled because of the pandemic obviously. Now in July 2023, I am still asking for my refund. I call every week, and send emails with all the information, and still, no one has got back to me with a real solution. Won't recommend a hotel that doesn't refund their customers correctly and keep their money.

We had a great stay here this week. Rooms were extremely clean and nicely decorated. The bed was one of the comfiest I have slept in!
The staff were very helpful and friendly. The food in the restaurant was tasty and not very expensive.
Our stay was quiet and comfortable, and the easy access to the metro meant we were just far enough away from the centre but could get there quickly if needed.
I would definitely stay here again. Thank you for a lovely experience.

Myrto was exceptional in our visit in Athens, I would recommend the stay here for anyone visiting Athens. She arranged our transit to the airport and recommended a mini breakfast box right before our flight back to the U.S. I love you Greece 🇬🇷 and warm welcomes you have my wife and I during our honeymoon.

We as a family checked in this so called 4 star hotel but were shocked to see the very average facilities.
Especially the reception people had poor communication skills and were  very rude .
The hotel room was the smallest size with the just minimal facilities and very little space available to move around.
The bath cubicle was just good enough for an average build person to stand and I hit the glass door more number of times than my heart rate while taking the bath . Water was leaking from the lower end to spoil the dry area.
We were shocked to know that even the first day water bottles were chargeable which we were not informed at the time of checking.
Even the additional coffee sachet was available @5£/ sachet ( such thing which we never experienced in the last 30 years).
We had rhe worst experience in this hotel & We strongly DON'T recommend this hotel to anyone.
Excellent option in Athens!!! Warm and kind staff. Clean room, outstanding view from the rooftop restaurant and plenty of options for breakfast! It's a 5/5 recommendation for your trip in Athens.

The location and service were nice but the rooms had such thin walls. We can hear people talking in the hallway and the room. We also heard vacuuming / cleaning from upstairs room at 7:30 am. Not sure if this is correct time to do this.

We stayed at the Polis Grand a few times during our trip in Greece. The rooms were comfortable, and the staff were all very friendly and helpful! I wanted to call special attention to Costas! He was there almost every time we were in the lobby, and he made a point to remember us and greet us with familiarity, making the check in / check out process feel a lot smoother! I really appreciated him!!

We ate breakfast at this hotel and it was delicious. Coffee excellent too! And the service was amazing! The restaurant was very busy with lots of patrons and the servers were still 'on top of it all. Highly recommend!

The hotel is passable, if you are not expecting any lux. The shower cabin is suitable only for skinny women, so probably you will need to take a shower outside of it. The reception guys are polite and friendly. For the money I paid I guess this is what one can get. It's near the historical centre of Athens (25 min. walking distance) but the area is dodgy, dirty and noisy. I would not recommend to anyone.

Booked a room here and stayed for a week in December 2016. Booked a premium room but when we got to the room it was awful. The door to the balcony was broken. Hair all over the bed. The floor and the bathroom were dirty. Area not great but we knew that as my boyfriend is greek and knows the area and close to the metro. When we went to ask to change rooms the lady at reception told us there were no other deluxe rooms available. She said she had one other room and we asked to go and see it. It was a lot smaller with no balcony but cleaner however the shower didn't stay in position which was annoying but it was an improvement. Our room wasn't cleaned while we were there and when we asked to be refunded the difference of the two rooms we were refused which I personally think is awful seen as it wasn't our fault that we moved in the first place.

The area where's the hotel isn't so sure, especially in the night. The guys at the reception were very nice and they speak a good English. But the room was kind dirty, we've found a wet old towel on the top the bed, just to say. Also the toilet wasn't brilliant in cleaning. The price was low, but also the quality. Nope.

The hotel is really worth staying in. The location is convenient enough. Our room was clean, cozy and had everything you need to have in a hotel room. The staff was very friendly and was always willing to help, if there were any questions. Highly recommend to choose the hotel for your stay to be pleasant.

author_name: ひきこもり。高橋正臣。Masaomi Eric Takahashi.
text:
The finest, cleanest and most comfortable place in the entire Greece! I strongly recommend this 5 stars hotel! It’s facing the main road so you’ll not feel too lonely.
I love the soft beautiful mattress and the fluffy pillows! It’s fantastic! Amazing price!
I just have to give this place a review of 5 Stars.
Google doesn’t condone to any reviews less than 5 Stars! Otherwise, they’ll send you a shrill and condescending message!

The owner of the property in the same day told me to go to the hotel Golden city to pick up the keys. I replied ok and I will arrive by 11pm.

Surprise. Once arrived to the hotel they did not leave anything. I called by phone 3 times. No answer at all.

They left me stranded at 11pm in the middle of a dangerous neighborhood. There were many people with drug addiction problems.

I highly recommend to look the others reviews carefully and advise to where to report them to avoid it happens again to future tourists.

It is dangerous really.

The rooms are comfortable and good, but there is a flaw in that they are not sound insulated, but given that they are sunny and good in terms of ventilation. The staff is nice and energetic, and the receptionist is nice and energetic, which makes customers excited to visit the hotel again

STAY AWAY!!!
Horrible horrible horrible place. Probably the worst place I've ever spent a night.
The neighborhood is full of junkies, we're talking zombies with their arms rotting off. Feels extremly unsafe, we we're even warned by our driver to come to this part of Athens.
The rooms look nothing like the pictures, the walls are made from thin plastic sheets. The window wouldn't close so the noise from the street was horrible. We had to plug our ears with toiletpaper go get a wink of sleep. The rooms felt dirty and our bed had a huge stain from what looked like piss in the mattress. It was a nightmare and I will never go back, if you have ANY other choice don't come here.

Failure all along the line. Photos published on the Internet don't square with what we found in this place, especially in our rooms. The first surprise was the lift being so small-sized that it cannot accommodate three people – maximally two of them and they have to be almost on top of one another. Secondly, a state of rooms. Beds are crooked, toilets haven't any islanded space to take a shower so after cleaning oneself up the whole room is being turned into small pool. Wi-Fi doesn't work fast as well, it lasts a minute to load one simple website. There were fortunately clean towels and sheets, tips about our room were transparently written and sent by the owner too. Additionally, it sits in the area of drug users and other dodgy men (two of them were sitting at the door of this place) so families with children and those who travel alone should give a wide berth to this place. One of few advantages, that tempted us to book here a room, were keen prices but – as this example shows – it's not everything. This place looks as if it was going to decline.

ERASMUS STUDENTS, DO NOT RENT HERE. PAY MORE ELSEWHERE THAN LIVE HERE. IT IS NOT WORTH IT

This place is fine if you don't mind living like in a zoo. If you are a tourist and don't plan to stay for a long time, the apartment is sufficient to spend the night. The wifi is okay at best, but it does not perform very well due to the location. Other living commodities are pretty decent. If you plan to rent the double room, I suggest renting elsewhere for the same price, there are plenty of options. If you plan to rent in the shared bedroom, I suggest you don't. There is usually at least one resident who is ether, like some sort of escaped convict, a very sensitive person, or just a creepy person, so he will get on your nerves. There is no table, no proper storage besides a small locker. Kichen is very small (it's like 4m²) so it can get pretty crowded. The rooms have an ongoing bed bug problem that is not really resolved (it is for all bedrooms). The landlord will tell you that there was a professional cleaning done to remove them, while in reality, it was just his sister and cousin doing just a pretty ordinary cleaning with, I guess little extra steaming. Another thing to note is that the landlord, while being polite and nice, is a bit incompetent. The location is close to the center, so that's a plus. Neibourhood is definitely "real Athens," so you should expect to see dirty streets with crackheads all around.
Overall, not recommended, especially for Erasmus students. Do yourself a favor and pay more for better. This is not worth it.

Aggressive landlord, mold everywhere, bad wifi, camera that can record audio and video in the living room. Forces you to join a WhatsApp group where he puts daily messages, and if you miss your cleaning day he tries to charge 15 euros, and publicly shaming you in the WhatsApp group. also, make sure to like this landlord because you will see his face A LOT. Comes checking in on the apartment and his tenants three times a week at least. Tried to get physically aggressive because he was called a bad name. The roommates were very friendly though, that's the only positive about this place.
Very nice apartment in central Athens, conveniently located near the Metaxourgeio metro. There is everything you need for a pleasant stay, and a well-equipped kitchen for eating in.

Location is great, close to central areas like Omonoia which did feel quite unsafe at night, but accommodation area felt safe. Dorm is okay, can get quite noisy due to doors, beds and common room. Cleaner comes once a day and is friendly. An alright place to stay and easy to get to.

Very nice place to stay for longer and shorter. With bathroom and balony. Ideal rooms for 2 people. Good location in city center. All what musy be see is at your walking distance. If you need go further subeay and tramways are very close. Recommend strongly.

At first, I was concerned. The area did not look great. But the room was decent, clean. The staff was wonderful. The breakfast was amazing and was 15 minute walk to basically everything. We did walk up to the central square at night as well as back from site seeing about 10pm. No issues for us. But still be aware of your surroundings.

We (17 people) stayed here for 7 nights in several rooms. The staff was very friendly and helpful. The rooms were clean and the breakfast was very basic but ok.

Unfortunately we had bed bugs in two rooms and 3 members of our group had bug bites all over there arms. I asked the staff to check for bed bugs and they promised they would search the room while we were sightseeing. I got a call from the manager that they couldnt find any bed bugs but changed all the towels and sheets. When we came back later in the day we wanted to make sure that there are no bugs anymore. We found them immediately after 2 minutes of searching. We then got 2 new rooms.

In another room (1st floor) it constantly smelled like someone smoked inside. My guess is that the cleaning staff smokes right next to the AC units and the smoke is sucked up and distributed to the rooms.

The neighborhood is full of drug addicts, prostitutes and dealers and there is a crazy smell of urin right in front of the hotel.

Would I recommend the hotel? If you are a male person and do not want to stay longer than 2 nights it might be ok. Don’t come here with children.

I enjoyed my time at the hotel, everything was so nice both the food and the service by Stelios, Stella, Sophia ,Vivi and Sissy. They were very polite,very welcoming and made me and my family feel like home. Especially in the kitchen I would like to thank Chrysa she was really kind ,humble and honest.(they really know how to treat customers like family)
Bed bugs! Stayed there for 4 nights first night my son was bit at least 20+ from bed bugs, he was given another room but zero compensation. Location is terrible, drug users all around the outside.

A basic hotel in central Athens. Nice location, v handy for all the touristy things. Staff lovely. Rooms very basic and bathroom v small. Go for the 4th floor as very quiet. AC which was great. Would stay again because of staff and location. A great restaurant nearby in Omania, great lamb chops!
Great and spacious apartment in a great location. It has everything you need in a quiet neighborhood. The host is wonderful, he explained everything to us and was there for all our questions.

My family and I had a great stay at this apartment. It was  and equpped with everything we needed. The host was very accommodating and quick to respond to any questions we had. We would definitely stay here again!

Booked this apartment online and the listing states free parking,not true.When I told the owner by email that I could not stay there because I had a rental car I had to park and there were tow away  signs, he sent me an automated email with paid garage instructions and areas in Athens to avoid.Also the area it is in is not the safest looking,people on drugs walking around and such.Then after I contacted my bank the owner called me directly by phone and asked me to get the money back I just disputed because he had bills to pay.
This is an update on this "host" through booking.com,this individual decided he did not like the review I left so decided to slander me on the response,Do Not Book Anything from this person,I won't name names but he has another listing on another booking site.Google has been notified of the harassing behavior.
Comfortable, clean and bright apartment, located next to the metro and train station, with central attractions within walking distance.

Ugly, dirty place.
1. Cockroaches (karaluchy!!!) - if you never leaves at slums it's a good place.
2. Air condition don't work (It was very old making biger noise than cold and installed only in bedroom). At living room was two dirty fans.
3. Kitchen was dirty and has only one spoon.
4. Owner makes many rules wich was send before camig eg. disable hot water because electrisity was broken.
5. Entrance to the building stick and is totally broken.
6. We ask owner to cancel rent after one night because we disgusted cockroaches (karaluchy) but he say apartment is ok and payment policy ... then I say then I write all at comment.

All disadwantages was at other opinion.
Only nice photos! Congratulations to photographer. God job.
The prices are expensive, the hotel is very bad and unclean, the service is very bad, and everything there is very disgusting. I feel sorry that I had this experience.

The worst Hotel, the location is perfect but it’s the only good thing about this place.
The worst receptionist that I met in my entire life, they won’t even store your luggage at the check out for a couple of hours. Everyone is rude and they won’t help you.
We even paid 5€ for the air conditioning and they didn’t give us the remote control, without any refund.
They didn’t change sheets and towels.
So please spend a little bit of more money to have a better and pleasant journey.

Without any booking we successfully
managed to find a room only in this hotel and were pretty happy.
They don't provide breakfast or any service, the  hotel is located in the nosy, dirty area, there's a permanent smoke smell in the room but we weren't going to get upset. Clean sheets, warm water are enough.
But the personel... Really that grumpy, grumbling old man at the reception... He was unpleased, impolite and didn't let us leave two small bags for 2 hours. Disgusting impression.

According to me it was not a hotel. It was like a store room. Their toilet condition was very low. The washroom was very bad. Water falls from the ceiling of the washroom. It is very annoying. There was not enough space to take a shower. Inside the hotel, next to the stairs, inside the room, the dirty smell is very uncontrollable. Their behavior was very bad. The behavior of the owner and the staff and the responsible people were very bad. In a word, everything was at a low level. I didn't like it at all.

If someone comes to visit with family or friends, I think they should not go to the hotel. It will only harass you.👎👎👎👎👎

It's just ok. Nothing special it's a place to sleep. I'm not going to trash this place. I was only there for a few hours. But it was my least favorite of the 3 I stayed in. It did have a balcony. A decent bathroom and the room was rather large in my opinion. And the internet worked.

0 stars
We left our luggage here for 2 days and half our belongings were STOLEN!!!
Sketchy area and sketchy people around it. They said they “locked up” our suitcase and showed me where, so that only means staff was who rummaged through our suitcase and stole my belongings.
They stole my shoes, my hair brush, my clothes, my husbands clothes, our personal items, ALL of our Athens souvenirs. The list goes on and on. The property manager said the cameras do not show where the suitcase was stored and refused a refund through third party and never responded to my whats app request for one. I am disgusted and feel so violated by this. I would never stay here again or recommend some to. To think I’m extorting them for a free stay is insane. I want to warn others to beware.

Faisal was great from making sure we made it to giving tips on where to go and how to get there. The metro is like a 5 minute walk. And the blue line for hop on and off is 1.3 kilometers we walked in 18 minutes with no issues. I forgot to let them know I had rented a vehicle and Faisal message me every day if we could use the space or not since I didn't recerve in advance, but not to worry I was able to park at front safely I spend 4 days in Greece and when I come back I will definitely get back to Alekos to stay.

After initial misunderstanding as to the apartments we reserved and not being able to find our reservations, the host cleared things up with my sister and the owner and let us stay in some other apartments and then transferred us to the apartments we booked, the next day.

Clean apartments that come with a bottle of water in the fridge, a table and chairs, microwave, hot plates to cook, couch,TV, clean towels, and it's air conditioned. It's also in a quiet neighborhood which is good. They empty the trash cans almost every day. The host let us washed our clothes as well which was nice, since my husband didn't being enough clothes.

The reason I am ranking it the way it is, is because the metro is 10 to 20 minutes away, depending on the speed of your party members and not out front like some of the other hotels / apartments. Depending on the taxi person they might not want to come get you from these apartments. We also came during their Easter so when we arrived most everything was closed for two days so we ended up going to Plaka to get food so have no food recommendations
Receptionist very nice. Will advise you on something. Rooms on the 4th/5th/6th floor very large. Rooms below are smaller but also cool. I recommend this place to stay to everyone!
It is in a residential area but near the metro. It has an elevator. The beds are firm but I prefer firm beds especially when traveling. Market and restaurants are within walking distance.

This Hostel is nice and cheap. Most important: Beds, toilets and rooms are very clean. They pay much attention to that. Common space is basic but you can socialize if you want. Staff is helpful too. Kitchen is missing many things. As about the location I really like it. You can get cheap food and drinks around. Also the metro station is less than a minute away.
I would definitely come back for all the reasons above.

I don’t think I could have asked for a better experience especially considering the price. The staff are incredible, I felt safe, met the loveliest people and made memories I’ll never forget. The rooms are simple and clean. There’s a communal space downstairs where I worked when needed (and the wifi works perfectly). I walked to Syntagma and Plaka every day with no trouble, while staying vigilant as a woman traveling alone. This is a student area and it’s nice to see them hanging out at cafes and bars close by. Zorbas Hostel has a special place in my heart.

Been in the country for almost 2 months and stayed in 7 different hostels. I can honestly say Zorbas ranks #1 out of all of them. The A/C works wonderfully, the rooms and beds are comfortable, and there’s always a staff member awake if you need anything. They are very kind and helpful. Every other hostel I’ve stayed in has major drawbacks that keep me from feeling totally comfortable but not Zorbas.

Pro tip: spend the extra few euros and upgrade to the 4 person room. Trust me on this. My first stay was in the 6-person room and you share the upstairs bathroom with a lot of other people (there’s more downstairs though) and there’s only one charging strip. The 4 person rooms have charging outlets by the beds, they’re cozier, and have a bathroom.

I like this place. Rooms are clean, bed comfortable and sheets are fresh. People very helpful and sweet. Good price. It is quiet place and I had good sleep. The only issue was shower glass that was only on the one side of shower and it is impossible to shower without making wet everything around.

Great reception desk team, pretty clean place and close to Victoria station. Definitely recommend if you are looking for an affordable hostel in downtown Athens. Just be wary that the area is somewhat considered unsafe, so keeping an eye to your belongings while around isn't a bad idea.

I do NOT recommend!!! My friend and I just stayed at this hotel for 4 nights. First of all, it’s more like a 2-star, not a 4-star. Our room was not great. We arrived and the room had no hand soap or shower soap and no towels! One of the sink stoppers was missing. The shower is completely open and floods the floor when you shower. The lighting in the bathroom was terrible. The staff was helpful, however, I have a big complaint. They are extremely unresponsive via email and phone. Before we left the US my friend tried sending emails 4 times asking about our reservation because we had 2 separate reservations but wanted to combine them. She also sent a separate email with another question. 4 emails and no answers to any. They don’t respond and it’s impossible to call them because you either get a number that’s not in service or it just rings and no one picks it up. Surprising because there was always someone at the front desk. I accidentally left a notebook at the front desk and tried NUMEROUS times to contact them explaining the issue but to no avail. I was happy to pay the postage costs. There was an older, lovely woman at the front desk who was kind enough to give me ice for my swollen ankle and hand from a bike accident. I thought surely she would answer but she did not😔Another comment, the hotel is in a getto area. The first night we walked to dinner and saw a number of •riot police”!!! Quite unsettling to say the least. We were told the riots have stopped and other the police presence. Run down neighborhood, graffiti and trash everywhere, sidewalks are broken and falling apart. Had we known the area was so bad we would have picked a different hotel. My hope is this review will wake them up and give others a heads up as to what they are getting into. The pool looks much better in photos and the other rooms as well. I wouldn’t go in that pool. I’m also hopeful they will read my email and respond about getting my book back. I don’t recommend staying here!

It’s not worth it!!!!!! You might think this hotel is a good deal, I promise it’s not. The location sucks. It’s in a creepy neighborhood with junkies and prostitutes. It does not feel safe at all. The hotel is awful. The swimming pools look nice on the pictures, they are not usable and disgusting. The rooms are old and everything is falling apart. The are no bed sheets, the safe is not safe. Someone broke into our room at 2AM in the middle of the night. How did they even give our key to someone else???? We are pretty sure that there were prostitutes in the lobby and in the rooms. We changed hotel and didn’t even complete our stay. This hotel should not be recommended by any sites!!!! There are so many better options in Athens.

Affordable hotel with decent, clean air conditioned rooms and good service. However be mindful that while it is close to some great sites and the metro, it is in a rather rough part of the city. (Though you walk for 5-10min and things get much better) I was randomly clawed on my arm by a homeless woman while walking  and there are vagabonds loitering nearby. This place pales in comparison to American ghettos and overall not too bad. Overall,  I would say this is a great option for groups on a budget but I would be a bit weary as a solo traveller staying here.
Looks good in photos, but is a different thing in person, not very clean rooms, bad management and is located in one of the worst area of Athens,

Not good location, with a lot of homeless around!
The room's clean is not that good
But the staff were very kind.

Lovely lovely!!! Employees were so kind and helpful, the hotel is just two stops away from the center so it's less pricey and quieter but not too far at all. The room itself is sweet and I had a nice balcony to sit on and get some fresh air. Very happy with my stay here! :)
The staff was really kind and helpful, the neighbourhood is silent and easily accessible by metro.

The room is very comfortable with excellent water pressure and plenty of hot water.  It would appear that they have just done a remodeling.
I had to check in late, and the desk was very professional and understanding.  He was also helpful in pointing out places of interest in Athens.  The location is on a quiet street and off the beaten path.  I stayed here 4 nights and will would return.

Very clean and nice rooms.
No elevator. Very quiet place.
All the staff of the hotel are very kind and thay give you hot and friendly feelings.
It is very near to the underground train that goes directly to the airport and the city center.

Good service,its close from train station
and all Market,im thankeful 🙏✅

The hotel is ok for a night or two. It's not the cleanest I've ever been and the rooms are in need of general repairs, but it's cheap and situated in the heart of the city. Staff was friendly and helpful. To those who complained about weird people in the streets: Yes, they're there. As in every big metropolis. They mind their own business - mind yours and there's no problem.

We stayed here two nights. The location is great next to the bus stop and also walkable to many restaurants and attractions. 24 hour reception was a huge plus. Breakfast was included but we didn’t get a chance to have it because it only runs from 8- 10am and we were out every day before then

**Review of Diros Hotel (3 stars)**

Diros Hotel, located in a central but slightly unsafe area, offers an acceptable stay experience, though with some notable drawbacks. The hotel's structure appears somewhat rundown and poorly maintained, leaving much to be desired in terms of overall cleanliness.

The rooms, while seemingly clean, are nothing exceptional and reflect what one would expect from a three-star hotel. A critical point is the lack of daily bed linen changes, which could negatively impact guest comfort during longer stays.

We stayed for two days, and overall, the experience was okay. However, we strongly recommend exercising caution when moving around the hotel area, especially in the evening, due to the perceived danger of the location.

In summary, Diros Hotel is an acceptable option for short stays but has significant room for improvement.

The Diros hotel was just slightly out of the center but this didn’t feel bothersome - 30mins from Diros hotel to Plaka. It just means you get to get your steps in!
The room was as described (although we didn’t have any kind of view). Air conditioning was functioning which was crucial in this hot weather! The hosts were very helpful and nice!
The bathroom was a a little small, and the shower head didn’t hook onto the wall so we could only have a handheld shower. The water did get really cold though which was great!
The final thing to say is that the walls are super thin so you can hear people coughing who are in the room next to you!

Overall, it’s good value for money, clean, and well located!

There are bedbugs in the hotel. We were a group and most of them were bitten which came out after 1-2 days. We had all stayed for over a week in another place before and there were no problems. We stayed in the Hotel on Diros for one night and the next day there were already bite marks. Scandal.

Excellent location, at the Omonia Square. It was very convenient for us, as it was the pick-up and drop point for all tour buses. the rooms were very spacious and house keeping kept it neat and clean. The only disappointment was the Balcony was not accessible, as it was a common balcony covering many rooms. Breakfast was standard continental and starts at 6.30 am which was very convenient.

We were a group of seven people. We had an amazing stay at this hotel very recently. Rooms, though a bit small for our liking, are very comfortable. All the necessary amenities are provided. Location wise, it’s very accessible to lots of interesting places. Staff is very friendly and extremely helpful. They even gave us packed breakfast, when we needed to leave early in the morning.

We spent only one night at the Athens Tiare Hotel, but we loved it. The rooms are clean and spacious and the staff is kind, helpful and accommodating. We were a party of 8, and two members are elderly. Our bathroom had a steep bathtub to step into while the others had walk in showers (unbeknownst to us) which we felt was a kindness to the elderly members in our party. We ate dinner and breakfast at the hotel restaurant. The meals were fresh and delicious and we just overall loved our time here - even though it was short. Highly recommend staying here!

Great staff service let down by a hotel that overall needs a refurbishment. All staff were very helpful, friendly, considerate and understanding. Our room was OK. A good strong shower.  A shame we couldn't access the balcony. The decor and beds simply weren't upto a 4*standard. Location was fine and rooms were surprisingly quiet considering how busy the area was.

They offered me a room on the first floor but I didn't accept due to the traffic noise and asked for alternative room. Then they offered me another room with twin beds but I didn't accept it either because I was willing to stay in a king room. Then the receptionist canceled my reservation without issuing a refund! They got my money and they said go away. Stay away from this scam hotel.

We stayed at this hotel last week. Overall, the stay was good. The location was decent, we walked everywhere in Athens. I would not recommend walking out late at night alone, seems a bit sketchy. We had issues with our air conditioning not working but they responded quickly to resolve this. You are unable to turn the air lower than 22 degrees C which is a bit warmer than I would like. The breakfast was pretty good, they give you many options. Last thing, they advertise some of the rooms having a view of the Acropolis, however, there is not a view at all from any room. Overall, not bad.

Read very carefully. I can assure you this is 100% true what I’m about to write:
We arrived around 20:00. there were a lot of  homeless people and addicted people walking around the location of the hotel. 2 policemen were also doing a search on them.
We saw also 2 sex workers waiting in the street in front of the hotel (yes, we are 100% sure that they were prostitutes).

We enter the hotel because we were scared to stay outside any longer. The staff gives us the keys and we go up.

The room : the room was big, but the furniture was cheap (this should be a 5 star hotel) , the floor very ruined, the air conditioning BROKEN, and one window was impossible to close .

We went out to eat, but all the activities around had obviously very bad hygiene problems.

I rarely write negative ratings. But I hope that no more people have to experience this.

Don’t trust the positive reviews! They are made intentionally by other people that have interest in keeping this hotel going!

Dirty pillow cases and bedsheets. Pls see pictures. Looks like dirt stains. The hotel seems to be new but the housekeeping needs to do a better job. I found breadcrumbs next to the sofa and hair all over the floor.

I booked online and came to the hotel and they couldn't find my booking, which is fine but the lady at the reception was rude. The whole tone of voice is impolite. She asked for 1-2 minutes and only after 25 minutes the situation was resolved.
There was another tall gentleman in the lobby who offered me water while I was waiting and he clearly knew about customer service and the basics of smiling.

The hotel is at a busy intersection. I stayed on Saturday and the grocery store across the hotel kept playing loud music until 2:30am. I called the reception to notify them of LOUD music and the receptionist never got back to me. He told me he will see what he can do and get back to me but never did. I went down to the shopkeeper to tell him to turn off the music and to stop causing Boise disturbance at 2:30am. They claim to have double glazed windows and sound proofing but I could hear motorbikes passing by all night and some people speaking outside on the streets. I couldn't sleep at all.

The toilet is so small that I could barely fit inside, really small. The AC doesn't work and has some sort of a timer on and it turns off on its own.

Overall it was a bad experience. Better pay 20 euro more and have a proper accomodation. This was not good.
We loved this hotel. Great location.  Beautiful,  staff was helpful, and the breakfast was included and delicious.

The room I stayed at first floor was spacious but the balcony was closed, the walls were dirty and furniture dusty.  The choices and food quality for dinner and breakfast are poor for a 4 star hotel category. And some kitchen staff was rude, telling me that some dinner dishes were only for certain group of guests in the hotel and that my group shoul have come to dinner at later time that day.
The area around the hotel is creepy with junkies at every corner at any hour of the day.

Nice and clean room with refrigerator,TV, water heater for coffee & tea, and a very kind welcome @ arrival.
Excellent location,close to a Metro station and a nice Bakery & Cafè that opens @ 6am. Absolutely recommended place, very good value for the money.

The building is old, but the rooms are new and comfortable. Clean and with a great air conditioning (it was 35oC when we went there late June). It is located on a quiet street that is really close to everything, it is on the edge of a not so nice part of the street, but just don’t go left when leaving the hotel ;)
Walking distance from the main square and from there to Acropolis and others.

An ideal place to visit Athens.
