Clean rooms with en-suite bathrooms, cleaned every day, friendly and helpful hotel staff and a location in the very centre are undoubted advantages of this place. Add to this the delicious patisserie-bakery just around the corner, where you can have a good breakfast, and you have a recipe for the perfect place to visit Athens.

Next to metro station, just one stop to Monastraki. Easily transport to everywhere, rooms are clean, has balcony you can smoke , netflix and youtube are installed on tv. Refrigerator and AC works well.Thank you for  friendly welcome and smiley faces.
really bad experience. Thay are really rude, specially when you ask for proper sheets. the toilets smell horrible... ! Would not recommed, even if it's cheap it's not worth it.

First of all the location is incorrect if you using phone maps it takes you somewhere else and we really struggled getting there. We searched for Eurobank which was next door and that let us reach there finally…
The service was very basic (we were charged a tourist tax fee of 1.50 euro which is ok but I never been charged that before anywhere else).
The bedrooms were fairly clean but chairs had stains and mattresses had also (please check the underside) our mattress looked really dirty almost like someone did pee or similar things on it. We left 2 days ago and when we reach home now we had some bugs coming out of our bags and clothes 😢 myself and my daughter are covered in spots not sure what is this I’m hoping from the bottom of my heart that it is not bed bugs 😢 I’m terrified and now washing everything in 90C plus taking everything to tumble dry.
I would not visit again sorry.

Would not stay here again. The hotel is quite rundown and while I am not one to complain normally, the shower did not have hot water the first 2 days of my stay and would go from hot to freezing when it did finally work. I have also never slept on such an uncomfortable bed (even hostels have been better). Lastly, THIS AREA IS NOT SAFE. I did not realize when booking, but this is the worst neighbourhood in Athens to stay in and you will not feel safe walking around. Please be careful and book at a better place

This hotel is fantastic. I had an amazing sleep. The room and bed are comfortable. Great AC. There were no bed bugs as I read in other reviews. The area is close to the city centre. Hondos centre has a great breakfast area with views of the Acropolis. Just 100 metres up the road. I would come back again

Extremely loud place. Noise from the street I could live with but the noise made by everyone was unbelievable. Slammed doors, thin walls, hearing people through the vents. Every door in this building was designed to be slammed. The location was ok, 25 min to the acropolis. Also pictures look very nice on the site but they are not showing the truth.
Spartan facility at cost effective price. Clean. Cordial staff. Great location. Good wifi. Refrigerator in room. Would stay here again while touring Athens.

Upon arrival the receptionist was nice and changed my room because it wasn't like the pictures. After that I had a bad experience. I found bed bugs the next day and they bit me and I informed the reception at 3am that day. They told me we could change the room, but I couldn't move all my stuff at 3am and I couldn't imagine that I would wake up the next day to a disaster. I slept and woke up with more bites on my body and called the reception to get medicine. He told me to go to the hospital and the hotel wouldn't pay anything because they have a bigger problem than me. They will come to clean the room from bed bugs and it doesn't matter who I am or what body the bed bugs bit. I went to the hospital and wasted my whole day there. I came back late to check out and he told me that I would have to leave the room in half an hour. I told him not to leave the room until I get to the airport. Bad experience and they won't handle my problem properly. I would not recommend anyone to visit this place.

When I first entered I was like, did I enter the wrong hotel?

This cannot be a 1-star hotel

Rooms cleaned with steam, friendly staff, location close to everything in Athens, and at an unbelievable price!

Staying here you get a 4 star experience with a zero missing in the price! Totally recommended!

The room was dirty and there were a lot of bedbugs and they attacked all my body, the furniture was in bad conditions.
Really I spent three bad nights there,
An absolute garbage dump of a hotel. Wifi in rooms doesn’t work. Paper thin windows with the loud roar of traffic 24/7. Junkies shooting up right outside. Rude staff. Avoid at all costs.

Not the very best hotel I've stayed in but for under £30 for the night it was fine.
Very basic but clean and tidy.

Super hotel, clean and staff are very friendly. Ask for an iron and it's there in minutes. Location is great minutes from everything. Only downside is that Athens, like any major city has a problem with homeless & drug abuse, which is largely situated in the hotel area. My advice would be it's great for walking in the day. However, to return to the hotel at night get a taxi

average hotel doesn't deserve 4 stars
good stuff. clean room.
breakfast is mediocre.
service is below average as the standard and executive rooms don't have kettle!!! and there wasn't water refill after the 1st day in addition to the lack of slippers!!! how come 4 stars Hotel lacks that much???
the location isn't interesting and the surrounding area is filled with homeless and durg addicts but there is also police in the area

The staff were really lovely and helpful. I was a bit disappointed with the vegan breakfast options (there was fruit, peanut butter toast and muesli only) I thought there would be more options as they said they could cater to different dietary needs on their website.

Maybe adding some vegan sausages or something to the menu, and having vegan margarine to go on toast could help with that (maybe?)

Overall the hotel was nice

My stay at the hotel was not good, except for the night staff and the cleaning lady, who were nice and very professional. The breakfast was very simple, and the coffee was not good. The room was very noisy because of the cars, and the area did not feel safe. I don’t recommend this hotel.

Our room was clean , with a big balcon and had everything you need. The area around the hotel is not the best, but i will give 5 stars because the lady on the reception was really thoughtful and friendly.

Hello we have booked a hotel in Athens, the one above send by lien.A our great surprise the owner has changed our apartment.
The latter "imposed" at the last minute, being better said, because very recent, happens to be a cellar transformed into an apartment.
Finishes are not realized.
Our biggest worry is the smell of stubborn cellar moisture.
Breathing problem, scratching eyes and throats.
We had booked the first apartment with the washing machine and TV, service not available in the replacement apartment.
No view to the balcony,tiny w.c and shower(also smells nasty...),unfair deal (booking32€,we pay50,50€!!!),no water for welcoming.first time in my life,happened something like that...never again...
Very cool
Cozy apartment in quite area. Room is spacious, clean and tidy.There is nice balcony. Good for a group of 3-4 people.
Top

The apartment is in a neighborhood 1.5 km from the center in a somewhat abandoned area. Around there is a cheaper gastronomic variety but they are all Kebabs. The apartment is quite neglected. When we arrived there was no faucet and it took them 2 days to fix it. When we arrived we had met at 3:00 p.m. and they didn't arrive until 5:30 p.m., leaving us on the street with our luggage. It took the man more than two hours to come from when we called him. The sheets had snot, to say the least... Disgusting. Only one set of towels for the entire stay without the possibility of cleaning them because there is no washing machine. Thank God the elevator worked... But the noise inside the apartment is horrible, thank God it is not used much.
We asked for the possibility of a transfer to the airport and they wanted to steal 15 euros more from us than the taxi drivers...
Excelente local para descasar e praia. Bom restairante e instalações. Os donos de alguns apartamentos são a nódoa. Acham que são fomos de tudo...
The place was spectacular, very comfortable and close to everything, but the host was short on information, which is why I didn't give it five stars.
Very nice room, completely new and very well equipped. High comfort and great balcony. Neighbourhood is not the nicest but 10 minutes walking distance from the city center.

Had a good 4 night stay. Clean room, with kettle, fridge and microwave. Always someone at the front desk to give security and a welcome. The walk from the city centre is not great,  but various options, so choose one that feels more secure at night. But it's just 10 minutes away. The only slight negative would be we could hear every sound made in the room next to us. Good light and sound proofing from outside.

The apartment was just spotless clean! The receptionist was soooo helpful i really enjoyed my short stay in the penthouse apartment. Would recommend anytime. I also really liked the pillows. So comfortable. AC works like a charm too cooled down the room in minutes. Very nice if you travel alone or even for two.

Amazing location to explore Athenes. Literally 500m walk to all major attractions. It's a China Town district with surroundings that may seem somewhat dirty. But that is of no concern. The environment is safe and friendly. The street itself is somewhat busy, can hear noises during the night. Otherwise, fantastic value for money.

Very bad experience in this hotel.
- First time in Grece I felt unsecure in a neighbourhood (I saw 2 people with SYRINGE on my way from the metro...).
- I got bitten by BEDBUGD.
- The hotel manager, beside being just cordial, refuse to acknowledge any wrong, even with multiple pictures and receipt for products as proof.
You cannot  find a worst, most stressful return home
I would recommand this hotel to nobody.

Staff were very nice, the rooms were well-updated, the breakfast quite complete, and the roof-top bar and welcome drink (for Accor members) quite pleasant in the evening.
The only issue is when we decided to walk back from Monastiraki at night, which took us through a couple dodgy streets, but nothing of concern.  Overall the location of the property is average.

overall, it was a mixed experience. The room was spacious, clean, and comfortable, and the service from the staff was excellent. However, the breakfast was a major letdown. The food was not tasty at all, and unfortunately, I even got sick after eating it. Honestly, I would have preferred to skip breakfast altogether. While the hotel is a bit far from the main attractions, the quality of the room and service made the stay pleasant. If you’re not too concerned about breakfast, it’s a solid option.

I had a good stay in this hotel, also because I got upgraded to a suite. This room was slightly outdated, but spacious, clean and the bed was good. The breakfast buffet was very nice with a great choice of products. The view from the rooftop pool and terrace is beautiful, with the Acropolis in the distance. The hotel staff is efficient but not overly friendly. The area around the hotel is somewhat sketchy, so we took taxis to move around (which are rather cheap).

Spent 3 days at the hotel after two weeks in Cyclades. Everything was great. The staff were friendly and knowledgeable. Due to the heatwave, we spent all our time in the hotel and enjoyed our time in the rooftop terrace and pool. A great place to prepare for the journey home.
We will definitely be back!

I had the best experience in Novotel! Everything was great! The stuff was unbelievable good in their jobs!The guys in the pool were really helpful and full of joy! They make the start of my day wonderful and really upgrade the whole experience! I will totally visit again this hotel

I would have given this hotel 5 stars if it were not for the welcome we received upon our arrival.  Our overseas flight landed at 8 am and we were at this hotel by 10.  We could not get a room until 2 pm!  This was after a long day of traveling.  After speaking with other hotel guests, this seems to be a persistent problem.  Suggestions, perhaps?  Even if you could spare a glass of water and a cool place to sit would have been nice. Maybe a free breakfast or cup of coffee/tea?  I appreciate that the rooms need cleaned, but I'm sure there were some  people that left early.  An early arrival room for weary travelers would be appreciated.  Also, Theo must have been tired of dealing with weary travelers as he wasn't very pleasant.
The positives - rooms are extremely nice with a Jacuzzi tub and a shower with body sprays. Beds are super comfortable.  Rooms were clean and nicely prepared.
Rooftop pool, bar, and dining area are just beautiful to relax after a day of sightseeing.
All other staff were more than helpful and very kind.

This was our first time staying at this hotel chain and we were pleasantly surprised. The customer service was top notch, every single person was attentive and went out of their way to assist us. The rooms were very nice and the beds were very comfy. The roof that bar was really nice and had great views. I personally wouldn’t stay at this hotel again due to the location. I would stay at this chain but I’m sure there’s far greater locations. The area surrounding the hotel has lots of homeless and druggies just lingering and sleeping in the streets.

Loved this hotel. Very convenient for walks to the city centre and historical sites in Athens. The sky lounge is very popular for an afternoon swim and sun. It becomes magical at night for a romantic dinner with a view of the Acropolis.

We enjoyed our stay very much. Staff was very polite. Our room was very clean and comfortable. We liked the overhead shower but could not get our handheld to work. Housekeeping came twice a day. A nice tray of cookies and dried fruit awaited us when we arrived. We brought our own universal plug adapter but were disappointed that there were no USB ports in the room or outlet in the bathroom.
The pool was wonderful! Hot tub looked inviting but water was not warm. Food and drinks on the rooftop we're good but menu options were limited.
Breakfast buffet was Amazing!

I had a great stay in Athens—my second visit after 11 years, and the experience was just as good as I remembered.

Location: While the hotel is slightly removed from the city’s main attractions, it's only a 15-minute walk or a quick 5-minute metro ride to the lively parts of town. The Omonia metro station is conveniently close by.

Room: The room was well-furnished and spacious enough to accommodate an extra bed for our child. The bathroom was also quite large, though the hydro-massage shower felt outdated and a bit tricky to navigate as it’s quite high to climb. However, the cleanliness was excellent throughout.

Breakfast: The breakfast offered a good variety of hot and cold dishes—perfect if you want to avoid the hassle of searching for breakfast in the city early in the morning. The only downside was the coffee, which, like in other Melia hotels, wasn’t great. I’m still not sure if it’s the machine or the coffee beans they use.

Rooftop Pool: One of the highlights was the rooftop pool, a rarity in Athens. Although it’s small, it’s a refreshing escape on those hot days, with the added bonus of a bar. However, I wasn’t thrilled that non-hotel guests can access the pool for a fee, as it made the area feel a bit less secure.

Excellent customer service. This hotel has the most amazing staff ever. They deal with any issues you may have swiftly, always with a smile and genuine concern for you. I really would like the reception staff, the breakfast staff and the rooftop staff to be congratulated for their professionalism. The rooms are comfortable and the bathrooms are massive. The only negative is the bed which is two small beds pushed together like in most hotels in Europe. I am a big guy so I would always end up in between the two beds :-(  The breakfast was really good and varied. Lots of choices! Location is perfect.

Went there on my birthday and the front staff has upgraded me to a premium room (park view) with free breakfast. The pool, restaurant are great and you are free to use the gym and sauna after checkout. I would say this is definitely a highlight or my birthday trip and the staff was really friendly - also the apple pie in the restaurant was so good!

We loved our stay here, everything was perfect! The rooms are super clean and nice, the hotel is gorgeous, the location fantastic, the rooftop bar amazing, the breakfast is a nice buffet, and the staff is wonderful! Everyone we interacted with was so pleasant! I actually have family in Athens so, whenever I'm back, I'll be staying at this hotel for sure! Everything was lovely!

A good hotel with a beautiful outside rooftop.

The staff is superb, polite and with a sense of humor.

Rooftop restaurant had some nice finger foods and snacks.

The hotel itself is showing its age, though. One of the elevators is not working. The AC in the room is very noisy. The decor in the room is kind of dark and outdated.

Linens and towels were clean.
Good coffee and tea selection.
Great view from our room.

Might not be worth the price for some as it is expensive.

Overall, not a bad hotel but also has areas to be improved.

Nice hotel, very helpful staff and good location opposite the Park.  Had a superior room with a view of the acropolis from the large balcony.  Unfortunately let down by the south facing balcony which was exposed to the sun all day and way too hot to use.  No umbrellas or awnings or any shade whatsoever.  The couch exposed to the sun was severely damaged.  Wouldn't stay in this room again.

The hotel is a nice budget place, near the centre for a reasonable price. Breakfast is ok, but the same every day, enough not to be hungry. The good thing you do not to be  on time, all food is brought even if you are after  the time. The staff is  friendly and really nice, speak a lot of languages. The neighborhood is disgusting, be aware of your suitcases, immigrants and drug addicts may steal if you do not pay attention.

The staff in the hotel is good and the morning breakfast offered is quite tasty as well. The main attraction about the hotel is it's proximity to all major historical and archeological tourist attraction in Athens and it's competitive pricing. The locality around the hotel though feels a little unsafe, especially late at night. The room we used was basic and the decor was old, but everything was fully functional. Do not expect any view kind of good view as most of the rooms face other buildings or the congested street

In the winter (I can't speak for the summer), this is an excellent budget hotel.  At an exceedingly low price, I got friendly service, an ample breakfast, a decent room, a hot shower, and a location close to downtown.  The neighborhood is not upscale, but it is quite decent--far better than some comments would have you believe.  I forgot pants and 2 shirts in the closet when I left for Crete.  When I came for a second stay 3 weeks later, the staff, who had kept them not knowing that I would return, delivered them to my new room.  In the budget category, this hotel is hard to beat.

The staff is amazing. Some of the best people we have ever met. Main problem we had was that the a.c isn't working great in some rooms. Also it's not in the greatest of neighborhoods. We enjoyed this place despite those facts.

Decent enough. Breakfast includes eggs, sausages, cheese, cereal, basic items. Bedroom was a bit spare and only had one visible outlet halfway up the wall which was annoying. The hotel is located in a rather dismal looking area, but the main square and metro are less than ten minutes away.

I have led multiple groups overseas. This is a fantastic hotel. Centrally located. Excellent food rooms are clean and very comfortable. The rooftop restaurant and bar has an unbelievable view of the Acropolis. Highly recommend.

Even though the hotel is an older one, service was excellent - the staff was very attentive and friendly. Breakfast and coffee were excellent and the rooftop view was a fabulous one. The elevators are busy in the morning as can be the breakfast bar at around 8 so get to there early!

Reasonably good hotel overall despite some old outlooks and slowness — your elevator in the peak morning hours can easily take 5 minutes to come. The staffs are helpful and friendly and the breakfast area is simply fantastic. The location is pretty awesome. It would’ve been lovely if the room keeping service included the water refill, especially when we stayed for four consecutive nights.

The hotel location was awesome. The view from.the top floor restaurant was amazing. The breakfast was expansive and delicious. The front entrance is elegant but our room was run down. We had a window that was covered so really did not see daylight until we ventured out. Bed was horrible and shower was challenging.
This was the worst hotel we stayed at in Greece. Avoid room 314 at all cost. It was small, smelled like Goodwill and you could feel every coil on the old mattress. Only good thing was location

I recently stayed at Parnon Hotel and had a generally positive experience. The hotel’s location is a major plus, as it’s conveniently close to many of the main attractions, making it easy to explore the city. However, the hotel itself is a bit dated, and the internet connection was unreliable in some rooms. We also noticed that some areas around the hotel didn’t feel very safe. On the bright side, the breakfast was excellent, and the service was top-notch. Overall, it’s a good choice if you prioritize location and great service.

Had a great stay here, beds were comfy, room had everything you needed, plenty of sockets, fridge, hair dryer etc And the breakfast wasn't to bad either with some hot food included. The thing that made up for it was the staff, everyone was polite and helpful, and the reception team was only happy to help and give you their time! For a three star hotel this was a lot of bang for your buck!

Conveniently located close to the metro station.  My friend & I had a wonderful stay at this hotel.   The room was impeccably clean   the breakfast was superb plus the staff were professional and helpful.   I'll definitely stay here a second time.   Thank you!

I've been in Athens for 20 days now, I've stayed in many high-class hotels, but I've never seen such service in any hotel. Wonderful service especially at the reception, wonderful people, special thanks to Angelos and Nektarios Thank you guys. If I return to Athens one day, I will only return here. Cleanliness 10/10

Amazing small hotel,
Staff are sooo friendly nothing Is to much, they give you map and show you best ways to get to places things to do
Rooms are good size balcony views are nice.
Breakfast is refreshing and someone is  always there to help

Great hotel

The hotel was quite comfortable to relax in with no any distraction or noise nearby and had the necessary facilities. I liked the way the hotel rooms were organized, and thanks for getting our request well in advance of arrival. Regarding the service cleaning, there should have been slightly better routines, the cleaners are hardly visible to the guests in the hotel, it is good that they are doing their job, but we have noticed that they knock and open the door at the same time without us knowing when they plan to clean our room. The other negative view is that the shower could have been prepared a little better as the water runs out to the basin and onto the floor in some cases without a glass protector or shower curtain. Otherwise, I appreciate the breakfast service at the hotel, they had a very delicious and hearty breakfast that was served every morning, we really liked this and the staff who work there are very nice and calm people.

Me and my partner recently visited this hotel for a weeks stay in Athens. The room was basic and we had booked a double room but instead we were given two single beds pushed together. The comfort of the beds was fine for us both, worth noting here that we are a young couple. The shower was powerful, hot and the hotel provided clean towels. They also provided a hairdryer but the one in our room was broke, I asked the first morning for a new one and they gave us one. There was a mini fridge in the room which was great to keep a couple of bits cool and the air conditioning worked great. The staff were friendly and helped if needed. The hotel is a 15 min walk from the centre which was ideal for us. The breakfast was nice, we are vegetarian and there was enough options for variety thought out the week.

This hotel will be ideal for anybody who wants somewhere to stay while in Athens but are not looking for any luxury.

Great value for money as it's centrally located within bus stops and metro station within few minutes of walk. Staff was generous to change my room from single beds to double bed, as my booking agent erroneously booked single beds.
Note that the lane around the hotels doesn't seem to be safe and be cautious while roaming around at night

i would really not advice going out past midnight because the area is generally known for being dangerous at night. that said, at day time you’re close to the center, the metro, and the big shops. rooms were good and breakfast was great, although the coffee was super watery. they should consider getting rid of the fat balding guy at the reception though.

Cons:
- The rooms were very very basic and the cig smoke would come right into the room.
-tv wouldn't work in any other language.
-Breakfast was not good and if you're not there the first hour, it's heavily picked over
-Bedding is very itchy and towels are hard
-Location I would give a 3 out of 5
-No microwave
-Rooms could be cleaner, but thats mostly a dust issue (note, I didn't open the windows)
-Noise carries A LOT. If there are ppl in the hall, it will feel like theyre in your room

Pros:
- Spacious rooms and BIG bathrooms
-Accept night walkins
-Market/Cafe diagonally across the street
-Has mini fridge
-Air con worked well
-A bit more on the pricier side for what you get, but still not bad
-Staff were not rude. Though we didn't interact with them much

Things to note:
- This area is a bit loud
-This is an area where there are A LOT of the unhoused community. They were not aggressive at all. Some places yell at them, but I don't think they deserved it.
-The train in this area isn't great and is a hot spot for pickpockets
-To get to the busy and social area, you will realize that youre on the outskirt of the area.

Didn't have good experience with this hotel at all.
Carpet in the room and blankets aren't washed for years. It stinks so bad and there is no air conditioning (it got broken apparently).
Food is fine, I can't say it was bad.

I came during a peak season when all hotels were booked, so I ended up staying at the only available hotel in the area. It lacked amenities like air conditioning and only accepted cash. The night staff were unfriendly and unsmiling, making for an unpleasant experience. However, the morning staff, particularly a cheerful old lady, improved the mood. The hotel itself was outdated. I was relieved my stay was only for one night.

Expect basic accommodations if you book here.

❗️❗️❗️good to know before staying there❗️❗️❗️
- Cockroach-No airconditioning-Weird stuff -

The neighbourhood was disgusting and seemed dangerous, sadly there were a lot of homeless people. We did not feel safe there.
In the whole hotel the airconditioning was not working, although they have provided a fan, it was dirty. Also the stuff was weird they did not not speak so good english although it was an international hotel… there were cockroach on the floor (in the hall and also in the room...)
The experience there was awful, I definitely do not recommend this place to stay in Athens.

1.  Someone broke into my room in middle of night and when I woke up ran away
2 no air conditioner they lie that it's only going to be broken while your staying at hotel                      3   don't take credit card they make up story that there computer is broken and credit card machine is broken.                                                   4. Dangerous area.                              V v

Someone came into my room almost 440a.m. in the morning was alone thanks God I had my own personal lock so whoever it was wasn't able to open it more than 3 inches and I had an alarm that went off and I woke up what a disgusting creepy way to wake up didn't know what to do so I called my embassy to keep tabs on me to make sure I'm safe and barricaded my room and stayed up the whole night from then didn't know if I should call the front desk cuz thought maybe it's one of the workers of the hotel and wasn't sure if to call police or not then in the morning once I was sure the streets were busy already I left the hotel with all my belongings to go get money from the ATM machine to pay the hotel the worker stopped me why I'm leaving of my luggage and why I can't leave it with him to watch I didn't want to trust him after what happened with this intruder trying to get into my room at night so I declined but he kept on persisting first I thought he was trying to be nice but then he told me he doesn't trust me because I didn't pay it so I told him he could come with me to ATM machine prior to that I didn't want to tell where I'm going cuz I didn't feel comfortable with them knowing I'm going to the time machine if the break in had something to do with them the night before but now I realized that it was actually better because the area is it dangerous area in Athens after that I paid and left and my whole day was basically ruined from going around touring because of the break in since I only slept like two and a half hours just happy to be alive who knows what could have happened if that intruder would have got him before I woke up who knows what he was up to but it's not the only problem this hotel there is no air conditioner the place stinks it doesn't look As good as a pictures and they don't take credit card only cash even though it doesn't say when booking the also a dangerous area

It’s easy to walk to restaurants, train station and all the attractions that bring people to Athens. The staff was super helpful and kind. The air conditioner worked really well. The WiFi went in and out. The area is a bit dodgy. I wouldn’t want to walk around there at night. You get what you pay for

Okay hotel in a neighbourhood with many sketchy people, not recommended to arrive at too late times, especially because one day after arriving around 2:00 am the hotel doors were closed and the number I had to call was always busy after even 5 tries. Breakfast is 8 euros, and you would expect more variety or some warm food like scrambled eggs for this price. Rooms on the fourth floor are nice with nice view from balcony, but the cards have to be reprogrammed every day at the reception. In the second room they put me the AC wasn't working, the TV signal was horrible. I would only recommend it to brave people who have plan B when their hotel locks them out for the night.

So the city is just sadly so dirty, tagged and filled with the homeless. It felt dirty but not dangerous. The hotel itself was nice as a previous review art was interesting but it was lying around in places. It looked like they had not gotten done putting it up. The television did not  work and no idea how to call down. The staff was lovely and kind. We did not ever partake in the breakfast but there were so many awesome bakeries/coffee shops. It was easy to get around but we will probably stay in plaka or mythraoki next visit. It just felt more vacation spot.

For me a bed has to be comfortable and the AC needs to work and it’s quiet. It meets alll those needs. Breakfast is good and the service from the owner lady is 5*
They could use minor your ups like the grout was moldy looking in the shower but that’s common.
For the price it’s a great value and I keep coming back. Not in the core center and not the nicest neighborhood but again the Hotel is clean and safe.

Good hotel but a bit pricey for what it offers (approx €100 euros a night including taxes)
You need to walk up a set of maybe 5-8 steps to get to the hotel so not really wheelchair friendly.
Receptionist and housekeeping were respectful and helpful
Room was a bit tight and toilets had no lock and the door was glass albeit frosted glass.
They don’t have money exchange service.
Lift doors have no sensors so you must be careful.
Nice quiet rooms
Has a swimming pool
Cosy reception area

You get what you pay for. 200 eur for 6 days, you probably already know this is the cheapest option for a hotel in Athens. Location-wise it's magnificent. Next to the Omonia metro station with easy connection to Acropolis, Plaka, Monasteraki and other sites. I expected rooms to be a bit shabby, so that wasn't an issue for me. The cleaning ladies are super nice and have regularly maintained our room. Slightly hiccoughy WiFi but we have unlimited data in the EU so it wasn't an issue. Air-con worked flawlessly and it was included in the price.
Not bad, considering downtown location, you got everything, prices ok, no bed bugs, water was warm, bathroom clean, WI Fi slow though, but old school hotel is recommended...

Bed bugs. That’s the only criteria that I have in a hotel, that it not have bed bugs. I did get bit by bed bugs here and it’s very itchy & painful now, so I wish I’d stayed somewhere else.  Bed bugs aside, I encountered the following Claridge Hotel:

-there’s only sheets on the bed and an optional old blanket from WWII in the closet
-a tremendous amount of noise
-vagrants/addicts/refugees milling around the area (and pulling their pants down right out front pissing)
-a toilet that continuously ran and bathroom light bulbs that burnt out
-a paint job that hasn’t been touched up since the Berlin Wall was still up
-weak wifi that you have to compete with others to get to a website
-old key system where you leave the metal key with the front desk when you go out.

None of these things would have bothered me enough to cancel the stay except for the bed bugs. Of course I inspected the sheets when we arrived but I didn’t see any so I didn’t mind the 70 year old furniture and old peeling paint.

chances are, if you’re reading this review you already know this hotel is the cheapest option in athens that’s not a hostel, and comes with a private bathroom; you’ve seen the low star reviews & you’re trying to decide if you should chance it & stay here because maybe you think everyone else is a bunch of spoiled whiny complainers.
I’m giving them a generous 2 stars because
1. the staff is nice.  both the helpful front desk & the housekeepers that do clean
2. the convenient location near the train, restaurants, stores, museums, walking distance to Acropolis
3. the cheap price, lowest on the travel booking sites
My spouse permanently revoked my hotel booking privileges after this place.   He said “if you have respect for yourself don’t stay here”. But my kids age 16 & 26 were fine with it.

Location wise, it is very accessible, a few steps away from Omonoia Station. Not to expect inside the hotel since it’s a 2 star, good for sleeping. The Old Omonoia restaurant is just outside and the food was tasteful with a good size of serving.

The 3 receptionist throughout the day were all very kind people. The rooms were alright quality with it needing a fresh lick of paint and upgraded TVs. The air conditioning was old but I saw new boxes of air conditioning in the hallway so I think they will be upgraded. The location was also great with a lovely view of a beautiful fountain and it was right next to great restaurants.

Pros:
- Good location, quiet.
- Walkable, though not super central.
- Nice rooms.
Cons:
1. Absolutely no shower pressure.
2. Towels provided are either hand-sized or very small for the body.
3. The wardrobe has only 4 mismatched hangers and no shelves ;)

Very basic room furniture, dark room with an awful smell all over the hotel probably from the Airconditioning. The TV was not working, the flush toilet was broken, the hair dryer also not working. Towels and bedsheets not super white probably are old and need replacing with new ones. The location is convenient but was expensive for the quality it offers

Warning! Service prices changing on the way
After a refundable reservation I've received an email asking if I wanted the taxi at a certain price, I replied the day after asking for the service, the I got a reply after a couple of hours with a higher price.
I called them and a woman literally told me with a rude manner: the manager has changed the price, do you want it or not?
Be aware to book with them and stay in a harsh and stressed environment as normally Greece is a relaxing place to visit for tourists.

My stay for one night was HORRIBLE. It was super hot in the room and it was so noisy from the street I couldn't sleep and I got in at 3AM. The AC also didn't work. terrible experience wouldn't recommend.

We were  coming back from the islands and had to have a night in Athens before catching the flight next evening. We wanted to be close to kolonaki as I love this area and we needed a possibility to leave our luggage for few hours after check out. So we needed an accommodation with reception. Athens Way was good price - value ratio. lots of cozy cafes nearby, good supermarket and Metro - 10 min walk.

Excellent location and the neighborhood is a vibe! Really nice to explore the center of Athens from here. Also a great view from the rooftop. The rooms are not excellent quality but honestly more than fine if you’re out the entire day exploring the city.

Located in the heart of the Exarcheia district, this hotel offers basic amenities at a very reasonable price. Our room came with a balcony that provided a nice view of the urban surroundings. We spent a week here without any problems. The room was cleaned daily by the friendly housekeeping staff. While the breakfast provided is quite basic, the area has numerous excellent bakeries if you prefer to grab breakfast on the go.

Perfect location in vibrant and safe area. Good food and drinks everywhere. Room was basic but ok for what we paid. Issue with this hotel - staff in lobby are NOT friendly or welcoming at all. Traveling as a same sex couple - was asked rudely twice upon check in if double bed was ok. He was very surprised when we said ‘yes’. If you’re going to work in a hotel in a large vibrant city, get used to it. Be more friendly and welcoming.

Breakfast was the same every day, not the best options. Discovered bedbugs, there was random holes in the walls in the room, cockroches (I saw them on stairs). The staff was ok, the cleaning was fine. In my room, the refrigerator didn't even work. I liked the location, but I'd be still careful in that neighbourhood.
Rooms very basic, breakfast even more so. However Manager very friendly and approachable. In the center of the city transport links to everything just a few minutes walk away.

I suspected bc they can’t find our prepaid booking, we got a small room with an antique bathroom, just icky! Towels were stiff & old looking, didn’t use them. I used paper napkins I brought instead.

My husband was doing the booking and only looked at location, not the reviews nor pictures of the place. And because we arrived almost midnight to leave to cruise port at 9:30 am. It was doable for the night.
If you are on a budget and looking for alternatives to hostel, this could work for you. Be sure to check a day or 2 before arrival ensuring they have your reservation. Don’t want to be without a room if you arrived late like us

A perfect place to stay overnight in Athens.  The room was tired (cracked shower base and bathroom door with notches cut in it so it could be opened/closed without hitting the toilet), but it had everything I needed, including a fridge, air-conditioning and a comfy bed (the pillows were like bricks tbh!). I took my own food and drink.
The guy on reception was curt, but, thanks to Google maps, I found my way to Omonia station easily, and from there to the airport for only €9!

Good hotel, the price is very good for the service, you can say its the best hotel for that price and that area.
Highly recommended

Very basic hotel, quite clean. Old decor, shower is very small and the room did smell of smoke. The room has air conditioning and a balcony. Hotel staff were mostly friendly and helpful. The location isn't ideal especially for a solo female traveler. Closest square is Monastiraki which is a good 20-25 minutes away. Price is reasonable.

We came to Athens for a short city break, so we were looking for accommodation that was close to the centre and not too expensive. The hotel was good value for money, the room was small, but for such a short stay - when you just go home to sleep - it was fine. The problem was with the area, huge rubbish, dirt, just bad faces, scary people, druggies. It was such a bad neighbourhood that we didn't even go downstairs at night. Otherwise, I recommend it to everyone, but be prepared that you might not dare to walk down the street.
All night we couldnt sleep because of narkoman people who used drags and prostitutes. They are crying loudly and fighting.  I dont know what to do impossible to sleep
All the staffs are friendly. Very affordable. The room is neat. Walking distance from main tourist spots and train station.
Terrible is the word
XXX Hotel but if the night catches you it's a good choice.
Good

Myrto was exceptional in our visit in Athens, I would recommend the stay here for anyone visiting Athens. She arranged our transit to the airport and recommended a mini breakfast box right before our flight back to the U.S. I love you Greece 🇬🇷 and warm welcomes you have my wife and I during our honeymoon.

We as a family checked in this so called 4 star hotel but were shocked to see the very average facilities.
Especially the reception people had poor communication skills and were  very rude .
The hotel room was the smallest size with the just minimal facilities and very little space available to move around.
The bath cubicle was just good enough for an average build person to stand and I hit the glass door more number of times than my heart rate while taking the bath . Water was leaking from the lower end to spoil the dry area.
We were shocked to know that even the first day water bottles were chargeable which we were not informed at the time of checking.
Even the additional coffee sachet was available @5£/ sachet ( such thing which we never experienced in the last 30 years).
We had rhe worst experience in this hotel & We strongly DON'T recommend this hotel to anyone.
Excellent option in Athens!!! Warm and kind staff. Clean room, outstanding view from the rooftop restaurant and plenty of options for breakfast! It's a 5/5 recommendation for your trip in Athens.

The location and service were nice but the rooms had such thin walls. We can hear people talking in the hallway and the room. We also heard vacuuming / cleaning from upstairs room at 7:30 am. Not sure if this is correct time to do this.

We stayed at the Polis Grand a few times during our trip in Greece. The rooms were comfortable, and the staff were all very friendly and helpful! I wanted to call special attention to Costas! He was there almost every time we were in the lobby, and he made a point to remember us and greet us with familiarity, making the check in / check out process feel a lot smoother! I really appreciated him!!

Amazing little hotel, only a 5 min walk from Metro stations. Great buffet breakfast and the best reception staff ever!  Area has its ups  and downs but felt safe the whole time.Local restaurants are also a must.. If you love city breaks this is the hotel for you..

TERRIBLE HOTEL EXPERIENCE

Our group of 100 students had a distressing stay at this hotel.

Firstly, the hotel is located in a very unsafe area of Athens, rife with junkies. These individuals roamed the streets, frequently engaging in theft and yelling at all hours. We could even see them openly using drugs from our balcony.

Upon entering our rooms, we were met with a foul odor of mold and cigarettes. Opening the balcony door was not an option due to the pervasive smell from outside.

We were forced to order food to the hotel, as it was unsafe to walk the streets, even in the evening.

The room itself was filthy. The sheets and mattress were stained with vomit, urine, and other substances. There was hair everywhere, the mattress had cigarette burns, and there were holes in it.

Housekeeping was extremely rude and disrespectful when we requested new sheets. The receptionist was unhelpful and even yelled at us for inspecting the bed.

At least 10 other rooms reported bedbugs. Proceed with caution!

The locks one some of the rooms were not working, the doors could be opened without a keycard.

The air conditioning couldn't be controlled and ran all night, leaving me with a sore throat. Additionally, there was mold around the unit.

Breakfast was mediocre, though the food was fresh.

Overall, this hotel is a tourist trap. Avoid it at all costs!

Had a lovely stay here with the family . Staff were lovely, very pleasant and ever so helpful . Hotel room was very clean , fresh towels etc daily . Breakfast was lovely. Metro and other transport within three mins walk from the hotel itself

The hotel is located in the worst neighborhood in all of Greece. Junkies and and homeless people seen roaming arround all the time. I would feel unsafe walking around on my own. The hotel however was quite nice, with a really nice bathroom and a rather spacious room. The food selection was not the biggest but it tasted great. No issues with the staff at all.

The hotel is fine. The apartments are not. While the apartment itself is acceptable, the entrance is horrible, you must walk through a street filled with homeless individuals and filthy and unpleasant, we prepaid for 3 nights and left after the first  losing payment for the remaining nights.

We got the room after they told us the apartment we booked at the near neighbourhood is not available.
To be honest, after we passed on that neighborhood on our way to the hotel we were glad since that street seems really sketchy and quite crappy.
The room itself in the hotel is pleasant, vintage looking, clean with great wifi and a small balcony.
The stuff were friendly and welcoming, the only thing to improve is the vegan options in the breakfast buffet.

The rooms are small but really comfortable. Air conditioning and smart TV. I really liked the breakfast with plenty of options. The view from the top floor is also really nice. I recommend it to everyone. The neighbourhood is a bit ugly if you have a car you need to look for a parking lot.

Terrible Service – Beware of the Apartment Scam: This hotel also manages apartments, and when we arrived at the reception to collect the keys for our apartment, we were told that they had double-booked it, and the apartment was no longer available (despite us booking for two nights). Instead, they put us in a hotel room that was clearly meant for staff, not guests.

After reading other reviews, it seems this "missing apartment keys" scam has happened to several other people as well, so beware if you're planning to book an apartment here!

To make matters worse, the receptionist was rude and acted annoyed, as if the situation was somehow our fault instead of their mistake.

It's disgraceful. I can’t understand how this place is still in business. On top of that, it's located in a very run-down area, with junkies hanging around right outside the entrance. I won’t be returning here, ever.

The hotel is in a less than wonderful area, with people sleeping on the streets close by. It's in a handy location to metro and bus for exploring the sights.
The rooms are OK, breakfast quite good, but cold. Don't have the buffet dinner, again cold and very limited choice. Staff were pleasant and helpful.

The only good thing about the hotel is friendly staff. Maybe also the dolmadaki we ate at breakfast. The rooms were very tiny. The bathroom is not proper. While showering, the water spreads all over the bathroom floor. No slippers or flip flops. Wet floor no slippers no hygiene unfortunately. The district in which the hotel exists is full of homeless people and junkies. They gather towards evening. We were afraid while returning to hotel in the evening. Streets were not safe. No coffee table,  no table no chairs in the room or balcony. No place to eat or drink sth in the room. No table for women to put makeup stuff in the room.
I love this place..very nice n comfort..the best hospitality ever❤..very central u can easily rich the center n all around places..thanks..
There were bedbucks, a lot!!! I had more than 70 bites, after this I tried to talk with the owner and he didn’t answer me and don’t want to talk about it, very bad treatment!
Very simple accomodation,  but comfortable enough and inexpensive.
Nice place, but there is crime in the neighborhood, because lately there is smuggling and drug trade nearby... But there is police
Excellent apartment. Very close to the metro. Direct access.

Very nice apartment and a very friendly host. For example, the host brings you clean towels if you need some. The apartment is clean and the nearest metro station (Victora) is a 5 minute walk, so the location is also pretty decent.

Better than sleeping out on the streets. But absolutly not recommendable because of different reasons. Loud and annoying traffic all the day (no sleeping). And not the safest part of Athens especially in the evening and night.
It's the best place to stay for a few days. Near downtown. Nice apartment and big. I strongly  recommend you.
Very comfortable place with a lot of benefits like air conditioning and good help if needed, also fully equipped
The apartment isn't bad, otherwise I would never come to Athens again, we were robbed on arrival, we're not the only ones apparently.... Bad experience..
Horrible apartment...
Excellent apartment. Excellent professional approach. Comfortable house
Very beautiful apartments

Although located in a difficult area with many drug addicted lying on the streets, this hotel is well-kept, quiet, tidy and clean. The staff is very friendly and does their best to satisfy the clients' wishes. They do not offer breakfast but the coffee shop Dodici can be accessed by means of a passageway directly from the hotel. Besides the neighbourhood is full of coffee shops and eating facilities. The hotel is very close to the Metaxourgeio metro station and the OSE railroad station. The hotel has a favourable price-performance ratio.

A staple for me in the last 4 years, whenever available.
Never had a bad experience here; top notch cleanliness and very polite staff.
The surrounding area can be dodgy, especially at night, but at the end of the day this is a central point of a touristy metropolis.
Considering the price tag, couldn't recommend it more.

For the special rate of 56 euro for my overnight stay I was very satisfied 3 important things for me number 1 rooms were immaculately clean . 2. Front of staff Valentina and the Gentlemen are always friendly and welcoming and extremely helpful I was offerded a late complementry check out also  as it's also  my second visit to the Minoa. Regarding the Location is great and a few minutes walk to the Metro to connect you to Synigma square Acroplis ect, some reviews have implicated the surronding neighborhoods it's Athens harden up . I had no proplems this time around or on my last stay.
Arrived late (1am) and needed a place to sleep for one night, great 24hr check-in, clean rooms, very friendly and helpful people. Good value at the price point
Definitely good services, very close to the metro station, good price 💯 but back streets are dangerous u need to be careful
The prices are expensive, the hotel is very bad and unclean, the service is very bad, and everything there is very disgusting. I feel sorry that I had this experience.

The worst Hotel, the location is perfect but it’s the only good thing about this place.
The worst receptionist that I met in my entire life, they won’t even store your luggage at the check out for a couple of hours. Everyone is rude and they won’t help you.
We even paid 5€ for the air conditioning and they didn’t give us the remote control, without any refund.
They didn’t change sheets and towels.
So please spend a little bit of more money to have a better and pleasant journey.

Without any booking we successfully
managed to find a room only in this hotel and were pretty happy.
They don't provide breakfast or any service, the  hotel is located in the nosy, dirty area, there's a permanent smoke smell in the room but we weren't going to get upset. Clean sheets, warm water are enough.
But the personel... Really that grumpy, grumbling old man at the reception... He was unpleased, impolite and didn't let us leave two small bags for 2 hours. Disgusting impression.

According to me it was not a hotel. It was like a store room. Their toilet condition was very low. The washroom was very bad. Water falls from the ceiling of the washroom. It is very annoying. There was not enough space to take a shower. Inside the hotel, next to the stairs, inside the room, the dirty smell is very uncontrollable. Their behavior was very bad. The behavior of the owner and the staff and the responsible people were very bad. In a word, everything was at a low level. I didn't like it at all.

If someone comes to visit with family or friends, I think they should not go to the hotel. It will only harass you.👎👎👎👎👎

It's just ok. Nothing special it's a place to sleep. I'm not going to trash this place. I was only there for a few hours. But it was my least favorite of the 3 I stayed in. It did have a balcony. A decent bathroom and the room was rather large in my opinion. And the internet worked.

This Hostel is nice and cheap. Most important: Beds, toilets and rooms are very clean. They pay much attention to that. Common space is basic but you can socialize if you want. Staff is helpful too. Kitchen is missing many things. As about the location I really like it. You can get cheap food and drinks around. Also the metro station is less than a minute away.
I would definitely come back for all the reasons above.

I don’t think I could have asked for a better experience especially considering the price. The staff are incredible, I felt safe, met the loveliest people and made memories I’ll never forget. The rooms are simple and clean. There’s a communal space downstairs where I worked when needed (and the wifi works perfectly). I walked to Syntagma and Plaka every day with no trouble, while staying vigilant as a woman traveling alone. This is a student area and it’s nice to see them hanging out at cafes and bars close by. Zorbas Hostel has a special place in my heart.

Been in the country for almost 2 months and stayed in 7 different hostels. I can honestly say Zorbas ranks #1 out of all of them. The A/C works wonderfully, the rooms and beds are comfortable, and there’s always a staff member awake if you need anything. They are very kind and helpful. Every other hostel I’ve stayed in has major drawbacks that keep me from feeling totally comfortable but not Zorbas.

Pro tip: spend the extra few euros and upgrade to the 4 person room. Trust me on this. My first stay was in the 6-person room and you share the upstairs bathroom with a lot of other people (there’s more downstairs though) and there’s only one charging strip. The 4 person rooms have charging outlets by the beds, they’re cozier, and have a bathroom.

I like this place. Rooms are clean, bed comfortable and sheets are fresh. People very helpful and sweet. Good price. It is quiet place and I had good sleep. The only issue was shower glass that was only on the one side of shower and it is impossible to shower without making wet everything around.

Great reception desk team, pretty clean place and close to Victoria station. Definitely recommend if you are looking for an affordable hostel in downtown Athens. Just be wary that the area is somewhat considered unsafe, so keeping an eye to your belongings while around isn't a bad idea.

The hotel is a walkable distance from the metro station. There are quite a lot of mini marts also nearby.
The room was a little musty, but the service was really nice. All in all, it was of good value.

Avoid at all cost no value for money even as a two star. This is definitely the worst hotel I have ever experienced.

First of experienced one staff member to be rude in his mannerism, you would have thought he paid me to be here.

Rooms are outdated as well as dirty ants spiders probably bed bugs.

I'm not sure if you will get a good night sleep due to the random noise that occurs from the neighbours.

For what you pay the area is not in the best locations in terms of concerns of safety.

Myself is OK however a lady,  elderly couple, or a family with young children. I'm not so sure if that to be the case.

An unease amount of homeless people and alot of people in large amount of people on drugs down most streets.

Feel as though ripped of the price just not worth it because on a walk out you then come back to a none nice hotel.

Yes you do see alot of nice hotels in the same area but the experience changes if you pay that €10-15 more on a better decent hotel at least you feel comfortable when sleeping.

To clarify this was a stop over however your better of not doing that here.

Not sure what else to mention other than don't come here.

One of the best hotels of the city
I got there last minute with my father and sister and it was great.
What can I say,how cleaner it was ,how quiet it was and the staff was amazing.
The hotel was near the city center. I could walk to many places. I enjoyed my stay.
Definitely going there any time I visit Athens.

Upfront this hotel is probably circa 1970 so presents as “dated” however it was immaculately clean, the beds were very comfortable, air conditioning worked well, staff were exceptionally helpful and it has 24hr reception. We only spent 1 night here arriving at midnight and out by 10am. For its budget price it was very good value. Did find some decent places for food within a short walk.

This review is very much based on price point.

The first thing you should know, this is not a good area at all. For myself, I wouldn't care so much. However, I brought my girlfriend and very much wished I had booked into a more expensive hotel in a better area.

The area was very dirty, full of drug use, and definitely questionable for night walking.

Now on the price point of less than 50€ per night in Athens and only a 20 min walk from the Acropolis, this hotel is great!

The rooms are nice (for the price) and the service is good.
Lovely  boutique  hotel. Small but very adequate  rooms, with a balcony. Much better than average breakfast included. Great location, very helpful staff. Highly recommended.

Clean room and good air conditioning. Good bathroom.
We got 2 singles, rather than a double/queen which was odd.
Nearabouts Omonia square/metro. Dodgy part of town, lots of drug use. Taxi driver told me not to walk the streets alone.
But I found it fine in the daytime, it’s actually fine. It was so central - I walked to all the sites including Acropolis etc. Perfect location that way. Just be careful about pickpockets/etc and stick to main roads, and travel in the daytime.
Breakfast was continental buffet.
And we were able to have cereal/coffee/biscuits when we needed a really early checkout.
One odd thing - encountered a hostile receptionist when returning to the hotel who demanded to recheck my room number/that I was a hotel patron… a bit of a shame as otherwise I thought people were fine.

The experience was extremely bad. Have you guys ever heard of any hotel charges extra 25 euros for bring people back. I can understand if the reception asked the second person to check in for security reasons, but what is the point for them to ask for extra charge? The receptionist said you paid for one person’s price and you didn’t pay for the second one. Are they charging not based on the number of room but on the number of person? Strange, absolutely SCAM. On top of that, the room is not clean at all. limescale in the baths and toilet, disgusting. AVOID THEM!!!

Great hotel and good sized rooms for solo travelers. The only thing I personally did not like was the view of the abandoned block of flats in the back of the hotel where my room was. But overall it was great.

They have a great breakfast variety with good coffee and juice selection. Very friendly and welcoming staff.

First of all: apart from one lady, all staff members were friendly and accommodating. The hotel is very central and there's a great breakfast buffet. There's a tiny elevator which helps you to get your luggage upstairs. The bed in my second room was very comfy (the first room looked nice but the bed was hard as a rock). Overall, good value for money. 👍

Now my issues: The first night I was in a room with a very hard bed. It was very uncomfortable and I could barely sleep. When I told the receptionist in the morning I'd like to change rooms, she first told me that all rooms had the same mattresses but eventually agreed to let me move. In the second room the bed was super comfy, so she definitely didn't say the truth about all mattresses being the same. Also, after moving (on my second night) someone opened my door around midnight even though I had locked it from the inside. It was someone who had a key card to my room, not sure whether another guest's key card accidentally worked on my door or if it was a staff member. Anyway, I screamed and the person left. Afterwards, I didn't feel safe in the hotel anymore. When I told the staff about it the next morning, she said that was not possible and didn't take my concern seriously. Instead , she implied I was making it up. Very disappointing reaction! I asked for the camera recordings of that night to prove that I was telling the truth and she told me there were no cameras upstairs. What the heck?! 🤨

Otherwise I really liked the hotel and all the other staff members were friendly, but this one lady clearly doesn't like people. 🤷

There’s something special about this place. And the people who work here. It’s without a doubt the best two-star hotel I have ever stayed at.

And for women out there, some of the rooms have the best make-up table there exists in a hotel!

The only improvement I would do would be to change doors / isolate better for hallway noise. But still, at night it’s super quiet. The cleaning personal are the kindest. So are the staff in reception. I love the 70’s art deco vibe in the rooms. I would definitely stay again.

Clean, tidy hotel.
Lovely staff, very helpful and friendly.
Little bit away from main tourist area, 2 - 4 km easy walk depending where you are going.
Felt safe walking around.
Close to all public transport options.
Great food options, all nearby .
Will stay here again.
Room on smaller side, but we were only sleeping there, out most days.
The shower had great water pressure and good hot water.
Housekeeping is great.

Our stay at the Victory inn was very pleasant. Our room was very large with ample space for our luggage. The bed was comfortable but the pillows quite thin. As we only stayed for two nights we made do. The bathroom was a bit on the smaller side but fine for a couple.

Good proximity to public trans, easy places to eat and supermarket. Nice local neighborhood 2 blocks away; restaurants, bars, art etc. Place was very clean, staff extremely acommodating. AC works well, room was comfortable and quiet with all standard amenities and private balcony. Simple and fresh selection of local breakfast options served daily. Perfect for a night or 2 while traveling.
34 room boutique hotel! Smallish rooms but very clean and tidy. Not far (walking distance from most of the major Athens attractions. Good food options nearby!

At first, I was concerned. The area did not look great. But the room was decent, clean. The staff was wonderful. The breakfast was amazing and was 15 minute walk to basically everything. We did walk up to the central square at night as well as back from site seeing about 10pm. No issues for us. But still be aware of your surroundings.

We (17 people) stayed here for 7 nights in several rooms. The staff was very friendly and helpful. The rooms were clean and the breakfast was very basic but ok.

Unfortunately we had bed bugs in two rooms and 3 members of our group had bug bites all over there arms. I asked the staff to check for bed bugs and they promised they would search the room while we were sightseeing. I got a call from the manager that they couldnt find any bed bugs but changed all the towels and sheets. When we came back later in the day we wanted to make sure that there are no bugs anymore. We found them immediately after 2 minutes of searching. We then got 2 new rooms.

In another room (1st floor) it constantly smelled like someone smoked inside. My guess is that the cleaning staff smokes right next to the AC units and the smoke is sucked up and distributed to the rooms.

The neighborhood is full of drug addicts, prostitutes and dealers and there is a crazy smell of urin right in front of the hotel.

Would I recommend the hotel? If you are a male person and do not want to stay longer than 2 nights it might be ok. Don’t come here with children.

I enjoyed my time at the hotel, everything was so nice both the food and the service by Stelios, Stella, Sophia ,Vivi and Sissy. They were very polite,very welcoming and made me and my family feel like home. Especially in the kitchen I would like to thank Chrysa she was really kind ,humble and honest.(they really know how to treat customers like family)
Bed bugs! Stayed there for 4 nights first night my son was bit at least 20+ from bed bugs, he was given another room but zero compensation. Location is terrible, drug users all around the outside.

A basic hotel in central Athens. Nice location, v handy for all the touristy things. Staff lovely. Rooms very basic and bathroom v small. Go for the 4th floor as very quiet. AC which was great. Would stay again because of staff and location. A great restaurant nearby in Omania, great lamb chops!

STAY AWAY

I stayed there last night !
bed was missing a plank (as the bed was bending) that was the balcony,I didn’t bother and I did it myself.
in the evening there was a heavy engine working all night (after midnight I arrived) that wouldn’t let us sleep. I tried to call to ask what’s happening but the phone was not working as well.
The flush was  not working in the bathroom as well in result to all the  room smell after I used the bathroom without being able to flush.The cleaning lady this morning said that on the high floors the flush doesn’t work in general.
if you check my profile I am not a complainer but despite the fact that the young lady at the reception was very kind, the manager on the phone was attacking and lying that everything works properly besides the phone which he said “in busy hotels these things happen”.

very disrespectfully he said “what do you want,I’ll give you 10€ discount” .
just describing my experience

I missed my bus to Karditsa when I landed in Athens and a friendly taxi driver brought me here.   I asked to take me to a clean inexpensive spot to sleep for the night.   I was not disappointed.  The staff spoke English and understood my poor Greek.  The room was clean and the balcony was nice.   My only complaint was that it seemed a little out of date and the air condition was not working.   Thankful for the nice breeze that night

Not a good spot. We told a girl the first night our room had bed bugs and asked if we could be moved. To her credit she was very nice and said they didn’t have any available that night, but the next night we could. We then asked the next day about changing and the owner said no. He also wouldn’t offer us any sort of refund for the bed bugs, and told us if we wanted a different room the following day (we had reserved for 3 nights) it would cost us more money. He also didn’t seem even slightly surprised / caught off guard when we told him about the bed bugs. The younger girl we dealt with was great and you could tell the next day she felt really bad when the owner showed zero sympathy. Shes the only reason we gave a 2 star rather than 1 for service, but I would strongly recommend staying anywhere else.

We are very happy to were there. The Service there is very good . They helped us many many Times. The 2 Girls which worked at the Reception are very friendly . Special Thanks from us to both and also a great Thank you to the girl from the Roomservice , it was excellent . Greetings from us to all There.
Carmen & Robert Ilias

I don't know why this Hotel does not have 5 stars, because this Hotel exeeds all of my expectations.

Super air conditioner, nice, clean room with view of THE ACROPOLIS and oh man, the reception person is very friendly.

Excellent Hotel in Athens for this price!🤩

Nice and clean room with refrigerator,TV, water heater for coffee & tea, and a very kind welcome @ arrival.
Excellent location,close to a Metro station and a nice Bakery & Cafè that opens @ 6am. Absolutely recommended place, very good value for the money.

The building is old, but the rooms are new and comfortable. Clean and with a great air conditioning (it was 35oC when we went there late June). It is located on a quiet street that is really close to everything, it is on the edge of a not so nice part of the street, but just don’t go left when leaving the hotel ;)
Walking distance from the main square and from there to Acropolis and others.

An ideal place to visit Athens.

Clean rooms with en-suite bathrooms, cleaned every day, friendly and helpful hotel staff and a location in the very centre are undoubted advantages of this place. Add to this the delicious patisserie-bakery just around the corner, where you can have a good breakfast, and you have a recipe for the perfect place to visit Athens.

Next to metro station, just one stop to Monastraki. Easily transport to everywhere, rooms are clean, has balcony you can smoke , netflix and youtube are installed on tv. Refrigerator and AC works well.Thank you for  friendly welcome and smiley faces.
really bad experience. Thay are really rude, specially when you ask for proper sheets. the toilets smell horrible... ! Would not recommed, even if it's cheap it's not worth it.

We stayed for 3 nights together with the group I had and I can say that we have all been extremely satisfied.
The breakfast is satisfying.
The rooms are large and with a balcony.
Always clean, and sheets/towels changed every day.
What can one say about the owners. Excellent people where they really bring out the feeling of hospitality.
We highly recommend and hope to meet again soon.
Thank you very much members of Sismanidis Travel.

Quiet hours disturbance all the time by deranged Erasmus students that behave without any respect.

Take your apartments away from buildings that families actually live inside and want to sleep.

I booked this house in december and I red the review only few days before going there. The house was okay there was some mold around but the flat was decent. The house was completly dark but was not the fault of the agency.
But despite the decent condition of the flat the big problem was the agency. When you come they call you to sign up some documents and they tell you that for the bed sheets and the cleaning lady you have to pay. They will remove this money from the deposit. The cleaning lady is mandatory and is a way for the agency to control the tentants and what they are doing. The bed sheets are dirty and they are almost transparent because they washed it thousands times. The agency and the cleaning lady they entered all the time without knocking or ringing the bell, with their own keys. At the end of my staying there they came (with their keys without announcing them) to see for some reparations inside the flat. They checked and they told me that they had to do some reparetion in the empty room of my flatmates. The day after I found them inside my flat and they removed the painting from the ceiling in my bathroom. I was really angry because they didn't tell me anything so I went to the office to talk with them and they told me was my fault because I didn't open the window during the shower. They also said that the cleaning lady took a picture every week and the mold situation was every week worst. They did the reparation in my last three days there and they left a disaster around the house. There was dust all around, smeel of painting and when they finished they didn't clean anything. You have to write to them for the deposit back because they didn't ask for it. So at the end I can say: run away from the agency they are the worst and with the fact that they always enter without asking you will not feel respect and in your space. They will not give you your privacy.

This agency is disgusting. I would not recommend it if you are planning to live in Athens, specially if you are Erasmus. My experience here was awful. At first they are kind because they want to STEAL your money. They obligate you by signing a contract which later they won’t fulfill. My roommates and me paid the deposit they claimed and after our stay they took 100 euros from each person without any valid and legal explanation. Apart from this, we had to ask many times to please DO NOT ENTER to our house with their own set of keys. Also, if you compare it with other agencies or particulars, it is more expensive and the quality of the flat does not accomplish the price-quality.
I am still having a horrible experience with them and I hope they lose their customers.

Be careful with them! (ERasmus Rooms Athens - picture uploaded bellow)...
Writing this as a warning to all my Erasmus colleagues.
They extra charge you for things they don't mention until the signing of the contract (like borrowing/washing towels and linen for 60 euros, cleaning, etc.). Furthermore, you're lucky if you get a fully functioning apartment. Most of them are old, in buildings where neighbours don't really appreciate students and complain/call the police and the apartments themselves are falling apart! This is the biggest issue. They have poorly maintained ours for months, trying to fix the toilets, both of the refrigerators weren't working, it took them almost a month to provide us with wifi and so on.
Don't expect them to refund you for anything, after they kick you out they wont send any word until they give you your deposit back without explanation.

Please be careful, this company is misleading. I was an Erasmus student in Athens, I lived there for 4 months, in Omonia. First I found the company via internet, everything was fine with them. I paid a deposit in the value of 1 month's rent. Upon arrival, they contacted me immediately to come sign the documents. They state uniform accommodation prices everywhere, but upon arrival they start charging meaningless fees, e.g. for bed linen, cleaning lady (she really cleaned), towels, etc. The room was relatively fine, but during my entire stay they weren’t able to fix a toilet bowl (even though I warned them several times afterwards). The bathroom wasn’t in the best condition. At the end of my stay, I was completely surprised that they didn’t send me the deposit within the agreed time. They sent it to me only after I contacted them, of course later than stated in the contract. I didn't even get half of the agreed amount back. Their justification was additional nonsensical charges for linen, towels, bank charges and they even said they had to specially clean my “dirty” room. When I contacted them again for a refund, they didn’t reply me. I definitely don’t recommend this company for accommodation in Athens, it’s not so cheap and u cannot trust hem. My Erasmus colleagues had much nicer accommodation and even much cheaper, where I spent more time. Take care.
Nice and comfy place (lot of space to 10 people). Very good location. Recommend to travelling families

Deceptive company that tries to deceive all its tenants (Erasmus students) and treats them in inhumane ways. I was living in one of their apartments throughout the year and they did not fix some humidity and during the week of July that I stayed in another of their apartments they did not clean it all week and I was living among dirt and garbage. Furthermore, the treatment on their part was despicable, having received threats on the last day of my stay there.
Spacious and clean apartment in a good location and good handling

Great attentive and helpful staff, amazing breakfast included. Rooms are very urban as well as location. It's an easy walk to the main areas but one thing I wish we had known was that just a side street off the plaza across the street is a more charming walk into the best areas towards Plaka. We walked the rather dirty & congested main road up & down many times before realizing there are incredible streets and restaurants, cafes along the way.

Feeling at home from arrival to departure. The best choice in Athens; I was very satisfied with their kindness and professionalism. The customer is showered with attention. Upon my arrival, they informed me that they were offering me a free room upgrade, assigning me the suite. The room was perfect, clean, spacious, and equipped with all the comforts—we couldn't have asked for anything better. The staff were always attentive, smiling, and kind. The hotel's location is excellent; you can reach all points of interest on foot without needing public transport. It also has a secure and monitored parking lot. We will definitely return

Very attentive and friendly staff. When we requested anything, it arrived to our room within minutes. Walkable to the Acropolis and many famous landmarks in Athens.

Air conditioning works better than many other hotels we’ve stayed in Europe. The only negative was the doors close pretty loudly so it can wake you up in the room when someone else closes their door, but we would still recommend this hotel to others staying in Athens!

Very disappointing stay!
Hotel is old and the room feels very used. 200 years old thin supper used towels...
We booked a suite from Agoda on arrival received standard room, they didn't want to provide us what we paid for in the beginning..Make sure you get what you booked because it feels like a trick..

Over priced.
Low value for money.
Not recommended

Wonderful stay. The staff at the hotel reminds you what great, friendly service feels like.
Restaurant is wonderful and the breakfast was amazing. Location is perfect with easy walk to everywhere.
Would 100% recommend.

author_name: ひきこもり。高橋正臣。Masaomi Eric Takahashi.
text:
The finest, cleanest and most comfortable place in the entire Greece! I strongly recommend this 5 stars hotel! It’s facing the main road so you’ll not feel too lonely.
I love the soft beautiful mattress and the fluffy pillows! It’s fantastic! Amazing price!
I just have to give this place a review of 5 Stars.
