Google doesn’t condone to any reviews less than 5 Stars! Otherwise, they’ll send you a shrill and condescending message!

The owner of the property in the same day told me to go to the hotel Golden city to pick up the keys. I replied ok and I will arrive by 11pm.

Surprise. Once arrived to the hotel they did not leave anything. I called by phone 3 times. No answer at all.

They left me stranded at 11pm in the middle of a dangerous neighborhood. There were many people with drug addiction problems.

I highly recommend to look the others reviews carefully and advise to where to report them to avoid it happens again to future tourists.

It is dangerous really.

The rooms are comfortable and good, but there is a flaw in that they are not sound insulated, but given that they are sunny and good in terms of ventilation. The staff is nice and energetic, and the receptionist is nice and energetic, which makes customers excited to visit the hotel again

STAY AWAY!!!
Horrible horrible horrible place. Probably the worst place I've ever spent a night.
The neighborhood is full of junkies, we're talking zombies with their arms rotting off. Feels extremly unsafe, we we're even warned by our driver to come to this part of Athens.
The rooms look nothing like the pictures, the walls are made from thin plastic sheets. The window wouldn't close so the noise from the street was horrible. We had to plug our ears with toiletpaper go get a wink of sleep. The rooms felt dirty and our bed had a huge stain from what looked like piss in the mattress. It was a nightmare and I will never go back, if you have ANY other choice don't come here.

Failure all along the line. Photos published on the Internet don't square with what we found in this place, especially in our rooms. The first surprise was the lift being so small-sized that it cannot accommodate three people – maximally two of them and they have to be almost on top of one another. Secondly, a state of rooms. Beds are crooked, toilets haven't any islanded space to take a shower so after cleaning oneself up the whole room is being turned into small pool. Wi-Fi doesn't work fast as well, it lasts a minute to load one simple website. There were fortunately clean towels and sheets, tips about our room were transparently written and sent by the owner too. Additionally, it sits in the area of drug users and other dodgy men (two of them were sitting at the door of this place) so families with children and those who travel alone should give a wide berth to this place. One of few advantages, that tempted us to book here a room, were keen prices but – as this example shows – it's not everything. This place looks as if it was going to decline.

Lovely lovely!!! Employees were so kind and helpful, the hotel is just two stops away from the center so it's less pricey and quieter but not too far at all. The room itself is sweet and I had a nice balcony to sit on and get some fresh air. Very happy with my stay here! :)
The staff was really kind and helpful, the neighbourhood is silent and easily accessible by metro.

The room is very comfortable with excellent water pressure and plenty of hot water.  It would appear that they have just done a remodeling.
I had to check in late, and the desk was very professional and understanding.  He was also helpful in pointing out places of interest in Athens.  The location is on a quiet street and off the beaten path.  I stayed here 4 nights and will would return.

Very clean and nice rooms.
No elevator. Very quiet place.
All the staff of the hotel are very kind and thay give you hot and friendly feelings.
It is very near to the underground train that goes directly to the airport and the city center.

Good service,its close from train station
and all Market,im thankeful 🙏✅

Only mins walk to the Larissa Train Station. Rooms are ok except there was persistent smell of cigarette. You can get hot water from the reception at the ground floor. And bonus: they allow you to leave your luggages for storage if you need to get away for a while.

The room was cheap. We payed €65 per night but even this felt too much for what we got. The only good thing about this place is that it’s close to the Athens Central Station.

The walls were paper thin. When I say paper I mean single ply see thru tissue paper. The mattress was the worst possible combination of a concrete slab and a cardboard box. The bathroom was useable but tiny and the provided shampoo and soap were astoundingly bad. The room, bathroom, and hotel infrastructure in general felt very dated.

The only good things I have to say is that the staff was friendly, our room was ready immediately, and the place was relatively clean.

Perhaps worst of all, was the rest of the clientele at the Hotel. We were awoken multiple times between 11:30pm and 1am by doors slamming. Not just closing forcefully, but ridiculously loud and hard slams. One after another after another. Then, at 5:45am, we were awoken by a shouting match between the two guests in the room above us. Obviously this aspect is out of the control of the hotel, but it’s something to keep in mind when considering the price of the room.

Good location, but the bathroom was beyond dirty, the air vents were closed shut with duct tape, later it turned out that there were cockroaches in the room. We asked to get changed into another, cockroach-free room, but that side not happen, we checked out after 2 nights and moved to a different hotel. There was no sorry, no assistance, no refund, nothing. We suspect the receptionists do not speak English, all our inquiries have been ignored. Avoid, if you can.

Extremely clean room. Clean bedsheets , clean towels, the balconies is a plus and bigger plus is the metro in 2 minutes and the huge supermarket opposite. The price also was very reasonable. But that's it. Don't expect something more than this

1. The rooms were very old and even had fungus in the bathrooms
2. We were charged 3 Euros per room per night as Tax (which in all other Hotels across Greece is 3 Euros)
3. There were cockroaches in the room
4. Locality was very deserted after 8PM

Overall for the prices being charged, other Hotels were far better.

I do NOT recommend!!! My friend and I just stayed at this hotel for 4 nights. First of all, it’s more like a 2-star, not a 4-star. Our room was not great. We arrived and the room had no hand soap or shower soap and no towels! One of the sink stoppers was missing. The shower is completely open and floods the floor when you shower. The lighting in the bathroom was terrible. The staff was helpful, however, I have a big complaint. They are extremely unresponsive via email and phone. Before we left the US my friend tried sending emails 4 times asking about our reservation because we had 2 separate reservations but wanted to combine them. She also sent a separate email with another question. 4 emails and no answers to any. They don’t respond and it’s impossible to call them because you either get a number that’s not in service or it just rings and no one picks it up. Surprising because there was always someone at the front desk. I accidentally left a notebook at the front desk and tried NUMEROUS times to contact them explaining the issue but to no avail. I was happy to pay the postage costs. There was an older, lovely woman at the front desk who was kind enough to give me ice for my swollen ankle and hand from a bike accident. I thought surely she would answer but she did not😔Another comment, the hotel is in a getto area. The first night we walked to dinner and saw a number of •riot police”!!! Quite unsettling to say the least. We were told the riots have stopped and other the police presence. Run down neighborhood, graffiti and trash everywhere, sidewalks are broken and falling apart. Had we known the area was so bad we would have picked a different hotel. My hope is this review will wake them up and give others a heads up as to what they are getting into. The pool looks much better in photos and the other rooms as well. I wouldn’t go in that pool. I’m also hopeful they will read my email and respond about getting my book back. I don’t recommend staying here!

It’s not worth it!!!!!! You might think this hotel is a good deal, I promise it’s not. The location sucks. It’s in a creepy neighborhood with junkies and prostitutes. It does not feel safe at all. The hotel is awful. The swimming pools look nice on the pictures, they are not usable and disgusting. The rooms are old and everything is falling apart. The are no bed sheets, the safe is not safe. Someone broke into our room at 2AM in the middle of the night. How did they even give our key to someone else???? We are pretty sure that there were prostitutes in the lobby and in the rooms. We changed hotel and didn’t even complete our stay. This hotel should not be recommended by any sites!!!! There are so many better options in Athens.

Affordable hotel with decent, clean air conditioned rooms and good service. However be mindful that while it is close to some great sites and the metro, it is in a rather rough part of the city. (Though you walk for 5-10min and things get much better) I was randomly clawed on my arm by a homeless woman while walking  and there are vagabonds loitering nearby. This place pales in comparison to American ghettos and overall not too bad. Overall,  I would say this is a great option for groups on a budget but I would be a bit weary as a solo traveller staying here.
Looks good in photos, but is a different thing in person, not very clean rooms, bad management and is located in one of the worst area of Athens,

Not good location, with a lot of homeless around!
The room's clean is not that good
But the staff were very kind.

Excellent location, at the Omonia Square. It was very convenient for us, as it was the pick-up and drop point for all tour buses. the rooms were very spacious and house keeping kept it neat and clean. The only disappointment was the Balcony was not accessible, as it was a common balcony covering many rooms. Breakfast was standard continental and starts at 6.30 am which was very convenient.

We were a group of seven people. We had an amazing stay at this hotel very recently. Rooms, though a bit small for our liking, are very comfortable. All the necessary amenities are provided. Location wise, it’s very accessible to lots of interesting places. Staff is very friendly and extremely helpful. They even gave us packed breakfast, when we needed to leave early in the morning.

We spent only one night at the Athens Tiare Hotel, but we loved it. The rooms are clean and spacious and the staff is kind, helpful and accommodating. We were a party of 8, and two members are elderly. Our bathroom had a steep bathtub to step into while the others had walk in showers (unbeknownst to us) which we felt was a kindness to the elderly members in our party. We ate dinner and breakfast at the hotel restaurant. The meals were fresh and delicious and we just overall loved our time here - even though it was short. Highly recommend staying here!

Great staff service let down by a hotel that overall needs a refurbishment. All staff were very helpful, friendly, considerate and understanding. Our room was OK. A good strong shower.  A shame we couldn't access the balcony. The decor and beds simply weren't upto a 4*standard. Location was fine and rooms were surprisingly quiet considering how busy the area was.

They offered me a room on the first floor but I didn't accept due to the traffic noise and asked for alternative room. Then they offered me another room with twin beds but I didn't accept it either because I was willing to stay in a king room. Then the receptionist canceled my reservation without issuing a refund! They got my money and they said go away. Stay away from this scam hotel.

Conveniently located in the middle of a city , can easily get around with buses or metro.
Walking distance to many of the famous attractions like Acropolis, museums and many others.
Plenty of good restaurants nearby. Hence , this hotel is an ideal location to stay and explore this beautiful city, Athens.

Prices : reasonable , a very good breakfast is included
Rooms : spacious, bathrooms are quite nice.
Staff is generally OK. They would help you when you need.
Special thanks should go to their wonderful housekeeping staff. They do a perfect job !!

Family friendly: they provided a good cot bed for our toddler ( need to request)
Hotel looks a bit old , has a vintage appearance. However, it’s clean and well arranged.

If we had to stay here again , we would definitely choose this hotel.

Only main problem we faced was , the surrounding can be a bit noisy at night , and wouldn’t feel safe with various kind of people roaming around the area. But police is there all the time , hence no security issues occurred during our stay .
Efharisto!!!

I did not know that all the hotels were full. Fortunately, I went to them and there was a lady at the reception and explained my situation to him. She was really helpful with me and allowed us to spend the first night in the reception hall because all the rooms were full. On the second day we got a room and everything was really clean. Everything was there. I advise everyone to go to them because the place is also close to the tourist places. 🥇😀🙏🩵

Having stayed there for around four days, I can confirm that the negative comments you're probably reading here are either coming from clueless tourists or people who have expectations way too high.

For a three star hotel, I'd say this is the definitive choice for anyone. The prices are incredibly cheap, the breakfast is just a solid ten out of ten, it's clean and comfortable, the staff are some of the most kindhearted people I've come across and they clean the rooms often.

Only minor nitpick I have is in regards to the walls of the room, which could receive a bit of a paint job.

But aside from that? I'm satisfied.
Any issues I thought I'd have didn't happen, and others that those other reviewers seem to mention are not the problems of the hotel but rather the area itself.
It's Omonoia after all, the very epitome of crime in Athens. What the heck do you expect?! Sunshine and rainbows?!

Pros:
Price - Got a room  for a very good deal for a day.
Rooms - I had booked the Double Room but was very glad when the lady at the reception upgraded our room to  superior room.
Rooms were very spacious.
Food - Breakfast was not that great but i must appreciate that the receptionist was very kind and made arrangements to pack  food for me and my wife and to deliver it to us in advance as we had to board an early morning flight
Location - Location was just a block away from the main street which i loved as it was not so noisy but yet very easily walkable to the main junction. Lots of Taxis available all the time.

Cons:
Nothing much to comment

The staff was extremely polite and friendly !!!

The maids did an excellent job upkeeping the room and overall the quality of the room!

The hallway was nice, and they were kind enough to offer us a place to temporarily store our luggage until our ship arrived !

My huge complaint came from the room it could use a bit of painting !! It looked absolutely horrible, which made me subtract one star from the overall experience however, price to quality ratio it was worth it.

We stayed for 4 nights,  breakfast and wifi included, and we left super satisfied with the overall experience. I just wish the room was in a slightly better state. If you pay close attention to the wall picture, you can see it looks horrible.  The breakfast was also AMAZING they had an amazing selection, and my belly thanked them!!!!

This place is honestly so cute. Great library. Beautiful vintage furniture. Such friendly special guests and such a charming location.  Don't think, just book.  All hostels should be like this. Such a nice cafe down the road for aperitivo.

Nice location, very cute hostel & amazing staff!
Unfortunately if you are a hot sleeper- look elsewhere. The AC was to be adjusted by staff only and was mostly turned off during my time here, so we had to open a window during the night which let in many mosquitos! You also heard anyone in the courtyard on the phone throughout the night, luckily only one night someone stayed up late talking. Breakfast was small but a good way to start the morning! :)

Very clean accommodation with a big private room that I stayed in. Vintage and quirky decor, but the in the courtyard furniture is old. The armoire in the room had a musky smell. Good stay if you’re not a social solo traveler, everyone minds their own business, even the staff. Breakfast is free which is nice, but don’t expect anything great—it’s no frills. Situated in a neighbourhood where locals live and eat out, which I really liked. Away from the super crowded touristy areas (20 mins walk from Monistiraki and Plaka). A bus stop around the corner or a 13 mins walk to the nearest metro. Surrounded by tons of amenities (I needed a pharmacy and coffee shop). Great local restaurants and street foods all around it.

I was sent text with code while I was in flight so arriving in Athens I had no internet availability. Reservation had been made 2 weeks before. Cleaning lady does a fab job, she makes your bed each day even. Very nice. They have thought of having everything a person needs. About 45 min. Walk to center.

I only stayed here for a night but they let me check in early and assisted me with my bags. The accommodations are simple but very clean and they have a lovely back yard. Breakfast is free and they have complimentary coffee and tea available all day. Each bed had a light and plugs available and my room even had a small balcony! All the staff is kind and helpful and it was easy to walk to some great restaurants and tavernas nearby. Absolutely charming and I would stay here again.

Upon arrival the receptionist was nice and changed my room because it wasn't like the pictures. After that I had a bad experience. I found bed bugs the next day and they bit me and I informed the reception at 3am that day. They told me we could change the room, but I couldn't move all my stuff at 3am and I couldn't imagine that I would wake up the next day to a disaster. I slept and woke up with more bites on my body and called the reception to get medicine. He told me to go to the hospital and the hotel wouldn't pay anything because they have a bigger problem than me. They will come to clean the room from bed bugs and it doesn't matter who I am or what body the bed bugs bit. I went to the hospital and wasted my whole day there. I came back late to check out and he told me that I would have to leave the room in half an hour. I told him not to leave the room until I get to the airport. Bad experience and they won't handle my problem properly. I would not recommend anyone to visit this place.

When I first entered I was like, did I enter the wrong hotel?

This cannot be a 1-star hotel

Rooms cleaned with steam, friendly staff, location close to everything in Athens, and at an unbelievable price!

Staying here you get a 4 star experience with a zero missing in the price! Totally recommended!

The room was dirty and there were a lot of bedbugs and they attacked all my body, the furniture was in bad conditions.
Really I spent three bad nights there,
An absolute garbage dump of a hotel. Wifi in rooms doesn’t work. Paper thin windows with the loud roar of traffic 24/7. Junkies shooting up right outside. Rude staff. Avoid at all costs.

Not the very best hotel I've stayed in but for under £30 for the night it was fine.
Very basic but clean and tidy.

DO NOT EVER STAY HERE! We arrived at 1am from the airport to find a dodgy room that was dirty stains on the bed, stains all over the bed head dirty marks on every wall! Shower was disgusting and ripped towels were made available. We stayed the night and booked somewhere else and left first thing in the morning. No refund by the owner just a rude remark saying he noted that it was dirty etc. DO NOT STAY HERE!

My stay at this hotel was incredibly disappointing. Upon arrival, we were left stranded outside as there were no instructions for check-in and no reception available. Despite having confirmation emails, we were told our reservation was "canceled" in error. The instructions provided were inaccurate, leaving us without access to our room. The room itself was dirty upon arrival, and  the cleaning staff arrived without a key, despite us being told otherwise. On the day of check-out, we were rudely expelled from our room before the designated time by an unpleasant cleaning staff member. Additionally, the noise from the nearby pub made sleeping impossible until 1 am. To top it off, the room did not match the photos advertised online. Even after the cleaning, we found dirty dishes inside the kitchen cabinet. Overall, this experience was a major letdown.

Large apartment. Very clean. Toilet wouldn't stop flushing which was a bit annoying. Easy to get to transport links or to just walk into the centre so is ideal. Provided a very good and large cot which was great as we have a big baby! We had read the reviews about the noise so we did kind of expect it... but it is VERY noisy. From the bars across the road. Ear plugs were provided and it is double double glazed! So not really sure what else they could do about it. Lift is TINY only one of us could barely fit with the pram which was more of a nuisance than a problem. We were overall happy with our stay

Great space, 10 min walk to main down town area of the city. The hot water system is extremely hot, so be mindful of that! (The hot water tank was reading at a mid level temp on the gauge, when we checked afterwards, yet still it caused my girlfriend's hand to develop blisters on her hand from rinsing the dishes, even with the tap only set to a medium hot setting.) Otherwise a very lovely stay. Comfy bed, well equipped, huge apartment, good communication from host.

Really nice apartment, easy to self check in and was reasonably priced

Pretty decent location near to ominia square

We stayed at this hotel last week. Overall, the stay was good. The location was decent, we walked everywhere in Athens. I would not recommend walking out late at night alone, seems a bit sketchy. We had issues with our air conditioning not working but they responded quickly to resolve this. You are unable to turn the air lower than 22 degrees C which is a bit warmer than I would like. The breakfast was pretty good, they give you many options. Last thing, they advertise some of the rooms having a view of the Acropolis, however, there is not a view at all from any room. Overall, not bad.

Read very carefully. I can assure you this is 100% true what I’m about to write:
We arrived around 20:00. there were a lot of  homeless people and addicted people walking around the location of the hotel. 2 policemen were also doing a search on them.
We saw also 2 sex workers waiting in the street in front of the hotel (yes, we are 100% sure that they were prostitutes).

We enter the hotel because we were scared to stay outside any longer. The staff gives us the keys and we go up.

The room : the room was big, but the furniture was cheap (this should be a 5 star hotel) , the floor very ruined, the air conditioning BROKEN, and one window was impossible to close .

We went out to eat, but all the activities around had obviously very bad hygiene problems.

I rarely write negative ratings. But I hope that no more people have to experience this.

Don’t trust the positive reviews! They are made intentionally by other people that have interest in keeping this hotel going!

Dirty pillow cases and bedsheets. Pls see pictures. Looks like dirt stains. The hotel seems to be new but the housekeeping needs to do a better job. I found breadcrumbs next to the sofa and hair all over the floor.

I booked online and came to the hotel and they couldn't find my booking, which is fine but the lady at the reception was rude. The whole tone of voice is impolite. She asked for 1-2 minutes and only after 25 minutes the situation was resolved.
There was another tall gentleman in the lobby who offered me water while I was waiting and he clearly knew about customer service and the basics of smiling.

The hotel is at a busy intersection. I stayed on Saturday and the grocery store across the hotel kept playing loud music until 2:30am. I called the reception to notify them of LOUD music and the receptionist never got back to me. He told me he will see what he can do and get back to me but never did. I went down to the shopkeeper to tell him to turn off the music and to stop causing Boise disturbance at 2:30am. They claim to have double glazed windows and sound proofing but I could hear motorbikes passing by all night and some people speaking outside on the streets. I couldn't sleep at all.

The toilet is so small that I could barely fit inside, really small. The AC doesn't work and has some sort of a timer on and it turns off on its own.

Overall it was a bad experience. Better pay 20 euro more and have a proper accomodation. This was not good.
We loved this hotel. Great location.  Beautiful,  staff was helpful, and the breakfast was included and delicious.

The room I stayed at first floor was spacious but the balcony was closed, the walls were dirty and furniture dusty.  The choices and food quality for dinner and breakfast are poor for a 4 star hotel category. And some kitchen staff was rude, telling me that some dinner dishes were only for certain group of guests in the hotel and that my group shoul have come to dinner at later time that day.
The area around the hotel is creepy with junkies at every corner at any hour of the day.

The hotel is ok for a night or two. It's not the cleanest I've ever been and the rooms are in need of general repairs, but it's cheap and situated in the heart of the city. Staff was friendly and helpful. To those who complained about weird people in the streets: Yes, they're there. As in every big metropolis. They mind their own business - mind yours and there's no problem.

We stayed here two nights. The location is great next to the bus stop and also walkable to many restaurants and attractions. 24 hour reception was a huge plus. Breakfast was included but we didn’t get a chance to have it because it only runs from 8- 10am and we were out every day before then

**Review of Diros Hotel (3 stars)**

Diros Hotel, located in a central but slightly unsafe area, offers an acceptable stay experience, though with some notable drawbacks. The hotel's structure appears somewhat rundown and poorly maintained, leaving much to be desired in terms of overall cleanliness.

The rooms, while seemingly clean, are nothing exceptional and reflect what one would expect from a three-star hotel. A critical point is the lack of daily bed linen changes, which could negatively impact guest comfort during longer stays.

We stayed for two days, and overall, the experience was okay. However, we strongly recommend exercising caution when moving around the hotel area, especially in the evening, due to the perceived danger of the location.

In summary, Diros Hotel is an acceptable option for short stays but has significant room for improvement.

The Diros hotel was just slightly out of the center but this didn’t feel bothersome - 30mins from Diros hotel to Plaka. It just means you get to get your steps in!
The room was as described (although we didn’t have any kind of view). Air conditioning was functioning which was crucial in this hot weather! The hosts were very helpful and nice!
The bathroom was a a little small, and the shower head didn’t hook onto the wall so we could only have a handheld shower. The water did get really cold though which was great!
The final thing to say is that the walls are super thin so you can hear people coughing who are in the room next to you!

Overall, it’s good value for money, clean, and well located!

There are bedbugs in the hotel. We were a group and most of them were bitten which came out after 1-2 days. We had all stayed for over a week in another place before and there were no problems. We stayed in the Hotel on Diros for one night and the next day there were already bite marks. Scandal.

DO NOT STAY HERE YOU WILL REGRET IT!!!!!

We couldn’t stay here. We checked in and checked out, there was so many reasons we couldn’t put ourselves through the stay. Firstly, all of our furniture was falling apart. I got locked in the bathroom because the handle fell off the door and then the only chair provided collapsed, the sheets are ripped. The floors were dirty, it was horrible. The photos online are catfishes and the hotel is in a disgusting location.
Within an hour we were harassed by people on drugs for money, our arrival taxi even detoured to avoid the area. We found a SYRINGE outside of the hotel and felt so unsafe.

Oh and the best part: they refuse to offer any compensation claiming that the hotel is fine. Even ignoring the exterior, the only provided furniture is falling apart and is broken!

Do not stay here unless you want to risk your safety both inside and outside of the hotel. Do not bring children here. Do not book here, it was the worst hotel I’ve been to in my life and I’ve travelled the world.

The owner has rudely dismissed our request for even a partial refund, so I’d expect a rude reply on here too. Please take my advice.

The rooms are quite spartan, but it's expected given the very low price. The staff was very lovely and let us leave the bags there on our last day. The hotel is located in a bit of sketchy area of the town, unfortunately. It felt a bit unsafe when we arrived late at night.

The staff are amazing, the cleaner will eventually ignore the do not disturb sign, be careful where you step in the near vicinity of the hotel, drug users about. If you want local food for breakfast head left out of the hotel then left again, a little ways up is a great cafe.

I stayed at this hotel in Athens from 20th to 24th June for a business trip with my colleagues. If I had come for a holiday alone or with my child, I would have been terrified. This hotel is situated in Athens' drug trafficking area, making the experience absolutely dreadful.
Every evening, returning to the hotel was a horrifying ordeal. No one does anything about the addicts injecting heroin in every corner. The streets surrounding the hotel are filled with rubbish, syringes everywhere, and addicts sleeping beneath the hotel.
We informed the reception because we thought there was a dead girl in front of the hotel doors. Their response was to do nothing: no action was taken. We had to call the police ourselves. The hotel staff are friendly but completely indifferent to the dramatic and dangerous situation around the hotel.
We were not warned at all about what we would encounter. Terrible experience. Avoid this place at all costs.

Stayed there for one night only as we had to catch next flight to Fira. Didn’t expected much. Had to check in late night because of flight timings but it was smooth as they were aware about our flight timings. We didn’t booked the breakfast but decided in morning to have and it was not a problem for hotel. Overall, room space, air conditioning, accessibility and breakfast was good. Breakfast price was 6 Euros and the offering was good considering it was self service. (Boiled egg, bread cake, bread, butter, jam, cheese, coffee, tea, juice, salat (cucumber and tomatoes), greek curd, cornflakes, chocos etc.

We needed the hotel for an emergency (by the way we were of family of 5) and they told 60 euros at first, but when we said we would take it, they increased the price of up to 81.5 euros.

Not just that, but the room was horrible, not clean and smelt like this weird smell. The air-condition didn't work because of how old it was, and the bathroom had previous paper towels of people's waste in the toilet bin.

One of my worst experiences in my life I wouldn't recommend it.

Good location as its very close to the metro station.
The rooms arent very clean. The bedsheets are either stained or have holes in them.
The toilet is small and not great either. Shower is very small and the curtain is very short which leads to the whole bathroom floor getting wet.
There is a lot of noise from outside if you keep your window open. Every night of our stay the trasb would get picked up and that was very loud.
The noise also comes from appartments in the neighbouring buildings.
From our room we faced an appartment where the people made noise untill very early in the am,music or loud talking.
However for us its was okay and we managed to get a good sleep and start rather fresh every day.

The staff is quite friendly  but really can't do much to the dilapidating place. The room was very stuffy
The showers are very small and one has to use a hand held shower head. I had a problem of ants all over the room. Though it is near the train station and bus station, the neighbourhood just gives of an unsafe vibe.
The little  bar fridge was a nice touch though. Only stay if really in a tight situation.

Really unfriendly staff. I could not understand what they where saying because their English was to bad. The girl at the enterance started screaming at me. Really unfriendly welcoming. Bad dirty rooms. Insane this hotel still exists. No breakfast like stated.

During my stay I was keen to sightseeing for a 3-day trip thus, I have chosen a cheap accommodation in the outskirts of Athens.

Pro: The Hotel totally met my expectations and the reviews all around the web. The location of the hotel is nice, very close to one of the metro stations (5 mins walk only). The room was clean & tidy, while the staff was helpful and all of them spoke well English.

Cons: I chose a room with bathroom facility: the bathroom is well equipped but extremely small, and the showering place is very little... The water always went out to the floor. The breakfast is okay, but there is not a wide range of variety of foods.

Just got back from a lovely family trip to Athens, where we stayed at the Pinacota suites. We stayed in the 3 bedroom apartment and were blown away when we walked in! So spacious, clean and beautifully decorated. Location was perfect as it was out of the busy central parts, but close enough to walk to any of their public transport options (of which there are many!). The owner was very polite and helpful, providing us with instructions and answering our questions. Wouldn’t hestitate to go back! Thank you for a lovely stay in Athens!

Gorgeous appartments, big, spatious, neat and clean. With washing machine and many amenities. We loved the place, it was so great to come back each day after visiting such a wonderful city and rest in a space that feels so much better than any hotel or tourist appartment. The quality of the suite was superb, and Yannis was more than helpful. Near many bus lines and metro stations. Waking distance from sightseeing buses. And near the most trendy “soho” area filled with amazing bars and restaurants.

A perfect place for a perfect stay in Athens. Great quality apartments, a very friendly and responsive owner and located opposite a very good value bar. And, only a short walk to the Metro.
✨⭐️🌟💫✨

Great location! Near both metro and bus and a walking distance to most of the sights in athens. Easy and secure checkin/checkout. The apartment was cozy, fresh and stylish. It had all you could ask for in facilities, since i stayed for 3 months. Great host with good communication and quick responses to questions. Would love to come back.

Luxurious Home away from home!

I spent a week for work in Athens at the 303 Pinacota Suite!
A luxurious and very well equipped condo with all the amenities that can make your stay memorable. I really felt like home and I even did home working one day,  just to get some time in my beautiful suite.
In the city center but at the same time very calm.
The suite was fully equipped and much bigger than any hotel room. Decoration is amazing but not intimidating.
Personalised service and advice by the owner.
The concierge service is great!

I’ve already booked the two bedroom suite for my next trip to Athens in 2 months!

Looking forward to staying again in these beautiful and lush suites!

We ate breakfast at this hotel and it was delicious. Coffee excellent too! And the service was amazing! The restaurant was very busy with lots of patrons and the servers were still 'on top of it all. Highly recommend!

The hotel is passable, if you are not expecting any lux. The shower cabin is suitable only for skinny women, so probably you will need to take a shower outside of it. The reception guys are polite and friendly. For the money I paid I guess this is what one can get. It's near the historical centre of Athens (25 min. walking distance) but the area is dodgy, dirty and noisy. I would not recommend to anyone.

Booked a room here and stayed for a week in December 2016. Booked a premium room but when we got to the room it was awful. The door to the balcony was broken. Hair all over the bed. The floor and the bathroom were dirty. Area not great but we knew that as my boyfriend is greek and knows the area and close to the metro. When we went to ask to change rooms the lady at reception told us there were no other deluxe rooms available. She said she had one other room and we asked to go and see it. It was a lot smaller with no balcony but cleaner however the shower didn't stay in position which was annoying but it was an improvement. Our room wasn't cleaned while we were there and when we asked to be refunded the difference of the two rooms we were refused which I personally think is awful seen as it wasn't our fault that we moved in the first place.

The area where's the hotel isn't so sure, especially in the night. The guys at the reception were very nice and they speak a good English. But the room was kind dirty, we've found a wet old towel on the top the bed, just to say. Also the toilet wasn't brilliant in cleaning. The price was low, but also the quality. Nope.

The hotel is really worth staying in. The location is convenient enough. Our room was clean, cozy and had everything you need to have in a hotel room. The staff was very friendly and was always willing to help, if there were any questions. Highly recommend to choose the hotel for your stay to be pleasant.
Cozy apartment in quite area. Room is spacious, clean and tidy.There is nice balcony. Good for a group of 3-4 people.
Top

The apartment is in a neighborhood 1.5 km from the center in a somewhat abandoned area. Around there is a cheaper gastronomic variety but they are all Kebabs. The apartment is quite neglected. When we arrived there was no faucet and it took them 2 days to fix it. When we arrived we had met at 3:00 p.m. and they didn't arrive until 5:30 p.m., leaving us on the street with our luggage. It took the man more than two hours to come from when we called him. The sheets had snot, to say the least... Disgusting. Only one set of towels for the entire stay without the possibility of cleaning them because there is no washing machine. Thank God the elevator worked... But the noise inside the apartment is horrible, thank God it is not used much.
We asked for the possibility of a transfer to the airport and they wanted to steal 15 euros more from us than the taxi drivers...
Excelente local para descasar e praia. Bom restairante e instalações. Os donos de alguns apartamentos são a nódoa. Acham que são fomos de tudo...
The place was spectacular, very comfortable and close to everything, but the host was short on information, which is why I didn't give it five stars.
Great and spacious apartment in a great location. It has everything you need in a quiet neighborhood. The host is wonderful, he explained everything to us and was there for all our questions.

My family and I had a great stay at this apartment. It was  and equpped with everything we needed. The host was very accommodating and quick to respond to any questions we had. We would definitely stay here again!

Booked this apartment online and the listing states free parking,not true.When I told the owner by email that I could not stay there because I had a rental car I had to park and there were tow away  signs, he sent me an automated email with paid garage instructions and areas in Athens to avoid.Also the area it is in is not the safest looking,people on drugs walking around and such.Then after I contacted my bank the owner called me directly by phone and asked me to get the money back I just disputed because he had bills to pay.
This is an update on this "host" through booking.com,this individual decided he did not like the review I left so decided to slander me on the response,Do Not Book Anything from this person,I won't name names but he has another listing on another booking site.Google has been notified of the harassing behavior.
Comfortable, clean and bright apartment, located next to the metro and train station, with central attractions within walking distance.

Ugly, dirty place.
1. Cockroaches (karaluchy!!!) - if you never leaves at slums it's a good place.
2. Air condition don't work (It was very old making biger noise than cold and installed only in bedroom). At living room was two dirty fans.
3. Kitchen was dirty and has only one spoon.
4. Owner makes many rules wich was send before camig eg. disable hot water because electrisity was broken.
5. Entrance to the building stick and is totally broken.
6. We ask owner to cancel rent after one night because we disgusted cockroaches (karaluchy) but he say apartment is ok and payment policy ... then I say then I write all at comment.

All disadwantages was at other opinion.
Only nice photos! Congratulations to photographer. God job.

Hello we have booked a hotel in Athens, the one above send by lien.A our great surprise the owner has changed our apartment.
The latter "imposed" at the last minute, being better said, because very recent, happens to be a cellar transformed into an apartment.
Finishes are not realized.
Our biggest worry is the smell of stubborn cellar moisture.
Breathing problem, scratching eyes and throats.
We had booked the first apartment with the washing machine and TV, service not available in the replacement apartment.
No view to the balcony,tiny w.c and shower(also smells nasty...),unfair deal (booking32€,we pay50,50€!!!),no water for welcoming.first time in my life,happened something like that...never again...
Very cool

First of all the location is incorrect if you using phone maps it takes you somewhere else and we really struggled getting there. We searched for Eurobank which was next door and that let us reach there finally…
The service was very basic (we were charged a tourist tax fee of 1.50 euro which is ok but I never been charged that before anywhere else).
The bedrooms were fairly clean but chairs had stains and mattresses had also (please check the underside) our mattress looked really dirty almost like someone did pee or similar things on it. We left 2 days ago and when we reach home now we had some bugs coming out of our bags and clothes 😢 myself and my daughter are covered in spots not sure what is this I’m hoping from the bottom of my heart that it is not bed bugs 😢 I’m terrified and now washing everything in 90C plus taking everything to tumble dry.
I would not visit again sorry.

Would not stay here again. The hotel is quite rundown and while I am not one to complain normally, the shower did not have hot water the first 2 days of my stay and would go from hot to freezing when it did finally work. I have also never slept on such an uncomfortable bed (even hostels have been better). Lastly, THIS AREA IS NOT SAFE. I did not realize when booking, but this is the worst neighbourhood in Athens to stay in and you will not feel safe walking around. Please be careful and book at a better place

This hotel is fantastic. I had an amazing sleep. The room and bed are comfortable. Great AC. There were no bed bugs as I read in other reviews. The area is close to the city centre. Hondos centre has a great breakfast area with views of the Acropolis. Just 100 metres up the road. I would come back again

Extremely loud place. Noise from the street I could live with but the noise made by everyone was unbelievable. Slammed doors, thin walls, hearing people through the vents. Every door in this building was designed to be slammed. The location was ok, 25 min to the acropolis. Also pictures look very nice on the site but they are not showing the truth.
Spartan facility at cost effective price. Clean. Cordial staff. Great location. Good wifi. Refrigerator in room. Would stay here again while touring Athens.

Super hotel, clean and staff are very friendly. Ask for an iron and it's there in minutes. Location is great minutes from everything. Only downside is that Athens, like any major city has a problem with homeless & drug abuse, which is largely situated in the hotel area. My advice would be it's great for walking in the day. However, to return to the hotel at night get a taxi

average hotel doesn't deserve 4 stars
good stuff. clean room.
breakfast is mediocre.
service is below average as the standard and executive rooms don't have kettle!!! and there wasn't water refill after the 1st day in addition to the lack of slippers!!! how come 4 stars Hotel lacks that much???
the location isn't interesting and the surrounding area is filled with homeless and durg addicts but there is also police in the area

The staff were really lovely and helpful. I was a bit disappointed with the vegan breakfast options (there was fruit, peanut butter toast and muesli only) I thought there would be more options as they said they could cater to different dietary needs on their website.

Maybe adding some vegan sausages or something to the menu, and having vegan margarine to go on toast could help with that (maybe?)

Overall the hotel was nice

My stay at the hotel was not good, except for the night staff and the cleaning lady, who were nice and very professional. The breakfast was very simple, and the coffee was not good. The room was very noisy because of the cars, and the area did not feel safe. I don’t recommend this hotel.

Our room was clean , with a big balcon and had everything you need. The area around the hotel is not the best, but i will give 5 stars because the lady on the reception was really thoughtful and friendly.

Great location- short walk to train, tram and metro. Very clean, well kept, comfortable place and cordial staff.  Cozy coffee lounge at ground floor.. Was upgraded to bigger room, which was actually quite big and very comfortable, although gets faint traffic noise.. was upgraded on room.  Huge beg, Plush bathroom, coffee maker, fridge… very comfortable stay overall.. would definitely recommend..!

Clean, modern rooms with everyday cleaning if You wish to. Hotel reception stuff is friendly and helpful. Great contact with no problems. Location is close to center and main tourist attractions only 30min walk to Acropolis.

We thoroughly enjoyed our 7 night stay in the middle of April. The room was large, clean and comfortable. The staff were friendly and helpful, and the location was pretty much perfect. Close to the metro and within easy walking distance of the city centre, with plenty of restaurants nearby. I would definitely stay again.

The room was very clean a welcoming. So was the guy in the reseption.
The balconi is bigger than the room and is nice to sit in at evening.
Locted a few blocks from the Metro. Many bus stops near by.
Not a walking distance from the akropolis area.

Nice hotel, the lobby area and the room were very clean, the hotel is located relatively close to the center and the metro station is really close, Harris was very kind and welcoming, was available for any question and was always right to help. I will definitely come again.

Great location right next to Viktoria station, with quick access to centre of town. The room was nice and big, clean and just what we needed for a few nights in Athens. Thank you to the host for being hospitable and welcoming.
Great service. Thank you for helping me with a last minute booking.

5 stars for service is definitely not enough considering how much time Mrs. Iza spent with us. A wonderful woman, knows the city and its history. both ancient and modern. I would definitely recommend it to anyone going to Athens!! :)

We were very pleased to spend two nights at Petaluda House 3. Excellent Comfort and spaciousness of the rooms, unknown to me at this price. I recommend it with a clear conscience.

Ps. Iza, if you are reading this - marry me 💙
Dirty hotel that provides poor service, but advisable for half day stays or on hourly basis.
Not good at all there is no service and not recommend it. No soap and no comfortable bed and small tv.
Great  staff always clean rooms and very cheap
Good room
Cheap and good ,but not good environment.

The room was completely dirty. dirty. Dirty. I have never seen a place with so low cleaning standards.
When we arrived the room was a mess, with dirty chips bags all over the place, the sheets were still the ones from the previous costumer (full of hairs, food, crumbs, stains) and the bed was half made half untied. The curtains were torned and completely brown (dirty ! They were “white” on the top). The main problem of The room was in fact the door: it was broken! It would open without key.
The shared bathroom was the most disgusting toilet we have ever been. Full of hairs, insects of all kinds and dust everywhere. The shower...apart from being completely destroyed, we could see the brown marks (dirtiness) on its walls - if you stay in this place don’t forget to bring your own flipflops! Don’t touch the floor!
In the corridor there is a small fridge than can be used, but the interior was  covered in mold.. if you just need to put a bottle of water it should be alright, otherwise I wouldn’t advise.
There is no internet in the house/“hotel”. The rooter is there, but it doesn’t work.

If you want a cheap quick bed, this place is ok. But don’t expect anything else as all the pictures on booking are FAKE.

Be careful with the window, do not open it! We got stung by 5 mosquitos and we ended up with around 20 bites each - and unfortunately one of us is allergic.

Location is good.

KesTour CANCELLED my reservation upon very short notice, however, they did NOT give me a refund. Any and all attempts to contact them, with directly by phone, or via booking.com (which is the site I had booked on) failed, they were neither replying to their phone nor to emails. Beware, it could happen to you!
No no no never no. Avoid! Key needs to be obtained far away. WiFi not functional. Cockroaches. No air conditioning.

Hotel to avoid, broken lock, questionable electrical installation, moldy shower, having to pick up and drop off the keys by taxi at another location.

No hygiene facilities offered (shower gel, shampoo, soap, etc.)

I'll try to describe as fairly as possible.

Hotel staff are friendly. Street is absolutely disgusting. Feels dangerous but most of the druggies outside are too busy in their own world. General location is really great for exploring central Athens. Really close to Omonia Metro station (super dodgy area still, watch your belongings). Really close to the Airport bus stop. 30min walk or so from Acropolis.

As others mentioned, toilet has no door. Not a huge issue but worth mentioning. Also, it is rare to have a quiet night. It's not super noisy, however people are screaming etc at random parts of the night.

Elevator is janky, but works. Room is relatively clean.

All in all, it was a great price and amazing for exploring Athens, but I will never be back aslong as I can help it. Staff were helpful, but it was just too much for me.

Me and my friends booked a room for 3 at this hotel. When we arrived they told to us to go in another one, Lido hotel, under the same company without explainetion. The neighborhood was probably the worst place in Athens: broken streets and buildings and people on the side of the road eating pills.

Worst hotel I‘ve ever been!!! Dirty room. The bed was covered with a plastic. No hot water in the shower. The „showerhead“ where comes the water out, was not really working. The door of the hotelroom has a window and in the night the light on the floor shines in the room. We booked 2 nights and after the first night in the cold room with a thin sheets and a not working AC (and the cold shower) we leaved. Drugpeoples around the hotel.
So think two times about to sleep there.

The location is a drug addicts spot the exact street in particular is dangerous and full of shady characters. Walking out there I felt I could get stabbed any moment so please take my comment seriously YOU DO NOT WALK OUT THERE AT NIGHT.
In terms if the hotel: the towels in our room had blood stains on them mixed with black stains and when we asked for replacements they new towels came with blood stains too. Overal a terrible experience pls stay away !

Good staff very good service all over a nice place to stay but out side I don't think you would even want to go around even day time not that good it's not got to do anything with the hotel that is a problem all over ammonia have a nice stay

Worst airbnb I have ever stayed at, and I’ve stayed at many. The apartment was cramped and small. We stayed for five nights total and the host was unwilling to provide extra towels, so we had damp towels the whole stay. The shower leaked all over the bathroom. There was no hot water and despite following the host’s instructions perfectly, the host was uncooperative with checking on the hot water or helping us. We followed the instructions provided, and he said the hot water should work for a 15 minute period before shutting off, but the longest the hot water ever stayed on was 1-2 minutes. It was a very unpleasant stay because we could not have a hot shower after a long day. The kitchen was dirty with food that expired last year still in the fridge and an unclean sink. The bed was uncomfortable and we had to use the “couch” pillows because not enough bed pillows were provided to sleep with. The “couch” was a palate with a cushion on it and the “table” was a wood palate on the floor. Curtains were see through so the apartment stayed bright all night, even with the wooden slats. In contrast, other hostels and air bnbs we booked along our trip were the same price but a world of a difference. Host continues to try to gaslight us that there could be no fault with his airbnb, and refuses to look into the hot water or clean his kitchen. Save your money and stay elsewhere for a more comfortable stay.

The Athens Zafolia hotel was chosen by my tour group. The hotel is located in the heart of Athens. Very easy to get to all major areas of interest. Dining easy to find, and the staff was very accommodating. We had a few doorman to help with our luggage. When we needed directions, the desk staff was very helpful, and provided a map. The pool was on the roof, and my one complaint was no shade. We stayed during the heatwave in June '24. They even closed the Acropolis, it was THAT hot. I couldn't stand to be even in the water during that time without a place to find some shade. Other than that, the breakfast was great. Good selection, great coffee and juice. Hotel staff were very friendly. The rooms and bathrooms could use some updating. Although, they were very clean. For the time we stayed, it was very nice.

Everything is quite above average except location is off the tourist area. The room is quite spacious which is a plus. However, It will take 20 min taxi (10 Euro) to reach the Plaka area. There's not much shopping or restaurant choices nearby.
...After reading the hotel feedback on my post, I apologize for my wrong perception that the hotel is not in city center.

Close enough to the metro and still close enough to some sites.. customer service was pretty bad. Experienced racism. So, our group of 11 (6 rooms) booked extra days stay after our tour ended.  Our group, along with other members of the tour company arrive early.  All the other members,  who happen to be white got checked in early,  except our group, who are  black ladies were told we could not until 3pm. Despite other groups  coming in after us and also getting checked in. A member of our group went to the desk three times and was refused. They made us wait until exactly 3pm to check us in. Needless to say there was no one else left to be checked in after us. However there was one lady there who helped us with directions to the metro and also called us cabs when we requested.  So sorry I forgot her name. Please keep her there. I would not recommend this hotel to my black friends... my white friends maybe.

40 euros from Airport to this hotel on the outer rim of the city perimeter. No doorman to help with luggage - reception was perfunctory. Room neat enough but dusty floor. Bathroom has issues. 2 old lifts labour bringing a couple with a suitcase each up. Breakfast buffet is as expected in 3 star hotel. Don’t try dinner at the restaurant where waiters sneer at anyone not understanding Greek & joke with those who do. Expensive.  Interesting shopping precinct up 2 blocks. Can’t really recommend this place. Totally understand why Greece is in economic decline. Beware of taxi drivers, restaurants & shop keepers none of whom are grateful all want tips for no service. The pool was closed.

Comfortable, relatively inexpensive, half hour walk from Syntagma Square. Slightly faded/well-worn feel to the place e.g. room key cards  needed replacing 3
times in 48 hours. Pool on top excellent facility. Extremely dull, central casting breakfast buffet - more fuel than delicious!

This hotel may not be a five star hotel but it has a character and touch of the owner's dedication to make the guests' stay a comfortable and pleasant one. I loved the cleanliness and the friendly attitude. Breakfast is simple, sausage and scrambled eggs with different kinds of breads, jams and jelly, fresh juices and coffee. It filled me up, so I'm not going to complain.  If I want more varieties, I can  pay more money and stay at an expensive hotel for some extra cheese for breakfast. But I chose this hotel and happy to get what they're offering. Acropolis, Plaka, Monastriki 10 minutes away and Uber cost about 6 euros. The rooms are good size and the best thing is the little private patio to chill.  There's a "safe locker" in the room. Internet speed is very good. My experience here is very satisfying and I highly recommend this hotel to the "non fussy" travellers for a comfortable, inexpensive stay in Athens.

I don't know who rated this hotel as high as they did which caused me to choose the hotel but... The air conditioner leaked on the bed and night table it is clearly an ongoing issue as you can see rotting wood down by the floor. The hotel is on a dirty side street. The older male manager or owner is not pleasant, at all! The girl who worked days was pleasant and helpful! I do not recommend Hotel Nicola.

This is possibly one of the best value for money hotels that I have stayed at on my travels -- and I've been on the road since July 2017. The place is spacious and clean. The WiFi works! The breakfast was generous, the bar prices very reasonable. Best thing about this hotel is the staff -- they are all friendly and ready to help. Also a mine of info about what to do and see in Athens. Highly recommended.

Nice and cozy hotel with balcony at very reasonable price compared to other hotels in Athens. If you are not too picky, you will be fine. Free breakfast and the area is safe and closed to bus and taxi. Service is awesome as owner and staff will go above and beyond to help with request to make guest happy. Rooms are well cleaned. My suggedtions for improvement are: better wifi which is not strong and brither lights around lobby area.

This is a small gem for the people looking for a clean and neet bed and breakfast in Athens Greece  it is a family managed hotel where all the family make you feel that you are an extended family member not a guest very courteous understanding of guests needs they do their best to accommodate you and serve you with great attitude very helpful speak English. I do recommend this place for families and business

Excellent customer service. This hotel has the most amazing staff ever. They deal with any issues you may have swiftly, always with a smile and genuine concern for you. I really would like the reception staff, the breakfast staff and the rooftop staff to be congratulated for their professionalism. The rooms are comfortable and the bathrooms are massive. The only negative is the bed which is two small beds pushed together like in most hotels in Europe. I am a big guy so I would always end up in between the two beds :-(  The breakfast was really good and varied. Lots of choices! Location is perfect.

Went there on my birthday and the front staff has upgraded me to a premium room (park view) with free breakfast. The pool, restaurant are great and you are free to use the gym and sauna after checkout. I would say this is definitely a highlight or my birthday trip and the staff was really friendly - also the apple pie in the restaurant was so good!

We loved our stay here, everything was perfect! The rooms are super clean and nice, the hotel is gorgeous, the location fantastic, the rooftop bar amazing, the breakfast is a nice buffet, and the staff is wonderful! Everyone we interacted with was so pleasant! I actually have family in Athens so, whenever I'm back, I'll be staying at this hotel for sure! Everything was lovely!

A good hotel with a beautiful outside rooftop.

The staff is superb, polite and with a sense of humor.

Rooftop restaurant had some nice finger foods and snacks.

The hotel itself is showing its age, though. One of the elevators is not working. The AC in the room is very noisy. The decor in the room is kind of dark and outdated.

Linens and towels were clean.
Good coffee and tea selection.
Great view from our room.

Might not be worth the price for some as it is expensive.

Overall, not a bad hotel but also has areas to be improved.

Nice hotel, very helpful staff and good location opposite the Park.  Had a superior room with a view of the acropolis from the large balcony.  Unfortunately let down by the south facing balcony which was exposed to the sun all day and way too hot to use.  No umbrellas or awnings or any shade whatsoever.  The couch exposed to the sun was severely damaged.  Wouldn't stay in this room again.

Pros:
- Good location, quiet.
- Walkable, though not super central.
- Nice rooms.
Cons:
1. Absolutely no shower pressure.
2. Towels provided are either hand-sized or very small for the body.
3. The wardrobe has only 4 mismatched hangers and no shelves ;)

Very basic room furniture, dark room with an awful smell all over the hotel probably from the Airconditioning. The TV was not working, the flush toilet was broken, the hair dryer also not working. Towels and bedsheets not super white probably are old and need replacing with new ones. The location is convenient but was expensive for the quality it offers

Warning! Service prices changing on the way
After a refundable reservation I've received an email asking if I wanted the taxi at a certain price, I replied the day after asking for the service, the I got a reply after a couple of hours with a higher price.
I called them and a woman literally told me with a rude manner: the manager has changed the price, do you want it or not?
Be aware to book with them and stay in a harsh and stressed environment as normally Greece is a relaxing place to visit for tourists.

My stay for one night was HORRIBLE. It was super hot in the room and it was so noisy from the street I couldn't sleep and I got in at 3AM. The AC also didn't work. terrible experience wouldn't recommend.

We were  coming back from the islands and had to have a night in Athens before catching the flight next evening. We wanted to be close to kolonaki as I love this area and we needed a possibility to leave our luggage for few hours after check out. So we needed an accommodation with reception. Athens Way was good price - value ratio. lots of cozy cafes nearby, good supermarket and Metro - 10 min walk.
Super
good place
Super

The reception doesn't speak Greek..(only broken English...)
It's more like a hot spot, than a hotel... I don't think I need to say anything else... if you're brave enough to find a room (rare), double-lock it at night, for better or worse... more ghetto than a hotel..
The fact that the reviews are almost all in Arabic... says it all.. shame because it used to be decent...

Previously, it was an ordinary hotel like any hotel, but now the European Union has rented it to refugees only to families who have health problems because the place is close to the hospital, and after completing treatment, they move them to homes.

This place is honestly so cute. Great library. Beautiful vintage furniture. Such friendly special guests and such a charming location.  Don't think, just book.  All hostels should be like this. Such a nice cafe down the road for aperitivo.

Nice location, very cute hostel & amazing staff!
Unfortunately if you are a hot sleeper- look elsewhere. The AC was to be adjusted by staff only and was mostly turned off during my time here, so we had to open a window during the night which let in many mosquitos! You also heard anyone in the courtyard on the phone throughout the night, luckily only one night someone stayed up late talking. Breakfast was small but a good way to start the morning! :)

Very clean accommodation with a big private room that I stayed in. Vintage and quirky decor, but the in the courtyard furniture is old. The armoire in the room had a musky smell. Good stay if you’re not a social solo traveler, everyone minds their own business, even the staff. Breakfast is free which is nice, but don’t expect anything great—it’s no frills. Situated in a neighbourhood where locals live and eat out, which I really liked. Away from the super crowded touristy areas (20 mins walk from Monistiraki and Plaka). A bus stop around the corner or a 13 mins walk to the nearest metro. Surrounded by tons of amenities (I needed a pharmacy and coffee shop). Great local restaurants and street foods all around it.

I was sent text with code while I was in flight so arriving in Athens I had no internet availability. Reservation had been made 2 weeks before. Cleaning lady does a fab job, she makes your bed each day even. Very nice. They have thought of having everything a person needs. About 45 min. Walk to center.

I only stayed here for a night but they let me check in early and assisted me with my bags. The accommodations are simple but very clean and they have a lovely back yard. Breakfast is free and they have complimentary coffee and tea available all day. Each bed had a light and plugs available and my room even had a small balcony! All the staff is kind and helpful and it was easy to walk to some great restaurants and tavernas nearby. Absolutely charming and I would stay here again.

Just got back from a lovely family trip to Athens, where we stayed at the Pinacota suites. We stayed in the 3 bedroom apartment and were blown away when we walked in! So spacious, clean and beautifully decorated. Location was perfect as it was out of the busy central parts, but close enough to walk to any of their public transport options (of which there are many!). The owner was very polite and helpful, providing us with instructions and answering our questions. Wouldn’t hestitate to go back! Thank you for a lovely stay in Athens!

Gorgeous appartments, big, spatious, neat and clean. With washing machine and many amenities. We loved the place, it was so great to come back each day after visiting such a wonderful city and rest in a space that feels so much better than any hotel or tourist appartment. The quality of the suite was superb, and Yannis was more than helpful. Near many bus lines and metro stations. Waking distance from sightseeing buses. And near the most trendy “soho” area filled with amazing bars and restaurants.

A perfect place for a perfect stay in Athens. Great quality apartments, a very friendly and responsive owner and located opposite a very good value bar. And, only a short walk to the Metro.
✨⭐️🌟💫✨

Great location! Near both metro and bus and a walking distance to most of the sights in athens. Easy and secure checkin/checkout. The apartment was cozy, fresh and stylish. It had all you could ask for in facilities, since i stayed for 3 months. Great host with good communication and quick responses to questions. Would love to come back.

Luxurious Home away from home!

I spent a week for work in Athens at the 303 Pinacota Suite!
A luxurious and very well equipped condo with all the amenities that can make your stay memorable. I really felt like home and I even did home working one day,  just to get some time in my beautiful suite.
In the city center but at the same time very calm.
The suite was fully equipped and much bigger than any hotel room. Decoration is amazing but not intimidating.
Personalised service and advice by the owner.
The concierge service is great!

I’ve already booked the two bedroom suite for my next trip to Athens in 2 months!

Looking forward to staying again in these beautiful and lush suites!

Great location- short walk to train, tram and metro. Very clean, well kept, comfortable place and cordial staff.  Cozy coffee lounge at ground floor.. Was upgraded to bigger room, which was actually quite big and very comfortable, although gets faint traffic noise.. was upgraded on room.  Huge beg, Plush bathroom, coffee maker, fridge… very comfortable stay overall.. would definitely recommend..!

Clean, modern rooms with everyday cleaning if You wish to. Hotel reception stuff is friendly and helpful. Great contact with no problems. Location is close to center and main tourist attractions only 30min walk to Acropolis.

We thoroughly enjoyed our 7 night stay in the middle of April. The room was large, clean and comfortable. The staff were friendly and helpful, and the location was pretty much perfect. Close to the metro and within easy walking distance of the city centre, with plenty of restaurants nearby. I would definitely stay again.

The room was very clean a welcoming. So was the guy in the reseption.
The balconi is bigger than the room and is nice to sit in at evening.
Locted a few blocks from the Metro. Many bus stops near by.
Not a walking distance from the akropolis area.

Nice hotel, the lobby area and the room were very clean, the hotel is located relatively close to the center and the metro station is really close, Harris was very kind and welcoming, was available for any question and was always right to help. I will definitely come again.

I can’t say enough good things about this hotel. Hotel Frida has been one of the best things about our stay in Athens. We booked 6 nights and wouldn’t have minded staying 6 more. The folks who run the hotel are incredibly nice. The hotel is very clean, and a great bang for your buck. You can walk to almost anywhere you need to go, but it is a longer walk. We didn’t mind so much because we saw so much cool stuff, it was a great way to explore the city. Just for reference we spent a lot of time in the hip Exarcheia neighborhood (10 min walk), took a taxi to the Vouliagmeni beach (18 euros) and the Acropolis (10 euros or so) and walked to the Playce board game cafe (30 mins). It’s been a great trip, and having hotel Frida as a home base was a big part of why it’s been so great!

Nice, clean, affordable, silent good place, of you are looking for quite place to sleep after a long journey.
If you looking for socializing, this ain't a place.
Nice and clean hotel near the city center!
Amazin location and service. I had a great experience.
Cheap hotel, 20 minutes walk from the center.

Lovely apartment easy access to Athens centre. Supplies put in the fridge from katarina the owner as we had a late flight in. She also booked our taxi from the airport for us and gave us loads of helpful tips to get us set for a good stay

Fabulous apartment with all amenities. Very handy for city sights. Taxi from here to central Athens inexpensive but you can also walk to the archaeological museum and get on one of the sightseeing buses or take the local bus service. Great if you want to feel local rather than be a tourist.

We stayed here with our family for 4 days. The apartment is so comfortable, clean and very nice decorated. It has all the amenities you will need and more. Located very close to the city sites. The hosts are the most amazing people, helped us with everything we needed and gave us many useful tips. We left with the best memories and surly will return soon.
Very good apartment. Plenty of space to store his suitcases. Nice staff. The inner city can be reached within minutes by taxi.
It is really really a nice place. The owner is so kind and helpful. It think is the best accomodation in Athens.

Very friendly staff. Clean room in general.  But too hot. Had to turn on the ac to save ourselves from hell's gates! The handcuffs where fun, until my hubby started having trouble unlocking them. Overall, it was a fun experience.  The friendly staff deserves all the credits!
Love has no limits ... in love in bed and in wine and dish tasting there are no rules simply dream explore choose your own experience and enjoy recommented ❤
Hello I want to book my room with my wife and I on February 14 how much room rent one day
Too expensive for my taste
Super
Great location for the money paid- good value. Clean and well organised for check in. Good AC!
Nice room with private bathroom. Pros: clean, comfortable bed and pillow, spacious bathroom. Cons: inefficient ventilation (high humidity inside the room).
Amazing, clean and tidy👍

An ideal apartment for someone who would like to visit Athens. The means of transport serve to visit all the museums of Athens and of course the historic center of the city where it is very close to you. The owners were very helpful in everething we asked them and their hospitality was wonderfull. The house was very clean too.  I will visit soon Aristotle's studio apartment.
The best Airbnb in the city of Athens! Thank you for your hospitality!

Beautiful small, cute apartment in a quiet place, for a stay of one person as well as a couple. We didn't miss anything, and Aris and Anna were kind and helpful in everything we needed, they helped us immediately, even with information on how to move to the center of Athens. Very close from the center, even on foot it's a nice walk around 35 minutes there. Lycabettus and Areos field also very close.
Quiet neighborhood, beautiful apartment, excellent service!

Beautiful apartment, clean, in a quiet place for a peaceful stay. Aris and Anna were kind and helpful in everything we needed, they helped us immediately, even with information on how to move to the center of Athens. Very close to the center, even with walking is a nice walk around 35 minutes there.Lykavittos and field of Ares also very close.

Customer service A1 from Jennifer! My sister and I were on vacation for her birthday, and we were greeted by Jennifer. The rooms are small but very clean and comfortable, so if you want something larger, book a larger room or go elsewhere. I absolutely love the decor. Desserts looked delicious. I like that you are able to have a drink of coffee☕️ or alcohol 🍹 before bed.  A suggestion is to book your own transportation through Uber(will save a lot of headaches at the end of the day, and you will know your price upfront).  Whoever was at the front desk at the end of our stay was a little rude in how she spoke to us about the transportation mixup, so minus 1 star. It's all about how things are said. Overall, I would definitely stay again.

I stayed at the Q Hotel for 4 nights and it was a divine experience. The staff was friendly and helpful - the breakfast was nourishing with a wide variety of sweet & savory options and included a fresh made coffee drink every morning (I suggest the freddo cappuccino!) The neighborhood was quiet and charming, with good street art and even better food. The bed was incredibly comfortable, which was a happy change from my stay on the more rustic islands! The rooftop balcony made my entire trip complete, a perfect place to bring dinner back to or enjoy a bottle of wine with friends. At one point there was an accordion player roaming the streets below as I was enjoying the balcony and it felt like the most magical european experience. The Q is close to the metro, but I opted to walk to & from the city center each day, and never once felt unsafe as a solo woman traveling. 10/10 experience, I will be staying here again!

We stayed here twice on our visit to Greece, in between going to Sifnos island. The first stay was top tier - floor 3 I think we were absolutely brilliant. No complaints.
The second stay was not as good, as the room on floor 1 wasn’t as nice, it was a lot smaller and also we could here a couple of people in the room next door who were talking long into the night. I don’t blame the hotel as you do get rude guests every now and then who don’t think about other guests but a shame the walls were thin enough to here. But a cool trendy hotel which is only a 20 min walk into Athens City Centre. Great value as well.

What a great find! This is a fab hotel in a very cool neighbourhood. It is a little walk from a metro, but there are plenty of buses which you can take. It was nice to get out of the tourist areas of athens to a quieter part. The service was great, nothing was too much trouble and the rooms are funky and exactly what's needed for a city break!

I had such a fantastic time at this hotel. Situated in the coolest neighborhood, close to all the sightseeing I wanted to do but also filled with its own history and personality, I can't wait to return and stay here again so I can explore more thoroughly.

We had an excellent exprerience in that appartment. Look better than the photos are posted. With all the amenities we needed, and more than that. Is so close to Athens centre, and the communication with the owners was so easy with a friendly approach.  We”ll come back for sure.
Excellent space, comfortable and in a very good location. An excellent choice.
Beautiful house, on Alexandras Avenue. It is fully equipped with everything you need.
The apartment provided me with everything I needed. Excellent choice!!!!
The hotel's and rooms' design is great, location is good. I loved the hill just beside it. Looks like the highest point in the center of Athens. The view from it is spectacular.

More than expected.
Brand new hotel, many shops around.
Bedroom very clean.
Kindness, availability from desk employees  Peter and Fotis.
To be booked for price/quality rate.

The neighbourhood is away from the main tourist track and, if you walk towards the PAOK stadium, it's a bit rough. It's a few kilometres away from the main centre, but people should be aware of that before they press "Book".

Rooms are nice, comfortable bed and a decent bathroom. Balcony overlooked the local open air cinema / theatre, which was entertaining ! Buses are two minutes walk away and the metro's about ten minutes away.

If you want to get away from the tourist honey pots, then it's a good choice, I'd happily return next time I'm in Athens.
