The neighborhood was very animated, with nice restaurants and clubs,  close enough to the major tourist sites even by foot. The host was very helpfull and wellcoming.

Will definetly stay there again if I visit Athens again in the future.

We got here for our honeymoon!🌹

Do not miss the breakfast  here!
The Greek breakfast platters by Helen are outstanding! It has a homemade touch to it.
The rooms are beautiful cozy and with automatic curtains and light controls.
Really unique stay!
The staff are really sweet and friendly
And helpful.Great vibes!!

They also have some cute free tea samples and sweets by the bar counter.
The pool was right outside our room however to cold to take the plunge, but very tempting.
🥂

Amazing.

Well surpassed my expectations. I was a bit skeptical due to other reviews complaining about the location. It ended up being the perfect oasis to go escape after busy day exploring the city.

The metro station is steps away and you are only one stop from the Monastiraki neighborhood.

The staff were beyond hospitable and everyone was so nice and accommodating. One day we were heading out early to The Acropolis so since we were going to miss breakfast they made us delicious sandwiches to go! The next day we did get to try the breakfast and it was amazing. Beautiful platter and eggs to order.

The courtyard is dreamy and the pool refreshing.

The rooms are amazing and well stocked with water, coffee and craft soaps.

Thank you NOT! Made our stay unforgivable.

We a great stay at Not Hotel Athen!

Perfect for a short stay to visit the city, close to everything, great atmosphere and the rooms are amazing. The hotel itself has been renovated with great industrial vibes while respecting the initial structure.

Rooms are large, with everything you need (fresh water being setup every day).

Staff is very friendly - maybe too much for the bar staff- and always happy to help. We had a small issue with our bathroom at the beginning of the stay, they fixed it in few minutes. Very proactive and professional staff.

Small disappointment regarding the bar/restaurant menu, it is quite expensive given what can be found around (glass of wine 10€ and coke 4,5€). Regarding breakfast, it’s very good if you stay for two days, then it lacks of diversity and we did not even take the last one after 5 days.

Very surprised at the negative comments regarding the location, it’s outside of the craziness of Athens, while being short walking distance.

5* well deserved.

I recently stayed at Not Hotel in Athens, and we were blown away by the experience. Every single detail of the hotel felt so authentic, giving it a unique charm that you don't find often.

The staff were incredibly kind and helpful, always there with a warm smile and ready to assist with anything we needed. Their hospitality made our stay even more enjoyable.

Our suite was absolutely wonderful – cozy, elegant, and just perfect for relaxing after a day of exploring Athens. We had a balcony that overlooked the pool, providing a serene and picturesque view that we loved. The authenticity of the hotel is evident in every aspect: the stone and wood used throughout, the well-thought-out amenities, the structure of the buildings, the charming windows, the stylish refrigerators, and the comfortable beds. It's not an ordinary hotel; it's something special. During our stay, we and other guests we didn’t know felt like one big family, which added a warm and welcoming touch to the experience.

The food at Not Hotel was another highlight. Every meal was a delightful journey through authentic local flavors, making our stay even more memorable.

Additionally, the hotel is located in a safe area and is within walking distance (15 minutes) of many key attractions, making it a convenient base for exploring the city.

We highly recommend this hotel to anyone who appreciates an authentic hotel experience.

Amazing hotel full of unique and thoughtful details. The hotel is in a great location and it's easy to walk to many places in the centre of Athens, yet the courtyard offers a luxurious and peaceful oasis away from the hustle and bustle. The room was comfortable and felt like it was individually designed for us. The food was also lovely and they offer fantastic vegan options.

All of the staff are friendly and attentive, and went the extra mile for us to make sure we had the perfect experience (thanks again for helping us make our flight to Kefalonia!).

The apartment is located at the famous area of Gkazi with many restaurants and bars. 10 min walking from acropolis/parthenon and 3 min walking from Metaxourgio subway station. Very vice design, very clean and the owner is very friendly.

This apartment is perfect for a solo traveler. The place gets almost completely blackout dark, even during the day. It's perfect for someone who needs to reset their jetlag.
It's in a busy area full of bars, so be ready for noises all night, but it's a part of its charm.
The hosts are amazing!
Amazing loft! Great experience and superb hosts. 10/10!
Modern flat, close to city center, clean, with all the utilities. This apartment has everything necessary to make your stay as comfortable as posibile. I recommend this place.
Very, very nice and convenient And always fantastic service, recommend it! I’m back again in April!!! Excellent 😎

I have stayed in Photo story apartment many times. Savvas is a great host, very kind and always willing to help. Apartment is very comfortable, clean, very well equipped and with a nice balcony that you can see Acropolis also. The view also from the terrace at night is simply amazing! Location is super, very close to city center and metro station, while also is in a friendly neighborhood, with no noise and full of restaurants and bars. I highly recommend photo story apartment ++++

I have recently stayed in Photo story art studio apartment and I have really enjoyed all of it! It is very cozy, comfortable, clean, very well equipped -it had even a baby crib!!- and has an amazing view from the balcony too. Location is also excellent, very close to city center and metro station, while also is in a friendly neighbourhood, full of life with plenty of choices of restaurants and bars, but at the same time not at all noisy. Savvas is also a great host, very kind and always there whenever you need any kind of help. Strongly recommend it !!!

Great apartment and hosting. We really enjoyed our vacations in Athens.
Very comfortable and super clean apartment with amazing view.
High recommended accommodation for any guest

Apartment is ok, looks bigger on the photos but in fact smaller in reality.  Our internet didn't work for the whole 3 days. As we tried to reach someone from the team there, but  noone answered or fixed the problem.  Very disappointing.
I was a guest togethter with B. Fenner and internet was NOT available and no service and no fixing of the problem . Thanks for this bad reaction.
Customer service is not your strength and also not a critic
So pleasant place , super clean, great location and excellent communication with the host.

The house was very big and comfortable. All the furniture was new and very high quality. Very close to the center of Athens and the acropolis. The metro was very close by foot. The host helped us with everything we needed. It is worth noting that the house had Netflix as well as coffee pods.

Unbelievable three-storey maisonette, very large, comfortable, very modernly decorated, fully equipped with everything we needed and in a quiet place. The communication manager next to us in whatever we needed at any time and moment. Simply perfect and we will definitely prefer it again.
Gorgeous

It was a mixed experience. The apartment has a good location, near the metro station, and it was adequately equipped. However, it was really hard to communicate with the owner. I tried to contact them several times via email and phone to confirm my reservation but I could not get ahold of them. Finally, I could reach them an Whatsapp. For some reason I did not receive the code for the door beforehand (this was not necessarily their fault), and it took at least 1,5 hours until I could reach someone and get in to the apartment which was really uncomfortable.

I travel all over around word, and this experience was as awful as I ever had. While, the apartment is clean and has a good location with an attached private backyard. Management is unprofessional and doesn't care if something happens to you. So, I went out of an apartment in this lovely backyard, and than I realized that the door was locked because there is no handle outside.  I stayed more than 3 hours with no help, manager Maria, who I called many times, tell me that she is 3000 km away, promised to relieve me but finally, I escaped from my neighbor's door.  While she promised to send me the owner to help open my apartment,  I had to deal with a door service technician who I called myself and was requested to pay 50€ for opening the door, which was insane. I DO NOT recommend this apartment because the management doesn't care if something happens with their customers.
The management is unprofessional and non-responsive

The host is lovely
The apartment is very pleasant, clean and very practical with our children.
Next to the metro station (acropolis...)
We loved it, the little shaded terrace is perfect and put us in a cocooning atmosphere.
I highly recommend.
Thank you for everything.

I've lived in really bad apartments (unfortunately), but Lambda Vintage has surpassed everything
Looked different than in the pictures, everything was damp and super loud. Ultimately we had to change apartments

The apartment was very close to all the “must see” places of Athens and close to transportation. The apartment was extremely clean, spacious and the beds were very comfortable. The hosts were very nice and left us some food, drinks and Greek goodies for our stay! they also provided advice on the best places and restaurants to visit. I would give more starts if I could! Would definitely recommend and would definitely return for a second stay!

My family and I really enjoyed our stay at BMV Apartments. Christos was a great host. He was very accommodating as we landed quite early and he had our rooms ready when we arrived. The complimentary snacks, fresh fruit and water was a great surprise. The location is perfect. As you are in walking distance of all of Athens attractions but also out of the busy city. We will be returning when we next say in Athens.

Chris, host was fantastic he told us how to get to all the local sites. The apartment was spotless and the bowl of fruit with the basket of tasty treats were an excellent surprise. I would recommend these apartments and am definitely going back again next time I am in Athens.

It’s been a long time since I stayed in a condo with such aura and class. Many thanks to the Hosts; both were extremely polite, friendly and helpful. There were also some treats in the apartment on the first day we arrived! I could not recommend BMV Apartments enough! Can’t wait to visit Athens again.

In a great location, in the city center! Very clean and beautiful! The hosts (Christos & Eugenia) so friendly and welcoming... Warm welcome and willing to help us with our every need!
With a big smile we renew our stay there and on our next visit to the city!

Congratulations to the owners . It is in a very convenient location . Walking distance to many good restaurants. The apartment was very clean in detail. Nice furniture, very comfortable. You get a lot of value for the money you pay ! I highly recommend.
Congratulations!!! Exceptional apartment, facilities and location! Very clean, perfect bed and shower! Amazing stay!
Excellent location, restaurants nearby, nice balcony and very clean property

Excellent rooms fully equipped! Spotlessly clean! Divine mattress! In the most lively area in the center of Athens! All amenities such as supermarkets, gastronomy and nightlife within walking distance. I highly recommend it.
Right location, in one of the liveliest restaurants and entertainment areas, amazing bed, very clean, fully equipped, with nice balconies and all the hot spots at your feet. I highly recommend it!
An excellent option to stay in Athens with a central location which is quiet and also not far from the main touristic area. And Efi the landlady is such a darling and very helpful and reliable.

Be aware, this is not a regular hotel, it is aimed at a particular clientele, if you want to sleep in a strip club or a brothel (literally) this is for you. It is not an adult “themed” hotel for a bit of a uniqueness or difference, it’s exactly for the purpose it appears. Just so you know.

Excellent hotel. Highly recommend for any couples who want to relax in a cozy place. There's very nice air conditioning, wonderful staff, and the room was very good, with an excellent breakfast every single day.

We have requested prior to booking that the room be deep cleaned, as the hotel allows smoking at the rooms. They've done an excellent job and we have not noticed any foul smell. Our room was honestly more than what we wanted, and it was excellent.

The view of the city from our floor was also very nice, and we enjoyed the landscape.

Only gripe I have is that not all the staff understand English to the fullest extent, which can lead to some awkward interactions and miscommunications. While not a big deal for the most part, try speaking clearly and with separated words for the best experience (in English).

Location is excellent and a 6 minute walk from the metro station. Only issue I personally had is that in Athens pedestrians are not the priority, so exercise caution crossing the road to the metro.
Great stay! The service from the staff was fantastic, always checking in to make sure everything was fine and aiming to make sure the stay was fantastic.
Nice hotel at the center of Athens!! Perfect for private times and amazing themed rooms!! 100% recommend.

Clean and cosy rooms, staff really nice and helpful . Great hotel for not exeggerated price.
Location also wonderful, nearby you can find good cafeteria and restaurants with delicious food. City square is located about 1.5 km on foot from this place.

Great apartment, spotlessly clean, great host!
Recommended!

I got my personal room and bathroom here. No issues at all. Location is excellent, all the top 10 sites are within a 30 minute walk. Streets were clean and safe, and some great food and bars options nearby. The rooftop bar has a truly exceptional view and friendly service. Would recommend to other friends.

Unfortunately I can't give 5 stars because the bathroom always flooded after people had a shower. But the position is excellent, the staff is very nice and friendly and the rooftop offers you an amazing view of the acropolis and the other archeological sites. It also has a bar with music where you can meet and talk to people at night.

We stayed in a private Acropolis view room. The room itself and balcony were very nice. However, the washroom was on the smaller side and the mattress was pretty awful. It felt like sleeping on a few old springs. Staff were all very friendly and approachable with answers to all our questions. The location is great. It is in proximity to all popular tourist attractions and the Metro. We opted for the breakfast package with our stay and it was the only regret we have. It was pretty terrible. Other than the breakfast and mattress, we thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
Perfect location. Quite area however there’s no soundproof in my private room and could hear my neighbours easily. Bed was very cozy. Water pressure and temperature was excellent.
It is a hostel but we stayed in a separate 2 people room. Acropolis view from the room was just amazing. You have a terrace in your room. Maybe one of the best views in Athens 🤩

Rooms were clean and comfortable - and large by European standards. Food and service at rooftop restaurant were fantastic. Front desk staff - in particular Rolland, but other staff as well - frequently went above and beyond to provide exceptional service. It is clear to see that this is a very well-managed hotel. The location is not the best, but it's not bad - and there is a stop for the "hop on, hop off" bus just outside.

Our stay at the Fresh Hotel was a delightful surprise. Arriving with high expectations, we were initially disappointed with our room, which lacked comfort despite the balcony. However, the hotel's willingness to accommodate our needs was exceptional. Upgrading to a "chill room" with an outdoor shower was an absolute game-changer. The new room was stylish, refreshing, and truly elevated our experience.
The breakfast spread was impressive, offering a wide variety of delicious options to start the day right. But what truly sets this hotel apart is its exceptional service. The staff was friendly, attentive, and went above and beyond to ensure our comfort.
The pool area was an oasis in the city, providing a welcome escape from the heat. The ability to order drinks poolside added to the relaxation factor.
The hotel's location is convenient, with the city center just a short walk away. While the surrounding neighborhood in the opposite direction leaves something to be desired, the hotel itself is a sanctuary.
Overall, we highly recommend the Fresh Hotel and would undoubtedly return on our next visit to Adams. It's a place where you can truly unwind and enjoy your stay.

Great experience in chill out room with the shower on the balcony and the view to Acropolis. Astonishing furniture, spacious. Very good breakfast, fresh orange juice, good coffee.
Good rooftop bar. Swimming pool small and cold but still good to have it. Great service. Walking distance to main attractions. We are very very satisfied.

Great location. Noisy at times from the streets but your in downtown Athens. From the states and first time in Athens. VERY good breakfast. Fresh juices, honeycomb. The works. Pool is small, but the rooftop has a great bar with a view and great drinks/food. Friendly helpful staff. Would stay again.

We were really impressed with this hotel!  It had everything you could imagine for a downtown location. Good gym, sauna and lovely clean and fresh facilities. Furnishings were gorgeous and well thought out designs. We had a suite and found it through the Marriott site as we are members. Truly unexpected value!

Great hotel, made by the staff. There was one blunt woman on reception when we arrived, but all the others were wonderful.

There was a nice chap there most of the time and he couldn’t do enough for us. The cleaners,  restaurant staff, and especially the ladies working at (a terrific) breakfast every morning made such a difference.

Can’t believe the reviews moaning constantly. We stayed several nights, so let me address a few.

The lift was fine. Wasn’t particularly slow. The pool was great and it has to close at some stage during the day to be cleaned. You could swim in it and look across to the Parthenon. Cool! The food was good, including that we purchased in the top floor restaurant. Yes, the rooms are a little dated, but they are clean and spacious and importantly the shower and bed were decent.

What more could you ask…

Finally, Athens is a working city, populated by several million people in the urban area. It will have ‘rough’ areas. But the hotel isn’t in one, as some reviews say. It’s just off a main shopping thoroughfare. We walked back to it, late, every night. There are homeless people, but they didn’t even talk to us, let alone threaten us.

Personally, I felt safer late at night than I do walking through my home town in England. Also, while we were there, we saw wonderful people helping them with food and especially water.

This part of the city has almost a grid pattern and there are plenty of alternative routes to walk to and from the hotel on. We walked past them, as I say, many times.

If you want a solid hotel, with great staff, smashing breakfast, rooftop views, in the centre of a vibrant, colourful, friendly city, then I think you could do a lot worse. We liked it very much.

Recently, I stayed in the Novus City Hotel in Athens, Greece, for 6 nights. The hotel's location is awesome, just 200 meters from the metro station. The room is clean and provides all modern facilities. The buffet breakfast contains a lot of options, and the food quality is very good. The rooftop restaurant gives a clear view of the Acropolis at night, and I recommend having your dinner once to enjoy the view with Greek food/salad. Last but not least, jelly chocolates at the reception are an excellent way to welcome the guests to the Hotel. Overall, I found the Novus City Hotel to be very good for travelers and recommended it for your stay in Athens, Greece.

Unsafe area!!! Every local we met told us this is the most unsafe area in the city. One block up there are homeless drug addicts all around. They didn’t seem aggressive but it was unsightly and sad. This hotel looks very nice on the surface but everything is cheap underneath. The woman at the front desk was very rude unfriendly and unhelpful. All of the other staff were excellent especially the front of house manager he was very nice. The upstairs restaurant pool and view were all great. the food was good and portions were huge. The furniture in the room was falling apart and there’s a window in the shower!? 🤷🏽‍♀️ our key deactivated every day and we had to go to the desk to reactivate it. The elevator is extremely slow but it runs fine and is clean.

We stayed here for two nights before starting our island hopping tour. The rooms are clean and spacious. The breakfast ample with a good variety. It is in a bit of a down trodden area but we turned right out of the hotel as well as left and wasn't threatened, just very sad to see so many lonely people

Location  is not great from the point of view of security being 200m from a drug addiction colony.

However  the stass are fantastic and gave obe approach warnings not to turn right when leaving the hotel and only turn left.

The pool has a great view of the Acropolis at night

This hotel is great if you are staying a couple of nights in Athens. The beds are comfy, the cleaning is great and everyone is super friendly. They will hold your bags if you have a later flight in the evening. The location is sensational to all the laneways which are full of restaurants and shops the atmosphere at night is buzzing. The view from the their rooftop is pretty spectacular too, we grabbed a beer and watch the sunset. Also walking distance to the acropolis.

Excellent Location for visiting Athens. Breakfast was just a delight. Our Room was very comfortable and had a great view from our room over Athens. The added bonus of the Roof Terrace was a wonderful area to relax after a busy day site seeing. The view of the Acropolis in the Evening is just wonderful.

We ended up booking the last available room at this hotel at the last minute after our first hotel was a bad experience, and it was a great decision! Our room had a balcony with an incredible view of the Akropolis and the bed was comfortable. The room was a bit tight, but manageable. There was some noise from the rooftop above and people moving around in the hall, but nothing too bad. The staff was incredibly kind and helpful and the location is convenient to the city center without being too busy. Loved this hotel!

Great hotel, perfect Location, amazing views to the Partenón from the rooms and great service. Anastasia and Anano from the kitchen serve delicious greek breakfast witth an amazingly kind and warm attitude. Only thing to improve is the air conditioning in the breakfast room. It was too warm there, but it should be easy to fix. All other aspects of the hotel are perfect, people in the reception also very kind . Great hotel in a great place !

We stayed at Arion the end of May.  While the room was a little small the bed was very comfortable and the staff was nice and helpful .  The breakfast was great and Anastasia was so accommodating in getting anything you asked for. You cannot beat the location.  Walkable to shopping and eating.  We would definitely stay here again!

Although located in a difficult area with many drug addicted lying on the streets, this hotel is well-kept, quiet, tidy and clean. The staff is very friendly and does their best to satisfy the clients' wishes. They do not offer breakfast but the coffee shop Dodici can be accessed by means of a passageway directly from the hotel. Besides the neighbourhood is full of coffee shops and eating facilities. The hotel is very close to the Metaxourgeio metro station and the OSE railroad station. The hotel has a favourable price-performance ratio.

A staple for me in the last 4 years, whenever available.
Never had a bad experience here; top notch cleanliness and very polite staff.
The surrounding area can be dodgy, especially at night, but at the end of the day this is a central point of a touristy metropolis.
Considering the price tag, couldn't recommend it more.

For the special rate of 56 euro for my overnight stay I was very satisfied 3 important things for me number 1 rooms were immaculately clean . 2. Front of staff Valentina and the Gentlemen are always friendly and welcoming and extremely helpful I was offerded a late complementry check out also  as it's also  my second visit to the Minoa. Regarding the Location is great and a few minutes walk to the Metro to connect you to Synigma square Acroplis ect, some reviews have implicated the surronding neighborhoods it's Athens harden up . I had no proplems this time around or on my last stay.
Arrived late (1am) and needed a place to sleep for one night, great 24hr check-in, clean rooms, very friendly and helpful people. Good value at the price point
Definitely good services, very close to the metro station, good price 💯 but back streets are dangerous u need to be careful

Amazing little hotel, only a 5 min walk from Metro stations. Great buffet breakfast and the best reception staff ever!  Area has its ups  and downs but felt safe the whole time.Local restaurants are also a must.. If you love city breaks this is the hotel for you..

TERRIBLE HOTEL EXPERIENCE

Our group of 100 students had a distressing stay at this hotel.

Firstly, the hotel is located in a very unsafe area of Athens, rife with junkies. These individuals roamed the streets, frequently engaging in theft and yelling at all hours. We could even see them openly using drugs from our balcony.

Upon entering our rooms, we were met with a foul odor of mold and cigarettes. Opening the balcony door was not an option due to the pervasive smell from outside.

We were forced to order food to the hotel, as it was unsafe to walk the streets, even in the evening.

The room itself was filthy. The sheets and mattress were stained with vomit, urine, and other substances. There was hair everywhere, the mattress had cigarette burns, and there were holes in it.

Housekeeping was extremely rude and disrespectful when we requested new sheets. The receptionist was unhelpful and even yelled at us for inspecting the bed.

At least 10 other rooms reported bedbugs. Proceed with caution!

The locks one some of the rooms were not working, the doors could be opened without a keycard.

The air conditioning couldn't be controlled and ran all night, leaving me with a sore throat. Additionally, there was mold around the unit.

Breakfast was mediocre, though the food was fresh.

Overall, this hotel is a tourist trap. Avoid it at all costs!

Had a lovely stay here with the family . Staff were lovely, very pleasant and ever so helpful . Hotel room was very clean , fresh towels etc daily . Breakfast was lovely. Metro and other transport within three mins walk from the hotel itself

The hotel is located in the worst neighborhood in all of Greece. Junkies and and homeless people seen roaming arround all the time. I would feel unsafe walking around on my own. The hotel however was quite nice, with a really nice bathroom and a rather spacious room. The food selection was not the biggest but it tasted great. No issues with the staff at all.

The hotel is fine. The apartments are not. While the apartment itself is acceptable, the entrance is horrible, you must walk through a street filled with homeless individuals and filthy and unpleasant, we prepaid for 3 nights and left after the first  losing payment for the remaining nights.

A super safe and super affordable hotel in Athens. I stayed here to be near the Painted Porch (it was under excavation oops), but found this hotel to be a 10-20m walk to all the ancient historical sites.

The front desk person was super nice, it was in a great and safe neighborhood, and I’d absolutely stay there again!

The free breakfast was a little limited but an awesome perk. The AC and wifi was a little hit or miss though

This hotel is only 5 minutes walk to the main square. The staff in the hotel seem really nice. There is a stand in the reception that has a number of leaflets that give a lot of information on Athens, which I thought was a nice touch. The room was very basic, but clean and warm. There is a rooftop terrace which serves breakfast and offers views of the Acropolis. The breakfast was nice enough I have to say. There is a lovely 24 hour bakery 2 minutes down the road which offers lovely home made sandwiches, cakes, break, juices and coffee. 5 minutes walk and you are at the main square which offers a lot in terms of restaurants, entertainment and bars. A really nice atmosphere. As I was only staying here for 1 day, this hotel was perfect and I would definitely recommend this for a basic, nice, clean and safe hotel.

This hotel is amazing. When we first entered our rooms were ready over 2 hours early and very clean, The staff was very kind constantly asking if we needed any replacements or services. On our final day at the hotel we had a flight and the hotel called us a van for the airport and packed us bagged breakfast. Very nice hotel would definitely recommend.

Hotel is ok for a 3 star. Breakfast was good. However in the amenities in their website they clearly list Parking and there is no parking. There is a private parking nearby, where they overcharge you 20 eur per day.
Great breakfast on roof overlooking acropolis, service was good and about a 5/10 minute walk from main square. Rooms were adequate but all a family would need.

The hotel is a walkable distance from the metro station. There are quite a lot of mini marts also nearby.
The room was a little musty, but the service was really nice. All in all, it was of good value.

Avoid at all cost no value for money even as a two star. This is definitely the worst hotel I have ever experienced.

First of experienced one staff member to be rude in his mannerism, you would have thought he paid me to be here.

Rooms are outdated as well as dirty ants spiders probably bed bugs.

I'm not sure if you will get a good night sleep due to the random noise that occurs from the neighbours.

For what you pay the area is not in the best locations in terms of concerns of safety.

Myself is OK however a lady,  elderly couple, or a family with young children. I'm not so sure if that to be the case.

An unease amount of homeless people and alot of people in large amount of people on drugs down most streets.

Feel as though ripped of the price just not worth it because on a walk out you then come back to a none nice hotel.

Yes you do see alot of nice hotels in the same area but the experience changes if you pay that €10-15 more on a better decent hotel at least you feel comfortable when sleeping.

To clarify this was a stop over however your better of not doing that here.

Not sure what else to mention other than don't come here.

One of the best hotels of the city
I got there last minute with my father and sister and it was great.
What can I say,how cleaner it was ,how quiet it was and the staff was amazing.
The hotel was near the city center. I could walk to many places. I enjoyed my stay.
Definitely going there any time I visit Athens.

Upfront this hotel is probably circa 1970 so presents as “dated” however it was immaculately clean, the beds were very comfortable, air conditioning worked well, staff were exceptionally helpful and it has 24hr reception. We only spent 1 night here arriving at midnight and out by 10am. For its budget price it was very good value. Did find some decent places for food within a short walk.

This review is very much based on price point.

The first thing you should know, this is not a good area at all. For myself, I wouldn't care so much. However, I brought my girlfriend and very much wished I had booked into a more expensive hotel in a better area.

The area was very dirty, full of drug use, and definitely questionable for night walking.

Now on the price point of less than 50€ per night in Athens and only a 20 min walk from the Acropolis, this hotel is great!

The rooms are nice (for the price) and the service is good.

Very nice and helpful staff, rooms are small but very clean and neat (in the end we are here to explore the city, not the hotel room). The location is extremely convenient in a short walking distance to all the main sights, restaurants and nightlife.
There is also luggage storage so we were able to leave our bags after the check out since we had a later flight (for free).
The most beautiful thing about this place is the rooftop terrace and the view over the city and Acropolis. Just wooow!
If ever again in Athens, for sure I'd stay here! Thank you so much!

Great location near Monastiraki metro station. Friendly staff, clean rooms, and a wonderful view from the roof garden. We were offered a free upgrade and enjoyed breakfast with a view of the Acropolis. We'll definitely come back!
We had a great view of the Acropolis from our balcony. The breakfast was simple but good and there is a large selection of awesome restaurants just around the corner.

Nice hotel with potential. The beds were comfortabel, we had a balcony to the street, good airco, and showers were good. Rooms are rather old fashioned, in general the hotel could use a renovation though everything still functional. Breakfast was ok, provided all the essentials, and the view from the rooftop terrace was amazing! Enjoy a drink up there. Only some music and more cozy furniture is missing there. All in all good choice for a hotel in Athens!

Really great hotel if you’re looking for something simple (with air conditioning) that is close to everything and has a spectacular view of acropolis. The service was the best, I had to checkout really early at 4am and they had a bag of breakfast for me. It made my day. I would absolutely recommend this hotel and would go back.

It's a very unique low-cost Hotel perfect for a very cheap night in Athens Greece around 50 bucks a night very cheap and it's a good hotel for that price whatever you get that's what you pay for the rooms are decent every room comes almost every room comes with a ball can you so that is good with stunning beautiful views from the sundown and the mountains around Athens Greece decent bathroom shower TV small bar refrigerator plugs by the beds comfortable beds you know decent it's in the center it's literally 5 minutes from do you know these small streets the real vibes from Athens Greece and the nice lobby to chill out what's a good massage you're over there that was the job for you instead of a real massage
The service is great

Hotel was reasonable.
Bakery next door was to die for. Great food and sweets.
Location of hotel - was NOT safe when  walking around at night.
Average 3* hotel.  However the bathroom was filthy. Long black hair on shower walls and all over the floors.  This indicates that the room was not cleaned properly. Good service.

Good value hotel, clean room, nice bathrooms, and decent service with a rooftop bar that has a view of the acropolis  but if you are expecting anything above average than this isn’t the place for you. Just about everything is pretty average for a hotel. My biggest gripe is the WiFi in the hotel. It is atrocious, for the amount of money they make on tourist their WiFi is horrendous. I ran a speed test on my phone and didn’t get more than 5mb download. I couldn’t even load the airport webpage to check in with my flight. I couldn’t use FaceTime and webpages either didn’t load or took minutes to load. Using apps wasn’t as bad but the second you try to use something like chrome or explorer good luck. if you need WiFi for any business don’t even try their WiFi it will not cut it, you will have to use your phones hotspot and hopefully you have an international plan with unlimited data. I guess free WiFi is free WiFi.
Didn’t stay here, but went for brunch. €10 per person. It was a good continental breakfast with hot eggs and coffees.

The immediate surroundings of this hotel were disturbing, disconcerting, and perceived as perilous by city residents. The hotel itself was tolerable, moderately clean, very friendly staff, and has delicious breakfast choices.  No clean or pleasant views, thin walls, and a rudimentary shower with barely a shower pan underfoot. My vacation was soured during my stay there. Good value if you can brave it.

The hotel itself is basic and perfect for travelling on a budget. Very close to everything you need. The immediate area can feel a bit ‘unpolished’ but coming from a city myself I didn’t mind this, and I never had any trouble. The rooms were clean, and fresh towels were supplied each day. The breakfast was basic but everything you might need to get your day started. Staff are very friendly and accommodating. Would recommend if on a budget, but if money is not an issue, there are many nicer, more modern places to stay in Athens.

A good value, basic hotel. The location is good for all the major sights and the city centre, but the immediate area outside the hotel is very run down. Wasn’t an issue for us, but some may find it a bit unsettling.
Rooms clean and comfortable. Very quiet and the staff in the hotel were friendly. Breakfast we thought was good. For the price, it’s a good hotel.

The rooms were decent and did the trick. AC worked amazing. The main issue was the shower did not keep water in well and flooded the bathroom pretty easily, so make sure you open the drain.

The service was great and the staff was always friendly and ready to help us. The breakfast in the morning was fine, better than most US hotel breakfasts but not incredible.

The location for sightseeing was great for the price - only a 20 minute walk from the acropolis and 10 minutes from nice areas to walk around and hang out. However, the specific street the hotel is on is out of the ordinary with the amount of homeless. I walked back later in the night multiple times and saw lots of people getting a free stay outside the hotel. This, in combination with the need for a security guard in the nighttime hours and a police presence on multiple nights makes me reluctant to recommend this. I was satisfied for the price but would not recommend a group of girls stay here and walk down this street at night unless they taxi straight to the door.

I would stay again - good for a frugal traveler.

Hotel rate with breakfast included is great value for the money!
You pay a small tourist tax when you check in but it’s cheaper than Rome or most other European cities.
There is no bar restaurant in the hotel.
The room is basic. Shower is small but lots of hot water. The staff are excellent and help in anyway they can. Breakfast (included for us on Air Canada Vacations) was very good. Location is a 5 minute walk to Omonoias Square and subway and 15 minutes to Ministraki Square.
Wifi included.

The area outside the hotel is rundown and we saw a couple of needle users across the street a block up. There are open businesses on the street with people sitting outside so there are people around. We were there a week and no one ever bothered us! Turn right out of the hotel when walking and the main drag is literally a short block and a half walk. Pay attention to the uneven sidewalks. Security guard on duty at night out front of hotel.

The hotel was quite comfortable to relax in with no any distraction or noise nearby and had the necessary facilities. I liked the way the hotel rooms were organized, and thanks for getting our request well in advance of arrival. Regarding the service cleaning, there should have been slightly better routines, the cleaners are hardly visible to the guests in the hotel, it is good that they are doing their job, but we have noticed that they knock and open the door at the same time without us knowing when they plan to clean our room. The other negative view is that the shower could have been prepared a little better as the water runs out to the basin and onto the floor in some cases without a glass protector or shower curtain. Otherwise, I appreciate the breakfast service at the hotel, they had a very delicious and hearty breakfast that was served every morning, we really liked this and the staff who work there are very nice and calm people.

Me and my partner recently visited this hotel for a weeks stay in Athens. The room was basic and we had booked a double room but instead we were given two single beds pushed together. The comfort of the beds was fine for us both, worth noting here that we are a young couple. The shower was powerful, hot and the hotel provided clean towels. They also provided a hairdryer but the one in our room was broke, I asked the first morning for a new one and they gave us one. There was a mini fridge in the room which was great to keep a couple of bits cool and the air conditioning worked great. The staff were friendly and helped if needed. The hotel is a 15 min walk from the centre which was ideal for us. The breakfast was nice, we are vegetarian and there was enough options for variety thought out the week.

This hotel will be ideal for anybody who wants somewhere to stay while in Athens but are not looking for any luxury.

Great value for money as it's centrally located within bus stops and metro station within few minutes of walk. Staff was generous to change my room from single beds to double bed, as my booking agent erroneously booked single beds.
Note that the lane around the hotels doesn't seem to be safe and be cautious while roaming around at night

i would really not advice going out past midnight because the area is generally known for being dangerous at night. that said, at day time you’re close to the center, the metro, and the big shops. rooms were good and breakfast was great, although the coffee was super watery. they should consider getting rid of the fat balding guy at the reception though.

Cons:
- The rooms were very very basic and the cig smoke would come right into the room.
-tv wouldn't work in any other language.
-Breakfast was not good and if you're not there the first hour, it's heavily picked over
-Bedding is very itchy and towels are hard
-Location I would give a 3 out of 5
-No microwave
-Rooms could be cleaner, but thats mostly a dust issue (note, I didn't open the windows)
-Noise carries A LOT. If there are ppl in the hall, it will feel like theyre in your room

Pros:
- Spacious rooms and BIG bathrooms
-Accept night walkins
-Market/Cafe diagonally across the street
-Has mini fridge
-Air con worked well
-A bit more on the pricier side for what you get, but still not bad
-Staff were not rude. Though we didn't interact with them much

Things to note:
- This area is a bit loud
-This is an area where there are A LOT of the unhoused community. They were not aggressive at all. Some places yell at them, but I don't think they deserved it.
-The train in this area isn't great and is a hot spot for pickpockets
-To get to the busy and social area, you will realize that youre on the outskirt of the area.
Stayed there between 9-11.09.2024 .Friendly staff welcomes you. Rooms ate clean and tidy, free wifi you can use. Close to Monastraki and metro station. Very very good breakfast.

Tiny rooms but clean and tidy.
Good breakfast and friendly staff.
Great location near omonia metro
Lots of food options and shops .

This hotel was absolutely beautiful, clean and very comfortable beds . Maid service was 100% front of desk friendly and very accommodating and on our last day allowed us to keep our luggage at hotel and return just before our flight . It can be very noisy as it is on Main Street but all of hotels are mainly on Main Street . Close to all site seeing if that’s what you are going to see . 100% would return that’s for sure 💜 comes with balcony if requested x
Stayed here for four nights during a tour and found the place to be nice.  Friendly staff and the breakfast buffet is nicely stocked.  I would stay here again most certainly.
Great hotel, nice rooms excellent service, a bonus we arrived at 9:30 am and were allowed to chechin right away. Good location close to main sites , recommended

The hotel itself is honestly very nice and clean, the staff downstairs are very friendly and were helpful to us and even held our luggage and set up a taxi.

The only negative in the room is that the AC in the room did not work at all.

Now the bar is a different story, staff did not seem as friendly. I will say though that the bar is beautiful and offers an amazing view of acropolis that you cannot beat.

However, the staff at the roof top bar were far from friendly. They offer a free voucher for a drink but don’t mention it’s only a cheap wine or bud light beer. They also seem annoyed if you use the voucher and only allow you sit in certain areas. In my opinion, maybe just don’t give out the voucher then.

Even when having drinks another night and paying for them, we found it hard to get service. The servers were dancing and drinking alcohol at the bar while we waiting for a while. I know they were just having a good time and I’m okay with that but when we asked for the anything we got some attitude and I’m not sure why…

Honestly, I would probably stay again due to the location, clean rooms and beautiful view of acropolis. Just giving my input in hopes some things can improve!!

Review is for the restaurant.
You will not be disappointed by the rooftop views. Reserve early to guarantee a table at the edge overlooking the square with unobstructed view of the Acropolis. We arrived just before sunset so got bit day and night views.
Casual to upscale feel and although menus limited the food is delicious. We hand the lamb appetizers and the grouper as the main course. The food was outstanding, possibly the best grilled fish we’ve ever eaten. Have dinner here and you won’t be disappointed.

Another GREAT rooftop bar/restaurant with views of the city and the Acropolis.  The environment is cozy and romantic.  Their drink menu is awesome with specialized crafted drinks made at your table. Friendly staff. Popular with trendy crowd. Highly recommend. We were recommended by a friend to check this bar out and it did not disappoint.

A for Athens is an exceptional hotel that offers a perfect blend of comfort, convenience, and stunning views. Located in the heart of the city, just steps away from Monastiraki Square, it’s the ideal base for exploring Athens’ historical landmarks. The rooms are modern, clean, and comfortable, but the highlight of this hotel is the rooftop bar, which offers breathtaking views of the Acropolis, especially at night. The breakfast is also fantastic, with a wide variety of delicious options. The staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful, making our stay even more enjoyable. If you’re looking for a central location with unbeatable views, A for Athens is a great choice!

We went to the rooftop bar for a sunset drink and the view was magnificent! The staff was friendly however if you just want to have a drink you cannot sit at the table. You can only stay standing near a middle block. I saw the food going past and it didn't appeal to me so I was happy to stand. The cocktails are interesting but if you want a classic you need to ask for it as not on the menu.

Hotel Katerina is outstanding. Exceeded our expectations. We've got a free upgrade to junior suite which was really great. The room was squeaky clean,  comfortable beds and freshly ironed sheets.
The breakfast was very tasty.
The safe hotel parking is extra helpful for the area. Pretty good walking distance to the centre. The staff was really polite and informative. They provided extra care and attention.  We would definitely stay here again.

Has aircon, location not that good, but Easy to get on the hop on, hop off bus route! Metro close by. Super breakfast. Extremely friendly staff. They go the extra mile. Giannis the Manager, Krista’s and Giorgos. We were able to leave our luggage in the reception area. Also has a laundromat across the road!
Robin and Dee from South Africa
its okay just the fact that homeless people are a few meters away from the hotel wich would be fine if they didnt smell

Staff all lovely and helpful with organising taxis. Breakfast bar has reasonably good variety. The rooms do seem outdated, they could probably change the curtains and paint the brown wooden furniture to a lighter colour to make it look fresh.
There is a metro nearby, but you could also walk 20-30 min to the centre. Some of the area seems dodgy, so we walked one day and took taxis another day. It costs no more than €10.
Personel at this hotel are very kind and supportive. The whole time was very clean and the breakfast is truly good.

STAY AWAY

I stayed there last night !
bed was missing a plank (as the bed was bending) that was the balcony,I didn’t bother and I did it myself.
in the evening there was a heavy engine working all night (after midnight I arrived) that wouldn’t let us sleep. I tried to call to ask what’s happening but the phone was not working as well.
The flush was  not working in the bathroom as well in result to all the  room smell after I used the bathroom without being able to flush.The cleaning lady this morning said that on the high floors the flush doesn’t work in general.
if you check my profile I am not a complainer but despite the fact that the young lady at the reception was very kind, the manager on the phone was attacking and lying that everything works properly besides the phone which he said “in busy hotels these things happen”.

very disrespectfully he said “what do you want,I’ll give you 10€ discount” .
just describing my experience

I missed my bus to Karditsa when I landed in Athens and a friendly taxi driver brought me here.   I asked to take me to a clean inexpensive spot to sleep for the night.   I was not disappointed.  The staff spoke English and understood my poor Greek.  The room was clean and the balcony was nice.   My only complaint was that it seemed a little out of date and the air condition was not working.   Thankful for the nice breeze that night

Not a good spot. We told a girl the first night our room had bed bugs and asked if we could be moved. To her credit she was very nice and said they didn’t have any available that night, but the next night we could. We then asked the next day about changing and the owner said no. He also wouldn’t offer us any sort of refund for the bed bugs, and told us if we wanted a different room the following day (we had reserved for 3 nights) it would cost us more money. He also didn’t seem even slightly surprised / caught off guard when we told him about the bed bugs. The younger girl we dealt with was great and you could tell the next day she felt really bad when the owner showed zero sympathy. Shes the only reason we gave a 2 star rather than 1 for service, but I would strongly recommend staying anywhere else.

We are very happy to were there. The Service there is very good . They helped us many many Times. The 2 Girls which worked at the Reception are very friendly . Special Thanks from us to both and also a great Thank you to the girl from the Roomservice , it was excellent . Greetings from us to all There.
Carmen & Robert Ilias

I don't know why this Hotel does not have 5 stars, because this Hotel exeeds all of my expectations.

Super air conditioner, nice, clean room with view of THE ACROPOLIS and oh man, the reception person is very friendly.

Excellent Hotel in Athens for this price!🤩

Let's start with the location, brilliant!! Right in the center of Athens, by the market and walkable distance to all the tourist spots.
It's a small hotel with a super small team, but everyone was kind and sweet.
The room i stayed in was on the smaller end, but who cares,  not moving in, just staying for a vacation. More important,. The room was clean and smelled nice, and had a huge balcony.
Definitely a good choice.

Carolina hotel is right in the heart of Placa area, nothing fancy, but you only need a shower and a good night sleep. The rest is out and about in Athens. Very friendly and kind people at the front desk and they helped us out to sit before seven in the morning so we can have breakfast before taking off to the airport. We will be back.

It’s very nice hotel with good price, room is small, neat and pretty good. I love the terrace. Clean washroom. Front desk staff is very friendly and helpful. Breakfast is not bad. Distance from the Monastiraki square is hardly 7 minutes. Lots of nice restaurants and bars in the same street.

Very nice place, clean, bright room, very good breakfast.

Close to the Acropolis, plenty of cafes around.
A fabulous hotel quite clean and very close to all that old Athens has to offer. Great staff. Great rooms and a brilliant breakfast ! Awesome value!

I've stayed here on a few occasions; the outside of the Hotel and the reception is rather deceiving. A simple, old reception desk and a reception area with not much space. However, the rooms are clean and comfortable for one or two people, the toilet/shower are clean with complementary shower gel. The Hotel is in an excellent and very central location and you can't beat the price!
Close to all the attractions. Big room with comfortable bed. Rooms are very clean. Shower small. Breakfast is basic but nice. Friendly staff. Great value for the price paid.

The hotel is a genuine 2-star hotel:
- The room was mostly small, and there was not a good air circulation for it to vent
- The elevator did work but didn’t feel 100% reliable/safe
- We got there after 09 pm and the man who checked us in only asked for our names and gave us the key. At no point did they check our passports 🫨

Good thing was that there was people at the front desk 24/7. However, we didn’t feel comfortable enough to ask for suggestions or anything like that

Breakfast is on site, and though there is not a lot of variety, the products are of very good quality

Greek yogurt was the best I’ve ever tried in my life! And the honey was also amazing

They also offered cold cuts and cheese. Coffee, milk, tea, orange juice. And I think there were also some fruits

The staff in charge of breakfast and cleaning the rooms were very friendly

Place looked mostly clean, rooms were made every day and clean towels were left by default

Athens may be filled with soulless Airbnb rentals, each furnished with the same generic IKEA pieces—but Hotel Cecil is a refreshing exception. Like the city itself, Hotel Cecil isn't perfect, but its abundant charm and old-world character easily overshadow any minor imperfections. The prime location, the vintage Otis elevator, and functional WiFi are just added perks. Will be on the top of my list for my next visit to the city.
Smaller rooms but clean and comfortable. Friendly staff. Continental breakfast. Lots of shopping around and close to lots of things to do and see

A budget-friendly and worthwhile place to stay. Highly recommended. While there's no common area for socializing or a shared kitchen, it's a great spot just for sleeping and conveniently close to the city center.

I was traveling alone. The reception staff were super friendly and always available. I needed to extend my reservation by one day and it was easy. I was in a shared room with 3 other women, so there were two bunk beds and a sink. I felt safe, the door has an electronic lock and you can leave your bags on the second floor. I recommend :)

Not worth the price, can find cheaper, better equipped places.
The terrace on the top looks worse than in the pictures, it's inhospitable, boasts some unusable remnants of a kitchen which this places doesn't have, can't see anything from there. The rooms are very bare, not even a coat hanger there.
A definite upside is that they have very nice bathrooms, clean and modern, with sufficiently but not totally opaque glass between them giving a taste of a kinky atmosphere. They have a spacious locker room for the luggage.

The location was the only good thing about this place but even that can't save it. The upstairs reeked of sewage and mildew. The hallways and bathroom looked like they've never been deep-cleaned a day in their life. Let alone a daily clean. We checked in and checked out within an hour because we couldn't stand the thought of sleeping there.

Not that good, but it matches the one-star classification.
Service is kind, 24 hours available.
Location is pretty central.
Rooms are small and with not good conditions.
Toilets are bad.
Good enough for one day if you are just stopping by.

I have led multiple groups overseas. This is a fantastic hotel. Centrally located. Excellent food rooms are clean and very comfortable. The rooftop restaurant and bar has an unbelievable view of the Acropolis. Highly recommend.

Even though the hotel is an older one, service was excellent - the staff was very attentive and friendly. Breakfast and coffee were excellent and the rooftop view was a fabulous one. The elevators are busy in the morning as can be the breakfast bar at around 8 so get to there early!

Reasonably good hotel overall despite some old outlooks and slowness — your elevator in the peak morning hours can easily take 5 minutes to come. The staffs are helpful and friendly and the breakfast area is simply fantastic. The location is pretty awesome. It would’ve been lovely if the room keeping service included the water refill, especially when we stayed for four consecutive nights.

The hotel location was awesome. The view from.the top floor restaurant was amazing. The breakfast was expansive and delicious. The front entrance is elegant but our room was run down. We had a window that was covered so really did not see daylight until we ventured out. Bed was horrible and shower was challenging.
This was the worst hotel we stayed at in Greece. Avoid room 314 at all cost. It was small, smelled like Goodwill and you could feel every coil on the old mattress. Only good thing was location

I was pleasantly surprised by this hotel. We didn't spend a huge amount of time here, out exploring Athens for the best part of the 2 days. But our room was clean and functional, our bathroom was huge and also clean. The beds weren't super comfy but did the purpose. Once we got the air con working, this was a joy. The hotel is dated but has some great ceiling decorations and character to it. We asked for extra towels and were provided these. The location isn't bad, right near a metro station. It is possible to walk through to the main area of Athens from here also, although you do see some less favourable neighbourhoods. The worst part of this hotel was definitely lugging our luggage up 3 floors on that spiral staircase. It's incredible value for money considering.

You get what you pay for. Old rooms but nice and clean for the most part. Nice warm shower although no hold up top so had to be hand held the whole time, no biggie. Staff were friendly. Location isn’t amazing but only about a 5 minute walk from the metro which takes you anywhere you might wanna see.

Hotel is two-stars, so it doesn’t deserve more than that. Super noisy, both in and outside. Staff doesn’t care about this and keep talking among themselves at high tones. It’s magical but your clothes will smell cigarette smoke, I concur. Bathrooms and toilets are shared. Clean but old. They could be a very very beautiful hotel with high ceilings and antique look of the halls and stairs. But sadly the hotel is not well maintained.

BED BUGS.

My goodness, where to start with this place? First of all, it was infested with bed bugs. We were moved through three separate rooms on two different floors, and each had full-grown bed bugs in them. On top of that the management/desk manager barely spoke English and offered absolutely zero assistance with this issue. They just brushed it off and tried to get more money from us when we had to stay an extra night in order to get our clothes and luggage treated because of this problem. Again, absolutely no help at all. Terrible service in a parasite-infested hotel. Don't go here.

DO NOT GO!!!
Ugly run down filthy hotel nothing like the pictures found stains on the pillows and sheets and smells of cigarettes, showers were tiny and leaking from the faucet which kept flooding the bathroom. Drop ceiling in the room was dipped in the middle almost falling off, Only one power socket in the room and it was juri rigged to the door(not even stuck straight)

Staff were very nice, the rooms were well-updated, the breakfast quite complete, and the roof-top bar and welcome drink (for Accor members) quite pleasant in the evening.
The only issue is when we decided to walk back from Monastiraki at night, which took us through a couple dodgy streets, but nothing of concern.  Overall the location of the property is average.

overall, it was a mixed experience. The room was spacious, clean, and comfortable, and the service from the staff was excellent. However, the breakfast was a major letdown. The food was not tasty at all, and unfortunately, I even got sick after eating it. Honestly, I would have preferred to skip breakfast altogether. While the hotel is a bit far from the main attractions, the quality of the room and service made the stay pleasant. If you’re not too concerned about breakfast, it’s a solid option.

I had a good stay in this hotel, also because I got upgraded to a suite. This room was slightly outdated, but spacious, clean and the bed was good. The breakfast buffet was very nice with a great choice of products. The view from the rooftop pool and terrace is beautiful, with the Acropolis in the distance. The hotel staff is efficient but not overly friendly. The area around the hotel is somewhat sketchy, so we took taxis to move around (which are rather cheap).

Spent 3 days at the hotel after two weeks in Cyclades. Everything was great. The staff were friendly and knowledgeable. Due to the heatwave, we spent all our time in the hotel and enjoyed our time in the rooftop terrace and pool. A great place to prepare for the journey home.
We will definitely be back!

I had the best experience in Novotel! Everything was great! The stuff was unbelievable good in their jobs!The guys in the pool were really helpful and full of joy! They make the start of my day wonderful and really upgrade the whole experience! I will totally visit again this hotel

Best choice we made won't be disappointed. Just very practical with everything you need. Clean rooms good staffs close to sight seeing spots and good price. Outside neighbors a bit run down but hotel itself good, did not bother us. Staff even packed us breakfast for our early departure, so very nice.

Oct 27-Oct 30/24
This was the best hotel for the price. It was less than CA$100 a night for two people (including tax). It also included free breakfast and free cancellation. I have never found one hotel, with this quality and price in any big cities. The most important thing was its location. This hotel is located within walking distances from all major world class attractions, such as museums and the Acropolis. Hundreds of restsurants and bakery shops. If you can walk, you don't even have to take any transportations. For me, the service was good and friendly. The room was clean and tidy. The breakfast was good enough for me.
This hotel was my top choice if I travel to Athens again.

We stay one night at this hotel and it did its job. We use it only to sleep as we were out and about all day.
The staff is friendly, the location is great and it includes breakfast (continental breakfast), with mostly anything that you need (nothing wow, but it was good).
The shower was tiny and it didn’t feel clean :(
The room is a bit outdated and so is the building, but I guess that’s why it’s no pricey for the location!

I spent a week here and I am very satisfied. This hotel serves as a perfect haven for active tourists. Convenient location, air conditioning, private toilets, new towels every day, friendly and helpful staff, good breakfasts - it's all there, and it comes at a pretty good price.

Great value for money.
Hotel is old but VERY CLEAN. Location is perfect, just few hundred meters from Monastiraki square like 5 minutes walk . The staffs are very friendly and helpful. 24hrs receptionist.
Breakfast is good too!! It’s been such a pleasure experience . I will definetly book
again whenever I come back to Athens .

Overall a pleasant experience. Fantastic rooftop bar and restaurant with great food and stunning views of the acropolis. The service staff were all very friendly and d helpful. The breakfast buffet leaves a bit of room for improvement. The rooms are clean and have a comfy bed and seem to be well soundproofed as I did not hear my neighbors which is often a nuisance in some other hotels. Would stay there again.

From reading some of these reviews a week before I arrived I thought I was going to be staying in the worst hotel on earth, the reality was very much the opposite.

The room was a really decent size for a European city hotel! The bathroom was fine, some room for improvement in that area but perfectly adequate!

The reception staff were extremely helpful and friendly, the breakfast buffet was also very good!

The rooftop is the star of the show, we ate at the restaurant twice during our week in Athens, the food there was exquisite and the view is sensational! Also the staff made us feel very welcome and special! I didn't use the pool but other members of my party did and have no complaints but it can get busy on certain days and times.

Location is perfect! The metro is right outside the hotel, which is very accessible! 4 stops to the acropolis, what more could you want?!

Also top tip, there's a cafe just on the corner of the hotel which is actually part of the Stanley, it's the perfect place to get a quick bite to eat!

The hotel is great. Best service. Rooftop facilities are the best!

The bar is too expensive. That's really a shame.
The rooms are okay (superior), bathroom way too small. Minibar with free drinks is actually just water refill daily. Expectstion on that benefit was too high.

Location is okay. Near the subway which is very nice.

The amenity space on the rooftop is the best feature of this hotel. The pool area is really large and very nice. It's very busy so it's not easy getting a spot to sit but it's worth the effort.
The free breakfast on the rooftop is also very good. Very good selection of food and the views are amazing.
The rooms themselves are dated and the bathrooms are so extremely small. One would expect a more luxury feeling for this price point in terms of being modern and larger. The front lobby feels dated as well.
Seems they have spent all their money on the rooftop.
There is a subway right out front so that's helpful but the area is a 5 out of 10 you really need to walk about 20 minutes before you get anywhere nice.

The best thing about this hotel is its rooftop pool that looks on to the acropolis. The showers were also incredible, but the beds weren't particularly comfy.

The food was amazing. We had this incredible orzo and shrimp dish for lunch. It was incredible.

great hotel with super friendly staff, all touristic sites are in walking distance. superb water pressure in the shower. breakfast buffet is large. maybe one little thing they can do is to place a ashtray outside for the smokers. great place to stay when you are in athens

The free breakfast is a joke. It’s disgusting. I will never come here again. This hotel cost too much per day. The food is horrible as far as the breakfast in the morning. too many people and it appears they have never eaten before, but it’s not Greek traditional food very a lot of bread products. The hard boiled eggs are over cooked as an example, very disappointed. I do not blame the help, They were overwhelmed and did the best they could with restocking and turning over tables. The rooftop upstairs bar great place. The views are amazing..

Great location  near to metro but average room quality for the price.
Has many rooms and kept clean.
The roof too has a distant view of the Acropolis. Many restaurants and tavernas nearby also the market is walking distance.

I really didn't like the room I was in, mostly because it didn't even remotely resemble what was advertised on the hotel's website. I was given a very poor room.
Apart from that, sheets were stained, I saw blood and who knows what else on those. The bottom sheet had little holes in it. I'd recommend changing sheets when they're no longer fit for use.
The carpet was full of hair and dirty.
The shower temperature would constantly change, which didn't add to the experience.
Pillows were uncomfortable.
Around 8.30 in the morning, the cleaning staff simply entered my room, like that.

Now, the staff is the only positive note on an otherwise very substandard stay. Staff was polite and helpful throughout the experience and I am very grateful for them.
One note: the rooftop restaurant is beautiful.

The rooms was tiny! Not what you expected regarding the pictures.
The carpet wasn't clean, there were clumps of hair in it. There was a pubic hair still on the toilet.
The bathroom was maybe 1.5m2 big. The shower was tiny tiny corner in a triangle shape to save space. So when you showered you touched that dirty shower curtain and I wished that I could take a shower all over again, but I would touch it again because it was so small (less than 0.5m2).

The breakfast was ok, which gives them 2 stars instead of 1.

Breakfast buffet was excellent and fresh. Best scrambled eggs ever. Loved the espresso machine. Shower decent size for Greece. Elevators were reliable and fast.  Front desk staff very helpful. Do not hesitate to double park in front to load and unload.

I stayed here in 2018 with my family and when re-visiting Athens again this year I immediately booked a stay here as it was perfect then and I am more than pleased to say it is still perfect now!  So central, great friendly staff, fantastic view of the Acropolis from the lovely rooftop bar.  Perfect breakfast – what more can I say!!

I highly recommend this hotel! The cost-benefit is excellent!! Some positive points: the staff is very kind, polite and attentive; the hotel is right in the city center, you can visit the Acropolis and other tourist attractions on foot; the hotel has a terrace with a beautiful view of the Acropolis; the rooms are clean, well-equipped, with good amenities and the room service is great; the breakfast is quite decent.

Whenever I am in Athens, this hotel is my top choice. Conveniently located just a few minutes from Monastiraki metro station, it offers both comfort and accessibility.
The staff are always friendly and courteous, ensuring a pleasant stay. The rooms are consistently clean and well-maintained. Breakfast is simple yet delicious, catering to various preferences. The highlight is the charming rooftop bar, providing a lovely view of the city and the Acropolis.

Great location, amazing view from rooftop bar! Staff is so friendly and helpful. Rooms are very nice; not huge, but have everything you need. Now I know why a friend of mine stays here whenever she comes to Athens!

Everything about this apartment is fantastic—the location, the facilities, and the spacious terrace where you can enjoy the lively music from the tavern downstairs while people-watching.

The team of the property is the most amazing, welcoming and friendly team! 🫰

The apartment has everything you need for a comfortable stay, and it's ideally located near bars, restaurants, dance clubs, Monastiraki station, and sightseeing spots. Just bring earplugs for the noise, and you'll sleep perfectly! We had a wonderful time and would love to return.

We wanted somewhere that would showcase the best of Athens and Live in Athens did just this. Our accomodation was impeccable, clean and comfortable. It would us at the heart of life in Athens. Our kids loved it and so did we. We could walk to all the main attractions. We were surrounded by hundreds of restaurants and bars. We fell asleep listening to the nightlife of Athens. The noise never bothered us. It made us feel part of the life in Athens.

Cody was our best friend. Full of advice. He never let us done once and his advice made a different to our stay.

Wonderful hotel and extremely good value for money. The bed was large and very comfortable and the apartment was large and very clean. They have a stove and microwave so you can cook food. Amazing location in the heart of the city. Will stay here the next time I am in Athens!

Can't go wrong with these apartments. Part of the trend, it's situated at the very centre of Athens.

You're literally at the center point to reach any destination you wish to whilst visiting Athens.

Don't forget to have a look around all the bars and eateries dotted around.

The people who work for Live in Athens are excellent. They’re responsive, clear and kind.

The apartment is also pleasant.

If you don’t party late into the evening though (I’m writing this after 1am listening to drunks cackle, hoot and holler), don’t stay in the Psyri apartment. In addition to a very lively night life (could be a plus for you) they have a space on their roof where you can take your alcoholic drinks. I’m listening to people make a lot of noise scrapping chairs and tables right now right above my bedroom.  If you want to sleep, choose another one of their locations.

You get what you pay for. 200 eur for 6 days, you probably already know this is the cheapest option for a hotel in Athens. Location-wise it's magnificent. Next to the Omonia metro station with easy connection to Acropolis, Plaka, Monasteraki and other sites. I expected rooms to be a bit shabby, so that wasn't an issue for me. The cleaning ladies are super nice and have regularly maintained our room. Slightly hiccoughy WiFi but we have unlimited data in the EU so it wasn't an issue. Air-con worked flawlessly and it was included in the price.
Not bad, considering downtown location, you got everything, prices ok, no bed bugs, water was warm, bathroom clean, WI Fi slow though, but old school hotel is recommended...

Bed bugs. That’s the only criteria that I have in a hotel, that it not have bed bugs. I did get bit by bed bugs here and it’s very itchy & painful now, so I wish I’d stayed somewhere else.  Bed bugs aside, I encountered the following Claridge Hotel:

-there’s only sheets on the bed and an optional old blanket from WWII in the closet
-a tremendous amount of noise
-vagrants/addicts/refugees milling around the area (and pulling their pants down right out front pissing)
-a toilet that continuously ran and bathroom light bulbs that burnt out
-a paint job that hasn’t been touched up since the Berlin Wall was still up
-weak wifi that you have to compete with others to get to a website
-old key system where you leave the metal key with the front desk when you go out.

None of these things would have bothered me enough to cancel the stay except for the bed bugs. Of course I inspected the sheets when we arrived but I didn’t see any so I didn’t mind the 70 year old furniture and old peeling paint.

chances are, if you’re reading this review you already know this hotel is the cheapest option in athens that’s not a hostel, and comes with a private bathroom; you’ve seen the low star reviews & you’re trying to decide if you should chance it & stay here because maybe you think everyone else is a bunch of spoiled whiny complainers.
I’m giving them a generous 2 stars because
1. the staff is nice.  both the helpful front desk & the housekeepers that do clean
2. the convenient location near the train, restaurants, stores, museums, walking distance to Acropolis
3. the cheap price, lowest on the travel booking sites
My spouse permanently revoked my hotel booking privileges after this place.   He said “if you have respect for yourself don’t stay here”. But my kids age 16 & 26 were fine with it.

Location wise, it is very accessible, a few steps away from Omonoia Station. Not to expect inside the hotel since it’s a 2 star, good for sleeping. The Old Omonoia restaurant is just outside and the food was tasteful with a good size of serving.

The 3 receptionist throughout the day were all very kind people. The rooms were alright quality with it needing a fresh lick of paint and upgraded TVs. The air conditioning was old but I saw new boxes of air conditioning in the hallway so I think they will be upgraded. The location was also great with a lovely view of a beautiful fountain and it was right next to great restaurants.

Great attentive and helpful staff, amazing breakfast included. Rooms are very urban as well as location. It's an easy walk to the main areas but one thing I wish we had known was that just a side street off the plaza across the street is a more charming walk into the best areas towards Plaka. We walked the rather dirty & congested main road up & down many times before realizing there are incredible streets and restaurants, cafes along the way.

Feeling at home from arrival to departure. The best choice in Athens; I was very satisfied with their kindness and professionalism. The customer is showered with attention. Upon my arrival, they informed me that they were offering me a free room upgrade, assigning me the suite. The room was perfect, clean, spacious, and equipped with all the comforts—we couldn't have asked for anything better. The staff were always attentive, smiling, and kind. The hotel's location is excellent; you can reach all points of interest on foot without needing public transport. It also has a secure and monitored parking lot. We will definitely return

Very attentive and friendly staff. When we requested anything, it arrived to our room within minutes. Walkable to the Acropolis and many famous landmarks in Athens.

Air conditioning works better than many other hotels we’ve stayed in Europe. The only negative was the doors close pretty loudly so it can wake you up in the room when someone else closes their door, but we would still recommend this hotel to others staying in Athens!

Very disappointing stay!
Hotel is old and the room feels very used. 200 years old thin supper used towels...
We booked a suite from Agoda on arrival received standard room, they didn't want to provide us what we paid for in the beginning..Make sure you get what you booked because it feels like a trick..

Over priced.
Low value for money.
Not recommended

Wonderful stay. The staff at the hotel reminds you what great, friendly service feels like.
Restaurant is wonderful and the breakfast was amazing. Location is perfect with easy walk to everywhere.
Would 100% recommend.

The hotel is a nice budget place, near the centre for a reasonable price. Breakfast is ok, but the same every day, enough not to be hungry.
