The good thing you do not to be  on time, all food is brought even if you are after  the time. The staff is  friendly and really nice, speak a lot of languages. The neighborhood is disgusting, be aware of your suitcases, immigrants and drug addicts may steal if you do not pay attention.

The staff in the hotel is good and the morning breakfast offered is quite tasty as well. The main attraction about the hotel is it's proximity to all major historical and archeological tourist attraction in Athens and it's competitive pricing. The locality around the hotel though feels a little unsafe, especially late at night. The room we used was basic and the decor was old, but everything was fully functional. Do not expect any view kind of good view as most of the rooms face other buildings or the congested street

In the winter (I can't speak for the summer), this is an excellent budget hotel.  At an exceedingly low price, I got friendly service, an ample breakfast, a decent room, a hot shower, and a location close to downtown.  The neighborhood is not upscale, but it is quite decent--far better than some comments would have you believe.  I forgot pants and 2 shirts in the closet when I left for Crete.  When I came for a second stay 3 weeks later, the staff, who had kept them not knowing that I would return, delivered them to my new room.  In the budget category, this hotel is hard to beat.

The staff is amazing. Some of the best people we have ever met. Main problem we had was that the a.c isn't working great in some rooms. Also it's not in the greatest of neighborhoods. We enjoyed this place despite those facts.

Decent enough. Breakfast includes eggs, sausages, cheese, cereal, basic items. Bedroom was a bit spare and only had one visible outlet halfway up the wall which was annoying. The hotel is located in a rather dismal looking area, but the main square and metro are less than ten minutes away.

We only booked a day room for our last day in Athens, and it provided everything we needed. The room was okay, had air conditioning (although it was weak, but it made the room definitely cooler and more comfortable than the outside temperature). The bathtub was leaking a little, but it wasn't a huge issue. The location of the hotel is great - close to metro station and will make it easy for you to tourist around.
As there's no 24-hour reception, you might need to wait a bit at the door when you get there. After all, you get what you pay for and this offers good value for the price.

This is the worst hotel in the world. I was supposed to stay one night but I couldn’t because this hotel is horrible. Nothing worked, air conditioning was broken, safe box didn’t work, the bathroom had a leak in the ceiling and if you go out you must leave the room key. But the worst of all was the owner or the manager, a rude man. He insulted me and almost hit me because I told him the safe box was not working so I couldn’t leave the room keys.
I should have reported him to the police but I didn’t have enough time to do it cause I had a flight next morning.

Please don’t stay in this hotel!!!! The owner/manager is very dangerous

Not everybody's experience is the same, but I had a wonderful visit here. The room was adequate, with a nice open air balcony.  The service friendly, and the location was perfect to go down and visit the shops, museums, and sites.

first off picture on the net is photo shopped does not look anything like the picture. The only thing I liked about the hotel was the location and the fact that we had a balcony.  there are alot of great restaurants in walking distance especially Alexander the GREAT Restaurant which I highly recommend. breakfast was horrible for me. I don't know what kind of milk they served but it was not moo milk🤮🤮. they advertised a restaurant but was not opened. the owners were really friendly and very helpful 🙂

We stayed in the hotel for 7 nights. The hotel has a wonderful sphere. Our room wasn't big, but cozy, with lovely interior details. There was a bathtub in the bathroom, of a very comfortable shape. A small mini-fridge, so we could keep there berries and yogurt.  A lovely balcony with 2 chairs.   Comfortable mattresses on old stile beds,  sleeping there was nice. Air-co worked properly. Wi-fi worked perfectly everywhere. The owners/ personnel are friendly and helpful. After mentioning that I'd like to have tea in the room, the lady owner suggested to lend me a kettle for the room, and she lent us also a knife. I felt there at home, and would stay there again.

I recently stayed at Parnon Hotel and had a generally positive experience. The hotel’s location is a major plus, as it’s conveniently close to many of the main attractions, making it easy to explore the city. However, the hotel itself is a bit dated, and the internet connection was unreliable in some rooms. We also noticed that some areas around the hotel didn’t feel very safe. On the bright side, the breakfast was excellent, and the service was top-notch. Overall, it’s a good choice if you prioritize location and great service.

Had a great stay here, beds were comfy, room had everything you needed, plenty of sockets, fridge, hair dryer etc And the breakfast wasn't to bad either with some hot food included. The thing that made up for it was the staff, everyone was polite and helpful, and the reception team was only happy to help and give you their time! For a three star hotel this was a lot of bang for your buck!

Conveniently located close to the metro station.  My friend & I had a wonderful stay at this hotel.   The room was impeccably clean   the breakfast was superb plus the staff were professional and helpful.   I'll definitely stay here a second time.   Thank you!

I've been in Athens for 20 days now, I've stayed in many high-class hotels, but I've never seen such service in any hotel. Wonderful service especially at the reception, wonderful people, special thanks to Angelos and Nektarios Thank you guys. If I return to Athens one day, I will only return here. Cleanliness 10/10

Amazing small hotel,
Staff are sooo friendly nothing Is to much, they give you map and show you best ways to get to places things to do
Rooms are good size balcony views are nice.
Breakfast is refreshing and someone is  always there to help

Great hotel
Lovely  boutique  hotel. Small but very adequate  rooms, with a balcony. Much better than average breakfast included. Great location, very helpful staff. Highly recommended.

Clean room and good air conditioning. Good bathroom.
We got 2 singles, rather than a double/queen which was odd.
Nearabouts Omonia square/metro. Dodgy part of town, lots of drug use. Taxi driver told me not to walk the streets alone.
But I found it fine in the daytime, it’s actually fine. It was so central - I walked to all the sites including Acropolis etc. Perfect location that way. Just be careful about pickpockets/etc and stick to main roads, and travel in the daytime.
Breakfast was continental buffet.
And we were able to have cereal/coffee/biscuits when we needed a really early checkout.
One odd thing - encountered a hostile receptionist when returning to the hotel who demanded to recheck my room number/that I was a hotel patron… a bit of a shame as otherwise I thought people were fine.

The experience was extremely bad. Have you guys ever heard of any hotel charges extra 25 euros for bring people back. I can understand if the reception asked the second person to check in for security reasons, but what is the point for them to ask for extra charge? The receptionist said you paid for one person’s price and you didn’t pay for the second one. Are they charging not based on the number of room but on the number of person? Strange, absolutely SCAM. On top of that, the room is not clean at all. limescale in the baths and toilet, disgusting. AVOID THEM!!!

Great hotel and good sized rooms for solo travelers. The only thing I personally did not like was the view of the abandoned block of flats in the back of the hotel where my room was. But overall it was great.

They have a great breakfast variety with good coffee and juice selection. Very friendly and welcoming staff.

First of all: apart from one lady, all staff members were friendly and accommodating. The hotel is very central and there's a great breakfast buffet. There's a tiny elevator which helps you to get your luggage upstairs. The bed in my second room was very comfy (the first room looked nice but the bed was hard as a rock). Overall, good value for money. 👍

Now my issues: The first night I was in a room with a very hard bed. It was very uncomfortable and I could barely sleep. When I told the receptionist in the morning I'd like to change rooms, she first told me that all rooms had the same mattresses but eventually agreed to let me move. In the second room the bed was super comfy, so she definitely didn't say the truth about all mattresses being the same. Also, after moving (on my second night) someone opened my door around midnight even though I had locked it from the inside. It was someone who had a key card to my room, not sure whether another guest's key card accidentally worked on my door or if it was a staff member. Anyway, I screamed and the person left. Afterwards, I didn't feel safe in the hotel anymore. When I told the staff about it the next morning, she said that was not possible and didn't take my concern seriously. Instead , she implied I was making it up. Very disappointing reaction! I asked for the camera recordings of that night to prove that I was telling the truth and she told me there were no cameras upstairs. What the heck?! 🤨

Otherwise I really liked the hotel and all the other staff members were friendly, but this one lady clearly doesn't like people. 🤷

The location is perfect - it is in a very lively area, and close walking distance to everything. Despite being in a busy area, noise is not an issue. It is close to lots of shops and excellent food/dining options. The place also feels secure/safe given the 24 hour reception. We were warmly greeted each time we came and went from the accommodation. We were lucky to have a balcony and view of the acropolis from our room on the 4th floor! Other pros of this accommodation is it has a comparatively earlier check-in time. Shared bathroom is not too bad surprisingly, good water pressure and it is all kept clean.

Main con: Wifi did not work well in the room at all. There are lots of stairs to climb if you’re on the 4th floor, but this is not uncommon in Europe! They have a lift available for luggage only.

I enjoyed my stay here :) I had my own room with its own bathroom, which altogether was clean and great, the location was good and the staff were friendly. I remember breaking something in the bathroom by accident, but the staff were kind about it.

Superb location! Very good rooms considering the cost, they were clean and bigger than other cheap accommodation options i’ve stayed in. I also stayed on the 4th floor and there was no street noise at all, but I couldn’t say for lower levels. Finally, the staff were incredibly friendly, and “available 24/7 for your service.” Excellent experience and I would stay again. Highly recommend for young travellers on a budget!

Avoid!!!  AVOID 🆘🆘🆘 Wasn’t clean.  I was  abused by the male boss. don’t be fool by its good location, you need feel safe, have a clean room for a  good night sleep after a day out and back to your room with  welcoming  staffs, not missable faces  and tiny area

My room was simple (might not be to everyone's liking, but suited me fine.) Clean and the staff very helpful. The view from the 5th floor terrace was worth the hike up the stairs and to enjoy take out dinner! Well located not for from Metro station,  restaurants and the sights.

I would have given this hotel 5 stars if it were not for the welcome we received upon our arrival.  Our overseas flight landed at 8 am and we were at this hotel by 10.  We could not get a room until 2 pm!  This was after a long day of traveling.  After speaking with other hotel guests, this seems to be a persistent problem.  Suggestions, perhaps?  Even if you could spare a glass of water and a cool place to sit would have been nice. Maybe a free breakfast or cup of coffee/tea?  I appreciate that the rooms need cleaned, but I'm sure there were some  people that left early.  An early arrival room for weary travelers would be appreciated.  Also, Theo must have been tired of dealing with weary travelers as he wasn't very pleasant.
The positives - rooms are extremely nice with a Jacuzzi tub and a shower with body sprays. Beds are super comfortable.  Rooms were clean and nicely prepared.
Rooftop pool, bar, and dining area are just beautiful to relax after a day of sightseeing.
All other staff were more than helpful and very kind.

This was our first time staying at this hotel chain and we were pleasantly surprised. The customer service was top notch, every single person was attentive and went out of their way to assist us. The rooms were very nice and the beds were very comfy. The roof that bar was really nice and had great views. I personally wouldn’t stay at this hotel again due to the location. I would stay at this chain but I’m sure there’s far greater locations. The area surrounding the hotel has lots of homeless and druggies just lingering and sleeping in the streets.

Loved this hotel. Very convenient for walks to the city centre and historical sites in Athens. The sky lounge is very popular for an afternoon swim and sun. It becomes magical at night for a romantic dinner with a view of the Acropolis.

We enjoyed our stay very much. Staff was very polite. Our room was very clean and comfortable. We liked the overhead shower but could not get our handheld to work. Housekeeping came twice a day. A nice tray of cookies and dried fruit awaited us when we arrived. We brought our own universal plug adapter but were disappointed that there were no USB ports in the room or outlet in the bathroom.
The pool was wonderful! Hot tub looked inviting but water was not warm. Food and drinks on the rooftop we're good but menu options were limited.
Breakfast buffet was Amazing!

I had a great stay in Athens—my second visit after 11 years, and the experience was just as good as I remembered.

Location: While the hotel is slightly removed from the city’s main attractions, it's only a 15-minute walk or a quick 5-minute metro ride to the lively parts of town. The Omonia metro station is conveniently close by.

Room: The room was well-furnished and spacious enough to accommodate an extra bed for our child. The bathroom was also quite large, though the hydro-massage shower felt outdated and a bit tricky to navigate as it’s quite high to climb. However, the cleanliness was excellent throughout.

Breakfast: The breakfast offered a good variety of hot and cold dishes—perfect if you want to avoid the hassle of searching for breakfast in the city early in the morning. The only downside was the coffee, which, like in other Melia hotels, wasn’t great. I’m still not sure if it’s the machine or the coffee beans they use.

Rooftop Pool: One of the highlights was the rooftop pool, a rarity in Athens. Although it’s small, it’s a refreshing escape on those hot days, with the added bonus of a bar. However, I wasn’t thrilled that non-hotel guests can access the pool for a fee, as it made the area feel a bit less secure.

We got the room after they told us the apartment we booked at the near neighbourhood is not available.
To be honest, after we passed on that neighborhood on our way to the hotel we were glad since that street seems really sketchy and quite crappy.
The room itself in the hotel is pleasant, vintage looking, clean with great wifi and a small balcony.
The stuff were friendly and welcoming, the only thing to improve is the vegan options in the breakfast buffet.

The rooms are small but really comfortable. Air conditioning and smart TV. I really liked the breakfast with plenty of options. The view from the top floor is also really nice. I recommend it to everyone. The neighbourhood is a bit ugly if you have a car you need to look for a parking lot.

Terrible Service – Beware of the Apartment Scam: This hotel also manages apartments, and when we arrived at the reception to collect the keys for our apartment, we were told that they had double-booked it, and the apartment was no longer available (despite us booking for two nights). Instead, they put us in a hotel room that was clearly meant for staff, not guests.

After reading other reviews, it seems this "missing apartment keys" scam has happened to several other people as well, so beware if you're planning to book an apartment here!

To make matters worse, the receptionist was rude and acted annoyed, as if the situation was somehow our fault instead of their mistake.

It's disgraceful. I can’t understand how this place is still in business. On top of that, it's located in a very run-down area, with junkies hanging around right outside the entrance. I won’t be returning here, ever.

The hotel is in a less than wonderful area, with people sleeping on the streets close by. It's in a handy location to metro and bus for exploring the sights.
The rooms are OK, breakfast quite good, but cold. Don't have the buffet dinner, again cold and very limited choice. Staff were pleasant and helpful.

The only good thing about the hotel is friendly staff. Maybe also the dolmadaki we ate at breakfast. The rooms were very tiny. The bathroom is not proper. While showering, the water spreads all over the bathroom floor. No slippers or flip flops. Wet floor no slippers no hygiene unfortunately. The district in which the hotel exists is full of homeless people and junkies. They gather towards evening. We were afraid while returning to hotel in the evening. Streets were not safe. No coffee table,  no table no chairs in the room or balcony. No place to eat or drink sth in the room. No table for women to put makeup stuff in the room.

360 Degrees Hotel in Athens is a perfect place to stay for every reason you could think of! It’s in the Monostiraki neighborhood which is the center of all the neighborhoods. It is very close to shopping, good restaurants and all the attractions; Agora, Hadrian’s Library, Parthenon etc…

We checked in easily (online) and our room was directly viewing the bustling square. It was clean and had all the amenities we needed. Breakfast was 10+! Everything was neatly put out, fresh with a huge variety of items! The coffee was fresh brewed every morning and they continually put out hot steamed cream.
Surprisingly, the restaurant for dinner wasn’t that good and I’d have to rate that a 2. All food items were sub par and many people around us complained to the manager or left the food. The view of the Parthenon is amazing from this rooftop so I would highly recommend just going there for a drink and elsewhere for a nice dinner.
I would definitely stay here again as it’s the perfect location for everything you want to do in Athens! Hotel front desk staff were all very nice and super helpful!

Went to the rooftop. Terrible service. The manager, his second (bouncer?), and the waiter all had a terrible attitude. Very arrogant. The rooftop has a great view of the Acropolis if you get seated by that end. So they believe service is not important. Yet I saw at least four more rooftops around that have the same view. The tables not on the side of the Acropolis could have a great view as well, but they put some trees that block it. Definitely need a new decoration person.
They had a very poor food offering, only 5 different dishes.
I recommend going to one of the other nice rooftops around.

I highly recommend this hotel for a variety of reasons.
1. The location is amazing. You are a short walking distance from all the major sites in downtown Athens.
2. The breakfast is extremely fresh and delicious.
3. The view from the breakfast area is breathtaking.
4.The Room is spacious and clean.
5. The staff is very friendly and helpful.

However, if you are a light sleeper, you might need ear plugs as the rooftop bar does not turn down the music until 2-3 AM.
Located near the main attractions that Athens has to offer including historical sites, shopping, food, and drinks.  Enjoyed our stay, would recommend!
Very clean with good staff. But no fire safety or exits. No power in my room. Nowhere to hang towels in the bathroom. No outdoor space. Low flow shower. No coffee in the room. No bin .

I vacationed with my family in this "hotel", last year in August. The accommodation itself is very good. However, the reason I gave the lowest rating is that we had a very unpleasant experience with the owner of the "hotel". Namely, although I arranged everything directly with the owner, although I called two days before arrival and received an email that the room would be ready at the agreed time, when we arrived (and we were traveling with a 10-month-old baby), we received a short notice that is our room occupied until the next day and that we can spend the night in accommodation in the immediate vicinity!?! We agreed to it because with a baby in Greece in August, we didn't even want to try to find another accommodation. And maybe I wouldn't even write all this if another group of guests didn't have the same problem during our stay there. At the same time, the owner's attitude about the whole situation was that this often happens because it is the season and there is a lot of interest!?! The owner himself did not find it necessary to formally apologize, let alone anything more... During check-out, when the husband went to settle all the expenses, he had another unpleasant contact with the owner, who shouted that we had no right to be angry, that he has the right to do as he wants, that if we don't like it, we never have to come again, and he didn't even say goodbye when he left. And although I would have more to write, I think I have said enough for some future guests. We have been vacationing in Greece for years, I have always arranged accommodation directly with the owners, and this, unfortunately, was our first negative experience....

It is a nice and clean hotel not far from the Acropolis. Our hotel room had a view of the Parthenon. The bathroom was clean and had enough towels, but the shower was quite small. The bed was smaller than usual (narrower and shorter) and it would have been more comfortable for me if it was softer, but the pillows were soft. The hotel is located in an area where there are many restaurants. The staff was pleasant and helpful. Breakfast was tasty and the meals from the breakfast menu can be ordered in the restaurant in front of the hotel, and this is also a significant problem for the hotel, because on the first day we waited about 20 minutes for the preparation of sunny side up eggs after ordering. On the second day, we waited for the preparation of sunny side up eggs for over 30 minutes, and on the third day, we waited for the preparation of the fruit plate (two sliced ​​apples, five sliced ​​strawberries, one sliced ​​banana and six grapes) for over 45 minutes, so speed is a big weakness in the hotel. We were missing a tissue box in the room. The walls were very thin and we could hear the neighbors next to us and other guests in the hallway. Last night I heard my neighbor through the wall snoring 😳. The noise was even through the windows (which were well closed).  The floor creaked in places. So staying at the hotel for a price of over 150 Euros per night was very disappointing.  Everything cannot be excused or to say that we paid a higher price for the view. We came to the hotel looking for quality services, which we did not receive.
Their rooftop dining was a beautiful experience. Food and drink we made perfectly, staff was friendly and efficient, and the view!!! It is a moderate walk from the Acropolis museum.

We loved our stay at this little hotel with a big heart. Great view of the Acropolis. Quiet at night. Walking distance to restaurants, shopping and everything you want to see. Terry and the rest of the staff were so welcoming and jumped at the chance to help us with anything we needed. It felt like staying with family (in a good way) We would definitely stay again.
The staff were very friendly and helpful. Great view of the Acropolis! We only stayed 2 nights but would have been happy to stay more. Very clean and comfortable room to accommodate the 4 of us.

We were a family of 3 Adults and stayed in the Family room. The accommodation was very clean and comfortable and our balcony off the main bedroom gave an interrupted view of the Acropolis. Staff were very helpful, especially Terry, who went out of his way to accommodate our needs. Breakfast was very generous in its quality and choice. The Hotel is located with restaurants and bars in very close proximity.  Thank you to all the staff from 3 happy Aussie travellers..

I stay here every time I am in Athens. It is very affordable and about a block from the metro. It is a 10 minute walk to Psiri and 15 to Monasteraki. The people are amazing. I highly recommend it. The pics show the view from my room.

The hotel is very simple as the breakfast. But the treatment of the receptionist was extremely rude at me when I notice that calling a Uber was half of her taxi, and when other costumers heard me she started to scream in front of everyone. Not classy at all and super disrespectful. Never staying there for sure. I do NOT recommend this hotel

Here’s the translation to English:

"Very modest rooms. For showering in the bathroom, I recommend bringing your own slippers. Breakfast is mediocre... All in all, acceptable for an overnight stay, nothing more..."

The hotel is clean, friendly staff, and has a delicious fresh breakfast.  Good internet.

It is located in a transitioning neighborhood across from Wyndham.

I definitely recommend the Apollo Hotel.

Really good hotel, close too the centre of athens. Around 20 mins walk to the centre so not so bad. The hotel itself is budget but we loved it, cost room, aircon, TV, bathroom with amazing shower. I would highly recommend this hotel. Perfect base for city exploring. The staff were nice and friendly and the cleaners were nice.
All night we couldnt sleep because of narkoman people who used drags and prostitutes. They are crying loudly and fighting.  I dont know what to do impossible to sleep
All the staffs are friendly. Very affordable. The room is neat. Walking distance from main tourist spots and train station.
Terrible is the word
XXX Hotel but if the night catches you it's a good choice.
Good

Nice hotel and great for the price. Rooms are adequate, clean and with everything you need for a city break. Where this hotel sets it apart is the amazing roof top bar and pool area. Wonderful views to end a busy day of site seeing, with a nice cold glass of wine. 👌

The rooms were clean and sufficiently equipped. The location is a bit far from the acropolis but it does have a great rooftop view of it and is just four stops away on the metro. It's a little farther from the airport (about 45 minutes). The rooftop pool is also nice though small and the surrounding deck gets SUPER hot so wear your flip flops until you step in! The food servers were fantastic but the front desk courtesy was hit or miss. One of the gentlemen who worked it was super friendly and helpful and the other was very aloof.

Stayed here for 2 nights in one room as a family of 4. Honestly this hotel was nothing special - sounds harsh but in our opinion the photos are deceiving. The rooftop and breakfast are the best parts so I’ll start there, the rooftop has friendly and attentive staff when ordering food/drinks and a beautiful view of the Acropolis with ample seating and a small pool. The included breakfast is the best we have ever had at a hotel. Tons of variety with sweets and fresh fruit, breads, eggs, meat, cheese, Greek yogurt, specialty coffee etc. The breakfast is the only reason I didn’t give 2/5. The not so good part - If you’re travelling with small kids who will want to splash and play in a pool - note that this pool is small and other people in the pool will be splashed. The room was very cramped and lacked outlets (no outlet in the washroom) so many of us had to take turns charging our devices. We also had 4 single beds, which we didn’t mind, but they weren’t very comfy. Overall we agreed that this is a “bare minimum” hotel - but the breakfast and rooftop were nice, as well as the location. Very close to the subway which was a plus!!

Nice hotel highlighted by an amazing rooftop bar and pool with a city view including the acropolis - manned by helpful and friendly bar staff. The location is good enough, whilst not central it is close to the efficient metro. Breakfast wasn’t amazing however cannot think of any other negatives for the value of this hotel!

The rooms are not all of the same quality. We had two, and the last one needed some attention.
The location is not great!
Rooftop is pleasant. It has a view of the Acropolis. Unfortunately, the pool is way too small!

This hotel was brilliantly placed for restaurants, shops, acropolis and agora. The facilities are clean and all work, the breakfast is also fabulous. To top it off, the staff are fantastic, friendly, helpful and fun.
The view from the roof garden is amazing.
We had a brilliant stay, thank you.

Very good hotel in an amazing location.  Nothing fancy, just a really great hotel with brilliant breakfast.

Most of the main attractions are walkable,  and lots of shops and restaurants nearby.

Icing on the cake is the view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar.

Right in the prime location for visiting Athens, this hotel was clean, well run, and the rooftop restaurant offered an incredible view of the Acropolis in addition to excellent food & drinks.  Would revisit next time I’m in Athens.

I gave it 5 stars for the value of money. It really met the qualifications of a 3 star hotel. My room had a shower and bath tub. Be aware that the restaurant does not have view of Acropolis. But it was nice. The location of hotel is very good close to every where.

Comfy beds, location couldn't be better, great breakfast with lots of options, ONLY downside was very little sound proofing between rooms.
Loved the view from the rooftop restaurant. Food was excellent, but service was lacking.

Good value hotel with excellent service (thanks Maria) good buffet breakfast, and convenient location.  Comfy bed and rooms well cleaned daily.  Stunning view of Acropolis from rooftop cafe and some rooms.  Immediate area looks downtrodden but is quite vibrant with the market and restaurants/shops nearby.  Easy walk to sites and metro to airport.

The hotel it great, with very clean rooms and comfortable beds. Breakfast is also very nice, with gluten-free options such as biscuits or bread available and well packaged to avoid cross-contamination (celiac disease). The hotel is very recommended.

Location is also very nice, less than 10 minutes away from the city center. However, there are a few streets (one or two) that need to be crossed walking from the city center that are a bit dark and where you experience "strange" people/atmosphere.

I would repeat this hotel anyway, because the services are great and it is practically in the city center.

Loved the location near the central market and great restaurants and nightlife. Much preferred this area to the tourist clogged Plaka. Hotel room was clean and the staff were truly exceptional, they were so helpful with anything we needed. And we had the best view from our room! I would gladly stay here again.

I'm happy that I stayed at this hotel, because its central location, close to everywhere, is in the Munistiraki area. close to the metro.
The hotel is very clean, rooms at an excellent level of maintenance. good breakfast.
excellent service from all the employees and especially many thanks to the smiling and sympathetic Angela from the reception office.

Once you get passed the food market in front of the hotel it's pretty okay. Rooms are clean worth the price. The lady servicing drinks on the roof top was really personaliabe and nice. The views from roof top are breathtaking. We stayed one night before our cruise.

DO NOT STAY HERE YOU WILL REGRET IT!!!!!

We couldn’t stay here. We checked in and checked out, there was so many reasons we couldn’t put ourselves through the stay. Firstly, all of our furniture was falling apart. I got locked in the bathroom because the handle fell off the door and then the only chair provided collapsed, the sheets are ripped. The floors were dirty, it was horrible. The photos online are catfishes and the hotel is in a disgusting location.
Within an hour we were harassed by people on drugs for money, our arrival taxi even detoured to avoid the area. We found a SYRINGE outside of the hotel and felt so unsafe.

Oh and the best part: they refuse to offer any compensation claiming that the hotel is fine. Even ignoring the exterior, the only provided furniture is falling apart and is broken!

Do not stay here unless you want to risk your safety both inside and outside of the hotel. Do not bring children here. Do not book here, it was the worst hotel I’ve been to in my life and I’ve travelled the world.

The owner has rudely dismissed our request for even a partial refund, so I’d expect a rude reply on here too. Please take my advice.

The rooms are quite spartan, but it's expected given the very low price. The staff was very lovely and let us leave the bags there on our last day. The hotel is located in a bit of sketchy area of the town, unfortunately. It felt a bit unsafe when we arrived late at night.

The staff are amazing, the cleaner will eventually ignore the do not disturb sign, be careful where you step in the near vicinity of the hotel, drug users about. If you want local food for breakfast head left out of the hotel then left again, a little ways up is a great cafe.

I stayed at this hotel in Athens from 20th to 24th June for a business trip with my colleagues. If I had come for a holiday alone or with my child, I would have been terrified. This hotel is situated in Athens' drug trafficking area, making the experience absolutely dreadful.
Every evening, returning to the hotel was a horrifying ordeal. No one does anything about the addicts injecting heroin in every corner. The streets surrounding the hotel are filled with rubbish, syringes everywhere, and addicts sleeping beneath the hotel.
We informed the reception because we thought there was a dead girl in front of the hotel doors. Their response was to do nothing: no action was taken. We had to call the police ourselves. The hotel staff are friendly but completely indifferent to the dramatic and dangerous situation around the hotel.
We were not warned at all about what we would encounter. Terrible experience. Avoid this place at all costs.

Stayed there for one night only as we had to catch next flight to Fira. Didn’t expected much. Had to check in late night because of flight timings but it was smooth as they were aware about our flight timings. We didn’t booked the breakfast but decided in morning to have and it was not a problem for hotel. Overall, room space, air conditioning, accessibility and breakfast was good. Breakfast price was 6 Euros and the offering was good considering it was self service. (Boiled egg, bread cake, bread, butter, jam, cheese, coffee, tea, juice, salat (cucumber and tomatoes), greek curd, cornflakes, chocos etc.

Conveniently located in the middle of a city , can easily get around with buses or metro.
Walking distance to many of the famous attractions like Acropolis, museums and many others.
Plenty of good restaurants nearby. Hence , this hotel is an ideal location to stay and explore this beautiful city, Athens.

Prices : reasonable , a very good breakfast is included
Rooms : spacious, bathrooms are quite nice.
Staff is generally OK. They would help you when you need.
Special thanks should go to their wonderful housekeeping staff. They do a perfect job !!

Family friendly: they provided a good cot bed for our toddler ( need to request)
Hotel looks a bit old , has a vintage appearance. However, it’s clean and well arranged.

If we had to stay here again , we would definitely choose this hotel.

Only main problem we faced was , the surrounding can be a bit noisy at night , and wouldn’t feel safe with various kind of people roaming around the area. But police is there all the time , hence no security issues occurred during our stay .
Efharisto!!!

I did not know that all the hotels were full. Fortunately, I went to them and there was a lady at the reception and explained my situation to him. She was really helpful with me and allowed us to spend the first night in the reception hall because all the rooms were full. On the second day we got a room and everything was really clean. Everything was there. I advise everyone to go to them because the place is also close to the tourist places. 🥇😀🙏🩵

Having stayed there for around four days, I can confirm that the negative comments you're probably reading here are either coming from clueless tourists or people who have expectations way too high.

For a three star hotel, I'd say this is the definitive choice for anyone. The prices are incredibly cheap, the breakfast is just a solid ten out of ten, it's clean and comfortable, the staff are some of the most kindhearted people I've come across and they clean the rooms often.

Only minor nitpick I have is in regards to the walls of the room, which could receive a bit of a paint job.

But aside from that? I'm satisfied.
Any issues I thought I'd have didn't happen, and others that those other reviewers seem to mention are not the problems of the hotel but rather the area itself.
It's Omonoia after all, the very epitome of crime in Athens. What the heck do you expect?! Sunshine and rainbows?!

Pros:
Price - Got a room  for a very good deal for a day.
Rooms - I had booked the Double Room but was very glad when the lady at the reception upgraded our room to  superior room.
Rooms were very spacious.
Food - Breakfast was not that great but i must appreciate that the receptionist was very kind and made arrangements to pack  food for me and my wife and to deliver it to us in advance as we had to board an early morning flight
Location - Location was just a block away from the main street which i loved as it was not so noisy but yet very easily walkable to the main junction. Lots of Taxis available all the time.

Cons:
Nothing much to comment

The staff was extremely polite and friendly !!!

The maids did an excellent job upkeeping the room and overall the quality of the room!

The hallway was nice, and they were kind enough to offer us a place to temporarily store our luggage until our ship arrived !

My huge complaint came from the room it could use a bit of painting !! It looked absolutely horrible, which made me subtract one star from the overall experience however, price to quality ratio it was worth it.

We stayed for 4 nights,  breakfast and wifi included, and we left super satisfied with the overall experience. I just wish the room was in a slightly better state. If you pay close attention to the wall picture, you can see it looks horrible.  The breakfast was also AMAZING they had an amazing selection, and my belly thanked them!!!!

It’s easy to walk to restaurants, train station and all the attractions that bring people to Athens. The staff was super helpful and kind. The air conditioner worked really well. The WiFi went in and out. The area is a bit dodgy. I wouldn’t want to walk around there at night. You get what you pay for

Okay hotel in a neighbourhood with many sketchy people, not recommended to arrive at too late times, especially because one day after arriving around 2:00 am the hotel doors were closed and the number I had to call was always busy after even 5 tries. Breakfast is 8 euros, and you would expect more variety or some warm food like scrambled eggs for this price. Rooms on the fourth floor are nice with nice view from balcony, but the cards have to be reprogrammed every day at the reception. In the second room they put me the AC wasn't working, the TV signal was horrible. I would only recommend it to brave people who have plan B when their hotel locks them out for the night.

So the city is just sadly so dirty, tagged and filled with the homeless. It felt dirty but not dangerous. The hotel itself was nice as a previous review art was interesting but it was lying around in places. It looked like they had not gotten done putting it up. The television did not  work and no idea how to call down. The staff was lovely and kind. We did not ever partake in the breakfast but there were so many awesome bakeries/coffee shops. It was easy to get around but we will probably stay in plaka or mythraoki next visit. It just felt more vacation spot.

For me a bed has to be comfortable and the AC needs to work and it’s quiet. It meets alll those needs. Breakfast is good and the service from the owner lady is 5*
They could use minor your ups like the grout was moldy looking in the shower but that’s common.
For the price it’s a great value and I keep coming back. Not in the core center and not the nicest neighborhood but again the Hotel is clean and safe.

Good hotel but a bit pricey for what it offers (approx €100 euros a night including taxes)
You need to walk up a set of maybe 5-8 steps to get to the hotel so not really wheelchair friendly.
Receptionist and housekeeping were respectful and helpful
Room was a bit tight and toilets had no lock and the door was glass albeit frosted glass.
They don’t have money exchange service.
Lift doors have no sensors so you must be careful.
Nice quiet rooms
Has a swimming pool
Cosy reception area

Myrto was exceptional in our visit in Athens, I would recommend the stay here for anyone visiting Athens. She arranged our transit to the airport and recommended a mini breakfast box right before our flight back to the U.S. I love you Greece 🇬🇷 and warm welcomes you have my wife and I during our honeymoon.

We as a family checked in this so called 4 star hotel but were shocked to see the very average facilities.
Especially the reception people had poor communication skills and were  very rude .
The hotel room was the smallest size with the just minimal facilities and very little space available to move around.
The bath cubicle was just good enough for an average build person to stand and I hit the glass door more number of times than my heart rate while taking the bath . Water was leaking from the lower end to spoil the dry area.
We were shocked to know that even the first day water bottles were chargeable which we were not informed at the time of checking.
Even the additional coffee sachet was available @5£/ sachet ( such thing which we never experienced in the last 30 years).
We had rhe worst experience in this hotel & We strongly DON'T recommend this hotel to anyone.
Excellent option in Athens!!! Warm and kind staff. Clean room, outstanding view from the rooftop restaurant and plenty of options for breakfast! It's a 5/5 recommendation for your trip in Athens.

The location and service were nice but the rooms had such thin walls. We can hear people talking in the hallway and the room. We also heard vacuuming / cleaning from upstairs room at 7:30 am. Not sure if this is correct time to do this.

We stayed at the Polis Grand a few times during our trip in Greece. The rooms were comfortable, and the staff were all very friendly and helpful! I wanted to call special attention to Costas! He was there almost every time we were in the lobby, and he made a point to remember us and greet us with familiarity, making the check in / check out process feel a lot smoother! I really appreciated him!!

I had an especially enjoyable 3 night stay at Phidias Hotel Athens. It is located just far enough away from the city core to be away from the noise, traffic, crowds & hectic busyness of the city core, but close enough to walk to attractions in 15 minutes, give or take. It looks out on the lively promenade that leads to the city core's attractions & is lined w/outdoor cafes, a park & soccer field - it draws people of all ages to meet, enjoy a meal or drink, to just stroll & people watch, jog, bike ride, play soccer, walk to nearby places, or just hang out. This is also your view from the hotel rooms w/balconies. I looked forward every evening to watching this scene of people just enjoying life. I owe a special thank you to the receptionist/manager "G" for her exceptional Kenyan/Greek hospitality. She routinely went "above and beyond" to accommodate me and every guest at the hotel & truly deserves 5 gold stars! And although I did not have much contact with the day receptionist, she too went out of her way to ensure my taxi was ordered in time to board my ferry. I recommend it & will always try to stay at Phidias Hotel Athens when I visit. The hotel has an elevator, rooms are very clean w/comfortable beds, if lacking somewhat in storage (drawers/dresser). It is a very good value for this 2-to-3 star hotel.

Loved Phidias Hotel in Athens. Location is very convenient close to the metro and to the Acropolis and Greek restaurants scattered across the street. Their breakfast was really lovely and worth it. I wasn't available on most mornings and Poppy would pack it for me always.

Grace was very helpful in storing my luggage safely 🧳 before my flight since I had an early checkout to go to Eben Beach.

Value for money!!

Incredible location right by acropolis views incredible, adjacent to the grounds, quiet, safe, no traffic. Rooms very clean, service wonderful, Grace who works in reception was incredibly helpful as were all other members of staff. WiFi solid, breakfast plentiful, very close to metro and great value for money. Very under rated

Our stay at Phidias Hotel was disappointing. When we arrived early on August 29th, we were told there was a "technical issue" with our room. It turned out our room had been double-booked, and we were offered a taxi to Golden City Hotel, but the driver wasn’t informed that the fare would be covered, making us uncomfortable with the arrangement. The communication from the staff was confusing and unprofessional, and it took hours before we could access our room. The whole situation felt disorganized and ruined our experience.

We had to cancel our stay due to a sudden family loss, and the hotel showed no compassion or understanding. Despite explaining our situation and providing proof, they coldly denied our request for a refund. The lack of empathy was shocking. I wouldn’t recommend this hotel to anyone.

Our family of 4 stayed in a family room for 3 nights before a cruise.  We planned to to be out touring all day and we just needed a clean, reasonably priced place to sleep in Athens.
Positives -
- The room was larger than expected  - one section had a queen or a king bed, while the other section of the room had 2 twin beds.  We had plenty of room.  The room was not fancy but it was clean and more than met our needs.  The cost of the room was very reasonable during the high summer season.  We struggled to find rooms that would accommodate 4 people, so we we were very happy to find this one.
- The bath was split - one room had the shower, while the other room had the toilet which was convenient with 4 people getting ready.
- the air conditioning was very good.  We visited during August and it was extremely hot outside.  We all slept great in the room.
- there was a filter water tap for refilling water bottles which was convenient.
- the location was excellent- we walked everywhere.  Metro station was close by. There were grocery stores within a 15 minute walk.  The area came alive at night and there were lots of restaurants within walking distance.
- there is a kitchen and a fridge to store food.  Food must be labelled.  We placed some leftovers in the fridge one night and the next day, the box remained but it was empty- someone had eaten the leftovers. I can’t blame the hostel for the action of their guests.
- reception was very nice and always willing to answer questions.
Overall, we were very happy with our stay here! The hostel was everything it advertised itself to be.

The best hostel I’ve stayed at so far! The location is excellent—right in the city center and in a pretty safe neighborhood, so you can enjoy a night out and return to the hostel without any stress (a big plus as a female solo traveler). The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful. The rooms were clean and comfortable, with daily cleaning and very cozy beds. The common area was stylish and welcoming, and they offer plenty of activities, tours, and food options.
Would definitely recommend this hostel !

Ps : there is also a sweet little kitty living next door. He’s super friendly so if you could give him some pets that would be awesome ! He doesn’t have teeth so can’t eat solid food but he would  absolutely love soft cat wet food ❤️

It's a safe and friendly place! It's also very clean! The location is perfect! I only have one complaint with my room door, the lock was a bit noisy, but it can be quickly fixed with a little adjustment.

Fantastic place to stay for a couple of nights. Your own room with a private bathroom and shower, fresh sheets everyday if you want them, the reception was very nice and helpful, you can even keep your bags there if you are checked out but exploring the city. Communal living area and kitchen were a great perk and more amenities than we expected. Location is fantastic.

“My stay at City Circus was made unforgettable thanks to the wonderful Greek receptionist. From the moment I walked in, they greeted me with a warm smile and made me feel right at home. They were incredibly helpful, offering great tips on local attractions and ensuring I had everything I needed throughout my stay. Their friendly and welcoming attitude truly enhanced my experience, and I would definitely stay here again because of the exceptional service. Highly recommended!"

1. Unclean small and stuffy room - the bedsheets had stains..looked quite disappointing. The shower was a bidet. Was not sure if I wanted to take a shower with a huge bidet.
2. Broken plug points - the plug points to charge the phone was broken. The socket was hanging off the wall.
3. Noisy air-con - the room had an odour..probably due to unserviced air conditioner.
4. Not recommended for families.
5. Not recommended to anyone who is looking for cleanliness.
6. Smelly passage to the room.
7. The lift is extremely small.
8. Due to the above disappointing features avoidable if you are looking for a vacation experience.
9. Overpriced at 81 EUR per night..!!
10. Had to leave the property the very next day due to the poor hygiene.
11. I had booked a room for 3 nights…and cancelled the very next day and had to book another hotel nearby. Absolute waste of money. Avoidable if looking for clean and vacation experience.

Pella Inn Hostel is an accommodation that gives overall good value for money, the place has a number of notable advantages such as its location in the city center, very close to Monastiraki square, the balcony on the fifth floor with the wonderful open view towards the Acropolis, and the kind and friendly staff who are always ready to help and have a good command of the English language  and in several other languages.  In particular, I would like to give a good mention to Maria, who works at the reception desk mainly during the night shifts, she is a professional and educated worker, who is always ready to help you in anything, patient, and also an interesting and smart person.
Regarding the shortcomings of this accommodation: there is no kitchen that you can use and cook food for yourself, unlike what is customary in a large number of hostels throughout Europe. In the room that I have stayed in which was on the first floor, there were a small number of electrical outlets and they were located in inconvenient locations around the room, for example do not expect an electrical outlet next to your bed, out of 8 beds that were inside the room there was an electrical outlet in maybe only two beds out of the total eight.  Also the toilet and shower room are separate from each other and are located inside the room, which gives more privacy and comfort of course, but they were very small in size, outdated and in despair needy for renovation, the shower head was small and the flow was relatively very weak.  The dimensions of the bathroom will not suit a wide person with wide and tall physical dimensions with a relatively big weight. and also the diameter of the sink in the bathroom was very narrow and small, which causes water to constantly fall on the floor and wet it.  with an extra towel.  It is important to note that the towels are borrowed for free at the reception desk, unlike other accommodations that charge money for towels and sometimes also for bed linen.  In terms of cleanliness, the staff cleans the rooms every day and the toilets and showers are also thoroughly cleaned with disinfectants.  In addition to this, it is worth noting that there is a rather large refrigerator available for use by guests of the hostel, which is located one floor above the reception floor.  In conclusion, this is a cheap accommodation, in a central location, with a great and professional staff, just take into account the shortcomings of the bathroom and the noise from the street from the cars, motorcycles and other vehicles, but this is due to the central location of the hostel right in the heart of the city of Athens.  I wish you a pleasant stay at the place and many thanks to the staff of the hostel.

Perfect location in the midst of everything but inside very quiet , room small but clean and functional ( very traditional Greek).
Roof top terrace fabulous and you are welcome to take up own food n drink .
Will be staying again

The rooms were as basic as it gets. One of the biggest frustrations was the lack of personal charging sockets, leaving everyone in the room scrambling for one of the four available outlets—which, by the way, had to be shared among eight beds. It felt like a constant battle just to charge your phone. If you’re a light sleeper, be prepared: the hostel is on a busy street, and any open windows mean you’re in for a night of loud, jarring noise from the traffic below. The bathroom also has a low ceiling, so if you’re taller than 6'3", you’ll definitely need to watch your head.

All in all, it’s exactly what you’d expect for the price—but don’t expect much more than the basics.

Location, amazing! Workers were so nice for the most part. The room was the issue. The door did not stay closed and we were in a private room. 1st time the door was open we thought it was us. The second and third time we knew it was just broken and not us closing the door incorrectly. It's a serious problem when you have to bring the actual door handle down to the front desk because it is THAT broken. No one should wake up or come back with the door wide open all because everyone who can fix the door is on vacation. I'd give safety a negative 1.

We were also there 3 days and the rooftop bar was never open. Lots of false marketing and not worth it.

Very bad location, it's a street of homelessness' that doing drugs all the night, they don't hurt but it's very bas situation to walk in such street to your hotel at night.

There is no service at all, because this hotel have nothing to offer for customer, the elevator is small, 80*80cm max, the rooms old, the bed is bad and not comfortable, the water leaking outside from the bathroom.

The front desk staff was kind and helpful, but it's not their fault that I gave this review to this hotel.

Incredible small shower,  i gurss 40 X 40 cm. Room is ok, central in Athens,  15-20 minutes walk to almost everything.
Don't think about asking the Hotel for transfer to/from airport.  They don't do it, even if it say so on their page. I asked via email twice,  no reply.  When we left i asked the reception but they didn't want to do it.  An Uber is 42 €, so if you are familiar with metro, you save a lot of money
Friendly staff,
Clean room
Good value for the money.

Even if we paid for it pretty cheap I think the hotel could have been in other conditions. Our main door was broken, same the toilet door, we were unable to close it to have privacy, bad smell, bedsheet not clear at all and bed was not comfortable.
About the neighborhood was a mess, full of yonkis, homeless and people naked and yelling. Not recommended even if it’s cheap.

What can I say? It's a very affordable place to stay for a 1-2 persons, who need a clean, safe, friendly place to sleep, to take a shower and keep their stuff. My room was clean, bright and cozy, had a small balcony, two single-beds, AC, TV, internet and a bathroom - honestly I didn't need anything else. Staff was very friendly and reception works 24/7. One thing that I've found missing (and I would be willing to pay extra for) was breakfast - on the first floor I even saw a small dining hall with a bar(?), so I guess it wouldn't be such a huge problem to add it to the offer :)

And regarding the neighborhood... Yes, it's extremely dirty and disgusting. Especially during the late hours there are dozens of homeless folks, drunks & junkies. I always tried to return to the hotel before dark..... But I wouldn't necessarily blame it on the hotel itself - the entire block between Omonia and Larissa Station looks like a southamerican slums... Blame city authorities for it!

The hotel was clean but no kettle, no hairdryer, no basic things to enjoy your stay .  The shower is so small that you keep banging the side panels .  If you want luxury or a drink in the morning ... Do not stay here.  As for the neighbourhood , we'll DO NOT TAKE YOUR KIDS HERE ! .  Junkies are shooting up openly in this area, and needles are left  on the pavement .  It is the  Bronx of Athens .  Avoid this area and book somewhere nicer .   On a plus side, the lady in the hotel reception is a lovely lady .
The prices are expensive, the hotel is very bad and unclean, the service is very bad, and everything there is very disgusting. I feel sorry that I had this experience.

The worst Hotel, the location is perfect but it’s the only good thing about this place.
The worst receptionist that I met in my entire life, they won’t even store your luggage at the check out for a couple of hours. Everyone is rude and they won’t help you.
We even paid 5€ for the air conditioning and they didn’t give us the remote control, without any refund.
They didn’t change sheets and towels.
So please spend a little bit of more money to have a better and pleasant journey.

Without any booking we successfully
managed to find a room only in this hotel and were pretty happy.
They don't provide breakfast or any service, the  hotel is located in the nosy, dirty area, there's a permanent smoke smell in the room but we weren't going to get upset. Clean sheets, warm water are enough.
But the personel... Really that grumpy, grumbling old man at the reception... He was unpleased, impolite and didn't let us leave two small bags for 2 hours. Disgusting impression.

According to me it was not a hotel. It was like a store room. Their toilet condition was very low. The washroom was very bad. Water falls from the ceiling of the washroom. It is very annoying. There was not enough space to take a shower. Inside the hotel, next to the stairs, inside the room, the dirty smell is very uncontrollable. Their behavior was very bad. The behavior of the owner and the staff and the responsible people were very bad. In a word, everything was at a low level. I didn't like it at all.

If someone comes to visit with family or friends, I think they should not go to the hotel. It will only harass you.👎👎👎👎👎

It's just ok. Nothing special it's a place to sleep. I'm not going to trash this place. I was only there for a few hours. But it was my least favorite of the 3 I stayed in. It did have a balcony. A decent bathroom and the room was rather large in my opinion. And the internet worked.
Budget hotel in a good part of Athens.  I arrived really late (1am) and the staff was very helpful.  Bed and pillows were okay. Overall, the room was adequate and the cost was very reasonable.

I stayed at this hotel for six nights. For budget travellers just needing a place to sleep, this hotel is perfect. Although the bedsheets were stained and slightly torn, they were changed almost every night. The 24-hour reception is also a huge plus, especially given the less reputable location. They only let you in the building if you ring the bell and they check the security cameras constantly. Thankfully, we didn't encounter any issues during our stay. In short, if hygiene isn't your top priority and you only need a place to sleep, this hotel offers great value for your money.

DO NOT stay at this place. Hotel was in very poor condition. The bedsheets had holes in them, the toilet flush didn’t work and you’d have to raise the tank cover to flush. The main lights in the room didn’t work. The air conditioning was rusty and very old, causing a very unusual smell. There were wires coming out of the walls where the outlets were located and the towels were smelly.

The owner of the hotel was very fussy. We stayed 3 times, using the hotel as a hub to move around. Every time, he asked us to pay a €1,50 fee on top of the reservation and tried to charge us for a night we didn’t book. He woke us up at 6am due to his loud noise on the street. Any other place in Athens would be a better pick.

Absolutely God awful experience. Place reeked of cigarette smoke in the walls and mildew. It was so gross that both I and the friend that I traveled with slept on top of the comforter. We promptly checked out first thing in the morning to find somewhere new to shower and stay.
I travel alone and want a place I can sleep and shower. Not the height of luxury but clean and staff polite and helpful. Would use again.

Metro station is 50m from the hotel, which is very convenient. The rooms are reasonably new and spacious.
Overall a good option for travelers on a budget who are after a 3 stars comfort. I personally wouldn't consider this a 4 stars hotel.
The only disappointing bit is the buffet: the croissants are not fresh, and the coffee is really really bad. If you want to order coffee from the bar, you need to pay extra...

For the price we paid for our holiday it was worth it. The area is not the best but haven't had any trouble. The metro is 30 seconds away from the front door so that's brilliant. Room was very clean. Came with air conditioning that worked well and it was away from you so just leave it on to keep the room cool. Had its own fridge which was surprisingly spacious for a mini fridge, can get big bottle's in it. Wardrobe with a safe in it that you put your own code in and it's own bathroom with shower. Read a review that said it had low water pressure but not had any troubles. If you are on a medium budget then this is a very good choice. Tons of food places around and on 2 stops away from the party district with the 360 bar that has an amazing view of the Acropolis

Small rooms, but no hot water in the shower. Breakfast is okay, better than average. There are no fancy views from the rooms. Neighborhood may be a bit unattractive, but it is safe in a way if you take your precautions. Staff is super helpful and always kind. The hotel is really close to the metro station, and going to Akropolis would take you max 20 minutes. Good value for money.

I booked a trip to Greece in this hotel in 2020, canceled because of the pandemic obviously. Now in July 2023, I am still asking for my refund. I call every week, and send emails with all the information, and still, no one has got back to me with a real solution. Won't recommend a hotel that doesn't refund their customers correctly and keep their money.

We had a great stay here this week. Rooms were extremely clean and nicely decorated. The bed was one of the comfiest I have slept in!
The staff were very helpful and friendly. The food in the restaurant was tasty and not very expensive.
Our stay was quiet and comfortable, and the easy access to the metro meant we were just far enough away from the centre but could get there quickly if needed.
I would definitely stay here again. Thank you for a lovely experience.

At first, I was concerned. The area did not look great. But the room was decent, clean. The staff was wonderful. The breakfast was amazing and was 15 minute walk to basically everything. We did walk up to the central square at night as well as back from site seeing about 10pm. No issues for us. But still be aware of your surroundings.

We (17 people) stayed here for 7 nights in several rooms. The staff was very friendly and helpful. The rooms were clean and the breakfast was very basic but ok.

Unfortunately we had bed bugs in two rooms and 3 members of our group had bug bites all over there arms. I asked the staff to check for bed bugs and they promised they would search the room while we were sightseeing. I got a call from the manager that they couldnt find any bed bugs but changed all the towels and sheets. When we came back later in the day we wanted to make sure that there are no bugs anymore. We found them immediately after 2 minutes of searching. We then got 2 new rooms.

In another room (1st floor) it constantly smelled like someone smoked inside. My guess is that the cleaning staff smokes right next to the AC units and the smoke is sucked up and distributed to the rooms.

The neighborhood is full of drug addicts, prostitutes and dealers and there is a crazy smell of urin right in front of the hotel.

Would I recommend the hotel? If you are a male person and do not want to stay longer than 2 nights it might be ok. Don’t come here with children.

I enjoyed my time at the hotel, everything was so nice both the food and the service by Stelios, Stella, Sophia ,Vivi and Sissy. They were very polite,very welcoming and made me and my family feel like home. Especially in the kitchen I would like to thank Chrysa she was really kind ,humble and honest.(they really know how to treat customers like family)
Bed bugs! Stayed there for 4 nights first night my son was bit at least 20+ from bed bugs, he was given another room but zero compensation. Location is terrible, drug users all around the outside.

A basic hotel in central Athens. Nice location, v handy for all the touristy things. Staff lovely. Rooms very basic and bathroom v small. Go for the 4th floor as very quiet. AC which was great. Would stay again because of staff and location. A great restaurant nearby in Omania, great lamb chops!

Excellent location, at the Omonia Square. It was very convenient for us, as it was the pick-up and drop point for all tour buses. the rooms were very spacious and house keeping kept it neat and clean. The only disappointment was the Balcony was not accessible, as it was a common balcony covering many rooms. Breakfast was standard continental and starts at 6.30 am which was very convenient.

We were a group of seven people. We had an amazing stay at this hotel very recently. Rooms, though a bit small for our liking, are very comfortable. All the necessary amenities are provided. Location wise, it’s very accessible to lots of interesting places. Staff is very friendly and extremely helpful. They even gave us packed breakfast, when we needed to leave early in the morning.

We spent only one night at the Athens Tiare Hotel, but we loved it. The rooms are clean and spacious and the staff is kind, helpful and accommodating. We were a party of 8, and two members are elderly. Our bathroom had a steep bathtub to step into while the others had walk in showers (unbeknownst to us) which we felt was a kindness to the elderly members in our party. We ate dinner and breakfast at the hotel restaurant. The meals were fresh and delicious and we just overall loved our time here - even though it was short. Highly recommend staying here!

Great staff service let down by a hotel that overall needs a refurbishment. All staff were very helpful, friendly, considerate and understanding. Our room was OK. A good strong shower.  A shame we couldn't access the balcony. The decor and beds simply weren't upto a 4*standard. Location was fine and rooms were surprisingly quiet considering how busy the area was.

They offered me a room on the first floor but I didn't accept due to the traffic noise and asked for alternative room. Then they offered me another room with twin beds but I didn't accept it either because I was willing to stay in a king room. Then the receptionist canceled my reservation without issuing a refund! They got my money and they said go away. Stay away from this scam hotel.

I asked for a quiet room and was given a room that faced the street and was noisy until 2am.  Apparently this was the only room available, when I booked, although I was unaware of this.  Have specifically asked for a quiet room the hotel should have made me aware of the noise and given me the option to book elsewhere.  I was told to email my complaint in, which I did but no reply.  The room was expensive and, along with the poor customer service and disappointing breakfast, did not represent value for money.

What an incredible gem in the heart of Psiri in Athens. The hotel itself is clean and modern. Staff are excellent - helpful and knowledgeable. We have stayed twice and will continue to stay when we
come to Greece.

You will get some noise at night being so close to restaurants and bars but it is a trade off that is worth it as everything else about the hotel surpasses this small inconvenience.

Great Location, but Noisy Nights and Some Hygiene Concerns

The hotel's location was fantastic for feeling "part of it," right in the heart of the action, which made it convenient for exploring the city. The industrial decor gave the place a trendy and cool vibe.

However, I have to mention that the noise level during the night was quite disruptive. If you're a light sleeper or are trying to get rest during your stay, this might not be the ideal place for you. The constant noise (starting at about 5pm through 4am) made it challenging to relax in the hotel in the evening or get a good night's sleep, although the earplugs in the room were a nice touch and certainly helped. I am not sure what I would have done without my computer on full volume playing white noise though, as there was music from the bars below until about 3am and people talking/yelling in the streets until 4 or so both nights (even Sunday night!) We were on the second floor though, so perhaps higher up rooms don’t get quite as much of the noise.

The cleanliness of the hotel was satisfactory overall and the cleaning service was fantastic. However, one thing that bothered me was the open drain in the bathroom - right in front of the toilet there was an exposed drain that you could see the water run through when flushing the toilet, so it created a bit of a smell in the bathroom. It made me feel a bit uneasy about the hygiene standards.

Despite the noise and the hygiene concern, the hotel's location and trendy decor do have their appeal. Additionally, the staff was welcoming and helpful. Overall, I would recommend this hotel if you're willing to compromise on some comfort for a great location (if you are joining in on the bar fun) and a unique aesthetic experience. You also can't beat the price for a full service hotel so close to the city center.

Stats:
- Couple in their early 30s visiting from the US
- Stayed here in April 2024 over a weekend (Fri-Sun)

It does have the problem of noise from the bars up till 2-3AM. I took care of it with my noise canceling earbuds which were needed for the long flights anyway. Love this cozy hotel and genuinely nice staff.
Fantastic, incredible, clean, friendly stay in the heart of the city. Highly recommended. Top shelf. Purple ribbon.

In the room was quite space, bed was comfortable and staff was nice. Now the cons. Quite a lot of mosquitos there, but service has electric device againts them, so no problem. Moreover In the room were little ants and this is quite bad, because sleeping with mind of beeing bitten by this isnt so nice. And finally the placement of the hotel is bad, outside of the hotel was quite ugly and brakfast box definitely doesnt worth the money spent.

Worst hotel experience I have had. The room had recently been smoked in and they refused to move us to another room. The front desk guy was very nice but the manager refused to speak to us. Definitely do not stay here
We booked a non smoking room and when we arrived it smelled so strongly of cigarette smoke. They did not have any other rooms available and wouldn’t refund us. Such a sad experience.

We stayed here for two nights prior to a cruise. The price was very reasonable so we weren’t expecting much. We arrived to the hotel VIA their car service which was amazing! We got to the hotel just to be met with the friendliest staff you could find. We went to our room which we absolutely loved and had everything we needed, along with a great view of the Acropolis. We also used their car service for a 5 hour tour of Athens as well as to get to the ship. It was all worth every penny. The only negative things there were was the breakfast, and possibly the surroundings. Some. May love the breakfast however the offerings just weren’t to my liking. The Surroundings initially make you think you’re in a rough area, however I don’t believe you really are. I felt safe the entire time and even walked 1.9 Kilometers to the center for food and browsing. We plan on staying here The next time we come and would recommend this to anyone. We absolutely loved this place.

Nice enough place, a bit dingey in appearance though. The staff is truly lovely and there's a coffee, wine, spirits bar in the lobby. Sadly not a place for folks with cat allergies, they have a pet cat that lounges on everything in the lobby and there's no enclosure for luggage keeping.
Really a decent place to say, great size rooms just not my ideal.

We ate breakfast at this hotel and it was delicious. Coffee excellent too! And the service was amazing! The restaurant was very busy with lots of patrons and the servers were still 'on top of it all. Highly recommend!

The hotel is passable, if you are not expecting any lux. The shower cabin is suitable only for skinny women, so probably you will need to take a shower outside of it. The reception guys are polite and friendly. For the money I paid I guess this is what one can get. It's near the historical centre of Athens (25 min. walking distance) but the area is dodgy, dirty and noisy. I would not recommend to anyone.

Booked a room here and stayed for a week in December 2016. Booked a premium room but when we got to the room it was awful. The door to the balcony was broken. Hair all over the bed. The floor and the bathroom were dirty. Area not great but we knew that as my boyfriend is greek and knows the area and close to the metro. When we went to ask to change rooms the lady at reception told us there were no other deluxe rooms available. She said she had one other room and we asked to go and see it. It was a lot smaller with no balcony but cleaner however the shower didn't stay in position which was annoying but it was an improvement. Our room wasn't cleaned while we were there and when we asked to be refunded the difference of the two rooms we were refused which I personally think is awful seen as it wasn't our fault that we moved in the first place.

The area where's the hotel isn't so sure, especially in the night. The guys at the reception were very nice and they speak a good English. But the room was kind dirty, we've found a wet old towel on the top the bed, just to say. Also the toilet wasn't brilliant in cleaning. The price was low, but also the quality. Nope.

The hotel is really worth staying in. The location is convenient enough. Our room was clean, cozy and had everything you need to have in a hotel room. The staff was very friendly and was always willing to help, if there were any questions. Highly recommend to choose the hotel for your stay to be pleasant.

It can’t get any better than this. The view alone is worth staying here. Add to that elegant rooms and common areas, attentive staff and delicious food and you have the perfect mix. It is close to everything. Rooms are immaculate. The gym is small but, from what I can see, the hotel doesn’t target that clientele so it’s ok .. I would definitely recommend and come back here!

Everything is extraordinary about this historic hotel in the heart of Athens, near Syntagma Square, just opposite the Greek Parliament. Even if you don’t (choose to) stay there, you should visit the Spa, which offers wonderful treatments in a calm, cosy, yet luxurious atmosphere. A brunch or an afternoon tea at the Winter Lounge complete the pleasurable experience. And of course there is the Roof Garden restaurant with a breathtaking view.

Beautiful hotel with nice facilities and spa area. My gripes are:
(1) the service wasn’t as refined as I would hope for a hotel of this class. Not much greeting or going out of their way for guests.
(2) my wife’s spa experience was sub standard. Her back was bruised and sensitive for days after a massage, and her hair blowout didn’t turn out great compared to spas back home.

I rarely write reviews, but felt compelled to as the front desk staff really need to up their game. My partner and I stayed for a night recently and upon arrival the first question the receptionist asked was what time we were checking out the next day. I was slightly thrown by this but didn't take offence though am increasingly irritated that high end hotels expect an 11am check out, and not midday. But more on this later.

Our flight had been delayed so we arrived at the hotel at about 3pm hungry for lunch. We asked the receptionist where we might eat and she said that the restaurant on the 8th floor was full. We said OK, but can we maybe get a snack at the pool bar. She also said that wasn't possible. Rather puzzled we went to our room and then decided to explore. Upon entering the pool area we saw an operational kitchen and were greeted by friendly staff and duly had a pleasant bite to eat, confused that the receptionist had tried to put us off going!

The next morning at 10.30am as we were getting ready to leave our room there was a ring on the doorbell obviously to chivy us along. But not only that a few minutes later the phone also rang (with nobody at the other end when I picked up). I could understand it if it was after 11am but I really take issue with this sort of behaviour in any hotel let alone one that is notionally the best in Athens.

As a hotel it's OK, nothing particularly special. We moved onto the Four Seasons on the coast which is in a completely different league and I would thoroughly recommend.

Old world elegance with modern luxury service. The guest rooms have maintained their old school appointments, but the upstairs lounge and bar are very much updated with modern decor. The rooftop bar/restaurant is definitely the highlight, although evening cocktails are 24 euro, so you're paying for the view and ambiance.  The breakfast is 5 star in every way; we booked a rate that included breakfast and it was definitely worth it.
