The hotel is centrally located and about 5-10 minute walk to all of the Athens sites.  We felt safe walking the area at all hours.  The inside was nice with different areas to sit and relax. The morning breakfast was superb.  Lots of options and it was delicious.  The rooms and bathrooms were small but expected as most European hotels.  The beds were very comfortable.  The rooftop restaurant was great with wonderful views of the acropolis.

The location is perfect for shopping and restaurants near by. The room was nice but very small. The breakfast was good except for the scrambled eggs were mushy and half raw and same with the bacon. They should have a microwave so people can use it to cook the scrambled eggs more and bacon, the poached eggs (fried eggs) were perfectly delicious. Very clean Hotel

Central Hotel is located in the Plaka neighborhood in Athens. The rooms were very comfortable and the staff was very helpful and kind. The breakfast was very good, and the rooftop bar/restaurant was spectacular.  It's view of the Acropolis was amazing.

I am afraid price quality is not right here. Location is perfect for sure. Superior room so small. Be careful big school next door where children take a break every 15 minutes starting at 08.00am. Can get very loud during day. No other TV than Greek TV. Breakfast mediocre.

The hotel is located within walking distance of every attraction and entertainment area which is excellent, the rooms are not large but adequate, when the window is closed the noise level is very, very low and does not disturb sleep at all, the breakfast is basic and sufficient, the hotel has paid or free parking and also late check-out is pre-approved depending on the type of room  Those who choose should pay attention to the fine print when booking the room, we got a room with a balcony that was listed on the website as having a view but the view was a cluster of very old buildings and not beyond.  The restaurent on the roof of the hotel  has view of the Acropolis giving a pleasant atmosphere to sit down for dinner or even just to have a drink, recommended.

Rooms were clean and comfortable - and large by European standards. Food and service at rooftop restaurant were fantastic. Front desk staff - in particular Rolland, but other staff as well - frequently went above and beyond to provide exceptional service. It is clear to see that this is a very well-managed hotel. The location is not the best, but it's not bad - and there is a stop for the "hop on, hop off" bus just outside.

Our stay at the Fresh Hotel was a delightful surprise. Arriving with high expectations, we were initially disappointed with our room, which lacked comfort despite the balcony. However, the hotel's willingness to accommodate our needs was exceptional. Upgrading to a "chill room" with an outdoor shower was an absolute game-changer. The new room was stylish, refreshing, and truly elevated our experience.
The breakfast spread was impressive, offering a wide variety of delicious options to start the day right. But what truly sets this hotel apart is its exceptional service. The staff was friendly, attentive, and went above and beyond to ensure our comfort.
The pool area was an oasis in the city, providing a welcome escape from the heat. The ability to order drinks poolside added to the relaxation factor.
The hotel's location is convenient, with the city center just a short walk away. While the surrounding neighborhood in the opposite direction leaves something to be desired, the hotel itself is a sanctuary.
Overall, we highly recommend the Fresh Hotel and would undoubtedly return on our next visit to Adams. It's a place where you can truly unwind and enjoy your stay.

Great experience in chill out room with the shower on the balcony and the view to Acropolis. Astonishing furniture, spacious. Very good breakfast, fresh orange juice, good coffee.
Good rooftop bar. Swimming pool small and cold but still good to have it. Great service. Walking distance to main attractions. We are very very satisfied.

Great location. Noisy at times from the streets but your in downtown Athens. From the states and first time in Athens. VERY good breakfast. Fresh juices, honeycomb. The works. Pool is small, but the rooftop has a great bar with a view and great drinks/food. Friendly helpful staff. Would stay again.

We were really impressed with this hotel!  It had everything you could imagine for a downtown location. Good gym, sauna and lovely clean and fresh facilities. Furnishings were gorgeous and well thought out designs. We had a suite and found it through the Marriott site as we are members. Truly unexpected value!

I would have given this hotel 5 stars if it were not for the welcome we received upon our arrival.  Our overseas flight landed at 8 am and we were at this hotel by 10.  We could not get a room until 2 pm!  This was after a long day of traveling.  After speaking with other hotel guests, this seems to be a persistent problem.  Suggestions, perhaps?  Even if you could spare a glass of water and a cool place to sit would have been nice. Maybe a free breakfast or cup of coffee/tea?  I appreciate that the rooms need cleaned, but I'm sure there were some  people that left early.  An early arrival room for weary travelers would be appreciated.  Also, Theo must have been tired of dealing with weary travelers as he wasn't very pleasant.
The positives - rooms are extremely nice with a Jacuzzi tub and a shower with body sprays. Beds are super comfortable.  Rooms were clean and nicely prepared.
Rooftop pool, bar, and dining area are just beautiful to relax after a day of sightseeing.
All other staff were more than helpful and very kind.

This was our first time staying at this hotel chain and we were pleasantly surprised. The customer service was top notch, every single person was attentive and went out of their way to assist us. The rooms were very nice and the beds were very comfy. The roof that bar was really nice and had great views. I personally wouldn’t stay at this hotel again due to the location. I would stay at this chain but I’m sure there’s far greater locations. The area surrounding the hotel has lots of homeless and druggies just lingering and sleeping in the streets.

Loved this hotel. Very convenient for walks to the city centre and historical sites in Athens. The sky lounge is very popular for an afternoon swim and sun. It becomes magical at night for a romantic dinner with a view of the Acropolis.

We enjoyed our stay very much. Staff was very polite. Our room was very clean and comfortable. We liked the overhead shower but could not get our handheld to work. Housekeeping came twice a day. A nice tray of cookies and dried fruit awaited us when we arrived. We brought our own universal plug adapter but were disappointed that there were no USB ports in the room or outlet in the bathroom.
The pool was wonderful! Hot tub looked inviting but water was not warm. Food and drinks on the rooftop we're good but menu options were limited.
Breakfast buffet was Amazing!

I had a great stay in Athens—my second visit after 11 years, and the experience was just as good as I remembered.

Location: While the hotel is slightly removed from the city’s main attractions, it's only a 15-minute walk or a quick 5-minute metro ride to the lively parts of town. The Omonia metro station is conveniently close by.

Room: The room was well-furnished and spacious enough to accommodate an extra bed for our child. The bathroom was also quite large, though the hydro-massage shower felt outdated and a bit tricky to navigate as it’s quite high to climb. However, the cleanliness was excellent throughout.

Breakfast: The breakfast offered a good variety of hot and cold dishes—perfect if you want to avoid the hassle of searching for breakfast in the city early in the morning. The only downside was the coffee, which, like in other Melia hotels, wasn’t great. I’m still not sure if it’s the machine or the coffee beans they use.

Rooftop Pool: One of the highlights was the rooftop pool, a rarity in Athens. Although it’s small, it’s a refreshing escape on those hot days, with the added bonus of a bar. However, I wasn’t thrilled that non-hotel guests can access the pool for a fee, as it made the area feel a bit less secure.

Clean and nice rooms. Great breakfast room with plenty of hot and cold food options. Located just meters from the main road and many restaurants and shops close by. Old churches and infrastructure in the area as well, so don't have to walk far to get a taste of the sites and experiences. Be prepared to wait for elevators at peak times.

The location is excellent next to the most busy area of city centre of Athens. It is an old hotel but it is clean. They made some improvements the bed and all the furniture are new. The bathroom was very small even for a solo traveler.
A nice hotel walking distance from the Acropolis and a large park.  The room was small by American standards but clean and functional.   A very nice breakfast buffet.
Love this hotel I have been staying  here for years. Convenient to  everything. Clean,breakfast is good.
Decent rooms. Decent breakfast. Staff really friendly. The rooftop garden is brilliant, take your own drinks and a picnic

I have led multiple groups overseas. This is a fantastic hotel. Centrally located. Excellent food rooms are clean and very comfortable. The rooftop restaurant and bar has an unbelievable view of the Acropolis. Highly recommend.

Even though the hotel is an older one, service was excellent - the staff was very attentive and friendly. Breakfast and coffee were excellent and the rooftop view was a fabulous one. The elevators are busy in the morning as can be the breakfast bar at around 8 so get to there early!

Reasonably good hotel overall despite some old outlooks and slowness — your elevator in the peak morning hours can easily take 5 minutes to come. The staffs are helpful and friendly and the breakfast area is simply fantastic. The location is pretty awesome. It would’ve been lovely if the room keeping service included the water refill, especially when we stayed for four consecutive nights.

The hotel location was awesome. The view from.the top floor restaurant was amazing. The breakfast was expansive and delicious. The front entrance is elegant but our room was run down. We had a window that was covered so really did not see daylight until we ventured out. Bed was horrible and shower was challenging.
This was the worst hotel we stayed at in Greece. Avoid room 314 at all cost. It was small, smelled like Goodwill and you could feel every coil on the old mattress. Only good thing was location

I got my personal room and bathroom here. No issues at all. Location is excellent, all the top 10 sites are within a 30 minute walk. Streets were clean and safe, and some great food and bars options nearby. The rooftop bar has a truly exceptional view and friendly service. Would recommend to other friends.

Unfortunately I can't give 5 stars because the bathroom always flooded after people had a shower. But the position is excellent, the staff is very nice and friendly and the rooftop offers you an amazing view of the acropolis and the other archeological sites. It also has a bar with music where you can meet and talk to people at night.

We stayed in a private Acropolis view room. The room itself and balcony were very nice. However, the washroom was on the smaller side and the mattress was pretty awful. It felt like sleeping on a few old springs. Staff were all very friendly and approachable with answers to all our questions. The location is great. It is in proximity to all popular tourist attractions and the Metro. We opted for the breakfast package with our stay and it was the only regret we have. It was pretty terrible. Other than the breakfast and mattress, we thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
Perfect location. Quite area however there’s no soundproof in my private room and could hear my neighbours easily. Bed was very cozy. Water pressure and temperature was excellent.
It is a hostel but we stayed in a separate 2 people room. Acropolis view from the room was just amazing. You have a terrace in your room. Maybe one of the best views in Athens 🤩

This hotel is great if you are staying a couple of nights in Athens. The beds are comfy, the cleaning is great and everyone is super friendly. They will hold your bags if you have a later flight in the evening. The location is sensational to all the laneways which are full of restaurants and shops the atmosphere at night is buzzing. The view from the their rooftop is pretty spectacular too, we grabbed a beer and watch the sunset. Also walking distance to the acropolis.

Excellent Location for visiting Athens. Breakfast was just a delight. Our Room was very comfortable and had a great view from our room over Athens. The added bonus of the Roof Terrace was a wonderful area to relax after a busy day site seeing. The view of the Acropolis in the Evening is just wonderful.

We ended up booking the last available room at this hotel at the last minute after our first hotel was a bad experience, and it was a great decision! Our room had a balcony with an incredible view of the Akropolis and the bed was comfortable. The room was a bit tight, but manageable. There was some noise from the rooftop above and people moving around in the hall, but nothing too bad. The staff was incredibly kind and helpful and the location is convenient to the city center without being too busy. Loved this hotel!

Great hotel, perfect Location, amazing views to the Partenón from the rooms and great service. Anastasia and Anano from the kitchen serve delicious greek breakfast witth an amazingly kind and warm attitude. Only thing to improve is the air conditioning in the breakfast room. It was too warm there, but it should be easy to fix. All other aspects of the hotel are perfect, people in the reception also very kind . Great hotel in a great place !

We stayed at Arion the end of May.  While the room was a little small the bed was very comfortable and the staff was nice and helpful .  The breakfast was great and Anastasia was so accommodating in getting anything you asked for. You cannot beat the location.  Walkable to shopping and eating.  We would definitely stay here again!

Loved the hotel. Very good location, close to tourist attractions as well as the metro. Staff were very friendly and welcoming. Breakfast had a lot of choices and the food quality was great! The room was clean and modern (a bit small though + the view wasn’t great, which is expected due to it’s location). I saw a comment here about the AC temperature - in my case, it was working perfectly fine and was able to set the temperature without restrictions. Internet speed and connection was great.
Can definitely recommend it!

The hotel does not let you set the temperature of the air conditioning in the room. It is set to 27c (80.6f) degrees!

Other than the hot rooms, the hotel is very nice with modern touches. The breakfast in the morning is great. There is some street and bar noise outside at night due to the location.

The location is perfect. I was easily able to walk everywhere I wanted with most points of interest less than 30 min away.

It was quiet at night allowing me to sleep, I did have an inner room.  My room was right in front of elevators and that sound in the mornings does come through easily.

Breakfast had a variety of food and drinks.

The staff were friendly and quickly replied to any questions I had.

The room and restroom were big for Europe standards. If I ever got to come back to Athens I’d stay here again.

We visited the roof bar of the hotel , it had a breathtaking view
The breakfast was rich and delicious
We thank Michael for everything!
Gypsies passed by our table at the lobby bar and tried to steel our iPhones in the morning and he stopped them.
Michael, thank you so much for everything you were very professional and helpful in everything we asked you for.
Till next year, my American friend !

The good - location was excellent and the 7th floor sky bar makes this hotel worth staying at.

The rooms are dated and sparse, the thermostats are locked, and we had to wait over an hour after check-in time for our room to be ready.

Overall not a horrible place but a little disappointed with the experience. You can't beat the location and the bar on top which was a five star experience.

Nice hotel in a busy touristy area with plenty of restaurants and tons of shopping.
Hotel appearance and lobby were excellent.
Room was large by EU standards. The only caution on the room is the normal EU half shower makes the smooth marble floors very slick.
The included rooftop breakfast buffet was really good with a fantastic view of the Acropolis.
Included WiFi was adequate.
Staff was efficient and friendly.
Would stay here again.

The Acropolis view from room is breathtaking. The staff is very helpful and nice. The location is perfect, it is very easy to access the metro line to Acropolis and airport as well. Breakfast is very delicious. One small minus point is the room is little old and small, but it is really not a big deal compared to other plus points. I had a very nice time during the time when I stayed here. Highly recommendation.

Astor Hotel is highly recommended for anyone visiting Athens. Its central location, close to numerous restaurants and the iconic Syntagma Square, makes it an ideal base for exploring the city. The area is lively, adding to the overall charm of the stay.

The staff were incredibly friendly, pleasant, and kind, always willing to help. Although the room felt a bit small, it more than made up for it with its cleanliness and comfort.

The highlight of our stay was definitely the breakfast on the rooftop, offering a breathtaking view of the city. Watching the sunset over the sun-kissed Acropolis was an unforgettable experience. We thoroughly enjoyed our stay here and would love to return.

Initial impression of this Hotel is disappointing. We are in a family room and only have 2 people. The set out of room feels like I'm in a caravan. Not much room to move around. We booked a larger room as I'm in a wheelchair. I can't get my wheelchair past the entrance to the room. Location is good but the pavements around the hotel are very poor for wheelchair users. We picked this hotel as it said it was accessible. It's not. Website said there was a restaurant in this Hotel. However it's only open for Breakfast and for drinks in the evening. Lunch and dinner is not served.

Best acropolis view from rooms and smack in the nicer section for market shopping and easy access to restaurants

Service was nice. Our AC unit broke and they quickly moved us to another nice room.

Breakfast is very nice with superb view of the acropolis.

Large lobby with resting area couches etc.

Elevators are a bit small but manageable.

Let's start with the location, brilliant!! Right in the center of Athens, by the market and walkable distance to all the tourist spots.
It's a small hotel with a super small team, but everyone was kind and sweet.
The room i stayed in was on the smaller end, but who cares,  not moving in, just staying for a vacation. More important,. The room was clean and smelled nice, and had a huge balcony.
Definitely a good choice.

Carolina hotel is right in the heart of Placa area, nothing fancy, but you only need a shower and a good night sleep. The rest is out and about in Athens. Very friendly and kind people at the front desk and they helped us out to sit before seven in the morning so we can have breakfast before taking off to the airport. We will be back.

It’s very nice hotel with good price, room is small, neat and pretty good. I love the terrace. Clean washroom. Front desk staff is very friendly and helpful. Breakfast is not bad. Distance from the Monastiraki square is hardly 7 minutes. Lots of nice restaurants and bars in the same street.

Very nice place, clean, bright room, very good breakfast.

Close to the Acropolis, plenty of cafes around.
A fabulous hotel quite clean and very close to all that old Athens has to offer. Great staff. Great rooms and a brilliant breakfast ! Awesome value!

Pros:
- Good location, quiet.
- Walkable, though not super central.
- Nice rooms.
Cons:
1. Absolutely no shower pressure.
2. Towels provided are either hand-sized or very small for the body.
3. The wardrobe has only 4 mismatched hangers and no shelves ;)

Very basic room furniture, dark room with an awful smell all over the hotel probably from the Airconditioning. The TV was not working, the flush toilet was broken, the hair dryer also not working. Towels and bedsheets not super white probably are old and need replacing with new ones. The location is convenient but was expensive for the quality it offers

Warning! Service prices changing on the way
After a refundable reservation I've received an email asking if I wanted the taxi at a certain price, I replied the day after asking for the service, the I got a reply after a couple of hours with a higher price.
I called them and a woman literally told me with a rude manner: the manager has changed the price, do you want it or not?
Be aware to book with them and stay in a harsh and stressed environment as normally Greece is a relaxing place to visit for tourists.

My stay for one night was HORRIBLE. It was super hot in the room and it was so noisy from the street I couldn't sleep and I got in at 3AM. The AC also didn't work. terrible experience wouldn't recommend.

We were  coming back from the islands and had to have a night in Athens before catching the flight next evening. We wanted to be close to kolonaki as I love this area and we needed a possibility to leave our luggage for few hours after check out. So we needed an accommodation with reception. Athens Way was good price - value ratio. lots of cozy cafes nearby, good supermarket and Metro - 10 min walk.

The hotel was quite comfortable to relax in with no any distraction or noise nearby and had the necessary facilities. I liked the way the hotel rooms were organized, and thanks for getting our request well in advance of arrival. Regarding the service cleaning, there should have been slightly better routines, the cleaners are hardly visible to the guests in the hotel, it is good that they are doing their job, but we have noticed that they knock and open the door at the same time without us knowing when they plan to clean our room. The other negative view is that the shower could have been prepared a little better as the water runs out to the basin and onto the floor in some cases without a glass protector or shower curtain. Otherwise, I appreciate the breakfast service at the hotel, they had a very delicious and hearty breakfast that was served every morning, we really liked this and the staff who work there are very nice and calm people.

Me and my partner recently visited this hotel for a weeks stay in Athens. The room was basic and we had booked a double room but instead we were given two single beds pushed together. The comfort of the beds was fine for us both, worth noting here that we are a young couple. The shower was powerful, hot and the hotel provided clean towels. They also provided a hairdryer but the one in our room was broke, I asked the first morning for a new one and they gave us one. There was a mini fridge in the room which was great to keep a couple of bits cool and the air conditioning worked great. The staff were friendly and helped if needed. The hotel is a 15 min walk from the centre which was ideal for us. The breakfast was nice, we are vegetarian and there was enough options for variety thought out the week.

This hotel will be ideal for anybody who wants somewhere to stay while in Athens but are not looking for any luxury.

Great value for money as it's centrally located within bus stops and metro station within few minutes of walk. Staff was generous to change my room from single beds to double bed, as my booking agent erroneously booked single beds.
Note that the lane around the hotels doesn't seem to be safe and be cautious while roaming around at night

i would really not advice going out past midnight because the area is generally known for being dangerous at night. that said, at day time you’re close to the center, the metro, and the big shops. rooms were good and breakfast was great, although the coffee was super watery. they should consider getting rid of the fat balding guy at the reception though.

Cons:
- The rooms were very very basic and the cig smoke would come right into the room.
-tv wouldn't work in any other language.
-Breakfast was not good and if you're not there the first hour, it's heavily picked over
-Bedding is very itchy and towels are hard
-Location I would give a 3 out of 5
-No microwave
-Rooms could be cleaner, but thats mostly a dust issue (note, I didn't open the windows)
-Noise carries A LOT. If there are ppl in the hall, it will feel like theyre in your room

Pros:
- Spacious rooms and BIG bathrooms
-Accept night walkins
-Market/Cafe diagonally across the street
-Has mini fridge
-Air con worked well
-A bit more on the pricier side for what you get, but still not bad
-Staff were not rude. Though we didn't interact with them much

Things to note:
- This area is a bit loud
-This is an area where there are A LOT of the unhoused community. They were not aggressive at all. Some places yell at them, but I don't think they deserved it.
-The train in this area isn't great and is a hot spot for pickpockets
-To get to the busy and social area, you will realize that youre on the outskirt of the area.

What an incredible hotel! My favorite place to stay in Athens. Nothing better than having a meal or a drink and enjoying this gorgeous view. The rooms are so nice, the pool is incredible, and the staff is so helpful and accomidating. We've already stayed there 2 times and we'll be coming back asap!

Amazon invited us to this hotel for a networking dinner. It was great. The food was tasty, the view was breathtaking, and the staff was very kind. The location is difficult, but as you can see in my photos, you can see as far as Piraeus to the sea.

We would like to give a special thank you to Panagiota, lady at the swimming pool. Such a nice and helpful lady speaking fluently in English.

Also thanks to the lady who cleaned every day our room. We don’t know her name, she was very friendly.

Regret we had a very poor experience from beginning to end.  There was no welcoming hospitality from staff and deputy manager during check in.

We booked 8 rooms and all of our rooms were at back side of hotel, next to air condition units and were very noisy. At midnight, there was a electricity cut and we woke up with huge generator noise. (By the way, generator was only for main hotel places but not for the rooms.) We had no power and air condition for few hours in our rooms. None of us could sleep during the night.

In the morning they didn’t even come to us and apologies for the inconvenience or offered any complimentary for an excuse. Besides they just made our check outs like nothing happened also charged for coffees we had during check out process.

Overall they don’t deserve 5 star  and don’t act like that at all for sure.

Don’t suggest anyone to have this bad experience. There are way better hotels in Athens which you can get what you paid for.

Hotel was great and in a cool location. The rooftop pool is amazing and we spent a lot of time up there. Unfortunately the service at the rooftop bar was pretty bad. They didn't have a lot of cold options, our drinks were not always made well, etc.

Very nice and helpful staff, rooms are small but very clean and neat (in the end we are here to explore the city, not the hotel room). The location is extremely convenient in a short walking distance to all the main sights, restaurants and nightlife.
There is also luggage storage so we were able to leave our bags after the check out since we had a later flight (for free).
The most beautiful thing about this place is the rooftop terrace and the view over the city and Acropolis. Just wooow!
If ever again in Athens, for sure I'd stay here! Thank you so much!

Great location near Monastiraki metro station. Friendly staff, clean rooms, and a wonderful view from the roof garden. We were offered a free upgrade and enjoyed breakfast with a view of the Acropolis. We'll definitely come back!
We had a great view of the Acropolis from our balcony. The breakfast was simple but good and there is a large selection of awesome restaurants just around the corner.

Nice hotel with potential. The beds were comfortabel, we had a balcony to the street, good airco, and showers were good. Rooms are rather old fashioned, in general the hotel could use a renovation though everything still functional. Breakfast was ok, provided all the essentials, and the view from the rooftop terrace was amazing! Enjoy a drink up there. Only some music and more cozy furniture is missing there. All in all good choice for a hotel in Athens!

Really great hotel if you’re looking for something simple (with air conditioning) that is close to everything and has a spectacular view of acropolis. The service was the best, I had to checkout really early at 4am and they had a bag of breakfast for me. It made my day. I would absolutely recommend this hotel and would go back.
Stayed there between 9-11.09.2024 .Friendly staff welcomes you. Rooms ate clean and tidy, free wifi you can use. Close to Monastraki and metro station. Very very good breakfast.

Tiny rooms but clean and tidy.
Good breakfast and friendly staff.
Great location near omonia metro
Lots of food options and shops .

This hotel was absolutely beautiful, clean and very comfortable beds . Maid service was 100% front of desk friendly and very accommodating and on our last day allowed us to keep our luggage at hotel and return just before our flight . It can be very noisy as it is on Main Street but all of hotels are mainly on Main Street . Close to all site seeing if that’s what you are going to see . 100% would return that’s for sure 💜 comes with balcony if requested x
Stayed here for four nights during a tour and found the place to be nice.  Friendly staff and the breakfast buffet is nicely stocked.  I would stay here again most certainly.
Great hotel, nice rooms excellent service, a bonus we arrived at 9:30 am and were allowed to chechin right away. Good location close to main sites , recommended

Amazing little hotel, only a 5 min walk from Metro stations. Great buffet breakfast and the best reception staff ever!  Area has its ups  and downs but felt safe the whole time.Local restaurants are also a must.. If you love city breaks this is the hotel for you..

TERRIBLE HOTEL EXPERIENCE

Our group of 100 students had a distressing stay at this hotel.

Firstly, the hotel is located in a very unsafe area of Athens, rife with junkies. These individuals roamed the streets, frequently engaging in theft and yelling at all hours. We could even see them openly using drugs from our balcony.

Upon entering our rooms, we were met with a foul odor of mold and cigarettes. Opening the balcony door was not an option due to the pervasive smell from outside.

We were forced to order food to the hotel, as it was unsafe to walk the streets, even in the evening.

The room itself was filthy. The sheets and mattress were stained with vomit, urine, and other substances. There was hair everywhere, the mattress had cigarette burns, and there were holes in it.

Housekeeping was extremely rude and disrespectful when we requested new sheets. The receptionist was unhelpful and even yelled at us for inspecting the bed.

At least 10 other rooms reported bedbugs. Proceed with caution!

The locks one some of the rooms were not working, the doors could be opened without a keycard.

The air conditioning couldn't be controlled and ran all night, leaving me with a sore throat. Additionally, there was mold around the unit.

Breakfast was mediocre, though the food was fresh.

Overall, this hotel is a tourist trap. Avoid it at all costs!

Had a lovely stay here with the family . Staff were lovely, very pleasant and ever so helpful . Hotel room was very clean , fresh towels etc daily . Breakfast was lovely. Metro and other transport within three mins walk from the hotel itself

The hotel is located in the worst neighborhood in all of Greece. Junkies and and homeless people seen roaming arround all the time. I would feel unsafe walking around on my own. The hotel however was quite nice, with a really nice bathroom and a rather spacious room. The food selection was not the biggest but it tasted great. No issues with the staff at all.

The hotel is fine. The apartments are not. While the apartment itself is acceptable, the entrance is horrible, you must walk through a street filled with homeless individuals and filthy and unpleasant, we prepaid for 3 nights and left after the first  losing payment for the remaining nights.

Great hotel! We really enjoyed our time here. Our room was large and the balcony was also a good size - it included 2 chairs and a table. The air conditioning worked well, thankfully, as it was very hot when we were there. The location was very convenient - plenty of nearby restaurants. We loved having a nice cocktail at the rooftop bar - beautiful views! Hotel staff were welcoming and very helpful. I highly recommend staying here!

We stayed 3 nights at New Hotel as we explored the city of Athens. It was a wonderful experience. The staff was friendly and helpful. The rooms were beautiful and very clean. I loved the decor of the hotel, the use of found pieces of furniture, cabinetry and wood on the walls was something that I had never seen before and it was so interesting and really pretty. The breakfast was wonderful and we had drinks on the rooftop bar overlooking the acropolis, a truly special experience. The location of the hotel was great for seeing the sights as well.

We have stayed at the New Hotel the last several years when we have been in Athens.  Convenient location with clean and comfortable rooms.  The staff is extremely friendly and accommodating.  We always sample the rooftop bar and they always have interesting concoctions.  Plan to come back next year

Fantastic experience at the best location walking distance to everything in Athens at a much better price point than the bougier options closer to Parliament. Staff was beyond accommodating - we stayed at beginning and end of our Greek trip, leaving some bags with the hotel, they were great with local info (needed a haircut and some car service to the airport - they came through with both on short notice), and the rooftop bar restaurant craft cocktails and food were great. Highly recommend!

I loved the design of this hotel. Our room had a great view and balcony. The breakfast was also very delicious. It’s very close to the acropolis and the surrounding area is very beautiful and fun to walk around. We had a great time!

I recently stayed at Parnon Hotel and had a generally positive experience. The hotel’s location is a major plus, as it’s conveniently close to many of the main attractions, making it easy to explore the city. However, the hotel itself is a bit dated, and the internet connection was unreliable in some rooms. We also noticed that some areas around the hotel didn’t feel very safe. On the bright side, the breakfast was excellent, and the service was top-notch. Overall, it’s a good choice if you prioritize location and great service.

Had a great stay here, beds were comfy, room had everything you needed, plenty of sockets, fridge, hair dryer etc And the breakfast wasn't to bad either with some hot food included. The thing that made up for it was the staff, everyone was polite and helpful, and the reception team was only happy to help and give you their time! For a three star hotel this was a lot of bang for your buck!

Conveniently located close to the metro station.  My friend & I had a wonderful stay at this hotel.   The room was impeccably clean   the breakfast was superb plus the staff were professional and helpful.   I'll definitely stay here a second time.   Thank you!

I've been in Athens for 20 days now, I've stayed in many high-class hotels, but I've never seen such service in any hotel. Wonderful service especially at the reception, wonderful people, special thanks to Angelos and Nektarios Thank you guys. If I return to Athens one day, I will only return here. Cleanliness 10/10

Amazing small hotel,
Staff are sooo friendly nothing Is to much, they give you map and show you best ways to get to places things to do
Rooms are good size balcony views are nice.
Breakfast is refreshing and someone is  always there to help

Great hotel

The immediate surroundings of this hotel were disturbing, disconcerting, and perceived as perilous by city residents. The hotel itself was tolerable, moderately clean, very friendly staff, and has delicious breakfast choices.  No clean or pleasant views, thin walls, and a rudimentary shower with barely a shower pan underfoot. My vacation was soured during my stay there. Good value if you can brave it.

The hotel itself is basic and perfect for travelling on a budget. Very close to everything you need. The immediate area can feel a bit ‘unpolished’ but coming from a city myself I didn’t mind this, and I never had any trouble. The rooms were clean, and fresh towels were supplied each day. The breakfast was basic but everything you might need to get your day started. Staff are very friendly and accommodating. Would recommend if on a budget, but if money is not an issue, there are many nicer, more modern places to stay in Athens.

A good value, basic hotel. The location is good for all the major sights and the city centre, but the immediate area outside the hotel is very run down. Wasn’t an issue for us, but some may find it a bit unsettling.
Rooms clean and comfortable. Very quiet and the staff in the hotel were friendly. Breakfast we thought was good. For the price, it’s a good hotel.

The rooms were decent and did the trick. AC worked amazing. The main issue was the shower did not keep water in well and flooded the bathroom pretty easily, so make sure you open the drain.

The service was great and the staff was always friendly and ready to help us. The breakfast in the morning was fine, better than most US hotel breakfasts but not incredible.

The location for sightseeing was great for the price - only a 20 minute walk from the acropolis and 10 minutes from nice areas to walk around and hang out. However, the specific street the hotel is on is out of the ordinary with the amount of homeless. I walked back later in the night multiple times and saw lots of people getting a free stay outside the hotel. This, in combination with the need for a security guard in the nighttime hours and a police presence on multiple nights makes me reluctant to recommend this. I was satisfied for the price but would not recommend a group of girls stay here and walk down this street at night unless they taxi straight to the door.

I would stay again - good for a frugal traveler.

Hotel rate with breakfast included is great value for the money!
You pay a small tourist tax when you check in but it’s cheaper than Rome or most other European cities.
There is no bar restaurant in the hotel.
The room is basic. Shower is small but lots of hot water. The staff are excellent and help in anyway they can. Breakfast (included for us on Air Canada Vacations) was very good. Location is a 5 minute walk to Omonoias Square and subway and 15 minutes to Ministraki Square.
Wifi included.

The area outside the hotel is rundown and we saw a couple of needle users across the street a block up. There are open businesses on the street with people sitting outside so there are people around. We were there a week and no one ever bothered us! Turn right out of the hotel when walking and the main drag is literally a short block and a half walk. Pay attention to the uneven sidewalks. Security guard on duty at night out front of hotel.

You get what you pay for. 200 eur for 6 days, you probably already know this is the cheapest option for a hotel in Athens. Location-wise it's magnificent. Next to the Omonia metro station with easy connection to Acropolis, Plaka, Monasteraki and other sites. I expected rooms to be a bit shabby, so that wasn't an issue for me. The cleaning ladies are super nice and have regularly maintained our room. Slightly hiccoughy WiFi but we have unlimited data in the EU so it wasn't an issue. Air-con worked flawlessly and it was included in the price.
Not bad, considering downtown location, you got everything, prices ok, no bed bugs, water was warm, bathroom clean, WI Fi slow though, but old school hotel is recommended...

Bed bugs. That’s the only criteria that I have in a hotel, that it not have bed bugs. I did get bit by bed bugs here and it’s very itchy & painful now, so I wish I’d stayed somewhere else.  Bed bugs aside, I encountered the following Claridge Hotel:

-there’s only sheets on the bed and an optional old blanket from WWII in the closet
-a tremendous amount of noise
-vagrants/addicts/refugees milling around the area (and pulling their pants down right out front pissing)
-a toilet that continuously ran and bathroom light bulbs that burnt out
-a paint job that hasn’t been touched up since the Berlin Wall was still up
-weak wifi that you have to compete with others to get to a website
-old key system where you leave the metal key with the front desk when you go out.

None of these things would have bothered me enough to cancel the stay except for the bed bugs. Of course I inspected the sheets when we arrived but I didn’t see any so I didn’t mind the 70 year old furniture and old peeling paint.

chances are, if you’re reading this review you already know this hotel is the cheapest option in athens that’s not a hostel, and comes with a private bathroom; you’ve seen the low star reviews & you’re trying to decide if you should chance it & stay here because maybe you think everyone else is a bunch of spoiled whiny complainers.
I’m giving them a generous 2 stars because
1. the staff is nice.  both the helpful front desk & the housekeepers that do clean
2. the convenient location near the train, restaurants, stores, museums, walking distance to Acropolis
3. the cheap price, lowest on the travel booking sites
My spouse permanently revoked my hotel booking privileges after this place.   He said “if you have respect for yourself don’t stay here”. But my kids age 16 & 26 were fine with it.

Location wise, it is very accessible, a few steps away from Omonoia Station. Not to expect inside the hotel since it’s a 2 star, good for sleeping. The Old Omonoia restaurant is just outside and the food was tasteful with a good size of serving.

The 3 receptionist throughout the day were all very kind people. The rooms were alright quality with it needing a fresh lick of paint and upgraded TVs. The air conditioning was old but I saw new boxes of air conditioning in the hallway so I think they will be upgraded. The location was also great with a lovely view of a beautiful fountain and it was right next to great restaurants.

This hotel was brilliantly placed for restaurants, shops, acropolis and agora. The facilities are clean and all work, the breakfast is also fabulous. To top it off, the staff are fantastic, friendly, helpful and fun.
The view from the roof garden is amazing.
We had a brilliant stay, thank you.

Very good hotel in an amazing location.  Nothing fancy, just a really great hotel with brilliant breakfast.

Most of the main attractions are walkable,  and lots of shops and restaurants nearby.

Icing on the cake is the view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar.

Right in the prime location for visiting Athens, this hotel was clean, well run, and the rooftop restaurant offered an incredible view of the Acropolis in addition to excellent food & drinks.  Would revisit next time I’m in Athens.

I gave it 5 stars for the value of money. It really met the qualifications of a 3 star hotel. My room had a shower and bath tub. Be aware that the restaurant does not have view of Acropolis. But it was nice. The location of hotel is very good close to every where.

Comfy beds, location couldn't be better, great breakfast with lots of options, ONLY downside was very little sound proofing between rooms.
Loved the view from the rooftop restaurant. Food was excellent, but service was lacking.

The hotel itself is honestly very nice and clean, the staff downstairs are very friendly and were helpful to us and even held our luggage and set up a taxi.

The only negative in the room is that the AC in the room did not work at all.

Now the bar is a different story, staff did not seem as friendly. I will say though that the bar is beautiful and offers an amazing view of acropolis that you cannot beat.

However, the staff at the roof top bar were far from friendly. They offer a free voucher for a drink but don’t mention it’s only a cheap wine or bud light beer. They also seem annoyed if you use the voucher and only allow you sit in certain areas. In my opinion, maybe just don’t give out the voucher then.

Even when having drinks another night and paying for them, we found it hard to get service. The servers were dancing and drinking alcohol at the bar while we waiting for a while. I know they were just having a good time and I’m okay with that but when we asked for the anything we got some attitude and I’m not sure why…

Honestly, I would probably stay again due to the location, clean rooms and beautiful view of acropolis. Just giving my input in hopes some things can improve!!

Review is for the restaurant.
You will not be disappointed by the rooftop views. Reserve early to guarantee a table at the edge overlooking the square with unobstructed view of the Acropolis. We arrived just before sunset so got bit day and night views.
Casual to upscale feel and although menus limited the food is delicious. We hand the lamb appetizers and the grouper as the main course. The food was outstanding, possibly the best grilled fish we’ve ever eaten. Have dinner here and you won’t be disappointed.

Another GREAT rooftop bar/restaurant with views of the city and the Acropolis.  The environment is cozy and romantic.  Their drink menu is awesome with specialized crafted drinks made at your table. Friendly staff. Popular with trendy crowd. Highly recommend. We were recommended by a friend to check this bar out and it did not disappoint.

A for Athens is an exceptional hotel that offers a perfect blend of comfort, convenience, and stunning views. Located in the heart of the city, just steps away from Monastiraki Square, it’s the ideal base for exploring Athens’ historical landmarks. The rooms are modern, clean, and comfortable, but the highlight of this hotel is the rooftop bar, which offers breathtaking views of the Acropolis, especially at night. The breakfast is also fantastic, with a wide variety of delicious options. The staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful, making our stay even more enjoyable. If you’re looking for a central location with unbeatable views, A for Athens is a great choice!

We went to the rooftop bar for a sunset drink and the view was magnificent! The staff was friendly however if you just want to have a drink you cannot sit at the table. You can only stay standing near a middle block. I saw the food going past and it didn't appeal to me so I was happy to stand. The cocktails are interesting but if you want a classic you need to ask for it as not on the menu.

Great location, near shopping and touristic areas. The service is exceptional, professional and efficient. We found the room to be a bit small but it was comfortable, quiet and modern. The roof top restaurant with a view on the acropolis was excellent. They accommodated to our needs and the staff was very helpful. This helps relax and take in the atmosphere of Athens.

We are currently staying at the Amalia Hotel in Athens Greece. This hotel is wonderful. All the staff have been so helpful and nice. Location is in the Plaka neighborhood. So close to the best shopping area and historic sites. The Whisper rooftop restaurant bar has great prices, view of the Acropolis and staff. Air conditioned, clean room and mini fridge. Great price! Don’t over look this one!

Perfect spot. Everything is close enough to walk to and if you need to spend some time in the evening and don’t know what to do the park right across the street is so nice.
The dinner here is so good, and the service as well.

Showers are very small🫠🫣

3 stars, definitely not 4.
I don't know if they have renovated rooms but mine was extremely outdated with old, stained carpets. Bed was a little worn down but not HORRIBLE.
Very convenient location to shops and restaurants. Right across a nice park

Such warm hospitality! One of the best experiences I’ve had with a hotel EVER. The location is very much central and the crew is initiative, friendly and caring. If you see Andreas you know you’re in good hands!

I've stayed here on a few occasions; the outside of the Hotel and the reception is rather deceiving. A simple, old reception desk and a reception area with not much space. However, the rooms are clean and comfortable for one or two people, the toilet/shower are clean with complementary shower gel. The Hotel is in an excellent and very central location and you can't beat the price!
Close to all the attractions. Big room with comfortable bed. Rooms are very clean. Shower small. Breakfast is basic but nice. Friendly staff. Great value for the price paid.

The hotel is a genuine 2-star hotel:
- The room was mostly small, and there was not a good air circulation for it to vent
- The elevator did work but didn’t feel 100% reliable/safe
- We got there after 09 pm and the man who checked us in only asked for our names and gave us the key. At no point did they check our passports 🫨

Good thing was that there was people at the front desk 24/7. However, we didn’t feel comfortable enough to ask for suggestions or anything like that

Breakfast is on site, and though there is not a lot of variety, the products are of very good quality

Greek yogurt was the best I’ve ever tried in my life! And the honey was also amazing

They also offered cold cuts and cheese. Coffee, milk, tea, orange juice. And I think there were also some fruits

The staff in charge of breakfast and cleaning the rooms were very friendly

Place looked mostly clean, rooms were made every day and clean towels were left by default

Athens may be filled with soulless Airbnb rentals, each furnished with the same generic IKEA pieces—but Hotel Cecil is a refreshing exception. Like the city itself, Hotel Cecil isn't perfect, but its abundant charm and old-world character easily overshadow any minor imperfections. The prime location, the vintage Otis elevator, and functional WiFi are just added perks. Will be on the top of my list for my next visit to the city.
Smaller rooms but clean and comfortable. Friendly staff. Continental breakfast. Lots of shopping around and close to lots of things to do and see

My wife and I have just returned from Greece and stayed at this magnificent hotel on the first few nights and again on the last night. We could not have been happier! The rooms were clean and spacious with large comfortable beds, bathrooms were spotless and had thick towels, nice slippers and robes to enjoy. More importantly, I need to mention the kindness and professionalism of the staff!  Every one of them. Congratulations to the management for excellent training. Upon check in I was greeted by Dora who made me feel comfortable and welcome from the start. We asked her for dinner location suggestions and she offered many outstanding choices. She worked long hours. The bell staff was always willing to go out of their way to help. The breakfast was more than anyone could eat, and an extravagant display of so many items! Our rooms had balcony's and a great view of the city landscape. I enjoyed standing on them and watching the changing of the guards!  And finally on the last day Manio was so kind with our checkout. Sad to leave, but with fond memories of an enjoyable stay!

We actually checked in on the day after we were supposed to. My travel agents fault. No problem, they moved the day of check in and off we went. Took all of 5 minutes to correct and we were given a beautiful room with a view of the Acropolis. Rarely will you stay at such a clean, friendly and generous place. And even the bed was incredible. Would definitely stay again. Thank you.

A nice hotel with excellent service. The location is close to everything you would want to see in Athens! Rooms were pretty clean but they are a little dated. The breakfast had a good selection of items from bacon, sausage, eggs, fruit and pastries. The only comment on the breakfast would be the coffee and cappuccinos didn't taste great. Overall a nice hotel if you're visiting Athens!

The rooms are large! Rooms are clean! The staff is very accommodating. We enjoyed a breakfast buffet. There is a bar open in the evening on the mezzanine level.  There is a restaurant in the lobby.  Be aware that this hotel does not have a pool. This hotel was picked as part of our tour. Overall this hotel is a good choice.

Probably the noisiest hotel I have ever stayed at. The bedroom doors are very thin and you can hear housekeepings every move knock and bang, even the noise of plastic bags rustling. I’m sure another room or floor may have been different but even 6 floors up no peace! I leave my room impeccable and do not use all the towels and clutter available, so how about some mutual respect in the morning, smashing vacuum cleaners against the wall is war! Very clean room on arrival.

A budget-friendly and worthwhile place to stay. Highly recommended. While there's no common area for socializing or a shared kitchen, it's a great spot just for sleeping and conveniently close to the city center.

I was traveling alone. The reception staff were super friendly and always available. I needed to extend my reservation by one day and it was easy. I was in a shared room with 3 other women, so there were two bunk beds and a sink. I felt safe, the door has an electronic lock and you can leave your bags on the second floor. I recommend :)

Not worth the price, can find cheaper, better equipped places.
The terrace on the top looks worse than in the pictures, it's inhospitable, boasts some unusable remnants of a kitchen which this places doesn't have, can't see anything from there. The rooms are very bare, not even a coat hanger there.
A definite upside is that they have very nice bathrooms, clean and modern, with sufficiently but not totally opaque glass between them giving a taste of a kinky atmosphere. They have a spacious locker room for the luggage.

The location was the only good thing about this place but even that can't save it. The upstairs reeked of sewage and mildew. The hallways and bathroom looked like they've never been deep-cleaned a day in their life. Let alone a daily clean. We checked in and checked out within an hour because we couldn't stand the thought of sleeping there.

Not that good, but it matches the one-star classification.
Service is kind, 24 hours available.
Location is pretty central.
Rooms are small and with not good conditions.
Toilets are bad.
Good enough for one day if you are just stopping by.

Best choice we made won't be disappointed. Just very practical with everything you need. Clean rooms good staffs close to sight seeing spots and good price. Outside neighbors a bit run down but hotel itself good, did not bother us. Staff even packed us breakfast for our early departure, so very nice.

Oct 27-Oct 30/24
This was the best hotel for the price. It was less than CA$100 a night for two people (including tax). It also included free breakfast and free cancellation. I have never found one hotel, with this quality and price in any big cities. The most important thing was its location. This hotel is located within walking distances from all major world class attractions, such as museums and the Acropolis. Hundreds of restsurants and bakery shops. If you can walk, you don't even have to take any transportations. For me, the service was good and friendly. The room was clean and tidy. The breakfast was good enough for me.
This hotel was my top choice if I travel to Athens again.

We stay one night at this hotel and it did its job. We use it only to sleep as we were out and about all day.
The staff is friendly, the location is great and it includes breakfast (continental breakfast), with mostly anything that you need (nothing wow, but it was good).
The shower was tiny and it didn’t feel clean :(
The room is a bit outdated and so is the building, but I guess that’s why it’s no pricey for the location!

I spent a week here and I am very satisfied. This hotel serves as a perfect haven for active tourists. Convenient location, air conditioning, private toilets, new towels every day, friendly and helpful staff, good breakfasts - it's all there, and it comes at a pretty good price.

Great value for money.
Hotel is old but VERY CLEAN. Location is perfect, just few hundred meters from Monastiraki square like 5 minutes walk . The staffs are very friendly and helpful. 24hrs receptionist.
Breakfast is good too!! It’s been such a pleasure experience . I will definetly book
again whenever I come back to Athens .

Great location on a relatively quiet street about a 5 minute walk from the Syntagma metro. Practically all the sights we visited were within walking distance (at most, 25 minutes). Tons of restaurants in the area and the neighborhood has a great vibe. Very friendly and professional staff. Good breakfast is included. Our room was very quiet.

Modern hotel in the heart of Athens.  Walking distance to numerous restaurants and the acropolis.  The elevators are tiny.  Rooms were quiet.  The view is an adjacent building.  There's a parking garage next door but we had a tour driver so we didn't need parking.

The place is convenient and near all attractions.  However, the room was noticeably infested with bed bug.
My daughter and I got bitten all over our bodies despite we had different room.
A little old style hotel in the city main area in a narrow street. Rooms were clean with balcony. The location was excellent.

Prime location, easy access, great rooms, comfy mattresses, spacey bathroom, filling breakfast with many options and above all, excellent staff. I mostly interacted with Giannis and he's definitely the most professional and also the friendliest hotel employee I've ever interacted with. We were a group of 13 people and everyone had a story where he helped us, all in just 2 days! A short, but absolutely amazing experience. Thank you!

It’s easy to walk to restaurants, train station and all the attractions that bring people to Athens. The staff was super helpful and kind. The air conditioner worked really well. The WiFi went in and out. The area is a bit dodgy. I wouldn’t want to walk around there at night. You get what you pay for

Okay hotel in a neighbourhood with many sketchy people, not recommended to arrive at too late times, especially because one day after arriving around 2:00 am the hotel doors were closed and the number I had to call was always busy after even 5 tries. Breakfast is 8 euros, and you would expect more variety or some warm food like scrambled eggs for this price. Rooms on the fourth floor are nice with nice view from balcony, but the cards have to be reprogrammed every day at the reception. In the second room they put me the AC wasn't working, the TV signal was horrible. I would only recommend it to brave people who have plan B when their hotel locks them out for the night.

So the city is just sadly so dirty, tagged and filled with the homeless. It felt dirty but not dangerous. The hotel itself was nice as a previous review art was interesting but it was lying around in places. It looked like they had not gotten done putting it up. The television did not  work and no idea how to call down. The staff was lovely and kind. We did not ever partake in the breakfast but there were so many awesome bakeries/coffee shops. It was easy to get around but we will probably stay in plaka or mythraoki next visit. It just felt more vacation spot.

For me a bed has to be comfortable and the AC needs to work and it’s quiet. It meets alll those needs. Breakfast is good and the service from the owner lady is 5*
They could use minor your ups like the grout was moldy looking in the shower but that’s common.
For the price it’s a great value and I keep coming back. Not in the core center and not the nicest neighborhood but again the Hotel is clean and safe.

Good hotel but a bit pricey for what it offers (approx €100 euros a night including taxes)
You need to walk up a set of maybe 5-8 steps to get to the hotel so not really wheelchair friendly.
Receptionist and housekeeping were respectful and helpful
Room was a bit tight and toilets had no lock and the door was glass albeit frosted glass.
They don’t have money exchange service.
Lift doors have no sensors so you must be careful.
Nice quiet rooms
Has a swimming pool
Cosy reception area

Excellent location and the neighborhood is a vibe! Really nice to explore the center of Athens from here. Also a great view from the rooftop. The rooms are not excellent quality but honestly more than fine if you’re out the entire day exploring the city.

Located in the heart of the Exarcheia district, this hotel offers basic amenities at a very reasonable price. Our room came with a balcony that provided a nice view of the urban surroundings. We spent a week here without any problems. The room was cleaned daily by the friendly housekeeping staff. While the breakfast provided is quite basic, the area has numerous excellent bakeries if you prefer to grab breakfast on the go.

Perfect location in vibrant and safe area. Good food and drinks everywhere. Room was basic but ok for what we paid. Issue with this hotel - staff in lobby are NOT friendly or welcoming at all. Traveling as a same sex couple - was asked rudely twice upon check in if double bed was ok. He was very surprised when we said ‘yes’. If you’re going to work in a hotel in a large vibrant city, get used to it. Be more friendly and welcoming.

Breakfast was the same every day, not the best options. Discovered bedbugs, there was random holes in the walls in the room, cockroches (I saw them on stairs). The staff was ok, the cleaning was fine. In my room, the refrigerator didn't even work. I liked the location, but I'd be still careful in that neighbourhood.
Rooms very basic, breakfast even more so. However Manager very friendly and approachable. In the center of the city transport links to everything just a few minutes walk away.

Breakfast buffet was excellent and fresh. Best scrambled eggs ever. Loved the espresso machine. Shower decent size for Greece. Elevators were reliable and fast.  Front desk staff very helpful. Do not hesitate to double park in front to load and unload.

I stayed here in 2018 with my family and when re-visiting Athens again this year I immediately booked a stay here as it was perfect then and I am more than pleased to say it is still perfect now!  So central, great friendly staff, fantastic view of the Acropolis from the lovely rooftop bar.  Perfect breakfast – what more can I say!!

I highly recommend this hotel! The cost-benefit is excellent!! Some positive points: the staff is very kind, polite and attentive; the hotel is right in the city center, you can visit the Acropolis and other tourist attractions on foot; the hotel has a terrace with a beautiful view of the Acropolis; the rooms are clean, well-equipped, with good amenities and the room service is great; the breakfast is quite decent.

Whenever I am in Athens, this hotel is my top choice. Conveniently located just a few minutes from Monastiraki metro station, it offers both comfort and accessibility.
The staff are always friendly and courteous, ensuring a pleasant stay. The rooms are consistently clean and well-maintained. Breakfast is simple yet delicious, catering to various preferences. The highlight is the charming rooftop bar, providing a lovely view of the city and the Acropolis.

Great location, amazing view from rooftop bar! Staff is so friendly and helpful. Rooms are very nice; not huge, but have everything you need. Now I know why a friend of mine stays here whenever she comes to Athens!

Great attentive and helpful staff, amazing breakfast included. Rooms are very urban as well as location. It's an easy walk to the main areas but one thing I wish we had known was that just a side street off the plaza across the street is a more charming walk into the best areas towards Plaka. We walked the rather dirty & congested main road up & down many times before realizing there are incredible streets and restaurants, cafes along the way.

Feeling at home from arrival to departure. The best choice in Athens; I was very satisfied with their kindness and professionalism. The customer is showered with attention. Upon my arrival, they informed me that they were offering me a free room upgrade, assigning me the suite. The room was perfect, clean, spacious, and equipped with all the comforts—we couldn't have asked for anything better. The staff were always attentive, smiling, and kind. The hotel's location is excellent; you can reach all points of interest on foot without needing public transport. It also has a secure and monitored parking lot. We will definitely return

Very attentive and friendly staff. When we requested anything, it arrived to our room within minutes. Walkable to the Acropolis and many famous landmarks in Athens.

Air conditioning works better than many other hotels we’ve stayed in Europe. The only negative was the doors close pretty loudly so it can wake you up in the room when someone else closes their door, but we would still recommend this hotel to others staying in Athens!

Very disappointing stay!
Hotel is old and the room feels very used. 200 years old thin supper used towels...
We booked a suite from Agoda on arrival received standard room, they didn't want to provide us what we paid for in the beginning..Make sure you get what you booked because it feels like a trick..

Over priced.
Low value for money.
Not recommended

Wonderful stay. The staff at the hotel reminds you what great, friendly service feels like.
Restaurant is wonderful and the breakfast was amazing. Location is perfect with easy walk to everywhere.
Would 100% recommend.

The location is perfect - it is in a very lively area, and close walking distance to everything. Despite being in a busy area, noise is not an issue. It is close to lots of shops and excellent food/dining options. The place also feels secure/safe given the 24 hour reception. We were warmly greeted each time we came and went from the accommodation. We were lucky to have a balcony and view of the acropolis from our room on the 4th floor! Other pros of this accommodation is it has a comparatively earlier check-in time. Shared bathroom is not too bad surprisingly, good water pressure and it is all kept clean.

Main con: Wifi did not work well in the room at all. There are lots of stairs to climb if you’re on the 4th floor, but this is not uncommon in Europe! They have a lift available for luggage only.

I enjoyed my stay here :) I had my own room with its own bathroom, which altogether was clean and great, the location was good and the staff were friendly. I remember breaking something in the bathroom by accident, but the staff were kind about it.

Superb location! Very good rooms considering the cost, they were clean and bigger than other cheap accommodation options i’ve stayed in. I also stayed on the 4th floor and there was no street noise at all, but I couldn’t say for lower levels. Finally, the staff were incredibly friendly, and “available 24/7 for your service.” Excellent experience and I would stay again. Highly recommend for young travellers on a budget!

Avoid!!!  AVOID 🆘🆘🆘 Wasn’t clean.  I was  abused by the male boss. don’t be fool by its good location, you need feel safe, have a clean room for a  good night sleep after a day out and back to your room with  welcoming  staffs, not missable faces  and tiny area

My room was simple (might not be to everyone's liking, but suited me fine.) Clean and the staff very helpful. The view from the 5th floor terrace was worth the hike up the stairs and to enjoy take out dinner! Well located not for from Metro station,  restaurants and the sights.

great hotel with super friendly staff, all touristic sites are in walking distance. superb water pressure in the shower. breakfast buffet is large. maybe one little thing they can do is to place a ashtray outside for the smokers. great place to stay when you are in athens

The free breakfast is a joke. It’s disgusting. I will never come here again. This hotel cost too much per day. The food is horrible as far as the breakfast in the morning. too many people and it appears they have never eaten before, but it’s not Greek traditional food very a lot of bread products. The hard boiled eggs are over cooked as an example, very disappointed. I do not blame the help, They were overwhelmed and did the best they could with restocking and turning over tables. The rooftop upstairs bar great place. The views are amazing..

Great location  near to metro but average room quality for the price.
Has many rooms and kept clean.
The roof too has a distant view of the Acropolis. Many restaurants and tavernas nearby also the market is walking distance.

I really didn't like the room I was in, mostly because it didn't even remotely resemble what was advertised on the hotel's website. I was given a very poor room.
Apart from that, sheets were stained, I saw blood and who knows what else on those. The bottom sheet had little holes in it. I'd recommend changing sheets when they're no longer fit for use.
The carpet was full of hair and dirty.
The shower temperature would constantly change, which didn't add to the experience.
Pillows were uncomfortable.
Around 8.30 in the morning, the cleaning staff simply entered my room, like that.

Now, the staff is the only positive note on an otherwise very substandard stay. Staff was polite and helpful throughout the experience and I am very grateful for them.
One note: the rooftop restaurant is beautiful.

The rooms was tiny! Not what you expected regarding the pictures.
The carpet wasn't clean, there were clumps of hair in it. There was a pubic hair still on the toilet.
The bathroom was maybe 1.5m2 big. The shower was tiny tiny corner in a triangle shape to save space. So when you showered you touched that dirty shower curtain and I wished that I could take a shower all over again, but I would touch it again because it was so small (less than 0.5m2).

The breakfast was ok, which gives them 2 stars instead of 1.

The hotel is a nice budget place, near the centre for a reasonable price. Breakfast is ok, but the same every day, enough not to be hungry. The good thing you do not to be  on time, all food is brought even if you are after  the time. The staff is  friendly and really nice, speak a lot of languages. The neighborhood is disgusting, be aware of your suitcases, immigrants and drug addicts may steal if you do not pay attention.

The staff in the hotel is good and the morning breakfast offered is quite tasty as well. The main attraction about the hotel is it's proximity to all major historical and archeological tourist attraction in Athens and it's competitive pricing. The locality around the hotel though feels a little unsafe, especially late at night. The room we used was basic and the decor was old, but everything was fully functional. Do not expect any view kind of good view as most of the rooms face other buildings or the congested street

In the winter (I can't speak for the summer), this is an excellent budget hotel.  At an exceedingly low price, I got friendly service, an ample breakfast, a decent room, a hot shower, and a location close to downtown.  The neighborhood is not upscale, but it is quite decent--far better than some comments would have you believe.  I forgot pants and 2 shirts in the closet when I left for Crete.  When I came for a second stay 3 weeks later, the staff, who had kept them not knowing that I would return, delivered them to my new room.  In the budget category, this hotel is hard to beat.

The staff is amazing. Some of the best people we have ever met. Main problem we had was that the a.c isn't working great in some rooms. Also it's not in the greatest of neighborhoods. We enjoyed this place despite those facts.

Decent enough. Breakfast includes eggs, sausages, cheese, cereal, basic items. Bedroom was a bit spare and only had one visible outlet halfway up the wall which was annoying. The hotel is located in a rather dismal looking area, but the main square and metro are less than ten minutes away.

I had the pleasure of staying at the Electra Palace Hotel in Athens for nearly a week, and I can’t recommend it enough! The hotel’s location is absolutely fantastic – both from my room and the rooftop restaurant, I had breathtaking views of the Acropolis, making every morning and evening unforgettable.

I was particularly impressed with the outstanding service. The staff were always friendly, professional, and incredibly attentive, which made the stay even more enjoyable. A special thanks to the concierge, who provided excellent tips and was always helpful in making sure I experienced the best of the city.

For those driving, the parking in the city center is a huge advantage. In Athens, this is not something you find easily, and the hotel made it super convenient to explore the city without any hassle.

Overall, it was a wonderful stay at an excellent hotel – I’ll definitely be back!”

I stayed at this property two different times for my trip.  The first time we found the rooms to not be very clean but we were exhausted and only here one night so we opted to just not make a big deal about it.  After traveling around the islands we came back for our last night.  The rooms were the same. NASTY! This time we complained.  The front desk acted like they didn't believe me.  I offered to go up and show them what I was talking about but no one seemed to care.  Instead they send housekeeping up to look around.  They also told me they arranged a transfer to the airport in the morning.  When we got down to check out no one arranged anything.  Pitiful customer service.  Would never stay here again.  I would however come eat at the rooftop restaurant. It is good. One last note. Since the ac only runs with the room key it took until 1am for the room to be cool.

Great place to go for a late night after drink. After having dinner downtown, we decided to walk up to the rooftop bar and check it out. The view was so awesome. We decided to stay for a nightcap. My wife had the port and I had the Blantons old-fashioned. Beautiful evening nice breeze great company. Highly recommend if you have a chance to go by.

This hotel has excellent location in the down town of Athens, close to all the shopping areas, historic places and restaurants. It has a beautiful roof top restaurant with views to Acropolis and very good quality food. I highly recommend this hotel for your stay in Athens.

We had dinner at the roof restaurant of the hotel. The food was exceptional the service was impecable and most importantly Lazaros our server was the best! Always with a smile he was so patient and suggested the best food and wine. Really made our evening!!! Thank you. We will definitely come back.

Myrto was exceptional in our visit in Athens, I would recommend the stay here for anyone visiting Athens. She arranged our transit to the airport and recommended a mini breakfast box right before our flight back to the U.S. I love you Greece 🇬🇷 and warm welcomes you have my wife and I during our honeymoon.

We as a family checked in this so called 4 star hotel but were shocked to see the very average facilities.
Especially the reception people had poor communication skills and were  very rude .
The hotel room was the smallest size with the just minimal facilities and very little space available to move around.
The bath cubicle was just good enough for an average build person to stand and I hit the glass door more number of times than my heart rate while taking the bath . Water was leaking from the lower end to spoil the dry area.
We were shocked to know that even the first day water bottles were chargeable which we were not informed at the time of checking.
Even the additional coffee sachet was available @5£/ sachet ( such thing which we never experienced in the last 30 years).
We had rhe worst experience in this hotel & We strongly DON'T recommend this hotel to anyone.
Excellent option in Athens!!! Warm and kind staff. Clean room, outstanding view from the rooftop restaurant and plenty of options for breakfast! It's a 5/5 recommendation for your trip in Athens.

The location and service were nice but the rooms had such thin walls. We can hear people talking in the hallway and the room. We also heard vacuuming / cleaning from upstairs room at 7:30 am. Not sure if this is correct time to do this.

We stayed at the Polis Grand a few times during our trip in Greece. The rooms were comfortable, and the staff were all very friendly and helpful! I wanted to call special attention to Costas! He was there almost every time we were in the lobby, and he made a point to remember us and greet us with familiarity, making the check in / check out process feel a lot smoother! I really appreciated him!!

The WiFi is so terrible I wasn’t unable to send an important message, there are barely appliances in the hotel room, small tv screen, there wasn’t any toiletries like shampoo and toothbrush, although the location was in the middle of the city so it was perfect to go to places. Not recommended if staying for a long period of time. If u r just looking for a place to sleep but also shop and do sightseeing, then it is recommended

This is a horrible depressing place. The beds are like rocks. The bedding is disgusting and you could kill yourself in the shower because you need to step over a 2 foot wall to get into it. There are so many hotels in this same area for same price. We paid 180CAD. It’s beyond awful. the only star is for location

The only good thing about this place is the location.

The morning and cleaning staff are nice, but the late night staff is awful. Doesn't bother to leave the couch and when you ask for anything he mumbles back at you in Greek, clearly pissed. We asked for simple towels and he threw a fit.

The rooms are super dated. Simple maintenance can elevate the experience. The bathrooms need a lot of love, lucky I'm shirt and can fit. But the half size tub that spills water everywhere is a nightmare.

Luckily no bed bugs found so that's another win.

Oh and the weirdest thing ever, they ask you to give your card to them whenever you leave the hotel for "security" reasons, but ironically they opened the room once and provided an extra key for us simply asking for that and a room number, no verification of identy whatsoever. I can literally access anyone's room?

Wouldn't stay there. Was disappointed and honestly can't wait to leave it.
