Firstly, I'll just state that the location is PHENOMENAL! Right in the centre, seemingly no more than a 10-15 minute walk from any of the sights. Brilliant.

What must be said though is wow this hotel is feeling tired and dated. It's perfectly serviceable, but only just. The rooms a very narrow, but somewhat long, so the square metre space sounds a lot bigger than it feels. The tv is about 20" and high up on wall at an angle that made it unviewable. Not that you could watch it anyway, as it only had terrestrial channels, all in Greek, so if travelling with a toddler/child and you wanted to put tv on for 10 minutes to distract them while you get a shower, that's out the window. Speaking of out the window, the balcony is about as wide as a small chair (which it conveniently had one placed on it, for size reference) but it was very rickety so you wouldn't want to spend any significant time out there somebody probably needs to replace the piece of wood that they had as a handrail there, as it was very wobbly. Oh, and that tv I mentioned? Very inconveniently placed at head height, right next to the patio door, so if you're 6 foot plus like I am, right in line for you to really crack your head on it 👍 wonderful. Actually caused me to split my head bleeding the final time it happened.

Another way the room ain't built for anybody 6 foot and over, the shower height, which genuinely had the shower head at about chest height on me, and I had to duck/bend my head anyway, as the wall jutted out where I should stand up straight.

Honestly though, all that said, the people were lovely, the room still served us as a place to sleep, but I probably wouldn't visit again or recommend it to anybody unless there were no other options nearby, which there are dozens of.

Edit: wanted to pop back to point out another issue actually which might not effect or be important to everybody but it certainly did us, and that's the fridge in the room was woeful. Genuinely put the tiniest "chill" on things but that was it. Can't even call it a chill really, but kept things just colder than room temperature. For somebody travelling with a baby that needs to store milk this isn't ideal. The milk kept going off and ended up resulting in our little lad actually being sick one night. What's odd is the fridge was giving off a tremendous amount of heat too, so it's probably just outright broke but not being looked at, which is just another sign of the outdated nature of muchnofnthe hotel.

A diamond on the rough! The Price really fantastic, Very Central to everything, restaurants, coffee shops, market, different shops around. Very Clean, and Secure. The staff are very friendly and helpful.
The Amazon Hotel, Athens was centrally located. Within walking distance of all attractions in Athens. All staff were very helpful and friendly. Wouldn't hesitate to stay there again 🙂

This is a very nice hotel. It is close to the syntagma and it is just by the ermou streets. You can walk to the main attractions  including monastraki and plaka. The hotel has a very nice breakfast and the staff is friendly. The rooms are clean and they are airconditioned.

Perfect location …right next to ermou with all shops and restaurants minutes away!
Rooms are a little small ,but we didn’t spend much time inside so not a big deal!

There was SO MUCH noise in our room like we could barely sleep it was insane. WOULD NOT RECOMMEND. this was on a Friday but still our room was right in the center of things and I wouldn't ever stay here again. Very disappointed

Full value for money.  Located 5 minutes from the Matrot station and right in the center of the parking lots and shopping avenues.  A small and clean hotel, well maintained and a varied and excellent breakfast.  highly recommend

The room was clean, great location! We walked 10 minutes to the acropolis and museum. The rooftop bar has center view of the acropolis and spectacular at night! The breakfast was good. The front desk was very helpful booking transportation for us for the airport. Restaurants are all close by as well no car needed. The price was very reasonable.

The Hotel Adonis Athens was recommended to me by a friend and It was just fabulous. It is in a quiet street just two minutes walk from beautiful restaurants and shopping and walking distance to the Acropolis and other attractions.   From the moment we arrived we were made to feel so welcome and the staff were all amazing and so helpful, even organising our taxis for us.  The room we booked was wonderful with a large balcony and view of the Acropolis. It was spotless clean and lovely bathroom.  There is a lovely bar open in the evening overlooking the Acropolis which is lovely and the breakfast had a good selection of food, served in the same area overlooking the Acropolis.  I would most definitely come back to this hotel again and recommend it.

we enjoyed our stay at this hotel, as the location was excellent, and the room was comfortable and had everything we needed. The air conditioning was very effective.  Quite a bit of sound is transmitted through the hallway and open stairs, but we generally slept relatively well, except when someone had an early flight.  Breakfast from the roof terrace was outstanding.
The staff were great and the location is very near shops, food, and the Acropolis, which the hotel roof dining area provided a great view. The hotel worked out perfectly! Rooms are small but clean.

Very clean hotel located in Plaka situated close  to attractions and great restaurants.  The breakfast was great and the staff, very polite and helpful. Our room was small, but all we needed, very quiet  with comfortable bed. Oh!  And the view  of the Acropolis, from the roof, where breakfast was served, was amazing! I’d  stay here again and recommend it to others! Thanks, Rick Steves,  for the recommendation!
Lovely  boutique  hotel. Small but very adequate  rooms, with a balcony. Much better than average breakfast included. Great location, very helpful staff. Highly recommended.

Clean room and good air conditioning. Good bathroom.
We got 2 singles, rather than a double/queen which was odd.
Nearabouts Omonia square/metro. Dodgy part of town, lots of drug use. Taxi driver told me not to walk the streets alone.
But I found it fine in the daytime, it’s actually fine. It was so central - I walked to all the sites including Acropolis etc. Perfect location that way. Just be careful about pickpockets/etc and stick to main roads, and travel in the daytime.
Breakfast was continental buffet.
And we were able to have cereal/coffee/biscuits when we needed a really early checkout.
One odd thing - encountered a hostile receptionist when returning to the hotel who demanded to recheck my room number/that I was a hotel patron… a bit of a shame as otherwise I thought people were fine.

The experience was extremely bad. Have you guys ever heard of any hotel charges extra 25 euros for bring people back. I can understand if the reception asked the second person to check in for security reasons, but what is the point for them to ask for extra charge? The receptionist said you paid for one person’s price and you didn’t pay for the second one. Are they charging not based on the number of room but on the number of person? Strange, absolutely SCAM. On top of that, the room is not clean at all. limescale in the baths and toilet, disgusting. AVOID THEM!!!

Great hotel and good sized rooms for solo travelers. The only thing I personally did not like was the view of the abandoned block of flats in the back of the hotel where my room was. But overall it was great.

They have a great breakfast variety with good coffee and juice selection. Very friendly and welcoming staff.

First of all: apart from one lady, all staff members were friendly and accommodating. The hotel is very central and there's a great breakfast buffet. There's a tiny elevator which helps you to get your luggage upstairs. The bed in my second room was very comfy (the first room looked nice but the bed was hard as a rock). Overall, good value for money. 👍

Now my issues: The first night I was in a room with a very hard bed. It was very uncomfortable and I could barely sleep. When I told the receptionist in the morning I'd like to change rooms, she first told me that all rooms had the same mattresses but eventually agreed to let me move. In the second room the bed was super comfy, so she definitely didn't say the truth about all mattresses being the same. Also, after moving (on my second night) someone opened my door around midnight even though I had locked it from the inside. It was someone who had a key card to my room, not sure whether another guest's key card accidentally worked on my door or if it was a staff member. Anyway, I screamed and the person left. Afterwards, I didn't feel safe in the hotel anymore. When I told the staff about it the next morning, she said that was not possible and didn't take my concern seriously. Instead , she implied I was making it up. Very disappointing reaction! I asked for the camera recordings of that night to prove that I was telling the truth and she told me there were no cameras upstairs. What the heck?! 🤨

Otherwise I really liked the hotel and all the other staff members were friendly, but this one lady clearly doesn't like people. 🤷

Everything about this apartment is fantastic—the location, the facilities, and the spacious terrace where you can enjoy the lively music from the tavern downstairs while people-watching.

The team of the property is the most amazing, welcoming and friendly team! 🫰

The apartment has everything you need for a comfortable stay, and it's ideally located near bars, restaurants, dance clubs, Monastiraki station, and sightseeing spots. Just bring earplugs for the noise, and you'll sleep perfectly! We had a wonderful time and would love to return.

We wanted somewhere that would showcase the best of Athens and Live in Athens did just this. Our accomodation was impeccable, clean and comfortable. It would us at the heart of life in Athens. Our kids loved it and so did we. We could walk to all the main attractions. We were surrounded by hundreds of restaurants and bars. We fell asleep listening to the nightlife of Athens. The noise never bothered us. It made us feel part of the life in Athens.

Cody was our best friend. Full of advice. He never let us done once and his advice made a different to our stay.

Wonderful hotel and extremely good value for money. The bed was large and very comfortable and the apartment was large and very clean. They have a stove and microwave so you can cook food. Amazing location in the heart of the city. Will stay here the next time I am in Athens!

Can't go wrong with these apartments. Part of the trend, it's situated at the very centre of Athens.

You're literally at the center point to reach any destination you wish to whilst visiting Athens.

Don't forget to have a look around all the bars and eateries dotted around.

The people who work for Live in Athens are excellent. They’re responsive, clear and kind.

The apartment is also pleasant.

If you don’t party late into the evening though (I’m writing this after 1am listening to drunks cackle, hoot and holler), don’t stay in the Psyri apartment. In addition to a very lively night life (could be a plus for you) they have a space on their roof where you can take your alcoholic drinks. I’m listening to people make a lot of noise scrapping chairs and tables right now right above my bedroom.  If you want to sleep, choose another one of their locations.
All night we couldnt sleep because of narkoman people who used drags and prostitutes. They are crying loudly and fighting.  I dont know what to do impossible to sleep
All the staffs are friendly. Very affordable. The room is neat. Walking distance from main tourist spots and train station.
Terrible is the word
XXX Hotel but if the night catches you it's a good choice.
Good

Our stay at the Pan hotel was absolutely fantastic! The property is stunning, with beautiful surroundings and impeccably clean rooms that made us feel right at home. The staff were exceptional, providing top-notch service throughout our stay. A special shout-out to Yianni (receptionist), who truly went above and beyond to assist my family and me in planning our travels to our next destination. His help was invaluable, and he made our trip even more enjoyable. Big thanks to Liza (breakfast room) such a lovely person.Highly recommend the Pan hotel and look forward to returning in the future!

I would like to commend the staff at the front desk, I had the pleasure of meeting the concierges of each shift and their level of costumer service was outstanding. The hotel is in a perfect location, walking distance from the center and the area has lots of restaurants and activity so it is very safe.
The room although very basic was very clean and had everything one needs. I suggest that management uses some decorative accent on the walls to make it more appealing, just a touch and also some touches to revamp the bathroom. In conclusion, I recommend this hotel, the staff really made the difference.

Good location and nice breakfast however we had a few issues. The main one was that the street and restaurants were so loud all night, you could hear people chatting and singing all night and the windows didn’t block out the sound. The curtains also didn’t block out the light. There was mould on the bathroom ceiling, the bathroom was very small and our mini fridge was wobbly. I wouldn’t stay here again.

The Pan Hotel staff were very helpful. A special mention to John on Reception. We stayed here twice pre and post our visit to some nearby islands. The rooms were very clean and the breakfast good  The location is excellent. Very good value. Thank you

The hotel is very central and an easy walk to Syntagma Square for access to the metro system. The staff were helpful and the breakfast was good.  We were well looked after by staff for breakfast.  The lift is tiny - a tight squeeze with your luggage. Its central location means that it is quite noisy (even when on the 8th floor).  The walls are pretty thin and we had a very noisy neighbour.  The beds were comfortable and clean.  The bathrooms were clean (but very small showers).  We only stayed one night as an overnight stop on our journey home.

Great hotel, made by the staff. There was one blunt woman on reception when we arrived, but all the others were wonderful.

There was a nice chap there most of the time and he couldn’t do enough for us. The cleaners,  restaurant staff, and especially the ladies working at (a terrific) breakfast every morning made such a difference.

Can’t believe the reviews moaning constantly. We stayed several nights, so let me address a few.

The lift was fine. Wasn’t particularly slow. The pool was great and it has to close at some stage during the day to be cleaned. You could swim in it and look across to the Parthenon. Cool! The food was good, including that we purchased in the top floor restaurant. Yes, the rooms are a little dated, but they are clean and spacious and importantly the shower and bed were decent.

What more could you ask…

Finally, Athens is a working city, populated by several million people in the urban area. It will have ‘rough’ areas. But the hotel isn’t in one, as some reviews say. It’s just off a main shopping thoroughfare. We walked back to it, late, every night. There are homeless people, but they didn’t even talk to us, let alone threaten us.

Personally, I felt safer late at night than I do walking through my home town in England. Also, while we were there, we saw wonderful people helping them with food and especially water.

This part of the city has almost a grid pattern and there are plenty of alternative routes to walk to and from the hotel on. We walked past them, as I say, many times.

If you want a solid hotel, with great staff, smashing breakfast, rooftop views, in the centre of a vibrant, colourful, friendly city, then I think you could do a lot worse. We liked it very much.

Recently, I stayed in the Novus City Hotel in Athens, Greece, for 6 nights. The hotel's location is awesome, just 200 meters from the metro station. The room is clean and provides all modern facilities. The buffet breakfast contains a lot of options, and the food quality is very good. The rooftop restaurant gives a clear view of the Acropolis at night, and I recommend having your dinner once to enjoy the view with Greek food/salad. Last but not least, jelly chocolates at the reception are an excellent way to welcome the guests to the Hotel. Overall, I found the Novus City Hotel to be very good for travelers and recommended it for your stay in Athens, Greece.

Unsafe area!!! Every local we met told us this is the most unsafe area in the city. One block up there are homeless drug addicts all around. They didn’t seem aggressive but it was unsightly and sad. This hotel looks very nice on the surface but everything is cheap underneath. The woman at the front desk was very rude unfriendly and unhelpful. All of the other staff were excellent especially the front of house manager he was very nice. The upstairs restaurant pool and view were all great. the food was good and portions were huge. The furniture in the room was falling apart and there’s a window in the shower!? 🤷🏽‍♀️ our key deactivated every day and we had to go to the desk to reactivate it. The elevator is extremely slow but it runs fine and is clean.

We stayed here for two nights before starting our island hopping tour. The rooms are clean and spacious. The breakfast ample with a good variety. It is in a bit of a down trodden area but we turned right out of the hotel as well as left and wasn't threatened, just very sad to see so many lonely people

Location  is not great from the point of view of security being 200m from a drug addiction colony.

However  the stass are fantastic and gave obe approach warnings not to turn right when leaving the hotel and only turn left.

The pool has a great view of the Acropolis at night

360 Degrees Hotel in Athens is a perfect place to stay for every reason you could think of! It’s in the Monostiraki neighborhood which is the center of all the neighborhoods. It is very close to shopping, good restaurants and all the attractions; Agora, Hadrian’s Library, Parthenon etc…

We checked in easily (online) and our room was directly viewing the bustling square. It was clean and had all the amenities we needed. Breakfast was 10+! Everything was neatly put out, fresh with a huge variety of items! The coffee was fresh brewed every morning and they continually put out hot steamed cream.
Surprisingly, the restaurant for dinner wasn’t that good and I’d have to rate that a 2. All food items were sub par and many people around us complained to the manager or left the food. The view of the Parthenon is amazing from this rooftop so I would highly recommend just going there for a drink and elsewhere for a nice dinner.
I would definitely stay here again as it’s the perfect location for everything you want to do in Athens! Hotel front desk staff were all very nice and super helpful!

Went to the rooftop. Terrible service. The manager, his second (bouncer?), and the waiter all had a terrible attitude. Very arrogant. The rooftop has a great view of the Acropolis if you get seated by that end. So they believe service is not important. Yet I saw at least four more rooftops around that have the same view. The tables not on the side of the Acropolis could have a great view as well, but they put some trees that block it. Definitely need a new decoration person.
They had a very poor food offering, only 5 different dishes.
I recommend going to one of the other nice rooftops around.

I highly recommend this hotel for a variety of reasons.
1. The location is amazing. You are a short walking distance from all the major sites in downtown Athens.
2. The breakfast is extremely fresh and delicious.
3. The view from the breakfast area is breathtaking.
4.The Room is spacious and clean.
5. The staff is very friendly and helpful.

However, if you are a light sleeper, you might need ear plugs as the rooftop bar does not turn down the music until 2-3 AM.
Located near the main attractions that Athens has to offer including historical sites, shopping, food, and drinks.  Enjoyed our stay, would recommend!
Very clean with good staff. But no fire safety or exits. No power in my room. Nowhere to hang towels in the bathroom. No outdoor space. Low flow shower. No coffee in the room. No bin .

Good value hotel with excellent service (thanks Maria) good buffet breakfast, and convenient location.  Comfy bed and rooms well cleaned daily.  Stunning view of Acropolis from rooftop cafe and some rooms.  Immediate area looks downtrodden but is quite vibrant with the market and restaurants/shops nearby.  Easy walk to sites and metro to airport.

The hotel it great, with very clean rooms and comfortable beds. Breakfast is also very nice, with gluten-free options such as biscuits or bread available and well packaged to avoid cross-contamination (celiac disease). The hotel is very recommended.

Location is also very nice, less than 10 minutes away from the city center. However, there are a few streets (one or two) that need to be crossed walking from the city center that are a bit dark and where you experience "strange" people/atmosphere.

I would repeat this hotel anyway, because the services are great and it is practically in the city center.

Loved the location near the central market and great restaurants and nightlife. Much preferred this area to the tourist clogged Plaka. Hotel room was clean and the staff were truly exceptional, they were so helpful with anything we needed. And we had the best view from our room! I would gladly stay here again.

I'm happy that I stayed at this hotel, because its central location, close to everywhere, is in the Munistiraki area. close to the metro.
The hotel is very clean, rooms at an excellent level of maintenance. good breakfast.
excellent service from all the employees and especially many thanks to the smiling and sympathetic Angela from the reception office.

Once you get passed the food market in front of the hotel it's pretty okay. Rooms are clean worth the price. The lady servicing drinks on the roof top was really personaliabe and nice. The views from roof top are breathtaking. We stayed one night before our cruise.

The hotel boasts a fantastic location and has recently undergone renovations. While the rooms may be smaller compared to other establishments in the same price range and area, the staff and service team are exceptionally friendly and accommodating. I would recommend this hotel for short stays, such as one or two nights.

While the hotel has been renovated the suite we had was comprised of several small rooms with a gigantic and essentially useless dressing/closet area.  The light switches were incredibly  confusing and I'm a 30 year construction person.

The bar next door had great views although the bartender  thought a martini meant martini & rossi

We stayed here before and after a Ritz Carlton Cruise. The location is absolutely fantastic. Book a balcony room for sure. The changing of the guard at the Palace captured our attention every single time we were in the room and the views of the Acropolis are magnificent. The location is also quite convenient for sightseeing, shopping, and dining options.

The 3 stars comes from the less than impressed experience we had with one very pompous maitre d, George. We returned from a day of sightseeing and shopping and stopped at the front desk to ask where we might get a glass of wine. They directed us to the (only) bar in the hotel that happens to be the Michelin starred restaurant. We went up to the bar only to be given the once-over by George and turned away because my husband was wearing shorts- now keep in mind, it was July and temperature reached the upper 90’s if not over 100 degrees Fahrenheit. We went back to our room; he changed into long pants and we returned. We sat at the bar and finally enjoyed the glasses of wine. We watched other couples arrive. Apparently baseball caps, tshirts, and jeans are appropriate dress whereas khaki shorts and a collared shirt are not. I think the whole experience was compounded by the fact that the front desk saw our attire and directed us to the bar- as well as the fact that George could have handled the whole situation with a lot more tact and a lot less arrogance.

The best experience I’ve ever had in a hotel. Between the King George and their sister property next door, my wife and I were able to explore all the amenities- spa, pool, sauna, steam rooms, both gyms, and more. Everything was top quality and exceptional. We said multiple times that this hotel greatly exceeded our expectations in every way. When we booked it we knew it was nice, but the feeling of grandeur, the amenities, and the staff made it perfect. Every single person we interacted with was incredibly kind and willing to help with any question or request. Lastly, we ate at Tudor Hall one night (Michelin star restaurant on 7th floor of hotel)  and it was some of the best food I’ve ever had. Can’t recommend this hotel and its people any more highly.

We had late flight out of Athens and our plan was to site see all day but it ended up being 100 degrees Fahrenheit that day. They even close the acropolis. We called to see if we could get a day use room and they were completely booked. The staff and manage can't say NO. The service found a way to provide us with a room until 5pm and we were so grateful. Best staff and great rooms. Really really good location and the restaurant has an amazing view of the city.

A wonderful hotel.
The best location possible.
The rooms may not be large, but they are pleasant, clean, and have a great bathroom.
The staff is exceptionally courteous, service-oriented, and friendly.
And I almost forgot... an excellent breakfast.
A wholehearted recommendation for the hotel and its staff.
I have no doubt I will stay there again on my next visit.

Front desk staff was exceptionally kind and made sure we felt welcomed and appreciated. The room was small but cozy and well designed, with modern touches, and both the room and bathroom were very clean and comfortable. The breakfast spread was impressive, with hot options as well as cold ones (everything was so fresh!). The building is right downtown in the Plaka, with easy walking access to restaurants, shops, monuments... it was ideal! There's parking directly across the street, which is very handy. Overall, we had a wonderful time at the Sweet Home and would definitely return! Can't say enough good things about this lovely hotel.

An absolute treasure of a hotel right in the heart of the city. Despite its small size it had everything we needed, and we could walk to most sights in 10-15 minutes. I loved the decor of the hall and rooms, they brought a nice level of class to the whole stay. The breakfasts featured local ingredients and delicacies, and quickly became one of the highlights of our day. The staff was excellent and helpful at every turn, bringing it all together into the best hotel experience I ever had.

This hotel is incredible! The service was top notch, amazing breakfast, great location, quiet and clean rooms. We couldn't have asked for anything more. Will stay here again and recommend to anyone traveling to Athens. Alexia is the best!!

Easy access to everywhere! 20mins walk to Acropolis & many restaurants/cafes are around. My partner and I had 3 days trip to Athens and the receptionist recommended us for a ferry day tip to the Aegina island, wonderful experience we had!

All staffs were smiling throughout our stay. What we enjoyed the most during our stay was breakfast. Spinach pie, Greek yogurt with their homemade berries & cherries were amazing! It’s not a typical European breakfast. They had many homemade Greek style selections!

The room we stayed (304) was quite small as we had 02 luggages:( Besides that everything was good. I’d highly recommend this hotel✨

We got the room after they told us the apartment we booked at the near neighbourhood is not available.
To be honest, after we passed on that neighborhood on our way to the hotel we were glad since that street seems really sketchy and quite crappy.
The room itself in the hotel is pleasant, vintage looking, clean with great wifi and a small balcony.
The stuff were friendly and welcoming, the only thing to improve is the vegan options in the breakfast buffet.

The rooms are small but really comfortable. Air conditioning and smart TV. I really liked the breakfast with plenty of options. The view from the top floor is also really nice. I recommend it to everyone. The neighbourhood is a bit ugly if you have a car you need to look for a parking lot.

Terrible Service – Beware of the Apartment Scam: This hotel also manages apartments, and when we arrived at the reception to collect the keys for our apartment, we were told that they had double-booked it, and the apartment was no longer available (despite us booking for two nights). Instead, they put us in a hotel room that was clearly meant for staff, not guests.

After reading other reviews, it seems this "missing apartment keys" scam has happened to several other people as well, so beware if you're planning to book an apartment here!

To make matters worse, the receptionist was rude and acted annoyed, as if the situation was somehow our fault instead of their mistake.

It's disgraceful. I can’t understand how this place is still in business. On top of that, it's located in a very run-down area, with junkies hanging around right outside the entrance. I won’t be returning here, ever.

The hotel is in a less than wonderful area, with people sleeping on the streets close by. It's in a handy location to metro and bus for exploring the sights.
The rooms are OK, breakfast quite good, but cold. Don't have the buffet dinner, again cold and very limited choice. Staff were pleasant and helpful.

The only good thing about the hotel is friendly staff. Maybe also the dolmadaki we ate at breakfast. The rooms were very tiny. The bathroom is not proper. While showering, the water spreads all over the bathroom floor. No slippers or flip flops. Wet floor no slippers no hygiene unfortunately. The district in which the hotel exists is full of homeless people and junkies. They gather towards evening. We were afraid while returning to hotel in the evening. Streets were not safe. No coffee table,  no table no chairs in the room or balcony. No place to eat or drink sth in the room. No table for women to put makeup stuff in the room.

Although located in a difficult area with many drug addicted lying on the streets, this hotel is well-kept, quiet, tidy and clean. The staff is very friendly and does their best to satisfy the clients' wishes. They do not offer breakfast but the coffee shop Dodici can be accessed by means of a passageway directly from the hotel. Besides the neighbourhood is full of coffee shops and eating facilities. The hotel is very close to the Metaxourgeio metro station and the OSE railroad station. The hotel has a favourable price-performance ratio.

A staple for me in the last 4 years, whenever available.
Never had a bad experience here; top notch cleanliness and very polite staff.
The surrounding area can be dodgy, especially at night, but at the end of the day this is a central point of a touristy metropolis.
Considering the price tag, couldn't recommend it more.

For the special rate of 56 euro for my overnight stay I was very satisfied 3 important things for me number 1 rooms were immaculately clean . 2. Front of staff Valentina and the Gentlemen are always friendly and welcoming and extremely helpful I was offerded a late complementry check out also  as it's also  my second visit to the Minoa. Regarding the Location is great and a few minutes walk to the Metro to connect you to Synigma square Acroplis ect, some reviews have implicated the surronding neighborhoods it's Athens harden up . I had no proplems this time around or on my last stay.
Arrived late (1am) and needed a place to sleep for one night, great 24hr check-in, clean rooms, very friendly and helpful people. Good value at the price point
Definitely good services, very close to the metro station, good price 💯 but back streets are dangerous u need to be careful
The prices are expensive, the hotel is very bad and unclean, the service is very bad, and everything there is very disgusting. I feel sorry that I had this experience.

The worst Hotel, the location is perfect but it’s the only good thing about this place.
The worst receptionist that I met in my entire life, they won’t even store your luggage at the check out for a couple of hours. Everyone is rude and they won’t help you.
We even paid 5€ for the air conditioning and they didn’t give us the remote control, without any refund.
They didn’t change sheets and towels.
So please spend a little bit of more money to have a better and pleasant journey.

Without any booking we successfully
managed to find a room only in this hotel and were pretty happy.
They don't provide breakfast or any service, the  hotel is located in the nosy, dirty area, there's a permanent smoke smell in the room but we weren't going to get upset. Clean sheets, warm water are enough.
But the personel... Really that grumpy, grumbling old man at the reception... He was unpleased, impolite and didn't let us leave two small bags for 2 hours. Disgusting impression.

According to me it was not a hotel. It was like a store room. Their toilet condition was very low. The washroom was very bad. Water falls from the ceiling of the washroom. It is very annoying. There was not enough space to take a shower. Inside the hotel, next to the stairs, inside the room, the dirty smell is very uncontrollable. Their behavior was very bad. The behavior of the owner and the staff and the responsible people were very bad. In a word, everything was at a low level. I didn't like it at all.

If someone comes to visit with family or friends, I think they should not go to the hotel. It will only harass you.👎👎👎👎👎

It's just ok. Nothing special it's a place to sleep. I'm not going to trash this place. I was only there for a few hours. But it was my least favorite of the 3 I stayed in. It did have a balcony. A decent bathroom and the room was rather large in my opinion. And the internet worked.

DO NOT STAY HERE YOU WILL REGRET IT!!!!!

We couldn’t stay here. We checked in and checked out, there was so many reasons we couldn’t put ourselves through the stay. Firstly, all of our furniture was falling apart. I got locked in the bathroom because the handle fell off the door and then the only chair provided collapsed, the sheets are ripped. The floors were dirty, it was horrible. The photos online are catfishes and the hotel is in a disgusting location.
Within an hour we were harassed by people on drugs for money, our arrival taxi even detoured to avoid the area. We found a SYRINGE outside of the hotel and felt so unsafe.

Oh and the best part: they refuse to offer any compensation claiming that the hotel is fine. Even ignoring the exterior, the only provided furniture is falling apart and is broken!

Do not stay here unless you want to risk your safety both inside and outside of the hotel. Do not bring children here. Do not book here, it was the worst hotel I’ve been to in my life and I’ve travelled the world.

The owner has rudely dismissed our request for even a partial refund, so I’d expect a rude reply on here too. Please take my advice.

The rooms are quite spartan, but it's expected given the very low price. The staff was very lovely and let us leave the bags there on our last day. The hotel is located in a bit of sketchy area of the town, unfortunately. It felt a bit unsafe when we arrived late at night.

The staff are amazing, the cleaner will eventually ignore the do not disturb sign, be careful where you step in the near vicinity of the hotel, drug users about. If you want local food for breakfast head left out of the hotel then left again, a little ways up is a great cafe.

I stayed at this hotel in Athens from 20th to 24th June for a business trip with my colleagues. If I had come for a holiday alone or with my child, I would have been terrified. This hotel is situated in Athens' drug trafficking area, making the experience absolutely dreadful.
Every evening, returning to the hotel was a horrifying ordeal. No one does anything about the addicts injecting heroin in every corner. The streets surrounding the hotel are filled with rubbish, syringes everywhere, and addicts sleeping beneath the hotel.
We informed the reception because we thought there was a dead girl in front of the hotel doors. Their response was to do nothing: no action was taken. We had to call the police ourselves. The hotel staff are friendly but completely indifferent to the dramatic and dangerous situation around the hotel.
We were not warned at all about what we would encounter. Terrible experience. Avoid this place at all costs.

Stayed there for one night only as we had to catch next flight to Fira. Didn’t expected much. Had to check in late night because of flight timings but it was smooth as they were aware about our flight timings. We didn’t booked the breakfast but decided in morning to have and it was not a problem for hotel. Overall, room space, air conditioning, accessibility and breakfast was good. Breakfast price was 6 Euros and the offering was good considering it was self service. (Boiled egg, bread cake, bread, butter, jam, cheese, coffee, tea, juice, salat (cucumber and tomatoes), greek curd, cornflakes, chocos etc.
Stayed for 5 nights first part of our holiday in Athens. Going back.  Third time we have stayed here in 26yrs.3rd generation owner friendly staff.  Great breakfast and very clean comfy rooms.

The Acropolis House is very well located, with shops, supermarkets and tourist attractions only a few meters away. Its staff is very welcoming, the breakfast was very good too. The family room ok for a couple of nights!

Very old and not renovated at all. Didn't even have a bottle of water in the room and very noisy at night and also in the morning since floor is wooden and it makes noise when you walk. Great location though.
Great staff and great location. Loved our stay. Very clean otherwise.  Rooms could be a bit bigger but understandable since it's an old building. Loved the antique feeling inside.
Location is amazing, next to everything, the service is very friendly and the rooms are okay :) we can’t ask more

Conveniently located in the middle of a city , can easily get around with buses or metro.
Walking distance to many of the famous attractions like Acropolis, museums and many others.
Plenty of good restaurants nearby. Hence , this hotel is an ideal location to stay and explore this beautiful city, Athens.

Prices : reasonable , a very good breakfast is included
Rooms : spacious, bathrooms are quite nice.
Staff is generally OK. They would help you when you need.
Special thanks should go to their wonderful housekeeping staff. They do a perfect job !!

Family friendly: they provided a good cot bed for our toddler ( need to request)
Hotel looks a bit old , has a vintage appearance. However, it’s clean and well arranged.

If we had to stay here again , we would definitely choose this hotel.

Only main problem we faced was , the surrounding can be a bit noisy at night , and wouldn’t feel safe with various kind of people roaming around the area. But police is there all the time , hence no security issues occurred during our stay .
Efharisto!!!

I did not know that all the hotels were full. Fortunately, I went to them and there was a lady at the reception and explained my situation to him. She was really helpful with me and allowed us to spend the first night in the reception hall because all the rooms were full. On the second day we got a room and everything was really clean. Everything was there. I advise everyone to go to them because the place is also close to the tourist places. 🥇😀🙏🩵

Having stayed there for around four days, I can confirm that the negative comments you're probably reading here are either coming from clueless tourists or people who have expectations way too high.

For a three star hotel, I'd say this is the definitive choice for anyone. The prices are incredibly cheap, the breakfast is just a solid ten out of ten, it's clean and comfortable, the staff are some of the most kindhearted people I've come across and they clean the rooms often.

Only minor nitpick I have is in regards to the walls of the room, which could receive a bit of a paint job.

But aside from that? I'm satisfied.
Any issues I thought I'd have didn't happen, and others that those other reviewers seem to mention are not the problems of the hotel but rather the area itself.
It's Omonoia after all, the very epitome of crime in Athens. What the heck do you expect?! Sunshine and rainbows?!

Pros:
Price - Got a room  for a very good deal for a day.
Rooms - I had booked the Double Room but was very glad when the lady at the reception upgraded our room to  superior room.
Rooms were very spacious.
Food - Breakfast was not that great but i must appreciate that the receptionist was very kind and made arrangements to pack  food for me and my wife and to deliver it to us in advance as we had to board an early morning flight
Location - Location was just a block away from the main street which i loved as it was not so noisy but yet very easily walkable to the main junction. Lots of Taxis available all the time.

Cons:
Nothing much to comment

The staff was extremely polite and friendly !!!

The maids did an excellent job upkeeping the room and overall the quality of the room!

The hallway was nice, and they were kind enough to offer us a place to temporarily store our luggage until our ship arrived !

My huge complaint came from the room it could use a bit of painting !! It looked absolutely horrible, which made me subtract one star from the overall experience however, price to quality ratio it was worth it.

We stayed for 4 nights,  breakfast and wifi included, and we left super satisfied with the overall experience. I just wish the room was in a slightly better state. If you pay close attention to the wall picture, you can see it looks horrible.  The breakfast was also AMAZING they had an amazing selection, and my belly thanked them!!!!

The good things about the hotel was the location & the staff.  The room was clean but very small & the bathroom was tiny.  It was almost impossible to shower!  They offer breakfast for a charge but felt it wasn't worth the money.  There is a wonderful bakery on the corner just steps from the hotel.  We enjoyed our stay in Athens but probably wouldn't stay at Athos Hotel on our next visit.

Do not believe in four star reviews for this hotel.  As a solo traveller I decided to stay at this hotel based on the reviews. While the location of the hotel is close to the central location. The beds were ridden with bed bugs and there were cockroaches under the bathroom sink. The bed sheets had stain. It was the most disgusting place I have ever stayed in. The hotel is located in a dark alley and it also took me a while to find it. The staff was most unwelcome. There were also few unwanted characters hanging around the place. A warning for solo women travellers pay a little bit more and be safe. It was also very noisey.

The highly overpriced hotel is run down boasting old and cheap furniture with a gloomy reception and even more gloomy breakfast room.

There is a major noisy construction worksite opposite the hotel which starts at 7am, of course the front desk “forgot” to tell us about when we made the reservation. The superior room which has a glimpse over the Acropolis looks like a cheap standard room in a 2 star motel with a tiny balcony facing the scaffolding.

The only positive comment about this place is its location.
Rooms are extremely small as is the bathroom, but who goes to Greece to sit in the room, Staff are awesome, so is the location . Has a good view from the roof top lounge area.

Small hotel in a quiet yet central location.  Easy access to transportation, restaurants and shopping. Very walkable to many major tourist sites. Staff are very customer focused with an easy approach.

The rooms are super clean and renovated! Everything you need for your stay is available! Housekeeping comes everyday.
The stuff is extremely helpful and polite, they arrange everything you need. The location is 10/10, in the center of Athens and everything you need is a few minutes away. We had a lovely stay, we’re definitely staying here again. Strongly recommend !

Amazing cozy hotel for the first leg of our honeymoon! Perfect location centrally located to historical and cultural activities; we stayed for two nights and walked everywhere. Staff was incredibly friendly and helpful - if we find ourselves in Athens again we will come back for sure! We were so pleasantly surprised by the spacious balcony, it was perfect for sipping espresso!

Niki Athens was a wonderful introduction to Greece for me and my husband’s honeymoon. The front desk attendants are very nice and gave us all the details we needed for our stay at their hotel. The price point is great, we stayed in a typical room with a king bed and it was much more affordable than those on the nearby islands. The room is equipped with vegan toiletries and we even had a tray of pound cakes waiting for us when we arrived. The rooms are very cozy but felt perfect for just the two of us.
The hotel is cozy all around, it feels personal with a turn key and much smaller number of rooms than a traditional American hotel.
We chose this hotel specifically because we wanted to have the safety net of a vegan restaurant attached to the hotel. Unfortunately, the restaurant is not open on Mondays and Tuesdays, which was a majority of our stay, but we were delighted that next door is a vegan bakery, and across the street there is a vegetarian restaurant next door to a health food store, which offered food security (although we didn’t need it! Athens has soooo many vegan options! I was never worried about finding my next meal!)
Not only is food easily walkable, but we felt very lucky to have chosen a hotel so close to literally everything we wanted to see! The acropolis museum was maybe a 20 minute walk, restaurants we were interested in trying were never more than 15 minutes away by foot, and a taxi is always close by for the longer excursions.
When we finally got to try the restaurant, it was delicious!!!
The housekeeping staff is very pleasant and detail oriented, our room was perfect every afternoon when we came back from exploring.
Niki Athens was extremely good to us when we asked for anything. We are happy with our stay and will absolutely stay again when we come back!

This hotel was a treasure.  We booked the family loft on floors 5 and 6.  The second floor balcony had a beautiful view of the Acropolis.  The air conditioning was great and cooled the room well.  One oddity compared to US hotels: the elevator was very small and hot, but not a reason to pick a different hotel.  The location was a few blocks from restaurants and shops.  I would stay here again.

We stayed for 4 days.
The place needs a serious renovation.
Room furniture and floor full of dust.
The floor was never cleaned during our stay.
The bathroom was ok in terms hygiene and cleaning. The bed was comfortable.
The location is very good, close to Syntagma Square and the city center.
Not a hotel i would visit again though.

Excellent hotel to stay in while visiting Athens. Very clean, staff very helpful and the location is perfect for dining and shopping. The Acropolis is within walking distance, uphill of course. I highly recommend this hotel. In fact we are going back next year. Staff also speak English.

This place is so centrally located, I could practically high-five the Parthenon from my window.
The staff were so helpful, they gave me great local tips on where to eat, how to avoid tourist traps, and even where to get the best souvlaki without a 30-minute wait. (Pro tip: trust their food recommendations—they know their stuff!).
10/10, would definitely stay here again if my crippling depression wasn't catching up to me.

In amongst the action. Balconied room overlooking thriving street. Great shower. Housekeeping thorough and daily. Views of Acropolis from top floor breakfast terrace. Very quick and able reception, nothing too much trouble. Great value would recommend highly.

Adrian has a great location. Near Ermou shopping, Metro is very near. Adrian is very helpful.. Can leave bags if needed.... Late flight etc. Breakfast on roof terrace with view of Acropolis is brilliant.. Really lovely. Shower room was OK... Could do with an update but OK for now. Small balcony and aircon.
Room price is v good for the budget... Book... Long weekend in Adrian is worth it.

I’m surprised by the high reviews. My room was so grubby and there were strange stains all over (peeling wallpaper, and yellow stains over the wall, red stain on the light switch, the remote was filthy, and the carpet felt matted). I felt really uncomfortable with how dirty the hotel was.

I missed housekeeping the first day. I asked for water replenishment told me to just “buy some” down the road. I would have had better amenities at an Airbnb.

Two highlights:

1. The location
2. The fresh squeezed orange juice at breakfast is excellent.

STAY AWAY

I stayed there last night !
bed was missing a plank (as the bed was bending) that was the balcony,I didn’t bother and I did it myself.
in the evening there was a heavy engine working all night (after midnight I arrived) that wouldn’t let us sleep. I tried to call to ask what’s happening but the phone was not working as well.
The flush was  not working in the bathroom as well in result to all the  room smell after I used the bathroom without being able to flush.The cleaning lady this morning said that on the high floors the flush doesn’t work in general.
if you check my profile I am not a complainer but despite the fact that the young lady at the reception was very kind, the manager on the phone was attacking and lying that everything works properly besides the phone which he said “in busy hotels these things happen”.

very disrespectfully he said “what do you want,I’ll give you 10€ discount” .
just describing my experience

I missed my bus to Karditsa when I landed in Athens and a friendly taxi driver brought me here.   I asked to take me to a clean inexpensive spot to sleep for the night.   I was not disappointed.  The staff spoke English and understood my poor Greek.  The room was clean and the balcony was nice.   My only complaint was that it seemed a little out of date and the air condition was not working.   Thankful for the nice breeze that night

Not a good spot. We told a girl the first night our room had bed bugs and asked if we could be moved. To her credit she was very nice and said they didn’t have any available that night, but the next night we could. We then asked the next day about changing and the owner said no. He also wouldn’t offer us any sort of refund for the bed bugs, and told us if we wanted a different room the following day (we had reserved for 3 nights) it would cost us more money. He also didn’t seem even slightly surprised / caught off guard when we told him about the bed bugs. The younger girl we dealt with was great and you could tell the next day she felt really bad when the owner showed zero sympathy. Shes the only reason we gave a 2 star rather than 1 for service, but I would strongly recommend staying anywhere else.

We are very happy to were there. The Service there is very good . They helped us many many Times. The 2 Girls which worked at the Reception are very friendly . Special Thanks from us to both and also a great Thank you to the girl from the Roomservice , it was excellent . Greetings from us to all There.
Carmen & Robert Ilias

I don't know why this Hotel does not have 5 stars, because this Hotel exeeds all of my expectations.

Super air conditioner, nice, clean room with view of THE ACROPOLIS and oh man, the reception person is very friendly.

Excellent Hotel in Athens for this price!🤩

What an incredible hotel! My favorite place to stay in Athens. Nothing better than having a meal or a drink and enjoying this gorgeous view. The rooms are so nice, the pool is incredible, and the staff is so helpful and accomidating. We've already stayed there 2 times and we'll be coming back asap!

Amazon invited us to this hotel for a networking dinner. It was great. The food was tasty, the view was breathtaking, and the staff was very kind. The location is difficult, but as you can see in my photos, you can see as far as Piraeus to the sea.

We would like to give a special thank you to Panagiota, lady at the swimming pool. Such a nice and helpful lady speaking fluently in English.

Also thanks to the lady who cleaned every day our room. We don’t know her name, she was very friendly.

Regret we had a very poor experience from beginning to end.  There was no welcoming hospitality from staff and deputy manager during check in.

We booked 8 rooms and all of our rooms were at back side of hotel, next to air condition units and were very noisy. At midnight, there was a electricity cut and we woke up with huge generator noise. (By the way, generator was only for main hotel places but not for the rooms.) We had no power and air condition for few hours in our rooms. None of us could sleep during the night.

In the morning they didn’t even come to us and apologies for the inconvenience or offered any complimentary for an excuse. Besides they just made our check outs like nothing happened also charged for coffees we had during check out process.

Overall they don’t deserve 5 star  and don’t act like that at all for sure.

Don’t suggest anyone to have this bad experience. There are way better hotels in Athens which you can get what you paid for.

Hotel was great and in a cool location. The rooftop pool is amazing and we spent a lot of time up there. Unfortunately the service at the rooftop bar was pretty bad. They didn't have a lot of cold options, our drinks were not always made well, etc.

In June of 2024, we stayed for a week at the Periscope Hotel in the  Kolonaki section of  Athens, Greece. The Hotel  is in a great location just a few blocks from a Metro Station, in a quite area with lots of shops and restaurants within walking distance.   It is a boutique style hotel so it is small but very clean and beautiful. They have a wonderful breakfast buffet that converts to a 24 hour complementary snack bar which was a fantastic bonus.  Our room was very nice, clean and had a sliding glass door that gave the room lots of natural light.  Nikos is the manager and was always concerned that everything was perfect for our stay.  The the hotel staff includes Anastasia, Nelli and Diana.  They are a very profession staff, yet they were also very nice, friendly and helpful.  Whether we needed directions, restaurant suggestions or just a quick question, they were always happy to assist. They will go out of their way to make sure that you are comfortable and that you have a memorable stay in Athens.  I highly recommend this hotel for anyone planning a stay in Athens Greece.

I spent two nights at this sharp hotel in the Kolonaki neighborhood and I enjoyed my time very much. It is small, only about twenty rooms, but nicely designed and in a great location near to many upscale shops and restaurants, but yet still a quiet neighborhood feel. In fact, it feels as if you might be staying at the apartment of a friend (who happens to be a designer). The staff is so gentle and personable - so much so that I looked forward to seeing them each day. All smiles. I think this place is a little gem. Very cool. Thank you!

We truly enjoyed our recent stay at the Periscope Hotel in Athens. Everything was top-notch: the location, the staff, the room and amenities and the breakfast. We not only highly recommend this property, we plan to come again!

The service at Periscope Hotel is probably the best that we have had during our trips in Greece. All the staff members are amazing, always nice, keen to help and assist.
We even got upgraded on our second night there, after returning from the islands.
We will definitely stay back here when we come back to Athens.
In addition, the hotel is nicely decorated, clean, tidy and within walking distance to Plaka and the Acropolis.
There are also lots of nice restaurants around.
Best place to stay when you are visiting Athens!

Staff here is amazing, welcoming, and so helpful.  After long flight delays and lost luggage, they helped us settle in, got us directions to various stores and pharmacies to purchase needed items.  They constantly checked in on us to ensure we had everything we needed.  The staff at Periscope are a great testament to the kindness and generosity of the Greek people.  That, and the rooms were super comfortable, clean, and beds/shower were amazing

Pros:
- Good location, quiet.
- Walkable, though not super central.
- Nice rooms.
Cons:
1. Absolutely no shower pressure.
2. Towels provided are either hand-sized or very small for the body.
3. The wardrobe has only 4 mismatched hangers and no shelves ;)

Very basic room furniture, dark room with an awful smell all over the hotel probably from the Airconditioning. The TV was not working, the flush toilet was broken, the hair dryer also not working. Towels and bedsheets not super white probably are old and need replacing with new ones. The location is convenient but was expensive for the quality it offers

Warning! Service prices changing on the way
After a refundable reservation I've received an email asking if I wanted the taxi at a certain price, I replied the day after asking for the service, the I got a reply after a couple of hours with a higher price.
I called them and a woman literally told me with a rude manner: the manager has changed the price, do you want it or not?
Be aware to book with them and stay in a harsh and stressed environment as normally Greece is a relaxing place to visit for tourists.

My stay for one night was HORRIBLE. It was super hot in the room and it was so noisy from the street I couldn't sleep and I got in at 3AM. The AC also didn't work. terrible experience wouldn't recommend.

We were  coming back from the islands and had to have a night in Athens before catching the flight next evening. We wanted to be close to kolonaki as I love this area and we needed a possibility to leave our luggage for few hours after check out. So we needed an accommodation with reception. Athens Way was good price - value ratio. lots of cozy cafes nearby, good supermarket and Metro - 10 min walk.

Was put up here by an airline after a cancelled flight, so can’t really complain since I didn’t pay for this. But comfortable rooms, good facilities, and their version of room service is a great idea - a selection of local eateries offering some of their popular dishes available for delivery, with the reception calling you when it’s down there ready for pickup. Unfortunately, only down side is the walls are paper thin, so sounds from hallways and neighbouring rooms are clear as day. Good selection for (inclusive) breakfast, however mini bar options were pretty poor.

Highly recommend this hotel. Fantastic room, spotlessly clean, great staff and a lovely breakfast. Free mini bar and drink voucher for bar. Close to local amenities. Will be re booking next time we are in Athens

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Ilisia Hotel. From start to finish, my experience was exceptional. The staff was friendly, attentive and always ready to assist, the rooms were very comfortable and tastefully decorated, the location is conveniently located at the heart of downtown. I recommend it, it’s a hidden gem that combines comfort, convenience, and outstanding service.

Fantastic location, good rooms and big balcony’s with a table and chairs. My room was very cosy with lots of wardrobe space, tv, desk with usb plugs. This hotel impressed me the most because the staff were absolutely lovely and couldn’t do enough for you.

The staff are very caring and attentive.

Great breakfasts.

Stocked complimentary minibar is a nice touch.

Single rooms are a bit small, and rooms immediately next to the marble stairwell are a bit noisy.

It can’t get any better than this. The view alone is worth staying here. Add to that elegant rooms and common areas, attentive staff and delicious food and you have the perfect mix. It is close to everything. Rooms are immaculate. The gym is small but, from what I can see, the hotel doesn’t target that clientele so it’s ok .. I would definitely recommend and come back here!

Everything is extraordinary about this historic hotel in the heart of Athens, near Syntagma Square, just opposite the Greek Parliament. Even if you don’t (choose to) stay there, you should visit the Spa, which offers wonderful treatments in a calm, cosy, yet luxurious atmosphere. A brunch or an afternoon tea at the Winter Lounge complete the pleasurable experience. And of course there is the Roof Garden restaurant with a breathtaking view.

Beautiful hotel with nice facilities and spa area. My gripes are:
(1) the service wasn’t as refined as I would hope for a hotel of this class. Not much greeting or going out of their way for guests.
(2) my wife’s spa experience was sub standard. Her back was bruised and sensitive for days after a massage, and her hair blowout didn’t turn out great compared to spas back home.

I rarely write reviews, but felt compelled to as the front desk staff really need to up their game. My partner and I stayed for a night recently and upon arrival the first question the receptionist asked was what time we were checking out the next day. I was slightly thrown by this but didn't take offence though am increasingly irritated that high end hotels expect an 11am check out, and not midday. But more on this later.

Our flight had been delayed so we arrived at the hotel at about 3pm hungry for lunch. We asked the receptionist where we might eat and she said that the restaurant on the 8th floor was full. We said OK, but can we maybe get a snack at the pool bar. She also said that wasn't possible. Rather puzzled we went to our room and then decided to explore. Upon entering the pool area we saw an operational kitchen and were greeted by friendly staff and duly had a pleasant bite to eat, confused that the receptionist had tried to put us off going!

The next morning at 10.30am as we were getting ready to leave our room there was a ring on the doorbell obviously to chivy us along. But not only that a few minutes later the phone also rang (with nobody at the other end when I picked up). I could understand it if it was after 11am but I really take issue with this sort of behaviour in any hotel let alone one that is notionally the best in Athens.

As a hotel it's OK, nothing particularly special. We moved onto the Four Seasons on the coast which is in a completely different league and I would thoroughly recommend.

Old world elegance with modern luxury service. The guest rooms have maintained their old school appointments, but the upstairs lounge and bar are very much updated with modern decor. The rooftop bar/restaurant is definitely the highlight, although evening cocktails are 24 euro, so you're paying for the view and ambiance.  The breakfast is 5 star in every way; we booked a rate that included breakfast and it was definitely worth it.

The Athens Zafolia hotel was chosen by my tour group. The hotel is located in the heart of Athens. Very easy to get to all major areas of interest. Dining easy to find, and the staff was very accommodating. We had a few doorman to help with our luggage. When we needed directions, the desk staff was very helpful, and provided a map. The pool was on the roof, and my one complaint was no shade. We stayed during the heatwave in June '24. They even closed the Acropolis, it was THAT hot. I couldn't stand to be even in the water during that time without a place to find some shade. Other than that, the breakfast was great. Good selection, great coffee and juice. Hotel staff were very friendly. The rooms and bathrooms could use some updating. Although, they were very clean. For the time we stayed, it was very nice.

Everything is quite above average except location is off the tourist area. The room is quite spacious which is a plus. However, It will take 20 min taxi (10 Euro) to reach the Plaka area. There's not much shopping or restaurant choices nearby.
...After reading the hotel feedback on my post, I apologize for my wrong perception that the hotel is not in city center.

Close enough to the metro and still close enough to some sites.. customer service was pretty bad. Experienced racism. So, our group of 11 (6 rooms) booked extra days stay after our tour ended.  Our group, along with other members of the tour company arrive early.  All the other members,  who happen to be white got checked in early,  except our group, who are  black ladies were told we could not until 3pm. Despite other groups  coming in after us and also getting checked in. A member of our group went to the desk three times and was refused. They made us wait until exactly 3pm to check us in. Needless to say there was no one else left to be checked in after us. However there was one lady there who helped us with directions to the metro and also called us cabs when we requested.  So sorry I forgot her name. Please keep her there. I would not recommend this hotel to my black friends... my white friends maybe.

40 euros from Airport to this hotel on the outer rim of the city perimeter. No doorman to help with luggage - reception was perfunctory. Room neat enough but dusty floor. Bathroom has issues. 2 old lifts labour bringing a couple with a suitcase each up. Breakfast buffet is as expected in 3 star hotel. Don’t try dinner at the restaurant where waiters sneer at anyone not understanding Greek & joke with those who do. Expensive.  Interesting shopping precinct up 2 blocks. Can’t really recommend this place. Totally understand why Greece is in economic decline. Beware of taxi drivers, restaurants & shop keepers none of whom are grateful all want tips for no service. The pool was closed.

Comfortable, relatively inexpensive, half hour walk from Syntagma Square. Slightly faded/well-worn feel to the place e.g. room key cards  needed replacing 3
times in 48 hours. Pool on top excellent facility. Extremely dull, central casting breakfast buffet - more fuel than delicious!

Very nicely located hotel. We had an apartment and double room. Both spotless clean. An apartment with a view, cozy and spacious rooms. There was no washing up liquid, a sponge or a cloth in the kitchen. That would be nice to have when you stay only for a short period of time. Only thing I did not like was breakfast. Eatable but not the best one.

Ideal for families that have 4-6members, decent breakfast with green dishes, close to the most posh area in Athens - near Kolonaki- , metro 5min walk.
I recommend as best option for those also that they like the ‘70-80s- decoration which gives a sense of quality and simplicity!!!
Fair price compared to other hotels around 🤓

Staying at Delice apartments was very pleasant, the apartments were very spacious with everything that you would need. They were cleaned well every day. The location was also excellent, very close to the Evangelismo metro station and only a stop or two to the centre of the city. Would definitely recommend!

Good hotel for a short stay near the metro in Athens.
Rooms are neat although very simple.
The breakfast is good but would need a few improvement touches to make it even better.
Overall the experience was very good although I would expect from a 4* hotel more amenities and a wider variety of products available for breakfast.

Best bedroom mattress I've EVER slept on! Staff were extremely nice!! They got us a taxi when we needed one and the taxi showed up on time! Room was huge! Very happy with Delice and if I'm ever in that neighborhood, I will definitely stay there again!!!

great alternative to a hotel, in the best place in Athens. Very calm and and easy access to any point of the city, thanks to the nearby Metro station. The prices in comparison to a hotel are very good. Some people may not like the place as the interiors haven't been renovated since the opening in the 70s. I personally love the rosewood elements, it's very authentic and vintage. The apartments are spacious, with a bedroom, a living room and a separate kitchen. The balcony is enormous, with an unobstructed view to the city and mount Emitos. In front of the hotel, there's a church and office building related to it. it's really quiet.

I stayed here while looking for a last minute booking after I had to extend my stay in Athens for 1 more day. Although the location was nice and the staff were friendly, I have to say I was a bit disappointed upon entering the room due to how old the room and furniture were...and I don't mean old as in vintage classic way. The carpet had stains accumulated from 20 years and the furniture/walls/kitchen/overall interior looked like a cheap motel from the 1960's. I made the booking based on the 4-star description I saw on Google. Little did I know, those were user review 4 stars and not classification of the hotel as a 4-star hotel.

It's has good position, but it's still 40min by foot to Acropolis. The biggest problem of this hotel - it's really old: hard to open or close balcony or windows and stuff like that. But it's nice place to crash for few nights.

Large apartment, clean, well-equipped and with 24-hour reception. The location is excellent, walking distance to two train stations, and to the city centre. A safe neighbourhood. I will stay here again.

Very old. No renovation since 19xx? However Room is very big and clean.wifi. Hot water. Kitchen. And balcony over look city. Location is OK. When we stayed may 1. No aircon it's hot daytime. Big problem for us.
Super
good place
Super

The reception doesn't speak Greek..(only broken English...)
It's more like a hot spot, than a hotel... I don't think I need to say anything else... if you're brave enough to find a room (rare), double-lock it at night, for better or worse... more ghetto than a hotel..
The fact that the reviews are almost all in Arabic... says it all.. shame because it used to be decent...

Previously, it was an ordinary hotel like any hotel, but now the European Union has rented it to refugees only to families who have health problems because the place is close to the hospital, and after completing treatment, they move them to homes.

This place is honestly so cute. Great library. Beautiful vintage furniture. Such friendly special guests and such a charming location.  Don't think, just book.  All hostels should be like this. Such a nice cafe down the road for aperitivo.

Nice location, very cute hostel & amazing staff!
Unfortunately if you are a hot sleeper- look elsewhere. The AC was to be adjusted by staff only and was mostly turned off during my time here, so we had to open a window during the night which let in many mosquitos! You also heard anyone in the courtyard on the phone throughout the night, luckily only one night someone stayed up late talking. Breakfast was small but a good way to start the morning! :)

Very clean accommodation with a big private room that I stayed in. Vintage and quirky decor, but the in the courtyard furniture is old. The armoire in the room had a musky smell. Good stay if you’re not a social solo traveler, everyone minds their own business, even the staff. Breakfast is free which is nice, but don’t expect anything great—it’s no frills. Situated in a neighbourhood where locals live and eat out, which I really liked. Away from the super crowded touristy areas (20 mins walk from Monistiraki and Plaka). A bus stop around the corner or a 13 mins walk to the nearest metro. Surrounded by tons of amenities (I needed a pharmacy and coffee shop). Great local restaurants and street foods all around it.

I was sent text with code while I was in flight so arriving in Athens I had no internet availability. Reservation had been made 2 weeks before. Cleaning lady does a fab job, she makes your bed each day even. Very nice. They have thought of having everything a person needs. About 45 min. Walk to center.

I only stayed here for a night but they let me check in early and assisted me with my bags. The accommodations are simple but very clean and they have a lovely back yard. Breakfast is free and they have complimentary coffee and tea available all day. Each bed had a light and plugs available and my room even had a small balcony! All the staff is kind and helpful and it was easy to walk to some great restaurants and tavernas nearby. Absolutely charming and I would stay here again.

Beautiful apartment set in one of the most stunning areas of Athens.  Definitely one of the most amazing apartments I stayed in whilst visiting Athens and definitely one of my favourite areas of Greece.  Booking and access was easy and the apartment contained everything I needed for my stay.  Loved it and would definitely stay there again.

Simply the best! Chic decorated, fully equipped, crystal clean apartment in a building of famous architects in the posh area of Athens (Kolonaki), near to all important sightseeing. Loved every corner of it!

Μodern luxurious apartment amazingly decorated and equipped, completely clean and provided with all necessary. Perfectly located in the posh neighborhood of Kolonaki, in 5-20 min walking distance from all major sightseeing of the city. Highly recommended!
Simply the best apartment of Athen's centre I have ever been! Amazing decoration, luxuriously furnished, fully equipped! Best ever!
Fabulous,highly designed,spotless clean and convinient apartment,in walking distance of all important sightseeing of Athens,plus the best host ever!We had a great time and for sure we will come back!
Bathroom & shower area is very small. Location is very nice, walking distance to all places
Great hosts, quiet, clean, nice restaurants and bars in the area.
Clean, very good choice for anyone who wants an apartment in the center, very nice staff

We had an amazing experience at this hotel. Our flight arrived very early in the morning and they offered us complimentary breakfast right when we arrived. Our room was also ready by 9:30 am! They were incredibly accommodating and had great recommendations. The hotel is located on a beautiful street and is very central. We will definitely be back.

This hotel is a gem!  It's beautiful, quiet, the rooms are lovely, it's in a great location, and every member of the staff is so helpful and friendly.  The breakfast and coffee were delicious.  I would stay here again in a heartbbeat, and I would highly recommend it to anyone coming to Athens.  And a special thank you to Marietta, for taking such good care of us.

We spent 4 nights at the Lozenge Hotel Athens before joining a cruise - it was the perfect place for our short stay. The hotel is located in a small mostly pedestrian street within walking distance of Syntagma Square and the metro. Our room was a very decent size with a lovely bathroom. The service was exceptional - Vasia, Marietta and the whole team were extremely friendly and helpful. We really enjoyed our stay there and can highly recommend it!

Awesome place.
Close to everything centre of Athens
Front desk lady Yota was awesome.
Restaurant staff very attentive.
Would definitely stay there again.

Our recent stay at Hotel Lozenge was nothing short of exceptional. The moment we walked through the doors, we were greeted by a warm and inviting atmosphere that instantly made me feel at home. The attention to detail in every aspect of the hotel, from the beautifully decorated rooms to the impeccable service, was truly remarkable. The staff esp. Jenny at the restaurant and Marietta at the front desk were incredibly attentive, helpful and warm. They went out of their way to ensure that our every need was met. The hotel's central location made it convenient for exploring the city. Overall, our experience at Hotel Lozenge was unforgettable, and I can't wait to return for another incredible stay.

We stayed the Holiday Suites for 5 nights and were very pleasantly surprised at how nice it was! When checking in, we were offered and shown multiple rooms and choose a basement room with a small covered courtyard area. The room was extremely spacious and the AC worked well which was nice since Athens was in the middle of a hot streak. The staff was friendly and very accommodating. Would definitely stay again!
Wonderful rooms and great ac. Not a ton of hotels got cool enough for us, but this one was amazing!

Well, there are much better hotels in this area in the same price category. The rooms are okay, but little things like no drinking water available or hot water/air conditioning not working or working poorly ruins the stay a little. The food is not great, room service dinner arrived cold, the breakfasts were not included in our reservation, we tried it the first day and did not like it, but at the check out still got charged for all days for breakfasts we did not have 🤷🏻‍♀️ also tried to book the conference room, the staff is very polite but not so helpful for resolving the issues.

Room is clean and comfortable. Kitchen is with all the amenities in order to prepare something.
Breakfast is very good. Position is fantastic, very close to metro and to the city centre.
Gym organization is not comfortable because it is not present in the hotel and a closer hotel's Gym should be used.
Absolute wonderful place to stay. Would recommend to anyone. Looking forward to coming back

I stayed there for four nights. The design of the rooms is very odd with half side on one side and hand basin in middle. It also needs some upgradation especially the flushing system which was the common problem of the other rooms as mentioned by other residents. The noise at night coming from night club next door makes it questionable.  Though the staff is really friendly and welcoming

A gem of a hotel which is located on the quiet back streets behind the main shopping district. It's a small and intimate hotel, all the staff was very friendly and welcoming. The hotel is for adults only which is great for couples just wanting a nice peaceful stay within the noise of any screaming kids! The breakfast selection was great, there is so much of it that you won't go hungry. All in all a very peaceful and pleasant stay.

Let me start by saying that this was the BEST customer service I’ve ever had. The staff’s hospitality was fantastic and they go above and beyond to make sure you’re ok. The location is in a very nice area, lots of stuff to do locally. The hotel is also so beautiful. I highly recommend this place. Probably my best experience in a hotel ever.
Very nice staff. But rooms are good looking but not functional (lights, toilet flushing time). Also during the night there is loud music coming from the club nearby. I will not go there again.

A really lovely boutique hotel (just outside) the heart of Athens. Well within walking distance. Very modern, clean stylish rooms.
