Nice little accommodation inexpensive, was just nice, not far to the train, restaurants all around, good location, always happy, highly recommended
Super well appointed studio.  The owner is very friendly and available. Calm neighborhood.

Cosy, the manager was very friendly.
In the kitchen you have coffee and tea, everything is well organised, in the room you have a small private fridge.
A bit annoying is the train passing in front of the room, but only until 8pm if I remember correctly.

Comfortable and beautiful rooms, great location for seeing everything in Athens and a good neighbourhood. Accomodating and friendly hosts. Kitchen had everything you could need and including a great selection of tea and coffee and a pretty garden to drink it in.
I love it here private living shared kitchen with access to washing balcony private or upper view.  Management just à text away. Local neighborhood markets and restaurant.
The furniture and stuff is like from a flea market. It's clean, specious, well equipped, easy check-in.

Gorgeous accommodation, cozy , clean, really nice decoration and aesthetic! Near the city center and very convenient location for every destination!
As about the staff super friendly and polite ☺️
I highly recommend it!
Perfect !!

Terrible location just 10 minutes by foot from Thisio.. Very nice neighborhood with lots of shops in the surrounding streets..
Renovated and fully functional and warm house...

Completely renovated accommodation with excellent equipment for any need.
Spacious and suitable for a family.
In an ideal location in the center of Athens very close to Thisio.
The hosts are very hospitable.
We will definitely use it again in the future.

We visited Grecian Residences last week. The experience was excellent on all levels. Nice decoration, cleanliness, spacious for a family and great convenience in parking. An ideal spot since it combines entertainment (Gazi) and ease of transportation with the Metro line which is 10 minutes away by foot (Kerameikos Station) I believe it was the best choice we could make.

Great stay in the center of Athens.
Very clean and spacious accommodation in a very quiet neighborhood just a few minutes from Thisio.
Very helpful hosts.
Perfect host ! Highly recommended 100%

Be aware, this is not a regular hotel, it is aimed at a particular clientele, if you want to sleep in a strip club or a brothel (literally) this is for you. It is not an adult “themed” hotel for a bit of a uniqueness or difference, it’s exactly for the purpose it appears. Just so you know.

Excellent hotel. Highly recommend for any couples who want to relax in a cozy place. There's very nice air conditioning, wonderful staff, and the room was very good, with an excellent breakfast every single day.

We have requested prior to booking that the room be deep cleaned, as the hotel allows smoking at the rooms. They've done an excellent job and we have not noticed any foul smell. Our room was honestly more than what we wanted, and it was excellent.

The view of the city from our floor was also very nice, and we enjoyed the landscape.

Only gripe I have is that not all the staff understand English to the fullest extent, which can lead to some awkward interactions and miscommunications. While not a big deal for the most part, try speaking clearly and with separated words for the best experience (in English).

Location is excellent and a 6 minute walk from the metro station. Only issue I personally had is that in Athens pedestrians are not the priority, so exercise caution crossing the road to the metro.
Great stay! The service from the staff was fantastic, always checking in to make sure everything was fine and aiming to make sure the stay was fantastic.
Nice hotel at the center of Athens!! Perfect for private times and amazing themed rooms!! 100% recommend.

Clean and cosy rooms, staff really nice and helpful . Great hotel for not exeggerated price.
Location also wonderful, nearby you can find good cafeteria and restaurants with delicious food. City square is located about 1.5 km on foot from this place.

Great new rooms and service. They provide all you need: washer, dryer, hair dryer, coffee machine.Mini kitchen. Nice terrace with tables on top of the building. Area is very quiet. Walkable distance to Acropolis. Highly recommend. It is possible to book directly using their website.

Incredible!
I felt in love with this place 💘
So kind, friendly and very helpful owner! When we came he guided us about what to see in Greece and how to get their, gave his recommendations about the restraints and cafes.
Room was so comfy, nice and very clean.
The location is incredible 🤩 everything is close. I would like to came back again 😍

One of our favourite apartments we have ever stayed in! The owners were super friendly and the rooms are lovely and clean. Plus upon arrival we were notified that there is a rooftop terrace which was an amazing bonus! Cannot recommend this place enough!

My trip to Athens was only two nights with a small group of girlfriends. Accommodations were perfect for us, everyone got a bed, we felt safe and were within walking distance of many notable sites, including the acropolis. Our apartment also had a kitchen which had we stayed longer would have been helpful to heat up delicious leftovers or to make our own frappes! The owners were incredibly helpful, setting up our transportation to and from the airport. They also gave us a quick overview of the city marking some places of interest on a map for us to take with us. And even gave us their cell phone number if we got lost or needed help, I can’t recommend this location enough and if I’m back in Athens, I will absolutely stay there again.

Beautiful accommodation in the heart of Thisseio. Friendly host Nikolaos, who offers six modernly furnished apartments. Very well equipped, well thought out concept. Washing machine and dryer are available and very practical. Good starting point to explore the historic city center of Athens. With the windows closed it was pleasantly quiet and we slept well. Parking for our rental car was also available. One front of the house is on a busy street, the other part is in a quiet dead end road. Café 6 is just around the corner, great for breakfast. WLAN worked. Heating was also very reliable. Roof terrace is great, lovely city view, but not of the Acropolis. We recommend the accommodation for a stay in Athens.

I needed a one night stay.  Booked hotel and was able to get room.  Thought new hotel would be great.  However the room was so hot and humid and still struggling to cool it down after an hour.  The worst part was when you pull back the sheets and two very large COCKROACHES jump out!   Checked in at 8 and left by 11:30! Contacted management and he refused to give a refund.  He just wanted to give a different room.  That was not acceptable.  They denied they have a bug problem, but if this was on the 3rd floor, I can only imagine what was on the lower floors!  If you don’t mind COCKROACHES, then it’s the place for you!

This place was cute and accommodated our group well. They are definitely new -- no breakfast provided yet, and there was a weird moment where the concierge wasn't sure they'd have an extra pillow for me (they did end up having them!) but it was fine. The area is on the edge of sketchy area - min a 15-20 min walk from the area where you'd probably go for dinner or from any of the sights. It wasn't bad for us but if you're hoping to be in the middle of everything, then try another spot.

a fairly new and quite modern 'hotel' or guest house, located about 50 minutes (by cab) from the airport, but being close to the metro line (4 min to Kerameikos station) makes it a convenient location to stay at.

rooms are spacious (we had the superior double with balcony & city view), bed comfortable, and the bathroom well equipped. there's no safe in the room unfortunately, and just an open wardrobe area.

maintenance and cleaning is ok, although we had some issues with clogging of the drains under the shower which caused some flooding.

the open shower concept is absolutely nonsensical as there's water everywhere, leading to unnecessary waste of towels.

overall, a decent option for 2-3 night stay.

Upon arrival we were greeted by a friendly staff member who explained everything to us. The suites are very spacious and nicely designed with a good eye for color, they feel bright, modern and livable. The hotel is very new, so there is a little but of construction going on, but it’s not noticeable in the room. Everything is perfectly clean, and the room has all equipment one expects. Good bars and restaurants as well as the train station are just within walking distance, you can see the Acropolis from the side streets. Just a perfectly pleasant location. Will come back next time!

The rooms are really modern and are equipped with all requirements one needs. The AC unit is really good and behind the apartments one can find the metro station. Also there is no issue with food as there are quite a few restaurants around, there is also a shop that is open all through the night selling food. I arrived at 1am and it was not an issue they gave me access to the door which I really liked and it was really easy to use.

Fantastic place, absolutely loved it here, it's spotless and you have everything you need (toiletries, hair dryer, iron, ear plugs, towelling robes etc......).  I would definitely recommend the place for anyone that wants to visit Greece.  It's very central to everything, and a direct line from the airport.  Fabulous place to stay.

We spent an awesome time in Athens at the Iris Management Penthouse Condo.  It's located in an excellent location,  with restaurants and bars nearby as is the metro - just two blocks away.  The condo is a beautifully furnished penthouse with a view of the Acropolis from the balcony.  The condo owners are very helpful, informative and were a great source for recommendations.  We will definitely stay here again on our next visit to Athens.  Thank you Iris Management!!!

A perfect place.  Close to metro  and easy to get to all the tourist spots.  In a safe neighbourhood and close to great restaurants.  Place was very clean and beautiful.  Outdoor space was great to relax and enjoy a drink in the evening.  The hosts were great,  very responsive prior to and during our stay.

We had an absolutely amazing stay at the Iris House. The hosts were especially proactive and helped us nicely navigate what was our first visit to Greece. We had a few challenges with flight delays and having such attentive hosts to promptly answer any questions and offer guidance helped make our stay wonderful and most memorable. We will certainly be back again.

Do not book.

Jeff is unethical and will accuse you of causing damages to the Airbnb.

Do not ruin your vacation by booking this Airbnb.

Edit: please see below the “damage” we were falsely accused of. Again, do not book.
Friendly stuff, beautiful rooms and all new. There is one thing, during weekends it’s difficult to sleep, because of the bars and clubs nearby. Earplugs helps me.

Excellent apartment in a modern and secure residence. Rarely seen such a well-equipped apartment. Very clean and functional, close to the metro, 15-20 minutes walk from places of interest in the city center. Very friendly owner/manager. In short, great experience, I highly recommend for your stay in Athens.

Amazing apartment!. With everything in it really. Well maintained, brand new, spotless. The hosts are welcoming and kind.
I recommend it without a second thought!

Great apartment, spotlessly clean, great host!
Recommended!

Mold, Not Clean, AirCon Not Working, Awful!

This has been one of my worst experiences staying at an apartment.

Problems:  Air Condition
I contacted the property letting them know the AC was not working in bedroom on Sunday at 10:30am. They didn’t get back to me until 5PM letting me know they would try to send someone there. I waited there from 5-8:30pm until they told me they couldn’t.  Monday came and then nothing - they didn’t try to fix it, they didn’t try to call me, they didn’t even call the platform I booked it on.
Kitchen
There was black mold in the refrigerator and the kitchen was not prepared - the utensils were broken and there was no garbage to throw things away.
There was also no paper towels to clean things up with.
The dish sponge was old and disgusting.

Remotes:
The batteries were dead in the remotes and that was a challenge trying to turn things on/off.

Area: The hallway was terrible - the smells as other descriptions said was awful.
Because it is street level you can hear all the noises.
When people were coming and going - you could hear the front door slam shut and it would echo through the apartment.

Wifi
The wifi needs to be reset every 4-5 hours and it was slow. Can not handle media calls.
Very nice apartment.!! With very friendly and always available host.!!
The place is near everything and easy to get to.
Perfect!

Apartment with weaknesses... All the good reviews don't correspond to reality, I'd like to add a few current pictures so everyone can get their own impression.
The location is relatively good, about 300 meters from the metro and the area is otherwise quite busy and pleasant. The apartment itself is relatively dark, the bathroom is almost disgusting but the rest is quite nice. There are 2 air conditioners in the apartment, one in the living room, the other in the bedroom, they work great. The processing and handover of the apartment also went smoothly. I would also like to point out that it wasn't entirely clean; we found hair in the bathroom from previous guests...

We had an amazing stay at the Traditional Thiseio Home. The location was perfect, with easy access to local attractions and the nearby train station. The home felt safe for our kids, and they loved playing in the small garden. Toula was very helpful throughout our stay, ensuring we had everything we needed. We highly recommend this place for families!

Great location in an area populated by locals, quiet and well connected with city centre - about 20 minutes walk. Nymphs hill located in immediate proximity allows great views onto the Acropolis and the Athens in general - a nightime visit is recommended! Two metro stations in a walking distance. The area doesn't feel touristy at all and is surrounded by great restaurants frequented by locals. The house is equipped with an AC both in the living area and in the bedroom. There was a basic set of bathroom liquids (except the toothpaste) and a complimentary water bottle cooled down in the fridge - it was a very nice touch! The space was tidy and generally clean but the couch was a bit beaten up. Our host, Tula, was very nice, gave great recommendations for the first-time Athens visit and was all the time available if we needed help. We recommend and would definitely book again!
Beautiful clean apartment to rest in whilst on holiday in Athens. Very comfortable and bright. The outside sitting area is such a bonus as well. Tula was very welcoming and hospitable.
An excellent hospitality, very clean, quiet location, very well equipped! Definitely recommend it!

A very nice apartment, I can only recommend it. Everything was very clean. the facilities were great. The personal care and help from the landlady was also good. The apartment has a cute well-kept front garden. It is located on a very quiet side street.
There is air conditioning and a well-equipped kitchen that was very clean.
The bathroom was also new and very clean and you immediately felt comfortable.

I got my personal room and bathroom here. No issues at all. Location is excellent, all the top 10 sites are within a 30 minute walk. Streets were clean and safe, and some great food and bars options nearby. The rooftop bar has a truly exceptional view and friendly service. Would recommend to other friends.

Unfortunately I can't give 5 stars because the bathroom always flooded after people had a shower. But the position is excellent, the staff is very nice and friendly and the rooftop offers you an amazing view of the acropolis and the other archeological sites. It also has a bar with music where you can meet and talk to people at night.

We stayed in a private Acropolis view room. The room itself and balcony were very nice. However, the washroom was on the smaller side and the mattress was pretty awful. It felt like sleeping on a few old springs. Staff were all very friendly and approachable with answers to all our questions. The location is great. It is in proximity to all popular tourist attractions and the Metro. We opted for the breakfast package with our stay and it was the only regret we have. It was pretty terrible. Other than the breakfast and mattress, we thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
Perfect location. Quite area however there’s no soundproof in my private room and could hear my neighbours easily. Bed was very cozy. Water pressure and temperature was excellent.
It is a hostel but we stayed in a separate 2 people room. Acropolis view from the room was just amazing. You have a terrace in your room. Maybe one of the best views in Athens 🤩

Truly in the heart of Athens, just blocks from the Acropolis. Our room was spacious considering the size of the hotel, and the service has been outstanding! We arrived well before check in and the hotel staff did what they could to get us in early (our flight landed nearly an hour ahead of schedule). From that point on, the front desk staff attended to all of our needs. This excellent level of service is especially meaningful, as we are on the first leg of our honeymoon. I highly recommend this hotel for its location, quality and service, hope to stay here again on our next visit to Athens!

Hotel is in a great location convenient for the Acropolis site and Acropolis museum, but in a relatively quiet location. Many good bars and restaurants close by. Rooms are comfortable with a high standard of housekeeping. Great lounge/bar area on the ground floor and fabulous roof terrace bar and restaurant with great views of the Acropolis. Service was friendly and extremely helpful.

My husband and I had the pleasure of enjoying The Herodion Hotel on our recent trip to Athens. We had never been to the city before and were spending a short time there before departing for a cruise. Our decision to stay here was based on reviews online and we could not be more impressed. My husband and could not stop singing the Herodions praises, I lost count of how many times he would just sit back and say what a great choice the hotel had been and how much he enjoyed everything about it.
The location could not have been better. A short walk from the Acropolis, located in a quiet area of a vast city just bordering the historic Plaka neighborhood. Metro was located nearby making access to other districts simple.
The hotels rooms were absolutely pristine, with a wonderful shower and comfortable bed. We had the pleasure of booking a room with a view of the Acropolis and enjoyed morning coffees on our balcony. We used the hotels service to book transportation from airport to hotel and arrived before check in but they staff was wonderful. From the moment we arrived and had a member of staff rush down to the street to help with our luggage, to the front desk securing our bags while we took lunch and explored the area prior to check in.
We were told we could also utilize the hot tubs (which have fantastic views as well) even before our room was ready if we liked.
We had lunch in the conservatory room which was delicious and dinner at the rooftop restaurant which was probably the most delicious food I had on my vacation.
When we left the hotel we were able to once again enjoy the ease of having the welcoming staff arrange our transport to the Port.
We can not recommend this Hotel more, from cleanliness to hospitality there was no fault to be seen and we plan to stay here on any future stays as well as recommend it to family and friends.

We had a great stay at this hotel. Very friendly and welcoming staff. The room we had was great and could fit a crib in for our little one. Easy walk to Acropolis (5mins) and plenty of restaurants and bars close by. They also offer a great breakfast in the morning. Would definitely stay here again.

The hotel staff have been wonderful the entire stay. Every staff member is warm and exceptionally friendly. No task is too difficult and every request is met with a smile. The hotel restaurant on the roof top has phenomenal views of the Acropolis, with a spacious rooftop bar and restaurant. Our party of 4 enjoyed gourmet food, with each dish looking more like art than the last. The rooms are stylish and well maintained. We would highly recommend the Herodion hotel, we could not fault it. Overall, an exceptional experience.

A super safe and super affordable hotel in Athens. I stayed here to be near the Painted Porch (it was under excavation oops), but found this hotel to be a 10-20m walk to all the ancient historical sites.

The front desk person was super nice, it was in a great and safe neighborhood, and I’d absolutely stay there again!

The free breakfast was a little limited but an awesome perk. The AC and wifi was a little hit or miss though

This hotel is only 5 minutes walk to the main square. The staff in the hotel seem really nice. There is a stand in the reception that has a number of leaflets that give a lot of information on Athens, which I thought was a nice touch. The room was very basic, but clean and warm. There is a rooftop terrace which serves breakfast and offers views of the Acropolis. The breakfast was nice enough I have to say. There is a lovely 24 hour bakery 2 minutes down the road which offers lovely home made sandwiches, cakes, break, juices and coffee. 5 minutes walk and you are at the main square which offers a lot in terms of restaurants, entertainment and bars. A really nice atmosphere. As I was only staying here for 1 day, this hotel was perfect and I would definitely recommend this for a basic, nice, clean and safe hotel.

This hotel is amazing. When we first entered our rooms were ready over 2 hours early and very clean, The staff was very kind constantly asking if we needed any replacements or services. On our final day at the hotel we had a flight and the hotel called us a van for the airport and packed us bagged breakfast. Very nice hotel would definitely recommend.

Hotel is ok for a 3 star. Breakfast was good. However in the amenities in their website they clearly list Parking and there is no parking. There is a private parking nearby, where they overcharge you 20 eur per day.
Great breakfast on roof overlooking acropolis, service was good and about a 5/10 minute walk from main square. Rooms were adequate but all a family would need.

Every single staff member was obliging and helpful. The restaurant was amazing. The location perfect. The Divani Acropolis hotel had lovely energy..
That iced water station in the entry was the perfect touch after coming in from the scorching Athens heat!

We recently stayed here and loved the location and enjoyed the pool. The room was clean and comfortable and a clear priority. The cleaning and support staff were genuinely friendly and helpful.
The only issue was the attitude and demeanor of many of the staff members; a few front desk staffers, the breakfast servers, and bar tenders. They were short and unfriendly. It was very strange to be received this way.  We were practically yelled at when we shared our disappointment about something. Even though the problem was rectified, I felt embarrassed by the person shaming us for a complaint. The breakfast servers avoided bringing us coffee and getting our own wasn’t an option. Finally, on the last day there was a taxi strike, but we received no notification. An email would have been nice. I’m guessing this review will make little difference, but it honestly left such a bad impression, I was happy to leave.

Big shout out to Bell Captain Michael for saving our trip! We dropped our bag with all our money, credit cards and passports in it. We were 1/2 way up the hill and he came running after us to see if it belonged to someone in our group. Not only did he chase us down but also asked us to verify the contents to confirm it was getting to the rightful owner. Also want to give commendation to Maria in the restaurant who has the sweetest smile and friendly demeanor.  Such a great stay in this beautiful hotel with a very honest and friendly staff. We will stay here every time we return to Greece thanks to them.

Stayed 3 nights and was impressed with this hotel. The most wonderful experience in Athens was the restaurant on the roof of the hotel as the view of the Acropolis at night has to be seen to be believed. Don't go by the website reservations as tables were available even when they weren't shown. We visited this restaurant each of our 3 nights there and received wonderful service even if just asking to have drinks. The photos we took of the Acropolis where so good that friends accused us of using a postcard or the web. It's really that good. Breakfasts at their downstairs restaurant were also excellent. The hotel is very conveniently located to tour both the Acrpolis and the Acropolis museum by foot. I can't recommend it enough.

Michael, the Bell Captain was a highlight of our stay.  Genial and professional, he made us feel more at home and comfortable. Helpful in information about surrounding area. Here’s an asset to your hotel.

Thermis at the desk, an extraordinary ambassador! Welcoming and helpful to an all of us traveling from Hawaii!

Dinning waitstaff is superb!

The hotel itself is honestly very nice and clean, the staff downstairs are very friendly and were helpful to us and even held our luggage and set up a taxi.

The only negative in the room is that the AC in the room did not work at all.

Now the bar is a different story, staff did not seem as friendly. I will say though that the bar is beautiful and offers an amazing view of acropolis that you cannot beat.

However, the staff at the roof top bar were far from friendly. They offer a free voucher for a drink but don’t mention it’s only a cheap wine or bud light beer. They also seem annoyed if you use the voucher and only allow you sit in certain areas. In my opinion, maybe just don’t give out the voucher then.

Even when having drinks another night and paying for them, we found it hard to get service. The servers were dancing and drinking alcohol at the bar while we waiting for a while. I know they were just having a good time and I’m okay with that but when we asked for the anything we got some attitude and I’m not sure why…

Honestly, I would probably stay again due to the location, clean rooms and beautiful view of acropolis. Just giving my input in hopes some things can improve!!

Review is for the restaurant.
You will not be disappointed by the rooftop views. Reserve early to guarantee a table at the edge overlooking the square with unobstructed view of the Acropolis. We arrived just before sunset so got bit day and night views.
Casual to upscale feel and although menus limited the food is delicious. We hand the lamb appetizers and the grouper as the main course. The food was outstanding, possibly the best grilled fish we’ve ever eaten. Have dinner here and you won’t be disappointed.

Another GREAT rooftop bar/restaurant with views of the city and the Acropolis.  The environment is cozy and romantic.  Their drink menu is awesome with specialized crafted drinks made at your table. Friendly staff. Popular with trendy crowd. Highly recommend. We were recommended by a friend to check this bar out and it did not disappoint.

A for Athens is an exceptional hotel that offers a perfect blend of comfort, convenience, and stunning views. Located in the heart of the city, just steps away from Monastiraki Square, it’s the ideal base for exploring Athens’ historical landmarks. The rooms are modern, clean, and comfortable, but the highlight of this hotel is the rooftop bar, which offers breathtaking views of the Acropolis, especially at night. The breakfast is also fantastic, with a wide variety of delicious options. The staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful, making our stay even more enjoyable. If you’re looking for a central location with unbeatable views, A for Athens is a great choice!

We went to the rooftop bar for a sunset drink and the view was magnificent! The staff was friendly however if you just want to have a drink you cannot sit at the table. You can only stay standing near a middle block. I saw the food going past and it didn't appeal to me so I was happy to stand. The cocktails are interesting but if you want a classic you need to ask for it as not on the menu.

Took me a while to find this hotel while looking for one with a pool.
Location is top marks very close to all the sites Plaka, Acropolis, hop on busses, etc.
The hotel made an effort to give us a room with a view of the Acropolis which made our stay brilliant.
Breakfast is minimal but good.
Pool is small but ok.
Rooms are small and be careful with doors, open outwards and I pulled a toenail off.
Roof terrace is amazing the view is superb.
All in all will return. Thank you.

Just for the location alone it’s easily 5 stars. I visited for my 30th birthday last November, the room was lovely & big considering it seemed it would be small from the outside. Staff were lovely (gave the guy on the front desk the rest of my cat biscuits for his kitty when leaving & he showed me photos of it). Super easy to walk to all the main sights even for a pair of fatties like my mum & I

The staff were very friendly and helpful with any questions we had. Room was nice, the pool is a nice small pool to get into on the hot days and we really enjoyed sitting outside on the outdoor patio with a glass or two of wine. Reasonably priced and good walking distance to the acropolis, restaurants and other points of interest.

Overall, this hotel is excellent.  I would definitely stay here again.

From the friendliness and helpfulness of the people on the front desk, to the cleanliness of the rooms and facilities, to the pool, to the food in the restaurant - all great.

The view on the terrace is outstanding during sunrise and it's a nice place to be in the evening too.  Unfortunately our room was directly below that terrace and the noise of people scraping their chairs and tables back and forth at 10:30pm wasn't the greatest.

The room was compact but effective.  The glass bathroom door, however, is transparent at night with a light on so a bit awkward!

The only downside was how I was spoken to by one of the cleaners.  We had (wrongly) assumed that rooms would be cleaned unless the 'DND' sign was put out.  That's how every other hotel i've ever stayed in works.  I was angrily and aggressively corrected by a cleaner, who brandished the sign stating "this is for you, this is for you" over and over.  Given i was having an excellent day i decided not to make more of the interaction but her attitude was completely wrong.

Before arriving at Acropolis Hill Hotel, we saw pictures online that showed stunning views of the Acropolis. We thought "this is too good to be true for the price," but were pleasantly surprised that those pictures were true. The rooftop of Acropolis Hill Hotel has an incredible, uninterrupted, view of Athen's most iconic site - the Acropolis. The roof was a beautiful place to watch the city wake up in the mornings and to watch the Acropolis light up at night.

The location of the hotel is excellent not just for its views, but also for its walkability to the main historical sites, parks, and a great variety of restaurants and shops. It's in a beautiful neighborhood with access to everything you could want.

The staff at Acropolis Hill were friendly and helpful, providing tips for the best tour options in the city, restaurant recommendations, and baggage storage.

The hotel is centrally located and about 5-10 minute walk to all of the Athens sites.  We felt safe walking the area at all hours.  The inside was nice with different areas to sit and relax. The morning breakfast was superb.  Lots of options and it was delicious.  The rooms and bathrooms were small but expected as most European hotels.  The beds were very comfortable.  The rooftop restaurant was great with wonderful views of the acropolis.

The location is perfect for shopping and restaurants near by. The room was nice but very small. The breakfast was good except for the scrambled eggs were mushy and half raw and same with the bacon. They should have a microwave so people can use it to cook the scrambled eggs more and bacon, the poached eggs (fried eggs) were perfectly delicious. Very clean Hotel

Central Hotel is located in the Plaka neighborhood in Athens. The rooms were very comfortable and the staff was very helpful and kind. The breakfast was very good, and the rooftop bar/restaurant was spectacular.  It's view of the Acropolis was amazing.

I am afraid price quality is not right here. Location is perfect for sure. Superior room so small. Be careful big school next door where children take a break every 15 minutes starting at 08.00am. Can get very loud during day. No other TV than Greek TV. Breakfast mediocre.

The hotel is located within walking distance of every attraction and entertainment area which is excellent, the rooms are not large but adequate, when the window is closed the noise level is very, very low and does not disturb sleep at all, the breakfast is basic and sufficient, the hotel has paid or free parking and also late check-out is pre-approved depending on the type of room  Those who choose should pay attention to the fine print when booking the room, we got a room with a balcony that was listed on the website as having a view but the view was a cluster of very old buildings and not beyond.  The restaurent on the roof of the hotel  has view of the Acropolis giving a pleasant atmosphere to sit down for dinner or even just to have a drink, recommended.

This hotel is great if you are staying a couple of nights in Athens. The beds are comfy, the cleaning is great and everyone is super friendly. They will hold your bags if you have a later flight in the evening. The location is sensational to all the laneways which are full of restaurants and shops the atmosphere at night is buzzing. The view from the their rooftop is pretty spectacular too, we grabbed a beer and watch the sunset. Also walking distance to the acropolis.

Excellent Location for visiting Athens. Breakfast was just a delight. Our Room was very comfortable and had a great view from our room over Athens. The added bonus of the Roof Terrace was a wonderful area to relax after a busy day site seeing. The view of the Acropolis in the Evening is just wonderful.

We ended up booking the last available room at this hotel at the last minute after our first hotel was a bad experience, and it was a great decision! Our room had a balcony with an incredible view of the Akropolis and the bed was comfortable. The room was a bit tight, but manageable. There was some noise from the rooftop above and people moving around in the hall, but nothing too bad. The staff was incredibly kind and helpful and the location is convenient to the city center without being too busy. Loved this hotel!

Great hotel, perfect Location, amazing views to the Partenón from the rooms and great service. Anastasia and Anano from the kitchen serve delicious greek breakfast witth an amazingly kind and warm attitude. Only thing to improve is the air conditioning in the breakfast room. It was too warm there, but it should be easy to fix. All other aspects of the hotel are perfect, people in the reception also very kind . Great hotel in a great place !

We stayed at Arion the end of May.  While the room was a little small the bed was very comfortable and the staff was nice and helpful .  The breakfast was great and Anastasia was so accommodating in getting anything you asked for. You cannot beat the location.  Walkable to shopping and eating.  We would definitely stay here again!

This hotel was brilliantly placed for restaurants, shops, acropolis and agora. The facilities are clean and all work, the breakfast is also fabulous. To top it off, the staff are fantastic, friendly, helpful and fun.
The view from the roof garden is amazing.
We had a brilliant stay, thank you.

Very good hotel in an amazing location.  Nothing fancy, just a really great hotel with brilliant breakfast.

Most of the main attractions are walkable,  and lots of shops and restaurants nearby.

Icing on the cake is the view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar.

Right in the prime location for visiting Athens, this hotel was clean, well run, and the rooftop restaurant offered an incredible view of the Acropolis in addition to excellent food & drinks.  Would revisit next time I’m in Athens.

I gave it 5 stars for the value of money. It really met the qualifications of a 3 star hotel. My room had a shower and bath tub. Be aware that the restaurant does not have view of Acropolis. But it was nice. The location of hotel is very good close to every where.

Comfy beds, location couldn't be better, great breakfast with lots of options, ONLY downside was very little sound proofing between rooms.
Loved the view from the rooftop restaurant. Food was excellent, but service was lacking.

It is a nice and clean hotel not far from the Acropolis. Our hotel room had a view of the Parthenon. The bathroom was clean and had enough towels, but the shower was quite small. The bed was smaller than usual (narrower and shorter) and it would have been more comfortable for me if it was softer, but the pillows were soft. The hotel is located in an area where there are many restaurants. The staff was pleasant and helpful. Breakfast was tasty and the meals from the breakfast menu can be ordered in the restaurant in front of the hotel, and this is also a significant problem for the hotel, because on the first day we waited about 20 minutes for the preparation of sunny side up eggs after ordering. On the second day, we waited for the preparation of sunny side up eggs for over 30 minutes, and on the third day, we waited for the preparation of the fruit plate (two sliced ​​apples, five sliced ​​strawberries, one sliced ​​banana and six grapes) for over 45 minutes, so speed is a big weakness in the hotel. We were missing a tissue box in the room. The walls were very thin and we could hear the neighbors next to us and other guests in the hallway. Last night I heard my neighbor through the wall snoring 😳. The noise was even through the windows (which were well closed).  The floor creaked in places. So staying at the hotel for a price of over 150 Euros per night was very disappointing.  Everything cannot be excused or to say that we paid a higher price for the view. We came to the hotel looking for quality services, which we did not receive.
Their rooftop dining was a beautiful experience. Food and drink we made perfectly, staff was friendly and efficient, and the view!!! It is a moderate walk from the Acropolis museum.

We loved our stay at this little hotel with a big heart. Great view of the Acropolis. Quiet at night. Walking distance to restaurants, shopping and everything you want to see. Terry and the rest of the staff were so welcoming and jumped at the chance to help us with anything we needed. It felt like staying with family (in a good way) We would definitely stay again.
The staff were very friendly and helpful. Great view of the Acropolis! We only stayed 2 nights but would have been happy to stay more. Very clean and comfortable room to accommodate the 4 of us.

We were a family of 3 Adults and stayed in the Family room. The accommodation was very clean and comfortable and our balcony off the main bedroom gave an interrupted view of the Acropolis. Staff were very helpful, especially Terry, who went out of his way to accommodate our needs. Breakfast was very generous in its quality and choice. The Hotel is located with restaurants and bars in very close proximity.  Thank you to all the staff from 3 happy Aussie travellers..

Everything about this apartment is fantastic—the location, the facilities, and the spacious terrace where you can enjoy the lively music from the tavern downstairs while people-watching.

The team of the property is the most amazing, welcoming and friendly team! 🫰

The apartment has everything you need for a comfortable stay, and it's ideally located near bars, restaurants, dance clubs, Monastiraki station, and sightseeing spots. Just bring earplugs for the noise, and you'll sleep perfectly! We had a wonderful time and would love to return.

We wanted somewhere that would showcase the best of Athens and Live in Athens did just this. Our accomodation was impeccable, clean and comfortable. It would us at the heart of life in Athens. Our kids loved it and so did we. We could walk to all the main attractions. We were surrounded by hundreds of restaurants and bars. We fell asleep listening to the nightlife of Athens. The noise never bothered us. It made us feel part of the life in Athens.

Cody was our best friend. Full of advice. He never let us done once and his advice made a different to our stay.

Wonderful hotel and extremely good value for money. The bed was large and very comfortable and the apartment was large and very clean. They have a stove and microwave so you can cook food. Amazing location in the heart of the city. Will stay here the next time I am in Athens!

Can't go wrong with these apartments. Part of the trend, it's situated at the very centre of Athens.

You're literally at the center point to reach any destination you wish to whilst visiting Athens.

Don't forget to have a look around all the bars and eateries dotted around.

The people who work for Live in Athens are excellent. They’re responsive, clear and kind.

The apartment is also pleasant.

If you don’t party late into the evening though (I’m writing this after 1am listening to drunks cackle, hoot and holler), don’t stay in the Psyri apartment. In addition to a very lively night life (could be a plus for you) they have a space on their roof where you can take your alcoholic drinks. I’m listening to people make a lot of noise scrapping chairs and tables right now right above my bedroom.  If you want to sleep, choose another one of their locations.

We stayed 2 nights and absolutely loved it.
They arranged pickup at the airport, and it was a seamless drive to the hotel.
A very friendly, easy check in, where we could decompress after our long journey.
Very close to all the attractions, a wonderful outdoor area, very comfortable bed, and lots of cats nearby outside.
And we had the same friendly driver back to the airport!

My husband and I stayed at the AVA HOTEL Athens, Greece - Wonderful Experience!!!!
The Ava Hotel was conveniently located to ALL Attractions.  The room was well equip with water and safe deposit box.  The impeccable service and hospitality of ALL the staff Mario, Penny, Magda to include but not limited to House Keeping made our stay most enjoyable/ pleasurable and delightful.  Furthermore, the breakfast  was scrumptious.  We were very impressed by the responsiveness of the hotel staff, who were extremely friendly, professional, and  very pleasant.  From start to finish, they were very attentive.  Don't look any further if you're going to be in  the City of  Athens  the AVA HOTEL is the place to be close to ALL Attractions within walking distance. The views to the Acropolis are amazing.
Very respectfully
From the Sunshine State of Florida
N/F

I can't say enough good things about this hotel! The service was outstanding. They were the most accommodating group of professionals we have ever had. We had chosen the studio on the top floor which has a huge open deck with panoramic views of the city and the Acropolis.  It was just what we wanted. We were the only ppl on that floor and it had everything we needed. A small frig, coffee and tea service,  free water, a comfy bed, room darkening shades with a tiny bath. We chose to include breakfast which was a feast each morning with the best fresh squeezed orange juice ever. The location of this hotel was perfect. We could walk to most everything we wanted to do. We were on the edge of the old city and steps from the Acropolis. They accommodated all our requests for cabs when needed as well as early and late check in and check out. It is a small boutique hotel but big on service and quality!

Absolutely lovely hotel. Our room was large and they even had two roll away beds already set-up in the living room for my children as we checked in. Room was spotless, well furnished, and beautiful. We had a huge balcony that we sat out on each night to watch the city lights and relax. Perfect location to get to all the sites we wanted to see, lots of shops and restaurants all around. The staff was so helpful and friendly and we all enjoyed the breakfast every morning. One morning our tour left really early and the staff kindly prepared to go bags of sandwiches, fruit and cheese so we had something to eat. Would definitely stay here again and can't recommend it enough.

We recently enjoyed an amazing three-night stay at this Athens hotel. The staff's friendliness and constant smiles created a welcoming atmosphere for us. The central location made exploring Athens effortless, with major sights like the acropolis within easy reach, which we could literally see from our balcony.

The rooms were quiet, clean, and attentively cared for, providing a peaceful retreat after a day of sightseeing. Despite its central location, the hotel maintained a serene ambiance, ensuring a restful night's sleep.

Overall, this hotel exceeded my expectations in service, location, and comfort. I highly recommend it for a memorable stay in Athens!

360 Degrees Hotel in Athens is a perfect place to stay for every reason you could think of! It’s in the Monostiraki neighborhood which is the center of all the neighborhoods. It is very close to shopping, good restaurants and all the attractions; Agora, Hadrian’s Library, Parthenon etc…

We checked in easily (online) and our room was directly viewing the bustling square. It was clean and had all the amenities we needed. Breakfast was 10+! Everything was neatly put out, fresh with a huge variety of items! The coffee was fresh brewed every morning and they continually put out hot steamed cream.
Surprisingly, the restaurant for dinner wasn’t that good and I’d have to rate that a 2. All food items were sub par and many people around us complained to the manager or left the food. The view of the Parthenon is amazing from this rooftop so I would highly recommend just going there for a drink and elsewhere for a nice dinner.
I would definitely stay here again as it’s the perfect location for everything you want to do in Athens! Hotel front desk staff were all very nice and super helpful!

Went to the rooftop. Terrible service. The manager, his second (bouncer?), and the waiter all had a terrible attitude. Very arrogant. The rooftop has a great view of the Acropolis if you get seated by that end. So they believe service is not important. Yet I saw at least four more rooftops around that have the same view. The tables not on the side of the Acropolis could have a great view as well, but they put some trees that block it. Definitely need a new decoration person.
They had a very poor food offering, only 5 different dishes.
I recommend going to one of the other nice rooftops around.

I highly recommend this hotel for a variety of reasons.
1. The location is amazing. You are a short walking distance from all the major sites in downtown Athens.
2. The breakfast is extremely fresh and delicious.
3. The view from the breakfast area is breathtaking.
4.The Room is spacious and clean.
5. The staff is very friendly and helpful.

However, if you are a light sleeper, you might need ear plugs as the rooftop bar does not turn down the music until 2-3 AM.
Located near the main attractions that Athens has to offer including historical sites, shopping, food, and drinks.  Enjoyed our stay, would recommend!
Very clean with good staff. But no fire safety or exits. No power in my room. Nowhere to hang towels in the bathroom. No outdoor space. Low flow shower. No coffee in the room. No bin .

Very nice and helpful staff, rooms are small but very clean and neat (in the end we are here to explore the city, not the hotel room). The location is extremely convenient in a short walking distance to all the main sights, restaurants and nightlife.
There is also luggage storage so we were able to leave our bags after the check out since we had a later flight (for free).
The most beautiful thing about this place is the rooftop terrace and the view over the city and Acropolis. Just wooow!
If ever again in Athens, for sure I'd stay here! Thank you so much!

Great location near Monastiraki metro station. Friendly staff, clean rooms, and a wonderful view from the roof garden. We were offered a free upgrade and enjoyed breakfast with a view of the Acropolis. We'll definitely come back!
We had a great view of the Acropolis from our balcony. The breakfast was simple but good and there is a large selection of awesome restaurants just around the corner.

Nice hotel with potential. The beds were comfortabel, we had a balcony to the street, good airco, and showers were good. Rooms are rather old fashioned, in general the hotel could use a renovation though everything still functional. Breakfast was ok, provided all the essentials, and the view from the rooftop terrace was amazing! Enjoy a drink up there. Only some music and more cozy furniture is missing there. All in all good choice for a hotel in Athens!

Really great hotel if you’re looking for something simple (with air conditioning) that is close to everything and has a spectacular view of acropolis. The service was the best, I had to checkout really early at 4am and they had a bag of breakfast for me. It made my day. I would absolutely recommend this hotel and would go back.

The location is perfect - it is in a very lively area, and close walking distance to everything. Despite being in a busy area, noise is not an issue. It is close to lots of shops and excellent food/dining options. The place also feels secure/safe given the 24 hour reception. We were warmly greeted each time we came and went from the accommodation. We were lucky to have a balcony and view of the acropolis from our room on the 4th floor! Other pros of this accommodation is it has a comparatively earlier check-in time. Shared bathroom is not too bad surprisingly, good water pressure and it is all kept clean.

Main con: Wifi did not work well in the room at all. There are lots of stairs to climb if you’re on the 4th floor, but this is not uncommon in Europe! They have a lift available for luggage only.

I enjoyed my stay here :) I had my own room with its own bathroom, which altogether was clean and great, the location was good and the staff were friendly. I remember breaking something in the bathroom by accident, but the staff were kind about it.

Superb location! Very good rooms considering the cost, they were clean and bigger than other cheap accommodation options i’ve stayed in. I also stayed on the 4th floor and there was no street noise at all, but I couldn’t say for lower levels. Finally, the staff were incredibly friendly, and “available 24/7 for your service.” Excellent experience and I would stay again. Highly recommend for young travellers on a budget!

Avoid!!!  AVOID 🆘🆘🆘 Wasn’t clean.  I was  abused by the male boss. don’t be fool by its good location, you need feel safe, have a clean room for a  good night sleep after a day out and back to your room with  welcoming  staffs, not missable faces  and tiny area

My room was simple (might not be to everyone's liking, but suited me fine.) Clean and the staff very helpful. The view from the 5th floor terrace was worth the hike up the stairs and to enjoy take out dinner! Well located not for from Metro station,  restaurants and the sights.

Super convenient location. Walking distance to the Parthenon and the museum. Half a block to tons of shopping and dining options.  1 block to the metro is a huge plus, too.

The hotel is well staffed with very helpful employees who truly want you to have a great experience.

The rooms are clean and well kept if slightly dated. GREAT water pressure and almost instant hot water!

The breakfast was good with lots of options to choose from. Cereal, pastries, bread, meats and cheeses, and of course fruit. With coffee, tea,orange juice,sour cherry, and milk to drink.

We would absolutely stay here again.

This hotel is in a perfect location and for the price you can’t beat it! Just one block from the Acropolis south entrance and the best walking street with so many amazing restaurants all around. The breakfast is phenomenal the staff are wonderful. They have the best deal in the bar for happy hour from 4pm-8pm BOGO drinks for €6.50 and the bar area and lobby are beautifully decorated. The rooms are basic but cozy and spacious. Overall had a very nice stay!

Great location, literally a step away from Acropolis. The MVP is the AM shift manager, was nice enough to give me the room as soon as possible which was a lifesaver after a 12hr red eye flight. The breakfast was good too. The only thing is the rooms are quite dated.

Exceptional hotel with excellent service, one of the best breakfasts ever at a hotel (included in rate), great location and expansive rooms. Location is central to major historical sites, a plethora of stores and restaurants, metro/bus/tram stops. We walked to everything except for our cool tram ride to the Greek Riviera. Front desk staff, housekeeping staff and breakfast staff are top notch. In the morning, you can treat yourself to a large variety of breakfast items, freshly made, hot and cold. Great communication prior to and during our 3 day stay.

This hotel is in a perfect location to walk to all the sites! The street is not noisy or too busy. The staff was very welcoming and helpful! The room was clean and had all you needed; the fixtures and linens are a little dated, but again all you needed. Nice breakfast choices too. Thank you!

Breakfast buffet was excellent and fresh. Best scrambled eggs ever. Loved the espresso machine. Shower decent size for Greece. Elevators were reliable and fast.  Front desk staff very helpful. Do not hesitate to double park in front to load and unload.

I stayed here in 2018 with my family and when re-visiting Athens again this year I immediately booked a stay here as it was perfect then and I am more than pleased to say it is still perfect now!  So central, great friendly staff, fantastic view of the Acropolis from the lovely rooftop bar.  Perfect breakfast – what more can I say!!

I highly recommend this hotel! The cost-benefit is excellent!! Some positive points: the staff is very kind, polite and attentive; the hotel is right in the city center, you can visit the Acropolis and other tourist attractions on foot; the hotel has a terrace with a beautiful view of the Acropolis; the rooms are clean, well-equipped, with good amenities and the room service is great; the breakfast is quite decent.

Whenever I am in Athens, this hotel is my top choice. Conveniently located just a few minutes from Monastiraki metro station, it offers both comfort and accessibility.
The staff are always friendly and courteous, ensuring a pleasant stay. The rooms are consistently clean and well-maintained. Breakfast is simple yet delicious, catering to various preferences. The highlight is the charming rooftop bar, providing a lovely view of the city and the Acropolis.

Great location, amazing view from rooftop bar! Staff is so friendly and helpful. Rooms are very nice; not huge, but have everything you need. Now I know why a friend of mine stays here whenever she comes to Athens!

I had a pleasant stay at this hotel, and there are several positive aspects to highlight.

Firstly, the location is excellent—it’s close to many tourist spots, which is a big plus for those looking to explore the city. The rooftop is really nice, offering a cozy view where you can even see the Acropolis, making it a perfect spot to enjoy the sunset. The hotel itself is nice, with well-distributed facilities and a decent breakfast. I also appreciated having access to the fridge, which came in handy.

However, there were a few issues that affected my overall experience. On the day of checkout, I was leaving early at 6:30 AM, but the reception was closed, and there was no staff available to assist. I wasn’t able to retrieve my food from the fridge, which I had prepared for my travel day, and the same thing happened to two other guests heading to the airport. There should definitely be someone available for early checkouts, even if it’s just for small tasks like greeting or farewelling guests.

Another minor inconvenience was the towel fee—while it’s common for hostels to provide towels for free, the 2 euro charge wasn’t too bad, but still, it’s worth mentioning.

Lastly, I noticed that the bedsheets had an unpleasant smell, like they were damp or not properly washed. I had the same sheets for three days, and the smell was quite strong. I recommend checking the linens carefully before using them for guests to ensure they are fresh.

Overall, it’s a good place to stay, especially for solo travelers looking to meet new people. The hotel offers a friendly atmosphere, and the breakfast is definitely worth it. However, some small improvements could make the experience even better.

Great location, my room was clean and secure, decent breakfast. As far as amenities, I've stayed in much nicer hostels, there's hardly any common area, and the best feature of the hostel, the rooftop, doesn't open till 6pm. Maintenance and cleanliness/supplies in bathrooms could be improved. But if you're looking for a cheap, safe stay near the Acropolis, a quick walk from the Metro, this is a good option.

This hostel has a great location to public transport,  attractions and restaurants. The rooms were clean and a good size with locker space for backpacks. Beds were comfy and had a night stand with plug. The AC was amazing in the heat aswell. Staff were polite but not overly warm. Breakfast was basic but substantial. They did allow us to leave our bags at reception prior to check in which was helpful.  Overall would recommend.

Stayed here in late May 2024.
Location is perfect for accessing the metro (Akropoli station), Acropolis Museum, and majority of the ruins (Acropolis, both Agoras, Hadrian's library, etc - buy the multi access ticket from the Hellenic Heritage website). A lot of food nearby and the Plaka neighborhood is not far for touristy shopping and more food.
Rooms are an okay size, lockers roomy enough for a backpack, BYO lock but also they sell them for a high price in the lobby vending machine. Multiple people's luggage cases can take up all the floor space. Bedding is not great and there were stains on the blankets. Bathrooms were clean but they do not clean out past guests' toiletries that were left behind, so it piles up, and they desperately need better ventilation and some towel racks in there.
Some staff are nice and helpful, some staff are incredibly rude. One person said we could fill our water bottles in the breakfast area, another yelled at us for trying to do it before the breakfast area was 'open.' No clear policy it seems. Breakfast is okay, more of a continental / UK breakfast fusion style.
The lobby is also the breakfast area and it's gated off at night so if you are trying to work remotely, there is virtually nowhere to sit. There is a rooftop bar with a sem-decent view of the Acropolis but the cheapest and most uncomfortable plastic chairs.
Okay for a one or two night stay/budget traveler, otherwise try one of the small boutique hotels in the neighborhood.

Location is great. The night time desk worker is creepy and mean. Made me wait outside for my 3am cab and there was a group of men being rowdy outside. Didn’t make me feel safe. Esp when I could have waited inside the seated area.  The linens smelled like cat pee. The location is great tho. And for what it is, it’s not bad but don’t expect great service.

Let's start with the location, brilliant!! Right in the center of Athens, by the market and walkable distance to all the tourist spots.
It's a small hotel with a super small team, but everyone was kind and sweet.
The room i stayed in was on the smaller end, but who cares,  not moving in, just staying for a vacation. More important,. The room was clean and smelled nice, and had a huge balcony.
Definitely a good choice.

Carolina hotel is right in the heart of Placa area, nothing fancy, but you only need a shower and a good night sleep. The rest is out and about in Athens. Very friendly and kind people at the front desk and they helped us out to sit before seven in the morning so we can have breakfast before taking off to the airport. We will be back.

It’s very nice hotel with good price, room is small, neat and pretty good. I love the terrace. Clean washroom. Front desk staff is very friendly and helpful. Breakfast is not bad. Distance from the Monastiraki square is hardly 7 minutes. Lots of nice restaurants and bars in the same street.

Very nice place, clean, bright room, very good breakfast.

Close to the Acropolis, plenty of cafes around.
A fabulous hotel quite clean and very close to all that old Athens has to offer. Great staff. Great rooms and a brilliant breakfast ! Awesome value!

Excellent hotel to stay in while visiting Athens. Very clean, staff very helpful and the location is perfect for dining and shopping. The Acropolis is within walking distance, uphill of course. I highly recommend this hotel. In fact we are going back next year. Staff also speak English.

This place is so centrally located, I could practically high-five the Parthenon from my window.
The staff were so helpful, they gave me great local tips on where to eat, how to avoid tourist traps, and even where to get the best souvlaki without a 30-minute wait. (Pro tip: trust their food recommendations—they know their stuff!).
10/10, would definitely stay here again if my crippling depression wasn't catching up to me.

In amongst the action. Balconied room overlooking thriving street. Great shower. Housekeeping thorough and daily. Views of Acropolis from top floor breakfast terrace. Very quick and able reception, nothing too much trouble. Great value would recommend highly.

Adrian has a great location. Near Ermou shopping, Metro is very near. Adrian is very helpful.. Can leave bags if needed.... Late flight etc. Breakfast on roof terrace with view of Acropolis is brilliant.. Really lovely. Shower room was OK... Could do with an update but OK for now. Small balcony and aircon.
Room price is v good for the budget... Book... Long weekend in Adrian is worth it.

I’m surprised by the high reviews. My room was so grubby and there were strange stains all over (peeling wallpaper, and yellow stains over the wall, red stain on the light switch, the remote was filthy, and the carpet felt matted). I felt really uncomfortable with how dirty the hotel was.

I missed housekeeping the first day. I asked for water replenishment told me to just “buy some” down the road. I would have had better amenities at an Airbnb.

Two highlights:

1. The location
2. The fresh squeezed orange juice at breakfast is excellent.

Our daughter and friend stayed in an Acropolis view room with balcony to celebrate their combined 30th birthdays (they loved the room!). I emailed the hotel to ask if it was possible to have champagne and glasses waiting for their arrival. The staff were very happy to arrange this - with a note, a little birthday cake and snacks on a lovely tray with the chilled champagne and glasses. The birthday girls were in tears at the lovely surprise and enjoyed the balcony and view with their champagne. Thank you Philippos Hotel for making their stay even more special.

Great location near Acropolis, Museums and La Plaka. Gracious, hardworking and helpful staff, gave restaurant recommendations, scheduled tour and taxi. Excellent Greek breakfast. Stayed here 15 years ago and the quality and value are consistent. Rooftop terrace with direct view of the Acropolis, wine available.

Amazing location, spotless clean, but missing a few basic amenities (e.g. plug socket on only 1 side of bed, no plugs at desk or light around mirror), staff were really friendly and very helpful. Rooftop space gives fantastic views

Great hotel in a central position close to the entrance to the acropolis. Rooms were clean and all seemed to have a balcony . The buffet breakfast was very good with a wide selection, including Greek cakes and yoghurt. The only slight downside was the smell from the drain in the bathroom but hey it's Greece and the drains always smell in Greece

This hotel was amazing. we were not meant to stay, but after getting late from the aeroport ( 5 in the morning ) we went inside asking for a room ( even tho we knew it was nearly impossible. The man saw that I was really anxious for being outside in the dark, and let us stay. It was really nice and also, we were so close from the Acropolis that we could go walking. Also the hotel super clean.

I had an especially enjoyable 3 night stay at Phidias Hotel Athens. It is located just far enough away from the city core to be away from the noise, traffic, crowds & hectic busyness of the city core, but close enough to walk to attractions in 15 minutes, give or take. It looks out on the lively promenade that leads to the city core's attractions & is lined w/outdoor cafes, a park & soccer field - it draws people of all ages to meet, enjoy a meal or drink, to just stroll & people watch, jog, bike ride, play soccer, walk to nearby places, or just hang out. This is also your view from the hotel rooms w/balconies. I looked forward every evening to watching this scene of people just enjoying life. I owe a special thank you to the receptionist/manager "G" for her exceptional Kenyan/Greek hospitality. She routinely went "above and beyond" to accommodate me and every guest at the hotel & truly deserves 5 gold stars! And although I did not have much contact with the day receptionist, she too went out of her way to ensure my taxi was ordered in time to board my ferry. I recommend it & will always try to stay at Phidias Hotel Athens when I visit. The hotel has an elevator, rooms are very clean w/comfortable beds, if lacking somewhat in storage (drawers/dresser). It is a very good value for this 2-to-3 star hotel.

Loved Phidias Hotel in Athens. Location is very convenient close to the metro and to the Acropolis and Greek restaurants scattered across the street. Their breakfast was really lovely and worth it. I wasn't available on most mornings and Poppy would pack it for me always.

Grace was very helpful in storing my luggage safely 🧳 before my flight since I had an early checkout to go to Eben Beach.

Value for money!!

Incredible location right by acropolis views incredible, adjacent to the grounds, quiet, safe, no traffic. Rooms very clean, service wonderful, Grace who works in reception was incredibly helpful as were all other members of staff. WiFi solid, breakfast plentiful, very close to metro and great value for money. Very under rated

Our stay at Phidias Hotel was disappointing. When we arrived early on August 29th, we were told there was a "technical issue" with our room. It turned out our room had been double-booked, and we were offered a taxi to Golden City Hotel, but the driver wasn’t informed that the fare would be covered, making us uncomfortable with the arrangement. The communication from the staff was confusing and unprofessional, and it took hours before we could access our room. The whole situation felt disorganized and ruined our experience.

We had to cancel our stay due to a sudden family loss, and the hotel showed no compassion or understanding. Despite explaining our situation and providing proof, they coldly denied our request for a refund. The lack of empathy was shocking. I wouldn’t recommend this hotel to anyone.

I've stayed here on a few occasions; the outside of the Hotel and the reception is rather deceiving. A simple, old reception desk and a reception area with not much space. However, the rooms are clean and comfortable for one or two people, the toilet/shower are clean with complementary shower gel. The Hotel is in an excellent and very central location and you can't beat the price!
Close to all the attractions. Big room with comfortable bed. Rooms are very clean. Shower small. Breakfast is basic but nice. Friendly staff. Great value for the price paid.

The hotel is a genuine 2-star hotel:
- The room was mostly small, and there was not a good air circulation for it to vent
- The elevator did work but didn’t feel 100% reliable/safe
- We got there after 09 pm and the man who checked us in only asked for our names and gave us the key. At no point did they check our passports 🫨

Good thing was that there was people at the front desk 24/7. However, we didn’t feel comfortable enough to ask for suggestions or anything like that

Breakfast is on site, and though there is not a lot of variety, the products are of very good quality

Greek yogurt was the best I’ve ever tried in my life! And the honey was also amazing

They also offered cold cuts and cheese. Coffee, milk, tea, orange juice. And I think there were also some fruits

The staff in charge of breakfast and cleaning the rooms were very friendly

Place looked mostly clean, rooms were made every day and clean towels were left by default

Athens may be filled with soulless Airbnb rentals, each furnished with the same generic IKEA pieces—but Hotel Cecil is a refreshing exception. Like the city itself, Hotel Cecil isn't perfect, but its abundant charm and old-world character easily overshadow any minor imperfections. The prime location, the vintage Otis elevator, and functional WiFi are just added perks. Will be on the top of my list for my next visit to the city.
Smaller rooms but clean and comfortable. Friendly staff. Continental breakfast. Lots of shopping around and close to lots of things to do and see

Hotel is located in a quaint and quiet area of Athens within walking distance of the Acropolis and much more. There are many restaurants close by. We arrived before our rooms were ready, but they had a secure area that we could store our luggage so we could go and explore. Hotel itself was secure as well as you had to have a code to enter. Rooms were clean and spacious. Only complaint was in one of our rooms there was no hot water in the shower and one of their front desk personnel wasn't very friendly. Overall though, we would stay again.

The location is nice- in Plaka beside the acropolis and flea market. However, we had a bad experience, would not stay here again and would not recommend it. There are plenty of other decent 2/3 star hotels in the area
First, upon check in, we were told we would have to change rooms. We got 4 different explanations including there was something wrong with your current room, this is not the room you wanted, we are doing you a favour etc. When I sent a formal email to the hotel, I was told that someone at the hotel made a “mistake” and gave our “original” room to someone else and now we were just returning to it but had to wait for them to check out.
When we got to our “new room”, there was a bad smell like sewage from the bathroom. I told the gentleman at the helpdesk who just said “sorry” and I emailed management again- no reply this time. The smell was brutal before we ever used the bathroom and unbearable. Made it difficult to stay in the room.
We asked for a taxi pickup from the hotel to the airport at 3:20am. When we came down to check out, the gentleman at the desk said his colleague made a note of the request but didn’t actually do it! We ended up having to drag our bags out on the street at 3:30am (with my mother) to flag a taxi. Too many screw ups for a hotel that we paid decent money for.

Exactly as we expected. Location was perfect - close to everything we planned to visit. Children loved the National Garden which were really close.
Breakfast is small but also did the job before we went out to explore.
A trip to the terrace in the evening was a nice way to wind down.

On 23/05 we made a reservation through Booking, but we did not observe the date. At the same time we saw the error, we contacted Booking, as well as the person responsible for the hotel, informing them of what had happened.
To our surprise, the hotel charged us practically the daily rate, even though we explained the mistake, including offering to change it to the correct date, which was not possible.
It's a shame for a hotel that doesn't show solidarity with the customer and ends up getting paid twice if there was another reservation.
We even understand that a fine could be applied, but it is unreasonable for it to be a daily rate.
Due to the lack of understanding, as the person in charge informed us that we requested a late cancellation, which was not true, I do not recommend the hotel.
Lack of empathy, understanding and common sense on the part of the hotel.

EXCELLENT LOCATION!!! My husband and I have stayed at the Adam's Hotel twice. We arrived early in the morning. We were able to set the luggage aside until the room was ready. Staff we polite. Rooms are small yet comfortable with a balcony (keep in mind it is a historic area). The terrace view is beautiful, especially at night of the Acropolis. Location is a quick short walk to many shops and restaurants. They sell hop-on hop-off bus tour tickets, which take a full tour of Greece main attractions. Highly recommended staying at the Adam's Hotel it was a hidden gem!!

Very basic room and hotel with no frills, but couldn't have been more pleased for what it was. Ended up being one of our favourite places we stayed while in Greece. Friendly staff, great neighbourhood and our terrace room had a certain kind of charm to it. If you are looking for a budget place to stay in a nice, quiet neighbourhood, I cannot recommend Art Gallery Hotel enough.

The Art Gallery hotel offers a cozy, family-owned atmosphere where guests are warmly welcomed, almost as if they were visiting relatives. During my three-night stay, I found the rooms to be adequately clean, comfortable, and spacious, albeit with some minor wear and tear on the room and bathroom amenities. For instance, I encountered an issue with the door lock, but the friendly reception staff promptly provided a workaround with a smile, suggesting a clever trick to operate it until it was fixed.

One of the hotel's standout features is undoubtedly its staff, who consistently go above and beyond to assist guests with a genuine kindness that enhances the overall experience. While the breakfast provided was basic, it served its purpose well enough to fuel explorations of Athens' ancient wonders, and it also conveniently provided a snack for later in the morning at nearby restaurants.

The hotel's location is another highlight, situated in a quiet area yet still within easy reach of key attractions like the Plaka and the Acropolis. Despite its proximity to tourist hotspots, the neighborhood maintains a peaceful ambiance, with plenty of excellent dining options and cafes nearby. Moreover, the reasonable pricing adds to the hotel's appeal, offering good value for money considering its prime location and attentive service.

In summary, the Art Gallery hotel provides a pleasant stay characterized by familial hospitality, comfortable accommodations, and a prime location that allows guests to enjoy the best of Athens without feeling overwhelmed by crowds.
Great location, the rooms were clean and spacious, the staff was friendly and attentive, we really enjoyed our short stay and hope to return one day

The rooms aren’t anything special, but they have everything you need and more! The staff are the sweetest, coolest people!! So friendly and warm. I stayed here with my study abroad group multiple times over our 3 week trip since Art Gallery was considered our “home base.” By the end of the trip everyone in my group referred to this place as “home.” I even stayed an extra day and a half here with my boyfriend after my studying was done and he loves it just as much as I do!! Location is SUPERB, breakfast is delicious, but the staff make this place AMAZING! I highly recommend hanging out in the front desk area to get to know the staff and cool off in the AC. I’ll be back soon! ❤️

The hotel has a fascinating history, its foundations having sheltered the current owners’ grandparents as a bunker in WWII before they made it first an art gallery and then, building upward, this hotel. Don’t expect spanking new state-of-the-art ANYTHING. Expect inimitable charm, cleanliness, authenticity, helpful staff, and an excellent location. We loved it.

An excellent location. We'd stay again for it's proximity tot he Acropolis and all of the walking and dining in Plaka. Easy walking to Temple of Zeus, Parliament and changing of the guard, the all-marble ancient Olympic stadium, Acropolis and Acropolis museum and more.

Rooms are a bit dated but nice. Standard European small bathrooms but everything was in working order. Small balcony and great views of Plaka and Acropolis. Rooftop lounge great for cocktails and small plates.

We took the budget room but there was no information available on booking.com that it is actually an underground room. The bathroom was actually 3 steps up inside the room making it very uncomfortable. So please don’t consider the budget room and make sure you get rooms 1st floor onwards. The location was more than perfect.
Very nice hotel in the middle of PLAKA, very hospitable and friendly staff. The owner makes all guests feel like one of the family. Highly recommended place and we will surely return.

Don’t buy the breakfast, it is not worth the €10. The hotel rooms are decent, but the bed was not so comfortable. Great location for walking to the historic monuments. The cleaning staff were very friendly.

The Good.

* Superb friendly reception staff, breath of fresh air to see genuine kindness

* Location brilliant as many have indicated

The Not So Good.

* Double room on floor 2 was so small, if we had 2 suitcases there would be zero room to stand, think TINY.

* Unfortunately one of noisiest hotel rooms I have ever been in, zero soundproofing. Noise from elevator and all bathrooms around us was horrible, beware if you are sensitive to alot of noise at night. You can hear all conversations in both corridors and surrounding rooms. We were woken on many occasions thinking someone was in room with us, those walking in corridors soubded so close.

* Yes a wet soggy mess on floor of bathroom after shower as door does not seal, beware dont leave anything on floor as it will be soaked.

* Bed is soft but with any lack of support so beware if you have back trouble

I stayed here at the end of July with my two boys, and we absolutely loved it! Such a cozy little gem! The hotel is perfectly located just a short walk from the city center, making it super easy to explore Athens on foot. Plus, there's public transport nearby, which made it so convenient to get to the beach!
