The staff were incredibly friendly and went above and beyond when we had an issue with a forgotten bag—they helped us recover it without any hassle. The highlight, though, is definitely the stunning rooftop. The view of the Acropolis is unbeatable, and the restaurant up there is top-notch. I would absolutely stay here again!

Wonderful experience! We stayed here one night prior to our cruise. The staff is very kind and helpful. We received welcome drinks at the rooftop bar and also had excellent authentic Greek food. The hotel itself is beautiful with a modern look. And you have a great view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar!
The room had a good amount of space for us. The shower was a little smaller than we are used. They were also to take our luggage for us while we explored the town before and after our reservation. The shop and restaurants are a short walk from the hotel. Overall great experience!!

Best part of the stay is the wonderful staff and the beautiful common area. The room was nicely appointed but poorly lit (need a light near the entry door) and the bathroom was poorly designed — strange tub with very little room to stand to take a shower then the shower curtain sticks to you. Hardly any room to step out of the tub/shower without bumping into the vanity or toilet. No outlet in bathroom so the hair dryer sits on the desk in the main part of the room and is plugged underneath. First room I had suffered from an unpleasant smell in the drains; second room was better. The staff absolutely could not be any nicer, but the room was a disappointment.

The staff is friendly, and the room is clean, though it is small. The elevator is tiny, but I later discovered that many elevators in Greek hotels are similar. The only downside was the shower. However, the location is perfect, just 5 minutes from the Acropolis Museum.

Everything about this place was perfect! The air conditioning was heaven after long hot days of exploring, great location to get around, stocked with everything we needed and the balcony was such a nice touch to enjoy our morning coffee (provided) and our wine & snacks from a local gem down the street. The only issue was the front door to the building itself definitely needs to be replaced, but it was worth every second of minor inconvenience! Highly recommend staying here if you're visiting Athens! Wonderfully romantic for couples.

1. Unclean small and stuffy room - the bedsheets had stains..looked quite disappointing. The shower was a bidet. Was not sure if I wanted to take a shower with a huge bidet.
2. Broken plug points - the plug points to charge the phone was broken. The socket was hanging off the wall.
3. Noisy air-con - the room had an odour..probably due to unserviced air conditioner.
4. Not recommended for families.
5. Not recommended to anyone who is looking for cleanliness.
6. Smelly passage to the room.
7. The lift is extremely small.
8. Due to the above disappointing features avoidable if you are looking for a vacation experience.
9. Overpriced at 81 EUR per night..!!
10. Had to leave the property the very next day due to the poor hygiene.
11. I had booked a room for 3 nights…and cancelled the very next day and had to book another hotel nearby. Absolute waste of money. Avoidable if looking for clean and vacation experience.

Pella Inn Hostel is an accommodation that gives overall good value for money, the place has a number of notable advantages such as its location in the city center, very close to Monastiraki square, the balcony on the fifth floor with the wonderful open view towards the Acropolis, and the kind and friendly staff who are always ready to help and have a good command of the English language  and in several other languages.  In particular, I would like to give a good mention to Maria, who works at the reception desk mainly during the night shifts, she is a professional and educated worker, who is always ready to help you in anything, patient, and also an interesting and smart person.
Regarding the shortcomings of this accommodation: there is no kitchen that you can use and cook food for yourself, unlike what is customary in a large number of hostels throughout Europe. In the room that I have stayed in which was on the first floor, there were a small number of electrical outlets and they were located in inconvenient locations around the room, for example do not expect an electrical outlet next to your bed, out of 8 beds that were inside the room there was an electrical outlet in maybe only two beds out of the total eight.  Also the toilet and shower room are separate from each other and are located inside the room, which gives more privacy and comfort of course, but they were very small in size, outdated and in despair needy for renovation, the shower head was small and the flow was relatively very weak.  The dimensions of the bathroom will not suit a wide person with wide and tall physical dimensions with a relatively big weight. and also the diameter of the sink in the bathroom was very narrow and small, which causes water to constantly fall on the floor and wet it.  with an extra towel.  It is important to note that the towels are borrowed for free at the reception desk, unlike other accommodations that charge money for towels and sometimes also for bed linen.  In terms of cleanliness, the staff cleans the rooms every day and the toilets and showers are also thoroughly cleaned with disinfectants.  In addition to this, it is worth noting that there is a rather large refrigerator available for use by guests of the hostel, which is located one floor above the reception floor.  In conclusion, this is a cheap accommodation, in a central location, with a great and professional staff, just take into account the shortcomings of the bathroom and the noise from the street from the cars, motorcycles and other vehicles, but this is due to the central location of the hostel right in the heart of the city of Athens.  I wish you a pleasant stay at the place and many thanks to the staff of the hostel.

Perfect location in the midst of everything but inside very quiet , room small but clean and functional ( very traditional Greek).
Roof top terrace fabulous and you are welcome to take up own food n drink .
Will be staying again

The rooms were as basic as it gets. One of the biggest frustrations was the lack of personal charging sockets, leaving everyone in the room scrambling for one of the four available outlets—which, by the way, had to be shared among eight beds. It felt like a constant battle just to charge your phone. If you’re a light sleeper, be prepared: the hostel is on a busy street, and any open windows mean you’re in for a night of loud, jarring noise from the traffic below. The bathroom also has a low ceiling, so if you’re taller than 6'3", you’ll definitely need to watch your head.

All in all, it’s exactly what you’d expect for the price—but don’t expect much more than the basics.

Location, amazing! Workers were so nice for the most part. The room was the issue. The door did not stay closed and we were in a private room. 1st time the door was open we thought it was us. The second and third time we knew it was just broken and not us closing the door incorrectly. It's a serious problem when you have to bring the actual door handle down to the front desk because it is THAT broken. No one should wake up or come back with the door wide open all because everyone who can fix the door is on vacation. I'd give safety a negative 1.

We were also there 3 days and the rooftop bar was never open. Lots of false marketing and not worth it.

Our family of 4 stayed in a family room for 3 nights before a cruise.  We planned to to be out touring all day and we just needed a clean, reasonably priced place to sleep in Athens.
Positives -
- The room was larger than expected  - one section had a queen or a king bed, while the other section of the room had 2 twin beds.  We had plenty of room.  The room was not fancy but it was clean and more than met our needs.  The cost of the room was very reasonable during the high summer season.  We struggled to find rooms that would accommodate 4 people, so we we were very happy to find this one.
- The bath was split - one room had the shower, while the other room had the toilet which was convenient with 4 people getting ready.
- the air conditioning was very good.  We visited during August and it was extremely hot outside.  We all slept great in the room.
- there was a filter water tap for refilling water bottles which was convenient.
- the location was excellent- we walked everywhere.  Metro station was close by. There were grocery stores within a 15 minute walk.  The area came alive at night and there were lots of restaurants within walking distance.
- there is a kitchen and a fridge to store food.  Food must be labelled.  We placed some leftovers in the fridge one night and the next day, the box remained but it was empty- someone had eaten the leftovers. I can’t blame the hostel for the action of their guests.
- reception was very nice and always willing to answer questions.
Overall, we were very happy with our stay here! The hostel was everything it advertised itself to be.

The best hostel I’ve stayed at so far! The location is excellent—right in the city center and in a pretty safe neighborhood, so you can enjoy a night out and return to the hostel without any stress (a big plus as a female solo traveler). The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful. The rooms were clean and comfortable, with daily cleaning and very cozy beds. The common area was stylish and welcoming, and they offer plenty of activities, tours, and food options.
Would definitely recommend this hostel !

Ps : there is also a sweet little kitty living next door. He’s super friendly so if you could give him some pets that would be awesome ! He doesn’t have teeth so can’t eat solid food but he would  absolutely love soft cat wet food ❤️

It's a safe and friendly place! It's also very clean! The location is perfect! I only have one complaint with my room door, the lock was a bit noisy, but it can be quickly fixed with a little adjustment.

Fantastic place to stay for a couple of nights. Your own room with a private bathroom and shower, fresh sheets everyday if you want them, the reception was very nice and helpful, you can even keep your bags there if you are checked out but exploring the city. Communal living area and kitchen were a great perk and more amenities than we expected. Location is fantastic.

“My stay at City Circus was made unforgettable thanks to the wonderful Greek receptionist. From the moment I walked in, they greeted me with a warm smile and made me feel right at home. They were incredibly helpful, offering great tips on local attractions and ensuring I had everything I needed throughout my stay. Their friendly and welcoming attitude truly enhanced my experience, and I would definitely stay here again because of the exceptional service. Highly recommended!"

I had the pleasure of staying at the Electra Palace Hotel in Athens for nearly a week, and I can’t recommend it enough! The hotel’s location is absolutely fantastic – both from my room and the rooftop restaurant, I had breathtaking views of the Acropolis, making every morning and evening unforgettable.

I was particularly impressed with the outstanding service. The staff were always friendly, professional, and incredibly attentive, which made the stay even more enjoyable. A special thanks to the concierge, who provided excellent tips and was always helpful in making sure I experienced the best of the city.

For those driving, the parking in the city center is a huge advantage. In Athens, this is not something you find easily, and the hotel made it super convenient to explore the city without any hassle.

Overall, it was a wonderful stay at an excellent hotel – I’ll definitely be back!”

I stayed at this property two different times for my trip.  The first time we found the rooms to not be very clean but we were exhausted and only here one night so we opted to just not make a big deal about it.  After traveling around the islands we came back for our last night.  The rooms were the same. NASTY! This time we complained.  The front desk acted like they didn't believe me.  I offered to go up and show them what I was talking about but no one seemed to care.  Instead they send housekeeping up to look around.  They also told me they arranged a transfer to the airport in the morning.  When we got down to check out no one arranged anything.  Pitiful customer service.  Would never stay here again.  I would however come eat at the rooftop restaurant. It is good. One last note. Since the ac only runs with the room key it took until 1am for the room to be cool.

Great place to go for a late night after drink. After having dinner downtown, we decided to walk up to the rooftop bar and check it out. The view was so awesome. We decided to stay for a nightcap. My wife had the port and I had the Blantons old-fashioned. Beautiful evening nice breeze great company. Highly recommend if you have a chance to go by.

This hotel has excellent location in the down town of Athens, close to all the shopping areas, historic places and restaurants. It has a beautiful roof top restaurant with views to Acropolis and very good quality food. I highly recommend this hotel for your stay in Athens.

We had dinner at the roof restaurant of the hotel. The food was exceptional the service was impecable and most importantly Lazaros our server was the best! Always with a smile he was so patient and suggested the best food and wine. Really made our evening!!! Thank you. We will definitely come back.

It was great the owner accommodated us with storing our luggage at the main office and the when we arrived to the apartment greater us there. Showed us around the place and the use of the home. From the Washing machine to the hot water for baths. We did report to the owner that the door nobs to several rooms had a issue of falling off or the latch spinning. They responded right away but we let them know that we can work with it until we leave and then they can come fix it for someone else. Over all it was a great place close to many tourists locations.

It was like staying at a 5-star hotel, but it was an entire apartment, walking distance from the Acropolis!

Akis went above and beyond for us. Everything was perfect. He was very kind, provided food, big TV with netflix in 5 different languages, big beds perfectly made, plenty of towels, air condition of course and the apartment was bigger than expected!

Couldn't recommend this place enough and even more so for the customer service. Thank you Aki, we will be back again!
Great location.

The apartment has an ideal location, convenient parking - there is a paid parking lot next to it, and free parking on the street. It is clean and very welcoming. The host is kind and responsive, he had left us breakfast in the fridge.

Excellent well-kept and clean apartment with every service, excellent location for visiting the city. Akis is a fantastic owner, kind and provided us with all the necessary information. I highly recommend it to everyone and if I were to return I would have no doubts. Thank you and congratulations again Akis 👍👍👍

I asked for a quiet room and was given a room that faced the street and was noisy until 2am.  Apparently this was the only room available, when I booked, although I was unaware of this.  Have specifically asked for a quiet room the hotel should have made me aware of the noise and given me the option to book elsewhere.  I was told to email my complaint in, which I did but no reply.  The room was expensive and, along with the poor customer service and disappointing breakfast, did not represent value for money.

What an incredible gem in the heart of Psiri in Athens. The hotel itself is clean and modern. Staff are excellent - helpful and knowledgeable. We have stayed twice and will continue to stay when we
come to Greece.

You will get some noise at night being so close to restaurants and bars but it is a trade off that is worth it as everything else about the hotel surpasses this small inconvenience.

Great Location, but Noisy Nights and Some Hygiene Concerns

The hotel's location was fantastic for feeling "part of it," right in the heart of the action, which made it convenient for exploring the city. The industrial decor gave the place a trendy and cool vibe.

However, I have to mention that the noise level during the night was quite disruptive. If you're a light sleeper or are trying to get rest during your stay, this might not be the ideal place for you. The constant noise (starting at about 5pm through 4am) made it challenging to relax in the hotel in the evening or get a good night's sleep, although the earplugs in the room were a nice touch and certainly helped. I am not sure what I would have done without my computer on full volume playing white noise though, as there was music from the bars below until about 3am and people talking/yelling in the streets until 4 or so both nights (even Sunday night!) We were on the second floor though, so perhaps higher up rooms don’t get quite as much of the noise.

The cleanliness of the hotel was satisfactory overall and the cleaning service was fantastic. However, one thing that bothered me was the open drain in the bathroom - right in front of the toilet there was an exposed drain that you could see the water run through when flushing the toilet, so it created a bit of a smell in the bathroom. It made me feel a bit uneasy about the hygiene standards.

Despite the noise and the hygiene concern, the hotel's location and trendy decor do have their appeal. Additionally, the staff was welcoming and helpful. Overall, I would recommend this hotel if you're willing to compromise on some comfort for a great location (if you are joining in on the bar fun) and a unique aesthetic experience. You also can't beat the price for a full service hotel so close to the city center.

Stats:
- Couple in their early 30s visiting from the US
- Stayed here in April 2024 over a weekend (Fri-Sun)

It does have the problem of noise from the bars up till 2-3AM. I took care of it with my noise canceling earbuds which were needed for the long flights anyway. Love this cozy hotel and genuinely nice staff.
Fantastic, incredible, clean, friendly stay in the heart of the city. Highly recommended. Top shelf. Purple ribbon.

The host was very sweet and nice! We stayed there for one night and we asked if we could check out a bit later because our flight was later in the afternoon. The host said yes without any hesitation and was very kind throughout our stay. The room itself was very modern and tidy but also spacious with a nice balcony, air conditioning and an inside the building small yard! We loved the decoration and the small details.

The room and the host were both amazing. The host was kind and made sure we were satisfied with our stay, even though we only spent one night there. The modern decoration of the house was beautiful and clean, and the location of the building was very convenient as it was near Ermou Street, making it easy for us to go shopping.

Amazing location! The house is so comfy , clean and spacious. The host is so nice, responsible and helpful.We asked if we can stay a little bit longer because our flight was late at night and she said yes without any hesitation. I definitely recommend it!!
excellent location close to acropolis. the apartment itself was specious,comfortable and clean!!the host was very polite with us and helpful.definitely recommend it!!

We stayed 6 nights. In Room 3, the biggest one.
Location is in the centre of old Athens, close to tourist attractions. Responsive host (Debi).
There are 5 rooms. 1-3 have street facing windows and balconies. 4-5 are at rear and face a small internal courtyard.
The room was clean, spacious and comfortable.
Noise from street (can hear people talking loudly at night) and music from downstairs (only occurred one night, but lasted until 3am) may be a problem.

Lovely apartment with extras we didnt expect. Tea and coffee and a few condiments were supplied . Close to cafes ,transport bars and shopping. It is on a busy street and  if you not used to Athens graffitti can be confronting.  Noise wasnt an real issue .. what do you expect in a entertainment area.  Booking and communication was a breeze.

I stayed only one night in this hotel and I wish it was longer! Super friendly staff, and a super cute and cozy room. Rooms have AC and a little fridge. For me the mattress was the perfect firmness. The location couldn't be more perfect either - walking distance to the Acropolis and a crazy amount of nearby cafes and restaurants. I would gladly stay here again when I come back to Athens in the future (because I will be back, what a gorgeous city)

Perfect location for exploring the historic sites of Athens. Our room was small but clean and comfortable. We especially loved sitting on our balcony and admiring the acropolis at night. It is close to lots of restaurants, shops, and metro stations for getting to the airport. We really enjoyed our stay!

Simple hotel perfect for visiting the Acropolis. Balcony overlooking church with bells ringing on Sunday morning. Good air-conditioning. Airy room. Charming lady on reception. Did not try breakfast. Lots of places near by for takeaway coffee, ice cream,  plus restaurants and snacks

Exactly what we were looking for- No more, no less.

My husband and I stayed here for a couple of nights during a trip to celebrate our 7th wedding anniversary. Athens was the first leg of a 3-destination trip, and it was the location we had the least amount of time in, so we wanted to hit the ground running! This hotel worked out absolutely perfectly for this.

The room itself is nice, nothing special. Small, but practical and clean. It had a nice-sized private balcony with a small table and two chairs, and we had a direct view of the Acropolis. After a full day of exploring it was nice to unwind on the balcony with some wine, look at some stars, and listen to the sounds of nightlife.

The main draw that led me to book this hotel was location, location, location! You could not ask for a better area to stay! Walking distance to everything you want and need. Close enough to some nice nightlife, but just far enough away to be able to retire to your room and not be bothered by unwanted outside noise. We ended up buying a 48hr pass for one of the 'hop-on hop-off' busses that stop around the major attractions of Athens, and the hotel was less than a 5min straight walk to one of the stops, so it made getting around the city extremely convenient.

Fancy? No. Extremely practical? Yes.
Overall, it was just what we needed.

Staff are super friendly. The location is perfect, you couldn’t get any closer to the main attractions in Athens. Bathroom is not attached to the room which can be quiet annoying. Otherwise, exactly what you pay for.
It is absolutely worth the price for the location and the acropolis of Athens views you get from the terrace.

What a beautiful place to stay!!! Superb location near to Acropolis, metro and in close proximity to all main attractions of the city.
Lots of cafes and restaurants in the neighborhood.
On arrival, the host Katerina, was friendly helpful and very welcoming. The apartment was comfortable, tastefully decorated and super clean. The best surprise was the rooftop. It offers breathtaking views of the Acropolis and across Athens.
Everything was great!
Would definitely recommend it and will visit again soon!!!

My stay at Theseum Hill has been an extremely positive  experience. Honestly, where to start? The hosts, Evan & Katerina are lovely people and most welcoming and helpful. They made the stay ever more comfortable. In addition to that, the apartment was very beautiful and squeaky clean. The decoration has a very nice aesthetic and cozy vibes. However, the terrace of the building was the biggest WOW factor for me! Acropolis, Lycabettus and Cine Thisio are all visible from the rooftop and you can actually even catch a free movie from the open air cinema! It's even better than the pictures!!!
All in all, if you're looking for a cute, cozy & comfy place to stay while visiting Athens, Theseum Hill is your best bet!!! Looking forward my next visit!

Great location, right in the center of Athens! A few meters from Acropolis hill (on foot) with wondeful view (from the terrace) to the Parthenon. The appartments are spacious, bright and clean. The owner is very helpful and kind. Totally recommend it!
Great place to stay, very good accommodation. And beutifull view to Acropolis from roof teras. Also from there you can watch movie from open cinema nex door
Very cool spot to book for a trip. Has a nice layout and you have the whole roof with a virtual 360 view of the city and Acropolis.

Very nice penthouse in an exciting location. Top view of Acropolis and the rest of Athens. Unfortunately, during our visit there were various defects such as roller blinds, showers or air conditioning. All the plants on the terrace were also dried out. But would still book again.
Incredible place with amazing decor and view, perfect for parties!
I spent 5 wonderful days
Fantastic loft

What you see isn't what you get. We paid 798€ for 3 nights , which is quite a lot of money. In the pictures, the place looked nice and clean, it really wasn't so nice, the photos were taken years ago, today the apartment needs painting, plus the smell of damp in 2 bedrooms and bathrooms, not recommended if you suffer from asthmaThe price is much too high for the place. I told them I was very disappointed, but had no answer from them. I think they don't care...

What an absolutely beautiful property! The apartments were modern, clean, spacious, perfect location and beautiful! It was absolutely perfect for our group of 10. Everything we wanted to visit and see was in walking distance. We were actually on the Acropolis sidewalk, but our location gave us peace from the busyness on the streets outside. We were a 5 minute walk to a great breakfast spot and street vendors. We were a 10-15 minute walk to Monastraski Square. Our host was amazing and accommodating. We had everything that we needed and could use WhatsApp to communicate if we needed anything (including recommendations on where to find delicious Loukamades)! ALA is part of the reason why our stay in Athens was absolutely amazing.

The apartments look exactly like the online pictures. There are 4 floors. The top floor bedroom host an outside deck that looks out over Athens and a patio on the Acropolis side with a hot tub. Beautiful! One wish was that the seating on the patio was clean and set up. We could say out with a glass of wine and admired the beauty of Athens. We did pay 110€ for airport transportation for a group of 10. It was worth it for us. The airport is about 45 minutes away.
Nice apartment. Friendly owner. Great view of the Acropolis from the terrace. Very central for the Acropolis and Plaka .
Lovely location. Nice apartment. Only too many levels.
Great service , views and extremely clean

The hotel was excellent in every aspect. I believe the hotel's location was one of the best areas, and with a little walk, you could see some of the most beautiful places, as well as experience the vibrant nightlife.
In terms of internet and, I have to say they were good. For breakfast, you had different options and fruits and everything was served neatly.
‏The staff's behavior was truly great, They also helped us to book a tour and were helpful and kind.

This hotel is UNSANITARY. my body (including face and neck) is covered with BED BUGS bites which are very painful and itchy.
The first room I was in had the worst shower head I've seen and in the morning I had to fight off a roach just to get in the shower. They moved me to a different room and there were the bed bugs.
The hotel never mentioned anything about sanitizing my things or offer any solution, they even suggested I might be lying (until they saw the bed bugs with their own eyes). Added pics and videos.

You have to drag your suitcases up a few flights of stairs and there's an elevator only between the 1st and 3rd floor. And there's no one to help. No kettle in the room as well. Those things just seem basic to me.

The only plus is the location.

Never had this kind of experience in any hotel. I feel bad for posting this but I feel it's more important that people will know before booking this hotel.

Fantastic hotel, really helped us when we had problems with our Airbnb.  Staff so friendly and helpful.  The room was conformable and had everything we needed.
The whole hotel was very clean and tidy.
The breakfast was lovely,...hot food, pastries, juices, hot drinks.
Hotel is close yo evening you would want to see.
Highly recommended 👌

We stayed here for a quick visit in Athens.  The location was really great, a short walk to the Plaka and Acropolis as well as Syntagma Square.
The room was a good size.  We were facing the main street, so there was a bit of noise from the traffic, but it quiets down eventually.  The bed was large and very comfortable.
Better than the location and comfortable bed was the staff.  I was trying to locate lost luggage and both Vassilis and Donna went above and beyond to help me.  They were just great.
The cost of the room was quite reasonable and it was nice that breakfast was included.  I would certainly stay again.

My family and I had a wonderful stay at the Acropolis Museum Boutique Hotel. While the hotel wasn't hip or modern (it feels like it was furnished by an eclectic antique shop,) it was very clean and comfortable feeling.

In response to many reviewers concerns about the noise, we didn't find it to be a problem. Yes you're on a heavily trafficked street, but there wasn't much honking, emergency vehicles, or loud people. It's not quiet, but hey you are staying in the middle of the city!

We really enjoyed staying in this neighborhood, as the side streets in the Koukaki area had great food options, and felt very local and not touristy.

It is important to note that there are a number of stairs you must climb before you're able to reach the elevator, making it a bit difficult for those with mobility issues, and those with lots of bags. You climb a large flight from the street to the lobby, along with a small flight from the lobby to the next floor until you reach an elevator. If you are able-bodied, this is no issue at all.

Overall we had a stress-free time, and felt like this was a good option for the money.
Very cozy apartment with a beautiful Acropolis view. The location is great, next to Monastriaki Square and the metro station. Highly recommend!

We had a great stay at Feel Athens Studio. Close to Monastiraki Square and many restaurants only a short walking distance. The amenities we great. The only thing that was missing was a toaster.
Great views of the Parthenon the Acropolis and Mount Lycabettus from our balcony. We stayed in the last week of January so it was quiet near our apartment. Very clean. Orestis and Katerina was quick to respond if there were any issues.
Better rates if you pay in cash. ;)

Aircon is terrible !
Bathroom sprays water everywhere!
Lift to get up dodgy

Very nice and cozy apartment in the historical center of Athens. Great location. Nice interior. Hospitable owner. I enjoyed the place.

A minus is that there is a little bit noisy sometimes. Near apartment vocal studio exists. And you can hear their exercises.
Magnificent view! The balconies are huge and the apartment was crystal clean! Last but not least the instructions given by the host were just on the spot for everything we needed!

Basic but reasonably priced and clean hotel in an excellent location. The room was small but had everything you need. Unfortunately our air conditioning did not work on the second night which made for a very uncomfortable night. The hotel were very apologetic though and offered to pay for our taxi to the airport to compensate which was very much appreciated. The staff were really friendly and I would definitely stay here again. There is also a nice little cafe opposite and a terrace upstairs, from which you can see the Acropolis.

This was perfect for us, easy access to all of the main sights in the centre of Athens. Room was not the biggest but was OK, bathroom was good. A very warm welcome and introduction to the hotel services and gaining entry through the front door. Good level of room cleaning on request.

I stayed in the hotel for 2 nights. It’s very simple, but super clean, staff is very nice and location is amazing. We were able to do all tourist attractions walking. The only fyi is to consider it has 3 floors and only stairs. It was not an issue for me and my family, but something to think about if you have reduced mobility. I’d stay again 100%. Thanks for the help calling the taxi to the airport!

We absolutely loved this location in Athens! The room was perfect for our one night, the air conditioning worked well and there was even a large balcony. The service we received was also excellent- the receptionist carried our luggage up the stairs for us- and it was fairly quiet considering we were in a busy city.

I don’t understand why they say one star hotel. More like three. Truly excellent location. Close neighbour of the Archbishop of Greece. Nice terrace on the top you can use. Very helpful staff. Good size room and big shower.

Great host, really helpful, cozy apartament, amazing communication, many shops in the neigbourghood and very close to the city center, for that price it is everything that you need. We enjoyed our stay very much. I recommend.

We had an outstanding experience staying in the Armoniko apartment! It was a short 5-days vacation for two in Greece. The Armoniko apartment is located within a 10-12 minutes walk distance from the ancient center, Parthenon, in a calm and very authentic district of Athens, with grocery stores, great restaurants, both modern and authentic Greek cuisines, and the major park areas close by. If you are into historical site-seeing, actually almost all the most important museums and historical places are located rather close. It is a nice, tidy, cozy, and a very romantic apartment for two, with all the necessary modern appliances and accessories included. Simone himself is a super Host! He is very friendly, helpful, and always keen on giving some great recommendations regarding places to go and sites to see in Athens. He made our stay indeed unforgettable and I can recommend him and the Armoniko apartment with no hesitation! We, my wife and I, will be happy to stay there next time we are in Athens.

I had a very warm welcome and a restful stay at Armoniko. The host Simone treated me like a family friend and helped me out with so many logistics. He really made my time in Athens run smoothly. He is a host that takes great pride in his work. The apartment had everything I needed - the bed was super comfy and the apartment was quiet and cosy. Highly recommended.

We were an extended family of 6 people, we stayed at some spectacular places in Greece - but staying at Simone's place was like coming home after a long day of sightseeing (Oh the hot showers were the best). The apartment is just walking distance from the Acropolis and Monastiraki. It is a also a very safe residential area (My brother looked like he wanted to settle down there).
The apartment is furnished with every amenity we would need and is a very cozy place.
More then anything else. Simone has such a nice personal touch - he is like Santa, you think of something you want, he gets it for you. Hand map of Athens - GRANTED.  Adaptor for our phones - GRANTED.  He even helped us out with cab bookings a week after we checked out. Rare to find such good-hearted folks these days.

Armoniko apartments it's the best place you can book for your stay in Atene! Close to the heart of the city, the apartment it's fantastic in every way: modern, very confortable and fully equipped! Simone is the best host ever, always smiling and ready to help! hightly recommend ;) I looking forward to come back soon!

A wonderful hotel.
The best location possible.
The rooms may not be large, but they are pleasant, clean, and have a great bathroom.
The staff is exceptionally courteous, service-oriented, and friendly.
And I almost forgot... an excellent breakfast.
A wholehearted recommendation for the hotel and its staff.
I have no doubt I will stay there again on my next visit.

Front desk staff was exceptionally kind and made sure we felt welcomed and appreciated. The room was small but cozy and well designed, with modern touches, and both the room and bathroom were very clean and comfortable. The breakfast spread was impressive, with hot options as well as cold ones (everything was so fresh!). The building is right downtown in the Plaka, with easy walking access to restaurants, shops, monuments... it was ideal! There's parking directly across the street, which is very handy. Overall, we had a wonderful time at the Sweet Home and would definitely return! Can't say enough good things about this lovely hotel.

An absolute treasure of a hotel right in the heart of the city. Despite its small size it had everything we needed, and we could walk to most sights in 10-15 minutes. I loved the decor of the hall and rooms, they brought a nice level of class to the whole stay. The breakfasts featured local ingredients and delicacies, and quickly became one of the highlights of our day. The staff was excellent and helpful at every turn, bringing it all together into the best hotel experience I ever had.

This hotel is incredible! The service was top notch, amazing breakfast, great location, quiet and clean rooms. We couldn't have asked for anything more. Will stay here again and recommend to anyone traveling to Athens. Alexia is the best!!

Easy access to everywhere! 20mins walk to Acropolis & many restaurants/cafes are around. My partner and I had 3 days trip to Athens and the receptionist recommended us for a ferry day tip to the Aegina island, wonderful experience we had!

All staffs were smiling throughout our stay. What we enjoyed the most during our stay was breakfast. Spinach pie, Greek yogurt with their homemade berries & cherries were amazing! It’s not a typical European breakfast. They had many homemade Greek style selections!

The room we stayed (304) was quite small as we had 02 luggages:( Besides that everything was good. I’d highly recommend this hotel✨

Fantastic location, close to the Acropolis and other Athens sites.
Breakfast was OK, the same each day but we only had 3 so no problem. Our room was 301 with no view, just a wall 3 metres away and next to the lift, although that didn't seem to be used much so no noise issues.
Staff were friendly and assisted with ordering a taxi for our transfer to the port
Would recommend for a cheap stay in a great location

Just the place. If you are looking for a hotel close to all the history and places to eat.
Staff were friendly and helpful.
Found out it was our anniversary and gave us a bottle of nice vine to enjoy.
Can not say enough nice things about them.

This hotel is great all around it near the acropolis it has great food and it’s very very clean.i liked the stuff there they where very nice and helpful also the view is nice for there.i would definitely recommend this hotel if you are looking of a nice and affordable place to stay in Greece 10/10

If you're looking for a clean and affordable place to stay close to the Acropolis, I think this is the hotel for you. It is clean, close to everything and covers all your needs at an affordable price.
Location is great, near to Acropolis. The price is quite price as for 2 star hotel, but the staff is very friendly and helpful.

We stayed in unit A for couple of days. Lovely apartment-spacious and clean, good A/C & WiFi, fully equipped kitchen. Good and fast communication with owner. Best location possible - close to everything and nice supermarket on corner of street.

My genuine advice would be to avoid this place.

Yes, the location is good. And to some extent we could have overlooked the ant infestation, the total lack of natural light, the shower that is totally unsuitable for a family of five due to the length of time the water takes to heat, and the angry neighbour who shouted and cried at us when we tried to use the advertised terrace. She told us that the owner had no right to be advertising this. We have no idea if this is right or wrong, but who wants a big argument on holiday!?

But the worst thing was the owner's reaction when we told her about all this on departure and suggested a small refund (there was no other suitable accommodation nearby to move to).You would have thought we had kicked a puppy!  No empathy, no professionalism and an attitude as if we had made it up!

Avoid.
Clean, well-kept, convenient (in the heart of Athens,) but without a window and with a guy in the adjacent gallery shouting and cursing every morning
Doll what an apartment

Conveniently located and no worries with luggage they will help you carry it. My room was right next to the shared kitchen which sucked cause the walls are definitely thin and you hear everything. If you can get a room away from the kitchen it may be quieter but expect to hear everybody’s business. Overall staff was helpful and friendly.

Great location since it is so close to all the restaurants and shops at Plaka, but it does have some drawbacks.

At first, we had a hard time finding it with google maps since Plaka is a cobblestone area with tight shops and buildings all around so it doesn't seem like your regular squared out streets with easily recognizable  landmarks.

There is no obvious entryway or signage (a plaque on the wall). There are 2 sets of french style wooden doors with doorbells and keypads attached. The first set leads to the reception area where you fill out a form, and they make a copy of your passport. Then they lead you back out to the second set of door where you use a pin to get inside to your actual rooms.

It is a modernized older building with many of its original charm so it does have air conditioning and a modern bathroom but be ready to walk up and down a spiraling three flights of stairs to your second floor room.

I wished I had booked their room with a balcony but don't remember if it was unavailable, or maybe I just wanted a cheaper room. I am paying less than $500 Euros for 3 nights and it includes all the free 2 liter water bottles you need (they have a small community kitchen where they have complimentary tea, coffee and water).

Our room is in the corner so we have two 6 feet tall french door style windows with original wood and hardware (so be gentle when closing them since both sides have to meet in the middle to close properly unlike newer styles where you close one first and the other side seals it or others where they close independently from each other). They meet in the middle, so one fits into the groove of the other, and then you twist the window handlebar to lock it.

The room key is an actual key that you have to use on the inside in order to lock your door (no twisting mechanism for easy locking). The advantage of this style of lock is not locking yourself out of your room with the keys left inside.

The doors of both the building and rooms are old, so it did feel a bit loose.

Since we are two old ladies, we couldn't possibly take our luggage up 3 flights of a spiral staircase so the two young gentlemen at the reception were very kind to help us. The tricky part is when we have to check out, I fear we might have to bother them again for help. I feel this type of lodging is best for younger people who travel light while staying to enjoy Plaka.

Air conditioning is a split system, and it is off, so turn it on with a high fan setting if you plan to rest within the hour of unpacking. You can always leave it off and just enjoy the fresh air by opening your windows, but be careful with the curtains on windy or rainy days.

The perk is being able to see the Acropolis right outside our window, and when you open your windows, you can smell all the delicious food from the restaurants downstairs.

I would definitely pick this place again if I were only packing a backpack or duffle bag while traveling with my siblings but unfortunately this time I have my mother who has arthritis so she did prefer a traditional hotel with elevators.

We recently had a marvelous stay at the Palladian Home Hotel in Athens. I must say we were pleasantly surprised. I would consider this a boutique hotel with just a handful of rooms. Very, very quant and it just added to our overall vacation experience. The only negative thing I can say is that our room was in the third floor (tight spiral staircase) and there are NO elevators, but even with that the staff helped us with our luggage coming and going. The staff was just outstanding and in particular, Panos. He was super friendly, accommodating and so very helpful.

Highly recommend this beautiful little gem, tucked away a stones throw from the Acropolis. Staff attentive, polite and nothing was too much bother. A great base for exploring all the local shops and for getting to the Acropolis early. Comfortable bed and a gorgeous shower.

Good location, the desire of the employees to provide service is good. Everything else is bad! The staff's professionalism in providing service is not good, 4 times we checked with the staff about ordering a taxi for a certain day and they said that everything is already arranged, a minute before you have to get into the taxi, the staff does not see the order, there is no order. under any criticism. The maintenance conditions are not good, when you take out the shower tube you cut your hand as you can see in the picture.

The good things about the hotel was the location & the staff.  The room was clean but very small & the bathroom was tiny.  It was almost impossible to shower!  They offer breakfast for a charge but felt it wasn't worth the money.  There is a wonderful bakery on the corner just steps from the hotel.  We enjoyed our stay in Athens but probably wouldn't stay at Athos Hotel on our next visit.

Do not believe in four star reviews for this hotel.  As a solo traveller I decided to stay at this hotel based on the reviews. While the location of the hotel is close to the central location. The beds were ridden with bed bugs and there were cockroaches under the bathroom sink. The bed sheets had stain. It was the most disgusting place I have ever stayed in. The hotel is located in a dark alley and it also took me a while to find it. The staff was most unwelcome. There were also few unwanted characters hanging around the place. A warning for solo women travellers pay a little bit more and be safe. It was also very noisey.

The highly overpriced hotel is run down boasting old and cheap furniture with a gloomy reception and even more gloomy breakfast room.

There is a major noisy construction worksite opposite the hotel which starts at 7am, of course the front desk “forgot” to tell us about when we made the reservation. The superior room which has a glimpse over the Acropolis looks like a cheap standard room in a 2 star motel with a tiny balcony facing the scaffolding.

The only positive comment about this place is its location.
Rooms are extremely small as is the bathroom, but who goes to Greece to sit in the room, Staff are awesome, so is the location . Has a good view from the roof top lounge area.

Small hotel in a quiet yet central location.  Easy access to transportation, restaurants and shopping. Very walkable to many major tourist sites. Staff are very customer focused with an easy approach.

A good value hotel just 8 mins flat walk from Monistiraki Metro station.  15 min walk to the restaurants in Plaka.  The staff are very friendly and helpful with airport and port transfers and directions.  We have stayed here twice over past 2 years and enjoy the breakfast: make your own fresh orange juice, eggs cooked to order, Greek yoghurt, Greek cakes and slices, a good selection of jams/spreads, cold cuts.  Vegetarian choices available.   For rooms on street side, they may have a great view of the Acropolis and get lots of natural light, but be prepared for some street noise from 9am till late due to Little Kook cafe across the street.  Consider booking a suite on the inside of the building.

Hotel was not bad, it fulfilled the requirements for couple of days. It was clean. But the bathroom lacked privacy. It had a glass window separator with blinds, that were half done, you could see everything from the room m. Well could be romantic for a couple but we were with a kid.

Small boutique hotel centrally located within walking distance of everything in downtown Athens.  Our room had an incredible view of the Parthenon.  The only bad side was the AC in our room was not functioning properly, but that was okay. Super friendly staff that make you feel more than welcome.

GREAT Hotel in the heart of Athens! FANTASTIC service, comfortable bed, Beautiful view of the Acropolis from their own rooftop patio. Also make sure you have their DELICIOUS breakfast, (which is included!)
Highly recommend Athens Losge to anyone!
Great location, close to the night life and restaurants. The rooms are spacious, and the staff is attentive. Breakfast could be improved. We had a good view of the acropolis from the room balcony.

I have no photos of the property but trust me - it looks exactly like the owners' photos!
The host and owner of the properties (hostel&apartments) is an AMAZING guy! Way beyond a host. He's a genuine person with heart of gold. He helped us with everything, and i mean *everything*. From finding our way around the city, to get city facilities, to food and entertainment recommendations.
Mike if you read this- we love you 😍.
The reception was at our service at all times and the crew was respectful and nice.
Now, the apartment we rented (2 double bedrooms - open plan) was huge, the beds are comfy, clean and smell fresh. Two towels (one big one small) per person served us right in our stay and we had the option to get clean ones from the reception. Bathroom was clean and fixed up. Kitchen has all you might need - refrigerator, plates, silverware,  glasses, soap and more. There's also a washing machine and a dry rack all u need is soap.
The apartment is modern and very stylish.
It is located in the middle of the city. 5min to Monastiraki Square, 7min to Parliament building, 20-25min to Acropolis entrance, 2min to restaurants, bars and nightlife.
You'll have no problem with street noise if you close the windows, but my suggestion is to join the noise and go out to enjoy the night city life! Amazing vibe!
Search for the local taverns!
The breakfast is served in a nearby restaurant called 'La Pizzetta', its a simple breakfast to fill you with energies for the rest of day - local pastries, boiled eggs, coffee, jam, honey, butter. Served at you table or takeaway.
All in all - great experience! Would stay there again and again.
Thank you for a fascinating experience 🥰
Management not attentive to customer requests
Nice
Excellent apartment with all the necessary amenities. The owners are very cordial.

First of all Miranda is a wonderfull warm woman. She waited for us upon arrival. The apartment is well equipped in a nice neighbourhood. Good airconditioning, silent at night en looks way better than in the pictures. Definitely recommend apartment!!! Book direct via mail.

Miranda’s apartment is cosy, comfortable and very well located. It’s walking distance to shops and restaurants. Miranda is the perfect host, she has thought of everything to ensure you have a comfortable stay. The apartment is well-equipped with every little thing you could need. Highly recommend and would definitely stay again!

The apartment is perfectly  cleaned, sheets and towels were smelling great  and is spacious ! mrs Miranda is the best host we have ever  met! Upon arrival we realized that the apartment had a welcome pack that was fabulous! Thank you so much !! We will be back for sure ! Value for money ! Absolutely we do recommend it ! 🙏🙏🙏
Lovely apartment in the heart of Athens. Extremely clean and well equipped. We really like it!
Excellent location, quiet building and neighbourhood, very clean and well equipped.

I truly loved staying at this place, located at a very centric area, close to most restaurants, bars and shopping places. Will definitely stay at their apartments whenever I visit again. The owners are super nice and very friendly. Thank you very much for your hospitality 🙏
Location is just perfect. A bit artistic decor. Very comfortable bed and sitting area. We stayed in apartment Niki. Communication with personnel was top notch.

So glad and fortunate to have found this place. Our original booking in Athens got cancelled last minute We were frantically searching and found this place available and booked it without much research, just going by the pictures posted. I have to say that was the best thing to happen and best way to start our Europe trip. GREAT location, walking distance from the major sight seeing spots. Great location for food, cafes. Extremely helpful and friendly host/manager Ms.Konstantina. A lovely apartment tastefully done. We adults and our kids loved everything about this place!

We had an amazing experience! The apartment is located down town, so we had access to all the bars and shops there.

I have to admit that the apartment was wonderfully decorated and in an excellent condition.

The host, Konstantina, is a lovely person. She helped us a lot and she suggested where to go and what to eat.

I will definitely come back next time I am going to visit Athens! I highly recommend it to everyone!

Great, very central location. Walking distance to everything. Perfect for 5 adults or 2 couples and some kids. Comfortable couch bed could reasonably sleep 2 (so 6 adults could stay comfortably if on a budget)

An excellent boutique located a short walk from all of the major tourist attractions and happening night life districts of Athens. The owner is quite helpful, his English is immaculate, and staff was willing to help us with anything we needed.

You do have to climb a flight of stairs before being able access the elevator, so people with mobility issues should take heed.

I would give the rooms a 4, but the sliding partition wall does not fully separate the two sleeping areas, this may have been a quirk of my room. The view from our terrace of the acropolis was a true highlight, both at night and in the morning.

Overall an excellent value and experience, will definitely stay here again.

We had a lovely stay at the Hellenic Hospitality House, albeit only one night. We stayed in the Zeus room as a couple, and it was HUGE! If it wasn’t one of the only rooms left we probably would have gotten a smaller room, but we loved being on the top deck and being able to see the Parthenon from the balcony. It’s a gorgeous apartment tucked in central Athens, and this room is perfect for a family who wants the ease of walkability around the city. Yes, as others have stated, there are two sets of stairs to walk up to the 1st floor, but there IS a small elevator to take to the 5th floor to check-in. If you’ve ever been to NYC or any other European country and stayed outside of a major hotel, you’ll be fine. If you have limitations with mobility, might not be a good fit.

We had a nice visit with the owner, Chris, who was pleasant and eager to make us feel welcome. We found him to be very reasonable—but it’s best to understand that he runs the operations independently, so he welcomes all guests individually that visit. That said, he has a family of his own to get home to, so best to reach out well in advance if you will arrive later than 7pm. Also, office opens at 10am, so dropping off bags earlier than that isn’t an option. But there are other options in the city to hold bags. We used Krassas Luggage storage around the corner for €5 per bag and it was perfect for us.

We enjoyed our stay and everything was beyond satisfactory! Wish we had more time!

Great experience. The rooms were very clean and comfortable and the location is unbeatable. Kristos, the owner, was very welcoming and helpful with advice, organising taxis, etc. I'd definitely stay there again.

Convenient location for the metro and very close to Acropolis. Has many food options and walking distance to local market, square, metro station, Acropolis, Flea market and roof top bars. Chris was very welcoming and provided many recommendations for first timers Greece travellers like ourselves to enjoy and explore. Clean and cozy rooms.
Great service. The guy who still owns it is very friendly. Straightforward and very central. Not all rooms come with an extravagant view. But the rooms are in top quality.

NEVER book this place! We booked an apartment 5 months prior to our arrival and paid for it in advance. ON THE DAY of our flight, while we're waiting at the airport with 3 kids, we get a cancellation from the place. When I called him to tell him we're already in the airport, he said that they double booked the room, and the guests that got there the day before, got our room. He said that Booking will find us an alternative solution... Don't book from someone you cannot trust. If they did it to us, they can do this to you... We had to book a place that is located 10km fron where we planned with all additional costs, and it seems that the owner care more about the reviews... You can see in the comments below that we were not the firat ones. NEVER book this place. Don't ruin your vacation.

The location is amazing!
Service was good but we couldn't open the door for a while and had to wait outside, it seems this was an error out of the owners control but instructions were unclear.
Rooms are nice, we stayed at 0B. A bit of mold in the bathroom, otherwise good. Noise is a big issue, creepy door from the bedroon opens to a basement, we could hear bats and every toilet flush in the complex.

Despite its central location, this apartment rental fell short due to misleading photos, management obsessive with written guidelines, subpar renovations, and a lackluster neighborhood ambiance. Choosing this space turned out to be a regrettable decision.

Excellent central location.  Renovated apartment clean new furniture , Bed and sheets clean.  Excellent air conditioners - we were on cold days and we had a warm and cozy apartment.  The apartment is in a beautiful building.  There are more apartments in the building we were on the first floor - it is very comfortable with luggage.  For Jewish travelers, Beit habd  is really close by, 3 minutes on foot.  The advantage of the location is that it is in the center but it is a very quiet street.  The owner is an amazing person, kind and caring.  Every problem we had he was available and dealt with quickly.  I also forgot an expensive object in the apartment and he made every effort to make sure it came back to me.  In conclusion .  Highly recommend staying in these apartments.
Beautiful house, amazing people, I had a great time here.

The location is unbeatable. Room is clean and nice. Has a very nice view of the Acropolis. Could be a little bit noisy at night, but I slept just fine. Like other reviewers said, there is some issue with the wifi. You may need to forget the network and then log in again for it to work.

We stayed in Athens for 3 days before getting on a cruise. We were pleasantly surprised by Central Stay! I did not quite realize when I booked that it was not a hotel and rather a condo/AirBNB situation but it was extremely spacious complete with a kitchen. A cleaning lady came in during our stay and cleaned, provided new towels. The location was absolutely perfect. We couldn’t have asked for a better, more central location in Psyri. The street we were on did have a few tourist traps but also some gems. But right off the main street there was some wonderful, authentic food. Very safe at night as well. As some mentioned, do prepare for it to be very loud at night. People are out drinking and partying till roughly 2am and the music is loud. They provide earplugs but we had our own from the plane and they did help. If you are not used to music/a loud environment, this stay might not be for you. We decided that the location for how short we were here was 100% worth it.

Nice rooms with great amenities in the center of Spiri, in a street full of restaurants and coffee shops.

It is a lively environment but it means that it is impossible to open the window or sit on the small balcony because of the niose and the strong smell of cooking.
The windows are good in silencing the noise, but you might need to use the ear plugs supplied, especially in weekends.

Beside that, the internet worked fine in times, but in other time it didn't (there was wifi, but no connection to the internet).

Location is perfect. Rooftop is across Akropolis and view was amazing especially at night with the lights. The room was clean also staff was very kind and helpful. Only problem was apartment is in the middle of the bars and caffes, so there is too much noisy. İf you are sensitive about noise , it can be annoying.

Clean and well equipped apartment, the room has a streetview, no Acropolis. It’s not a hotel in the proper sense of the word, no reception. Has a kitchen with everything, dishes, microwave, coffee machine etc. In the room iron, safe, tv. Bathroom is modern. In the building there is a laundry, roof terrace with magnificent view. Not recommeded for people looking for a quiet place, as it is located in a street full of bars with live music till 2am. Party goers would enjoy it!

I stayed at 14 Reasons Why for a business trip, and it was fantastic! The central location was convenient, yet the atmosphere provided quiet spaces to focus and work. The design with Seletti pieces is stunning, and the facilities are top-notch with lovely toiletries. The breakfast feels like a brunch-level restaurant, offering a variety of dishes and unlimited drinks. I loved my stay and would definitely recommend it!

Amazing location, walking distance from all the main sights. The room is modern and clean. The bed and pillows are super comfortable! Nice little balcony outside as well. Breakfast had great food and coffee. The staff is also kind and helpful. 10/10 would stay again!

My friend and I stayed at 14 reasons why for a reunion trip- I can’t recommend this place enough. The staff is lovely, the rooms are pristine, and it’s in an AMAZING location, which is walking distance from all of the main sites. To top it off, if you are a guest of the hotel, you get a gourmet breakfast included from the brunch spot downstairs! I am not one to leave reviews but this hotel is amazing and I wanted to give them all the credit that they deserve.

Easy to find and very close to the metro and a ton of restaurants and bars, easy walking to shops and attractions.

Room was well appointed and comfy for the price, air con was brilliant and the courtyard a nice bonus. Storage space was limited so wouldn't want to stay more than a couple of nights.

Staff were great and let us have our room at 10am as it was free, very accommodating.

I stayed here 3 years ago & came back purely for the best morning coffee made by Vaslis who I was so pleased still works here!

The staff are welcoming & friendly & the location I feel is in the heart of the city.

For me the rooms are fine, but I like simplicity as I prefer to explore rather than stay inside :)

Highly recommend! Also reconfirming it’s still the best coffee in Athens 3 years later!
There is everything you need in the apartment. The location is also quite central.
Great location! Great service! Very nice apartment.

Not a great experience and the flat certainly needs an upgrade, desperately...from the walls to the furniture. The place has an unrealised potential. I liked the balcony, but then there was water dripping from the ac and slippery tiles were all wet. The flat has two toilets and only 1 bathroom. Not great for such a spacious apartment. We stayed in a group of 11 people and we only had one pair of keys. I would suggest the owner changes the name from "beautiful" to basic homes. The good point was the location: walking distance to the Centre and excellent dining options all around. Overall I would not recommend.

We have booked this aparment via booking.com several weeks ago. I really understand that all prices went up due to war in Ukraine. If the price was deemed too low, it could be subject to further negotiation. But the owners did something absolutely unacceptable and refused to let us in on the day of our arrival! I suspect that this was due to reason that they booked it outside booking.com to a third party for a higher price. They only said over the intercom downstairs "Apartment is under renovation, goodbye" and we were suddenly forced to find 3 nights accomodation for 8 people and a baby. To add insult to the injury they informed booking.com that they cancel our stay because we did not check in!!! Combined with the reviews below about the standard, I really discourage everyone from staying here.

The Place doesn't even deserve 1 star but I can not give 0 stars. It was a total desaster. I travel much and I booked many accomodations with booking.com but it was the first time that I had a situation like this. I booked a stay for 3 nights in Athens in Veikou Beautiful Homes via booking in February. The booking succeeded and I even had a contact with the owner recording airport transfer. Everything seemed to be normal. At the day of our arrival i wrote her a message from the airport via booking that we will be there in about 30 mins. Ok asked how we can get to the apartment. She didnt reply. We took a taxi to the location. And I couldn't believe my eyes and ears. The location was closed. I called her and first she didn't pick up and then she picked up and said "We are having a renovation. Hotel is closed". And she hang up 🙈 I tried to call her many times but she didn't pick up. Then I asked her Greek  neighbor to call her from the Greek number. She picked up only for a moment and said something in Greek that I didn't understand. Then she cancelled our booking in booking.com and said the reason was we didn't show up 🤯🤯🙈🙈🙈 I never saw something like this. Such an ignorance. Probably there was no renovation because it didn't looked like this. I guess she just got different clients who probably paid more. No idea. But I was traveling with my whole family. 9 people and  (among the others 1 year old baby,one ill person and two seniors).  It was already late evening. We were hungry, with all baggages, and with no place to live the next 3 days. I was searching quickly for some apartment or suite or some hotel to stay in this area (because it was very good located, close to Acropolis. That's why I booked it). But everything was already booked or too small for our group or just too expensive to book it in the very last moment.

Please people - DON'T BOOK THIS PLACE!!!

THEY ARE CHEATING

FINGER WEG!!!

TRZYMAĆ SIĘ Z DALEKA!!! OSZUŚCI!!!!

DO NOT BOOK HERE
We (4 friends) booked here 2 months before our vacation (Booking.com) The booking said "Confirmed" so no problem, right? 2 days before arriving I messaged the owner to let them know about our arrival time - no response. At the airport of departure I messaged them again - no response. Then I found another review here on Maps about the same place, saying they cancelled lat minute (literally when they were already there outside the door). Same thing: no communication whatsoever. With that review we all tried calling as soon as we landed. The first time the call got through and rang 5 times before being sent to voicemail, then for all other calls, as soon as it rang they sent us to voicemail. At the 9th or 10th call they did answer and all they said was THAT THE APARTMENT DOESN'T EXIST ANYMORE. To not go there, and that it is a problem we should solve with Booking.com.
So we were left at the airport with no confirmed booking for our group 🙃

Aaaaand of course so far they haven't cancelled the booking, so on Booking.com it shows like if we want to cancel it they'll charge us €100+. Already requested via the site for the owner to waive the fees, will update if successful.

Do not book here for any reason, they don't answer, do not let you know about anything, do not even pick up the phone. Would be 0 stars if possible.

As always, Veikou Beautiful Homes is the perfect place to stay, or start the trip to the islands, with its spacious two apartments, quietly and centrally located close to the ancient town. Super clean and a lot of space at low cost. Top.

The hotel is centrally located and about 5-10 minute walk to all of the Athens sites.  We felt safe walking the area at all hours.  The inside was nice with different areas to sit and relax. The morning breakfast was superb.  Lots of options and it was delicious.  The rooms and bathrooms were small but expected as most European hotels.  The beds were very comfortable.  The rooftop restaurant was great with wonderful views of the acropolis.

The location is perfect for shopping and restaurants near by. The room was nice but very small. The breakfast was good except for the scrambled eggs were mushy and half raw and same with the bacon. They should have a microwave so people can use it to cook the scrambled eggs more and bacon, the poached eggs (fried eggs) were perfectly delicious. Very clean Hotel

Central Hotel is located in the Plaka neighborhood in Athens. The rooms were very comfortable and the staff was very helpful and kind. The breakfast was very good, and the rooftop bar/restaurant was spectacular.  It's view of the Acropolis was amazing.

I am afraid price quality is not right here. Location is perfect for sure. Superior room so small. Be careful big school next door where children take a break every 15 minutes starting at 08.00am. Can get very loud during day. No other TV than Greek TV. Breakfast mediocre.

The hotel is located within walking distance of every attraction and entertainment area which is excellent, the rooms are not large but adequate, when the window is closed the noise level is very, very low and does not disturb sleep at all, the breakfast is basic and sufficient, the hotel has paid or free parking and also late check-out is pre-approved depending on the type of room  Those who choose should pay attention to the fine print when booking the room, we got a room with a balcony that was listed on the website as having a view but the view was a cluster of very old buildings and not beyond.  The restaurent on the roof of the hotel  has view of the Acropolis giving a pleasant atmosphere to sit down for dinner or even just to have a drink, recommended.

It can’t get any better than this. The view alone is worth staying here. Add to that elegant rooms and common areas, attentive staff and delicious food and you have the perfect mix. It is close to everything. Rooms are immaculate. The gym is small but, from what I can see, the hotel doesn’t target that clientele so it’s ok .. I would definitely recommend and come back here!

Everything is extraordinary about this historic hotel in the heart of Athens, near Syntagma Square, just opposite the Greek Parliament. Even if you don’t (choose to) stay there, you should visit the Spa, which offers wonderful treatments in a calm, cosy, yet luxurious atmosphere. A brunch or an afternoon tea at the Winter Lounge complete the pleasurable experience. And of course there is the Roof Garden restaurant with a breathtaking view.

Beautiful hotel with nice facilities and spa area. My gripes are:
(1) the service wasn’t as refined as I would hope for a hotel of this class. Not much greeting or going out of their way for guests.
(2) my wife’s spa experience was sub standard. Her back was bruised and sensitive for days after a massage, and her hair blowout didn’t turn out great compared to spas back home.

I rarely write reviews, but felt compelled to as the front desk staff really need to up their game. My partner and I stayed for a night recently and upon arrival the first question the receptionist asked was what time we were checking out the next day. I was slightly thrown by this but didn't take offence though am increasingly irritated that high end hotels expect an 11am check out, and not midday. But more on this later.

Our flight had been delayed so we arrived at the hotel at about 3pm hungry for lunch. We asked the receptionist where we might eat and she said that the restaurant on the 8th floor was full. We said OK, but can we maybe get a snack at the pool bar. She also said that wasn't possible. Rather puzzled we went to our room and then decided to explore. Upon entering the pool area we saw an operational kitchen and were greeted by friendly staff and duly had a pleasant bite to eat, confused that the receptionist had tried to put us off going!

The next morning at 10.30am as we were getting ready to leave our room there was a ring on the doorbell obviously to chivy us along. But not only that a few minutes later the phone also rang (with nobody at the other end when I picked up). I could understand it if it was after 11am but I really take issue with this sort of behaviour in any hotel let alone one that is notionally the best in Athens.

As a hotel it's OK, nothing particularly special. We moved onto the Four Seasons on the coast which is in a completely different league and I would thoroughly recommend.

Old world elegance with modern luxury service. The guest rooms have maintained their old school appointments, but the upstairs lounge and bar are very much updated with modern decor. The rooftop bar/restaurant is definitely the highlight, although evening cocktails are 24 euro, so you're paying for the view and ambiance.  The breakfast is 5 star in every way; we booked a rate that included breakfast and it was definitely worth it.

Clean and nice rooms. Great breakfast room with plenty of hot and cold food options. Located just meters from the main road and many restaurants and shops close by. Old churches and infrastructure in the area as well, so don't have to walk far to get a taste of the sites and experiences. Be prepared to wait for elevators at peak times.

The location is excellent next to the most busy area of city centre of Athens. It is an old hotel but it is clean. They made some improvements the bed and all the furniture are new. The bathroom was very small even for a solo traveler.
A nice hotel walking distance from the Acropolis and a large park.  The room was small by American standards but clean and functional.   A very nice breakfast buffet.
Love this hotel I have been staying  here for years. Convenient to  everything. Clean,breakfast is good.
Decent rooms. Decent breakfast. Staff really friendly. The rooftop garden is brilliant, take your own drinks and a picnic

Service was impeccable, rooms were clean and beautiful. We stayed in a temple of Zeus view room. We wished that the balcony had some furniture, but other than that, our stay was truly amazing. The FOOD is so SO incredible!!!!! Breakfast buffet was to die for and we had a dinner as well which was just amazing.
The hotel is also within a 5 minute walk of the Acropolis and Acropolis museum, along with many shops and restaurants. we will definitely return next time we are in Athens. Thank you for a lovely stay!

First place visited off the plane and it was wonderful.

The staff is amazing.  One of our group was missing luggage, so we congregated on the rooftop bar to commiserate, and after some beer and ouzo and amazing views of the Acropolis, all our worries were far away.

I can't say enough how wonderful and accommodating the staff is.  (I would say 5 stars ++)

Breakfast was delicious with a wide range of options.  Eaten in the rooftop patio...again, what a view!

Perfectly located for exploring.  The Acropolis is near as is the Parliament, shops and other landmarks.

It was a wonderful stay

I recently stayed at this fantastic hotel in the heart of Athens, and it exceeded all my expectations. The highlight of my stay was the breathtaking view of the Acropolis, which could be enjoyed right from the breakfast restaurant. It was truly a sight to behold, especially at night when the Acropolis was beautifully illuminated.

The rooms were incredibly comfortable, spacious, and well decorated. The bed was plush and provided a perfect night's sleep, and the room was equipped with all the modern amenities one could need. The attention to detail in the room's design and cleanliness was good.

Breakfast at the hotel was another standout feature. Served in a charming dining area with equally stunning views of the Acropolis, the breakfast buffet offered a wide variety of delicious options, from fresh fruits and pastries to traditional Greek dishes. The quality of the food was excellent, and the staff were attentive and friendly, ensuring a delightful start to each day.

The hotel's central location made it incredibly convenient for exploring Athens. Major attractions, shops, and restaurants were all within walking distance, allowing me to make the most of my time in this vibrant city.

Overall, my experience at this hotel was exceptional. The combination of the prime location, comfortable rooms, spectacular views, and outstanding breakfast made it a memorable stay. I would highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Athens.

Absolutely top notch from the very beginning. Front desk staff helped us sort out a crooked cab driver. Rooms and location were great. Free breakfast at the roof top lounge has some amazing views. Would 100% recommend to anyone.

Book the rooftop restaurant for dinner!!! Please pleeee please do this as soon as you make a reservation here. Having dinner under the acropolis was our highlight. Not to mention, the food was tasty!! So worth it.

We loved how close it was to everything. Literally everything. You could walk to all the things you needed to see in Athens and you were also close to the train station.

The hotel felt very safe, so did the area.

The staff were amazing. We had the nicest girl
Greet us and the nicest man say good bye to us when we left. They gave us lots of tips not just about Athens,  but for the rest of our trip around the island.

The room was  clean and exactly what we needed.

We were recommend this by a friend and we will keep recommending to more friends.

We stayed here end of August 2024.

Great hotel! We really enjoyed our time here. Our room was large and the balcony was also a good size - it included 2 chairs and a table. The air conditioning worked well, thankfully, as it was very hot when we were there. The location was very convenient - plenty of nearby restaurants. We loved having a nice cocktail at the rooftop bar - beautiful views! Hotel staff were welcoming and very helpful. I highly recommend staying here!

We stayed 3 nights at New Hotel as we explored the city of Athens. It was a wonderful experience. The staff was friendly and helpful. The rooms were beautiful and very clean. I loved the decor of the hotel, the use of found pieces of furniture, cabinetry and wood on the walls was something that I had never seen before and it was so interesting and really pretty.
