The breakfast was wonderful and we had drinks on the rooftop bar overlooking the acropolis, a truly special experience. The location of the hotel was great for seeing the sights as well.

We have stayed at the New Hotel the last several years when we have been in Athens.  Convenient location with clean and comfortable rooms.  The staff is extremely friendly and accommodating.  We always sample the rooftop bar and they always have interesting concoctions.  Plan to come back next year

Fantastic experience at the best location walking distance to everything in Athens at a much better price point than the bougier options closer to Parliament. Staff was beyond accommodating - we stayed at beginning and end of our Greek trip, leaving some bags with the hotel, they were great with local info (needed a haircut and some car service to the airport - they came through with both on short notice), and the rooftop bar restaurant craft cocktails and food were great. Highly recommend!

I loved the design of this hotel. Our room had a great view and balcony. The breakfast was also very delicious. It’s very close to the acropolis and the surrounding area is very beautiful and fun to walk around. We had a great time!

We stayed 2 nights and absolutely loved it.
They arranged pickup at the airport, and it was a seamless drive to the hotel.
A very friendly, easy check in, where we could decompress after our long journey.
Very close to all the attractions, a wonderful outdoor area, very comfortable bed, and lots of cats nearby outside.
And we had the same friendly driver back to the airport!

My husband and I stayed at the AVA HOTEL Athens, Greece - Wonderful Experience!!!!
The Ava Hotel was conveniently located to ALL Attractions.  The room was well equip with water and safe deposit box.  The impeccable service and hospitality of ALL the staff Mario, Penny, Magda to include but not limited to House Keeping made our stay most enjoyable/ pleasurable and delightful.  Furthermore, the breakfast  was scrumptious.  We were very impressed by the responsiveness of the hotel staff, who were extremely friendly, professional, and  very pleasant.  From start to finish, they were very attentive.  Don't look any further if you're going to be in  the City of  Athens  the AVA HOTEL is the place to be close to ALL Attractions within walking distance. The views to the Acropolis are amazing.
Very respectfully
From the Sunshine State of Florida
N/F

I can't say enough good things about this hotel! The service was outstanding. They were the most accommodating group of professionals we have ever had. We had chosen the studio on the top floor which has a huge open deck with panoramic views of the city and the Acropolis.  It was just what we wanted. We were the only ppl on that floor and it had everything we needed. A small frig, coffee and tea service,  free water, a comfy bed, room darkening shades with a tiny bath. We chose to include breakfast which was a feast each morning with the best fresh squeezed orange juice ever. The location of this hotel was perfect. We could walk to most everything we wanted to do. We were on the edge of the old city and steps from the Acropolis. They accommodated all our requests for cabs when needed as well as early and late check in and check out. It is a small boutique hotel but big on service and quality!

Absolutely lovely hotel. Our room was large and they even had two roll away beds already set-up in the living room for my children as we checked in. Room was spotless, well furnished, and beautiful. We had a huge balcony that we sat out on each night to watch the city lights and relax. Perfect location to get to all the sites we wanted to see, lots of shops and restaurants all around. The staff was so helpful and friendly and we all enjoyed the breakfast every morning. One morning our tour left really early and the staff kindly prepared to go bags of sandwiches, fruit and cheese so we had something to eat. Would definitely stay here again and can't recommend it enough.

We recently enjoyed an amazing three-night stay at this Athens hotel. The staff's friendliness and constant smiles created a welcoming atmosphere for us. The central location made exploring Athens effortless, with major sights like the acropolis within easy reach, which we could literally see from our balcony.

The rooms were quiet, clean, and attentively cared for, providing a peaceful retreat after a day of sightseeing. Despite its central location, the hotel maintained a serene ambiance, ensuring a restful night's sleep.

Overall, this hotel exceeded my expectations in service, location, and comfort. I highly recommend it for a memorable stay in Athens!

I got my personal room and bathroom here. No issues at all. Location is excellent, all the top 10 sites are within a 30 minute walk. Streets were clean and safe, and some great food and bars options nearby. The rooftop bar has a truly exceptional view and friendly service. Would recommend to other friends.

Unfortunately I can't give 5 stars because the bathroom always flooded after people had a shower. But the position is excellent, the staff is very nice and friendly and the rooftop offers you an amazing view of the acropolis and the other archeological sites. It also has a bar with music where you can meet and talk to people at night.

We stayed in a private Acropolis view room. The room itself and balcony were very nice. However, the washroom was on the smaller side and the mattress was pretty awful. It felt like sleeping on a few old springs. Staff were all very friendly and approachable with answers to all our questions. The location is great. It is in proximity to all popular tourist attractions and the Metro. We opted for the breakfast package with our stay and it was the only regret we have. It was pretty terrible. Other than the breakfast and mattress, we thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
Perfect location. Quite area however there’s no soundproof in my private room and could hear my neighbours easily. Bed was very cozy. Water pressure and temperature was excellent.
It is a hostel but we stayed in a separate 2 people room. Acropolis view from the room was just amazing. You have a terrace in your room. Maybe one of the best views in Athens 🤩

I had a pleasant stay at this hotel, and there are several positive aspects to highlight.

Firstly, the location is excellent—it’s close to many tourist spots, which is a big plus for those looking to explore the city. The rooftop is really nice, offering a cozy view where you can even see the Acropolis, making it a perfect spot to enjoy the sunset. The hotel itself is nice, with well-distributed facilities and a decent breakfast. I also appreciated having access to the fridge, which came in handy.

However, there were a few issues that affected my overall experience. On the day of checkout, I was leaving early at 6:30 AM, but the reception was closed, and there was no staff available to assist. I wasn’t able to retrieve my food from the fridge, which I had prepared for my travel day, and the same thing happened to two other guests heading to the airport. There should definitely be someone available for early checkouts, even if it’s just for small tasks like greeting or farewelling guests.

Another minor inconvenience was the towel fee—while it’s common for hostels to provide towels for free, the 2 euro charge wasn’t too bad, but still, it’s worth mentioning.

Lastly, I noticed that the bedsheets had an unpleasant smell, like they were damp or not properly washed. I had the same sheets for three days, and the smell was quite strong. I recommend checking the linens carefully before using them for guests to ensure they are fresh.

Overall, it’s a good place to stay, especially for solo travelers looking to meet new people. The hotel offers a friendly atmosphere, and the breakfast is definitely worth it. However, some small improvements could make the experience even better.

Great location, my room was clean and secure, decent breakfast. As far as amenities, I've stayed in much nicer hostels, there's hardly any common area, and the best feature of the hostel, the rooftop, doesn't open till 6pm. Maintenance and cleanliness/supplies in bathrooms could be improved. But if you're looking for a cheap, safe stay near the Acropolis, a quick walk from the Metro, this is a good option.

This hostel has a great location to public transport,  attractions and restaurants. The rooms were clean and a good size with locker space for backpacks. Beds were comfy and had a night stand with plug. The AC was amazing in the heat aswell. Staff were polite but not overly warm. Breakfast was basic but substantial. They did allow us to leave our bags at reception prior to check in which was helpful.  Overall would recommend.

Stayed here in late May 2024.
Location is perfect for accessing the metro (Akropoli station), Acropolis Museum, and majority of the ruins (Acropolis, both Agoras, Hadrian's library, etc - buy the multi access ticket from the Hellenic Heritage website). A lot of food nearby and the Plaka neighborhood is not far for touristy shopping and more food.
Rooms are an okay size, lockers roomy enough for a backpack, BYO lock but also they sell them for a high price in the lobby vending machine. Multiple people's luggage cases can take up all the floor space. Bedding is not great and there were stains on the blankets. Bathrooms were clean but they do not clean out past guests' toiletries that were left behind, so it piles up, and they desperately need better ventilation and some towel racks in there.
Some staff are nice and helpful, some staff are incredibly rude. One person said we could fill our water bottles in the breakfast area, another yelled at us for trying to do it before the breakfast area was 'open.' No clear policy it seems. Breakfast is okay, more of a continental / UK breakfast fusion style.
The lobby is also the breakfast area and it's gated off at night so if you are trying to work remotely, there is virtually nowhere to sit. There is a rooftop bar with a sem-decent view of the Acropolis but the cheapest and most uncomfortable plastic chairs.
Okay for a one or two night stay/budget traveler, otherwise try one of the small boutique hotels in the neighborhood.

Location is great. The night time desk worker is creepy and mean. Made me wait outside for my 3am cab and there was a group of men being rowdy outside. Didn’t make me feel safe. Esp when I could have waited inside the seated area.  The linens smelled like cat pee. The location is great tho. And for what it is, it’s not bad but don’t expect great service.

Loved the hotel. Very good location, close to tourist attractions as well as the metro. Staff were very friendly and welcoming. Breakfast had a lot of choices and the food quality was great! The room was clean and modern (a bit small though + the view wasn’t great, which is expected due to it’s location). I saw a comment here about the AC temperature - in my case, it was working perfectly fine and was able to set the temperature without restrictions. Internet speed and connection was great.
Can definitely recommend it!

The hotel does not let you set the temperature of the air conditioning in the room. It is set to 27c (80.6f) degrees!

Other than the hot rooms, the hotel is very nice with modern touches. The breakfast in the morning is great. There is some street and bar noise outside at night due to the location.

The location is perfect. I was easily able to walk everywhere I wanted with most points of interest less than 30 min away.

It was quiet at night allowing me to sleep, I did have an inner room.  My room was right in front of elevators and that sound in the mornings does come through easily.

Breakfast had a variety of food and drinks.

The staff were friendly and quickly replied to any questions I had.

The room and restroom were big for Europe standards. If I ever got to come back to Athens I’d stay here again.

We visited the roof bar of the hotel , it had a breathtaking view
The breakfast was rich and delicious
We thank Michael for everything!
Gypsies passed by our table at the lobby bar and tried to steel our iPhones in the morning and he stopped them.
Michael, thank you so much for everything you were very professional and helpful in everything we asked you for.
Till next year, my American friend !

The good - location was excellent and the 7th floor sky bar makes this hotel worth staying at.

The rooms are dated and sparse, the thermostats are locked, and we had to wait over an hour after check-in time for our room to be ready.

Overall not a horrible place but a little disappointed with the experience. You can't beat the location and the bar on top which was a five star experience.

Truly in the heart of Athens, just blocks from the Acropolis. Our room was spacious considering the size of the hotel, and the service has been outstanding! We arrived well before check in and the hotel staff did what they could to get us in early (our flight landed nearly an hour ahead of schedule). From that point on, the front desk staff attended to all of our needs. This excellent level of service is especially meaningful, as we are on the first leg of our honeymoon. I highly recommend this hotel for its location, quality and service, hope to stay here again on our next visit to Athens!

Hotel is in a great location convenient for the Acropolis site and Acropolis museum, but in a relatively quiet location. Many good bars and restaurants close by. Rooms are comfortable with a high standard of housekeeping. Great lounge/bar area on the ground floor and fabulous roof terrace bar and restaurant with great views of the Acropolis. Service was friendly and extremely helpful.

My husband and I had the pleasure of enjoying The Herodion Hotel on our recent trip to Athens. We had never been to the city before and were spending a short time there before departing for a cruise. Our decision to stay here was based on reviews online and we could not be more impressed. My husband and could not stop singing the Herodions praises, I lost count of how many times he would just sit back and say what a great choice the hotel had been and how much he enjoyed everything about it.
The location could not have been better. A short walk from the Acropolis, located in a quiet area of a vast city just bordering the historic Plaka neighborhood. Metro was located nearby making access to other districts simple.
The hotels rooms were absolutely pristine, with a wonderful shower and comfortable bed. We had the pleasure of booking a room with a view of the Acropolis and enjoyed morning coffees on our balcony. We used the hotels service to book transportation from airport to hotel and arrived before check in but they staff was wonderful. From the moment we arrived and had a member of staff rush down to the street to help with our luggage, to the front desk securing our bags while we took lunch and explored the area prior to check in.
We were told we could also utilize the hot tubs (which have fantastic views as well) even before our room was ready if we liked.
We had lunch in the conservatory room which was delicious and dinner at the rooftop restaurant which was probably the most delicious food I had on my vacation.
When we left the hotel we were able to once again enjoy the ease of having the welcoming staff arrange our transport to the Port.
We can not recommend this Hotel more, from cleanliness to hospitality there was no fault to be seen and we plan to stay here on any future stays as well as recommend it to family and friends.

We had a great stay at this hotel. Very friendly and welcoming staff. The room we had was great and could fit a crib in for our little one. Easy walk to Acropolis (5mins) and plenty of restaurants and bars close by. They also offer a great breakfast in the morning. Would definitely stay here again.

The hotel staff have been wonderful the entire stay. Every staff member is warm and exceptionally friendly. No task is too difficult and every request is met with a smile. The hotel restaurant on the roof top has phenomenal views of the Acropolis, with a spacious rooftop bar and restaurant. Our party of 4 enjoyed gourmet food, with each dish looking more like art than the last. The rooms are stylish and well maintained. We would highly recommend the Herodion hotel, we could not fault it. Overall, an exceptional experience.

Super convenient location. Walking distance to the Parthenon and the museum. Half a block to tons of shopping and dining options.  1 block to the metro is a huge plus, too.

The hotel is well staffed with very helpful employees who truly want you to have a great experience.

The rooms are clean and well kept if slightly dated. GREAT water pressure and almost instant hot water!

The breakfast was good with lots of options to choose from. Cereal, pastries, bread, meats and cheeses, and of course fruit. With coffee, tea,orange juice,sour cherry, and milk to drink.

We would absolutely stay here again.

This hotel is in a perfect location and for the price you can’t beat it! Just one block from the Acropolis south entrance and the best walking street with so many amazing restaurants all around. The breakfast is phenomenal the staff are wonderful. They have the best deal in the bar for happy hour from 4pm-8pm BOGO drinks for €6.50 and the bar area and lobby are beautifully decorated. The rooms are basic but cozy and spacious. Overall had a very nice stay!

Great location, literally a step away from Acropolis. The MVP is the AM shift manager, was nice enough to give me the room as soon as possible which was a lifesaver after a 12hr red eye flight. The breakfast was good too. The only thing is the rooms are quite dated.

Exceptional hotel with excellent service, one of the best breakfasts ever at a hotel (included in rate), great location and expansive rooms. Location is central to major historical sites, a plethora of stores and restaurants, metro/bus/tram stops. We walked to everything except for our cool tram ride to the Greek Riviera. Front desk staff, housekeeping staff and breakfast staff are top notch. In the morning, you can treat yourself to a large variety of breakfast items, freshly made, hot and cold. Great communication prior to and during our 3 day stay.

This hotel is in a perfect location to walk to all the sites! The street is not noisy or too busy. The staff was very welcoming and helpful! The room was clean and had all you needed; the fixtures and linens are a little dated, but again all you needed. Nice breakfast choices too. Thank you!

Nice hotel in a busy touristy area with plenty of restaurants and tons of shopping.
Hotel appearance and lobby were excellent.
Room was large by EU standards. The only caution on the room is the normal EU half shower makes the smooth marble floors very slick.
The included rooftop breakfast buffet was really good with a fantastic view of the Acropolis.
Included WiFi was adequate.
Staff was efficient and friendly.
Would stay here again.

The Acropolis view from room is breathtaking. The staff is very helpful and nice. The location is perfect, it is very easy to access the metro line to Acropolis and airport as well. Breakfast is very delicious. One small minus point is the room is little old and small, but it is really not a big deal compared to other plus points. I had a very nice time during the time when I stayed here. Highly recommendation.

Astor Hotel is highly recommended for anyone visiting Athens. Its central location, close to numerous restaurants and the iconic Syntagma Square, makes it an ideal base for exploring the city. The area is lively, adding to the overall charm of the stay.

The staff were incredibly friendly, pleasant, and kind, always willing to help. Although the room felt a bit small, it more than made up for it with its cleanliness and comfort.

The highlight of our stay was definitely the breakfast on the rooftop, offering a breathtaking view of the city. Watching the sunset over the sun-kissed Acropolis was an unforgettable experience. We thoroughly enjoyed our stay here and would love to return.

Initial impression of this Hotel is disappointing. We are in a family room and only have 2 people. The set out of room feels like I'm in a caravan. Not much room to move around. We booked a larger room as I'm in a wheelchair. I can't get my wheelchair past the entrance to the room. Location is good but the pavements around the hotel are very poor for wheelchair users. We picked this hotel as it said it was accessible. It's not. Website said there was a restaurant in this Hotel. However it's only open for Breakfast and for drinks in the evening. Lunch and dinner is not served.

Best acropolis view from rooms and smack in the nicer section for market shopping and easy access to restaurants

Service was nice. Our AC unit broke and they quickly moved us to another nice room.

Breakfast is very nice with superb view of the acropolis.

Large lobby with resting area couches etc.

Elevators are a bit small but manageable.

I had a very late flight out of Athens. The reception staff were very warm and caring looking after my luggage and giving me a quiet place to relax until my transfer arrived. The decor is beautiful, and the hotel is situated very close to the Acropolis. They opened the rooftop bar before I left and made me a snack. Simply breathtaking!! 🥂♥️

Stayed for two nights post a cruise.

Good location, rooms a good size and I enjoyed a bath.  We were in an executive room.

Breakfast was good.

Unfortunately we didn't get to the rooftop due to a wedding.

Easy walking distance to Acropolis and Plaka.

Staff were very friendly and helpful.

This hotel is absolutely outstanding, especially in terms of the service provided.  From the moment we checked in, every single person we encountered was incredibly friendly and helpful.  We especially appreciated the help with our luggage, something that is rare these days unless you are staying in a much more expensive hotel.  The rooftop restaurant has a beautiful view of the Acropolis, and the staff there was amazing as well.  I would definitely recommend this hotel to anyone staying in Athens.  You will not be disappointed.

This hotel meet our needs and is located in an area that allows you to seek out and see many of Athens tourist highlights. It is located approx. 30 minutes away from the Ports and approx. 40 mins from airport dependent on traffic (there is a lot of traffic in Athens). Rooms were very clean and appointed nicely with the bed mattress being comfy. The staff were both pleasant and helpful, making sure we had a box breakfast on the morning of an early morning departure to catch a ferry. The restaurant and bar on the roof top with a spectacular view of the Acropolis at night is worthy of at least a cocktail visit.

We decided to have dinner at the Athenian Callirhoe Hotel Restaurant while visiting Athens. The staff was friendly and even though we did not have a reservation they seated us at a table with a view of the Acropolis and the Parthenon which was absolutely beautiful.  The menu included lamb chops, fresh fish--tsipoura (sea bream) and salads; we ordered one of each and we were not disappointed! They were delicious and plentiful! Will definitely consider it again when we find ourselves in Athens.

Great location, near shopping and touristic areas. The service is exceptional, professional and efficient. We found the room to be a bit small but it was comfortable, quiet and modern. The roof top restaurant with a view on the acropolis was excellent. They accommodated to our needs and the staff was very helpful. This helps relax and take in the atmosphere of Athens.

We are currently staying at the Amalia Hotel in Athens Greece. This hotel is wonderful. All the staff have been so helpful and nice. Location is in the Plaka neighborhood. So close to the best shopping area and historic sites. The Whisper rooftop restaurant bar has great prices, view of the Acropolis and staff. Air conditioned, clean room and mini fridge. Great price! Don’t over look this one!

Perfect spot. Everything is close enough to walk to and if you need to spend some time in the evening and don’t know what to do the park right across the street is so nice.
The dinner here is so good, and the service as well.

Showers are very small🫠🫣

3 stars, definitely not 4.
I don't know if they have renovated rooms but mine was extremely outdated with old, stained carpets. Bed was a little worn down but not HORRIBLE.
Very convenient location to shops and restaurants. Right across a nice park

Such warm hospitality! One of the best experiences I’ve had with a hotel EVER. The location is very much central and the crew is initiative, friendly and caring. If you see Andreas you know you’re in good hands!

We traveled as a party of 2 without our kids in June for the first time to celebrate our anniversary and we splurged on the Double Athenian Panorama Room. Wow, the wall of windows that overlooked the Temple of Zeus was breathtaking day and night. The room was very spacious and clean. It had a very comfortable bed, a sofa and seating area, a desk area, a large wardrobe, robes and slippers, a large marble bathroom with a luxurious double tub (which I’d never seen before), and a flat screen tv. It was perfect for our 3-night getaway in Athens. It was convenient to all the central sites, as we did a self-guided 12-hour walking tour and never needed a taxi. The breakfast included was good and the rooftop terrace restaurant was quiet and convenient. The staff were pleasant and helpful when we needed them, which we really didn’t except for a transfer to the airport. We didn’t try room service or the pool so we can’t comment on those amenities. We got everything we paid for regarding our accommodation here and couldn’t be more pleased. A great value. We’d stay in this room again in a heartbeat!

A lovely hotel in a perfect location, just 10 minutes walk to Acropolis southern entrance. Good sized, very clean rooms, nice sized bathrooms, with shower over bath. Top floor bar overlooking Acropolis and the park. Breakfast was good with lots of choice and good service, also located in restaurant on top floor to enjoy the views. Would certainly return here.

The room was clean and comfortable. It was huge and a very nice room. The bathroom was amazing. We enjoyed our time there. The breakfast was part of the room and delicious. The staff was helpful. The view is good but not as amazing as the hotel photos. The area is decent but I wasn't impressed with most of Athens.

Large well appointed rooms with big bathrooms.  Everyone at the hotel was friendly and eager to offer assistance. The rooftop bar and restaurant provided lovely views and friendly service. The bar did their best on cocktails....stick to the menu and you won't be disappointed.

Elegant hotel with fine furniture appointments. Satisfactory rooms, neat, clean, luxurious towels. Room lighting was low, no tea or coffee provided in the room, cental temperature control with delayed response. Helpful staff. Elegant roof top dining with panoramic views.

Let's start with the location, brilliant!! Right in the center of Athens, by the market and walkable distance to all the tourist spots.
It's a small hotel with a super small team, but everyone was kind and sweet.
The room i stayed in was on the smaller end, but who cares,  not moving in, just staying for a vacation. More important,. The room was clean and smelled nice, and had a huge balcony.
Definitely a good choice.

Carolina hotel is right in the heart of Placa area, nothing fancy, but you only need a shower and a good night sleep. The rest is out and about in Athens. Very friendly and kind people at the front desk and they helped us out to sit before seven in the morning so we can have breakfast before taking off to the airport. We will be back.

It’s very nice hotel with good price, room is small, neat and pretty good. I love the terrace. Clean washroom. Front desk staff is very friendly and helpful. Breakfast is not bad. Distance from the Monastiraki square is hardly 7 minutes. Lots of nice restaurants and bars in the same street.

Very nice place, clean, bright room, very good breakfast.

Close to the Acropolis, plenty of cafes around.
A fabulous hotel quite clean and very close to all that old Athens has to offer. Great staff. Great rooms and a brilliant breakfast ! Awesome value!

The room was clean, great location! We walked 10 minutes to the acropolis and museum. The rooftop bar has center view of the acropolis and spectacular at night! The breakfast was good. The front desk was very helpful booking transportation for us for the airport. Restaurants are all close by as well no car needed. The price was very reasonable.

The Hotel Adonis Athens was recommended to me by a friend and It was just fabulous. It is in a quiet street just two minutes walk from beautiful restaurants and shopping and walking distance to the Acropolis and other attractions.   From the moment we arrived we were made to feel so welcome and the staff were all amazing and so helpful, even organising our taxis for us.  The room we booked was wonderful with a large balcony and view of the Acropolis. It was spotless clean and lovely bathroom.  There is a lovely bar open in the evening overlooking the Acropolis which is lovely and the breakfast had a good selection of food, served in the same area overlooking the Acropolis.  I would most definitely come back to this hotel again and recommend it.

we enjoyed our stay at this hotel, as the location was excellent, and the room was comfortable and had everything we needed. The air conditioning was very effective.  Quite a bit of sound is transmitted through the hallway and open stairs, but we generally slept relatively well, except when someone had an early flight.  Breakfast from the roof terrace was outstanding.
The staff were great and the location is very near shops, food, and the Acropolis, which the hotel roof dining area provided a great view. The hotel worked out perfectly! Rooms are small but clean.

Very clean hotel located in Plaka situated close  to attractions and great restaurants.  The breakfast was great and the staff, very polite and helpful. Our room was small, but all we needed, very quiet  with comfortable bed. Oh!  And the view  of the Acropolis, from the roof, where breakfast was served, was amazing! I’d  stay here again and recommend it to others! Thanks, Rick Steves,  for the recommendation!

Every single staff member was obliging and helpful. The restaurant was amazing. The location perfect. The Divani Acropolis hotel had lovely energy..
That iced water station in the entry was the perfect touch after coming in from the scorching Athens heat!

We recently stayed here and loved the location and enjoyed the pool. The room was clean and comfortable and a clear priority. The cleaning and support staff were genuinely friendly and helpful.
The only issue was the attitude and demeanor of many of the staff members; a few front desk staffers, the breakfast servers, and bar tenders. They were short and unfriendly. It was very strange to be received this way.  We were practically yelled at when we shared our disappointment about something. Even though the problem was rectified, I felt embarrassed by the person shaming us for a complaint. The breakfast servers avoided bringing us coffee and getting our own wasn’t an option. Finally, on the last day there was a taxi strike, but we received no notification. An email would have been nice. I’m guessing this review will make little difference, but it honestly left such a bad impression, I was happy to leave.

Big shout out to Bell Captain Michael for saving our trip! We dropped our bag with all our money, credit cards and passports in it. We were 1/2 way up the hill and he came running after us to see if it belonged to someone in our group. Not only did he chase us down but also asked us to verify the contents to confirm it was getting to the rightful owner. Also want to give commendation to Maria in the restaurant who has the sweetest smile and friendly demeanor.  Such a great stay in this beautiful hotel with a very honest and friendly staff. We will stay here every time we return to Greece thanks to them.

Stayed 3 nights and was impressed with this hotel. The most wonderful experience in Athens was the restaurant on the roof of the hotel as the view of the Acropolis at night has to be seen to be believed. Don't go by the website reservations as tables were available even when they weren't shown. We visited this restaurant each of our 3 nights there and received wonderful service even if just asking to have drinks. The photos we took of the Acropolis where so good that friends accused us of using a postcard or the web. It's really that good. Breakfasts at their downstairs restaurant were also excellent. The hotel is very conveniently located to tour both the Acrpolis and the Acropolis museum by foot. I can't recommend it enough.

Michael, the Bell Captain was a highlight of our stay.  Genial and professional, he made us feel more at home and comfortable. Helpful in information about surrounding area. Here’s an asset to your hotel.

Thermis at the desk, an extraordinary ambassador! Welcoming and helpful to an all of us traveling from Hawaii!

Dinning waitstaff is superb!

This hotel was brilliantly placed for restaurants, shops, acropolis and agora. The facilities are clean and all work, the breakfast is also fabulous. To top it off, the staff are fantastic, friendly, helpful and fun.
The view from the roof garden is amazing.
We had a brilliant stay, thank you.

Very good hotel in an amazing location.  Nothing fancy, just a really great hotel with brilliant breakfast.

Most of the main attractions are walkable,  and lots of shops and restaurants nearby.

Icing on the cake is the view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar.

Right in the prime location for visiting Athens, this hotel was clean, well run, and the rooftop restaurant offered an incredible view of the Acropolis in addition to excellent food & drinks.  Would revisit next time I’m in Athens.

I gave it 5 stars for the value of money. It really met the qualifications of a 3 star hotel. My room had a shower and bath tub. Be aware that the restaurant does not have view of Acropolis. But it was nice. The location of hotel is very good close to every where.

Comfy beds, location couldn't be better, great breakfast with lots of options, ONLY downside was very little sound proofing between rooms.
Loved the view from the rooftop restaurant. Food was excellent, but service was lacking.

I had the pleasure of staying at the Electra Palace Hotel in Athens for nearly a week, and I can’t recommend it enough! The hotel’s location is absolutely fantastic – both from my room and the rooftop restaurant, I had breathtaking views of the Acropolis, making every morning and evening unforgettable.

I was particularly impressed with the outstanding service. The staff were always friendly, professional, and incredibly attentive, which made the stay even more enjoyable. A special thanks to the concierge, who provided excellent tips and was always helpful in making sure I experienced the best of the city.

For those driving, the parking in the city center is a huge advantage. In Athens, this is not something you find easily, and the hotel made it super convenient to explore the city without any hassle.

Overall, it was a wonderful stay at an excellent hotel – I’ll definitely be back!”

I stayed at this property two different times for my trip.  The first time we found the rooms to not be very clean but we were exhausted and only here one night so we opted to just not make a big deal about it.  After traveling around the islands we came back for our last night.  The rooms were the same. NASTY! This time we complained.  The front desk acted like they didn't believe me.  I offered to go up and show them what I was talking about but no one seemed to care.  Instead they send housekeeping up to look around.  They also told me they arranged a transfer to the airport in the morning.  When we got down to check out no one arranged anything.  Pitiful customer service.  Would never stay here again.  I would however come eat at the rooftop restaurant. It is good. One last note. Since the ac only runs with the room key it took until 1am for the room to be cool.

Great place to go for a late night after drink. After having dinner downtown, we decided to walk up to the rooftop bar and check it out. The view was so awesome. We decided to stay for a nightcap. My wife had the port and I had the Blantons old-fashioned. Beautiful evening nice breeze great company. Highly recommend if you have a chance to go by.

This hotel has excellent location in the down town of Athens, close to all the shopping areas, historic places and restaurants. It has a beautiful roof top restaurant with views to Acropolis and very good quality food. I highly recommend this hotel for your stay in Athens.

We had dinner at the roof restaurant of the hotel. The food was exceptional the service was impecable and most importantly Lazaros our server was the best! Always with a smile he was so patient and suggested the best food and wine. Really made our evening!!! Thank you. We will definitely come back.

Hotel is located in a quaint and quiet area of Athens within walking distance of the Acropolis and much more. There are many restaurants close by. We arrived before our rooms were ready, but they had a secure area that we could store our luggage so we could go and explore. Hotel itself was secure as well as you had to have a code to enter. Rooms were clean and spacious. Only complaint was in one of our rooms there was no hot water in the shower and one of their front desk personnel wasn't very friendly. Overall though, we would stay again.

The location is nice- in Plaka beside the acropolis and flea market. However, we had a bad experience, would not stay here again and would not recommend it. There are plenty of other decent 2/3 star hotels in the area
First, upon check in, we were told we would have to change rooms. We got 4 different explanations including there was something wrong with your current room, this is not the room you wanted, we are doing you a favour etc. When I sent a formal email to the hotel, I was told that someone at the hotel made a “mistake” and gave our “original” room to someone else and now we were just returning to it but had to wait for them to check out.
When we got to our “new room”, there was a bad smell like sewage from the bathroom. I told the gentleman at the helpdesk who just said “sorry” and I emailed management again- no reply this time. The smell was brutal before we ever used the bathroom and unbearable. Made it difficult to stay in the room.
We asked for a taxi pickup from the hotel to the airport at 3:20am. When we came down to check out, the gentleman at the desk said his colleague made a note of the request but didn’t actually do it! We ended up having to drag our bags out on the street at 3:30am (with my mother) to flag a taxi. Too many screw ups for a hotel that we paid decent money for.

Exactly as we expected. Location was perfect - close to everything we planned to visit. Children loved the National Garden which were really close.
Breakfast is small but also did the job before we went out to explore.
A trip to the terrace in the evening was a nice way to wind down.

On 23/05 we made a reservation through Booking, but we did not observe the date. At the same time we saw the error, we contacted Booking, as well as the person responsible for the hotel, informing them of what had happened.
To our surprise, the hotel charged us practically the daily rate, even though we explained the mistake, including offering to change it to the correct date, which was not possible.
It's a shame for a hotel that doesn't show solidarity with the customer and ends up getting paid twice if there was another reservation.
We even understand that a fine could be applied, but it is unreasonable for it to be a daily rate.
Due to the lack of understanding, as the person in charge informed us that we requested a late cancellation, which was not true, I do not recommend the hotel.
Lack of empathy, understanding and common sense on the part of the hotel.

EXCELLENT LOCATION!!! My husband and I have stayed at the Adam's Hotel twice. We arrived early in the morning. We were able to set the luggage aside until the room was ready. Staff we polite. Rooms are small yet comfortable with a balcony (keep in mind it is a historic area). The terrace view is beautiful, especially at night of the Acropolis. Location is a quick short walk to many shops and restaurants. They sell hop-on hop-off bus tour tickets, which take a full tour of Greece main attractions. Highly recommended staying at the Adam's Hotel it was a hidden gem!!

The hotel itself is honestly very nice and clean, the staff downstairs are very friendly and were helpful to us and even held our luggage and set up a taxi.

The only negative in the room is that the AC in the room did not work at all.

Now the bar is a different story, staff did not seem as friendly. I will say though that the bar is beautiful and offers an amazing view of acropolis that you cannot beat.

However, the staff at the roof top bar were far from friendly. They offer a free voucher for a drink but don’t mention it’s only a cheap wine or bud light beer. They also seem annoyed if you use the voucher and only allow you sit in certain areas. In my opinion, maybe just don’t give out the voucher then.

Even when having drinks another night and paying for them, we found it hard to get service. The servers were dancing and drinking alcohol at the bar while we waiting for a while. I know they were just having a good time and I’m okay with that but when we asked for the anything we got some attitude and I’m not sure why…

Honestly, I would probably stay again due to the location, clean rooms and beautiful view of acropolis. Just giving my input in hopes some things can improve!!

Review is for the restaurant.
You will not be disappointed by the rooftop views. Reserve early to guarantee a table at the edge overlooking the square with unobstructed view of the Acropolis. We arrived just before sunset so got bit day and night views.
Casual to upscale feel and although menus limited the food is delicious. We hand the lamb appetizers and the grouper as the main course. The food was outstanding, possibly the best grilled fish we’ve ever eaten. Have dinner here and you won’t be disappointed.

Another GREAT rooftop bar/restaurant with views of the city and the Acropolis.  The environment is cozy and romantic.  Their drink menu is awesome with specialized crafted drinks made at your table. Friendly staff. Popular with trendy crowd. Highly recommend. We were recommended by a friend to check this bar out and it did not disappoint.

A for Athens is an exceptional hotel that offers a perfect blend of comfort, convenience, and stunning views. Located in the heart of the city, just steps away from Monastiraki Square, it’s the ideal base for exploring Athens’ historical landmarks. The rooms are modern, clean, and comfortable, but the highlight of this hotel is the rooftop bar, which offers breathtaking views of the Acropolis, especially at night. The breakfast is also fantastic, with a wide variety of delicious options. The staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful, making our stay even more enjoyable. If you’re looking for a central location with unbeatable views, A for Athens is a great choice!

We went to the rooftop bar for a sunset drink and the view was magnificent! The staff was friendly however if you just want to have a drink you cannot sit at the table. You can only stay standing near a middle block. I saw the food going past and it didn't appeal to me so I was happy to stand. The cocktails are interesting but if you want a classic you need to ask for it as not on the menu.

My wife and I have just returned from Greece and stayed at this magnificent hotel on the first few nights and again on the last night. We could not have been happier! The rooms were clean and spacious with large comfortable beds, bathrooms were spotless and had thick towels, nice slippers and robes to enjoy. More importantly, I need to mention the kindness and professionalism of the staff!  Every one of them. Congratulations to the management for excellent training. Upon check in I was greeted by Dora who made me feel comfortable and welcome from the start. We asked her for dinner location suggestions and she offered many outstanding choices. She worked long hours. The bell staff was always willing to go out of their way to help. The breakfast was more than anyone could eat, and an extravagant display of so many items! Our rooms had balcony's and a great view of the city landscape. I enjoyed standing on them and watching the changing of the guards!  And finally on the last day Manio was so kind with our checkout. Sad to leave, but with fond memories of an enjoyable stay!

We actually checked in on the day after we were supposed to. My travel agents fault. No problem, they moved the day of check in and off we went. Took all of 5 minutes to correct and we were given a beautiful room with a view of the Acropolis. Rarely will you stay at such a clean, friendly and generous place. And even the bed was incredible. Would definitely stay again. Thank you.

A nice hotel with excellent service. The location is close to everything you would want to see in Athens! Rooms were pretty clean but they are a little dated. The breakfast had a good selection of items from bacon, sausage, eggs, fruit and pastries. The only comment on the breakfast would be the coffee and cappuccinos didn't taste great. Overall a nice hotel if you're visiting Athens!

The rooms are large! Rooms are clean! The staff is very accommodating. We enjoyed a breakfast buffet. There is a bar open in the evening on the mezzanine level.  There is a restaurant in the lobby.  Be aware that this hotel does not have a pool. This hotel was picked as part of our tour. Overall this hotel is a good choice.

Probably the noisiest hotel I have ever stayed at. The bedroom doors are very thin and you can hear housekeepings every move knock and bang, even the noise of plastic bags rustling. I’m sure another room or floor may have been different but even 6 floors up no peace! I leave my room impeccable and do not use all the towels and clutter available, so how about some mutual respect in the morning, smashing vacuum cleaners against the wall is war! Very clean room on arrival.

Great location on a relatively quiet street about a 5 minute walk from the Syntagma metro. Practically all the sights we visited were within walking distance (at most, 25 minutes). Tons of restaurants in the area and the neighborhood has a great vibe. Very friendly and professional staff. Good breakfast is included. Our room was very quiet.

Modern hotel in the heart of Athens.  Walking distance to numerous restaurants and the acropolis.  The elevators are tiny.  Rooms were quiet.  The view is an adjacent building.  There's a parking garage next door but we had a tour driver so we didn't need parking.

The place is convenient and near all attractions.  However, the room was noticeably infested with bed bug.
My daughter and I got bitten all over our bodies despite we had different room.
A little old style hotel in the city main area in a narrow street. Rooms were clean with balcony. The location was excellent.

Prime location, easy access, great rooms, comfy mattresses, spacey bathroom, filling breakfast with many options and above all, excellent staff. I mostly interacted with Giannis and he's definitely the most professional and also the friendliest hotel employee I've ever interacted with. We were a group of 13 people and everyone had a story where he helped us, all in just 2 days! A short, but absolutely amazing experience. Thank you!

Lovely little place in the heart of Athens.. I booked really late on the evening, but the host was really accommodating. she even helped me plan my next days trip to Delphi.

Self service breakfast was included in the extremely low price.. at the end of a few weeks traveling money is tight and this was such a lucky find 🙌
Was in Athens after a Mediterranean cruise and needed a place to stay for the night. It was perfect for a large group. There were nice beds, great courtyard, and a view of the Acropolis. I recommend.

The hostel is conveniently located in the center, it was reasonably clean.
The rooms were pretty small for 4 people. We actually booked a 3 person bedroom, which looked a lot more appealing, but we were given another dorm without notice or anyone to talk too, since staff was only there for check-out. Hostelworld and Google Maps Website says there is a possibility for luggage storage, but it was not available during our stay.
Doors in our dorm were very squeaky and loud, waking everyone up in the room as soon as someone enters or leaves. This was very annoying, since it seams easy to fix. Same can be said about the wooden bunk beds. They are very sketchy and shaky, seam to be for kids and are extremely noisy when making the slightest move. Not to mention the real fear of having the bed collapse during sleep (Im below 80kg ...).
Bathrooms were acceptably clean, but the only shower on our floor that you could lock was missing a curtain, so everything was constantly flooded. Its annoying and easily fixable issues like these that left a below average impression.

Good place to stay ata Athens. Very close to tourist place & public transport. Reasonable price with good facility. Receptionist also very nice & helpful, kuddos to Alex(one of staff here, very nice person & helpful). Will stay here again next time

thoroughly regrettable stay from start to end. Nadia at front desk was actively hostile, aggressive and racist. Took 5 minutes and tow calls just to open the front door, another 30 minutes and two more phone, several rounds of back and forth to complete check-in. At one point she tried to take the keys away from me. room did not feel safe, no personal locker in room, no fire extinguisher. Bathroom and shower, which is shared by the whole floor, are an afterthought: behind one locked door there is one toilet without a sink so can't even wash hands, and behind another locked door two connected showering cubicals which means only one can be used at a time. All sound - footsteps, chatting, doors - reverberate freely in the entire building. Beds do not have curtains for privacy. Did not bother with breakfast and promptly left the premise next morning.

The WiFi is so terrible I wasn’t unable to send an important message, there are barely appliances in the hotel room, small tv screen, there wasn’t any toiletries like shampoo and toothbrush, although the location was in the middle of the city so it was perfect to go to places. Not recommended if staying for a long period of time. If u r just looking for a place to sleep but also shop and do sightseeing, then it is recommended

This is a horrible depressing place. The beds are like rocks. The bedding is disgusting and you could kill yourself in the shower because you need to step over a 2 foot wall to get into it. There are so many hotels in this same area for same price. We paid 180CAD. It’s beyond awful. the only star is for location

The only good thing about this place is the location.

The morning and cleaning staff are nice, but the late night staff is awful. Doesn't bother to leave the couch and when you ask for anything he mumbles back at you in Greek, clearly pissed. We asked for simple towels and he threw a fit.

The rooms are super dated. Simple maintenance can elevate the experience. The bathrooms need a lot of love, lucky I'm shirt and can fit. But the half size tub that spills water everywhere is a nightmare.

Luckily no bed bugs found so that's another win.

Oh and the weirdest thing ever, they ask you to give your card to them whenever you leave the hotel for "security" reasons, but ironically they opened the room once and provided an extra key for us simply asking for that and a room number, no verification of identy whatsoever. I can literally access anyone's room?

Wouldn't stay there. Was disappointed and honestly can't wait to leave it.

Firstly, I'll just state that the location is PHENOMENAL! Right in the centre, seemingly no more than a 10-15 minute walk from any of the sights. Brilliant.

What must be said though is wow this hotel is feeling tired and dated. It's perfectly serviceable, but only just. The rooms a very narrow, but somewhat long, so the square metre space sounds a lot bigger than it feels. The tv is about 20" and high up on wall at an angle that made it unviewable. Not that you could watch it anyway, as it only had terrestrial channels, all in Greek, so if travelling with a toddler/child and you wanted to put tv on for 10 minutes to distract them while you get a shower, that's out the window. Speaking of out the window, the balcony is about as wide as a small chair (which it conveniently had one placed on it, for size reference) but it was very rickety so you wouldn't want to spend any significant time out there somebody probably needs to replace the piece of wood that they had as a handrail there, as it was very wobbly. Oh, and that tv I mentioned? Very inconveniently placed at head height, right next to the patio door, so if you're 6 foot plus like I am, right in line for you to really crack your head on it 👍 wonderful. Actually caused me to split my head bleeding the final time it happened.

Another way the room ain't built for anybody 6 foot and over, the shower height, which genuinely had the shower head at about chest height on me, and I had to duck/bend my head anyway, as the wall jutted out where I should stand up straight.

Honestly though, all that said, the people were lovely, the room still served us as a place to sleep, but I probably wouldn't visit again or recommend it to anybody unless there were no other options nearby, which there are dozens of.

Edit: wanted to pop back to point out another issue actually which might not effect or be important to everybody but it certainly did us, and that's the fridge in the room was woeful. Genuinely put the tiniest "chill" on things but that was it. Can't even call it a chill really, but kept things just colder than room temperature. For somebody travelling with a baby that needs to store milk this isn't ideal. The milk kept going off and ended up resulting in our little lad actually being sick one night. What's odd is the fridge was giving off a tremendous amount of heat too, so it's probably just outright broke but not being looked at, which is just another sign of the outdated nature of muchnofnthe hotel.

A diamond on the rough! The Price really fantastic, Very Central to everything, restaurants, coffee shops, market, different shops around. Very Clean, and Secure. The staff are very friendly and helpful.

The location is perfect - it is in a very lively area, and close walking distance to everything. Despite being in a busy area, noise is not an issue. It is close to lots of shops and excellent food/dining options. The place also feels secure/safe given the 24 hour reception. We were warmly greeted each time we came and went from the accommodation. We were lucky to have a balcony and view of the acropolis from our room on the 4th floor! Other pros of this accommodation is it has a comparatively earlier check-in time. Shared bathroom is not too bad surprisingly, good water pressure and it is all kept clean.

Main con: Wifi did not work well in the room at all. There are lots of stairs to climb if you’re on the 4th floor, but this is not uncommon in Europe! They have a lift available for luggage only.

I enjoyed my stay here :) I had my own room with its own bathroom, which altogether was clean and great, the location was good and the staff were friendly. I remember breaking something in the bathroom by accident, but the staff were kind about it.

Superb location! Very good rooms considering the cost, they were clean and bigger than other cheap accommodation options i’ve stayed in. I also stayed on the 4th floor and there was no street noise at all, but I couldn’t say for lower levels. Finally, the staff were incredibly friendly, and “available 24/7 for your service.” Excellent experience and I would stay again. Highly recommend for young travellers on a budget!

Avoid!!!  AVOID 🆘🆘🆘 Wasn’t clean.  I was  abused by the male boss. don’t be fool by its good location, you need feel safe, have a clean room for a  good night sleep after a day out and back to your room with  welcoming  staffs, not missable faces  and tiny area

My room was simple (might not be to everyone's liking, but suited me fine.) Clean and the staff very helpful. The view from the 5th floor terrace was worth the hike up the stairs and to enjoy take out dinner! Well located not for from Metro station,  restaurants and the sights.
The Amazon Hotel, Athens was centrally located. Within walking distance of all attractions in Athens. All staff were very helpful and friendly. Wouldn't hesitate to stay there again 🙂

This is a very nice hotel. It is close to the syntagma and it is just by the ermou streets. You can walk to the main attractions  including monastraki and plaka. The hotel has a very nice breakfast and the staff is friendly. The rooms are clean and they are airconditioned.

Perfect location …right next to ermou with all shops and restaurants minutes away!
Rooms are a little small ,but we didn’t spend much time inside so not a big deal!

There was SO MUCH noise in our room like we could barely sleep it was insane. WOULD NOT RECOMMEND. this was on a Friday but still our room was right in the center of things and I wouldn't ever stay here again. Very disappointed

Full value for money.  Located 5 minutes from the Matrot station and right in the center of the parking lots and shopping avenues.  A small and clean hotel, well maintained and a varied and excellent breakfast.  highly recommend

Perfect host - warm, friendly and knowledgeable. Perfect location - very accessible to restaurants, souvenir shops, museum and historical places. The hop on and off bus stop is right at our doorstep. Nice rental unit - We were given a unit with 3 bedrooms, very good for a family of 5. And the unit was generously stocked with food for breakfast and snacks. No washing machine so be prepared to send out for laundry if you intend to rent more than a week in our unit. Not a problem for us though.

Amazing location. We were last week with our 3 children.We were greeted by  Mr Takis Super helpful he gave us lots of tips and a map of Athens. He also picked us up from the airport and we used the private transfer service for more trips.The service of Mr.Chris was excellent.
The complimentary milk, tea, coffee and the makings of breakfast were very much appreciated.
We will definitely reach again,
Thank You !!
The apartment is big and a great price. It is very close to acropolis walking and restaurants in plaza. It was easy to reserve and the host and owner are great. Would definately come again
Perfect location in Athens. Everything in walking distance, but the best are the views from the rooms.
Good apartement and fab views but the windows need replacing  to keep the road noise out,which was horrendous and constant...I can sleep through anything except that..

Our stay at the Pan hotel was absolutely fantastic! The property is stunning, with beautiful surroundings and impeccably clean rooms that made us feel right at home. The staff were exceptional, providing top-notch service throughout our stay. A special shout-out to Yianni (receptionist), who truly went above and beyond to assist my family and me in planning our travels to our next destination. His help was invaluable, and he made our trip even more enjoyable. Big thanks to Liza (breakfast room) such a lovely person.Highly recommend the Pan hotel and look forward to returning in the future!

I would like to commend the staff at the front desk, I had the pleasure of meeting the concierges of each shift and their level of costumer service was outstanding. The hotel is in a perfect location, walking distance from the center and the area has lots of restaurants and activity so it is very safe.
The room although very basic was very clean and had everything one needs. I suggest that management uses some decorative accent on the walls to make it more appealing, just a touch and also some touches to revamp the bathroom. In conclusion, I recommend this hotel, the staff really made the difference.

Good location and nice breakfast however we had a few issues. The main one was that the street and restaurants were so loud all night, you could hear people chatting and singing all night and the windows didn’t block out the sound. The curtains also didn’t block out the light. There was mould on the bathroom ceiling, the bathroom was very small and our mini fridge was wobbly. I wouldn’t stay here again.

The Pan Hotel staff were very helpful. A special mention to John on Reception. We stayed here twice pre and post our visit to some nearby islands. The rooms were very clean and the breakfast good  The location is excellent. Very good value. Thank you

The hotel is very central and an easy walk to Syntagma Square for access to the metro system. The staff were helpful and the breakfast was good.  We were well looked after by staff for breakfast.  The lift is tiny - a tight squeeze with your luggage. Its central location means that it is quite noisy (even when on the 8th floor).  The walls are pretty thin and we had a very noisy neighbour.  The beds were comfortable and clean.  The bathrooms were clean (but very small showers).  We only stayed one night as an overnight stop on our journey home.
Stayed for 5 nights first part of our holiday in Athens. Going back.  Third time we have stayed here in 26yrs.3rd generation owner friendly staff.  Great breakfast and very clean comfy rooms.

The Acropolis House is very well located, with shops, supermarkets and tourist attractions only a few meters away. Its staff is very welcoming, the breakfast was very good too. The family room ok for a couple of nights!

Very old and not renovated at all. Didn't even have a bottle of water in the room and very noisy at night and also in the morning since floor is wooden and it makes noise when you walk. Great location though.
Great staff and great location. Loved our stay. Very clean otherwise.  Rooms could be a bit bigger but understandable since it's an old building. Loved the antique feeling inside.
Location is amazing, next to everything, the service is very friendly and the rooms are okay :) we can’t ask more

I stayed here at the end of July with my two boys, and we absolutely loved it! Such a cozy little gem! The hotel is perfectly located just a short walk from the city center, making it super easy to explore Athens on foot. Plus, there's public transport nearby, which made it so convenient to get to the beach! The staff were incredibly friendly and went above and beyond when we had an issue with a forgotten bag—they helped us recover it without any hassle. The highlight, though, is definitely the stunning rooftop. The view of the Acropolis is unbeatable, and the restaurant up there is top-notch. I would absolutely stay here again!

Wonderful experience! We stayed here one night prior to our cruise. The staff is very kind and helpful. We received welcome drinks at the rooftop bar and also had excellent authentic Greek food. The hotel itself is beautiful with a modern look. And you have a great view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar!
The room had a good amount of space for us. The shower was a little smaller than we are used. They were also to take our luggage for us while we explored the town before and after our reservation. The shop and restaurants are a short walk from the hotel. Overall great experience!!

Best part of the stay is the wonderful staff and the beautiful common area. The room was nicely appointed but poorly lit (need a light near the entry door) and the bathroom was poorly designed — strange tub with very little room to stand to take a shower then the shower curtain sticks to you. Hardly any room to step out of the tub/shower without bumping into the vanity or toilet. No outlet in bathroom so the hair dryer sits on the desk in the main part of the room and is plugged underneath. First room I had suffered from an unpleasant smell in the drains; second room was better. The staff absolutely could not be any nicer, but the room was a disappointment.

The staff is friendly, and the room is clean, though it is small. The elevator is tiny, but I later discovered that many elevators in Greek hotels are similar. The only downside was the shower. However, the location is perfect, just 5 minutes from the Acropolis Museum.

Everything about this place was perfect! The air conditioning was heaven after long hot days of exploring, great location to get around, stocked with everything we needed and the balcony was such a nice touch to enjoy our morning coffee (provided) and our wine & snacks from a local gem down the street. The only issue was the front door to the building itself definitely needs to be replaced, but it was worth every second of minor inconvenience! Highly recommend staying here if you're visiting Athens! Wonderfully romantic for couples.

The hotel was excellent in every aspect. I believe the hotel's location was one of the best areas, and with a little walk, you could see some of the most beautiful places, as well as experience the vibrant nightlife.
In terms of internet and, I have to say they were good. For breakfast, you had different options and fruits and everything was served neatly.
‏The staff's behavior was truly great, They also helped us to book a tour and were helpful and kind.

This hotel is UNSANITARY. my body (including face and neck) is covered with BED BUGS bites which are very painful and itchy.
The first room I was in had the worst shower head I've seen and in the morning I had to fight off a roach just to get in the shower. They moved me to a different room and there were the bed bugs.
The hotel never mentioned anything about sanitizing my things or offer any solution, they even suggested I might be lying (until they saw the bed bugs with their own eyes). Added pics and videos.

You have to drag your suitcases up a few flights of stairs and there's an elevator only between the 1st and 3rd floor. And there's no one to help. No kettle in the room as well. Those things just seem basic to me.

The only plus is the location.

Never had this kind of experience in any hotel. I feel bad for posting this but I feel it's more important that people will know before booking this hotel.

Fantastic hotel, really helped us when we had problems with our Airbnb.  Staff so friendly and helpful.  The room was conformable and had everything we needed.
The whole hotel was very clean and tidy.
The breakfast was lovely,...hot food, pastries, juices, hot drinks.
Hotel is close yo evening you would want to see.
Highly recommended 👌

We stayed here for a quick visit in Athens.  The location was really great, a short walk to the Plaka and Acropolis as well as Syntagma Square.
The room was a good size.  We were facing the main street, so there was a bit of noise from the traffic, but it quiets down eventually.  The bed was large and very comfortable.
Better than the location and comfortable bed was the staff.  I was trying to locate lost luggage and both Vassilis and Donna went above and beyond to help me.  They were just great.
The cost of the room was quite reasonable and it was nice that breakfast was included.  I would certainly stay again.

My family and I had a wonderful stay at the Acropolis Museum Boutique Hotel. While the hotel wasn't hip or modern (it feels like it was furnished by an eclectic antique shop,) it was very clean and comfortable feeling.

In response to many reviewers concerns about the noise, we didn't find it to be a problem. Yes you're on a heavily trafficked street, but there wasn't much honking, emergency vehicles, or loud people. It's not quiet, but hey you are staying in the middle of the city!

We really enjoyed staying in this neighborhood, as the side streets in the Koukaki area had great food options, and felt very local and not touristy.

It is important to note that there are a number of stairs you must climb before you're able to reach the elevator, making it a bit difficult for those with mobility issues, and those with lots of bags. You climb a large flight from the street to the lobby, along with a small flight from the lobby to the next floor until you reach an elevator. If you are able-bodied, this is no issue at all.

Overall we had a stress-free time, and felt like this was a good option for the money.

360 Degrees Hotel in Athens is a perfect place to stay for every reason you could think of! It’s in the Monostiraki neighborhood which is the center of all the neighborhoods. It is very close to shopping, good restaurants and all the attractions; Agora, Hadrian’s Library, Parthenon etc…

We checked in easily (online) and our room was directly viewing the bustling square. It was clean and had all the amenities we needed. Breakfast was 10+! Everything was neatly put out, fresh with a huge variety of items! The coffee was fresh brewed every morning and they continually put out hot steamed cream.
Surprisingly, the restaurant for dinner wasn’t that good and I’d have to rate that a 2. All food items were sub par and many people around us complained to the manager or left the food. The view of the Parthenon is amazing from this rooftop so I would highly recommend just going there for a drink and elsewhere for a nice dinner.
I would definitely stay here again as it’s the perfect location for everything you want to do in Athens! Hotel front desk staff were all very nice and super helpful!

Went to the rooftop. Terrible service. The manager, his second (bouncer?), and the waiter all had a terrible attitude. Very arrogant. The rooftop has a great view of the Acropolis if you get seated by that end. So they believe service is not important. Yet I saw at least four more rooftops around that have the same view. The tables not on the side of the Acropolis could have a great view as well, but they put some trees that block it. Definitely need a new decoration person.
They had a very poor food offering, only 5 different dishes.
I recommend going to one of the other nice rooftops around.

I highly recommend this hotel for a variety of reasons.
1. The location is amazing. You are a short walking distance from all the major sites in downtown Athens.
2. The breakfast is extremely fresh and delicious.
3. The view from the breakfast area is breathtaking.
4.The Room is spacious and clean.
5. The staff is very friendly and helpful.

However, if you are a light sleeper, you might need ear plugs as the rooftop bar does not turn down the music until 2-3 AM.
Located near the main attractions that Athens has to offer including historical sites, shopping, food, and drinks.  Enjoyed our stay, would recommend!
Very clean with good staff. But no fire safety or exits. No power in my room. Nowhere to hang towels in the bathroom. No outdoor space. Low flow shower. No coffee in the room. No bin .

An excellent location. We'd stay again for it's proximity tot he Acropolis and all of the walking and dining in Plaka. Easy walking to Temple of Zeus, Parliament and changing of the guard, the all-marble ancient Olympic stadium, Acropolis and Acropolis museum and more.

Rooms are a bit dated but nice. Standard European small bathrooms but everything was in working order. Small balcony and great views of Plaka and Acropolis. Rooftop lounge great for cocktails and small plates.

We took the budget room but there was no information available on booking.com that it is actually an underground room. The bathroom was actually 3 steps up inside the room making it very uncomfortable. So please don’t consider the budget room and make sure you get rooms 1st floor onwards. The location was more than perfect.
Very nice hotel in the middle of PLAKA, very hospitable and friendly staff. The owner makes all guests feel like one of the family. Highly recommended place and we will surely return.

Don’t buy the breakfast, it is not worth the €10. The hotel rooms are decent, but the bed was not so comfortable. Great location for walking to the historic monuments. The cleaning staff were very friendly.

The Good.

* Superb friendly reception staff, breath of fresh air to see genuine kindness

* Location brilliant as many have indicated

The Not So Good.

* Double room on floor 2 was so small, if we had 2 suitcases there would be zero room to stand, think TINY.

* Unfortunately one of noisiest hotel rooms I have ever been in, zero soundproofing. Noise from elevator and all bathrooms around us was horrible, beware if you are sensitive to alot of noise at night. You can hear all conversations in both corridors and surrounding rooms. We were woken on many occasions thinking someone was in room with us, those walking in corridors soubded so close.

* Yes a wet soggy mess on floor of bathroom after shower as door does not seal, beware dont leave anything on floor as it will be soaked.

* Bed is soft but with any lack of support so beware if you have back trouble

A wonderful hotel.
The best location possible.
The rooms may not be large, but they are pleasant, clean, and have a great bathroom.
The staff is exceptionally courteous, service-oriented, and friendly.
And I almost forgot... an excellent breakfast.
A wholehearted recommendation for the hotel and its staff.
I have no doubt I will stay there again on my next visit.

Front desk staff was exceptionally kind and made sure we felt welcomed and appreciated. The room was small but cozy and well designed, with modern touches, and both the room and bathroom were very clean and comfortable. The breakfast spread was impressive, with hot options as well as cold ones (everything was so fresh!). The building is right downtown in the Plaka, with easy walking access to restaurants, shops, monuments... it was ideal! There's parking directly across the street, which is very handy. Overall, we had a wonderful time at the Sweet Home and would definitely return! Can't say enough good things about this lovely hotel.

An absolute treasure of a hotel right in the heart of the city. Despite its small size it had everything we needed, and we could walk to most sights in 10-15 minutes. I loved the decor of the hall and rooms, they brought a nice level of class to the whole stay. The breakfasts featured local ingredients and delicacies, and quickly became one of the highlights of our day. The staff was excellent and helpful at every turn, bringing it all together into the best hotel experience I ever had.

This hotel is incredible! The service was top notch, amazing breakfast, great location, quiet and clean rooms. We couldn't have asked for anything more. Will stay here again and recommend to anyone traveling to Athens. Alexia is the best!!

Easy access to everywhere! 20mins walk to Acropolis & many restaurants/cafes are around. My partner and I had 3 days trip to Athens and the receptionist recommended us for a ferry day tip to the Aegina island, wonderful experience we had!

All staffs were smiling throughout our stay. What we enjoyed the most during our stay was breakfast. Spinach pie, Greek yogurt with their homemade berries & cherries were amazing! It’s not a typical European breakfast. They had many homemade Greek style selections!

The room we stayed (304) was quite small as we had 02 luggages:( Besides that everything was good. I’d highly recommend this hotel✨

Breakfast buffet was excellent and fresh. Best scrambled eggs ever. Loved the espresso machine. Shower decent size for Greece. Elevators were reliable and fast.  Front desk staff very helpful. Do not hesitate to double park in front to load and unload.

I stayed here in 2018 with my family and when re-visiting Athens again this year I immediately booked a stay here as it was perfect then and I am more than pleased to say it is still perfect now!  So central, great friendly staff, fantastic view of the Acropolis from the lovely rooftop bar.  Perfect breakfast – what more can I say!!

I highly recommend this hotel! The cost-benefit is excellent!! Some positive points: the staff is very kind, polite and attentive; the hotel is right in the city center, you can visit the Acropolis and other tourist attractions on foot; the hotel has a terrace with a beautiful view of the Acropolis; the rooms are clean, well-equipped, with good amenities and the room service is great; the breakfast is quite decent.

Whenever I am in Athens, this hotel is my top choice. Conveniently located just a few minutes from Monastiraki metro station, it offers both comfort and accessibility.
The staff are always friendly and courteous, ensuring a pleasant stay. The rooms are consistently clean and well-maintained. Breakfast is simple yet delicious, catering to various preferences. The highlight is the charming rooftop bar, providing a lovely view of the city and the Acropolis.

Great location, amazing view from rooftop bar! Staff is so friendly and helpful. Rooms are very nice; not huge, but have everything you need. Now I know why a friend of mine stays here whenever she comes to Athens!

The hotel boasts a fantastic location and has recently undergone renovations. While the rooms may be smaller compared to other establishments in the same price range and area, the staff and service team are exceptionally friendly and accommodating. I would recommend this hotel for short stays, such as one or two nights.

While the hotel has been renovated the suite we had was comprised of several small rooms with a gigantic and essentially useless dressing/closet area.  The light switches were incredibly  confusing and I'm a 30 year construction person.

The bar next door had great views although the bartender  thought a martini meant martini & rossi

We stayed here before and after a Ritz Carlton Cruise. The location is absolutely fantastic. Book a balcony room for sure. The changing of the guard at the Palace captured our attention every single time we were in the room and the views of the Acropolis are magnificent. The location is also quite convenient for sightseeing, shopping, and dining options.

The 3 stars comes from the less than impressed experience we had with one very pompous maitre d, George. We returned from a day of sightseeing and shopping and stopped at the front desk to ask where we might get a glass of wine. They directed us to the (only) bar in the hotel that happens to be the Michelin starred restaurant. We went up to the bar only to be given the once-over by George and turned away because my husband was wearing shorts- now keep in mind, it was July and temperature reached the upper 90’s if not over 100 degrees Fahrenheit. We went back to our room; he changed into long pants and we returned. We sat at the bar and finally enjoyed the glasses of wine. We watched other couples arrive. Apparently baseball caps, tshirts, and jeans are appropriate dress whereas khaki shorts and a collared shirt are not.
