I think the whole experience was compounded by the fact that the front desk saw our attire and directed us to the bar- as well as the fact that George could have handled the whole situation with a lot more tact and a lot less arrogance.

The best experience I’ve ever had in a hotel. Between the King George and their sister property next door, my wife and I were able to explore all the amenities- spa, pool, sauna, steam rooms, both gyms, and more. Everything was top quality and exceptional. We said multiple times that this hotel greatly exceeded our expectations in every way. When we booked it we knew it was nice, but the feeling of grandeur, the amenities, and the staff made it perfect. Every single person we interacted with was incredibly kind and willing to help with any question or request. Lastly, we ate at Tudor Hall one night (Michelin star restaurant on 7th floor of hotel)  and it was some of the best food I’ve ever had. Can’t recommend this hotel and its people any more highly.

We had late flight out of Athens and our plan was to site see all day but it ended up being 100 degrees Fahrenheit that day. They even close the acropolis. We called to see if we could get a day use room and they were completely booked. The staff and manage can't say NO. The service found a way to provide us with a room until 5pm and we were so grateful. Best staff and great rooms. Really really good location and the restaurant has an amazing view of the city.

The rooms are super clean and renovated! Everything you need for your stay is available! Housekeeping comes everyday.
The stuff is extremely helpful and polite, they arrange everything you need. The location is 10/10, in the center of Athens and everything you need is a few minutes away. We had a lovely stay, we’re definitely staying here again. Strongly recommend !

Amazing cozy hotel for the first leg of our honeymoon! Perfect location centrally located to historical and cultural activities; we stayed for two nights and walked everywhere. Staff was incredibly friendly and helpful - if we find ourselves in Athens again we will come back for sure! We were so pleasantly surprised by the spacious balcony, it was perfect for sipping espresso!

Niki Athens was a wonderful introduction to Greece for me and my husband’s honeymoon. The front desk attendants are very nice and gave us all the details we needed for our stay at their hotel. The price point is great, we stayed in a typical room with a king bed and it was much more affordable than those on the nearby islands. The room is equipped with vegan toiletries and we even had a tray of pound cakes waiting for us when we arrived. The rooms are very cozy but felt perfect for just the two of us.
The hotel is cozy all around, it feels personal with a turn key and much smaller number of rooms than a traditional American hotel.
We chose this hotel specifically because we wanted to have the safety net of a vegan restaurant attached to the hotel. Unfortunately, the restaurant is not open on Mondays and Tuesdays, which was a majority of our stay, but we were delighted that next door is a vegan bakery, and across the street there is a vegetarian restaurant next door to a health food store, which offered food security (although we didn’t need it! Athens has soooo many vegan options! I was never worried about finding my next meal!)
Not only is food easily walkable, but we felt very lucky to have chosen a hotel so close to literally everything we wanted to see! The acropolis museum was maybe a 20 minute walk, restaurants we were interested in trying were never more than 15 minutes away by foot, and a taxi is always close by for the longer excursions.
When we finally got to try the restaurant, it was delicious!!!
The housekeeping staff is very pleasant and detail oriented, our room was perfect every afternoon when we came back from exploring.
Niki Athens was extremely good to us when we asked for anything. We are happy with our stay and will absolutely stay again when we come back!

This hotel was a treasure.  We booked the family loft on floors 5 and 6.  The second floor balcony had a beautiful view of the Acropolis.  The air conditioning was great and cooled the room well.  One oddity compared to US hotels: the elevator was very small and hot, but not a reason to pick a different hotel.  The location was a few blocks from restaurants and shops.  I would stay here again.

We stayed for 4 days.
The place needs a serious renovation.
Room furniture and floor full of dust.
The floor was never cleaned during our stay.
The bathroom was ok in terms hygiene and cleaning. The bed was comfortable.
The location is very good, close to Syntagma Square and the city center.
Not a hotel i would visit again though.

Our daughter and friend stayed in an Acropolis view room with balcony to celebrate their combined 30th birthdays (they loved the room!). I emailed the hotel to ask if it was possible to have champagne and glasses waiting for their arrival. The staff were very happy to arrange this - with a note, a little birthday cake and snacks on a lovely tray with the chilled champagne and glasses. The birthday girls were in tears at the lovely surprise and enjoyed the balcony and view with their champagne. Thank you Philippos Hotel for making their stay even more special.

Great location near Acropolis, Museums and La Plaka. Gracious, hardworking and helpful staff, gave restaurant recommendations, scheduled tour and taxi. Excellent Greek breakfast. Stayed here 15 years ago and the quality and value are consistent. Rooftop terrace with direct view of the Acropolis, wine available.

Amazing location, spotless clean, but missing a few basic amenities (e.g. plug socket on only 1 side of bed, no plugs at desk or light around mirror), staff were really friendly and very helpful. Rooftop space gives fantastic views

Great hotel in a central position close to the entrance to the acropolis. Rooms were clean and all seemed to have a balcony . The buffet breakfast was very good with a wide selection, including Greek cakes and yoghurt. The only slight downside was the smell from the drain in the bathroom but hey it's Greece and the drains always smell in Greece

This hotel was amazing. we were not meant to stay, but after getting late from the aeroport ( 5 in the morning ) we went inside asking for a room ( even tho we knew it was nearly impossible. The man saw that I was really anxious for being outside in the dark, and let us stay. It was really nice and also, we were so close from the Acropolis that we could go walking. Also the hotel super clean.

Excellent hotel to stay in while visiting Athens. Very clean, staff very helpful and the location is perfect for dining and shopping. The Acropolis is within walking distance, uphill of course. I highly recommend this hotel. In fact we are going back next year. Staff also speak English.

This place is so centrally located, I could practically high-five the Parthenon from my window.
The staff were so helpful, they gave me great local tips on where to eat, how to avoid tourist traps, and even where to get the best souvlaki without a 30-minute wait. (Pro tip: trust their food recommendations—they know their stuff!).
10/10, would definitely stay here again if my crippling depression wasn't catching up to me.

In amongst the action. Balconied room overlooking thriving street. Great shower. Housekeeping thorough and daily. Views of Acropolis from top floor breakfast terrace. Very quick and able reception, nothing too much trouble. Great value would recommend highly.

Adrian has a great location. Near Ermou shopping, Metro is very near. Adrian is very helpful.. Can leave bags if needed.... Late flight etc. Breakfast on roof terrace with view of Acropolis is brilliant.. Really lovely. Shower room was OK... Could do with an update but OK for now. Small balcony and aircon.
Room price is v good for the budget... Book... Long weekend in Adrian is worth it.

I’m surprised by the high reviews. My room was so grubby and there were strange stains all over (peeling wallpaper, and yellow stains over the wall, red stain on the light switch, the remote was filthy, and the carpet felt matted). I felt really uncomfortable with how dirty the hotel was.

I missed housekeeping the first day. I asked for water replenishment told me to just “buy some” down the road. I would have had better amenities at an Airbnb.

Two highlights:

1. The location
2. The fresh squeezed orange juice at breakfast is excellent.

I've stayed here on a few occasions; the outside of the Hotel and the reception is rather deceiving. A simple, old reception desk and a reception area with not much space. However, the rooms are clean and comfortable for one or two people, the toilet/shower are clean with complementary shower gel. The Hotel is in an excellent and very central location and you can't beat the price!
Close to all the attractions. Big room with comfortable bed. Rooms are very clean. Shower small. Breakfast is basic but nice. Friendly staff. Great value for the price paid.

The hotel is a genuine 2-star hotel:
- The room was mostly small, and there was not a good air circulation for it to vent
- The elevator did work but didn’t feel 100% reliable/safe
- We got there after 09 pm and the man who checked us in only asked for our names and gave us the key. At no point did they check our passports 🫨

Good thing was that there was people at the front desk 24/7. However, we didn’t feel comfortable enough to ask for suggestions or anything like that

Breakfast is on site, and though there is not a lot of variety, the products are of very good quality

Greek yogurt was the best I’ve ever tried in my life! And the honey was also amazing

They also offered cold cuts and cheese. Coffee, milk, tea, orange juice. And I think there were also some fruits

The staff in charge of breakfast and cleaning the rooms were very friendly

Place looked mostly clean, rooms were made every day and clean towels were left by default

Athens may be filled with soulless Airbnb rentals, each furnished with the same generic IKEA pieces—but Hotel Cecil is a refreshing exception. Like the city itself, Hotel Cecil isn't perfect, but its abundant charm and old-world character easily overshadow any minor imperfections. The prime location, the vintage Otis elevator, and functional WiFi are just added perks. Will be on the top of my list for my next visit to the city.
Smaller rooms but clean and comfortable. Friendly staff. Continental breakfast. Lots of shopping around and close to lots of things to do and see

Fantastic location, close to the Acropolis and other Athens sites.
Breakfast was OK, the same each day but we only had 3 so no problem. Our room was 301 with no view, just a wall 3 metres away and next to the lift, although that didn't seem to be used much so no noise issues.
Staff were friendly and assisted with ordering a taxi for our transfer to the port
Would recommend for a cheap stay in a great location

Just the place. If you are looking for a hotel close to all the history and places to eat.
Staff were friendly and helpful.
Found out it was our anniversary and gave us a bottle of nice vine to enjoy.
Can not say enough nice things about them.

This hotel is great all around it near the acropolis it has great food and it’s very very clean.i liked the stuff there they where very nice and helpful also the view is nice for there.i would definitely recommend this hotel if you are looking of a nice and affordable place to stay in Greece 10/10

If you're looking for a clean and affordable place to stay close to the Acropolis, I think this is the hotel for you. It is clean, close to everything and covers all your needs at an affordable price.
Location is great, near to Acropolis. The price is quite price as for 2 star hotel, but the staff is very friendly and helpful.

This hotel is great if you are staying a couple of nights in Athens. The beds are comfy, the cleaning is great and everyone is super friendly. They will hold your bags if you have a later flight in the evening. The location is sensational to all the laneways which are full of restaurants and shops the atmosphere at night is buzzing. The view from the their rooftop is pretty spectacular too, we grabbed a beer and watch the sunset. Also walking distance to the acropolis.

Excellent Location for visiting Athens. Breakfast was just a delight. Our Room was very comfortable and had a great view from our room over Athens. The added bonus of the Roof Terrace was a wonderful area to relax after a busy day site seeing. The view of the Acropolis in the Evening is just wonderful.

We ended up booking the last available room at this hotel at the last minute after our first hotel was a bad experience, and it was a great decision! Our room had a balcony with an incredible view of the Akropolis and the bed was comfortable. The room was a bit tight, but manageable. There was some noise from the rooftop above and people moving around in the hall, but nothing too bad. The staff was incredibly kind and helpful and the location is convenient to the city center without being too busy. Loved this hotel!

Great hotel, perfect Location, amazing views to the Partenón from the rooms and great service. Anastasia and Anano from the kitchen serve delicious greek breakfast witth an amazingly kind and warm attitude. Only thing to improve is the air conditioning in the breakfast room. It was too warm there, but it should be easy to fix. All other aspects of the hotel are perfect, people in the reception also very kind . Great hotel in a great place !

We stayed at Arion the end of May.  While the room was a little small the bed was very comfortable and the staff was nice and helpful .  The breakfast was great and Anastasia was so accommodating in getting anything you asked for. You cannot beat the location.  Walkable to shopping and eating.  We would definitely stay here again!

The good things about the hotel was the location & the staff.  The room was clean but very small & the bathroom was tiny.  It was almost impossible to shower!  They offer breakfast for a charge but felt it wasn't worth the money.  There is a wonderful bakery on the corner just steps from the hotel.  We enjoyed our stay in Athens but probably wouldn't stay at Athos Hotel on our next visit.

Do not believe in four star reviews for this hotel.  As a solo traveller I decided to stay at this hotel based on the reviews. While the location of the hotel is close to the central location. The beds were ridden with bed bugs and there were cockroaches under the bathroom sink. The bed sheets had stain. It was the most disgusting place I have ever stayed in. The hotel is located in a dark alley and it also took me a while to find it. The staff was most unwelcome. There were also few unwanted characters hanging around the place. A warning for solo women travellers pay a little bit more and be safe. It was also very noisey.

The highly overpriced hotel is run down boasting old and cheap furniture with a gloomy reception and even more gloomy breakfast room.

There is a major noisy construction worksite opposite the hotel which starts at 7am, of course the front desk “forgot” to tell us about when we made the reservation. The superior room which has a glimpse over the Acropolis looks like a cheap standard room in a 2 star motel with a tiny balcony facing the scaffolding.

The only positive comment about this place is its location.
Rooms are extremely small as is the bathroom, but who goes to Greece to sit in the room, Staff are awesome, so is the location . Has a good view from the roof top lounge area.

Small hotel in a quiet yet central location.  Easy access to transportation, restaurants and shopping. Very walkable to many major tourist sites. Staff are very customer focused with an easy approach.

I stayed only one night in this hotel and I wish it was longer! Super friendly staff, and a super cute and cozy room. Rooms have AC and a little fridge. For me the mattress was the perfect firmness. The location couldn't be more perfect either - walking distance to the Acropolis and a crazy amount of nearby cafes and restaurants. I would gladly stay here again when I come back to Athens in the future (because I will be back, what a gorgeous city)

Perfect location for exploring the historic sites of Athens. Our room was small but clean and comfortable. We especially loved sitting on our balcony and admiring the acropolis at night. It is close to lots of restaurants, shops, and metro stations for getting to the airport. We really enjoyed our stay!

Simple hotel perfect for visiting the Acropolis. Balcony overlooking church with bells ringing on Sunday morning. Good air-conditioning. Airy room. Charming lady on reception. Did not try breakfast. Lots of places near by for takeaway coffee, ice cream,  plus restaurants and snacks

Exactly what we were looking for- No more, no less.

My husband and I stayed here for a couple of nights during a trip to celebrate our 7th wedding anniversary. Athens was the first leg of a 3-destination trip, and it was the location we had the least amount of time in, so we wanted to hit the ground running! This hotel worked out absolutely perfectly for this.

The room itself is nice, nothing special. Small, but practical and clean. It had a nice-sized private balcony with a small table and two chairs, and we had a direct view of the Acropolis. After a full day of exploring it was nice to unwind on the balcony with some wine, look at some stars, and listen to the sounds of nightlife.

The main draw that led me to book this hotel was location, location, location! You could not ask for a better area to stay! Walking distance to everything you want and need. Close enough to some nice nightlife, but just far enough away to be able to retire to your room and not be bothered by unwanted outside noise. We ended up buying a 48hr pass for one of the 'hop-on hop-off' busses that stop around the major attractions of Athens, and the hotel was less than a 5min straight walk to one of the stops, so it made getting around the city extremely convenient.

Fancy? No. Extremely practical? Yes.
Overall, it was just what we needed.

Staff are super friendly. The location is perfect, you couldn’t get any closer to the main attractions in Athens. Bathroom is not attached to the room which can be quiet annoying. Otherwise, exactly what you pay for.

Friendly and extremely helpful staff. Lovely rooms with balconies and all the products in the room were of superior quality. A strong lovely shower. The Rooftop breakfast area was gorgeous and peaceful and the selection was really good. Easy access to lots of restaurants and shops, and a short walk from Syntagma SQ and the metro. Highly recommended.

Spent 2 nights earlier this week, week of 21 Oct 2024. My main appreciation is for the people who work there. Extremely helpful and polite. Room was very nice (we were upgraded at check in) as was the complimentary breakfast, with Lavazza, which itself speaks volumes. Best shower I have had in any hotel in 35 years.
Recommended without hesitation, please do stay there when in Athens.
Room was exceptional in every way. Decor subtle yet purposeful.  Rena was a bright light at the breakfast,  her service and personality were super.
Our room faced the front and got a little loud in the evening when trying to sleep. I am a light sleeper so it woke me up. I would still stay there again because of the location.
Great location, breakfast was wonderful, staff very friendly, rooms clean.

Basic but reasonably priced and clean hotel in an excellent location. The room was small but had everything you need. Unfortunately our air conditioning did not work on the second night which made for a very uncomfortable night. The hotel were very apologetic though and offered to pay for our taxi to the airport to compensate which was very much appreciated. The staff were really friendly and I would definitely stay here again. There is also a nice little cafe opposite and a terrace upstairs, from which you can see the Acropolis.

This was perfect for us, easy access to all of the main sights in the centre of Athens. Room was not the biggest but was OK, bathroom was good. A very warm welcome and introduction to the hotel services and gaining entry through the front door. Good level of room cleaning on request.

I stayed in the hotel for 2 nights. It’s very simple, but super clean, staff is very nice and location is amazing. We were able to do all tourist attractions walking. The only fyi is to consider it has 3 floors and only stairs. It was not an issue for me and my family, but something to think about if you have reduced mobility. I’d stay again 100%. Thanks for the help calling the taxi to the airport!

We absolutely loved this location in Athens! The room was perfect for our one night, the air conditioning worked well and there was even a large balcony. The service we received was also excellent- the receptionist carried our luggage up the stairs for us- and it was fairly quiet considering we were in a busy city.

I don’t understand why they say one star hotel. More like three. Truly excellent location. Close neighbour of the Archbishop of Greece. Nice terrace on the top you can use. Very helpful staff. Good size room and big shower.

Conveniently located and no worries with luggage they will help you carry it. My room was right next to the shared kitchen which sucked cause the walls are definitely thin and you hear everything. If you can get a room away from the kitchen it may be quieter but expect to hear everybody’s business. Overall staff was helpful and friendly.

Great location since it is so close to all the restaurants and shops at Plaka, but it does have some drawbacks.

At first, we had a hard time finding it with google maps since Plaka is a cobblestone area with tight shops and buildings all around so it doesn't seem like your regular squared out streets with easily recognizable  landmarks.

There is no obvious entryway or signage (a plaque on the wall). There are 2 sets of french style wooden doors with doorbells and keypads attached. The first set leads to the reception area where you fill out a form, and they make a copy of your passport. Then they lead you back out to the second set of door where you use a pin to get inside to your actual rooms.

It is a modernized older building with many of its original charm so it does have air conditioning and a modern bathroom but be ready to walk up and down a spiraling three flights of stairs to your second floor room.

I wished I had booked their room with a balcony but don't remember if it was unavailable, or maybe I just wanted a cheaper room. I am paying less than $500 Euros for 3 nights and it includes all the free 2 liter water bottles you need (they have a small community kitchen where they have complimentary tea, coffee and water).

Our room is in the corner so we have two 6 feet tall french door style windows with original wood and hardware (so be gentle when closing them since both sides have to meet in the middle to close properly unlike newer styles where you close one first and the other side seals it or others where they close independently from each other). They meet in the middle, so one fits into the groove of the other, and then you twist the window handlebar to lock it.

The room key is an actual key that you have to use on the inside in order to lock your door (no twisting mechanism for easy locking). The advantage of this style of lock is not locking yourself out of your room with the keys left inside.

The doors of both the building and rooms are old, so it did feel a bit loose.

Since we are two old ladies, we couldn't possibly take our luggage up 3 flights of a spiral staircase so the two young gentlemen at the reception were very kind to help us. The tricky part is when we have to check out, I fear we might have to bother them again for help. I feel this type of lodging is best for younger people who travel light while staying to enjoy Plaka.

Air conditioning is a split system, and it is off, so turn it on with a high fan setting if you plan to rest within the hour of unpacking. You can always leave it off and just enjoy the fresh air by opening your windows, but be careful with the curtains on windy or rainy days.

The perk is being able to see the Acropolis right outside our window, and when you open your windows, you can smell all the delicious food from the restaurants downstairs.

I would definitely pick this place again if I were only packing a backpack or duffle bag while traveling with my siblings but unfortunately this time I have my mother who has arthritis so she did prefer a traditional hotel with elevators.

We recently had a marvelous stay at the Palladian Home Hotel in Athens. I must say we were pleasantly surprised. I would consider this a boutique hotel with just a handful of rooms. Very, very quant and it just added to our overall vacation experience. The only negative thing I can say is that our room was in the third floor (tight spiral staircase) and there are NO elevators, but even with that the staff helped us with our luggage coming and going. The staff was just outstanding and in particular, Panos. He was super friendly, accommodating and so very helpful.

Highly recommend this beautiful little gem, tucked away a stones throw from the Acropolis. Staff attentive, polite and nothing was too much bother. A great base for exploring all the local shops and for getting to the Acropolis early. Comfortable bed and a gorgeous shower.

Good location, the desire of the employees to provide service is good. Everything else is bad! The staff's professionalism in providing service is not good, 4 times we checked with the staff about ordering a taxi for a certain day and they said that everything is already arranged, a minute before you have to get into the taxi, the staff does not see the order, there is no order. under any criticism. The maintenance conditions are not good, when you take out the shower tube you cut your hand as you can see in the picture.

Everything about this apartment is fantastic—the location, the facilities, and the spacious terrace where you can enjoy the lively music from the tavern downstairs while people-watching.

The team of the property is the most amazing, welcoming and friendly team! 🫰

The apartment has everything you need for a comfortable stay, and it's ideally located near bars, restaurants, dance clubs, Monastiraki station, and sightseeing spots. Just bring earplugs for the noise, and you'll sleep perfectly! We had a wonderful time and would love to return.

We wanted somewhere that would showcase the best of Athens and Live in Athens did just this. Our accomodation was impeccable, clean and comfortable. It would us at the heart of life in Athens. Our kids loved it and so did we. We could walk to all the main attractions. We were surrounded by hundreds of restaurants and bars. We fell asleep listening to the nightlife of Athens. The noise never bothered us. It made us feel part of the life in Athens.

Cody was our best friend. Full of advice. He never let us done once and his advice made a different to our stay.

Wonderful hotel and extremely good value for money. The bed was large and very comfortable and the apartment was large and very clean. They have a stove and microwave so you can cook food. Amazing location in the heart of the city. Will stay here the next time I am in Athens!

Can't go wrong with these apartments. Part of the trend, it's situated at the very centre of Athens.

You're literally at the center point to reach any destination you wish to whilst visiting Athens.

Don't forget to have a look around all the bars and eateries dotted around.

The people who work for Live in Athens are excellent. They’re responsive, clear and kind.

The apartment is also pleasant.

If you don’t party late into the evening though (I’m writing this after 1am listening to drunks cackle, hoot and holler), don’t stay in the Psyri apartment. In addition to a very lively night life (could be a plus for you) they have a space on their roof where you can take your alcoholic drinks. I’m listening to people make a lot of noise scrapping chairs and tables right now right above my bedroom.  If you want to sleep, choose another one of their locations.

It exceeded all my expectations. First and foremost, the location of the apartment was superb. Situated in the heart of Athens, walking distance to Acropolis, Plaka. Airport bus stop is just 3 min walk. The apartment itself was nothing short of fantastic. Sleek design, clean, spacious bathroom, kitchen equipped with everything we need. We were early when we arrived, the host quickly cleaned up the apartment (as someone just checked out) and let us check in early. Additionally I'd like to highlight the extraordinary assistance provided by the host, Elena. I absentmindedly left behind my pouch (containing ID, bank cards, $100 British pound) in the apartment upon check out. She diligently packaged it and arranged for courier delivery to Singapore, my current location. The host's swift action, coupled with their willingness to assist even after my departure, truly impressed me. I am immensely grateful for their efforts and cannot recommend this apartment highly enough.

The apartment is more than amazing, with a thought in every single detail. It's sparkling clean, cozy, fully equipped and really stylish. The location is more than perfect - close to everything. The owner Elena is really nice and helpful, gives advice how to spend time in Athens, recommends restaurants, bars and beaches. She helped us with everything that she could and we are very thankful to this wonderful person. Thank you very much for everything Elena! We spent amazing week and we recommend Super Stylish Apartments in Syntagma Square as a perfect place to stay in the heart of Athens.

We recently spent a week in the Super Stylish Apartments in Syntagma Square, Athens. The lodging was excellent. Very clean, comfortable, kitchenette, spotless bathroom. The location was perfect and the staff was extraordinarily kind and helpful. Highly recommended!

A real gem!
SSA ticks all the boxes.
The apartment is clean, practical, quiet, very reasonably priced and its location could not be more central. Walking distance to most of Athens's landmarks and surrounded by an amazing array of restaurants and bars.
The host is welcoming and very helpful.

We had a fantastic weekend in this lovely, confortable and tidy apartment 2 years ago and now we are here again! We have another room and we comfirm the previous experience. Very close to the city centre, easy connection with the airport. Elena is the best owner you could find!

Stay away!
Beside the good location, everything is bad.
The room is only suitable for a couple.  Pay attention in the pictures, we got beds that even in a summer camp children gets better. Of course it was never mentinoned. We all have back pain.  Moreover,  the shower isnt clean and old.
We did not receive sheets for the baby bed.  The owner of the place is really not nice!
I would never recommend this place.

Please pay attention to the CHECK-IN time!!!I arrived at the apartment after 12 am, rang the doorbell no response, phone is useless, I guess it is not working hours. So on the first night in Athens, with nowhere to go, I emailed and learned that the apartment refused to cancel the reservation. A very sad and disappointing trip to Greece. On the side of the road very scared, but also by the passing car people water, really terrible😱😱😱😱 Unfortunately I lost 155 euros and felt hopeless on the streets of Athens in the middle of the night, an experience I will never forget😭

Very spacious appartment . Biggest room possible.
The owner was very accomodating
Great large room for 5. Walk to Acropolis. Good prices
Fantastic location!!!

Location can't be better, but still away from noise.
Host is very helpful, self checking using lockbox.
Some things are broken and hope will be fixed, softer towels maybe, but nothing serious.
Acropolis view is from the rooftop only
but still I rate 5 since it was great for our family.

Couldn’t have chosen a better place for my family.  It may have been one night but very memorable, central and clean.

Also amazing view from rooftop

Thank you Anna

Nice clean apartment, key is in a lockbox so checking in is super flexible. Location is incredible, near a lot of good eateries and near the main highstreet.

The view from the communal rooftop of the acropolis is stunning and the atmosphere in the evening is super relaxing. Got to meet some interesting people sharing the building.

Would absolutely stay here again if I came back.

Beautiful house - excellent hosts!
Great location in the center of Athens, close to everything in a walking distance. It is a very clean and well-maintained house.
The hosts are really friendly and made sure that we have the best vacation days in Athens! Thanks!

Well how can I write such a short review to explain what an amazing host Anna is?

I travel a lot, and use Airbnb regularly. So when the experience is so good I really appreciate it.

Anna's service was impeccable from start to finish. From the sudden booking change I had to make because of changing Covid circumstances (she found us a room within 10 minutes!). To any assistance we needed during our months stay. Anna is lightning fast with her responses whilst also being very relaxed host who makes you feel at ease.

The apartments are amazing value for money and some of the nicest places I've stayed. Especially the one in front of Monastiraki square.

Hope to stay at one of these apartments again!

It was great the owner accommodated us with storing our luggage at the main office and the when we arrived to the apartment greater us there. Showed us around the place and the use of the home. From the Washing machine to the hot water for baths. We did report to the owner that the door nobs to several rooms had a issue of falling off or the latch spinning. They responded right away but we let them know that we can work with it until we leave and then they can come fix it for someone else. Over all it was a great place close to many tourists locations.

It was like staying at a 5-star hotel, but it was an entire apartment, walking distance from the Acropolis!

Akis went above and beyond for us. Everything was perfect. He was very kind, provided food, big TV with netflix in 5 different languages, big beds perfectly made, plenty of towels, air condition of course and the apartment was bigger than expected!

Couldn't recommend this place enough and even more so for the customer service. Thank you Aki, we will be back again!
Great location.

The apartment has an ideal location, convenient parking - there is a paid parking lot next to it, and free parking on the street. It is clean and very welcoming. The host is kind and responsive, he had left us breakfast in the fridge.

Excellent well-kept and clean apartment with every service, excellent location for visiting the city. Akis is a fantastic owner, kind and provided us with all the necessary information. I highly recommend it to everyone and if I were to return I would have no doubts. Thank you and congratulations again Akis 👍👍👍
Nice
Excellent apartment with all the necessary amenities. The owners are very cordial.

Mixed feelings about this place.  As many people before me have said the view is great, you can see the Parthenon, really nice at night. The room is nice good size for two, well equipped, with nespresso machines and coffee, cookies, various condiments, as well as soap and shampoo. The room description mentions a safe, but as you will see in the photos it’s bolted to a shelf that you can walk away with. As for the surrounding outside, they say it’s safe, maybe .  The are many homeless people living in the park across the street, many shooting drugs. Before leaving we went and gave them a pair of shoes and a shirt. They were happy but seemed to be in bad shape, very sad. This is just a heads up for those who may not want to see this.

We love this place! Very well located, in the center of the city, very safe zone. The apartment is very clean, nice and cozy. We arrived later than we expected and the host waited us to give us the keys, so nice! We will definitely go back to Athens and we will stay here again.

Very nice furnished Appartments. It’s nice to sit outside at roof terrace with view to Akropolis.
Very friendly owner. Reasonable prices. Uncomplicated and relatable booking.
For us always our perfect stay.
Very nice and spacious apartment in the center of the city equipped with all mod cons.  The view from the balcony is great. You can even see the Parthenon while you enjoy your coffee or your drink.
Very clean, with a great location. The host had printed out information for us and has lockers for your bags after checkout incase you want to explore the city.

Very basic room and hotel with no frills, but couldn't have been more pleased for what it was. Ended up being one of our favourite places we stayed while in Greece. Friendly staff, great neighbourhood and our terrace room had a certain kind of charm to it. If you are looking for a budget place to stay in a nice, quiet neighbourhood, I cannot recommend Art Gallery Hotel enough.

The Art Gallery hotel offers a cozy, family-owned atmosphere where guests are warmly welcomed, almost as if they were visiting relatives. During my three-night stay, I found the rooms to be adequately clean, comfortable, and spacious, albeit with some minor wear and tear on the room and bathroom amenities. For instance, I encountered an issue with the door lock, but the friendly reception staff promptly provided a workaround with a smile, suggesting a clever trick to operate it until it was fixed.

One of the hotel's standout features is undoubtedly its staff, who consistently go above and beyond to assist guests with a genuine kindness that enhances the overall experience. While the breakfast provided was basic, it served its purpose well enough to fuel explorations of Athens' ancient wonders, and it also conveniently provided a snack for later in the morning at nearby restaurants.

The hotel's location is another highlight, situated in a quiet area yet still within easy reach of key attractions like the Plaka and the Acropolis. Despite its proximity to tourist hotspots, the neighborhood maintains a peaceful ambiance, with plenty of excellent dining options and cafes nearby. Moreover, the reasonable pricing adds to the hotel's appeal, offering good value for money considering its prime location and attentive service.

In summary, the Art Gallery hotel provides a pleasant stay characterized by familial hospitality, comfortable accommodations, and a prime location that allows guests to enjoy the best of Athens without feeling overwhelmed by crowds.
Great location, the rooms were clean and spacious, the staff was friendly and attentive, we really enjoyed our short stay and hope to return one day

The rooms aren’t anything special, but they have everything you need and more! The staff are the sweetest, coolest people!! So friendly and warm. I stayed here with my study abroad group multiple times over our 3 week trip since Art Gallery was considered our “home base.” By the end of the trip everyone in my group referred to this place as “home.” I even stayed an extra day and a half here with my boyfriend after my studying was done and he loves it just as much as I do!! Location is SUPERB, breakfast is delicious, but the staff make this place AMAZING! I highly recommend hanging out in the front desk area to get to know the staff and cool off in the AC. I’ll be back soon! ❤️

The hotel has a fascinating history, its foundations having sheltered the current owners’ grandparents as a bunker in WWII before they made it first an art gallery and then, building upward, this hotel. Don’t expect spanking new state-of-the-art ANYTHING. Expect inimitable charm, cleanliness, authenticity, helpful staff, and an excellent location. We loved it.

An excellent boutique located a short walk from all of the major tourist attractions and happening night life districts of Athens. The owner is quite helpful, his English is immaculate, and staff was willing to help us with anything we needed.

You do have to climb a flight of stairs before being able access the elevator, so people with mobility issues should take heed.

I would give the rooms a 4, but the sliding partition wall does not fully separate the two sleeping areas, this may have been a quirk of my room. The view from our terrace of the acropolis was a true highlight, both at night and in the morning.

Overall an excellent value and experience, will definitely stay here again.

We had a lovely stay at the Hellenic Hospitality House, albeit only one night. We stayed in the Zeus room as a couple, and it was HUGE! If it wasn’t one of the only rooms left we probably would have gotten a smaller room, but we loved being on the top deck and being able to see the Parthenon from the balcony. It’s a gorgeous apartment tucked in central Athens, and this room is perfect for a family who wants the ease of walkability around the city. Yes, as others have stated, there are two sets of stairs to walk up to the 1st floor, but there IS a small elevator to take to the 5th floor to check-in. If you’ve ever been to NYC or any other European country and stayed outside of a major hotel, you’ll be fine. If you have limitations with mobility, might not be a good fit.

We had a nice visit with the owner, Chris, who was pleasant and eager to make us feel welcome. We found him to be very reasonable—but it’s best to understand that he runs the operations independently, so he welcomes all guests individually that visit. That said, he has a family of his own to get home to, so best to reach out well in advance if you will arrive later than 7pm. Also, office opens at 10am, so dropping off bags earlier than that isn’t an option. But there are other options in the city to hold bags. We used Krassas Luggage storage around the corner for €5 per bag and it was perfect for us.

We enjoyed our stay and everything was beyond satisfactory! Wish we had more time!

Great experience. The rooms were very clean and comfortable and the location is unbeatable. Kristos, the owner, was very welcoming and helpful with advice, organising taxis, etc. I'd definitely stay there again.

Convenient location for the metro and very close to Acropolis. Has many food options and walking distance to local market, square, metro station, Acropolis, Flea market and roof top bars. Chris was very welcoming and provided many recommendations for first timers Greece travellers like ourselves to enjoy and explore. Clean and cozy rooms.
Great service. The guy who still owns it is very friendly. Straightforward and very central. Not all rooms come with an extravagant view. But the rooms are in top quality.

I truly loved staying at this place, located at a very centric area, close to most restaurants, bars and shopping places. Will definitely stay at their apartments whenever I visit again. The owners are super nice and very friendly. Thank you very much for your hospitality 🙏
Location is just perfect. A bit artistic decor. Very comfortable bed and sitting area. We stayed in apartment Niki. Communication with personnel was top notch.

So glad and fortunate to have found this place. Our original booking in Athens got cancelled last minute We were frantically searching and found this place available and booked it without much research, just going by the pictures posted. I have to say that was the best thing to happen and best way to start our Europe trip. GREAT location, walking distance from the major sight seeing spots. Great location for food, cafes. Extremely helpful and friendly host/manager Ms.Konstantina. A lovely apartment tastefully done. We adults and our kids loved everything about this place!

We had an amazing experience! The apartment is located down town, so we had access to all the bars and shops there.

I have to admit that the apartment was wonderfully decorated and in an excellent condition.

The host, Konstantina, is a lovely person. She helped us a lot and she suggested where to go and what to eat.

I will definitely come back next time I am going to visit Athens! I highly recommend it to everyone!

Great, very central location. Walking distance to everything. Perfect for 5 adults or 2 couples and some kids. Comfortable couch bed could reasonably sleep 2 (so 6 adults could stay comfortably if on a budget)

We had an amazing experience at this hotel. Our flight arrived very early in the morning and they offered us complimentary breakfast right when we arrived. Our room was also ready by 9:30 am! They were incredibly accommodating and had great recommendations. The hotel is located on a beautiful street and is very central. We will definitely be back.

This hotel is a gem!  It's beautiful, quiet, the rooms are lovely, it's in a great location, and every member of the staff is so helpful and friendly.  The breakfast and coffee were delicious.  I would stay here again in a heartbbeat, and I would highly recommend it to anyone coming to Athens.  And a special thank you to Marietta, for taking such good care of us.

We spent 4 nights at the Lozenge Hotel Athens before joining a cruise - it was the perfect place for our short stay. The hotel is located in a small mostly pedestrian street within walking distance of Syntagma Square and the metro. Our room was a very decent size with a lovely bathroom. The service was exceptional - Vasia, Marietta and the whole team were extremely friendly and helpful. We really enjoyed our stay there and can highly recommend it!

Awesome place.
Close to everything centre of Athens
Front desk lady Yota was awesome.
Restaurant staff very attentive.
Would definitely stay there again.

Our recent stay at Hotel Lozenge was nothing short of exceptional. The moment we walked through the doors, we were greeted by a warm and inviting atmosphere that instantly made me feel at home. The attention to detail in every aspect of the hotel, from the beautifully decorated rooms to the impeccable service, was truly remarkable. The staff esp. Jenny at the restaurant and Marietta at the front desk were incredibly attentive, helpful and warm. They went out of their way to ensure that our every need was met. The hotel's central location made it convenient for exploring the city. Overall, our experience at Hotel Lozenge was unforgettable, and I can't wait to return for another incredible stay.

The hotel staff were all incredibly friendly and welcoming, especially this lovely lady Miss Xenia who helped me split my day tour into two days according to my flight schedule. She even allowed me to check out later, so I didn't have to rush for the flight. I could come back, take a nice shower, rest, and pack. The breakfast and room service food were delicious, and the rooftop pool offered an amazing view. There were also many great restaurants nearby. Staying here for a week felt just as comfortable as being at home.

We arrived after our early morning flight and the bellman were very polite and took our baggage straight to storage but the front desk was quite rude to us about how early we arrived - I didn’t ask to check in and didn’t expect to, so there was no need to be rude.  We only had 1 day in Athens so being well located to the main sites was important.  This hotel was walkable to everything including the Parthenon.  After a long day of exploring we came back to the hotel for check in and were surprised with an upgrade to a large suite and a welcome drink.  We had to wait in line for quite a while because the front desk decided to let a group of over 10 flight attendants check in before us despite us having been in line before them
And for a long time.  The only good customer service we got was the woman who checked us in.  She was polite, accommodating and apologetic for the wait.

Our upgraded room huge!  Most importantly, it’s clean and comfortable. One thing that bothered me about the room and service though was that while we were out to dinner the cleaning staff came in and made our beds and left waters on our pillows.  We thought that was such a nice gesture.  Upon checkout we learned that the waters were 10 euro each . I was quite annoyed by this and noted to the front desk they should have been at the mini bar or a note included with the price.  I wouldn’t have drank otherwise.  All our other hotels provided complimentary water so we assumed this was the same.  She was very rude about it but ultimately did not charge us for which I was appreciative.

Also, the rooftop view of the city is wonderful!

Terrible experience. This is a 2 star hotel, at best. Let me take you on this stay with us. We arrived 4 hours before check in time and did not expect to be able to get into the room until 3.   Upon arrival the front desk told us they could make that work and it would only take an hour, which was music to our ears after a multiple day travel with no sleep. We headed up to the incredibly mediocre pool to wait for the room. An hour went past and we headed back downstairs to check if our room was ready, we were turned away again with a promise of 30 minutes. Repeat that process another 5 times until our check in time. If they would have told us from the beginning, “no so sorry can’t check in until 3” we wouldn’t have had a problem with that but being lied to for 4 hours straight was not great. On top of that, when we went down to check in at 3 the line was incredibly long and filled with grumpy people with the same issue and the issue of not being able to check in even after 3. Once we finally got into our room the beds were as hard as a rock. In terms of room it was decent. This was just a 2 night stop of our trip, but if the room is booked for 3 people maybe include more than 1 towel? Also perhaps replace the 1 towel when you clean the room? Feels obvious. I was then harassed multiple times by a employee that thought he left his phone in the room. He was aggressive and very creepy. I’m a young woman and he would wait until I was along in the room to knock on the door to ask if I had found a phone. The service workers in the restaurant were decent, until our buffet interaction. The man at the front was working with maybe 1 brain cell. I told him multiple times it was 3 people and he charged for 2. When my boyfriend joined us he was accused of “stealing” because there was a miscommunication. But ya know, obviously we should panic first and be rude to guests and accuse them of stealing gross food while literally sitting at the table. Glad to be leaving.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Divani Caravel hotel during a business trip, and I wanted to take a moment to express my gratitude for the exceptional service I received.

I would like to extend a special thank you to your concierge, Xenia, who was incredibly helpful with every question and request. Her professionalism and attentiveness truly enhanced my stay.

Additionally, I want to give my sincere thanks to Dimitra, who was the kindest receptionist I have ever encountered. Her warmth and friendliness made a lasting impression.

Thank you once again for making my stay so pleasant.

Excellent experience!  Flight cancelled and sent to Devini Caravel. Gorgeous reception and beautiful room. Greek people so friendly and kind. Took very well care for us. Perfect setting in safe area. View from the pool of Acropolis and St George church. Food and service plentiful and diverse.  Would come back to this hotel over and over.

What an incredible hotel! My favorite place to stay in Athens. Nothing better than having a meal or a drink and enjoying this gorgeous view. The rooms are so nice, the pool is incredible, and the staff is so helpful and accomidating. We've already stayed there 2 times and we'll be coming back asap!

Amazon invited us to this hotel for a networking dinner. It was great. The food was tasty, the view was breathtaking, and the staff was very kind. The location is difficult, but as you can see in my photos, you can see as far as Piraeus to the sea.

We would like to give a special thank you to Panagiota, lady at the swimming pool. Such a nice and helpful lady speaking fluently in English.

Also thanks to the lady who cleaned every day our room. We don’t know her name, she was very friendly.

Regret we had a very poor experience from beginning to end.  There was no welcoming hospitality from staff and deputy manager during check in.

We booked 8 rooms and all of our rooms were at back side of hotel, next to air condition units and were very noisy. At midnight, there was a electricity cut and we woke up with huge generator noise. (By the way, generator was only for main hotel places but not for the rooms.) We had no power and air condition for few hours in our rooms. None of us could sleep during the night.

In the morning they didn’t even come to us and apologies for the inconvenience or offered any complimentary for an excuse. Besides they just made our check outs like nothing happened also charged for coffees we had during check out process.

Overall they don’t deserve 5 star  and don’t act like that at all for sure.

Don’t suggest anyone to have this bad experience. There are way better hotels in Athens which you can get what you paid for.

Hotel was great and in a cool location. The rooftop pool is amazing and we spent a lot of time up there. Unfortunately the service at the rooftop bar was pretty bad. They didn't have a lot of cold options, our drinks were not always made well, etc.

In June of 2024, we stayed for a week at the Periscope Hotel in the  Kolonaki section of  Athens, Greece. The Hotel  is in a great location just a few blocks from a Metro Station, in a quite area with lots of shops and restaurants within walking distance.   It is a boutique style hotel so it is small but very clean and beautiful. They have a wonderful breakfast buffet that converts to a 24 hour complementary snack bar which was a fantastic bonus.  Our room was very nice, clean and had a sliding glass door that gave the room lots of natural light.  Nikos is the manager and was always concerned that everything was perfect for our stay.  The the hotel staff includes Anastasia, Nelli and Diana.  They are a very profession staff, yet they were also very nice, friendly and helpful.  Whether we needed directions, restaurant suggestions or just a quick question, they were always happy to assist. They will go out of their way to make sure that you are comfortable and that you have a memorable stay in Athens.  I highly recommend this hotel for anyone planning a stay in Athens Greece.

I spent two nights at this sharp hotel in the Kolonaki neighborhood and I enjoyed my time very much. It is small, only about twenty rooms, but nicely designed and in a great location near to many upscale shops and restaurants, but yet still a quiet neighborhood feel. In fact, it feels as if you might be staying at the apartment of a friend (who happens to be a designer). The staff is so gentle and personable - so much so that I looked forward to seeing them each day. All smiles. I think this place is a little gem. Very cool. Thank you!

We truly enjoyed our recent stay at the Periscope Hotel in Athens. Everything was top-notch: the location, the staff, the room and amenities and the breakfast. We not only highly recommend this property, we plan to come again!

The service at Periscope Hotel is probably the best that we have had during our trips in Greece. All the staff members are amazing, always nice, keen to help and assist.
We even got upgraded on our second night there, after returning from the islands.
We will definitely stay back here when we come back to Athens.
In addition, the hotel is nicely decorated, clean, tidy and within walking distance to Plaka and the Acropolis.
There are also lots of nice restaurants around.
Best place to stay when you are visiting Athens!

Staff here is amazing, welcoming, and so helpful.  After long flight delays and lost luggage, they helped us settle in, got us directions to various stores and pharmacies to purchase needed items.  They constantly checked in on us to ensure we had everything we needed.  The staff at Periscope are a great testament to the kindness and generosity of the Greek people.  That, and the rooms were super comfortable, clean, and beds/shower were amazing

It can’t get any better than this. The view alone is worth staying here. Add to that elegant rooms and common areas, attentive staff and delicious food and you have the perfect mix. It is close to everything. Rooms are immaculate. The gym is small but, from what I can see, the hotel doesn’t target that clientele so it’s ok .. I would definitely recommend and come back here!

Everything is extraordinary about this historic hotel in the heart of Athens, near Syntagma Square, just opposite the Greek Parliament. Even if you don’t (choose to) stay there, you should visit the Spa, which offers wonderful treatments in a calm, cosy, yet luxurious atmosphere. A brunch or an afternoon tea at the Winter Lounge complete the pleasurable experience. And of course there is the Roof Garden restaurant with a breathtaking view.

Beautiful hotel with nice facilities and spa area. My gripes are:
(1) the service wasn’t as refined as I would hope for a hotel of this class. Not much greeting or going out of their way for guests.
(2) my wife’s spa experience was sub standard. Her back was bruised and sensitive for days after a massage, and her hair blowout didn’t turn out great compared to spas back home.

I rarely write reviews, but felt compelled to as the front desk staff really need to up their game. My partner and I stayed for a night recently and upon arrival the first question the receptionist asked was what time we were checking out the next day. I was slightly thrown by this but didn't take offence though am increasingly irritated that high end hotels expect an 11am check out, and not midday. But more on this later.

Our flight had been delayed so we arrived at the hotel at about 3pm hungry for lunch. We asked the receptionist where we might eat and she said that the restaurant on the 8th floor was full. We said OK, but can we maybe get a snack at the pool bar. She also said that wasn't possible. Rather puzzled we went to our room and then decided to explore. Upon entering the pool area we saw an operational kitchen and were greeted by friendly staff and duly had a pleasant bite to eat, confused that the receptionist had tried to put us off going!

The next morning at 10.30am as we were getting ready to leave our room there was a ring on the doorbell obviously to chivy us along. But not only that a few minutes later the phone also rang (with nobody at the other end when I picked up). I could understand it if it was after 11am but I really take issue with this sort of behaviour in any hotel let alone one that is notionally the best in Athens.

As a hotel it's OK, nothing particularly special. We moved onto the Four Seasons on the coast which is in a completely different league and I would thoroughly recommend.

Old world elegance with modern luxury service. The guest rooms have maintained their old school appointments, but the upstairs lounge and bar are very much updated with modern decor. The rooftop bar/restaurant is definitely the highlight, although evening cocktails are 24 euro, so you're paying for the view and ambiance.  The breakfast is 5 star in every way; we booked a rate that included breakfast and it was definitely worth it.

Very nicely located hotel. We had an apartment and double room. Both spotless clean. An apartment with a view, cozy and spacious rooms. There was no washing up liquid, a sponge or a cloth in the kitchen. That would be nice to have when you stay only for a short period of time. Only thing I did not like was breakfast. Eatable but not the best one.

Ideal for families that have 4-6members, decent breakfast with green dishes, close to the most posh area in Athens - near Kolonaki- , metro 5min walk.
I recommend as best option for those also that they like the ‘70-80s- decoration which gives a sense of quality and simplicity!!!
Fair price compared to other hotels around 🤓

Staying at Delice apartments was very pleasant, the apartments were very spacious with everything that you would need. They were cleaned well every day. The location was also excellent, very close to the Evangelismo metro station and only a stop or two to the centre of the city. Would definitely recommend!

Good hotel for a short stay near the metro in Athens.
Rooms are neat although very simple.
The breakfast is good but would need a few improvement touches to make it even better.
Overall the experience was very good although I would expect from a 4* hotel more amenities and a wider variety of products available for breakfast.

Best bedroom mattress I've EVER slept on! Staff were extremely nice!! They got us a taxi when we needed one and the taxi showed up on time! Room was huge! Very happy with Delice and if I'm ever in that neighborhood, I will definitely stay there again!!!

Nice hostel, it’s small and the atmosphere is really cool. The location is good, you can walk to the city center and you can also take the bus. We had an amazing welcoming and lot of advices (thanks to you George you’re amazing). I recommend this place, people are lovely !

A very cool spot just outside of the city centre. The hang out social space is awesome for cooking and getting to know the rest of the guests and closes at an appropriate time to allow everyone to sleep comfortably.

The staff were very helpful and welcoming. George and Terry were always up for a chat and full of information and ideas about Athens and life in general.

I stayed for 3 nights at the end of a cycle tour through Greece and it was the perfect way to finish my trip. The rooms were well set up, had plenty of fans and locker space. Bathrooms were clean and there are laundry facilities if you need them.

I highly recommend this place and will be visiting again!

I recently stayed at this hostel in Athens, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. The place was cozy, friendly, and exactly as described online. Most of the staff were kind and helpful, but I had an uncomfortable experience with one employee named George.

George seemed to have a habit of drawing attention to himself, interrupting guests, and engaging in what felt like a strange flirtation with younger female guests. While these behaviors were off-putting, the situation that most affected my rating occurred on my last day.

A girl had fainted in the kitchen, and I stayed to help her until the emergency patrol arrived. George, however, insisted that I leave the room. I understand that the kitchen was small and crowded, so I eventually complied. However, the way he demanded it was disrespectful and abrupt, which made me feel unappreciated despite trying to help.

When I was leaving the hostel, George approached me at the exit door, and we began discussing what had happened. I calmly explained that his attitude might discourage guests from returning. In response, he violently slammed the heavy metal door in my face. While I can understand reacting emotionally in a stressful situation, such violence is inexcusable.

I initially planned to give this hostel a 4-star rating, but due to this incident, I’m giving it 2 stars instead. I hope George takes the time to work on managing his emotions and avoiding such aggressive actions in the future.

I’ve stayed in over a hundred hostels during my travels, and this is the first time I’ve ever experienced something like this.

Great hostel! Easy to get to with public transport or walking from the centre of Athens. The staff are super friendly, approachable and easy to talk to. Rooms are clean and it’s not noisy at night. Would definetly stay here again!!!

one of the best hostels i’ve stayed at. The staff really makes the place. Me and my friend stayed over a year ago and still regularly wonder how george and terry are doing. (hence why i’m leaving this review)

AMAZING FUN SAFE WELCOMING,, warm vibes all around

Was put up here by an airline after a cancelled flight, so can’t really complain since I didn’t pay for this. But comfortable rooms, good facilities, and their version of room service is a great idea - a selection of local eateries offering some of their popular dishes available for delivery, with the reception calling you when it’s down there ready for pickup. Unfortunately, only down side is the walls are paper thin, so sounds from hallways and neighbouring rooms are clear as day. Good selection for (inclusive) breakfast, however mini bar options were pretty poor.

Highly recommend this hotel. Fantastic room, spotlessly clean, great staff and a lovely breakfast. Free mini bar and drink voucher for bar. Close to local amenities. Will be re booking next time we are in Athens

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Ilisia Hotel. From start to finish, my experience was exceptional. The staff was friendly, attentive and always ready to assist, the rooms were very comfortable and tastefully decorated, the location is conveniently located at the heart of downtown. I recommend it, it’s a hidden gem that combines comfort, convenience, and outstanding service.

Fantastic location, good rooms and big balcony’s with a table and chairs. My room was very cosy with lots of wardrobe space, tv, desk with usb plugs. This hotel impressed me the most because the staff were absolutely lovely and couldn’t do enough for you.

The staff are very caring and attentive.

Great breakfasts.

Stocked complimentary minibar is a nice touch.

Single rooms are a bit small, and rooms immediately next to the marble stairwell are a bit noisy.

Great location, near shopping and touristic areas. The service is exceptional, professional and efficient. We found the room to be a bit small but it was comfortable, quiet and modern. The roof top restaurant with a view on the acropolis was excellent. They accommodated to our needs and the staff was very helpful. This helps relax and take in the atmosphere of Athens.

We are currently staying at the Amalia Hotel in Athens Greece. This hotel is wonderful. All the staff have been so helpful and nice. Location is in the Plaka neighborhood. So close to the best shopping area and historic sites. The Whisper rooftop restaurant bar has great prices, view of the Acropolis and staff. Air conditioned, clean room and mini fridge. Great price! Don’t over look this one!

Perfect spot. Everything is close enough to walk to and if you need to spend some time in the evening and don’t know what to do the park right across the street is so nice.
The dinner here is so good, and the service as well.

Showers are very small🫠🫣

3 stars, definitely not 4.
I don't know if they have renovated rooms but mine was extremely outdated with old, stained carpets. Bed was a little worn down but not HORRIBLE.
Very convenient location to shops and restaurants. Right across a nice park

Such warm hospitality! One of the best experiences I’ve had with a hotel EVER. The location is very much central and the crew is initiative, friendly and caring. If you see Andreas you know you’re in good hands!

Great hotel! We really enjoyed our time here. Our room was large and the balcony was also a good size - it included 2 chairs and a table. The air conditioning worked well, thankfully, as it was very hot when we were there. The location was very convenient - plenty of nearby restaurants. We loved having a nice cocktail at the rooftop bar - beautiful views! Hotel staff were welcoming and very helpful. I highly recommend staying here!

We stayed 3 nights at New Hotel as we explored the city of Athens. It was a wonderful experience. The staff was friendly and helpful. The rooms were beautiful and very clean. I loved the decor of the hotel, the use of found pieces of furniture, cabinetry and wood on the walls was something that I had never seen before and it was so interesting and really pretty. The breakfast was wonderful and we had drinks on the rooftop bar overlooking the acropolis, a truly special experience. The location of the hotel was great for seeing the sights as well.

We have stayed at the New Hotel the last several years when we have been in Athens.  Convenient location with clean and comfortable rooms.  The staff is extremely friendly and accommodating.  We always sample the rooftop bar and they always have interesting concoctions.  Plan to come back next year

Fantastic experience at the best location walking distance to everything in Athens at a much better price point than the bougier options closer to Parliament. Staff was beyond accommodating - we stayed at beginning and end of our Greek trip, leaving some bags with the hotel, they were great with local info (needed a haircut and some car service to the airport - they came through with both on short notice), and the rooftop bar restaurant craft cocktails and food were great. Highly recommend!

I loved the design of this hotel. Our room had a great view and balcony. The breakfast was also very delicious. It’s very close to the acropolis and the surrounding area is very beautiful and fun to walk around. We had a great time!

My wife and I have just returned from Greece and stayed at this magnificent hotel on the first few nights and again on the last night. We could not have been happier! The rooms were clean and spacious with large comfortable beds, bathrooms were spotless and had thick towels, nice slippers and robes to enjoy. More importantly, I need to mention the kindness and professionalism of the staff!  Every one of them. Congratulations to the management for excellent training. Upon check in I was greeted by Dora who made me feel comfortable and welcome from the start. We asked her for dinner location suggestions and she offered many outstanding choices. She worked long hours. The bell staff was always willing to go out of their way to help. The breakfast was more than anyone could eat, and an extravagant display of so many items! Our rooms had balcony's and a great view of the city landscape. I enjoyed standing on them and watching the changing of the guards!  And finally on the last day Manio was so kind with our checkout. Sad to leave, but with fond memories of an enjoyable stay!

We actually checked in on the day after we were supposed to. My travel agents fault. No problem, they moved the day of check in and off we went. Took all of 5 minutes to correct and we were given a beautiful room with a view of the Acropolis. Rarely will you stay at such a clean, friendly and generous place. And even the bed was incredible. Would definitely stay again. Thank you.

A nice hotel with excellent service. The location is close to everything you would want to see in Athens! Rooms were pretty clean but they are a little dated. The breakfast had a good selection of items from bacon, sausage, eggs, fruit and pastries. The only comment on the breakfast would be the coffee and cappuccinos didn't taste great. Overall a nice hotel if you're visiting Athens!

The rooms are large! Rooms are clean! The staff is very accommodating. We enjoyed a breakfast buffet. There is a bar open in the evening on the mezzanine level.  There is a restaurant in the lobby.  Be aware that this hotel does not have a pool. This hotel was picked as part of our tour. Overall this hotel is a good choice.

Probably the noisiest hotel I have ever stayed at. The bedroom doors are very thin and you can hear housekeepings every move knock and bang, even the noise of plastic bags rustling. I’m sure another room or floor may have been different but even 6 floors up no peace! I leave my room impeccable and do not use all the towels and clutter available, so how about some mutual respect in the morning, smashing vacuum cleaners against the wall is war! Very clean room on arrival.

Nice details!!! A little chocolate on your pillow, Nescafe, tea, drinks, air freshener, everything new, well thought out, I liked it. The wifi works perfectly, the hot water wonderful, the mattress divine. Cons, the bathroom gets all wet and when you come back in after showering, it's a disaster.

great alternative to a hotel, in the best place in Athens. Very calm and and easy access to any point of the city, thanks to the nearby Metro station. The prices in comparison to a hotel are very good. Some people may not like the place as the interiors haven't been renovated since the opening in the 70s. I personally love the rosewood elements, it's very authentic and vintage. The apartments are spacious, with a bedroom, a living room and a separate kitchen. The balcony is enormous, with an unobstructed view to the city and mount Emitos. In front of the hotel, there's a church and office building related to it. it's really quiet.

I stayed here while looking for a last minute booking after I had to extend my stay in Athens for 1 more day. Although the location was nice and the staff were friendly, I have to say I was a bit disappointed upon entering the room due to how old the room and furniture were...and I don't mean old as in vintage classic way. The carpet had stains accumulated from 20 years and the furniture/walls/kitchen/overall interior looked like a cheap motel from the 1960's. I made the booking based on the 4-star description I saw on Google. Little did I know, those were user review 4 stars and not classification of the hotel as a 4-star hotel.

It's has good position, but it's still 40min by foot to Acropolis. The biggest problem of this hotel - it's really old: hard to open or close balcony or windows and stuff like that. But it's nice place to crash for few nights.

Large apartment, clean, well-equipped and with 24-hour reception. The location is excellent, walking distance to two train stations, and to the city centre. A safe neighbourhood. I will stay here again.

Very old. No renovation since 19xx? However Room is very big and clean.wifi. Hot water. Kitchen. And balcony over look city. Location is OK. When we stayed may 1. No aircon it's hot daytime. Big problem for us.

The hotel boasts a fantastic location and has recently undergone renovations. While the rooms may be smaller compared to other establishments in the same price range and area, the staff and service team are exceptionally friendly and accommodating. I would recommend this hotel for short stays, such as one or two nights.

While the hotel has been renovated the suite we had was comprised of several small rooms with a gigantic and essentially useless dressing/closet area.  The light switches were incredibly  confusing and I'm a 30 year construction person.

The bar next door had great views although the bartender  thought a martini meant martini & rossi

We stayed here before and after a Ritz Carlton Cruise. The location is absolutely fantastic. Book a balcony room for sure. The changing of the guard at the Palace captured our attention every single time we were in the room and the views of the Acropolis are magnificent. The location is also quite convenient for sightseeing, shopping, and dining options.

The 3 stars comes from the less than impressed experience we had with one very pompous maitre d, George. We returned from a day of sightseeing and shopping and stopped at the front desk to ask where we might get a glass of wine. They directed us to the (only) bar in the hotel that happens to be the Michelin starred restaurant. We went up to the bar only to be given the once-over by George and turned away because my husband was wearing shorts- now keep in mind, it was July and temperature reached the upper 90’s if not over 100 degrees Fahrenheit. We went back to our room; he changed into long pants and we returned. We sat at the bar and finally enjoyed the glasses of wine. We watched other couples arrive. Apparently baseball caps, tshirts, and jeans are appropriate dress whereas khaki shorts and a collared shirt are not. I think the whole experience was compounded by the fact that the front desk saw our attire and directed us to the bar- as well as the fact that George could have handled the whole situation with a lot more tact and a lot less arrogance.

The best experience I’ve ever had in a hotel. Between the King George and their sister property next door, my wife and I were able to explore all the amenities- spa, pool, sauna, steam rooms, both gyms, and more. Everything was top quality and exceptional. We said multiple times that this hotel greatly exceeded our expectations in every way. When we booked it we knew it was nice, but the feeling of grandeur, the amenities, and the staff made it perfect. Every single person we interacted with was incredibly kind and willing to help with any question or request. Lastly, we ate at Tudor Hall one night (Michelin star restaurant on 7th floor of hotel)  and it was some of the best food I’ve ever had. Can’t recommend this hotel and its people any more highly.

We had late flight out of Athens and our plan was to site see all day but it ended up being 100 degrees Fahrenheit that day. They even close the acropolis. We called to see if we could get a day use room and they were completely booked. The staff and manage can't say NO. The service found a way to provide us with a room until 5pm and we were so grateful. Best staff and great rooms. Really really good location and the restaurant has an amazing view of the city.

Beautiful apartment set in one of the most stunning areas of Athens.  Definitely one of the most amazing apartments I stayed in whilst visiting Athens and definitely one of my favourite areas of Greece.  Booking and access was easy and the apartment contained everything I needed for my stay.  Loved it and would definitely stay there again.

Simply the best! Chic decorated, fully equipped, crystal clean apartment in a building of famous architects in the posh area of Athens (Kolonaki), near to all important sightseeing. Loved every corner of it!

Μodern luxurious apartment amazingly decorated and equipped, completely clean and provided with all necessary. Perfectly located in the posh neighborhood of Kolonaki, in 5-20 min walking distance from all major sightseeing of the city. Highly recommended!
Simply the best apartment of Athen's centre I have ever been! Amazing decoration, luxuriously furnished, fully equipped! Best ever!
Fabulous,highly designed,spotless clean and convinient apartment,in walking distance of all important sightseeing of Athens,plus the best host ever!We had a great time and for sure we will come back!

We had an amazing experience at this hotel. Our flight arrived very early in the morning and they offered us complimentary breakfast right when we arrived. Our room was also ready by 9:30 am! They were incredibly accommodating and had great recommendations. The hotel is located on a beautiful street and is very central. We will definitely be back.

This hotel is a gem!  It's beautiful, quiet, the rooms are lovely, it's in a great location, and every member of the staff is so helpful and friendly.  The breakfast and coffee were delicious.  I would stay here again in a heartbbeat, and I would highly recommend it to anyone coming to Athens.  And a special thank you to Marietta, for taking such good care of us.

We spent 4 nights at the Lozenge Hotel Athens before joining a cruise - it was the perfect place for our short stay.
