Room was nice, the pool is a nice small pool to get into on the hot days and we really enjoyed sitting outside on the outdoor patio with a glass or two of wine. Reasonably priced and good walking distance to the acropolis, restaurants and other points of interest.

Overall, this hotel is excellent.  I would definitely stay here again.

From the friendliness and helpfulness of the people on the front desk, to the cleanliness of the rooms and facilities, to the pool, to the food in the restaurant - all great.

The view on the terrace is outstanding during sunrise and it's a nice place to be in the evening too.  Unfortunately our room was directly below that terrace and the noise of people scraping their chairs and tables back and forth at 10:30pm wasn't the greatest.

The room was compact but effective.  The glass bathroom door, however, is transparent at night with a light on so a bit awkward!

The only downside was how I was spoken to by one of the cleaners.  We had (wrongly) assumed that rooms would be cleaned unless the 'DND' sign was put out.  That's how every other hotel i've ever stayed in works.  I was angrily and aggressively corrected by a cleaner, who brandished the sign stating "this is for you, this is for you" over and over.  Given i was having an excellent day i decided not to make more of the interaction but her attitude was completely wrong.

Before arriving at Acropolis Hill Hotel, we saw pictures online that showed stunning views of the Acropolis. We thought "this is too good to be true for the price," but were pleasantly surprised that those pictures were true. The rooftop of Acropolis Hill Hotel has an incredible, uninterrupted, view of Athen's most iconic site - the Acropolis. The roof was a beautiful place to watch the city wake up in the mornings and to watch the Acropolis light up at night.

The location of the hotel is excellent not just for its views, but also for its walkability to the main historical sites, parks, and a great variety of restaurants and shops. It's in a beautiful neighborhood with access to everything you could want.

The staff at Acropolis Hill were friendly and helpful, providing tips for the best tour options in the city, restaurant recommendations, and baggage storage.

I stayed here at the end of July with my two boys, and we absolutely loved it! Such a cozy little gem! The hotel is perfectly located just a short walk from the city center, making it super easy to explore Athens on foot. Plus, there's public transport nearby, which made it so convenient to get to the beach! The staff were incredibly friendly and went above and beyond when we had an issue with a forgotten bag—they helped us recover it without any hassle. The highlight, though, is definitely the stunning rooftop. The view of the Acropolis is unbeatable, and the restaurant up there is top-notch. I would absolutely stay here again!

Wonderful experience! We stayed here one night prior to our cruise. The staff is very kind and helpful. We received welcome drinks at the rooftop bar and also had excellent authentic Greek food. The hotel itself is beautiful with a modern look. And you have a great view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar!
The room had a good amount of space for us. The shower was a little smaller than we are used. They were also to take our luggage for us while we explored the town before and after our reservation. The shop and restaurants are a short walk from the hotel. Overall great experience!!

Best part of the stay is the wonderful staff and the beautiful common area. The room was nicely appointed but poorly lit (need a light near the entry door) and the bathroom was poorly designed — strange tub with very little room to stand to take a shower then the shower curtain sticks to you. Hardly any room to step out of the tub/shower without bumping into the vanity or toilet. No outlet in bathroom so the hair dryer sits on the desk in the main part of the room and is plugged underneath. First room I had suffered from an unpleasant smell in the drains; second room was better. The staff absolutely could not be any nicer, but the room was a disappointment.

The staff is friendly, and the room is clean, though it is small. The elevator is tiny, but I later discovered that many elevators in Greek hotels are similar. The only downside was the shower. However, the location is perfect, just 5 minutes from the Acropolis Museum.

Everything about this place was perfect! The air conditioning was heaven after long hot days of exploring, great location to get around, stocked with everything we needed and the balcony was such a nice touch to enjoy our morning coffee (provided) and our wine & snacks from a local gem down the street. The only issue was the front door to the building itself definitely needs to be replaced, but it was worth every second of minor inconvenience! Highly recommend staying here if you're visiting Athens! Wonderfully romantic for couples.

Our stay at the Grand Hyatt Athens was nothing short of spectacular! The hotel exudes luxury from the moment you walk through the doors. Our room was beautifully appointed, with modern decor and a comfortable bed that made for a restful night’s sleep. The rooftop pool and bar offer breathtaking views of the Acropolis – a perfect spot to relax after a day of sightseeing. The breakfast buffet was outstanding, with a wide variety of fresh, delicious options. What truly set our experience apart was the impeccable service; the staff were attentive, welcoming, and always ready to help. If you’re looking for a luxurious stay with exceptional amenities in the heart of Athens, the Grand Hyatt is the perfect choice!

Wow what a great hotel! The location is ideal it’s out of the busy acropolis area of the city but still close enough to walk to most of the sites if you wanted. They have a complimentary shuttle bus that will take and pick you up to the national gardens area from there you can hit everything pretty easily. We found the food and beverage offerings to be outstanding from the food to the service it was top notch. The breakfast buffet is one of the best I’ve had in Europe, seriously, it’s pretty incredible. The dinner menu is equally amazing it has a little bit of everything even sushi. The views from this place are worth just coming up for a drink but I would definitely eat here again. We also ate downstairs at the bar/lounge which had even more offerings and it was delicious. The service and the food at these places make it one of the better options to eat at in Athens we stopped by a few spots in the city and felt the offerings at the hotel were much better. The hostess at the dinner restaurant 9th floor was very kind she made sure my wife’s birthday was acknowledged which we really appreciated. The food and beverage manager was also very much on top of things we were very impressed with him and his team. The rooms are really nice great balconies with views of the city it was clean and well maintained. Reception was helpful and nice to deal with each time as well as the bell/valet team. When we’re back in Athens we’ll be staying here again for sure.

Thanks for the amazing event at Cyclades Hall yesterday!!! Beautiful and elegant space! The large screen and the high ceiling was giving the feeling of luxury and simplicity at the same time!!! The banquet exquisite, in compliance with the standards of Hyatt!!! But, above all, the employees were the key to the success!! Excellent service, all the staff was extremely polite, helpful and to the point! All the departments, from sales to banquet, from IT to the reception and the concierge! Congratulations to all the people of Grand Hyatt Athens!

What an amazing location!  The hotel is very lovely and the views from the restaurant on the 9th floor AMAZING!  The rooms are extremely ample, clean and the mattress and pillows outstanding.  The staff we encountered were very friendly and helpful. Don’t pass up their restaurants…Best Greek salad ever, breakfast buffet has the largest selection and the food extremely tasty.  Worth the stay for safety, location and service.  Memorable stay!!

For the price you really cannot beat this hotel. It's got a great modern vibe, huge luxurious rooms, and an absolute A+ spa that is free to visit (pool, jacuzzi, sauna, cold plunge etc) - comes in very handy after long days of walking and hiking). Service is really amazing, Erinda at the front desk was so nice and helped us get settled when we arrived a bit early before check-in. You're about 20 minutes from the city center but they have a free shuttle that gets you there and back in 5.

The hotel was excellent in every aspect. I believe the hotel's location was one of the best areas, and with a little walk, you could see some of the most beautiful places, as well as experience the vibrant nightlife.
In terms of internet and, I have to say they were good. For breakfast, you had different options and fruits and everything was served neatly.
‏The staff's behavior was truly great, They also helped us to book a tour and were helpful and kind.

This hotel is UNSANITARY. my body (including face and neck) is covered with BED BUGS bites which are very painful and itchy.
The first room I was in had the worst shower head I've seen and in the morning I had to fight off a roach just to get in the shower. They moved me to a different room and there were the bed bugs.
The hotel never mentioned anything about sanitizing my things or offer any solution, they even suggested I might be lying (until they saw the bed bugs with their own eyes). Added pics and videos.

You have to drag your suitcases up a few flights of stairs and there's an elevator only between the 1st and 3rd floor. And there's no one to help. No kettle in the room as well. Those things just seem basic to me.

The only plus is the location.

Never had this kind of experience in any hotel. I feel bad for posting this but I feel it's more important that people will know before booking this hotel.

Fantastic hotel, really helped us when we had problems with our Airbnb.  Staff so friendly and helpful.  The room was conformable and had everything we needed.
The whole hotel was very clean and tidy.
The breakfast was lovely,...hot food, pastries, juices, hot drinks.
Hotel is close yo evening you would want to see.
Highly recommended 👌

We stayed here for a quick visit in Athens.  The location was really great, a short walk to the Plaka and Acropolis as well as Syntagma Square.
The room was a good size.  We were facing the main street, so there was a bit of noise from the traffic, but it quiets down eventually.  The bed was large and very comfortable.
Better than the location and comfortable bed was the staff.  I was trying to locate lost luggage and both Vassilis and Donna went above and beyond to help me.  They were just great.
The cost of the room was quite reasonable and it was nice that breakfast was included.  I would certainly stay again.

My family and I had a wonderful stay at the Acropolis Museum Boutique Hotel. While the hotel wasn't hip or modern (it feels like it was furnished by an eclectic antique shop,) it was very clean and comfortable feeling.

In response to many reviewers concerns about the noise, we didn't find it to be a problem. Yes you're on a heavily trafficked street, but there wasn't much honking, emergency vehicles, or loud people. It's not quiet, but hey you are staying in the middle of the city!

We really enjoyed staying in this neighborhood, as the side streets in the Koukaki area had great food options, and felt very local and not touristy.

It is important to note that there are a number of stairs you must climb before you're able to reach the elevator, making it a bit difficult for those with mobility issues, and those with lots of bags. You climb a large flight from the street to the lobby, along with a small flight from the lobby to the next floor until you reach an elevator. If you are able-bodied, this is no issue at all.

Overall we had a stress-free time, and felt like this was a good option for the money.

Our daughter and friend stayed in an Acropolis view room with balcony to celebrate their combined 30th birthdays (they loved the room!). I emailed the hotel to ask if it was possible to have champagne and glasses waiting for their arrival. The staff were very happy to arrange this - with a note, a little birthday cake and snacks on a lovely tray with the chilled champagne and glasses. The birthday girls were in tears at the lovely surprise and enjoyed the balcony and view with their champagne. Thank you Philippos Hotel for making their stay even more special.

Great location near Acropolis, Museums and La Plaka. Gracious, hardworking and helpful staff, gave restaurant recommendations, scheduled tour and taxi. Excellent Greek breakfast. Stayed here 15 years ago and the quality and value are consistent. Rooftop terrace with direct view of the Acropolis, wine available.

Amazing location, spotless clean, but missing a few basic amenities (e.g. plug socket on only 1 side of bed, no plugs at desk or light around mirror), staff were really friendly and very helpful. Rooftop space gives fantastic views

Great hotel in a central position close to the entrance to the acropolis. Rooms were clean and all seemed to have a balcony . The buffet breakfast was very good with a wide selection, including Greek cakes and yoghurt. The only slight downside was the smell from the drain in the bathroom but hey it's Greece and the drains always smell in Greece

This hotel was amazing. we were not meant to stay, but after getting late from the aeroport ( 5 in the morning ) we went inside asking for a room ( even tho we knew it was nearly impossible. The man saw that I was really anxious for being outside in the dark, and let us stay. It was really nice and also, we were so close from the Acropolis that we could go walking. Also the hotel super clean.

Very basic room and hotel with no frills, but couldn't have been more pleased for what it was. Ended up being one of our favourite places we stayed while in Greece. Friendly staff, great neighbourhood and our terrace room had a certain kind of charm to it. If you are looking for a budget place to stay in a nice, quiet neighbourhood, I cannot recommend Art Gallery Hotel enough.

The Art Gallery hotel offers a cozy, family-owned atmosphere where guests are warmly welcomed, almost as if they were visiting relatives. During my three-night stay, I found the rooms to be adequately clean, comfortable, and spacious, albeit with some minor wear and tear on the room and bathroom amenities. For instance, I encountered an issue with the door lock, but the friendly reception staff promptly provided a workaround with a smile, suggesting a clever trick to operate it until it was fixed.

One of the hotel's standout features is undoubtedly its staff, who consistently go above and beyond to assist guests with a genuine kindness that enhances the overall experience. While the breakfast provided was basic, it served its purpose well enough to fuel explorations of Athens' ancient wonders, and it also conveniently provided a snack for later in the morning at nearby restaurants.

The hotel's location is another highlight, situated in a quiet area yet still within easy reach of key attractions like the Plaka and the Acropolis. Despite its proximity to tourist hotspots, the neighborhood maintains a peaceful ambiance, with plenty of excellent dining options and cafes nearby. Moreover, the reasonable pricing adds to the hotel's appeal, offering good value for money considering its prime location and attentive service.

In summary, the Art Gallery hotel provides a pleasant stay characterized by familial hospitality, comfortable accommodations, and a prime location that allows guests to enjoy the best of Athens without feeling overwhelmed by crowds.
Great location, the rooms were clean and spacious, the staff was friendly and attentive, we really enjoyed our short stay and hope to return one day

The rooms aren’t anything special, but they have everything you need and more! The staff are the sweetest, coolest people!! So friendly and warm. I stayed here with my study abroad group multiple times over our 3 week trip since Art Gallery was considered our “home base.” By the end of the trip everyone in my group referred to this place as “home.” I even stayed an extra day and a half here with my boyfriend after my studying was done and he loves it just as much as I do!! Location is SUPERB, breakfast is delicious, but the staff make this place AMAZING! I highly recommend hanging out in the front desk area to get to know the staff and cool off in the AC. I’ll be back soon! ❤️

The hotel has a fascinating history, its foundations having sheltered the current owners’ grandparents as a bunker in WWII before they made it first an art gallery and then, building upward, this hotel. Don’t expect spanking new state-of-the-art ANYTHING. Expect inimitable charm, cleanliness, authenticity, helpful staff, and an excellent location. We loved it.

Not a great experience and the flat certainly needs an upgrade, desperately...from the walls to the furniture. The place has an unrealised potential. I liked the balcony, but then there was water dripping from the ac and slippery tiles were all wet. The flat has two toilets and only 1 bathroom. Not great for such a spacious apartment. We stayed in a group of 11 people and we only had one pair of keys. I would suggest the owner changes the name from "beautiful" to basic homes. The good point was the location: walking distance to the Centre and excellent dining options all around. Overall I would not recommend.

We have booked this aparment via booking.com several weeks ago. I really understand that all prices went up due to war in Ukraine. If the price was deemed too low, it could be subject to further negotiation. But the owners did something absolutely unacceptable and refused to let us in on the day of our arrival! I suspect that this was due to reason that they booked it outside booking.com to a third party for a higher price. They only said over the intercom downstairs "Apartment is under renovation, goodbye" and we were suddenly forced to find 3 nights accomodation for 8 people and a baby. To add insult to the injury they informed booking.com that they cancel our stay because we did not check in!!! Combined with the reviews below about the standard, I really discourage everyone from staying here.

The Place doesn't even deserve 1 star but I can not give 0 stars. It was a total desaster. I travel much and I booked many accomodations with booking.com but it was the first time that I had a situation like this. I booked a stay for 3 nights in Athens in Veikou Beautiful Homes via booking in February. The booking succeeded and I even had a contact with the owner recording airport transfer. Everything seemed to be normal. At the day of our arrival i wrote her a message from the airport via booking that we will be there in about 30 mins. Ok asked how we can get to the apartment. She didnt reply. We took a taxi to the location. And I couldn't believe my eyes and ears. The location was closed. I called her and first she didn't pick up and then she picked up and said "We are having a renovation. Hotel is closed". And she hang up 🙈 I tried to call her many times but she didn't pick up. Then I asked her Greek  neighbor to call her from the Greek number. She picked up only for a moment and said something in Greek that I didn't understand. Then she cancelled our booking in booking.com and said the reason was we didn't show up 🤯🤯🙈🙈🙈 I never saw something like this. Such an ignorance. Probably there was no renovation because it didn't looked like this. I guess she just got different clients who probably paid more. No idea. But I was traveling with my whole family. 9 people and  (among the others 1 year old baby,one ill person and two seniors).  It was already late evening. We were hungry, with all baggages, and with no place to live the next 3 days. I was searching quickly for some apartment or suite or some hotel to stay in this area (because it was very good located, close to Acropolis. That's why I booked it). But everything was already booked or too small for our group or just too expensive to book it in the very last moment.

Please people - DON'T BOOK THIS PLACE!!!

THEY ARE CHEATING

FINGER WEG!!!

TRZYMAĆ SIĘ Z DALEKA!!! OSZUŚCI!!!!

DO NOT BOOK HERE
We (4 friends) booked here 2 months before our vacation (Booking.com) The booking said "Confirmed" so no problem, right? 2 days before arriving I messaged the owner to let them know about our arrival time - no response. At the airport of departure I messaged them again - no response. Then I found another review here on Maps about the same place, saying they cancelled lat minute (literally when they were already there outside the door). Same thing: no communication whatsoever. With that review we all tried calling as soon as we landed. The first time the call got through and rang 5 times before being sent to voicemail, then for all other calls, as soon as it rang they sent us to voicemail. At the 9th or 10th call they did answer and all they said was THAT THE APARTMENT DOESN'T EXIST ANYMORE. To not go there, and that it is a problem we should solve with Booking.com.
So we were left at the airport with no confirmed booking for our group 🙃

Aaaaand of course so far they haven't cancelled the booking, so on Booking.com it shows like if we want to cancel it they'll charge us €100+. Already requested via the site for the owner to waive the fees, will update if successful.

Do not book here for any reason, they don't answer, do not let you know about anything, do not even pick up the phone. Would be 0 stars if possible.

As always, Veikou Beautiful Homes is the perfect place to stay, or start the trip to the islands, with its spacious two apartments, quietly and centrally located close to the ancient town. Super clean and a lot of space at low cost. Top.

It was great the owner accommodated us with storing our luggage at the main office and the when we arrived to the apartment greater us there. Showed us around the place and the use of the home. From the Washing machine to the hot water for baths. We did report to the owner that the door nobs to several rooms had a issue of falling off or the latch spinning. They responded right away but we let them know that we can work with it until we leave and then they can come fix it for someone else. Over all it was a great place close to many tourists locations.

It was like staying at a 5-star hotel, but it was an entire apartment, walking distance from the Acropolis!

Akis went above and beyond for us. Everything was perfect. He was very kind, provided food, big TV with netflix in 5 different languages, big beds perfectly made, plenty of towels, air condition of course and the apartment was bigger than expected!

Couldn't recommend this place enough and even more so for the customer service. Thank you Aki, we will be back again!
Great location.

The apartment has an ideal location, convenient parking - there is a paid parking lot next to it, and free parking on the street. It is clean and very welcoming. The host is kind and responsive, he had left us breakfast in the fridge.

Excellent well-kept and clean apartment with every service, excellent location for visiting the city. Akis is a fantastic owner, kind and provided us with all the necessary information. I highly recommend it to everyone and if I were to return I would have no doubts. Thank you and congratulations again Akis 👍👍👍

Big Apartment with 2 bedrooms. Aircon in every room. Fully equiped kitchenette with oven, kettle, coffee maker, sandwich maker and the owner also keeps Greek coffee powder and Jam packets for breakfast. Close to Victoria station.

Good apartments for a good price! We had a basement apartment with a terrace, which doesn't have a good view, but it is really nice for just sitting there and having your breakfast. This apartment has everything you need for cooking, also clean room and comfortable bed. Highly recommend!

The apartment is situated at a reasonable distance from the main attractions- about 15 min walk. It is small but clean and averagely quiet. The check-in/out was super easy. Great option for a weekend stay.

Few apartments and rooms for rent. Very clean and loaded with everything needed, dishes, good coffee, wine and juice. Misses some tea and maybe explanation which exactly place do you get since some are with windows and living area while other are basement type. Overall well furnished and comfortable.

Location central ✔
Walkable✔
Comfortable Studio & bed✔
Gas leak in the apartment across the hall that prompted an evacution of building & 2 Fire Trucks✔
Made the best of the gas leak situation ✔
Slight lingering smell of gas in my bathroom for next 2 days✔
No nice Welcome Basket in room til mentioned it to owner✔
Got Basket later✔
Easy walk to metro✔
Restaurants and coffee walkable distance ✔
Very Good value. Small, but very charming & clean, quite central, and a welcome basket. Thumbs up!
There is everything you need in the apartment. The location is also quite central.
Great location! Great service! Very nice apartment.

We asked the owner in advance if we could leave our luggage prior to check-in. He told us yes. We arrived in the morning to leave our luggage, after an intense and long trip. The owner greeted us, very angry. I told him that he had told us we could leave our luggage and very reluctantly, he tells us to follow him. In a garage, he tells us to leave our luggage and leave quickly. I urgently needed to go to the bathroom, so l asked him for that. While he looked at us to leave. He told me not very aggressively and shouting, making a gesture with his hands in the shape of a cross, and that we should leave. We left very uncomfortable due to his treatment and waited outside to find somewhere to go to the bathroom. The owner comes out after 10 minutes and tells us to come with him. I thought he was going to lend me the bathroom, but he took us to the top floor of it, to show the view it had of the Acropolis. My partner got upset and asked him again that we were not interested in that, that we had only asked for the bathroom (the owner was trying to be nice and show off the amenities of his apartment), but when my partner asked him that, he got upset and. He rudely took us to the bathroom, while waiting next to the bathroom door for him to enter and exit quickly. I felt very uncomfortable, so without entering, I told him that I did not expect such treatment towards a human being and that it no longer mattered, that we could look for other accommodation. He got so upset that he didn't even let me explain anything to him and started yelling at me to keep my voice down, because other people were sleeping, while he told him that he was speaking softly and couldn't treat me like that. To which he begins to silence me with his finger. When we got down, I told him that he was a terrible person and he waved us off. I called booking, with poor handling and wasting all my cell phone minutes, I still haven't seen them respond to me at such a bad time we are experiencing. His name is Sergio

The shared bathroom on level 2 is very unclean and unhygienic, lots of mould on the ceiling. But for the low price the accommodation is decent and very fine for a short stay. The rooftop is a nice addition with views of the acropolis! :) Also a very nice man who received us at check in.
If you want cheap price this is the accomodation, the room of fine but very poor in cleaning, kitchen is very dirty…has everything but dirty.
The person in charge is really kind and helpful😊 big and comfortables rooms

hospitality 5/5  rooms 5/5 price 5/5  location 100% safe
it is the best.
some will say it is not  perfect clean, but its only 20/night. for its price it is the best it can be.   clean sheets towels the room is clean enough for the money you pay

A hotel right next to the Acropolis Museum. I think the location is very nice, flat and not too steep. The rooms were standard size and clean. The people at the reception were very helpful. We preferred breakfast, it was a delicious breakfast with beautiful products that appealed to many cultures.

Very nice hotel, great staff, beautiful breakfast room. Room balcony had Acropolis view. We had a slight problem with our air conditioning.  They offered to switch us to another room but we didn't because it was still functioning, just not perfectly.

Great location, situated behind the acropolis museum and very close to restaurants and shops. The hotel has friendly staff, is very accommodating, and it is easy to arrange hotel transport to the airport. They also have a rooftop restaurant with a beautiful view of the Acropolis.

We chose the Hera hotel because of the location and it being a luxury hotel as we can expect a certain standard. The staff were friendly on arrival, the cleaning lady was wonderful and the breakfast staff member was great as well. Breakfast was fantastic. The foyer and breakfast room were air-conditioned very well. Upon arrival to our room we found the room unbearably hot as the curtains were open and the sun was beaming into the room and it was 40 degrees Celsius outside. We quickly closed the curtains and put the air conditioning on 18 degrees Celsius full fan. The room did not cool at all but because of our jet lag we fell asleep. In the morning the room was still hot and the air conditioning was running all night. Before leaving for the day we asked the cleaning lady to leave the air conditioning on and the curtains closed hoping that when we returned the room would finally be cool. This was not the case. I called reception and the man told me that the system cannot keep up ( although the foyer was very cool). The next morning we woke drenched in sweat and couldn’t wait to check out. We mentioned it again to the staff upon check out and the response was that all of Athens is hot and the systems cannot keep up all over Athens. What a ridiculous response!!! Despite the great location we will never return to the Hera hotel. When you pay 241 euro per night including taxes you expect the air conditioning to be working. We were so disappointed.

One of the best stays I had during my two weeks in Greece: clean rooms, nice staff, great location. My room was small - as expected - but air conditioned and well proportioned. Would stay again if I visit Athens.

Conveniently located and close to many attractions, restaurants and shops.  The apartment is well maintained, clean and with everything needed for a comfortable stay.  Perfect for a group or family with children.  The hostess was kind and helpful.   I’d recommend without hesitation.

That apartament was more than we expected . It looks the same as on a pictures or even better- super specious, clean, modern with all  necessary equipment. Close to shops and walking distance from all important monuments. Definitely recommend! We loved our stay!

Excellent rental. Exactly as shown in the photos. Immaculate. TVs in each room and individually controlled air conditioning. Great location with many great restaurants within walking distance. Esmeralda arranged our transfer to and from the airport making it very convenient for us. Highly recommend My Greek Vacations rentals.

5 Stars - the apartment is in a great location, it’s been beautifully renovated, super clean, well equipped with extra towels and bed linen, along with all the mod cons. Restaurants, metro, tram, sites, shops, supermarkets etc are right on the doorstep. During our stay our needs changed from a 2 week holiday stay to 8 weeks due to Covid. This apartment was great for both a holiday stay and long term stay. All bedrooms had workstation so it meant everyone had a desk to do remote schooling and work from. Mary and Esmeralda were great hosts and responded immediately to all our text messages and calls and even provided a complimentary clean and change of bed linen, which was very generous and appreciated. We would stay at any of the apartments hosted by Mary and Esmeralda. It’s places and hosts like this that put ones faith in online bookings and Airbnb.

Lovely spacious, clean apartment in an area with lots of very tempting cafes, bakeries, bars and restaurants.
We walked to all the major sights and attractions from the accommodation - would thoroughly recommend.

Not sure where to start. Location: Was close to some of the bus lines so that was nice. Not much else is close by. Neighborhood was quiet and seemed safe.
Service: The cleaning ladies were very helpful with helping us check in which was great since there was not a reception desk and we were given no instructions upon booking (we booked through our credit card company so not sure if you book directly with them if you get instructions). We talked to the hotel and then I had to send them my ID over WhatsApp and then they sent the codes for everything. The service was was very quick.
Room: AC was working which was great. The place was spacious. Everything was new but not the greatest upkeep. Some floorboards were pulling apart. There was some hair in the shower. There wasn't hot (or warm) water in the shower. The toilet was broken (but my husband fixed it). There some stains on the floors (not sure if they were permanent or just not cleaned). Overall I would not stay here again. I think it would be good if you are on a hostel budget or looking for those vibes (but with more privacy).

The pictures on the website are NOT representative of reality. A dark and dingy hovel, dirty sofa, rickety furniture, zero decor. The two sparse bedrooms were located in a basement which smelt of bins. We were a family of five and the bed for the fifth person was not prepared nor was there sign of where it would be… praying not on that dirty sofa.

We could have perhaps tolerated the poor location if anything else had been good.

We stayed long enough to put our bags down, look around, take out our phones and book into a central hotel.

I guess you get what you pay for in the end but the photos and reviews gave us false hope.

would not recommend

This place is a dump, and it's being rented as a premium suite. It was disgusting, nothing worked, total disrepair, dead bugs and the Athens stay people didn't care to do anything about it after multiple complaints! The washing machine didn't work, no basic supplies and it was just dirty and broken everywhere!

In one of the apartments now. Very happy with it. Modern and well-equipped. Staff are accommodating.

It's in a good location close to bus routes. Lidl is a reasonably short walk away but there's a small supermarket close by, too. Easy walk to where it's all going on in Athens.

One tip to the manager/owner is some balcony furniture could be a useful addition.

Overall, great place. Very comfortable.

The apartment is beautiful, and well laid out. It was very comfortable for two of us but could easily have taken more. The apartment was perfectly cleaned and fully equipped with everything we needed. Everyone was very welcoming and left thoughtful items in the apartment. Overall, we had a really great stay and would certainly stay there again!

I've stayed at Neratzia in Februari and June (3 and 6 days). If I return to Athens I do not doubt to book at Neratzia again. I would recommend my friends to go here too. The rooms, including bathroom, are clean and suit your needs. The breakfast, served at the cafe below is perfect. Personnel are perfect. Always helpful and friendly. Keep up the good work :)

Myself and my dad stayed at Neratzia Rooms recently - perhaps the best stay of our trip. The staff were great, very friendly and accommodating, the inclusive breakfast was delicious, and the room was spacious and modern. I would highly recommend to anyone looking to stay in Athens.

A hotel that is an experience, mainly thanks to the owner and staff. Everyone wants to help and provide a good experience. Yanis the owner accompanied us with good advice and a warm attitude. The hotel also has a pleasant bar with a good atmosphere. We received exceptional service and a hug from Yannis even after we left. For sure we will come back again
Merav and Hanit

Well situated and in walking distance to restaurants and shops.
The rooms are as shown on the website. Room service is every day and very good.
Breakfast and it's selection of dishes are excellent
The cafe bar is open late at  night and you need to close the window, not be disturbed by the noise when sleeping. However, you have to close the window because of  the outside temperature/AC

We loved our stay at the Neratzia Rooms! This place is truly a hidden gem. The room was adorable and very clean. The beds were the most comfortable we slept in on our whole trip through Italy and Greece. Plenty of towels, coffee, bottled water, and even a toothbrush if you need it! The owner was so nice and all the employees were accommodating. Violet especially was so kind and on top of everything. She was so helpful! The food at the cafe was incredible! We were very happy with our stay here.

Booked to stay here very last minute, and couldn’t be happier with the service that we received! Jannis was very helpful and accommodating, sending local recommendations for food and places to visit! The house was clean and welcoming, and in a very friendly area. As two younger girls travelling alone, we felt safe and comfortable during our stay here. 10/10 would recommend and would happily stay here again! :))

Excellent experience! The location is unbeatable—situated in a vibrant, hip neighborhood that truly captures the spirit of the city. The street is filled with great cafes, bars and restaurants that offer a taste of the local culture. Whether you're looking for a cozy spot to enjoy coffee or a trendy place to have dinner, everything is just a short walk away.
Had a lovely stay, would definitely recommend! Easily accessible, in a convenient location, and good food around. Clean space, and hosts made sure our experience was great!

Great overall experience at Troon of Athens Apartments. Simple, clean, and tidy. The bed was very comfortable. The hot shower was amazing. The neighbourhood is great (relatively quiet with good food options close by). Wifi connection and speed was not that great and could be improved. Some loud neighbours in the early a.m. Nevertheless, we give it 5 stars. We stayed 4 nights (May 2024) and will certainly stay again on our next visit to Athens. - S & J

Well located in a beautiful area with many restaurants and bars nearby. The building is well maintained, the apartments are clean and provide everything we needed for a comfortable stay. Everything was as expected and there were no unpleasant surprises. This is our place of choice for our trips in Athens and we keep on coming back.

Wonderful service; all the hotel staff were very friendly and very attentive to everything we needed; they are very polite and warm people. The location of the hotel is spectacular; you can clearly see the amazing Parthenon from the bar, and certainly the facilities are wonderful. The breakfasts served are indescribable; they are a true Greek delight. Besides being buffet, there are options for all tastes, and all this while enjoying an unforgettable, beautiful scenery. An excellent hotel for visiting an amazing city. A hundred percent recommended!

The rooms are not as nice as the photos, quite run down and small. No where to lay out a small case. The breakfast is just ok, aside from breakfast you can't get any food here. I asked for my bed to be made up as a double as it was a twin room but it was never done (the booking was for a double). The staff were friendly and the location was good. Overall not good value for money.

Loved our stay here. Small rooms but lovely and clean.we had a wonderful balcony that had a perfect view of the Acropolis. There wasn’t a kettle for tea or coffee but when we asked about it, in less than 5 minutes we had one. All the staff were amazing and made us feel like we were friends. Very close to everything including transport. Can’t wait to visit Athens again and we will be making this our go to hotel.

Location close to metro and 10-15 minutes from the Plaka. Nice variety of choices for breakfast including fried eggs upon request. Very friendly and helpful service, especially Anna at the front desk. Rooms are moderately sized. Roomy corners shower with plenty of hot water. Highly recommend the room with the balcony. Excellent view of the Acropolis, especially front the 6th floor.
We had a great stay! Really clean rooms, very helpful and hospitable stuff. There is a rooftop bar where they serve complimentary, continental breakfast.

I have stayed in Marble House for 3 nights and it was very good experience. The place is very nice and clean, lobby has a lot of mirrors which I like a lot. Staff is very friendly and helpful. Our room had two bed, small fridge, closet and balcony. The restroom with shower was shared with one other room.

Within 3 minutes of walk, you have plenty of bars, coffee places and restaurants. There is also subway station 5 minutes walk away. Acropolis museum is about 15-20 minutes away.

I highly recommend this place for short stays near city center of Athens.

I stayed at Marble House for 6 nights. And I am happy that I did.
Marble House is located in a good neighbourhood, close to Metro station, and other services.
The rooms are clean, comfortable and satisfactory.
The staff are knowledgeable, courteous, helpful and friendly.
I would certainly stay at Marble House again.

I've got been 2 nights this week in Marble House. I've seen it in Lonely Planet, and after the seek I choose this place. Great choice, easy to reserve, welcome suggestions by email really useful. The place is nice, comfortable, clean, quiet (maybe in high season it changes, don't know). Very well located, the staff, they seems more like the owners or really friendly people, give me all information I needed. I can only speak well. If I have the change to come back to Athina sure repeat. Thanks, Rosa from Madrid.

We loved staying at Marble House! Such kind and accommodating staff. And fabulous AC! There were some nice cafe/restaurants in the area and it was a nice walk to the acropolis. Probably not the best if anyone in your group has a hard time with stairs, although it depends on what level your room is on. And the staff can carry your bags for you. They were also very accommodating for a late check out even during a busy time of year! Of course that can't always happen but we so appreciated the attention to detail on their end! The only suggestion I would have is a TV we could cast to.

We recently spent five nights at the Marble House Hotel in Athens, and it was an absolute delight. From the moment we arrived, we were struck by how impeccably clean our room was, and the warm, welcoming nature of the staff made us feel right at home. They went above and beyond, offering us insider tips on the best local restaurants and authentic Greek taverns. Thanks to their fantastic recommendations, we avoided tourist traps and enjoyed delicious meals daily, immersing ourselves in the true flavors of Athens.

The hotel’s location is perfect—nestled in a genuine Athens neighborhood that’s within comfortable walking distance of the city center, yet away from the hustle and bustle. If you’re looking for a cozy, family-run boutique hotel experience and prefer to steer clear of the larger chains, Marble House is the perfect choice. Our stay here was nothing short of wonderful, and we will definitely be returning whenever we find ourselves in Athens again!

Highly recommended!

What you see isn't what you get. We paid 798€ for 3 nights , which is quite a lot of money. In the pictures, the place looked nice and clean, it really wasn't so nice, the photos were taken years ago, today the apartment needs painting, plus the smell of damp in 2 bedrooms and bathrooms, not recommended if you suffer from asthmaThe price is much too high for the place. I told them I was very disappointed, but had no answer from them. I think they don't care...

What an absolutely beautiful property! The apartments were modern, clean, spacious, perfect location and beautiful! It was absolutely perfect for our group of 10. Everything we wanted to visit and see was in walking distance. We were actually on the Acropolis sidewalk, but our location gave us peace from the busyness on the streets outside. We were a 5 minute walk to a great breakfast spot and street vendors. We were a 10-15 minute walk to Monastraski Square. Our host was amazing and accommodating. We had everything that we needed and could use WhatsApp to communicate if we needed anything (including recommendations on where to find delicious Loukamades)! ALA is part of the reason why our stay in Athens was absolutely amazing.

The apartments look exactly like the online pictures. There are 4 floors. The top floor bedroom host an outside deck that looks out over Athens and a patio on the Acropolis side with a hot tub. Beautiful! One wish was that the seating on the patio was clean and set up. We could say out with a glass of wine and admired the beauty of Athens. We did pay 110€ for airport transportation for a group of 10. It was worth it for us. The airport is about 45 minutes away.
Nice apartment. Friendly owner. Great view of the Acropolis from the terrace. Very central for the Acropolis and Plaka .
Lovely location. Nice apartment. Only too many levels.
Great service , views and extremely clean
Loved this studio apartment, very clean and well presented. Host could not have been more helpful
Good location, attentive host, well stocked, A/C, fast WiFi, great value.
Perfect for an overnight

Cozy studio located in a central area from which it is easy to reach the main tourist attractions of Athens on foot, however a 10 minute walk from the nearest metro station.
Despite being located in a busy area it is not noisy and this is very good for sleep.
The studio is essentially furnished and is equipped with the necessary material to be able to cook and eat freely, the spaces are sufficient for a decent stay.
For breakfast, everything you need is left available (sweet, savory, fruit and drinks).
The wifi is fast and accurate.
As soon as you arrive you are a little disoriented as the building where the studio is located is quite dilapidated on the outside, but as soon as you enter the studio the disorientation stops.
The only flaw of the studio is the bathroom which is small and not easily usable, the shower is equipped with a shower curtain (common to almost all Greek bathrooms), so while taking a shower, if you are not careful, it tends to get all over the place wet. bath.
Furthermore, there is a persistent slight smell of sewage in the bathroom, so much so that it is advisable to always keep the bathroom door closed.
Considering the location, the quality/price ratio is quite high.

The apartment was comfortable, spotlessly clean and had literally everything. Space enough for one person as well as a couple. The location is very practical/convenient as it is located near a central hub, with the result that all transportation is available (taxi, trolley buses, Metro, etc.). The area (Kukaki) is well organized. In addition to the nearby shops, there are pedestrian streets with catering & entertainment shops at a distance of 5-10 minutes. For someone who likes to walk, Monastiraki and the Acropolis are about 15' away on main shopping streets. Finally, the owner is kind and completely cooperative. Overall the accommodation is an excellent choice and I will definitely prefer it again.

Spacious apartment.  4th floor, The view on the backyard(at least for the apartment for 4 people) I don't know about the others. Small but nice balcony. Very nice style of the apartment. Very Quiet air-cons. Well equipped kitchen. Beds are comfortable, but I think the mattress soon should be changed for the big bed :) The host was very nice and welcomed us well. Close to shops,bakery downstairs, relatively cheep restaurants downstairs. Safe neighborhood. 15-20 min. walking to Acropolis and to the center. No problem finding parking places on the streets.  The only thing which bothered me was that It was hard to open the doors when you enter to the building, you need a bit to struggle with it.  I think the lock should be changed.

It's 2am, and music outside is not loud, it's LOUD. Closing the windows doesn't help.
Don't stay here if you plan to sleep at night.
Really close to everything. The owner is so lovely and Helpful!
Nice apartment, everything you need is there. The center is just a stone's throw away. There are many cafes nearby where you can eat inexpensively and deliciously.

It is a very nice hotel, and the rooms are clean. There is no reception, but they provide you with very good information, and you do not encounter any problems. We were satisfied during our stay. Having coffee in the courtyard was quite enjoyable. There is a street market set up on the street where the hotel is located. It happened on Friday during our stay, but I'm not sure if it's there on other days. Because of the market, you might wake up a bit early on Friday due to the noise from the stalls being set up.

They said not to use internet but buy from website. I ended up paying $150 more. I was so annoyed and they tried to offer a crap explanation, but saw through their BS. Very tired. Light bulbs out. Black hair in bath plug hole. Front door like Fort Knox very difficult to open, their attention to detail is very poor and would definitely not recommend it. Left a very sore and bitter taste in my mouth
We were only there for one night but they were very friendly and very attentive to our needs. We were able to walk to the Acropolis.

This was my very first time in Greece(Sept 2022) and I was alone. I couldn’t have  picked a better place to stay! First, the hotel was quiet and clean. Second, it was within walking distance to many of the sites or a short taxi ride(Acropolis was an 8 minute walk tops). But what set this hotel apart, was how wonderful Konstantinos was! He did a great job communicating with me before I arrived. And when I did arrive, he sat down with me to let me know where to go and where not to go. He gave AMAZING recommendations for area bars, bakeries, and restaurants. Lastly, he set up cab rides for me whenever I needed them. Seriously, my stay was perfect here. I believe he is the reason why I am dying to go back to Greece. You will not be disappointed with this location, trust me, especially if you are traveling solo.

I stayed in the Blue Lilly room in Oct 2023. Beautiful well kept property. I was very happy, enjoy every minute I was there. Great location, food and restaurant everywhere! Mr K the host was fabulous! Loved my stay, miss it now!!!

Just amazing. The view from the balcony was stunning at all times of day or night.

Neighbourhood was pretty quiet, with the exception of market day. But, honestly, that just added to the charm of the trip!

Would 100% recommend staying here for any trip to Athens. So well located to transport links, historical sites, great restaurants.
Excelent view. Nice host and very kind. The apartment is very beautifull.
Nice place beautiful view!

Average apartment for the price.

The view from the balcony is great, especially in the evening. And it's close to everything, on foot. We were able to enter the apartment before the scheduled check-in time, a flexibility that was appreciated.

The neighbor downstairs shouted at us from her balcony not to rub the chairs.... however, we hadn't really made any noise and we had been on the balcony for 1 minute. It was quite unpleasant.

See nice and cozy accommodation with a view of the Acropolis. CRAZINESS! Everything was there from A to Z in the apartment. Everything within walking distance.  Parking spaces in front of the house are always available. Despite the proximity to the Acropolis and the center, quiet location. Landlord very very friendly and answered immediately. Ephcharisto

The villa is located in Philopappou, close by to excellent shopping and transport links.

The villa is spacious and has rooms spread over three floors. Unusually, the villa offers a guest a lift service and this is very useful when moving heavy cases at arrival and departure times. The villa was spotless on arrival and the agent arranged for our transportation to and from Athens Airport (80 euros each way / September 2023).

Minor niggles included a broken microwave but the agent replaced it immediately. The knives were all blunt and were replaced a few days later after requests were made to send photographs of them??? Slightly more concerning was an intermittent failing fridge freezer. This caused food in the freezer to melt on more than one occasion which was annoying. We reported the issue to the agent just before we left. Another area of improvement would be for the management company to collate a folder with useful information like, how to use the washing machine and dryer (all in Greek on the machines), where to eat, what to do in the local area, transport etc. There was a folder but it was full of blank sheets and forms for the agent to records occupants’ details. We have never stayed in a villa where this type of information has not been available.

The villa has an excellent outside courtyard however, be careful and watch out for mosquitoes!

We loved the Villa and the agent was extremely helpful. We would definitely stay there again.
Very lovely lodging for a large group of Great communication from management.
Beautiful home, everything you need,  great neighborhood with shops and restaurants walking distance to the Acropplis but on a quiet street below Philappapou Hill.
Wonderful Villa..enjoyed the balcony and lush plants
Spacious, clean and great hosts

Even before the trip, the host was very responsive and helpful.
The facilities were comfortable and clean. The location is great, easy access to the tourist spots and the neighborhood is safe. There was a grocery store nearby and a couple of great restaurants within 10min walk and even a quaint neighborhood bar ~2min away. We enjoyed the little backyard for fresh air and cooling down in the afternoon.

I stayed here with my family in early August, and we throughally enjoyed our stay. Very clean,comfortable and modern, and spacious. An ou yes AC as it was hot in Athens. Close to great restaurants and 20 minutes walk to the acropolis!!
Checkin was easy, and our host helped with a number of those typical tourist requests. We really appreciate our stay!!!
Very nice apartment with balcony in a nice area. Few minute walk away from nice bars etc. Would recommend!

Very comfortable apartments with all necessary facilities. Clean and tidy. The rooftop has a terrace with the barbecue grill. A bit away from the city centre, but taxi is very cheap (4-5 euros to Acropolis)
I can't say anything bad about this place, quite a good location. A perfect spot for a few days in Athens.

The apartments are located in the very center. Everything is very clean and cozy. The kitchen has everything for cooking (oil, spices, coffee, pasta), and the bathroom also has everything. The owner is friendly, speaks good English. We went by bus to the beach every day. There is an excellent coffee shop nearby. The Acropolis is 800 meters from the apartments.
A little far from the center but nice apartment
Meh..
Great staff

Are you Dutch and living in Athens? Or a Greek with something Dutch in your heart?  Come here every second Friday of the month (we call it casual friday)

Gezellig plekje voor Nederlanders of gewoon Nederland gangers die in Athene wonen. Iedere 2e vrijdag avond van de maand is de tent open.
Clubhouse of the Dutch Association in Greece. See websites NVIG.GR
If you want to be in the Netherlands for a while, this is the right place to be
nice place of the Dutch association.

Fantastic Weekend Stay at Athens Residence Apartments!

I recently stayed at Athens Residence Apartments over the weekend, and I couldn’t have been more pleased with my experience. The location is unbeatable—nestled in a quiet neighborhood but still within walking distance to the Acropolis and many other major attractions in Athens. It made exploring the city so convenient and stress-free.

The apartment itself was spacious, modern, and impeccably clean. It felt like a home away from home with all the amenities you could need, from a fully equipped kitchen to a comfortable living area. The bed was incredibly comfortable, ensuring we had restful nights after long days of sightseeing.

What really stood out was the attention to detail and the hospitality of Mr. Albert. He was welcoming and helpful, providing great recommendations for local restaurants and attractions. Check-in was smooth, and they even offered a handy guidebook with tips for making the most of our stay.

Overall, Athens Residence Apartments is perfect for anyone looking for a blend of comfort, convenience, and value. I highly recommend it for both short and extended stays. I’ll definitely be coming back next time I’m in Athens!

Albert is the best! This place is a great value for the money and was in a more residential area. Albert recommended places and we ate at a few. He provided cold water in our fridge and the ac was great. It is up a big hill  but is a great spot being 10 minutes from the Acropolis. Would stay again!

Albert is a legend, hats off to him. He helped us with everything, including the delivery of our lost luggage from an erratic airline company, he also relocated us in the best room for the same price because of an unexpected pipe problem in the basement. He’s an absolute gentleman and we were treated as kings. I would highly recommend this nice, modern, clean and tidy apartment so close to the Acropolis and Plaka areas (5 minutes walk)

Fantastic location in a peaceful area around 10 mins walk to the Acropolis. Stunning apartment with all the usual amenities, nice touches with the complimentary bathroom toiletries and flip-flops, and the bottle of water chilled in he fridge upon arrival. Lovely host who was very accommodating with our late check in, and also gave us fantastic recommendations on local places to eat and drink.

We have just spent 3 nights at the Apartment. Everything was great - from the initial arrangements over messages to organizing transfer from the airport, to meeting and greeting us and helping with information and all other needs. A huge thank you to Albert and Xenofon for such a wonderful first time experience in Athens!

The location is amazing, close to everything but not in the very noisy tourist area. The view from the balcony is amazing, you see the Acropolis from close. The apartment is cute, well furnished, very modern and high-tech. The host is very kind and helpful.

Extremely cozy and modern loft with a lovely, lovely view and a beautiful terrace!! It's very clean, well equipped and close to the subway which is a huge plus. Just beautiful, I truly enjoyed my stay!!

Also, the neighborhood is pretty quiet and safe so that's another huge plus! Overall, just absolutely lovely!!

Konstantina the manager was very nice, hospitable and super helpful. She suggested us a lot of places all of which were excellent. The loft itself is in a very good location, nicely decorated and well equipped. It's a bit too small for a couple especially during bad weather when the use of balcony is limited. However we enjoyed our stay a lot.

Everything was perfect! The loft is located near the museum of contemporary art, an area, where you find everything you need and the transportation to all the spots of interest was very easy! The loft is modern, cosy, felt like home. The terrace is wonderful! We sat every night enjoying the beautiful view of Athens and Acropolis! Many thanks to Konstantina who was super helpful and caring our during our stay making sure we have everything we need! I recommend you stay at the loft!

Excellent loft very nice view on Athens Acropolis
well designed, Comfortable, clean safe place
Excellent location, it has coffee machine and small refrigerator ,television,
Very big trace
Need to contact them before arrival to arrang the check in
Very helpful manager

Elena went above and beyond to make our stay unforgettable. From the moment we arrived, she greeted us with warmth and a friendly smile, instantly making us feel at home. Her local knowledge was invaluable, recommending hidden gems and the best dining spots nearby. Elena consistently checked in to ensure we had everything we needed, showing genuine care and professionalism. Thanks to her exceptional service, our trip was seamless and enjoyable. She truly made our stay special!

The room was clean and neat.

The shower would back up and cause slight flooding and there’s mold issues for sure.

The Kitchenette was great although the cooking items were old, dirty and scratched.

The noise from the road below is very loud. It felt like we were sleeping in the middle of the road. The windows didnt close well.

Location was otherwise a convenient short walk to Acropolis and some less touristy restaurants.

The highlight of our stay was undoubtedly the friendly and attentive staff. Upon arrival, they took the time to recommend top tourist attractions and local dining favorites, which added a personal touch to our visit. My wife and I stayed in the Attic Suite, the largest room in this seven-story hotel. Located on the 7th floor as the only room on that level, it features a private Wi-Fi network and daily breakfast delivery. The room was clean, spacious, and offered a spectacular view of the Acropolis through floor-to-ceiling glass panels. Blackout curtains ensured a restful night.

The hotel is conveniently located within an 8-minute walk from Plaka, one of Athens’ main hubs. While the old-style elevator, common in European buildings, can feel tight for two people with luggage, it efficiently serves up to the 6th floor. Guests staying in the Attic Suite will need to take a short flight of stairs to reach the 7th floor.

The suite includes a large bedroom, European and USB outlets for easy device charging, and a Nespresso machine with small portion cups. The bathroom, while functional, has limited counter space and a slightly awkward showerhead.

Additional conveniences include a 24/7 front desk, a secure entrance accessible with the room key, and a nearby gas station for snacks and essentials. The hotel’s location on a main road also makes it easy for taxis and Ubers to find.

The location is good, but the hotel's maintenance is terrible. Our room have a serious problem with the pipes. They give us a room with a blocked sink in the toilet and only fixed on the other day. While using the kitchen sink it bubbled into the bathroom drain. The shower was dirty with water liking from everywhere and also blocked, provoking a flood into de room. The sofa bed is super uncomfortable. The windows are massively dirty to the point that you can barely see outside. The curtains also needs maintenance as it goes outside the trail and can't block the sun properly. The hotel is an old residential building adapted to a hotel but with very low maintenance and only one staff available.

We stayed in this hotel for 4 nights in Sept 2024 and was big mistake.  We are very surprised and disappointed that there was NO HOT WATER most of the time.  We'd wake up to find no hot water, and then we would have to go to lobby to complain, and each time they said "OK we will turn on the hot water and please wait 30+ minutes".  After waiting the water would be only warm.  Very odd they would turn on water heater by request! Bathroom also very dirty and moldy see pictures.

Stayed at the Acropolis View for 3 nights before heading off to the Greek Islands. The hotel was clean, breakfast was good, and the rooftop views were excellent. The front desk staff were great, provided good insights into the city and answered any questions we had. The room was small but the bed was comfortable. The hotel is about a 10-15 min walk to all the hot spots, which we did a few times per day! We walked back to our hotel at 2AM one night and felt quite safe.

The BEST location for easy access to the Acropolis and museum in a quiet setting.  Great and extremely helpful staff.  Low traffic making for lower noise setting.
Rooftop dining and seating area offers a beautiful view of the Acropolis and city

Exceptional views of The Acropolis. The rooms were modern, very clean, the rooftop facilities (viewing The Acropolis and breakfast) were airy and bright. The staff, particularly on reception, were exceptional. We stayed there on 30 Sept/1 Oct 2024 and would be our first pick again when staying in Athens.

Absolutely fantastic stay. Staff was very helpful and hospitable. They provided a map and restaurant recommendations. 24hr bar is a plus. The view of the acropolis at night is spectacular. They even made us breakfast boxes and cappuccinos at 6am since we had to leave for the airport early. Thank you!

Stayed here for 4 nights while in Athens. Rooms are small but very comfortable. Beds are VERY comfortable. Our room had a queen size and twin bed with a patio facing the back. When making reservations make sure your room has a balcony. Not all rooms have a view of the Acropolis. Staff is incredible - welcoming, attentive, answer all your questions and take care of any need. Location is perfect…just a few minutes walking distance to the Acropolis, Plaka, and other choice locations.  Price is worth it and very reasonable. Roof-top patio has an incredible view of the Acropolis. Breakfast buffet is awesome. I would definitely stay here again on my next trip to Athens. Thank you!

Great little hotel, close to the Acropolis and good restaurants. We stayed in room #10, the rooftop terrace. The room isn't huge but it doesn't need to be. Everything was clean and well maintained. Only drawback is there's no people elevator (there is one just for your luggage), so you get a little workout climbing 4-5 flights of steps.  While the view from the rooftop terrace isn't as grandiose as from some other hotels, it's still very nice.  You can see the parthenon a little bit between two other buildings.  Room was very reasonably priced and to us, well worth the price.

OMG - these hosts go out of their way to make you happy. Comfortable as possible. We met the general manager and owner upon our stay.

We had booked directly with the hotel on the hotel site. When we arrived they did everything to get us a fast check in. After international flights one is always ready to get some rest!

Feel free to ask them about anything. If they don’t have the answer they will get it for you!

Very cozy and nice place to stay in Athens. Location is perfect, close to center, subway and car rentals. Not overpriced and has good breakfast package selections. Rooms are very clean and modern. Staff was always welcoming and smiling. Definitely recommended.

Had a lovely stay!  Staff was so very helpful and kind.  The apartment was exceptionally clean and modern and quiet. It was located close to dining, shopping and sight seeing. Definitely want to stay here again!!

Spotless and modern rooms that are well-furnished. Great location and price too. I appreciate the strong security of the hotel too. Check in service was great - we got our room early and the lady was friendly and polite. There was free coffee and juice at the reception too. Overall fantastic hotel stay experience!
Very nice and lovely apartment. Best location in the center of Historical Athens. Well done guys perfect job!
Nice and fancy. Only one toilette so thats why only 4 stars.
Rude host
Nice, clean comfortable and close to  the main tourist attractions.
Your Homme in Athens !!!

Absolutely perfect accommodation. These guys have thought of everything, and the quality is excellent. Perfectly clean and wanting nothing more, except a toaster. It was cool (2 air conditioners), quiet,  dark (shutters), dual big tvs, everything is new and works perfectly. They are friendly,  helpful (carried bags and there is a lift) and we loved the location. Extremely happy.

Fantastic location, beautifully presented, exceptionally clean, has everything you could ask for including 2 big screen tv's with Netflix, Bluetooth sound bar to enjoy your tunes, great kitchen, bathroom, laundry with dryer etc. Great service from Alex, these guys have thought of everything.  I loved  Bill and John's development. Top quality fit out, dual air conditioning, limitless hot water, etc etc.Dud i mention it was also a good price!

There is no better way to describe it than absolutely EXCELLENT!  We loved everything about it. Alex was our host and he met us when we arrived, showed us around the apartment and took us to the roof to see the amazing view. It was close to shopping, food, and attractions. I loved, loved, loved this place!

This suite is perfect, the house owner Bill and John are super nice. They introduced us the must visit places in the city and some good restaurants for sure!
And the hardware and location of the suite are perfect as well
Great suite, superb location to Acropolis, dining and shopping.  Everything perfect all the way to the coordinated transportation to airport.

Everything I would want and could say has already been said before. Whether it’s the location of the residence next to the Acropolis or its setting in a safe and quiet neighborhood. The decor of the apartment and its beauty, the comfortable beds, the jacuzzi. Every detail has been thought out to make it comfortable for the guests. What certainly stands out is the attention our host Andreas had for us. From the informative check-in, where our children were also wonderfully involved, to the helpfulness with questions. The recommendations for culture and dining were very welcome and we were well-prepared, yet still pleasantly surprised. What sticks with me the most is the consistency in his hospitality. I have read through the other reviews and all the aforementioned points consistently come up. I could go back six years in the reviews. I sincerely hope, Andreas, that in the coming years you will continue to welcome your guests in the natural, cheerful, and human manner you did for us last week. A week in which we felt at home. πολλα ευχαριστα

The residence has a perfect location, absolutely clean, comfortable and well designed:
Andreas is a wonderful host, thought of every single detail.
The hot tub is wonderful!
We will definitely come back

Feels like home! This is our second time in two weeks to stay at this gorgeous apartment and as I walked in the second time I said out loud “This feels like home!” It’s such a comfortable and well furnished apartment from the bottle of wine, beer, & coffee to the hot tub and cute seating area outside! The beds and bedding were super comfortable!

Location! Location! Location! This great apartment is just a couple blocks from the base of the Acropolis and just a few blocks from dozens of restaurants and stores. Andreas has suggested many wonderful places for breakfast, coffee, and dinner. Sadly, we only had 4 nights in Athens which wasn’t enough time to try them all. Andreas was also always available with suggestions, recommendations, ideas, etc any time we asked.

Bottom line: We would definitely stay here again when visiting Athens the next time!
We absolutely loved the place and we are sure you will too :) Location is perfect, set up perfect and the welcome and help from Andreas is exceptional! We would be very glad to come back in future

Our stay at SV Acropolis Residence, hosted by Andreas, was absolutely phenomenal! From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with genuine warmth and hospitality that made us feel right at home.

The apartment itself was nothing short of stunning. Its design and impeccable cleanliness left a lasting impression. The spacious layout provided more than enough room for our family of four, allowing us to unwind comfortably after long days of exploring the city.

The highlight of the residence was undoubtedly the breathtaking view of the Acropolis right outside our doorstep.

Andreas, our host, was simply outstanding. His attention to detail and dedication to ensuring our stay was perfect was evident in every interaction we had with him. He provided us with valuable recommendations for local attractions and restaurants, helping us make the most of our time in Athens.

The location of the residence was ideal, situated close to major landmarks the Acropolis and Monestiraki (Plaka). This convenience allowed us to effortlessly explore the historical wonders and vibrant culture of Athens.

Furthermore, the apartment's amenities were top-notch, including a fully equipped kitchen, airconditioning in all bedrooms and living toom, reliable Wi-Fi, and a hot tub, making our stay even more comfortable and enjoyable.

In summary, SV Acropolis Residence and our host Andreas exceeded all expectations, making our three-night stay in July an unforgettable experience for our family. If you're planning a trip to Athens, this is undoubtedly the place to stay. Thank you, Andreas, for your exceptional hospitality and for creating lasting memories for our family! We can't wait to return.

Marianna, our hostess,  epitomizes GREAT customer service and care. When our visits to the archaeology sites on the Acropolis, including the Parthenon  were at the height of the heat wave impacting Athens, she checked in on us when we returned and made sure we were okay. Her recommendations for restaurants were fantastic, from formal to casual. The food was wonderful as was the service. We had an unfortunate cancellation with our airline and when we reached out to her for two more nights stay, she accommodated us quickly,  “ Come back and relax”.

One more personal touch to add more,  she  saved the wine from the night before that my spouse and I didn’t finish. WOW!

I love this hotel but it is the service from Marianna, that takes this experience above and beyond.

Noble Suites was an awesome place to stay. The location is 10/10 which made a big difference. You are about a 5 min walk from Acropolis. Breakfast was also very good and peaceful.

Mariana did a very good job making sure everything was ok ALL the time. The rooms are not your average size rooms. I stayed in the 2 bedroom, 3 bathroom suite which was very big. This is not your average hotel either - the lobby is very small and the hotel only about 5 hotel rooms in the whole place. That being said it was a very enjoyable stay.

Such a wonderful boutique hotel and extraordinary service.  Our hostess, Mariana, made us feel at home with high touch service.  The location is less than 100 yards from the Acropolis. Mariana arranged transportation from the cruise port at Piraeus to the hotel and from the hotel to the airport.  Finally, she provided a wonderful recommendation for dinner at Dionysos Zonar's which had a spectacular view of the Acropolis.

We stayed in the Junior Suite for six nights and it was the perfect size for 2 adults and 2 kids. The room was very clean and our kids found the sofa bed very comfortable. It didn’t bother us at all that the room is technically in the basement. We still were able to get plenty of sunlight and fresh air from the windows and although you didn’t have a view it didn’t matter because the acropolis is a very short walk away. The bathroom was on the smaller side but it was very clean and served our needs. Also the shower is enclosed which apparently isn’t always the case in Athens. Breakfast was always delicious and the perfect way to start our day. The service we received from everyone during our stay was wonderful and we would not hesitate to recommend this hotel.

Spectacular little place to stay at the foot of the acropolis! It’s close to the major sights and public transit, and the service is amazing. I stayed there on my birthday and the staff, especially Mariana, treated me like family. It felt like less of a hotel stay and more like staying with your aunt. I am on a gluten-free diet and they went above and beyond to accommodate my needs!
It was a great experience. All the positive reviews were actually true. I advice everyone and especially families of it. Thank you for welcoming us
Wonderful place to stay.  Great service.  Will stay here next time. Great location.
Right next to Acropolis, a great apartment to stay!
Great hosts. Everything was perfect.
yummy

We stayed here for a long weekend break and it was absolutely perfect for us. We got the studio as all the other rooms were full and it is a small room but perfectly functional for a couple. Bed was very comfy, room and bathroom modern and clean.

Breakfast was served outside on in the seating area just outside the hotel lobby and served from one of the bars. We had perfect weather for this but not sure how that would be if it were cold and wet. Also there were a few birds that kept flying in to finish off peoples left overs when they were too lazy to move their plates to the clearing area.
Breakfast was great for us with a choice of hot or cold food and drinks.
The staff were very polite and helpful too.

Location is excellent as it’s just a five minute walk into the Main Street next to the acropolis museum. Loads of amazing restaurants and little shops right on your doorstep.

Loved this place and great pricing. Staff explained everything and were friendly upon arrival. Amazing views from room of the acropolis. Clean and spacious room. Location fantastic to walk to many sights. Loved our stay here. Only downside was the cleaners make noise in morning (I was trying to sleep in well past 9am) but they have a job to do and I am probly the only one sleeping that late 😅 great place!

Sooooo, interesting experience. We needed 5 beds, so our room had a Hostel feel. 1 bedroom with queen/bunk, and 2 single beds in the studio area. 1 bathroom, which isn't ideal with 3 teenagers.
Staff were great. Location was excellent. That's why we stayed here. Within walking (10 min) to Acropolis entrance.
Parking was an experience. Drove around block of hotel 4x until I could park.

Almost a perfect stay! The hotel truly has an incredible location, offering quick access to a lot of restaurants, and sites. The staff was very kind and the breakfast was good.

Unfortunately,we got bitten by a bedbug which we found and killed on our bed during our second night at the hotel.

Hoping the administration takes the necessary measures to inspect and sanitize the rooms properly. And a reminder to travellers to always check your rooms no matter where you go.

Wonderful position at less than 5 minutes walk from the Akropolis and the lively nearby area where very good restaurants are waiting for you.
Rooms are a bit small but clean and comfortable and the bathroom is bigger than you would expect.
Staff is very kind and gentle and shows a genuine will to help make your stay better.
Defnitely the hotel of choice for short stays in Athens.

Excellent downtown apartment located in Koukaki residential area. Koukaki is a pleasant neighborhood on the rise. Many eateries and shops nearby. All major archaeological sites within half a walking distance.
The apartment has two bedrooms with double beds, two bathrooms and a living room with kitchen.

We spent 5 days as a family in this apartment. It is very beautiful, clean, modern, equipped with everything. There are 4 of us and there are 2 bedrooms and two bathrooms.
