The kitchen is open in the living room, fully equipped and fully functional. The large TV and the very comfortable sofa complete the living area. The area is residential and quiet, convenient for reaching the archaeological sites of Athens on foot. It is definitely a place to have a wonderful holiday in Athens.

We had a great experience here! The host of this establishment is a very kind and helpful guy. He provides all the information you can possibly need and will make sure you have a great stay. Also the apartment was very clean and the location is exactly where you wanna be in Athens with lot of things to do in the area.
Absolutely loved our stay. Hosts were amazing and very accommodative. Lovely spot close to everything we wanted to see. The jacuzzi is definitely the star of this apartment!

Very cosy and comfortable apartment that has more than anyone could ask. The Jacuzzi is super good and helped us relax in a nicely decorated yard. Hosts gave us EVERYTHING we needed to have a nice time in Athens.
Amazing apartment and facilities. Superb location close to Acropolis and a number of shops and restaurants. Hosts are extremely helpful and accommodated all of our needs

Very clean, location was good and the host Hector
was very nice.

Loved the apartment and the owner (very nice and communicative woman). Location is perfect, makes it possible to get to the main attractions in the city centre by foot. But watch yourself with the air conditioning, i cranked it up a little too much and got a cold 🤧
Clean, with all the things needed. The owner is helpful. Even give me directions to a few places to visit.

The apartment was very good, check-in was easy. We followed the instructions that we received by message to take the keys in a little box before entering and with all the indications to know which is our door. The apartment is spacious, with all the comforts. It has a kettle, toaster and coffee maker. Very happy.
Very attentive treatment and homely place

Modern apartment fully equiped with every kind of utensils, excellent spot for someone who wants to stay in a safe and quiet neighbourhood just 15min walk away from Acropolis.
Clean and cozy with a 5* Bathroom , way better than many 5* hotels. Giannis the landlord was amazing and very helpfull and available at any time we needed him. I strongly recommend this apartment and I would definetely stay there again in my next trip to Athens.

The apartment was modern with stylish design and super clean. The kitchen was provided with coffee maker and everything else we needed.Also,the beds were very comfortable. Great location and super convenient to go anywhere. A good base to explore Athens. Really helpful and kind host, easily contactable using WhatsApp and provide non touristy information. Clean and modern settings - love it!I will definitely come back!!!!

I couldn’t have asked for a more comfortable, convenient, and welcoming place to stay in Athens!The best thing about Denaxas apts was the service. Giannis was absolutely lovely, helping us out with questions leading up to the trip, recommendations while we were there and everything else we needed. We loved the location and found the neighborhood to be vibrant and very close to the Acropolis. The nespresso machine is a huge plus for me as well and the showers are great. Would definitely stay here again!!
Lovely apartment, cosy, clean and well equipped. Ideal location very close to the Acropolis in a vibrant neighbourhood.
The apartment is in a very nice location full renovented beautiful decoration and its very near at acropolis museum and also near at metro station! I recommended to you!🙂

The apartment is immaculate in every way if you are noise tolerant. For me personally (I was traveling without a child), the noise did not interfere with my sleep, but I have St. Petersburg training, and I walked 10 km a day and got tired. And here is probably the noisiest street in the city: many restaurants and bars (life is in full swing from 7 pm to 4 am), cats are fighting, car alarms are blaring. But in the morning there is complete calm.

Otherwise, it is spotlessly clean, fully equipped down to the ironing board, and the hostess promptly answers questions. The TV was not very useful, since it is not a smart TV, alas. The air conditioners are silent and work great. The bed may be a little soft, but you can sleep on the sofa, it is harder.

From a location point of view, it’s ideal, 20 minutes walk to the Acropolis. Within walking distance are several pharmacies, supermarkets, and a second-hand bookstore with literature in different languages.

I definitely recommend it.

Beautiful and functional apartment , very clean and comfortable! Next to many walking attractions.
Consistent and reliable hosts.

Very nice and comfortable apartment, very clean. Gracious hostess. It is very close to the historic center and historical attractions. You can find nice shops for food and drink. I will visit again on my next trip.
Very clean apartment, very helpful hosts and very close to all the archaeological sites!
Very nice place, central and convenient!!!! very good hospitality!!!
Perfect place to stay!!

Perfect location, nice and clean apartment.
Highly recommended.

Spacious living room, fully equipped kitchen, 3 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms.
Great location.
nice apartment in a very good location.
Excellent apartment with great location
Nice and clean apartment in the heart of Athens!

It is a very comfortable, warm environment like your home, they have thought of everything and put it together for you. The location is very nice, there are many nice restaurants, cafes and markets around it to meet your needs. Transportation from the airport is very easy, you can walk to central places very easily, and you can also reach it very easily by metro. We recommend.
A great apartment in a good location (shops, bars, restaurants, monuments), at a very good price. Equipped with everything you need for relaxation. I sincerely recommend it

Service was impeccable, rooms were clean and beautiful. We stayed in a temple of Zeus view room. We wished that the balcony had some furniture, but other than that, our stay was truly amazing. The FOOD is so SO incredible!!!!! Breakfast buffet was to die for and we had a dinner as well which was just amazing.
The hotel is also within a 5 minute walk of the Acropolis and Acropolis museum, along with many shops and restaurants. we will definitely return next time we are in Athens. Thank you for a lovely stay!

First place visited off the plane and it was wonderful.

The staff is amazing.  One of our group was missing luggage, so we congregated on the rooftop bar to commiserate, and after some beer and ouzo and amazing views of the Acropolis, all our worries were far away.

I can't say enough how wonderful and accommodating the staff is.  (I would say 5 stars ++)

Breakfast was delicious with a wide range of options.  Eaten in the rooftop patio...again, what a view!

Perfectly located for exploring.  The Acropolis is near as is the Parliament, shops and other landmarks.

It was a wonderful stay

I recently stayed at this fantastic hotel in the heart of Athens, and it exceeded all my expectations. The highlight of my stay was the breathtaking view of the Acropolis, which could be enjoyed right from the breakfast restaurant. It was truly a sight to behold, especially at night when the Acropolis was beautifully illuminated.

The rooms were incredibly comfortable, spacious, and well decorated. The bed was plush and provided a perfect night's sleep, and the room was equipped with all the modern amenities one could need. The attention to detail in the room's design and cleanliness was good.

Breakfast at the hotel was another standout feature. Served in a charming dining area with equally stunning views of the Acropolis, the breakfast buffet offered a wide variety of delicious options, from fresh fruits and pastries to traditional Greek dishes. The quality of the food was excellent, and the staff were attentive and friendly, ensuring a delightful start to each day.

The hotel's central location made it incredibly convenient for exploring Athens. Major attractions, shops, and restaurants were all within walking distance, allowing me to make the most of my time in this vibrant city.

Overall, my experience at this hotel was exceptional. The combination of the prime location, comfortable rooms, spectacular views, and outstanding breakfast made it a memorable stay. I would highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Athens.

Absolutely top notch from the very beginning. Front desk staff helped us sort out a crooked cab driver. Rooms and location were great. Free breakfast at the roof top lounge has some amazing views. Would 100% recommend to anyone.

Book the rooftop restaurant for dinner!!! Please pleeee please do this as soon as you make a reservation here. Having dinner under the acropolis was our highlight. Not to mention, the food was tasty!! So worth it.

We loved how close it was to everything. Literally everything. You could walk to all the things you needed to see in Athens and you were also close to the train station.

The hotel felt very safe, so did the area.

The staff were amazing. We had the nicest girl
Greet us and the nicest man say good bye to us when we left. They gave us lots of tips not just about Athens,  but for the rest of our trip around the island.

The room was  clean and exactly what we needed.

We were recommend this by a friend and we will keep recommending to more friends.

We stayed here end of August 2024.

I had a very late flight out of Athens. The reception staff were very warm and caring looking after my luggage and giving me a quiet place to relax until my transfer arrived. The decor is beautiful, and the hotel is situated very close to the Acropolis. They opened the rooftop bar before I left and made me a snack. Simply breathtaking!! 🥂♥️

Stayed for two nights post a cruise.

Good location, rooms a good size and I enjoyed a bath.  We were in an executive room.

Breakfast was good.

Unfortunately we didn't get to the rooftop due to a wedding.

Easy walking distance to Acropolis and Plaka.

Staff were very friendly and helpful.

This hotel is absolutely outstanding, especially in terms of the service provided.  From the moment we checked in, every single person we encountered was incredibly friendly and helpful.  We especially appreciated the help with our luggage, something that is rare these days unless you are staying in a much more expensive hotel.  The rooftop restaurant has a beautiful view of the Acropolis, and the staff there was amazing as well.  I would definitely recommend this hotel to anyone staying in Athens.  You will not be disappointed.

This hotel meet our needs and is located in an area that allows you to seek out and see many of Athens tourist highlights. It is located approx. 30 minutes away from the Ports and approx. 40 mins from airport dependent on traffic (there is a lot of traffic in Athens). Rooms were very clean and appointed nicely with the bed mattress being comfy. The staff were both pleasant and helpful, making sure we had a box breakfast on the morning of an early morning departure to catch a ferry. The restaurant and bar on the roof top with a spectacular view of the Acropolis at night is worthy of at least a cocktail visit.

We decided to have dinner at the Athenian Callirhoe Hotel Restaurant while visiting Athens. The staff was friendly and even though we did not have a reservation they seated us at a table with a view of the Acropolis and the Parthenon which was absolutely beautiful.  The menu included lamb chops, fresh fish--tsipoura (sea bream) and salads; we ordered one of each and we were not disappointed! They were delicious and plentiful! Will definitely consider it again when we find ourselves in Athens.

I had a pleasant stay at this hotel, and there are several positive aspects to highlight.

Firstly, the location is excellent—it’s close to many tourist spots, which is a big plus for those looking to explore the city. The rooftop is really nice, offering a cozy view where you can even see the Acropolis, making it a perfect spot to enjoy the sunset. The hotel itself is nice, with well-distributed facilities and a decent breakfast. I also appreciated having access to the fridge, which came in handy.

However, there were a few issues that affected my overall experience. On the day of checkout, I was leaving early at 6:30 AM, but the reception was closed, and there was no staff available to assist. I wasn’t able to retrieve my food from the fridge, which I had prepared for my travel day, and the same thing happened to two other guests heading to the airport. There should definitely be someone available for early checkouts, even if it’s just for small tasks like greeting or farewelling guests.

Another minor inconvenience was the towel fee—while it’s common for hostels to provide towels for free, the 2 euro charge wasn’t too bad, but still, it’s worth mentioning.

Lastly, I noticed that the bedsheets had an unpleasant smell, like they were damp or not properly washed. I had the same sheets for three days, and the smell was quite strong. I recommend checking the linens carefully before using them for guests to ensure they are fresh.

Overall, it’s a good place to stay, especially for solo travelers looking to meet new people. The hotel offers a friendly atmosphere, and the breakfast is definitely worth it. However, some small improvements could make the experience even better.

Great location, my room was clean and secure, decent breakfast. As far as amenities, I've stayed in much nicer hostels, there's hardly any common area, and the best feature of the hostel, the rooftop, doesn't open till 6pm. Maintenance and cleanliness/supplies in bathrooms could be improved. But if you're looking for a cheap, safe stay near the Acropolis, a quick walk from the Metro, this is a good option.

This hostel has a great location to public transport,  attractions and restaurants. The rooms were clean and a good size with locker space for backpacks. Beds were comfy and had a night stand with plug. The AC was amazing in the heat aswell. Staff were polite but not overly warm. Breakfast was basic but substantial. They did allow us to leave our bags at reception prior to check in which was helpful.  Overall would recommend.

Stayed here in late May 2024.
Location is perfect for accessing the metro (Akropoli station), Acropolis Museum, and majority of the ruins (Acropolis, both Agoras, Hadrian's library, etc - buy the multi access ticket from the Hellenic Heritage website). A lot of food nearby and the Plaka neighborhood is not far for touristy shopping and more food.
Rooms are an okay size, lockers roomy enough for a backpack, BYO lock but also they sell them for a high price in the lobby vending machine. Multiple people's luggage cases can take up all the floor space. Bedding is not great and there were stains on the blankets. Bathrooms were clean but they do not clean out past guests' toiletries that were left behind, so it piles up, and they desperately need better ventilation and some towel racks in there.
Some staff are nice and helpful, some staff are incredibly rude. One person said we could fill our water bottles in the breakfast area, another yelled at us for trying to do it before the breakfast area was 'open.' No clear policy it seems. Breakfast is okay, more of a continental / UK breakfast fusion style.
The lobby is also the breakfast area and it's gated off at night so if you are trying to work remotely, there is virtually nowhere to sit. There is a rooftop bar with a sem-decent view of the Acropolis but the cheapest and most uncomfortable plastic chairs.
Okay for a one or two night stay/budget traveler, otherwise try one of the small boutique hotels in the neighborhood.

Location is great. The night time desk worker is creepy and mean. Made me wait outside for my 3am cab and there was a group of men being rowdy outside. Didn’t make me feel safe. Esp when I could have waited inside the seated area.  The linens smelled like cat pee. The location is great tho. And for what it is, it’s not bad but don’t expect great service.

Truly in the heart of Athens, just blocks from the Acropolis. Our room was spacious considering the size of the hotel, and the service has been outstanding! We arrived well before check in and the hotel staff did what they could to get us in early (our flight landed nearly an hour ahead of schedule). From that point on, the front desk staff attended to all of our needs. This excellent level of service is especially meaningful, as we are on the first leg of our honeymoon. I highly recommend this hotel for its location, quality and service, hope to stay here again on our next visit to Athens!

Hotel is in a great location convenient for the Acropolis site and Acropolis museum, but in a relatively quiet location. Many good bars and restaurants close by. Rooms are comfortable with a high standard of housekeeping. Great lounge/bar area on the ground floor and fabulous roof terrace bar and restaurant with great views of the Acropolis. Service was friendly and extremely helpful.

My husband and I had the pleasure of enjoying The Herodion Hotel on our recent trip to Athens. We had never been to the city before and were spending a short time there before departing for a cruise. Our decision to stay here was based on reviews online and we could not be more impressed. My husband and could not stop singing the Herodions praises, I lost count of how many times he would just sit back and say what a great choice the hotel had been and how much he enjoyed everything about it.
The location could not have been better. A short walk from the Acropolis, located in a quiet area of a vast city just bordering the historic Plaka neighborhood. Metro was located nearby making access to other districts simple.
The hotels rooms were absolutely pristine, with a wonderful shower and comfortable bed. We had the pleasure of booking a room with a view of the Acropolis and enjoyed morning coffees on our balcony. We used the hotels service to book transportation from airport to hotel and arrived before check in but they staff was wonderful. From the moment we arrived and had a member of staff rush down to the street to help with our luggage, to the front desk securing our bags while we took lunch and explored the area prior to check in.
We were told we could also utilize the hot tubs (which have fantastic views as well) even before our room was ready if we liked.
We had lunch in the conservatory room which was delicious and dinner at the rooftop restaurant which was probably the most delicious food I had on my vacation.
When we left the hotel we were able to once again enjoy the ease of having the welcoming staff arrange our transport to the Port.
We can not recommend this Hotel more, from cleanliness to hospitality there was no fault to be seen and we plan to stay here on any future stays as well as recommend it to family and friends.

We had a great stay at this hotel. Very friendly and welcoming staff. The room we had was great and could fit a crib in for our little one. Easy walk to Acropolis (5mins) and plenty of restaurants and bars close by. They also offer a great breakfast in the morning. Would definitely stay here again.

The hotel staff have been wonderful the entire stay. Every staff member is warm and exceptionally friendly. No task is too difficult and every request is met with a smile. The hotel restaurant on the roof top has phenomenal views of the Acropolis, with a spacious rooftop bar and restaurant. Our party of 4 enjoyed gourmet food, with each dish looking more like art than the last. The rooms are stylish and well maintained. We would highly recommend the Herodion hotel, we could not fault it. Overall, an exceptional experience.

We stayed 2 nights and absolutely loved it.
They arranged pickup at the airport, and it was a seamless drive to the hotel.
A very friendly, easy check in, where we could decompress after our long journey.
Very close to all the attractions, a wonderful outdoor area, very comfortable bed, and lots of cats nearby outside.
And we had the same friendly driver back to the airport!

My husband and I stayed at the AVA HOTEL Athens, Greece - Wonderful Experience!!!!
The Ava Hotel was conveniently located to ALL Attractions.  The room was well equip with water and safe deposit box.  The impeccable service and hospitality of ALL the staff Mario, Penny, Magda to include but not limited to House Keeping made our stay most enjoyable/ pleasurable and delightful.  Furthermore, the breakfast  was scrumptious.  We were very impressed by the responsiveness of the hotel staff, who were extremely friendly, professional, and  very pleasant.  From start to finish, they were very attentive.  Don't look any further if you're going to be in  the City of  Athens  the AVA HOTEL is the place to be close to ALL Attractions within walking distance. The views to the Acropolis are amazing.
Very respectfully
From the Sunshine State of Florida
N/F

I can't say enough good things about this hotel! The service was outstanding. They were the most accommodating group of professionals we have ever had. We had chosen the studio on the top floor which has a huge open deck with panoramic views of the city and the Acropolis.  It was just what we wanted. We were the only ppl on that floor and it had everything we needed. A small frig, coffee and tea service,  free water, a comfy bed, room darkening shades with a tiny bath. We chose to include breakfast which was a feast each morning with the best fresh squeezed orange juice ever. The location of this hotel was perfect. We could walk to most everything we wanted to do. We were on the edge of the old city and steps from the Acropolis. They accommodated all our requests for cabs when needed as well as early and late check in and check out. It is a small boutique hotel but big on service and quality!

Absolutely lovely hotel. Our room was large and they even had two roll away beds already set-up in the living room for my children as we checked in. Room was spotless, well furnished, and beautiful. We had a huge balcony that we sat out on each night to watch the city lights and relax. Perfect location to get to all the sites we wanted to see, lots of shops and restaurants all around. The staff was so helpful and friendly and we all enjoyed the breakfast every morning. One morning our tour left really early and the staff kindly prepared to go bags of sandwiches, fruit and cheese so we had something to eat. Would definitely stay here again and can't recommend it enough.

We recently enjoyed an amazing three-night stay at this Athens hotel. The staff's friendliness and constant smiles created a welcoming atmosphere for us. The central location made exploring Athens effortless, with major sights like the acropolis within easy reach, which we could literally see from our balcony.

The rooms were quiet, clean, and attentively cared for, providing a peaceful retreat after a day of sightseeing. Despite its central location, the hotel maintained a serene ambiance, ensuring a restful night's sleep.

Overall, this hotel exceeded my expectations in service, location, and comfort. I highly recommend it for a memorable stay in Athens!

Super convenient location. Walking distance to the Parthenon and the museum. Half a block to tons of shopping and dining options.  1 block to the metro is a huge plus, too.

The hotel is well staffed with very helpful employees who truly want you to have a great experience.

The rooms are clean and well kept if slightly dated. GREAT water pressure and almost instant hot water!

The breakfast was good with lots of options to choose from. Cereal, pastries, bread, meats and cheeses, and of course fruit. With coffee, tea,orange juice,sour cherry, and milk to drink.

We would absolutely stay here again.

This hotel is in a perfect location and for the price you can’t beat it! Just one block from the Acropolis south entrance and the best walking street with so many amazing restaurants all around. The breakfast is phenomenal the staff are wonderful. They have the best deal in the bar for happy hour from 4pm-8pm BOGO drinks for €6.50 and the bar area and lobby are beautifully decorated. The rooms are basic but cozy and spacious. Overall had a very nice stay!

Great location, literally a step away from Acropolis. The MVP is the AM shift manager, was nice enough to give me the room as soon as possible which was a lifesaver after a 12hr red eye flight. The breakfast was good too. The only thing is the rooms are quite dated.

Exceptional hotel with excellent service, one of the best breakfasts ever at a hotel (included in rate), great location and expansive rooms. Location is central to major historical sites, a plethora of stores and restaurants, metro/bus/tram stops. We walked to everything except for our cool tram ride to the Greek Riviera. Front desk staff, housekeeping staff and breakfast staff are top notch. In the morning, you can treat yourself to a large variety of breakfast items, freshly made, hot and cold. Great communication prior to and during our 3 day stay.

This hotel is in a perfect location to walk to all the sites! The street is not noisy or too busy. The staff was very welcoming and helpful! The room was clean and had all you needed; the fixtures and linens are a little dated, but again all you needed. Nice breakfast choices too. Thank you!

We traveled as a party of 2 without our kids in June for the first time to celebrate our anniversary and we splurged on the Double Athenian Panorama Room. Wow, the wall of windows that overlooked the Temple of Zeus was breathtaking day and night. The room was very spacious and clean. It had a very comfortable bed, a sofa and seating area, a desk area, a large wardrobe, robes and slippers, a large marble bathroom with a luxurious double tub (which I’d never seen before), and a flat screen tv. It was perfect for our 3-night getaway in Athens. It was convenient to all the central sites, as we did a self-guided 12-hour walking tour and never needed a taxi. The breakfast included was good and the rooftop terrace restaurant was quiet and convenient. The staff were pleasant and helpful when we needed them, which we really didn’t except for a transfer to the airport. We didn’t try room service or the pool so we can’t comment on those amenities. We got everything we paid for regarding our accommodation here and couldn’t be more pleased. A great value. We’d stay in this room again in a heartbeat!

A lovely hotel in a perfect location, just 10 minutes walk to Acropolis southern entrance. Good sized, very clean rooms, nice sized bathrooms, with shower over bath. Top floor bar overlooking Acropolis and the park. Breakfast was good with lots of choice and good service, also located in restaurant on top floor to enjoy the views. Would certainly return here.

The room was clean and comfortable. It was huge and a very nice room. The bathroom was amazing. We enjoyed our time there. The breakfast was part of the room and delicious. The staff was helpful. The view is good but not as amazing as the hotel photos. The area is decent but I wasn't impressed with most of Athens.

Large well appointed rooms with big bathrooms.  Everyone at the hotel was friendly and eager to offer assistance. The rooftop bar and restaurant provided lovely views and friendly service. The bar did their best on cocktails....stick to the menu and you won't be disappointed.

Elegant hotel with fine furniture appointments. Satisfactory rooms, neat, clean, luxurious towels. Room lighting was low, no tea or coffee provided in the room, cental temperature control with delayed response. Helpful staff. Elegant roof top dining with panoramic views.

Every single staff member was obliging and helpful. The restaurant was amazing. The location perfect. The Divani Acropolis hotel had lovely energy..
That iced water station in the entry was the perfect touch after coming in from the scorching Athens heat!

We recently stayed here and loved the location and enjoyed the pool. The room was clean and comfortable and a clear priority. The cleaning and support staff were genuinely friendly and helpful.
The only issue was the attitude and demeanor of many of the staff members; a few front desk staffers, the breakfast servers, and bar tenders. They were short and unfriendly. It was very strange to be received this way.  We were practically yelled at when we shared our disappointment about something. Even though the problem was rectified, I felt embarrassed by the person shaming us for a complaint. The breakfast servers avoided bringing us coffee and getting our own wasn’t an option. Finally, on the last day there was a taxi strike, but we received no notification. An email would have been nice. I’m guessing this review will make little difference, but it honestly left such a bad impression, I was happy to leave.

Big shout out to Bell Captain Michael for saving our trip! We dropped our bag with all our money, credit cards and passports in it. We were 1/2 way up the hill and he came running after us to see if it belonged to someone in our group. Not only did he chase us down but also asked us to verify the contents to confirm it was getting to the rightful owner. Also want to give commendation to Maria in the restaurant who has the sweetest smile and friendly demeanor.  Such a great stay in this beautiful hotel with a very honest and friendly staff. We will stay here every time we return to Greece thanks to them.

Stayed 3 nights and was impressed with this hotel. The most wonderful experience in Athens was the restaurant on the roof of the hotel as the view of the Acropolis at night has to be seen to be believed. Don't go by the website reservations as tables were available even when they weren't shown. We visited this restaurant each of our 3 nights there and received wonderful service even if just asking to have drinks. The photos we took of the Acropolis where so good that friends accused us of using a postcard or the web. It's really that good. Breakfasts at their downstairs restaurant were also excellent. The hotel is very conveniently located to tour both the Acrpolis and the Acropolis museum by foot. I can't recommend it enough.

Michael, the Bell Captain was a highlight of our stay.  Genial and professional, he made us feel more at home and comfortable. Helpful in information about surrounding area. Here’s an asset to your hotel.

Thermis at the desk, an extraordinary ambassador! Welcoming and helpful to an all of us traveling from Hawaii!

Dinning waitstaff is superb!

I stayed here at the end of July with my two boys, and we absolutely loved it! Such a cozy little gem! The hotel is perfectly located just a short walk from the city center, making it super easy to explore Athens on foot. Plus, there's public transport nearby, which made it so convenient to get to the beach! The staff were incredibly friendly and went above and beyond when we had an issue with a forgotten bag—they helped us recover it without any hassle. The highlight, though, is definitely the stunning rooftop. The view of the Acropolis is unbeatable, and the restaurant up there is top-notch. I would absolutely stay here again!

Wonderful experience! We stayed here one night prior to our cruise. The staff is very kind and helpful. We received welcome drinks at the rooftop bar and also had excellent authentic Greek food. The hotel itself is beautiful with a modern look. And you have a great view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar!
The room had a good amount of space for us. The shower was a little smaller than we are used. They were also to take our luggage for us while we explored the town before and after our reservation. The shop and restaurants are a short walk from the hotel. Overall great experience!!

Best part of the stay is the wonderful staff and the beautiful common area. The room was nicely appointed but poorly lit (need a light near the entry door) and the bathroom was poorly designed — strange tub with very little room to stand to take a shower then the shower curtain sticks to you. Hardly any room to step out of the tub/shower without bumping into the vanity or toilet. No outlet in bathroom so the hair dryer sits on the desk in the main part of the room and is plugged underneath. First room I had suffered from an unpleasant smell in the drains; second room was better. The staff absolutely could not be any nicer, but the room was a disappointment.

The staff is friendly, and the room is clean, though it is small. The elevator is tiny, but I later discovered that many elevators in Greek hotels are similar. The only downside was the shower. However, the location is perfect, just 5 minutes from the Acropolis Museum.

Everything about this place was perfect! The air conditioning was heaven after long hot days of exploring, great location to get around, stocked with everything we needed and the balcony was such a nice touch to enjoy our morning coffee (provided) and our wine & snacks from a local gem down the street. The only issue was the front door to the building itself definitely needs to be replaced, but it was worth every second of minor inconvenience! Highly recommend staying here if you're visiting Athens! Wonderfully romantic for couples.

The hotel was excellent in every aspect. I believe the hotel's location was one of the best areas, and with a little walk, you could see some of the most beautiful places, as well as experience the vibrant nightlife.
In terms of internet and, I have to say they were good. For breakfast, you had different options and fruits and everything was served neatly.
‏The staff's behavior was truly great, They also helped us to book a tour and were helpful and kind.

This hotel is UNSANITARY. my body (including face and neck) is covered with BED BUGS bites which are very painful and itchy.
The first room I was in had the worst shower head I've seen and in the morning I had to fight off a roach just to get in the shower. They moved me to a different room and there were the bed bugs.
The hotel never mentioned anything about sanitizing my things or offer any solution, they even suggested I might be lying (until they saw the bed bugs with their own eyes). Added pics and videos.

You have to drag your suitcases up a few flights of stairs and there's an elevator only between the 1st and 3rd floor. And there's no one to help. No kettle in the room as well. Those things just seem basic to me.

The only plus is the location.

Never had this kind of experience in any hotel. I feel bad for posting this but I feel it's more important that people will know before booking this hotel.

Fantastic hotel, really helped us when we had problems with our Airbnb.  Staff so friendly and helpful.  The room was conformable and had everything we needed.
The whole hotel was very clean and tidy.
The breakfast was lovely,...hot food, pastries, juices, hot drinks.
Hotel is close yo evening you would want to see.
Highly recommended 👌

We stayed here for a quick visit in Athens.  The location was really great, a short walk to the Plaka and Acropolis as well as Syntagma Square.
The room was a good size.  We were facing the main street, so there was a bit of noise from the traffic, but it quiets down eventually.  The bed was large and very comfortable.
Better than the location and comfortable bed was the staff.  I was trying to locate lost luggage and both Vassilis and Donna went above and beyond to help me.  They were just great.
The cost of the room was quite reasonable and it was nice that breakfast was included.  I would certainly stay again.

My family and I had a wonderful stay at the Acropolis Museum Boutique Hotel. While the hotel wasn't hip or modern (it feels like it was furnished by an eclectic antique shop,) it was very clean and comfortable feeling.

In response to many reviewers concerns about the noise, we didn't find it to be a problem. Yes you're on a heavily trafficked street, but there wasn't much honking, emergency vehicles, or loud people. It's not quiet, but hey you are staying in the middle of the city!

We really enjoyed staying in this neighborhood, as the side streets in the Koukaki area had great food options, and felt very local and not touristy.

It is important to note that there are a number of stairs you must climb before you're able to reach the elevator, making it a bit difficult for those with mobility issues, and those with lots of bags. You climb a large flight from the street to the lobby, along with a small flight from the lobby to the next floor until you reach an elevator. If you are able-bodied, this is no issue at all.

Overall we had a stress-free time, and felt like this was a good option for the money.

Hotel is located in a quaint and quiet area of Athens within walking distance of the Acropolis and much more. There are many restaurants close by. We arrived before our rooms were ready, but they had a secure area that we could store our luggage so we could go and explore. Hotel itself was secure as well as you had to have a code to enter. Rooms were clean and spacious. Only complaint was in one of our rooms there was no hot water in the shower and one of their front desk personnel wasn't very friendly. Overall though, we would stay again.

The location is nice- in Plaka beside the acropolis and flea market. However, we had a bad experience, would not stay here again and would not recommend it. There are plenty of other decent 2/3 star hotels in the area
First, upon check in, we were told we would have to change rooms. We got 4 different explanations including there was something wrong with your current room, this is not the room you wanted, we are doing you a favour etc. When I sent a formal email to the hotel, I was told that someone at the hotel made a “mistake” and gave our “original” room to someone else and now we were just returning to it but had to wait for them to check out.
When we got to our “new room”, there was a bad smell like sewage from the bathroom. I told the gentleman at the helpdesk who just said “sorry” and I emailed management again- no reply this time. The smell was brutal before we ever used the bathroom and unbearable. Made it difficult to stay in the room.
We asked for a taxi pickup from the hotel to the airport at 3:20am. When we came down to check out, the gentleman at the desk said his colleague made a note of the request but didn’t actually do it! We ended up having to drag our bags out on the street at 3:30am (with my mother) to flag a taxi. Too many screw ups for a hotel that we paid decent money for.

Exactly as we expected. Location was perfect - close to everything we planned to visit. Children loved the National Garden which were really close.
Breakfast is small but also did the job before we went out to explore.
A trip to the terrace in the evening was a nice way to wind down.

On 23/05 we made a reservation through Booking, but we did not observe the date. At the same time we saw the error, we contacted Booking, as well as the person responsible for the hotel, informing them of what had happened.
To our surprise, the hotel charged us practically the daily rate, even though we explained the mistake, including offering to change it to the correct date, which was not possible.
It's a shame for a hotel that doesn't show solidarity with the customer and ends up getting paid twice if there was another reservation.
We even understand that a fine could be applied, but it is unreasonable for it to be a daily rate.
Due to the lack of understanding, as the person in charge informed us that we requested a late cancellation, which was not true, I do not recommend the hotel.
Lack of empathy, understanding and common sense on the part of the hotel.

EXCELLENT LOCATION!!! My husband and I have stayed at the Adam's Hotel twice. We arrived early in the morning. We were able to set the luggage aside until the room was ready. Staff we polite. Rooms are small yet comfortable with a balcony (keep in mind it is a historic area). The terrace view is beautiful, especially at night of the Acropolis. Location is a quick short walk to many shops and restaurants. They sell hop-on hop-off bus tour tickets, which take a full tour of Greece main attractions. Highly recommended staying at the Adam's Hotel it was a hidden gem!!

Took me a while to find this hotel while looking for one with a pool.
Location is top marks very close to all the sites Plaka, Acropolis, hop on busses, etc.
The hotel made an effort to give us a room with a view of the Acropolis which made our stay brilliant.
Breakfast is minimal but good.
Pool is small but ok.
Rooms are small and be careful with doors, open outwards and I pulled a toenail off.
Roof terrace is amazing the view is superb.
All in all will return. Thank you.

Just for the location alone it’s easily 5 stars. I visited for my 30th birthday last November, the room was lovely & big considering it seemed it would be small from the outside. Staff were lovely (gave the guy on the front desk the rest of my cat biscuits for his kitty when leaving & he showed me photos of it). Super easy to walk to all the main sights even for a pair of fatties like my mum & I

The staff were very friendly and helpful with any questions we had. Room was nice, the pool is a nice small pool to get into on the hot days and we really enjoyed sitting outside on the outdoor patio with a glass or two of wine. Reasonably priced and good walking distance to the acropolis, restaurants and other points of interest.

Overall, this hotel is excellent.  I would definitely stay here again.

From the friendliness and helpfulness of the people on the front desk, to the cleanliness of the rooms and facilities, to the pool, to the food in the restaurant - all great.

The view on the terrace is outstanding during sunrise and it's a nice place to be in the evening too.  Unfortunately our room was directly below that terrace and the noise of people scraping their chairs and tables back and forth at 10:30pm wasn't the greatest.

The room was compact but effective.  The glass bathroom door, however, is transparent at night with a light on so a bit awkward!

The only downside was how I was spoken to by one of the cleaners.  We had (wrongly) assumed that rooms would be cleaned unless the 'DND' sign was put out.  That's how every other hotel i've ever stayed in works.  I was angrily and aggressively corrected by a cleaner, who brandished the sign stating "this is for you, this is for you" over and over.  Given i was having an excellent day i decided not to make more of the interaction but her attitude was completely wrong.

Before arriving at Acropolis Hill Hotel, we saw pictures online that showed stunning views of the Acropolis. We thought "this is too good to be true for the price," but were pleasantly surprised that those pictures were true. The rooftop of Acropolis Hill Hotel has an incredible, uninterrupted, view of Athen's most iconic site - the Acropolis. The roof was a beautiful place to watch the city wake up in the mornings and to watch the Acropolis light up at night.

The location of the hotel is excellent not just for its views, but also for its walkability to the main historical sites, parks, and a great variety of restaurants and shops. It's in a beautiful neighborhood with access to everything you could want.

The staff at Acropolis Hill were friendly and helpful, providing tips for the best tour options in the city, restaurant recommendations, and baggage storage.

Perfect host - warm, friendly and knowledgeable. Perfect location - very accessible to restaurants, souvenir shops, museum and historical places. The hop on and off bus stop is right at our doorstep. Nice rental unit - We were given a unit with 3 bedrooms, very good for a family of 5. And the unit was generously stocked with food for breakfast and snacks. No washing machine so be prepared to send out for laundry if you intend to rent more than a week in our unit. Not a problem for us though.

Amazing location. We were last week with our 3 children.We were greeted by  Mr Takis Super helpful he gave us lots of tips and a map of Athens. He also picked us up from the airport and we used the private transfer service for more trips.The service of Mr.Chris was excellent.
The complimentary milk, tea, coffee and the makings of breakfast were very much appreciated.
We will definitely reach again,
Thank You !!
The apartment is big and a great price. It is very close to acropolis walking and restaurants in plaza. It was easy to reserve and the host and owner are great. Would definately come again
Perfect location in Athens. Everything in walking distance, but the best are the views from the rooms.
Good apartement and fab views but the windows need replacing  to keep the road noise out,which was horrendous and constant...I can sleep through anything except that..

Lovely little place in the heart of Athens.. I booked really late on the evening, but the host was really accommodating. she even helped me plan my next days trip to Delphi.

Self service breakfast was included in the extremely low price.. at the end of a few weeks traveling money is tight and this was such a lucky find 🙌
Was in Athens after a Mediterranean cruise and needed a place to stay for the night. It was perfect for a large group. There were nice beds, great courtyard, and a view of the Acropolis. I recommend.

The hostel is conveniently located in the center, it was reasonably clean.
The rooms were pretty small for 4 people. We actually booked a 3 person bedroom, which looked a lot more appealing, but we were given another dorm without notice or anyone to talk too, since staff was only there for check-out. Hostelworld and Google Maps Website says there is a possibility for luggage storage, but it was not available during our stay.
Doors in our dorm were very squeaky and loud, waking everyone up in the room as soon as someone enters or leaves. This was very annoying, since it seams easy to fix. Same can be said about the wooden bunk beds. They are very sketchy and shaky, seam to be for kids and are extremely noisy when making the slightest move. Not to mention the real fear of having the bed collapse during sleep (Im below 80kg ...).
Bathrooms were acceptably clean, but the only shower on our floor that you could lock was missing a curtain, so everything was constantly flooded. Its annoying and easily fixable issues like these that left a below average impression.

Good place to stay ata Athens. Very close to tourist place & public transport. Reasonable price with good facility. Receptionist also very nice & helpful, kuddos to Alex(one of staff here, very nice person & helpful). Will stay here again next time

thoroughly regrettable stay from start to end. Nadia at front desk was actively hostile, aggressive and racist. Took 5 minutes and tow calls just to open the front door, another 30 minutes and two more phone, several rounds of back and forth to complete check-in. At one point she tried to take the keys away from me. room did not feel safe, no personal locker in room, no fire extinguisher. Bathroom and shower, which is shared by the whole floor, are an afterthought: behind one locked door there is one toilet without a sink so can't even wash hands, and behind another locked door two connected showering cubicals which means only one can be used at a time. All sound - footsteps, chatting, doors - reverberate freely in the entire building. Beds do not have curtains for privacy. Did not bother with breakfast and promptly left the premise next morning.

Our daughter and friend stayed in an Acropolis view room with balcony to celebrate their combined 30th birthdays (they loved the room!). I emailed the hotel to ask if it was possible to have champagne and glasses waiting for their arrival. The staff were very happy to arrange this - with a note, a little birthday cake and snacks on a lovely tray with the chilled champagne and glasses. The birthday girls were in tears at the lovely surprise and enjoyed the balcony and view with their champagne. Thank you Philippos Hotel for making their stay even more special.

Great location near Acropolis, Museums and La Plaka. Gracious, hardworking and helpful staff, gave restaurant recommendations, scheduled tour and taxi. Excellent Greek breakfast. Stayed here 15 years ago and the quality and value are consistent. Rooftop terrace with direct view of the Acropolis, wine available.

Amazing location, spotless clean, but missing a few basic amenities (e.g. plug socket on only 1 side of bed, no plugs at desk or light around mirror), staff were really friendly and very helpful. Rooftop space gives fantastic views

Great hotel in a central position close to the entrance to the acropolis. Rooms were clean and all seemed to have a balcony . The buffet breakfast was very good with a wide selection, including Greek cakes and yoghurt. The only slight downside was the smell from the drain in the bathroom but hey it's Greece and the drains always smell in Greece

This hotel was amazing. we were not meant to stay, but after getting late from the aeroport ( 5 in the morning ) we went inside asking for a room ( even tho we knew it was nearly impossible. The man saw that I was really anxious for being outside in the dark, and let us stay. It was really nice and also, we were so close from the Acropolis that we could go walking. Also the hotel super clean.

An excellent location. We'd stay again for it's proximity tot he Acropolis and all of the walking and dining in Plaka. Easy walking to Temple of Zeus, Parliament and changing of the guard, the all-marble ancient Olympic stadium, Acropolis and Acropolis museum and more.

Rooms are a bit dated but nice. Standard European small bathrooms but everything was in working order. Small balcony and great views of Plaka and Acropolis. Rooftop lounge great for cocktails and small plates.

We took the budget room but there was no information available on booking.com that it is actually an underground room. The bathroom was actually 3 steps up inside the room making it very uncomfortable. So please don’t consider the budget room and make sure you get rooms 1st floor onwards. The location was more than perfect.
Very nice hotel in the middle of PLAKA, very hospitable and friendly staff. The owner makes all guests feel like one of the family. Highly recommended place and we will surely return.

Don’t buy the breakfast, it is not worth the €10. The hotel rooms are decent, but the bed was not so comfortable. Great location for walking to the historic monuments. The cleaning staff were very friendly.

The Good.

* Superb friendly reception staff, breath of fresh air to see genuine kindness

* Location brilliant as many have indicated

The Not So Good.

* Double room on floor 2 was so small, if we had 2 suitcases there would be zero room to stand, think TINY.

* Unfortunately one of noisiest hotel rooms I have ever been in, zero soundproofing. Noise from elevator and all bathrooms around us was horrible, beware if you are sensitive to alot of noise at night. You can hear all conversations in both corridors and surrounding rooms. We were woken on many occasions thinking someone was in room with us, those walking in corridors soubded so close.

* Yes a wet soggy mess on floor of bathroom after shower as door does not seal, beware dont leave anything on floor as it will be soaked.

* Bed is soft but with any lack of support so beware if you have back trouble

Very basic room and hotel with no frills, but couldn't have been more pleased for what it was. Ended up being one of our favourite places we stayed while in Greece. Friendly staff, great neighbourhood and our terrace room had a certain kind of charm to it. If you are looking for a budget place to stay in a nice, quiet neighbourhood, I cannot recommend Art Gallery Hotel enough.

The Art Gallery hotel offers a cozy, family-owned atmosphere where guests are warmly welcomed, almost as if they were visiting relatives. During my three-night stay, I found the rooms to be adequately clean, comfortable, and spacious, albeit with some minor wear and tear on the room and bathroom amenities. For instance, I encountered an issue with the door lock, but the friendly reception staff promptly provided a workaround with a smile, suggesting a clever trick to operate it until it was fixed.

One of the hotel's standout features is undoubtedly its staff, who consistently go above and beyond to assist guests with a genuine kindness that enhances the overall experience. While the breakfast provided was basic, it served its purpose well enough to fuel explorations of Athens' ancient wonders, and it also conveniently provided a snack for later in the morning at nearby restaurants.

The hotel's location is another highlight, situated in a quiet area yet still within easy reach of key attractions like the Plaka and the Acropolis. Despite its proximity to tourist hotspots, the neighborhood maintains a peaceful ambiance, with plenty of excellent dining options and cafes nearby. Moreover, the reasonable pricing adds to the hotel's appeal, offering good value for money considering its prime location and attentive service.

In summary, the Art Gallery hotel provides a pleasant stay characterized by familial hospitality, comfortable accommodations, and a prime location that allows guests to enjoy the best of Athens without feeling overwhelmed by crowds.
Great location, the rooms were clean and spacious, the staff was friendly and attentive, we really enjoyed our short stay and hope to return one day

The rooms aren’t anything special, but they have everything you need and more! The staff are the sweetest, coolest people!! So friendly and warm. I stayed here with my study abroad group multiple times over our 3 week trip since Art Gallery was considered our “home base.” By the end of the trip everyone in my group referred to this place as “home.” I even stayed an extra day and a half here with my boyfriend after my studying was done and he loves it just as much as I do!! Location is SUPERB, breakfast is delicious, but the staff make this place AMAZING! I highly recommend hanging out in the front desk area to get to know the staff and cool off in the AC. I’ll be back soon! ❤️

The hotel has a fascinating history, its foundations having sheltered the current owners’ grandparents as a bunker in WWII before they made it first an art gallery and then, building upward, this hotel. Don’t expect spanking new state-of-the-art ANYTHING. Expect inimitable charm, cleanliness, authenticity, helpful staff, and an excellent location. We loved it.

I stayed only one night in this hotel and I wish it was longer! Super friendly staff, and a super cute and cozy room. Rooms have AC and a little fridge. For me the mattress was the perfect firmness. The location couldn't be more perfect either - walking distance to the Acropolis and a crazy amount of nearby cafes and restaurants. I would gladly stay here again when I come back to Athens in the future (because I will be back, what a gorgeous city)

Perfect location for exploring the historic sites of Athens. Our room was small but clean and comfortable. We especially loved sitting on our balcony and admiring the acropolis at night. It is close to lots of restaurants, shops, and metro stations for getting to the airport. We really enjoyed our stay!

Simple hotel perfect for visiting the Acropolis. Balcony overlooking church with bells ringing on Sunday morning. Good air-conditioning. Airy room. Charming lady on reception. Did not try breakfast. Lots of places near by for takeaway coffee, ice cream,  plus restaurants and snacks

Exactly what we were looking for- No more, no less.

My husband and I stayed here for a couple of nights during a trip to celebrate our 7th wedding anniversary. Athens was the first leg of a 3-destination trip, and it was the location we had the least amount of time in, so we wanted to hit the ground running! This hotel worked out absolutely perfectly for this.

The room itself is nice, nothing special. Small, but practical and clean. It had a nice-sized private balcony with a small table and two chairs, and we had a direct view of the Acropolis. After a full day of exploring it was nice to unwind on the balcony with some wine, look at some stars, and listen to the sounds of nightlife.

The main draw that led me to book this hotel was location, location, location! You could not ask for a better area to stay! Walking distance to everything you want and need. Close enough to some nice nightlife, but just far enough away to be able to retire to your room and not be bothered by unwanted outside noise. We ended up buying a 48hr pass for one of the 'hop-on hop-off' busses that stop around the major attractions of Athens, and the hotel was less than a 5min straight walk to one of the stops, so it made getting around the city extremely convenient.

Fancy? No. Extremely practical? Yes.
Overall, it was just what we needed.

Staff are super friendly. The location is perfect, you couldn’t get any closer to the main attractions in Athens. Bathroom is not attached to the room which can be quiet annoying. Otherwise, exactly what you pay for.

A hotel right next to the Acropolis Museum. I think the location is very nice, flat and not too steep. The rooms were standard size and clean. The people at the reception were very helpful. We preferred breakfast, it was a delicious breakfast with beautiful products that appealed to many cultures.

Very nice hotel, great staff, beautiful breakfast room. Room balcony had Acropolis view. We had a slight problem with our air conditioning.  They offered to switch us to another room but we didn't because it was still functioning, just not perfectly.

Great location, situated behind the acropolis museum and very close to restaurants and shops. The hotel has friendly staff, is very accommodating, and it is easy to arrange hotel transport to the airport. They also have a rooftop restaurant with a beautiful view of the Acropolis.

We chose the Hera hotel because of the location and it being a luxury hotel as we can expect a certain standard. The staff were friendly on arrival, the cleaning lady was wonderful and the breakfast staff member was great as well. Breakfast was fantastic. The foyer and breakfast room were air-conditioned very well. Upon arrival to our room we found the room unbearably hot as the curtains were open and the sun was beaming into the room and it was 40 degrees Celsius outside. We quickly closed the curtains and put the air conditioning on 18 degrees Celsius full fan. The room did not cool at all but because of our jet lag we fell asleep. In the morning the room was still hot and the air conditioning was running all night. Before leaving for the day we asked the cleaning lady to leave the air conditioning on and the curtains closed hoping that when we returned the room would finally be cool. This was not the case. I called reception and the man told me that the system cannot keep up ( although the foyer was very cool). The next morning we woke drenched in sweat and couldn’t wait to check out. We mentioned it again to the staff upon check out and the response was that all of Athens is hot and the systems cannot keep up all over Athens. What a ridiculous response!!! Despite the great location we will never return to the Hera hotel. When you pay 241 euro per night including taxes you expect the air conditioning to be working. We were so disappointed.

One of the best stays I had during my two weeks in Greece: clean rooms, nice staff, great location. My room was small - as expected - but air conditioned and well proportioned. Would stay again if I visit Athens.

It was great the owner accommodated us with storing our luggage at the main office and the when we arrived to the apartment greater us there. Showed us around the place and the use of the home. From the Washing machine to the hot water for baths. We did report to the owner that the door nobs to several rooms had a issue of falling off or the latch spinning. They responded right away but we let them know that we can work with it until we leave and then they can come fix it for someone else. Over all it was a great place close to many tourists locations.

It was like staying at a 5-star hotel, but it was an entire apartment, walking distance from the Acropolis!

Akis went above and beyond for us. Everything was perfect. He was very kind, provided food, big TV with netflix in 5 different languages, big beds perfectly made, plenty of towels, air condition of course and the apartment was bigger than expected!

Couldn't recommend this place enough and even more so for the customer service. Thank you Aki, we will be back again!
Great location.

The apartment has an ideal location, convenient parking - there is a paid parking lot next to it, and free parking on the street. It is clean and very welcoming. The host is kind and responsive, he had left us breakfast in the fridge.

Excellent well-kept and clean apartment with every service, excellent location for visiting the city. Akis is a fantastic owner, kind and provided us with all the necessary information. I highly recommend it to everyone and if I were to return I would have no doubts. Thank you and congratulations again Akis 👍👍👍

Big Apartment with 2 bedrooms. Aircon in every room. Fully equiped kitchenette with oven, kettle, coffee maker, sandwich maker and the owner also keeps Greek coffee powder and Jam packets for breakfast. Close to Victoria station.

Good apartments for a good price! We had a basement apartment with a terrace, which doesn't have a good view, but it is really nice for just sitting there and having your breakfast. This apartment has everything you need for cooking, also clean room and comfortable bed. Highly recommend!

The apartment is situated at a reasonable distance from the main attractions- about 15 min walk. It is small but clean and averagely quiet. The check-in/out was super easy. Great option for a weekend stay.

Few apartments and rooms for rent. Very clean and loaded with everything needed, dishes, good coffee, wine and juice. Misses some tea and maybe explanation which exactly place do you get since some are with windows and living area while other are basement type. Overall well furnished and comfortable.

Location central ✔
Walkable✔
Comfortable Studio & bed✔
Gas leak in the apartment across the hall that prompted an evacution of building & 2 Fire Trucks✔
Made the best of the gas leak situation ✔
Slight lingering smell of gas in my bathroom for next 2 days✔
No nice Welcome Basket in room til mentioned it to owner✔
Got Basket later✔
Easy walk to metro✔
Restaurants and coffee walkable distance ✔
Very Good value. Small, but very charming & clean, quite central, and a welcome basket. Thumbs up!
There is everything you need in the apartment. The location is also quite central.
Great location! Great service! Very nice apartment.

Stunning property! Impeccably clean, attentive management and beautifully furnished/designed! As a filmmaker that travels the world looking for property to film & to host my cast during filming this is truly one of the premier residences in Old Athens. From the leisurely, “on-the-go explorer/traveler” to the business traveler who wants a no-fuss luxury property in a prime location, the White House in Plaka is the place to call home in Athens. Kudos to the management and design team for offering such a stunning rental at an affordable price point. Thank you- I hope to be back sooner than later!
PM

This Villa made a Trip of a Lifetime all the more Magical.

It's Central Location, Luxurious Esthetics and Ample Space made our visit to Athens Perfect!

It is a stones throw or short walk away from Shopping, Restaurants, Post Office, Public Transportation, Night Life and The Acropolis.

It has been a fantastic experience staying in this historical build. Spacious bedrooms and loads of living space. Views to the acropolis and excellent hospitality. There was a strike in athens and the minivan could not reach close and we walked a bit but really this was an oasis within athens... coming back for grannies 70th birthday for sure..

True definition of home away from home.An amazing geographical location with an amazing view of key tourist sites.
Extremely welcoming staff .Sparkling clean rooms and clean surroundings.
It’s worth every penny spent.
A six star ⭐️ would have been more ideal.

23-25 is September 2018
We stayed at the White House in Athens
What seem to be an amazing home accommodating 11 of us and great location.
BUT that night I got severely Bad Bed Bugs !!
Still a month after our journey the marks on my legs and the itch will not stop. A local pharmacist and My Internist Dr in the US, can only describe it as “Super Duper-Super Duper-Super Duper bad bed bugs ”after prescription, Dr visits and visits to the  pharmacy !!!
4 of us had to relocate in the main living room on the sofa very inconvenient to us and the rest of the group!
Not to mention when I got home my entire suitcase had to be cleaned disinfectant and my entire wardrobe as well !!!
I only hope this problem is corrected and no child or any one should  go on Vacation and experience the worst kind of Bed Bugs there is!

And since a simple Apology did not return my days of Adventure and my once a year Vacations and the inconvenience I caused the rest of my group I decided to write a review !
I hope if you do plan to book it, Please Verify the reviews.

Thank you for your time
M. Morales

Conveniently located and close to many attractions, restaurants and shops.  The apartment is well maintained, clean and with everything needed for a comfortable stay.  Perfect for a group or family with children.  The hostess was kind and helpful.   I’d recommend without hesitation.

That apartament was more than we expected . It looks the same as on a pictures or even better- super specious, clean, modern with all  necessary equipment. Close to shops and walking distance from all important monuments. Definitely recommend! We loved our stay!

Excellent rental. Exactly as shown in the photos. Immaculate. TVs in each room and individually controlled air conditioning. Great location with many great restaurants within walking distance. Esmeralda arranged our transfer to and from the airport making it very convenient for us. Highly recommend My Greek Vacations rentals.

5 Stars - the apartment is in a great location, it’s been beautifully renovated, super clean, well equipped with extra towels and bed linen, along with all the mod cons. Restaurants, metro, tram, sites, shops, supermarkets etc are right on the doorstep. During our stay our needs changed from a 2 week holiday stay to 8 weeks due to Covid. This apartment was great for both a holiday stay and long term stay. All bedrooms had workstation so it meant everyone had a desk to do remote schooling and work from. Mary and Esmeralda were great hosts and responded immediately to all our text messages and calls and even provided a complimentary clean and change of bed linen, which was very generous and appreciated. We would stay at any of the apartments hosted by Mary and Esmeralda. It’s places and hosts like this that put ones faith in online bookings and Airbnb.

Lovely spacious, clean apartment in an area with lots of very tempting cafes, bakeries, bars and restaurants.
We walked to all the major sights and attractions from the accommodation - would thoroughly recommend.

Conveniently located and no worries with luggage they will help you carry it. My room was right next to the shared kitchen which sucked cause the walls are definitely thin and you hear everything. If you can get a room away from the kitchen it may be quieter but expect to hear everybody’s business. Overall staff was helpful and friendly.

Great location since it is so close to all the restaurants and shops at Plaka, but it does have some drawbacks.

At first, we had a hard time finding it with google maps since Plaka is a cobblestone area with tight shops and buildings all around so it doesn't seem like your regular squared out streets with easily recognizable  landmarks.

There is no obvious entryway or signage (a plaque on the wall). There are 2 sets of french style wooden doors with doorbells and keypads attached. The first set leads to the reception area where you fill out a form, and they make a copy of your passport. Then they lead you back out to the second set of door where you use a pin to get inside to your actual rooms.

It is a modernized older building with many of its original charm so it does have air conditioning and a modern bathroom but be ready to walk up and down a spiraling three flights of stairs to your second floor room.

I wished I had booked their room with a balcony but don't remember if it was unavailable, or maybe I just wanted a cheaper room. I am paying less than $500 Euros for 3 nights and it includes all the free 2 liter water bottles you need (they have a small community kitchen where they have complimentary tea, coffee and water).

Our room is in the corner so we have two 6 feet tall french door style windows with original wood and hardware (so be gentle when closing them since both sides have to meet in the middle to close properly unlike newer styles where you close one first and the other side seals it or others where they close independently from each other). They meet in the middle, so one fits into the groove of the other, and then you twist the window handlebar to lock it.

The room key is an actual key that you have to use on the inside in order to lock your door (no twisting mechanism for easy locking). The advantage of this style of lock is not locking yourself out of your room with the keys left inside.

The doors of both the building and rooms are old, so it did feel a bit loose.

Since we are two old ladies, we couldn't possibly take our luggage up 3 flights of a spiral staircase so the two young gentlemen at the reception were very kind to help us. The tricky part is when we have to check out, I fear we might have to bother them again for help. I feel this type of lodging is best for younger people who travel light while staying to enjoy Plaka.

Air conditioning is a split system, and it is off, so turn it on with a high fan setting if you plan to rest within the hour of unpacking. You can always leave it off and just enjoy the fresh air by opening your windows, but be careful with the curtains on windy or rainy days.

The perk is being able to see the Acropolis right outside our window, and when you open your windows, you can smell all the delicious food from the restaurants downstairs.

I would definitely pick this place again if I were only packing a backpack or duffle bag while traveling with my siblings but unfortunately this time I have my mother who has arthritis so she did prefer a traditional hotel with elevators.

We recently had a marvelous stay at the Palladian Home Hotel in Athens. I must say we were pleasantly surprised. I would consider this a boutique hotel with just a handful of rooms. Very, very quant and it just added to our overall vacation experience. The only negative thing I can say is that our room was in the third floor (tight spiral staircase) and there are NO elevators, but even with that the staff helped us with our luggage coming and going. The staff was just outstanding and in particular, Panos. He was super friendly, accommodating and so very helpful.

Highly recommend this beautiful little gem, tucked away a stones throw from the Acropolis. Staff attentive, polite and nothing was too much bother. A great base for exploring all the local shops and for getting to the Acropolis early. Comfortable bed and a gorgeous shower.

Good location, the desire of the employees to provide service is good. Everything else is bad! The staff's professionalism in providing service is not good, 4 times we checked with the staff about ordering a taxi for a certain day and they said that everything is already arranged, a minute before you have to get into the taxi, the staff does not see the order, there is no order. under any criticism. The maintenance conditions are not good, when you take out the shower tube you cut your hand as you can see in the picture.

Not a great experience and the flat certainly needs an upgrade, desperately...from the walls to the furniture. The place has an unrealised potential. I liked the balcony, but then there was water dripping from the ac and slippery tiles were all wet. The flat has two toilets and only 1 bathroom. Not great for such a spacious apartment. We stayed in a group of 11 people and we only had one pair of keys. I would suggest the owner changes the name from "beautiful" to basic homes. The good point was the location: walking distance to the Centre and excellent dining options all around. Overall I would not recommend.

We have booked this aparment via booking.com several weeks ago. I really understand that all prices went up due to war in Ukraine. If the price was deemed too low, it could be subject to further negotiation. But the owners did something absolutely unacceptable and refused to let us in on the day of our arrival! I suspect that this was due to reason that they booked it outside booking.com to a third party for a higher price. They only said over the intercom downstairs "Apartment is under renovation, goodbye" and we were suddenly forced to find 3 nights accomodation for 8 people and a baby. To add insult to the injury they informed booking.com that they cancel our stay because we did not check in!!! Combined with the reviews below about the standard, I really discourage everyone from staying here.

The Place doesn't even deserve 1 star but I can not give 0 stars. It was a total desaster. I travel much and I booked many accomodations with booking.com but it was the first time that I had a situation like this. I booked a stay for 3 nights in Athens in Veikou Beautiful Homes via booking in February. The booking succeeded and I even had a contact with the owner recording airport transfer. Everything seemed to be normal. At the day of our arrival i wrote her a message from the airport via booking that we will be there in about 30 mins. Ok asked how we can get to the apartment. She didnt reply. We took a taxi to the location. And I couldn't believe my eyes and ears. The location was closed. I called her and first she didn't pick up and then she picked up and said "We are having a renovation. Hotel is closed". And she hang up 🙈 I tried to call her many times but she didn't pick up. Then I asked her Greek  neighbor to call her from the Greek number. She picked up only for a moment and said something in Greek that I didn't understand. Then she cancelled our booking in booking.com and said the reason was we didn't show up 🤯🤯🙈🙈🙈 I never saw something like this. Such an ignorance. Probably there was no renovation because it didn't looked like this. I guess she just got different clients who probably paid more. No idea. But I was traveling with my whole family. 9 people and  (among the others 1 year old baby,one ill person and two seniors).  It was already late evening. We were hungry, with all baggages, and with no place to live the next 3 days. I was searching quickly for some apartment or suite or some hotel to stay in this area (because it was very good located, close to Acropolis. That's why I booked it). But everything was already booked or too small for our group or just too expensive to book it in the very last moment.

Please people - DON'T BOOK THIS PLACE!!!

THEY ARE CHEATING

FINGER WEG!!!

TRZYMAĆ SIĘ Z DALEKA!!! OSZUŚCI!!!!

DO NOT BOOK HERE
We (4 friends) booked here 2 months before our vacation (Booking.com) The booking said "Confirmed" so no problem, right? 2 days before arriving I messaged the owner to let them know about our arrival time - no response. At the airport of departure I messaged them again - no response. Then I found another review here on Maps about the same place, saying they cancelled lat minute (literally when they were already there outside the door). Same thing: no communication whatsoever. With that review we all tried calling as soon as we landed. The first time the call got through and rang 5 times before being sent to voicemail, then for all other calls, as soon as it rang they sent us to voicemail. At the 9th or 10th call they did answer and all they said was THAT THE APARTMENT DOESN'T EXIST ANYMORE. To not go there, and that it is a problem we should solve with Booking.com.
So we were left at the airport with no confirmed booking for our group 🙃

Aaaaand of course so far they haven't cancelled the booking, so on Booking.com it shows like if we want to cancel it they'll charge us €100+. Already requested via the site for the owner to waive the fees, will update if successful.

Do not book here for any reason, they don't answer, do not let you know about anything, do not even pick up the phone. Would be 0 stars if possible.

As always, Veikou Beautiful Homes is the perfect place to stay, or start the trip to the islands, with its spacious two apartments, quietly and centrally located close to the ancient town. Super clean and a lot of space at low cost. Top.

Wonderful service; all the hotel staff were very friendly and very attentive to everything we needed; they are very polite and warm people. The location of the hotel is spectacular; you can clearly see the amazing Parthenon from the bar, and certainly the facilities are wonderful. The breakfasts served are indescribable; they are a true Greek delight. Besides being buffet, there are options for all tastes, and all this while enjoying an unforgettable, beautiful scenery. An excellent hotel for visiting an amazing city. A hundred percent recommended!

The rooms are not as nice as the photos, quite run down and small. No where to lay out a small case. The breakfast is just ok, aside from breakfast you can't get any food here. I asked for my bed to be made up as a double as it was a twin room but it was never done (the booking was for a double). The staff were friendly and the location was good. Overall not good value for money.

Loved our stay here. Small rooms but lovely and clean.we had a wonderful balcony that had a perfect view of the Acropolis. There wasn’t a kettle for tea or coffee but when we asked about it, in less than 5 minutes we had one. All the staff were amazing and made us feel like we were friends. Very close to everything including transport. Can’t wait to visit Athens again and we will be making this our go to hotel.

Location close to metro and 10-15 minutes from the Plaka. Nice variety of choices for breakfast including fried eggs upon request. Very friendly and helpful service, especially Anna at the front desk. Rooms are moderately sized. Roomy corners shower with plenty of hot water. Highly recommend the room with the balcony. Excellent view of the Acropolis, especially front the 6th floor.
We had a great stay! Really clean rooms, very helpful and hospitable stuff. There is a rooftop bar where they serve complimentary, continental breakfast.

We had a really good time at Irene's apartment! It's clean, spaciouse apartment for 2 people, very well equipped, there was everything you need like coffee machine, toaster, equipements for cooking. The neighbourhood is calm and safe! The hisotrical city center is half an hour walk. The metro line 2 is a 5 minutes walk. Irene was really communicative and helpful!
