Everything was perfect from checkin to checkout! Comfortable beds, own closet, cleanliness, bathrooms for everyone and close to the subway! I felt very good, and I will definitely come back! You can also rent a towel for 2 euros, and you can wash your clothes for 5 euros!

Excellent location - in 1 minute walk to a metro station. Clean, comfortable bed and mattress, every bed has curtains which gives you personal space. Nice common area and patio with tables and chairs. Helpful and friendly staff.
Unbelievable dirt and awful musty smell

The most important part about this hotel is how helpful and polite the staff is. For example, they helped us with transport and to print documents. Additionally, the room was very clean and nice decorated. The only issue was that ruby rooms was about 30min away from the center of Athens, but thankfully there was a metro station close by.

We spent thee days in this hotel and it was a positive experience except a few details. The building is extremely clean and tidy , the staff was very helpful and ready to help us and answer any questions about Athens , best spots to visit, where to go.. The room was very big and cozy and most important it was very clean. The location was just 200m away from the metro station, something that made it very easy for us to travel everywhere every day. Our only complaint is that there’s no breakfast area and the breakfast is served in your room if you ask it and it was just standard. It was a toast with bread and cheese and one juice and one coffee with one chocolate croissant. Enough , but i have seen better breakfasts in almost every hotel i have been. Our second complaint was that the shower was not existent and it was just a shower head in the corner of the “bathroom” right next to the toilet. Every time we took shower , all the room ended up being a big pond and we had to leave the door open for everything to get dry after the water would go down the drain. The third thing that bothered us was that the rooms had terrible sound proof and we could hear all the noises from the floor rooms and even from the upper floor rooms. And the last complaint was that the room had no curtains so we had to close the balcony door and use the lights every time. The hotel is excellent if you want a clean and tidy place to rest at night for your next adventure and you are the type of person that spent as little time in the hotel as possible and just uses the hotel as a place of rest for the next day. But only for this. It’s obvious that the hotel is functioning as a short stay place , most of the times a single night stay and nothing more. But everything was clean and new and tidy and the personnel amazing and that is a plus.

We stop one night for our trip and we found an amazing room for good price. The staff is super friendly and accommodating, especially Nadia the receptionist. She is amazing! She went to find almond milk for breakfast to accomodate me because I'm vegan :) really good customer service!

The location is great :) the only thing I can complain about are the rooms, you have to be aware that not all of them are suitable for a group of friends, but for couples. There were a few things missing, but they were minor things :) In the end, I still recommend it!
It's not what it looks like in the photos... there was a problem with cleaning. The breakfast is inadequate. The staff is friendly and caring

Very good, clean and spacious apartments, in very good locations. Next to any kind of shop one might need, 2 minutes by foot from the metro (2 stops to Acropolis). In a quiet alley and as an extra bonus the 2 parking spaces it has.

Very nice apartments, clean, renovated. In a beautiful apartment building. The hosts are very kind and helpful. As soon as we asked for anything, they responded immediately.
Equipped with the necessary household utensils.  Be well!
Your friends from Crete.

Beautiful apartments and clean with nice layout and colors. Very close to Neos Kosmos metro and just a quarter of a walk from Thisio. Someone worth staying!! The hosts are kind and welcoming.  I will go again for sure. A wine was waiting for us upon our arrival.
everything was ok, very clean.. the only negative thing is that there is a problem with the wifi, but that's a small problem!
Excellent.. spotlessly clean with all amenities!

Numa Lofts is incredibly welcoming, immediately making guests feel valued and at ease. The lofts boast a pristine cleanliness that speaks volumes of their meticulous attention to detail.

The staff at Numa Lofts are not just helpful; they go above and beyond to ensure that every aspect of your stay is taken care of. Their assistance is always delivered with a smile, adding a personal touch to their professional service.

Location-wise, Numa Lofts could not be more convenient. Nestled close to the metro, it offers effortless access to the wider city while being a short distance from the vibrant heart of Athens. This makes it an ideal spot for those wishing to soak up the local atmosphere without straying far from the historical center.

Numa Lofts is more than just a recommendation for those visiting Athens; it’s a top choice for anyone who wants to experience the city’s authentic charm coupled with the comfort of modern amenities. Whether you’re here to explore ancient ruins or enjoy the contemporary culture, Numa Lofts provides a perfect home base.
In essence, Numa Lofts is a must-stay for travelers seeking a clean, friendly, and strategically located accommodation that perfectly captures the essence of Athenian hospitality.

Very disappointing hotel. No reception after 3 p.m. so you have to manage to find your room number and map at the café next door!! You have 15 cm to pass between the foot of the bed and the wall while leaning to avoid the TV. The view of the courtyard is lousy, moreover as the bed is mounted on a platform and the window sill reaches you mid-thigh, hence a significant risk of falling from the third floor. Dirty window.  Don't plan on taking a shower morning and evening because hot water is rationed. No explanation of how the air conditioning works, which turns off and on at night depending on power outages. Lots of flashiness but zero service. TO AVOID.

Disconcerting hotel at first glance. It is in fact an old building converted into a hotel, hence the entrance is difficult to find and the absence of reception.

Nevertheless, very good value for money, the rooms are well equipped, new and comfortable.

The location is very good, close to the metro and the city center.

Very good service to note, we had an unexpected event the day before departure and the team was very understanding and flexible regarding the checkout time.

I recommend for those looking for good value for money

We had a great time in the apartment! It was spotlessly clean, sun-splashed and equipped with anything you need. The location is great, just a few steps from the metro station, from where you can go easily everywhere in the city, including main attractions like Acropolis and Plaka. Plus, it is close to lovely bars, restaurants and supermarkets. Τhe hostess is super helpful. The communication was super easy and quick. I️ would 100% recommend a stay in Loft4All!

Better location than our previous time in Athens, we were able to find a bar after 11pm when the hotel bar had closed. Room was spacious, comfortable and lovely shower to freshen up.  Not the best breakfast yet sleep was more important

This was the first hotel of our trip. The staff was very helpful and pleasant. I can't rave enough about the breakfast. It was amazing! Hats off to the chef! The room was clean and had everything that you needed. The slippers provided were a nice touch. Thanks to the staff for making this a great first impression of Greece!

Excellent hotel!
My room was spacious and clean the whole stay, the room service was impeccable, the breakfast met expectations.
The reception staff were kind and I got an extension at check-out until 2:00 PM.
There is a beautiful yard, a cute lobby and a small gym.
Near the hotel there is a bank, an Italian restaurant, a cafe and even a supermarket.

Thank you very much.

Service is essential for me, and my recent experience was disappointing. During check-in, the receptionist was unfriendly and had a bit of an attitude. When I asked for help with my large bag, I received a rude response: "No, we don't provide assistance with carrying bags to the room." Instead, they provided a trolley for me to load up my bags myself. If I have to do everything on my own, I wonder why I'm paying a high price for my stay. I could have chosen another place for less cost and received better service. I've stayed at many hotels, and this is the first time I've encountered such a refusal. It seems standard for hotels, especially a well-known brand like IBIS, to assist with luggage. Due to this poor service, I will not be returning to this hotel. Additionally, the interior feels outdated, and the location is a bit far from major attractions (not within walking distance).

Nice hotel but it was not in a very nice area, I wouldn't feel safe walking out at night on my own. The real down fall was the hotel only did breakfast and there was nowhere in walking distance to get a hot meal so I had to have a taxi to take me to a restaurant on the three evenings I spent there. My travel agent did the booking as it was a two night stay before a cruise and a one night when I got back before flying home. I am a pensioner and felt it was a stupid place to put me. I will make sure I will not stay there again.
Apartment with everything necessary for a long stay. Access to all means of transport in minutes. Clean and quiet. Easy check in and detailed instructions from the hosts
Great apartment, close to the center, clean and quiet!
Nice apartment in a convenient area. The owners take care of their clients.

We stayed in this hotel while visiting Athens but it was a bad idea.
Although we enjoyed the excellent location and the safe neighborhood, we could not imagine the traffic noise would be so loud. The hotel is located on a heavy traffic road. We could hear motorcycles and cars even at 4 am. We did not really get accustomed to such hectic nights.
Also make sure they send you all the instructions including the entrance code before you arrived. We had not received the code upon arrival. As there is no front desk, the online assistance was difficult to reach. We waited outside for more than an hour to try to call somebody for help. No one answered. We finally called the assistance of the reservation platform who tried several times to reach them and a few minutes after they finally called us back.
In a nutshell, if you plan to stay in this hotel, don't forget your ear plugs!
Except from those issues, all went well.

10 day stay. Best thing here is the price. Clean modern rooms. Everything you need, except maybe a toaster. Nice bed, nice swower and bathroom, good A/C, cable TV, fast quiet elevator. Close to supermarket, coffee shops, bakeries, restaurants. Just over a mile to the center. This is not a hotel, its more like renting an apartment, no front desk, so you're on your own, checking in and adking questions by message, which is pretty timely, you have to do your own cleaning and laundry. The cleaning person I encountered in the hallway was rude, and indifferent, they won't lift a finger to help you with questions or anything, and absolutely ignored me like i was a ghost, when I tried to talk to her. There is a balcony. You can't hear your neighbors much, but the road noise is  constant, as it's right on a busy street. Parking is not available, and although not impossible, would be very difficult. It's an OK place to stay for the money.

Enjoyed my stay at WYZ appartements! I had a lovely studio, very confortable with calm and relaxing ambiance with a balcony. New furniture and appliance. Practical location, near the downtown but at the same time far from the city noise. 10 minutes walking to Syndagma. Would love to come back for every stay in Athens.

My husband and I stood at WYZ Apartments in the week of July 8th, 2022. Very clean  modern and comfortable place! Their Customer Service is on point. We needed to get in touch with them in the middle of the night, and they responded and assisted us promptly! Conveniently located to walk distance from Acropolis! The caffe and supermarket so close it is a plus! Very inexpensive place but with high quality!
Love it!!!! I recommend this place 100%. The only thing I was disappointed about was that they don’t change sheets or towels during your stay! My recommendation to WYZ management is to provide this service to be on point!
Ana and Marcos

Don’t come here, bad location
Very poor service, not answering if there is a problem and there is a lots of problems
Electricity problem, water problem, elevator problem
Stay away for your own good!!

Comfortable and well-equipped apartment.
Happy 👍🏻
Big apartment, comfortable, two bedrooms, well equipped, and well located, near Neos Cosmos metro station.

Excellent apartment, equipped with everything including washing machine, dishwasher, iron and ironing board, very clean, welcoming and very knowledgeable manager, congratulations. Possibility of moving around by public transport... as soon as we arrived the owner made some things available, such as water , coffee, bread not indifferent for those arriving directly from the airport. Thanks again

Just the right accommodation for a short holiday for two in Athens.
The location is great for getting around the city on foot, but also by tram or metro. Despite the urban location, it is very quiet. So you get a good night's sleep before you throw yourself into the urban action. The shower room is small but functional and can also be easily ventilated through a tilting window. The terrace, which is great to hang out on, is great and essentially doubles the size of the living room. The landlord is very personable, helps with all questions, provides very personal support and even arranged an excellent taxi ride to and from the airport for us (€48 at night, €45 in the morning). Otherwise, we used the 5-day public transport ticket for our stay, which was extremely cheap and practical for €8.20. We can only recommend this.
But a very nice house. The owner is very kind. There was breakfast and 1 bottle of wine in the fridge
Comfortable, clean & classy. All the key highlights are walkable ❤️

I recommend it to anyone who wants direct access to METRO (3 minutes) Modern furniture, pin code entrance. Supermarket 5 minutes away, cafes, restaurants nearby. The neighbourhood is friendly. Direct bus to the beach!
Clean. We paid for 3 people, but the 3rd space is for small children only.

Raise Athens Metro Apartments is a holiday apartment rental in Dafni, near Athens. It's very close to Dafni metro station on line 2 (only a 2 minute walk away!), and the city center from there is only 7 minutes away! This property has a very good price to rating ratio, so we couldn't resist! The building looks nice and modern and you can get to the apartments via stairs or elevator, so there's a nice element of accessibility! You get into the apartment by entering a key code that they give you via the Booking.com chat. Our apartment looked very nice! It had a living room with a sofa bed, a kitchen, a bedroom, a bathroom and a balcony with a beautiful mountain and city view! The living room and bedroom had air conditioners that worked wonderfully and the living room had Netflix on the TV, which we really appreciated. The kitchen had everything we needed (too bad there wasn't a microwave, but that wasn't a problem for us). The beds were comfy and tidy and the bathroom was nice and clean. The toilet flushed as expected, the shower worked nicely (adjusting the water temperature was a bit finicky, but it wasn't a big deal) and the washbasin wasn't clogged. But you must remember to put your toilet paper in the trash can next to the toilet. Greek pipes are too narrow to accommodate toilet paper. Overall, a great holiday apartment rental which I highly recommend for any Athenian tourist.
Very nice apartment but too loud as it is on a main road and no parking available

Such a wonderful experience in this hotel!
The room is very clean, the location is perfect (4 stops to go to the Acropolis thanks to the subway). The staff was very kind, everything was perfect ! And the pool was very appreciated ☀️👌 Thanks again!

Highly recommended! It was a great find! We came early before check-in time but the host let us in and while waiting for our room to be cleaned up, we were offered cold drinks by the pool. The host is very welcoming and very friendly. Our room was huge and super clean, and comfortable. The area is very safe, quiet and close to subway, 4 stations away from downtown (5mins). The area is full of local coffees and restaurants, at local prices. We would definitely stay here for our next trip on athens. 😊

Highly recommended.
Our room was basically perfect. Great lighting and very comfortable. The service was also very good, the staff was very polite and understanding, an easy five stars from me.
Just the only thing you want to do is bring towels with you for the swimming pool because the towels are for room use apparently.

First time in Athens and Grec Suites Dafni became home! Hospitable hosts and spacious clean and modern rooms! Loved it! There are shops and cafes close by for any of your needs. It’s also in a calm neighborhood but close enough to the metro to take you to all the top attractions! Highly recommend! Thank you Voula!

I’ll recommend this place to everyone. The rooms are really nice and very clean. The apartament building is located in quiet, residential area. So it’s safe to take a walk at night. The location is great. A metro station is 6 minutes away and the line that goes there  can take you to every major Athens’ attractions in just couple stops. If I ever be back to Athens I’ll book this place immediately.

I stayed at the Poseidon Residence in Athens with my friends.
The flat was very nice and the landlords helped with minor problems within hours. The location is very central and the kitchen has most of what you need. We felt very comfortable and would come back again.
We had a wonderful time. The accomodation is spacious and clean, and within walking distance of most of the main sites of Athens.
It's in a walking distance from the centre. There was some issue with the cleanliness - it could be much better. Free parking is available on the streets around the block.

Rooms look great, very cosy atmosphere in the backyard - everything was wonderful 😍
Our host Carolina is a very nice person ☺️ Thank you for all your advices! All questions were answered:)
Highly recommended!
Great place to stay,  I highly recommend it !

We had a really good time at Irene's apartment! It's clean, spaciouse apartment for 2 people, very well equipped, there was everything you need like coffee machine, toaster, equipements for cooking. The neighbourhood is calm and safe! The hisotrical city center is half an hour walk. The metro line 2 is a 5 minutes walk. Irene was really communicative and helpful! The self check-in and out is easy to handle!

We had the three room apartment on the first floor with balcony, perfectly equipped kitchen and living room. Everything was clean and nicely arranged. The elevator was appreciated for the luggage. Only 5 min from metro station an 1 min from bus stop line 227, the apartment was perfectly quiet during the night, when windows were closed. Very efficient mosquito defence for all windows. 20 min to Akropolis by metro, 30 min by foot. Irene was very kind and helpful with our questions and needs. Thank you very much for this pleasant stay.

Nice accommodation and a warm welcome from Greece:) Irene was super nice and eager to help with any questions about my stay here in Athens. Facilities were well equiped and i was surprised to see a wine in the dining room☺️and it was so great to have prepared icy water in the refrigerator after one hot long trip back from the outside!

We enjoyed two weekends at Casa D'Irene rustic appartment. There's plenty of space, so you feel like at home. By the way, it's full of details, like the wine everyone mentions. Irene also makes everything easy to help you with your stay.
The area is very quiet, and although it may look a bit scary at night, seems to be safe, at least, we didn't have any trouble, and we could see other people walking alone, and didn't look worried at all.
It's very well connected by metro line, and quite close to the city center, so you can get to the Achropolys in about 20 minutes on foot.
We are really happy to have chosen it for our visit to Athens!

A cozy and very nice appartment, located a bit away from strickt city center of Athens, is a very quality choice in case you are looking for staying in appartment during your holidays or other obligations you may have in this ancient Greek city. The apartment has two bedrooms, quite nice balcony (with a street view), bathroom with bath, and spacious living room presented together with kitchen. The appartment is clean and has all neccessary equipment for comfortable stay and cooking, together with some food items and spices you can use right away with your meals preparation - the owners are very accessible and open and are in general taking care that in appartment is all that is needed - a nice welcome touch from them is also presented as gift for newcomers in a form of a bottle of home made wine.
Otherwise, the appartment is just 350 m away from the nearest metro station (there is also a bus option), so the journey to other spots in city is simple and not far from main city attractions. In neighbourhood you can find bakeries and small shops with basic food and other basic equipment, while for those who are more demanding, the visit to supermarket would be essential - unfortunately there is no larger supermarket in vicinity of appartment, so you will have to search for it within at least 10 - 15 min walking distance.
The appartment is otherwise in the building on the first floor, and is easy accessible by stairs or by elevator, while the main entrance in the building itself is however a little bit clumsily, especially for people with disabilities, who could have some difficulties with entering to building.
The apartment has also an AC unit and free WiFi system.
In general, highly recommended for staying if you do not mind to not be in strickt city center of Athens.

DO NOT BOOK HERE. In addition to most of these photos being fabricated, the listing lies outright about the availability of WiFi. This is not a hostel, but an apartment crammed with as many beds as possible. It is dirty and dark, there is no lockbox, no towels, no evidence that the bathroom has been cleaned in the last 6 months, and the bed I stayed in was so jammed into the room that my head was next to the door handle that opens out into the street. The proprietoress is an opportunistic grifter with no interest in hospitality, only making money off tourists and young kids who don’t know better. If you are unfortunate enough to be dissatisfied with these conditions, you will not get your money back.

Had an amazing time here. Staff was so friendly and helpful. Pretty easy to get from hostel to center of city with busses. Very close to cafes and restaurants. Amazing views of the sea/ aegina island and also the surrounding mountains. Beds were very comfy and everything was kept very clean regularly.

Most of the times reception is deserted. Beds in dorms are uncomfortable and without any blankets. We were charged 6 euros only for waiting for an hour and not allowed access dorm beds.  Staff hardly speaks English. Washrooms are very uncomfortable to use with indefinite hot water.
Good place to stay as it is very near to the city centre. But there is no air conditioner here. So, think twice if you are looking for a dormitory with an air conditioner in the summer.

only on certain parts of the building
Cleanliness is not good
television old model and hubby have only one room is not smart tv

The good: Big appartment in an amazing location very close to the metro, coffee shops and markets. We never interacted with anyone and it everything was easily talked via chat. We were given codes to enter both the building and the apartment. They even booked a transfer for us from the airport (it's expensive as the airport is very far, but if arriving late at night, it's a blessing).

The bad: - Most of the times the code to open the apartment door wouldn't work. The first time it opened after 5min but the next days stopped working. I had to contact via booking to find a solution which was very quick even late at night.
- There's a key in a lockbox protected by a code. This code was inserted already!! Major security breach. Of course someone from outside would have to get into the building and know there's a key but still, felt unsafe after knowing that so I randomised the code and notified the hotel.
- The bathroom was not very clean. We found some hairs on the floor and even a contact lens... 🙄
- There's a stereo speaker connected... Apparently anyone can connect via Bluetooth. We thought it was off but it wasn't. At 5am some funny people decided to connect to it and blast music, which scared us a lot. Now we laugh at it but wasn't funny when it happened.

These few issues could have been isolated and only happen once but we're enough to bring the rating down but I WOULD comeback there if we comeback to Athens as overall it was good!

Great stay!!!! Clean, spacious and comfortable apartment, very close to the subway and within walking distance from major tourist attractions; it took us 25' to get to the Acropolis on foot. Very good air-conditioning, new home appliances, awesome mattress and the best of all; free access to the jacuzzi and sauna area on the 5th floor!! We are definitely coming back!!!

Everything was great! The room was comfortable and fully equipped, close to metro station and shops. The staff was very kind and helpful, and room was super clean. What else to ask for? We will be back on our next trip to Athens for sure!!!

One of the best accomodations I have been in.

The apartment looks way smaller on the pictures so you will really be suprised on how big it is.

There are literally no issues with the apartment.
It has a jacuzzi which is available to all Tennant's which was great and had.some privacy in.

It's all accessible by codes that you receive which is amazing as this really supports the current pandemic issues we have.

The house was really clean.
The staff was extremely nice and helped a lot.
Overall great experience 💛
Perfect stay, building full automated, clean and value for money! Staff everything that i needed reply to my messages and calls! Ill come back soon, see you and thanks for everything

+ We really liked our stay in this apartment. Dafni is very nice, not crowded and without tourists. The apartment and kitchen had everything we needed. The view was phenomenal!
- The wifi was not working and it was very hot in the bathroom. The floor tiles are in bad condition also. However these are not things that ruins your stay and we believe it will get improved.

It was clean with a good bed. Nice view from windows and from balcony. Small but good for one night accommodation. Floor had few pieces coming off so be careful with your toes. These are easy to fix so do it Mic😊

3rd floor apartment (no lift) in a nice area, 5 mins walk from Delphi tube station. Nice and clean and mostly modern, but dated kitchen. 1 bedroom with sofa bed in living room. Nice and quiet. View of Acropolis about 2km away over the roof tops. Really enjoyed our stay and good for city centre access via metro. Recommended!

Perfect to stay in Athens.
Hospitable and friendly hosts, close to Daphne metro station and free parking around the building.

Highly recommended!

+: Close to the metro station, bedroom is correct and quiet enough
-: Hanging wires, opened plugs, poor renovation, no finition were made, balcony with building material, ancient shutter from the last century. Third floor with no lift, the "Mountain View" is just Athen's suburbs with an ok mountain. The bathroom is shared with another, there are no curtains and the toilet ring is broken.

It's ok for one night but you can find better for a bit more expensive in Athens.

Do not order from this store, be careful. they send products in duplicate, but not the ones you ordered in the hope that you will not go through the return process, filling in a product return form, etc. This form is definitely impossible to fill in because it is disabled

A hidden gem close to the city center! Amazing and very stylish penthouse with beautiful furniture. The hosts were very friendly, super quick to respond to any message and always on hand to offer advice and suggestions.
A good place to stay.
Excellent

Comfortable and well-equipped apartment.
Happy 👍🏻
Great apartment, everything clean and renovated with very good taste. It has everything you need to spend a few days comfortably in Athens. Super recommended.

The most important part about this hotel is how helpful and polite the staff is. For example, they helped us with transport and to print documents. Additionally, the room was very clean and nice decorated. The only issue was that ruby rooms was about 30min away from the center of Athens, but thankfully there was a metro station close by.

We spent thee days in this hotel and it was a positive experience except a few details. The building is extremely clean and tidy , the staff was very helpful and ready to help us and answer any questions about Athens , best spots to visit, where to go.. The room was very big and cozy and most important it was very clean. The location was just 200m away from the metro station, something that made it very easy for us to travel everywhere every day. Our only complaint is that there’s no breakfast area and the breakfast is served in your room if you ask it and it was just standard. It was a toast with bread and cheese and one juice and one coffee with one chocolate croissant. Enough , but i have seen better breakfasts in almost every hotel i have been. Our second complaint was that the shower was not existent and it was just a shower head in the corner of the “bathroom” right next to the toilet. Every time we took shower , all the room ended up being a big pond and we had to leave the door open for everything to get dry after the water would go down the drain. The third thing that bothered us was that the rooms had terrible sound proof and we could hear all the noises from the floor rooms and even from the upper floor rooms. And the last complaint was that the room had no curtains so we had to close the balcony door and use the lights every time. The hotel is excellent if you want a clean and tidy place to rest at night for your next adventure and you are the type of person that spent as little time in the hotel as possible and just uses the hotel as a place of rest for the next day. But only for this. It’s obvious that the hotel is functioning as a short stay place , most of the times a single night stay and nothing more. But everything was clean and new and tidy and the personnel amazing and that is a plus.

We stop one night for our trip and we found an amazing room for good price. The staff is super friendly and accommodating, especially Nadia the receptionist. She is amazing! She went to find almond milk for breakfast to accomodate me because I'm vegan :) really good customer service!

The location is great :) the only thing I can complain about are the rooms, you have to be aware that not all of them are suitable for a group of friends, but for couples. There were a few things missing, but they were minor things :) In the end, I still recommend it!
It's not what it looks like in the photos... there was a problem with cleaning. The breakfast is inadequate. The staff is friendly and caring
The best place for meditation in Greece
Best Osho Meditation Center for me  !!!
The best place to breath and relax

A place where, with the help of various techniques of working with the body (the main of which is bioenergetic breathing) and meditation, you can return Life to the body and feelings, and find Joy in all its manifestations. This is where the path home begins...
The best meeting place in Athens. Where hearts open 😇

Nice and clean aparments, have all for visitors.... location is between centre and  sea coast.

Owner is good and provide all you needed for pleasant stay.  Wi fi is ok.

Noise is a bit higher because everyone drive a motorcycle in Athens (!? 🥶).

Honestly, it's alright, got a price to quality ratio you probably get what you pay for, it's located directly in the middle of two metro stations both a 9 minute walk (Dafni is the easier to get to). The room itself was modern, clean and was generally very nice. However  only the 1st and 4th floors have apartments the other floors are building sites which if the cleaner has disabled the lift you'll need to walk up and down the stairs!  And I'm not convinced that an extract fan going directly into a cupboard is going to extract much.

The biggest issue was the lack of sound proofing, you could hear everything from neighbours to the never end stream of mopeds.

Amazing apartment! Super clean and easy to check in. The building look old from the outside and the balcony is facing the main road . But once all doors close you can’t hear anything else.
Location is very good , about 5 min walk there is a metro station where you can take it to all the main attractions in Athens, takes us about 20 min to reach acropolis museum in one metro ride which I find it super convenient. Also there is supermarkets and restaurants within walking distant as well.

The best price-quality ratio. Very nice, even luxurious, recently renovated apartments with rich equipment. Well-functioning free Wifi. Air conditioning in every room. Very nice and helpful staff. I got answers to all my questions quickly.
Disadvantages: Not an interesting, poor district, 3km from the center. The only advantage of the location is close to the metro station (350m). What may not be ideal in an apartment (especially for a longer stay) is the lack of a dishwasher, washing machine and cabinets for clothes. And what surprised us the most (it was our first time in Greece) was that you can't throw toilet paper into the toilet. But this is apparently normal in Greece. For us it was a big surprise and a bit bothersome.
very clean, pristine rooms, all-new construction. I got my apartment, with special price, so it was even more great. Definitely i recommend it.
Comfortable, clean & classy. All the key highlights are walkable ❤️

I recommend it to anyone who wants direct access to METRO (3 minutes) Modern furniture, pin code entrance. Supermarket 5 minutes away, cafes, restaurants nearby. The neighbourhood is friendly. Direct bus to the beach!
Clean. We paid for 3 people, but the 3rd space is for small children only.

Raise Athens Metro Apartments is a holiday apartment rental in Dafni, near Athens. It's very close to Dafni metro station on line 2 (only a 2 minute walk away!), and the city center from there is only 7 minutes away! This property has a very good price to rating ratio, so we couldn't resist! The building looks nice and modern and you can get to the apartments via stairs or elevator, so there's a nice element of accessibility! You get into the apartment by entering a key code that they give you via the Booking.com chat. Our apartment looked very nice! It had a living room with a sofa bed, a kitchen, a bedroom, a bathroom and a balcony with a beautiful mountain and city view! The living room and bedroom had air conditioners that worked wonderfully and the living room had Netflix on the TV, which we really appreciated. The kitchen had everything we needed (too bad there wasn't a microwave, but that wasn't a problem for us). The beds were comfy and tidy and the bathroom was nice and clean. The toilet flushed as expected, the shower worked nicely (adjusting the water temperature was a bit finicky, but it wasn't a big deal) and the washbasin wasn't clogged. But you must remember to put your toilet paper in the trash can next to the toilet. Greek pipes are too narrow to accommodate toilet paper. Overall, a great holiday apartment rental which I highly recommend for any Athenian tourist.
Very nice apartment but too loud as it is on a main road and no parking available

My stay in the apartment was really excellent and left the best impressions! Its location was ideal, a stone's throw from everything you need, with easy access to the main attractions and transport. The apartment was very comfortable and neat, decorated with elegance and aesthetics that makes you immediately feel at home. In terms of amenities, it was fully equipped with quality electrical appliances, which made our stay even more enjoyable. The level of cleanliness was excellent and the details taken care of. The host was welcoming and very helpful, always willing to help us and give suggestions for local points of interest. I highly recommend this apartment to anyone looking for a special and comfortable stay!

Great house.
Spotless.
Beautiful surroundings.
The Owners very welcoming. It is in the center and accessible by metro. I highly recommend it.
New building, comfortable accommodation. I recommend this place.
Very clean, modern, Acropolis within walking distance and friendly landlord. Great, highly recommended.
Very friendly landlord. Apartment very clean and very well equipped. Three minutes to the metro station. Supermarket right nearby. So, we were completely satisfied. Would love to come back.

I had a fantastic stay at Yantra. The property was beautifully decorated and equipped with all the amenities needed for a comfortable stay. The location was perfect, close to local attractions and restaurants.

The highlight of my stay was the host, Apostolos, who was incredibly friendly, always available and went above and beyond to ensure I had everything I needed, making me feel truly at home.

Highly recommend this property for anyone looking for a great place to stay with an exceptional host!
Excellent place nearby downtown, metros, supermarkets museums etc! The best spot for visiting Athens

Avoid getting the bus in Athens unless you really need to!! It honestly was one of the worst experiences of my life, pushed up against the windows the bus was so full, people just kept getting on! No air conditioning & very dirty! Do not recommend! Only plus was that it was free however that was still overpriced for what we had to go through!

I had the opportunity to stay in this rental room for a few days and I can say that it exceeded all my expectations. The room was perfectly clean, spacious and very well equipped, with all the amenities one would need for a comfortable stay.

The bed was extremely comfortable, offering a restful sleep, while the room decor was warm and thoughtful. The space had natural lighting and gave a sense of calm and relaxation, which we greatly appreciated after our activities during the day.

The location is ideal, with easy access to local attractions, shops and restaurants. In addition, the neighborhood is quiet, which adds to the sense of calm and safety.

One of the biggest pluses was the friendliness and helpfulness of the owner. He was always available to answer any questions or give suggestions for local places worth visiting.

Overall, this is an excellent choice for accommodation in the area and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a combination of comfort, location and good service. We will definitely be back in the future!

Our stay at the hotel was excellent! The room was comfortable, clean and tastefully decorated, offering all the amenities we needed for a pleasant stay. The location was ideal, with easy access to the area's points of interest. The rooms had some signs of needing maintenance and there could have been more places to hang used towels and shelfs for placing clothes etc. Nice balcony! Drawback that the smell of smoke would come from the balcony.

Very practical that one can buy snacks and drinks at any time. The breakfast was always the same: toast, an egg, a chocolate croissant, a glass of juice, a glass of water and a choice of coffee/tea/cold milk. There are many cafeterias nearby where you can also eat breakfast.

It is easy to take public transportation to places like Acropolis and beaches. There's a direct bus from the airport (X96). The mini market right next to the hotel sells tickets. If you plan to make more than 6 single trips in Athens it is cheaper to buy the five day pass.

All in all the hotel is a good choice given the price. The biggest drawback is the constant smell of smoke/e-cigarettes in the reception area. Not sure if this is a common issue in Greece, but if you're very sensitive to smoke the hotel is not the best choice.

Only downside, our flight arrived early and there was nowhere to store our bags until check-in. Otherwise, we enjoyed a gorgeous double-rainbow over the city on the last night of our stay. :))

The apartment was modern, clean, and beautifully decorated, with all the amenities needed for a comfortable stay. The host was very accommodating and responsive, providing clear instructions and ensuring everything was in order for my stay. I especially appreciated the thoughtful touches, like the welcome pack and local tips. Overall, it was a wonderful experience, and I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a cozy and stylish place to stay in Kallithea."

The location is convenient, with trams nearby making it easy to reach everything. However, we did encounter an issue with cockroaches in the apartment; we found dead ones multiple days in a row. While it's clear that disinfection measures are in place, this might be unpleasant for future guests. Overall, a decent stay with a few drawbacks.

Stayed one night prior to transfer to the cruise ship. Air France misplaced luggage so we got into the hotel later than anticipated. For the little while we stayed there the hotel was great. The processed a lot of people in and out to the ships. Had dinner and breakfast in the hotel restaurant and was very good. Rooms were comfortable.

We had an extra night after our cruise. Room was ready  an hour earlier. We left our bags very early without any issues an started our scheduled sightseeing. Perfect location on the main Ave to access all the most famous sites. Entire hotel felt very high class compared to other US Marriotts.

Late night stop-over between island ferry and next day flight. Staff was AMAZING when we checked in and got all our details taken care of, including early airport cab and breakfast to go since we would be leaving before the restaurant opened. Rooftop view of Acropolis and all the shopping you need just outside the door. Just 10 or so minutes from the ferry port, and 45 minute cab ride to airport ($45). The hotel and room set-ups are a little wonky since the remodel but the staff and location were perfect for us. Would definitely return.

This hotel was booked as part of our air canada vacations.  It was okay but I wouldn't rate it as a 5 star.  Seems more suitable for conferences.  We weren't able to enjoy the free breakfast due to early morning bookings but it looked nice.  The hotel was at least a 1/2 hour drive to the main city and traffic was crazy!   It seemed to take forever so I wouldn't book it due to location.  Others with  our AC vacation group stayed at a hotel much closer to the city centre.  that looked very nice.

I really wanted to love this hotel. It's definitely a 2 star hotel, and honestly they're trying to do the best they can with what they have.
Pros
- good location for price. I needed one night before a sailing trip and this was a good compromise between my departure point and the sights of Athens.
- generous check in/out times. Both check in and check out is midday. And my room was ready when I arrived at 9am (planning to leave my bags) and they generously let me go straight in.
- bus stops nearby. You just need to have prepurchased your ticket! Also the x96 bus from the airport will drop you very close for 5.5 euro.
- if you stay here, make sure you go across the road to see the cultural centre with the most amazing "green roof"!
- it's clean
- very comprehensive breakfast that includes cooked eggs, bacon etc as well as continental and traditional Greek foods (spinach pies are so good). I can't vouch for the coffee but I think you can get espresso for extra €
-24hr front desk with helpful staff who speak very good English.
- effective air conditioning in the room
- elevator access
- good Wi-Fi

Cons
- it is old. Very run down.
- shower bracket was broken, which I reported, but it couldn't be fixed during my stay obviously.
- the shower temperature is affected easily? During one shower it went hot/cold /hot etc, but not every shower
- they provided a kettle in the room, but I don't know why. There was no tea etc or even any mugs.
- the beds were HARD. All my bony prominences hurt. The pillows were also quite hard.
- hotels.com advertised a rooftop terrace?? You can certainly access the roof, going past a collection of junk and broken things along with stored toilet paper, however it is certainly not intended for guests I'm sure. Which is a huge pity. It has great potential, but like so many places in Greece the economy has not been kind, and as I said, they seem to be doing the best they cash with what they have. I noted on my floor, one of the rooms appeared to be in the middle of refurbishment, so perhaps they are slowly getting around to each room.
All in all its a good budget stay.
I would stay here again.  For one or two people this perfect.  If I go to Greece alone, I will definitely use this Hotel again.

Wonderful hotel! Located in a very lovely area near the sea and close to several bus stops to city center as well as tram stop. Room was very clean and well mentained with a small balcony. Great classic European breakfast, shady lounge zone near the entrance for smokers and people who just want to chill outside. Helpful staff. All in all highly recommend and personally next time I go to Athens I'll sure choose Nestorion hotel again! Thank you!

I have stayed in this hotel for a total of 5 times with an average of 4 days per visit. The purpose of traveling was professional as I was attending seminars nearby. Each time I visited I stayed in a different room of either the 3rd or the 4th floor.

+ Pros
A great value-for-money hotel not far away from points of interest, especially for a tourist. Being right next to the SNF, it’s a great opportunity to go for a stroll around. There’re also several bus stops nearby. The beds are comfy, soft and always clean. The employees are all very pleasant and helpful. Room service, even in late hours, was always fast. There’s also free underground parking available.

- Cons
Your ability to join the hotel’s wifi will really depend on the room you will get. Sometimes I had perfect signal, other times I couldn’t even find it. The bathroom and specifically the shower is unfortunately very small resulting to water splashing all over the floor. A weird disadvantage is also that in some rooms the toilet flush-ability is really weak.

Overall, it was a pleasant stay!

The location is right behind a bus stop for 10. And an extended walk to a metro station (Sygou-fix). The service staff speak both Greek and English.

My only complaint is the bathroom.  It is very small which is okay, but doesn't have a shower curtain.  Therefore, water goes everywhere. I removed 2 stars for this.  If not for the shower oversight, then I would definitely stay again without hesitation.

Oh by the way, the 5 euro breakfast is not worth it. A sandwich with cheese,  coffee and oj.

We had a reservation for September 2023. We paid approximately 400 Euros in advance. 2 days before the travel date, I went to the hospital due to an urgent illness, and a decision was made for emergency surgery. I reached the hotel and explained the situation clearly. I guaranteed that I would send all reports and documents if necessary. I requested a postponement or refund of the reservation. However, they said in a very rude and arrogant way that this would not happen. They took all the money and it was not refunded. A new date was not offered.
Money is not everything in life. Sometimes you need to show your human side. I never liked the zero understanding approach to an important issue like illness. People and companies with this understanding should not survive. Therefore, you should definitely avoid establishing a commercial relationship with this hotel.

First, ignore most of not all of the one stars. They are locals unhappy that this place is sometimes used for parties.

We stayed in the terrace on top floor. It gave an incredible view of surrounding area including Acropolis. There are stairs that go to the roof that allow for a 360 degree view of the city. The hot water seems to be a common thing at AirBnBs and other booking places in Athens. Wasn't that much of a hassle. Hot tub gave great relaxation after a full day of walking. Metro was maybe a ten minute walk. Bus came right outside of apartment.

Very nice apartment to stay for a weekend in Athens! It is exactly close to supermarket, 15-20’ minutes walk to the Train Station of Kallithea and close to cafés, etc…
Moreover, the apartment was somehow clean and easy to access from the road which there is a lift outside (it takes ages to go up to the last floor!!!😂)!
Some ants 🐜 in the kitchen but overall OK! One thing was the shower water was all over the bathroom because of no fence/wall to cover it.
Nice veranda, sofa, living room etc.
At last, you pay what you get for!

The location is far from the main attractions. Metro is 20 minutes walk. By metro  the way to center takes about 30 minutes. The bus is nearby it takes about 30 minutes to the center. Taxi one way costs 10-15 euros. So its better to choose other place to stay if  the main goal in Athens is to visit main attractions.
Hot water  is not convenient. Every time before taking a hot shower, you need to turn on the water heater, after 20 minutes the water will be ready (it will turn off later automatically). When you return tired to the apartment, you won’t be able to immediately take a shower.
There is no administration in the building. Communication is remotely, via messengers. The administration do not always answer sometimes for 6 hours of waiting or ignore you and  have to ask twice.
Probably due to the lack of supervision, apartments are often rented for parties. And in the apartments for two guests, stay companies for 10-15 people. There is no soundproofing and it is not possible to sleep in neighboring apartments course of loud music and drunken screams until 6 am.
Good for night parties, bad for usual stay.

Though I did see ashtrays in hallways, I did not smell any smoke at all. With the exception of some loud talking from the people across the hall a few times, the place was very quiet. There was a heavy plastic “shade” door(which made the room pitch back in the middle of the day) and a heavy interior sliding glass door that drowned out most of the street noise. The parking situation is pretty bad. It’s in a neighborhood that is all street parking. It’s free and has very few no parking areas which is great but it can be difficult to find spaces. I did not deduct any stars because this is hardly the hotels fault. Just something to keep in mind if you intend to rent a car as I did. To be honest, unless you’re making a day trip at some point, you probably don’t need a car. Everything’s pretty close. I intend to visit again someday and I will absolutely look here first.

Easy check in, rooms are compact but have everything you need, well presented and comfortable. Breakfast is fairly basic but good. The roof top pool and bar is the real selling point, it's lovely. We used this hotel as a relaxing few days before our sightseeing truly began and was perfect for this otherwise would have been a lot of taxis back and forth from the main areas.

The rooms are very modern and well kept. Decent size as well but could be larger. The roof top pool is very nice. It's very busy so it's difficult to get a place to sit in the sun. You really aren't close to anything here and not close to the subway. There are a decent amount of local restaurants to walk to that are very affordable.
I realized after 1 nights stay that they put me in the wrong room. When I spoke to the front desk they said I had to come back to speak to a manger which doesn't make sense to me. I had to come back the next day. I told them that I booked a king size bed and they said that they don't have any available.
I over heads someone having the same issue. It seems they book people with a nicer room and then give them a regular room and don't say anything and don't offer any compensation. That's not a good way to run a hotel.

Short stay in Athens, initially own error when booking, as misdirected on website: Reservation was registered for another location. After discussion we found a compromise, for which we were very (!) grateful.
The hotel, which is still quite new, is very pleasant, the view from the rooftop restaurant is great: thanks to the location halfway between the city centre and the coast, the Acropolis on one side and the sea on the other. The pool above the rooftops is also great, just a little shallow. The staff were courteous. We would love to stay again. And above all, many thanks for the constructive, customer-friendly approach!!!

Loved this hotel. New, modern and clean. Rooms were spacious and comfortable. The rooftop pool and restaurant were very nice and the gym was nice. Not a huge gym, but enough for a workout. The staff were very nice. The location isn't central, but it was easy enough to get an uber. I'd definitely stay here again.

There were some good and some bad points  about this hotel, but overall, we wouldn’t stay there again, especially for the price.

Negative Points:
The location was the biggest letdown. We should have done more research, as there’s nothing within walking distance. It took at least 30 minutes to reach anything near the center, and the area around the hotel was dirty and felt unsafe, especially at night, with homeless and disoriented people in the area. We ended up using taxis for every trip, which cost 7-10 EUR each way, adding around 20 EUR extra to our daily expenses.

The rooms were another disappointment. The first room we booked was so incredibly small that we upgraded to a Premium double room, but even that wasn’t much bigger. After one night, we had to switch rooms again because the walls were so thin, making it feel like we were sharing the space with a stranger. We could hear every word and sound coming from next door. All the rooms we saw were very dark, with no natural light or view. A large building was just 2-3 meters away from the windows. Also, there’s no separate bathroom—the sink is right next to the bed, and the shower is just a step away, meaning the room constantly felt humid. When we moved the armchair, we saw mold on the walls. I also found the smell in the room unpleasant—it had a damp, tobacco-like odor. When I mentioned it to the receptionist, I was surprised to learn that it’s the hotel’s “signature scent,” intentionally used in all the rooms.

The pool area was generally nice, but it felt awkward to be half-naked while people in the restaurant watched, as the dining area and pool are in the same space.

Positive Points:
On the upside, the staff was very friendly and helpful throughout our stay. The gym was well-equipped with high-quality Technogym machines, and the breakfast was fantastic, with a great variety of fresh food, including plant-based milk options like oat and soy, as well as fresh vegetables, fruits, and pastries.

Conclusion:
Overall, given the price (over 1,100 EUR for 4 nights), this hotel did not meet expectations. The location, in particular, was a huge inconvenience, and the quality of the rooms was below what we expected.

Erta made our stay warm and very inviting, nothing was too much trouble, The rooms were spacious and clean, bathrooms and kitchenette were fully stocked. Very comfy bed and couch and the cafe up the road even delivered breakfast at a discounted rate through a deal PNOĒ has with Peggy Sue. If I could give more than 5 stars I would.
Thanks for having us :)
-Mel

Gorgeous Hotel with a stunning view! The rooms are big and everything is so clean. The staff is super friendly and helpful in any matter. We really enjoyed our stay here and will definitely pick this hotel again for our next visit in Athens!

This place is absolutely fantastic!  Brand new with a modern aesthetic, super clean, and spacious rooms. The staff is outstanding. They were extremely helpful and friendly, always greeting us with a smile and asking what they could do to improve our stay. I'd give them ten stars if I could!

We stayed for three nights and had a great time. The service was excellent; the staff is extremely friendly and goes out of their way to assist as much as possible. There are cafes and restaurants nearby, and the hotel is generally very accommodating! I wholeheartedly recommend it!

Great location, right between the centre of Athens and the sea. Bus stop 1min from hotel, amazing helpful stuff, they helped us with transport, told us everything important. Raise Athens Metro Apartments is a holiday apartment rental in Dafni, near Athens. It's very close to Dafni metro station on line 2 (only a 2 minute walk away!), and the city center from there is only 7 minutes away! This property has a very good price to rating ratio, so we couldn't resist! The building looks nice and modern and you can get to the apartments via stairs or elevator, so there's a nice element of accessibility! You get into the apartment by entering a key code that they give you via the Booking.com chat. Our apartment looked very nice! It had a living room with a sofa bed, a kitchen, a bedroom, a bathroom and a balcony with a beautiful mountain and city view! The living room and bedroom had air conditioners that worked wonderfully and the living room had Netflix on the TV, which we really appreciated. The kitchen had everything we needed (too bad there wasn't a microwave, but that wasn't a problem for us). The beds were comfy and tidy and the bathroom was nice and clean. The toilet flushed as expected, the shower worked nicely (adjusting the water temperature was a bit finicky, but it wasn't a big deal) and the washbasin wasn't clogged. But you must remember to put your toilet paper in the trash can next to the toilet. Greek pipes are too narrow to accommodate toilet paper. Overall, a great holiday apartment rental which I highly recommend for any Athenian tourist.

Raise Athens Metro Apartments is a holiday apartment rental in Dafni, near Athens. It's very close to Dafni metro station on line 2 (only a 2 minute walk away!), and the city center from there is only 7 minutes away! This property has a very good price to rating ratio, so we couldn't resist! The building looks nice and modern and you can get to the apartments via stairs or elevator, so there's a nice element of accessibility! You get into the apartment by entering a key code that they give you via the Booking.com chat. Our apartment looked very nice! It had a living room with a sofa bed, a kitchen, a bedroom, a bathroom and a balcony with a beautiful mountain and city view! The living room and bedroom had air conditioners that worked wonderfully and the living room had Netflix on the TV, which we really appreciated. The kitchen had everything we needed (too bad there wasn't a microwave, but that wasn't a problem for us). The beds were comfy and tidy and the bathroom was nice and clean. The toilet flushed as expected, the shower worked nicely (adjusting the water temperature was a bit finicky, but it wasn't a big deal) and the washbasin wasn't clogged. But you must remember to put your toilet paper in the trash can next to the toilet. Greek pipes are too narrow to accommodate toilet paper. Overall, a great holiday apartment rental which I highly recommend for any Athenian tourist.

The area is very calm and feels safe. Public transportation is near (tram/bus/trolley bus).

Clean and spacious rooms, given the price level. Make sure to tell the reception when you want the room cleaned, it is not done automatically. Clean towels are delivered packed in plastic. The air condition worked well and there was always enough hot water. There was a small fridge with a tiny freezer that worked well. The rooms had some signs of needing maintenance and there could have been more places to hang used towels and shelfs for placing clothes etc. Nice balcony! Drawback that the smell of smoke would come from the balcony.

The staff working during our visit was friendly and helpful. The reception is open 24/7 and the staff recognize everyone staying there so it feels rather safe. Very practical that one can buy snacks and drinks at any time. The breakfast was always the same: toast, an egg, a chocolate croissant, a glass of juice, a glass of water and a choice of coffee/tea/cold milk. There are many cafeterias nearby where you can also eat breakfast.

It is easy to take public transportation to places like Acropolis and beaches. There's a direct bus from the airport (X96). The mini market right next to the hotel sells tickets. If you plan to make more than 6 single trips in Athens it is cheaper to buy the five day pass.

All in all the hotel is a good choice given the price. The biggest drawback is the constant smell of smoke/e-cigarettes in the reception area. Not sure if this is a common issue in Greece, but if you're very sensitive to smoke the hotel is not the best choice.

It seemed to take forever so I wouldn't book it due to location.  Others with  our AC vacation group stayed at a hotel much closer to the city centre.  that looked very nice.

I really wanted to love this hotel. It's definitely a 2 star hotel, and honestly they're trying to do the best they can with what they have.
Pros
- good location for price. I needed one night before a sailing trip and this was a good compromise between my departure point and the sights of Athens.
- generous check in/out times. Both check in and check out is midday. And my room was ready when I arrived at 9am (planning to leave my bags) and they generously let me go straight in.
- bus stops nearby. You just need to have prepurchased your ticket! Also the x96 bus from the airport will drop you very close for 5.5 euro.
- if you stay here, make sure you go across the road to see the cultural centre with the most amazing "green roof"!
- it's clean
- very comprehensive breakfast that includes cooked eggs, bacon etc as well as continental and traditional Greek foods (spinach pies are so good). I can't vouch for the coffee but I think you can get espresso for extra €
-24hr front desk with helpful staff who speak very good English.
- effective air conditioning in the room
- elevator access
- good Wi-Fi

Cons
- it is old. Very run down.
- shower bracket was broken, which I reported, but it couldn't be fixed during my stay obviously.
- the shower temperature is affected easily? During one shower it went hot/cold /hot etc, but not every shower
- they provided a kettle in the room, but I don't know why. There was no tea etc or even any mugs.
- the beds were HARD. All my bony prominences hurt. The pillows were also quite hard.
- hotels.com advertised a rooftop terrace?? You can certainly access the roof, going past a collection of junk and broken things along with stored toilet paper, however it is certainly not intended for guests I'm sure. Which is a huge pity. It has great potential, but like so many places in Greece the economy has not been kind, and as I said, they seem to be doing the best they cash with what they have. I noted on my floor, one of the rooms appeared to be in the middle of refurbishment, so perhaps they are slowly getting around to each room.
All in all its a good budget stay.
I would stay here again.  For one or two people this perfect.  If I go to Greece alone, I will definitely use this Hotel again.

Wonderful hotel! Located in a very lovely area near the sea and close to several bus stops to city center as well as tram stop. Room was very clean and well mentained with a small balcony. Great classic European breakfast, shady lounge zone near the entrance for smokers and people who just want to chill outside. Helpful staff. All in all highly recommend and personally next time I go to Athens I'll sure choose Nestorion hotel again! Thank you!

Hotel room had bugs probably some kind of Bettel

If you need to go for the sightseeing

It is 20 euro it will take you to Acropolis etc.. the main city sightseeing places so you can explore in that bus and you can even come back to you are hotel from the same stop which is opposite site of the hotel they will drop you off 3 min walk to the hotel area.

There are no any restaurants near this hotel that's the only problem
If you are looking for asisan food you can't find any
Even KFC or MCDONALD there is nothing near by

Location is nice and convenient, but quite far from the airport and there is no shuttle service. The service is really nice, but the rooms are not very large and the bathroom in my room was tiny with even tinier shower space and sink.

Smallest bathroom sink ever. Whoever designed this hotel bathroom was an amateur. Hotel itself is nice, breakfast is mediocre, location excellent, very safe, next to government buildings with security, easily you can park outside.

I have stayed in this hotel for a total of 5 times with an average of 4 days per visit. The purpose of traveling was professional as I was attending seminars nearby. Each time I visited I stayed in a different room of either the 3rd or the 4th floor.

+ Pros
A great value-for-money hotel not far away from points of interest, especially for a tourist. Being right next to the SNF, it’s a great opportunity to go for a stroll around. There’re also several bus stops nearby. The beds are comfy, soft and always clean. The employees are all very pleasant and helpful. Room service, even in late hours, was always fast. There’s also free underground parking available.

- Cons
Your ability to join the hotel’s wifi will really depend on the room you will get. Sometimes I had perfect signal, other times I couldn’t even find it. The bathroom and specifically the shower is unfortunately very small resulting to water splashing all over the floor. A weird disadvantage is also that in some rooms the toilet flush-ability is really weak.

Overall, it was a pleasant stay!

Really different bright cheerful hotel. Staff brilliant  even room service which is unheard of in most places now.  The only downside was the bathroom  still large but very badly designed  as you sat on the toilet with your feet in the shower. No shower curtain or glass water everywhere. Hopefully justbthat room as I would definitely  return.

The location is right behind a bus stop for 10. And an extended walk to a metro station (Sygou-fix). The service staff speak both Greek and English.

My only complaint is the bathroom.  It is very small which is okay, but doesn't have a shower curtain.  Therefore, water goes everywhere. I removed 2 stars for this.  If not for the shower oversight, then I would definitely stay again without hesitation.

Oh by the way, the 5 euro breakfast is not worth it. A sandwich with cheese,  coffee and oj.
Worst experience I've ever had. Booked through air bnb and waited infront iof it for 2 hours for someone to bring keys. No one appeared.

“Our stay at the apartment was wonderful! The place has undergone a complete renovation, with modern and beautiful design, and the cleanliness was spotless. It has a lovely balcony and a large inner garden full of flowers. The location is excellent—just a three-minute walk to the supermarket and close to Nea Smyrni Square, great tavernas, and bakeries. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a comfortable and inviting place to stay in Athens!”

I stayed in the Nea Smyrni neighborhood in a newly designed, modern and inviting apartment, the owner was available and charming.
The building has been renovated from the ground up and is tastefully decorated with all the necessities needed in a modern home, the shower is well designed and fun.
Air conditioner in every room, heating by gas system, alarm system, intercom and complete feeling of security.
I loved the balcony overlooking the back yard with a deserted and colorful garden with lots of flowers and pastoral silence in the heart of the vibrant city.
The apartment is located near a bus station and a 12-minute walk to the tram in Nea Smyrni, 15 minutes to a metro station in the Dafni neighborhood and a 15-minute walk to the mall and shopping center in Agios Dimitrios.
We especially enjoyed the proximity to a local tavern with authentic bouzouki music and a Greek atmosphere, 8 minutes' walk away.
Proximity to a large and cheap supermarket.
I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a pleasant, quiet and safe place to sleep in Athens.
Five nights in Athens on a very quiet street. Amazing apartments, spacious rooms, clean, and modern. Excellent location, highly recommended! I will definitely return here on my next trip.

Everything is perfect.
For the service,
I could feel enough korean's warm-hearted hospitality here in this korean guest house from service like my mother treating me.
They provided wonderful guide tour not only inside athens but also outskirt of athens, interesting place such as walking city tour, 3 islands tour and corinthos area tour.
so I could make a beautiful memory through professional guide tour.
They guided me not only tour information of the area but also culture of greece.
The owner coached me how to travel in athens.
lastly, They provide me best korean foods for the breakfast which is Better than my mother's one hhh(Refert to the picture) and also the owner sometimes provided me a cup of wine to cheer me up when I am fully tiresome after long journey.
For the room,
They gave me clean and comfortable room.
It has beautiful view toward The temple of partenon which was clearly visible in the living room and terrace and even some rooms.(refer to the picture)
For the location,
It was 4minutes away from subway and you can reach the most crowded downtown by 2 stops and the location is where it is near united states ambassy and rich town so that it was very safe. I prefer to differentiate where I sleep and where I hangout so that here is the best place for me. even public transportation runs up to 1 am which give me free to move.

In summary, I will visit there again as soon as possible.

here is good choice.

We rented a two-bedroom apartment in Dafni, Greece, through Airbnb for 11 days in June, and our experience was exceptional. This was our first time using Airbnb, and Renata, our host, made it truly memorable. The apartment was impeccably clean, well-stocked, and exactly as depicted in the photos, making us feel instantly at home. The check-in and entry/exit process was seamless. Renata deserves every bit of her 5-star rating. We were especially delighted by the complimentary Rosé and savory snacks at check-in.
